entries.title: Space Crab
entries.content: This alien scavenger with no eyes skitters along the ground on all fours, raising its forelegs in response to threats. Only when it is pouncing midair does its target realize the enormous mouth on its underside. Level 0 Tiny Aberration Defense Traits | AC | 12 | 16 home field | | ------------------------------------------------- | ------------------------- | ----------------- | | HP | 20 | | | Poise | 15 | DRA 10 home field | | Saves | Fort -1, Refl +3, Will +0 | | Resistance - Necrotic Home Field Advantage - While attached to a creatures head, the space crab gains DRA 10 and +4 AC. Offense Traits (DC 12) | Bite (P) | +1 | | | -------------------------------------------------- | -------- | -------- | | | d8+1 (5) | +4 vs DD | Innate Techniques - Twist the Knife Sneak Attack - Against a DD target, a space crab does an additional 4 damage. New Home - Against a defeated creature, a space crab can attach to the creature's head and gain control over the actions of the soon to be corpse. After 1 hour, it kills the creature and reanimates it as a zombie as though casting animate dead, but the creature retains its full level. It can then spend all of its actions each turn to command this undead. Only through the space crab's or it's host's death will it detach. When the space crab is killed, the host automatically dies. The space crab must use this on a creature that is initially alive. It cannot use this ability on a creature that was already dead. Use the zombie stat block for generic humanoid host. Hosts can be anything; the space crab just has to get lucky. Weaknesses/Deep Lore What's Poppin? - A space crab can only make an attack roll in the same turn it takes the Jump action. It does not threaten. Other Traits Ability Scores - STR -1, DEX +1, LUK +1, INT -3, WIS +0, CHA +0 Senses - Sightless Blindsense, Lifesense (60 ft) Speed - 10 Land Speed Enhanced Leap - When the space crab jumps, it moves 30 feet, regardless of its speed, as long as it is not 0. Feats - Skill Focus Skills - +2 +Perception, +5 (adv) +Stealth

entries.title: Ayylmao
entries.content: A small creature fused with a flying saucer floats overhead. It gazes upon the farmer's cattle as it pulls out its probes. An electrified pulse of light reflects off of the terrified cow's eyes. Level 3 Small Aberration Defense Traits | AC | 15 | | | ------------------------------------------------- | ------------------------- | --- | | HP | 44 | | | Poise | 21 | | | Saves | Fort +3, Refl +5, Will +5 | | Flyby Attack - An ayylmao's movement is Elusive against targets it has attacked this round. Offense Traits (DC 15) | Probe (Zap) | +5 | | | ----------------------------------------------------- | -------- | ----------- | | | 3d6 (10) | +Discomfort | Innate Techniques (CL 6) - Detect Thoughts 1/day - Malleable Memory Discomfort - Ayylmao like sticking their probes in unusual places. Creatures are sent reeling and are made Clumsy 1 On-hit. Against a DD or grappled target, this increases to clumsy 2. Tractor Beam (Su) concentration - Unleash a ray at a creature within 30 ft. It is entangled (reflex negates). DC 20 Breakout. While entangled, the creature maintains the exact distance away from the ayylmao, even if it moves. This allows the ayylmao to slam it into the ground, drag it along walls etc. Every turn it moves at least 10 feet deals 4d6 (14) crush damage. If the ayylmao chooses, it can spend its own movement to beam the creature closer, or push it away, but no farther than 30 feet away. Weaknesses/Deep Lore Milkers - There's only one thing an ayylmao likes more than anal probing, and that's cows. If any cows or cow-like creatures are present, an ayylmao will always focus on kidnapping them with their tractor beams over anything else. A substantial quantity of cow milk gives creatures advantage on checks in social situations with ayylmao. Other Traits Ability Scores - STR +0, DEX +2, LUK +0, INT +3, WIS +0, CHA +0 Telepathy Cloaking Device - Ayylmao can take 10 on +Stealth (resulting in 22 for the average ayylmao). They are always considered to have partial concealment while the cloaking device is active. The cloaking device is deactivated when an ayylmao does anything other than movement, or it takes damage. The only exception is if the action has the subtle tag. Speed - 10, 40 Hover. An ayylmao can use its hover speed to fly, but only during its turn. Takeoff - An ayylmao can return to the mother ship by spending 3 consecutive standard actions activating turbo thrusters. It also leaves with any creature caught in its tractor beam. Equipment - Anal Probe Feats - Skill Focus, Signature Skill (stealth) Skill - +5 +Perception, 22 +Stealth, +8 Arcana, +12 Engineering, +8 +Cryptozoology, +8 +Nature

entries.title: Shadowling, Scout
entries.content: A black insectoid creature skitters along a wall, a ball of glowing red energy you assume to be its eye faces you. The last thing you remember is it vanishing into a vapor and entering your lungs. Level 3 Small Aberration Defense Traits | AC | 17 | | | ------------------------------------------------- | ------------------------- | --- | | HP | 44 | | | Poise | 23 | | | Saves | Fort +3, Refl +6, Will +6 | | Resistance - Cold Pool Recharge - As an swift action, a shadowling can turn into an amorphous dark liquid. They can choose to maintain this form indefinitely, but cannot attack while pooled. While in this form, they can move along any surface, including liquids, walls and roofs. They are also amorphous (AC+15). This form can be ended as a free action. This ability cannot be used in consecutive rounds. Offense Traits (DC 15) | Stomp (P) | +6 | | --------------------------------------------------- | ---------- | | | 2d6+3 (10) | Multiattack - Make 2 stomp attacks Innate Techniques - Wave of Darkness, Nearsighted, Toe Smasher, Frenzied Leap 1/day - Terminal Isolation (U2) Possession Su Recharge - A shadowling can transform into a vapor and advance rapidly towards a creature within 30 ft of them, inhabiting their body. The extent to which varies based on the targets level. Shadowlings cannot possess creatures that are a higher level than them. Ing always instinctually understand what they can possess and how to activate their hosts abilities. Against a defeated creature, possession always functions as though the creature was a minion (as long as the creature is not a higher LvL). - Same or Lower Level - The shadowling takes control of the creatures actions (will negates). During the possessed creatures turn, instead the ing acts. All damage taken by the host also damages the ing possessing the creature (up to a maximum of the hosts current hp). At the end of each turn the ing possesses, they get a new save to end the effect. The shadowling can choose to end this effect as a free action. When it fails or ends, willing or not, they appear within 10 ft of the creature they targeted. - Minion - The shadowling has full control over the creature while it possesses them. The effect has no save and lasts indefinitely. The shadowling takes damage equal to half the damage its host takes (up to a maximum of half hosts current hp). The ing can choose to end this effect as a free action. When it ends, willing or not, they appear within 10 ft of the creature they targeted. Weaknesses/Deep Lore The light, it burns! - While in direct sunlight, a shadowling is persistently set ablaze, but it instead deals true damage. This weakness is suppressed while it possesses a creature. Weakness - Holy 3. When a shadowling takes holy damage while possessing a creature, it must make a will save (DC 10+possessed creatures LvL) or be ejected. Other Traits Ability Scores - STR +0, DEX +3, LUK +0, INT +0, WIS +1, CHA +0 Languages - Primordial, Common Darkvision Speed - 45 Mad Hops Feats - Skill Focus, Signature Skill (stealth) Skill - +8 +Perception, +10 (adv) +Stealth, +10 +Acrobatics, +5 +Athletics

An electrified pulse of light reflects off of the terrified cow's eyes. entries.title: Cow Amalgam
entries.content: The ultimate being and the culmination of the Ayylmao research. This creature has the combined power (and flesh) of 100 cows! The greatest part? It's self replicating. Level 4 Large Aberration/Beast Defense Traits | AC | 18 | | | ------------------------------------------------- | -------------------------- | --- | | HP | 77 | | | Poise | 27 | | | Saves | Fort +6, Refl +11, Will +4 | | Amorphous - +15 Offense Traits (DC 16) | Unarmed (C) | +9 | | | ----------------------------------------------------- | ---------- | --- | | | 3d6+3 (13) | | Innate Techniques (CL 1) - Power Overwhelming Special Attack: 8-Sided Stomp - Make a stomp attack against all creatures within reach. Can be used an unlimited number of times a combat. Breath Weapon (Su) Poison Recharge - A cow amalgam can vomit a torrent of rotten milk from its many mouths. Targets a 30 ft cone, dealing 6d6 (21) poison damage, making creatures wet and sickened 2 (reflex halves damage and negates additional status). Must use its Calciumancer ability to recharge. Calciumancer (Su) - A cow amalgam can target any female mammalian creature within 30 ft and drain them of their milk (even if they aren't currently producing). The creature is drained 8 and made incredibly uncomfortable (fort reduces drain to stagger for 1 round). Spawn Companion - A cow amalgam under duress will spit out a single Cow Amalgam Spawn at the start of its turn unless it already has 2 fighting by its side. Weaknesses/Deep Lore Jolt - Whenever a cow amalgam takes zap damage, it immediately recoils away from the source. It and its spawn are unable to attack creatures that has dealt zap damage to it in the last 1 round. Other Traits Ability Scores - STR +2, DEX +3, LUK +0, INT -3, WIS +0, CHA +0 Speed - 45 Land Speed, can be used to walk along any surface Feats - Escape Route, Toughness Skill - +6 +Perception, +8 +Athletics], +9 +Stealth

entries.title: Shadow
entries.content: A small creature fused with a flying saucer floats overhead. It gazes upon the farmer's cattle as it pulls out its probes. An electrified pulse of light reflects off of the terrified cow's eyes. Level 4 Medium Aberration Defense Traits | AC | 18 | | | ------------------------------------------------- | ------------------------- | ------------- | | HP | 60 | | | Poise | 27 | DRA 2 | | Saves | Fort +7, Refl +8, Will +4 | | KYS Healing - Kopy yourself. While a creature a shadow is copying is defeated and within its natural reach, you can absorb data to repair damage as a swift action, healing 20 hp. Offense Traits (DC 16) | Unarmed (C) | +7 | +2 Real Deal | | ----------------------------------------------------- | ---------- | ------------ | | | 2d6+3 (10) | | Multiattack - Make 2 Melee Attacks Innate Techniques - Kidnap, Nice Catch 1/day - Toe Smasher, Feign Defeat Real Deal - While copying a creature, gain +2 attack against it. I'll Have What She's Having Su - As a swift action, a shadow can use it's copy ability to create a fully functional handheld object it can see within 30 ft. If the object is magical, it loses its magical properties. If the copied object gets more than 100 ft from the original, it disappears. Using copy in this way does not stun the shadow. Weaknesses/Deep Lore Don't Tread on Me - Shadows are just that, shadows. Anything it copies is merely a projection. As such, it does not like it when objects interact with the shadow of its projection. It avoids people stepping on it, creatures walking over it etc. If the shadow's shadow is targeted directly, or included in an AoE effect, its DRA is ignored. If you target a shadow with a piercing melee weapon, you can embed it in the surface and entangle the shadow on hit or with a successful poise break, replacing all other effects and losing access to the weapon. Projectiles like arrows require 3 hits. Shadows cannot remove the entangling items on their own. Other Traits Ability Scores - STR +3, DEX +2, LUK +0, INT +0, WIS +0, CHA +0 Languages - All non-secret. Copy (Su) poly, true - As a FRA, a shadow can emit a very bright flash of light while gazing upon a specific creature within 30 ft. When it does so It adds this creature to its memory. It can choose to immediately transform into this creature as part of this action. The replication process is almost perfect, giving the shadow access to the creatures memories, skill proficiencies, personality traits, current clothing, appearance, size etc. The shadow gains all of the race features of the creature it copies or all of the natural attacks (including multi attack options)/movement abilities if it is not a playable race. A shadow can "memorize" copies of 4 different creatures/objects (see offense traits). It can transform into one as a standard action, or revert to its true form as a free action. When copying a creature, treat its +Disguise roll as 26. Speed - Speed of copied creature. Can choose to enter a hybrid form, breaking its disguise to move at a 40 Land Speed or Climb speed Feats - Unarmed Combatant, Bonded Mind, Feint Partner Skill - +6 +Perception, +6 +Deception, +8 +Stealth ![image.avif|Shide, from the anime, [Summertime Render|321]]

entries.title: Mind Flayer
entries.content: Masters of the psionic arts, these tentacled-lipped mages are vaguely humanoid with wet undulating orifices. They scour the universe for hosts to fuel their endless hunger for knowledge...Literally. Level 8 Medium Aberration Defense Traits | AC | 23 (medium armor) | | | ------------------------------------------------- | --------------------------- | --- | | HP | 84 | | | Poise | 30 | | | Saves | Fort +9, Refl +10, Will +10 | | Resistance - Psychic Break Will Su Charm - As an immediate action when hit by an attack, a mind flayer can attempt to charm the attacking creature for 1 round (will negates). Offense Traits (DC 20) | Tentacles (C) | +10 | +4 MV | | ------------------------------------------------------- | -------- | ------------------------------- | | | d8+2 (6) | | Innate Techniques CL 14 - Mind Crush (U4), Detect Thoughts, Telekinetic Maneuver 1/day - Telekinetic Thrust Constant - Telekinetic Lift, Tongues Brain Blast (Su) Psychic Recharge - Creatures in a 30 ft cone take 6d6 (21) psychic damage and are dazed for 1 round (will halves damage and reduces stun to Staggered). A mind flayer must devour intellect, wait 1 minute or concentrate as a FRA to gain use of this ability again. Displacer (Su) (Psychic) - When a mind flayer causes a creature to take Fall Damage, it takes an additional 5 psychic damage. Devour Intellect (recharge) - Special: Can only be used against the creature that is dazed, grappled or defeated. Spend a standard action to chomp on a creatures brain. Creature takes 10d8 (45) pierce dmg (fort halves). A mind flayer must digest for at least an hour before it can devour intellect again. Weaknesses/Deep Lore Psychic Casting - A mind flayer requires a higher degree of concentration to use many of its psionic abilities. It cannot use its supernatural abilities or its innate techniques while it is frightened, dominated or confused. Other Traits Ability Scores - STR +1, DEX +2, LUK +0, INT +4, WIS +0, CHA +1 Telepathy - A mind flayer cannot speak. +Blindsense - Thoughtsense 30 ft Speed - 30, 40 hover During its turn, a mind flayer can use this speed to fly. Feats - Cooperative Casting, MM Coax, Combat Step, Maneuver Versatility, MM Persistence Skill - +10 +Perception, +11 +Deception, +11 +Persuasion, +14 +Cryptozoology, +14 Arcana, +14 +Planes

entries.title: Shadowling, Warrior
entries.content: The generals of the Shadowling armies. They seek to bring down powerful foes so that way they may take control of them. They use them to inhabit the brighter parts of the world so that they may spread their corrupting influence. Level 8 Large Aberration Defense Traits | AC | 21 | | | ------------------------------------------------- | --------------------------- | --- | | HP | 84 | | | Poise | 34 | | | Saves | Fort +12, Refl +11, Will +9 | | Resistance - Cold Pool Recharge - As an swift action, a shadowling can turn into an amorphous dark liquid. They can choose to maintain this form indefinitely, but cannot attack while pooled. While in this form, they can move along any surface, including liquids, walls and roofs. They are also amorphous (AC+15). This form can be ended as a free action. This ability cannot be used in consecutive rounds. Offense Traits (DC 20) Stromp (P) +11 2d8+3 (12) (+2/+4 predatory instincts) Gloombolt (Necrotic) +11 Short 6d6 (21) Multiattack - Make 2 stomp attacks Innate Techniques - Wave of Darkness, Nearsighted (U4), Toe Smasher, Frenzied Leap, Terminal Isolation (U2) 1/day - Unseen Predator Constant - Predatory Instincts Possession Su Recharge - A shadowling can transform into a vapor and advance rapidly towards a creature within 30 ft of them, inhabiting their body. The extent to which varies based on the targets level. Shadowlings cannot possess creatures that are a higher level than them. Ing always instinctually understand what they can possess and how to activate their hosts abilities. Against a defeated creature, possession always functions as though the creature was a minion (as long as the creature is not a higher LvL). - Same or Lower Level - The shadowling takes control of the creatures actions (will negates). During the possessed creatures turn, instead the shadowing acts. All damage taken by the host also damages the shadowing possessing the creature (up to a maximum of the hosts current hp). At the end of each turn the ing possesses, they get a new save to end the effect. The shadowling can choose to end this effect as a free action. When it fails or ends, willing or not, they appear within 10 ft of the creature they targeted. - Minion - The shadowling has full control over the creature while it possesses them. The effect has no save and lasts indefinitely. The shadowling takes damage equal to half the damage its host takes (up to a maximum of half hosts current hp). The shadowing can choose to end this effect as a free action. When it ends, willing or not, they appear within 10 ft of the creature they targeted. Corruption (Su) (Recharge) Necrotic - As a swift action, a shadowling can invade a creatures body without taking control of it, eroding it from the inside. The creature must be within reach. While within the creatures body, the shadowling cannot be targeted with attacks and the targeted creature is staggered. The shadowling exits and appears within 30 ft of the target at the start of its next turn. The target creature takes 8d6 (28) necrotic damage and is staggered for 1 round (fort halves dmg and negates stagger). Weaknesses/Deep Lore The light, it burns! - While in direct sunlight, a shadowling is persistently set ablaze, but it instead deals true damage. This weakness is suppressed while it possesses a creature. Weakness - Holy 3. When a shadowling takes holy damage while possessing a creature, it must make a will save (DC 10+possessed creatures LvL) or be ejected. Other Traits Ability Scores - STR +2, DEX +3, LUK +0, INT +0, WIS +1, CHA +1 Languages - Primordial, Common Darkvision Speed - 45 Mad Hops Feats - Skill Focus, Signature Skill (stealth), Combat Reflexes, Fighter's Lament, Escape Route Skill - +11 +Perception, +15 (adv) +Stealth, +15 +Acrobatics, +12 +Athletics, +10 +Cryptozoology, +10 +Planes

entries.title: Xenomorph
entries.content: A creature lurking in the shadows emerges with a horrifying scream. Even after repeated hits, it seems resilient beyond belief and relishes sitting in the middle of chaotic battlefield where Level 8 Large Aberration Defense Traits | AC | 22 | | | ------------------------------------------------- | --------------------------- | -------- | | HP | 116 | | | Poise | 34 | DR 3 | | Saves | Fort +13, Refl +11, Will +8 | | Resistance - Cold Danger Tail - Xenomorphs cannot be flanked Offense Traits (DC 20) | Claw (S) | +11 | (+4 MV, +2 TotF) | | ------------------------------------------------------------ | ---------- | ------------------------------------------------------------------ | | | 2d8+3 (12) | | | Tail (C or P or S) | +11 | (+4 MV, +2 TotF) | | | 2d8+3 (12) | | Multiattack - Make 3 melee attacks Innate Techniques - Jaws of the Beast, Ferocious Howl (U4) 1/day - Warcry Constant - Thick of the Fight Caustic Blood Poison Recharge - When a xenomorph loses more than 10 hp from an attack, or it takes bleed damage, its blood reacts explosively to open air. The xenomorph can choose a creature within 30 ft take 6d6 (21) poison damage (reflex halves). Can only happen 1/round. Bite^2 - If a xenomorph starts its turn grappling a creature, it can use its second mouth to blast a hole in the creature as a maintain option. The creature takes 8d6 (28) pierce damage and is drained 6 (fort halves pierce damage). Weaknesses/Deep Lore Hibernation - A xenomorph can willingly enter stasis permanently. It only leaves hibernation if it smells a creature. Stabilizing Compound - A xenomorphs blood reacts explosively when exposed to open air, but an informed alchemist can negate that. With a DC 23 alchemy check, an alchemist can create a compound that can disable a xenomorphs caustic blood feature for 1 minute as a FRA. You can apply it as a thrown attack, but it only functions for 1 round if you graze, and is consumed with no effect if you miss. If you poise break the xenomorph, you can apply it instead of the usual effects. Other Traits Ability Scores - STR +3, DEX +2, LUK +0, INT -3, WIS +0, CHA +0 Sightless Blindsense - Scent 10 ft, Echolocation 60 ft Speed - 40 Feats - Maneuver Versatility, Maneuver Master, Tangled, Combat Reflexes Skill - +10 (+8 Sightless Blindsense) +Perception, +12 +Acrobatics, +12 +Stealth, +13 +Athletics

entries.title: Grandma
entries.content: Crust Level 10 Medium Aberration Defense Traits | AC | 22 | | | ------------------------------------------------- | --------------------------- | --- | | HP | 80 | | | Poise | 27 | | | Saves | Fort +13, Refl +11, Will +8 | | Resistance - Cold No Breath - A grandma does not need to breathe and can survive in the vacuum of space. Offense Traits (DC 22) | Bite (P) | +12 | (+4 vs Cookies) | | ------------------------------------------------------------ | ---------- | --------------- | | | 2d8+2 (11) | | | 2h Rolling Pin (C) | +12 | (+4 vs Cookies) | | | 2d8+3 (11) | | Innate Techniques (CL 13) - Wandering Stars, Cosmic Cage, Hellscape, Dead Silent, Violent Accident 1/day - Drain Soul (U5), Rotting Alliance, Ice Beam, Wall of Force, Moonblast, Dark Matter (U5) Constant - Scholars Shield Cookiefication Su curse - With a touch, turn a creature into a sweet treat version of themselves (fort negates). Creatures with bite attacks gain a +4 bonus to attack against cookie creatures. A grandma can make a bite attack as an instant action against a creature that gets cookiefied. Summon Ally (companion) - 2 Wrinklers Grandma's Cookies - A grandma usually puts on a facade as some friendly old widow that has an eye for baked goods. She makes all kinds of cookies for those that live nearby. After 2 weeks of eating grandma's cookies regularly, she can turn a creature into an ally, as though using the Order 66 technique, but only in her true form. She can make this transformation as part of the action to trigger the takeover. Any creature she activates this ability on takes a more eldritch form, looking fleshier than usual. They share a grandma's teamwork feats and gain the Tentacles for Arms technique at will. Weaknesses/Deep Lore Cookies Make the World - Grandmas will treat cookies as currency, with the standard being the chocolate chip cookie being the equivalent of 10 gold pieces. Particularly high quality or rare cookies, and their recipes, can fetch even higher prices. Other Traits Ability Scores - STR +2, DEX +0, LUK +0, INT +3, WIS +0, CHA +3 Telepathy One Mind - A grandma forms a family with up to 3 other grandmas. They can telepathically communicate with their family over any distance. Shape Change (poly, true) - One humanoid grandma Speed - 30 Land Speed Feats - Cooperative Casting, Skill - +12 +Perception, +23 +Profession (baker), +15 Arcana,

entries.title: Beholder
entries.content: A large floating eye argues with itself as its many eye stalks twitch around and hum with magical energy. If finally decides which eye will be the one to seal your doom and it hums with black energy. Level 11 Large Aberration Boss Defense Traits | AC | 26 | | | ------------------------------------------------- | ---------------------------- | --- | | HP | 172 | | | Poise | 34 | | | Saves | Fort +12, Refl +12, Will +12 | | All Around Vision - Immune to being flanked Recovery Offense Traits (DC 24) Bite (P) +12 (+8 exploit) 3d6+1 (11) Innate Techniques (CL 15) - Dispel Magic Gaze Su - A beholder has many eyes, but one particularly large one it uses to cancel out magical effects. At the start of the beholder's turn, it decides to focus it's gaze on a single creature it can see (free action). The creature is siphoned for 1 round (will negates). It cannot use its eye stalks ability against this target for 1 round. Eyestalks (Su) - The beholder's extra eyes have the ability to unleash devastating laser beams at creatures within 100 ft. As a standard action it can shoot three of these laser beams at three different creatures. The effect is rolled randomly (reroll duplicates), more details below. Outside of combat, the beholders are able to exert more fine control over its eye stalks, allowing it to telekinetically move objects and disintegrate holes into things with ease. Feel free to change up a few eye beams to make your beholders extra special. | Roll | Name/Tags | Effect | | ---- | ------------------- | ------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | | 1 | Charm | Creature is dominated and spends its turns attacking its allies (will negates). It cannot use Su or Sp abilities. | | 2 | stun | Creature is stunned (fort negates). | | 3 | Fear | Creature is frightened (will negates). | | 4 | Slow | Creature is staggered (fort negates). | | 5 | drain | Creature is drained 15 (fort halves). This effect is permanent | | 6 | Telekinesis | Briefly take hold of a creature's body (will negates) and throw them across the battlefield. You move them 30 ft and they take 10d6 (35) bludgeoning damage. | | 7 | Sleep | Creature falls asleep (will negates). Creature does not get a new save against sleep each turn and must be manually woken up. | | 8 | Petrify | Targeted is entangled as its flesh turns to stone (fort negates). A creature entangled by this becomes permanently entombed at the end of its next turn (fort negates). | | 9 | Decay Necrotic | Target takes 10d8 (45) necrotic damage (fort halves). | | 10 | Death Ray Force | Target takes 10d10 (55) force damage (reflex halves). If this defeats the creature, they are turned into a fine dust. | Boss Actions - The beholder can perform 2 of the following actions each round. - Eyebeam - Fire a random eyestalk beam at an enemy after they end their turn. - Exploit - Bite a creature suffering a debilitating status from your eyestalks with a +8 bonus. - Inferior Magic recharge - Roll dispel magic to counter a spell as though you had prepared the action. Weaknesses/Deep Lore Boss Mechanics - Whenever a beholder is staggered, instead of its usual effects, it can can only shoot 2 eyestalks on its turn. When it is dazed, instead it can only shoot 1 eyestalk. When it is stunned, its eyestalk ability is disabled, but it can otherwise act normally. When a beholder is siphoned, instead of its usual effects, its primary eye closes and it cannot use its gaze. Whenever a beholder is blinded, instead of gaining the blinded condition, its eyestalk DC decreases by 2. Blurred vision is instead converted into -1 to its eyestalk DC. Other Traits Ability Scores - STR +1, DEX +1, LUK +0, INT +4, WIS -1, CHA -1 Languages - Common, Speed - 10, 35 Hover Feats - Skill Focus, Speak for the Dead, Defensive Training, Ghostbuster, Blind Fight, Matador Skill - +16 +Perception, +17 +Knowledge (all, adv Arcana)

entries.title: Besmolder
entries.content: A tiny floating eyeball shouts with glee as it chases you around. You try and hide, only for it to pop around the corner. "Found you!" It shouts as it shoots an eye beam at you, transforming you into a toy. Level 12 Smol Aberration Boss Defense Traits | AC | 27 | | | ------------------------------------------------- | ---------------------------- | --- | | HP | 184 | | | Poise | 35 | | | Saves | Fort +12, Refl +14, Will +13 | | All Around Vision - Immune to being flanked Recovery Offense Traits (DC 24) Boop (???) +13 ??? Innate Techniques (CL 11) - Dispel Magic Gaze Su Charm - A besmolder has many eyes, but one particularly large one it uses to cancel make new friends. At the start of the besmolder's turn, it decides to focus it's gaze on a single creature it can see (free action). The creature is charmed for 1 round (will negates). It cannot use its eye stalks ability against targets it charms. Eyestalks (Su) - The besmolder's extra eyes have the ability to unleash devastating laser beams at creatures within 100 ft. As a standard action it can shoot three of these laser beams at three different creatures. The effect is rolled randomly (reroll duplicates), more details below. Outside of combat, the besmolder is able to exert more fine control over its eye stalks, allowing it to summon friends, build little villages, telekinetically move objects and disintegrate holes into things with ease. Feel free to change up a few eye beams to make your besmolder's extra special | Roll | Name/Tags | Effect | | ---- | ------------------------------------------------- | --------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | | 1 | Candy Beam Healing, poly, true | Creature is turned into inanimate candy, effectively dazing them (fort negates). The besmolder can lick an adjacent piece of unattended candy as a swift action, dealing 10 True Damage and healing 10 hp. | | 2 | Sunshine and Rainbows | Creature is blinded (reflex negates). | | 3 | Kung Fu Grip | Telekinetically take control of a creature (will negates). You get to decide what they do on their next turn. You cannot activate Su or Sp abilities. Against a defeated creature, turns into a toy. | | 4 | Shrink Ray | Creatures reduced to the size of a mouse. -15 speed and deal half damage (will negates). | | 5 | Fun House | Creature is trapped inside of a tiny building (reflex negates). Instead of making additional saves, it requires an athletics or sleight of hand to check (standard action) to free the creature. | | 6 | Banana Split | A 20 ft radius around the creature gets covered in incredibly slippery banana peels. A creature that starts its turn or walks into the area is knocked down and takes 4d6 (14) crush damage (reflex or acrobatics to Balance negates). | | 7 | Bed Time | Creature falls asleep (will negates). Creature does not get a new save against sleep each turn and must be manually woken up. | | 8 | Bubblebeam Water | Creature is trapped inside of a bubble (reflex negates). Otherwise functions as per Bubble Breath without the concentration tag. | | 9 | Story Time Summon Ally, Companion | Summon a level 4 or lower creature adjacent to target. It acts immediately. (note, you may want to select the besmolder's friends beforehand to make things like tokens more seamless). | | | | Note: A besmolder can only have 2 creatures created at a time out of combat using Story Time but during combat, they can have up to 4. If they already have 4 and roll story time again, reroll that result.When defeated, the creature fades away. | | 10 | Oopsie! Force | Target takes 10d10 (55) force damage (reflex halves). If this defeats the creature, they are turned into a fine dust. | | 11 | Tea Time Mind-Affecting | Target creature is forced to drink from a small teacup until it is empty. They are pacified on their next turn (will negates). | | 12 | The Floor of Lava Persistent | A 40 ft radius around the creature gets covered in liquid Lava 3 ft deep. Creatures in the area, or that move into it, are immediately partially submerged.If the target is flying/hovering, the lava effects the ground directly beneath them. | Friend Magic! - A besmolder can use its MM Caster Minion feat with its eyestalks. Boss Actions - The beholder can perform 2 of the following actions each round. - Eyebeam - Fire a random eyestalk beam at an enemy after they end their turn. - Exploit - Bite a creature suffering a debilitating status from your eyestalks with a +8 bonus. - Inferior Magic recharge - Roll dispel magic to counter a spell as though you had prepared the action. Weaknesses/Deep Lore Boss Mechanics - Whenever a besmolder is staggered, instead of its usual effects, it can can only shoot 2 eyestalks on its turn. When it is dazed, instead it can only shoot 1 eyestalk. When it is stunned, its eyestalk ability is disabled, but it can otherwise act normally. When a besmolder is siphoned, instead of its usual effects, its primary eye closes and it cannot use its gaze. Whenever a besmolder is blinded, instead of gaining the blinded condition, its eyestalk DC decreases by 2. Blurred vision is instead converted into -1 to its eyestalk DC. Story Time! - A besmolder knows much more than even it knows. If prompted to tell stories, its knowledge of history is unmatched and it can practically "remember" anything with vivid detail, though it often exaggerates details to make the story more interesting. When it does so, its history bonus is effectively +30. By making a DC 30 history check, you can parse what parts of the story are facts and which are fiction. Other Traits Ability Scores - STR +1, DEX +2, LUK +2, INT -1, WIS -1, CHA +4 Languages - Common Band Practice - When not under duress, a besmolder can summon an instrument and play it. Speed - 10, 35 Hover Feats - Speak for the Dead, Defensive Training, Ghostbuster, Blind Fight, Matador, MM Caster Minion Skill - +16 +Perception, +16 +History, +17 +Deception, +17 +Persuasion, +27 +Profession (any instrument)

entries.title: Aberrations
entries.content: Aberrations Terrors born from the unknowable and the otherworldly. They cannot be reasoned with or rationalized and are utterly insurmountable nightmare that humanity has no hope of escaping. From the darkest depths of space, the ocean and other mysterious realms, aberrations are often alien creatures uninterested in the conventions of traditional life. Others are strange mixes of the organic and inorganic in unfamiliar animalistic shapes.

entries.title: Love Bug
entries.content: A large bug hovers in the air, a pleasant aroma washing over you from its pheromone-laced wings. Level 0 Small Beast Defense Traits | AC | 13 | | | ------------------------------------------------- | ------------------------- | --- | | HP | 20 | | | Poise | 19 | | | Saves | Fort +2, Refl +2, Will +0 | | Offense Traits (DC 12) | Grapple | +6 | +4 MV | | ------------------------------------------------- | -------------------- | ------------------------------- | | | Inflicts Charmed | | Pheremones Charm - Whenever a love bug successfully grapples a creature, it releases powerful pheromones, charming the creature for 1 minute (fort negates). This charm does not stop the love bug from making grapple attacks. Weaknesses/Deep Lore Juicy - You can extract the juices from a love bug with a DC 12 Alchemy or +Survival check. Used on its own it can inflict the charmed condition for 1 minute on the first creature it sees after ingesting. With a DC 20 alchemy check, it can be turned into a Love Potion. Charmed I'm Sure - Love bugs have Disadvantage on charm effects. Other Traits Ability Scores - STR +0, DEX +2, LUK +0, INT -3, WIS +1, CHA +0 Deific Boons - Animal Instincts Senses - Scent 5 ft, Nightvision Speed - 10 Land Speed, 30 Fly Feats - Maneuver Versatility Skills - +3 (+4 scent) +Perception

entries.title: Angry Chicken
entries.content: This unassuming beast meanders about with its kin. Only when you bother it does it reveal its menacing aura and incredible power. Level 1 Tiny Beast Defense Traits | AC | 13 | | | ------------------------------------------------- | -------------------------------------- | -------- | | HP | 32 | | | Poise | 23 | DR 2 | | Saves | Fort +3 (+4 Enraged), Refl +3, Will +2 | | Offense Traits (DC 13) | Peck (P) | +3 | +2 Enraged | | ------------------------------------------------------ | ---------- | ------------------------ | | | 2d6+2 (9) | +2 Enraged | | New Life (C) | +5 | Thrown, Recharge | | | 3d6+4 (14) | | Multiattack - Must be Enraged. Make 2 peck attacks Innate Techniques - Break Free, Reckless Charge Enrage - While at full hp, the angry chicken is aggressive, but typically not threatening. After it takes any damage, it enrages, increasing its STR to +4, gains a multi-attack and the New Life and Chicken Scratch attacks. Special Attack: Chicken Scratch - Flap up to a creatures face and peck at its eyes. On-hit apply blurred vision for 1 round. Weaknesses/Deep Lore Bed time - If an angry chicken is covered up, or otherwise subject to complete darkness, it must make a DC 10 will save or fall asleep and stop raging. If it is again exposed to light it awakens. Other Traits Ability Scores - STR +0, DEX +2, LUK +0, INT -3, WIS +1, CHA +0 Speed - 30 Land Speed, Glide Deific Boons - Animal Instincts Feats - Back to the Wall Skills - +4 +Perception

entries.title: Giant Frog
entries.content: A frog sits on a the waters surface, wary of these large travelers. Suddenly it grows to 10 ft tall and shoots its tongue out at you. Level 1 Large Beast Defense Traits | AC | 16 | | | ------------------------------------------------- | ------------------------- | ----------------------------- | | HP | 30 | | | Poise | 27 | DR 20 vs Grapple/Drag | | Saves | Fort +2, Refl +6, Will +2 | | Offense Traits (DC 13) | Bite (C) | +4 | | | ---------------------------------------------------- | ---------- | ----------------------------------------------------------------------------- | | | 2d8+3 (12) | | | Tongue (C) | +4 | +4 Drag, +4 MV, +30 ft of Reach during turn | | | 2d6+3 (10) | | Multiattack - Make a tongue and bite attack. Innate Techniques - Frenzied Leap, Kidnap, Metronome 1/day - Power Overwhelming, Lucky Star Average Amphibion - A giant frog can Surprise creatures by using its frog shape ability to pretend to be a tiny frog. Creatures must roll a +Perception check against the frog's DC to avoid being surprised. Swallow - Drained 1 and Sickened 1. Weaknesses/Deep Lore Munch - When you parry a frog's tongue attack, you can choose to feed it a particular item on your person or within reach. It gets Disadvantage on any saving throw against this item. If the item is a weapon, it takes the weapons base damage. Other Traits Ability Scores - STR +1, DEX +3, LUK +3, INT -3, WIS -1, CHA +0 Speed - 30 Land Speed, 30 Swim Hoppin' - A frogg can Jump as far and as high as its speed automatically. It can also jump as a free action. Deific Boons - frog shape, enlightened Blindsense - Scent (5 ft) Amphibious Feats - Maneuver Versatility Skills - +2 (+4) +Perception, +6 +Acrobatics, +4 +Athletics

entries.title: Horse
entries.content: A strange 4-legged creature with a long face trots around. It resembles a unicorn without a horn. Its powerful legs flex as it picks up the pace. entries.title: Stinger
entries.content: A massive insect with an even more massive stingers flies charges at you, the drone of its massive wings disorienting and frustrating you as the rest of the colony pump you with venom. Level 1 Small Beast Defense Traits | AC | 17 | +2 Drone | | ------------------------------------------------- | ------------------------- | -------- | | HP | 30 | | | Poise | 18 | | | Saves | Fort +3, Refl +5, Will +2 | | Offense Traits (DC 13) | Sting (P) | +3 | +2 Charge | | --------------------------------------------------- | --------- | ---------- | | | 2d6+2 (9) | +Venom | Multiattack - Can only be used against a creature with 0 speed. Make 2 sting attacks. Venom Recharge - Has a limitation that its venom can only be applied once per minute. Drone - A stinger makes a constant loud buzzing sound as it flies. It can choose to fly around an adjacent creature's head to bother it and taunt it for 1 round (will negates). A stinger gets +2 AC any turn it drones. Cannot use if it cannot fly. Special Attack - Lunge Special Attack: Skewer - Pierce through a creature, dealing an additional d6 (3) damage and entangling the stinger and the target On-hit until the start of the stingers next turn. Powerful Charge Weaknesses/Deep Lore Swat - A stinger is also grounded for 1 turn in addition to any other effect from being poise broken. When grounded, a stinger has 13 AC. Other Traits Ability Scores - STR +2, DEX +2, LUK +0, INT -3, WIS +1, CHA +0 Speed - 10 Land Speed, 30 Fly Senses - Nightvision, Scent (10 ft) Deific Boons - Animal Instincts Feats - Skill Focus Skills - +4 (adv) +Perception, +5 (adv) +Acrobatics

entries.title: Wolf
entries.content: An agitated canine bares its fangs at you. Before you know it, another one is to your left and to your right. The group slowly advances towards you to close off your escape. Level 1 Medium Beast Defense Traits | AC | 14 | | | ------------------------------------------------- | ------------------------- | --- | | HP | 30 | | | Poise | 23 | | | Saves | Fort +5, Refl +3, Will +2 | | Offense Traits (DC 13) | Bite (C) | +3 | +4 Topple, +adv flanking | | -------------------------------------------------- | --------- | ------------------------------------------- | | | 2d6+2 (9) | | Innate Techniques - Kidnap Pack Attack - Gain flanking as long as an ally threatens. Flanking also gives advantage for wolves, in addition to the usual +2 bonus. Howl Recharge - As a standard action while it threatens an enemy, a wolf can howl to have allies converge on its position. They can move 20 feet closer Elusive to it as an instant action. Only one would can howl each round in combat. Weaknesses/Deep Lore Lone Wolf - If a wolf cannot sense one of its companions that isn't defeated, it flees. Other Traits Ability Scores - STR +2, DEX +2, LUK +0, INT -3, WIS +1, CHA +0 Speed - 40 Land Speed Senses - Nightvision, Scent (10 ft) Deific Boons - Animal Instincts Feats - Escape Route Skills - +4 (+4) +Perception, +4(+2) +Survival, +7 +Stealth,

entries.title: Giant Eagle
entries.content: A majestic bird flies overhead. As you appreciate its beauty it flies down with incredible speed and gouges out your friends eyes. It screeches as it continues its onslaught. Level 2 Medium Beast Defense Traits | AC | 15 | | | ------------------------------------------------- | ---------- | ------------------------- | | HP | 40 | | | Poise | 25 | | | Saves | +3, +7, +3 | Advantage vs fear | Freedom - At the start of the eagle's turn, it loses the entangled and entombed conditions. It is freed from a grapple. Let Freedom Ring Su Recharge - As a standard action, an eagle can cry out to free all creatures within 100 ft. Any creature that is grappled, entangled or entombed automatically loses the condition and becomes immune to these effects for 1 round. Flyby Attack - An eagle's movement is elusive against targets it has attacked this round. Offense Traits (DC 14) | Peck (P) | +5 | | | --------------------------------------------------- | ---------- | ----------------------- | | | 2d8+3 (12) | | | Talon (S) | +5 | | | | 2d6+3 (10) | +d4 bleed | Multiattack - Make 2 melee attacks Innate Techniques - Break Free 1/day - Rend Vision Constant - Courage Distant Predator - An eagle can use +Stealth without Cover or Concealment as long as it is at least 200 ft above its opponent. Special Attack: Dive Bomb - Special: The eagle must be at least 60 ft above its target to initiate this attack. FRA move up to 300 ft downwards to strike an opponent. Deals a bonus +d8 dmg and deals the attacks damage as poise damage. If this poise breaks the creature, it is knocked down. If the eagle is larger than the creature, it can choose to to instead Grapple it and carry it 30 ft into the air. Weaknesses/Deep Lore Wounded Wings - An eagle below half hp has its fly speed reduces to 30 and it loses its ascension quality. Other Traits Ability Scores - STR +1, DEX +3, LUK +0, INT -3, WIS +1, CHA +1 Deific Boons - Animal Instincts Senses - Scent 5 ft, Nightvision Speed - 10 Land Speed, 60 Fly Ascension - An eagle takes no penalty to its speed when flying directly up. Far Sight - An eagle's vision allows it so see even small critters hundreds of feet away with great precision. Feats - Gang Up, Butterfly Sting Skills - +13 (+4 scent) +Perception, +7 +Stealth

entries.title: Harambe
entries.content: This slow paced ape walks on its knuckles before realizing your presence. It rears up on its hind legs to show off its massive size while it screeches and swings its arms. Level 2 Large Beast Defense Traits | AC | 17 | | | ------------------------------------------------- | ---------- | ------------------------- | | HP | 40 | | | Poise | 25 | | | Saves | +3, +7, +3 | Advantage vs fear | Freedom - At the start of the eagle's turn, it loses the entangled and entombed conditions. It is freed from a grapple. Let Freedom Ring Su Recharge - As a standard action, an eagle can cry out to free all creatures within 100 ft. Any creature that is grappled, entangled or entombed automatically loses the condition and becomes immune to these effects for 1 round. Flyby Attack - An eagle's movement is elusive against targets it has attacked this round. Offense Traits (DC 13) | Unarmed (C) | +5 | | | ---------------------------------------------------------- | ---------- | ------------------- | | | 2d8+3 (12) | | | Heavy Object (S) | +5 | +2 BE | | | 2d8+3 (12) | Thrown | Multiattack - Make 2 attacks, one of which must be a topple, dirty trick or grapple Innate Techniques - Boasting Taunt, Improvise, Wake-Up Slap 1/day - Foe Toss Constant - Ape Together Strong - Harambe are exceptionally good at assisting each other. They can aid an allied harambe as a swift action. They always grant +2 to skill bonuses. Special Attack: Dive Bomb - While in the presence of small and flexible trees, such as bamboo or saplings, a gorilla can bend it and have it slap a creature. The attack deals +d8 bonus crush dmg and makes the creature Clumsy 2 On-hit. Weaknesses/Deep Lore Never Forget - Harambe have weakness 2 to Gun attacks, and are shaken 1 on hit by them. Dominance Established - Harambe are often led by an elder male who calls the shots and protects the rest of the troop. In neutral scenarios where harambe encounter other creatures, they will attempt to intimidate them to assess their foes. By succeeding on an opposed +Intimidation check, a creature can usually dissuade harambe from engaging. Other Traits Ability Scores - STR +3, DEX +1, LUK -1, INT -3, WIS +1, CHA +1 Deific Boons - Animal Instincts Senses - Scent 5 ft Speed - 30 Land Speed, 40 Climb Feats - Gang Up, Bullseye Skills - +5 (+4 scent) +Perception, +7 +Athletics, +Intimidation ![SilverbackPatriarchfull.jpg|Harambe, [Hearthstone card art]]

entries.title: Huffer
entries.content: This aggressive boar huffs and puffs as it scrapes its hooves against the ground. Its impressive speed and sharp tusks are quick to show you the error of your ways. Level 2 Medium Beast Defense Traits | AC | 16 | | | ------------------------------------------------- | ---------- | --------------------------------------------- | | HP | 44 | | | Poise | 27 | DRA 1 | | Saves | +7, +3, +3 | Advantage vs avoid AoO | Offense Traits (DC 14) | Gore (P) | +5 | | | -------------------------------------------------- | ---------- | --- | | | 2d8+3 (12) | | Multiattack - Make 2 attacks, both must be part of a Core Rules, How to Play/Actions/Common Actions/Full Round Actions/Charge and one must be a Shove or Topple. Innate Techniques - Bull Rush, Break Free, Sprint, Reckless Charge Charger - When a huffer takes the charge action it gets... - +2 attack - No penalty to AC - Access to its multiattack Relentless - A huffer gets Advantage on reflex saves made against creatures attempting to use +Acrobatics to avoid AoO. Shockwave - As a standard action, huffer can slam its hooves into the ground to create a shockwave. Creatures within 10 feet of it take 10 crush damage and are knocked down (fort halves dmg and negates knockdown). Stampede - A huffer can Prepare to charge at the same time as another huffer. When it does so, it gets Advantage on its charge attacks. Quills - Whenever a huffer takes damage, its quills shoot out at it. If the attacker is within 5 ft it, it takes 3 pierce damage and becomes Clumsy 1. All quills (and thus the clumsy stacks) can be removed as a swift action, dealing 3*(# of quills) pierce damage, unless they are removed with first aid. Weaknesses/Deep Lore JuggerNOT - Stopping a huffer in the middle of its charge also causes it to get knocked down until the start of its next turn. Other Traits Ability Scores - STR +3, DEX +1, LUK +0, INT -3, WIS +1, CHA +0 Deific Boons - Animal Instincts Senses - Scent 5 ft, Nightvision Speed - 40 Land Speed Feats - Diehard, Sentinel Skills - +5 (+4 scent) +Perception, +9 +Athletics

entries.title: Macaw
entries.content: This colorful bird draws your eye with its impressive plumage. Once it starts to copy the spells of your allies as it shouts the words of power gleefully, you realize its true power. Level 2 Medium Beast Defense Traits | AC | 14 | | | ------------------------------------------------- | ---------- | --- | | HP | 32 | | | Poise | 18 | | | Saves | +1, +6, +3 | | Offense Traits (DC 14) | Peck (P) | +4 | | | -------------------------------------------------- | -------- | --- | | | d6+2 (5) | | Innate Techniques - Roar, Boasting Taunt Mimicry Su recharge - Whenever a macaw sees an enemy use a technique or special attack, it can use it on its next turn. All techniques function as though they were innate techniques and use the macaws statistics. If the macaw uses a mana burn or recharge ability, it cannot use mimicry again until it rests. It is limited to level 2 or lower techniques. The macaw cannot use this ability while silenced. Mimicry cannot be used in consecutive rounds. Shanty - A macaw can use a standard action to start singing a song. All allied pirates, seamen and sea creatures within 60 ft get a +2 bonus to attack. Continuing to sing on subsequent turns is a free action. The macaw cannot use this ability while silenced. Weaknesses/Deep Lore Tongue Twister - Fond of repetition, the macaw will often mimic interesting words and phrases. A creature trained in +Linguistics can roll an opposed linguistics check to as a swift action to distract the macaw if they are within 30 ft, or farther if they can increase the volume of their voice significantly. Success stops the macaw from singing any shanties and prevents it from taking any actions it on its next turn other than movement. This can only function once per combat. Other Traits Ability Scores - STR -1, DEX +2, LUK +3, INT -2, WIS +1, CHA +0 Languages - Common, +2 more Senses - Nightvision Deific Boons - Animal Instincts Intellectual Beast - An oddity amongst beasts, a macaw can understand and speak common, plus 2 other languages. It's ability to communicate complex ideas is still quite limited. It is exceptionally good at remembering voices and faces. In general its memory is good and it can intuit the functionality of simple devices like doors and pulley systems. Signature Skill - secret-ish, read lips Speed - 15 Land Speed, 30 Fly Feats - Signature Skill Skills - +5 +Perception, +2 +Linguistics, +6 +Sleight of Hand

entries.title: Piranha Swarm
entries.content: These colorful freshwater fish are efficient carnivores that form schools to consume prey many times larger than them. You find out just how tenacious they can be as they munch on your limbs. Level 2 Large Beast Group Defense Traits | AC | 14 | | | ------------------------------------------------- | ---------- | --- | | HP | 40 | | | Poise | - | | | Saves | +4, +4, +3 | | Poise Immunity - Group enemies do not have poise and cannot be poise broken. Single Target Resistance - Resistance to any damage that only affects a single creature, most common with attack rolls. Certain attacks like cleave can ignore this resistance, but you can only attack the swarm with such moves. Single target effects that deal no damage have no effect. Offense Traits (DC 14) | Swarm Attack (P) | 2d6 (7) | +d6 bleed | | ---------------------------------------------------------- | ------- | ----------------------- | Swarm Attack - A swarm does not threaten. These creatures cannot make attacks and must instead enter a creatures space to deal their damage automatically at the end of their turn. This allows them to damage multiple creatures if they are close together with a big enough swarm. Cling - Piranhas never let go. Their swarm attack damage happens again at the end of the target creature's turn. When the creature is damaged by anything other than a piranha swarm, this effect ends. The piranhas can also be forcefully ripped off with an +Athletics or +Medicine check as a standard action. Failure causes the creature to take the attack damage again immediately. At the end of a piranha swarm's turn, it loses 3 hp for each creature it uses its auto attack against, representing the dwindling swarm caused by the tenacious fish clinging to their enemies. Sense Weakness - If a creature without a Swim speed or +Athletics proficiency is detected by the piranha swarm, it gets a free dash action on its next turn. This ability also activates if a bleeding creature is detected. Weaknesses/Deep Lore Baited - While piranhas do attack living creatures, they prefer easier prey. If a fresh dead carcass is within range of their blood scent, piranhas will tear it to shreds before moving on. Medium or small creatures distract for one turn. Anything smaller only uses their movement. Anything larger than medium distracts for 1 more turn per size category. AoE Vulnerability - Takes 50% more dmg from any AoE effect. Other Traits Ability Scores - STR +0, DEX +2, LUK +0, INT -3, WIS +1, CHA +0 Deific Boons - Animal Instincts Senses - Scent 10 ft or blood scent (vs bleeding creatures, 100 ft) Speed - 5 Land Speed, 40 Swim Signature Skill - one eye open, quick scan Feats - Signature Skill Skills - +5 (+4 scent) +Perception, +9 +Athletics

entries.title: Hippo
entries.content: A pair of eyes and nostrils looks at you from inside the lake. It disappears under the water. A moment later an angry herbivore bursts from the lake and charges you down. Level 3 Large Beast Defense Traits | AC | 15 | | | ------------------------------------------------- | ---------- | --------------------------------------------- | | HP | 64 | | | Poise | 26 | DR 10 vs Grapple | | Saves | +8, +3, +4 | Advantage vs avoid AoO | Chunky - A hippo must take at least 6 damage in order to take damage. This essentially functions as DRA 5, but it doesn't reduce damage that is 6 or higher. Offense Traits (DC 15) | Gore (P) | +6 | | | -------------------------------------------------- | ---------- | --- | | | 2d8+3 (12) | | Multiattack - Make 2 attacks. One must be a Shove, Grapple or Topple. Innate Techniques - Roar, Headbutt 1/day - Burst of Speed, Thick of the Fight Muck Spreading recharge - A hippo can turn away from a creature and spin its tail rapidly while defecating targeting a 15 ft cone. Creature can choose to protect their mouth or their eyes. If they protect their mouth, they get blinded (reflex reduced to blurred vision). If they choose to protect their eyes, they are instead Sickened 3 (fort reduces to sickened 1). Either status lasts until they are sufficiently cleaned off, usually requiring a standard action (or the aid action). Weaknesses/Deep Lore Moist - A hippo requires water sources during hot days in order to maintain homeostasis. If a hippo takes fire damage, or is otherwise made dry, it is fatigued until it is made wet again. Aggro - Hippos are herbivores, but are territorial. When a creature of at least medium size enters their territory, they attack. This territory is marked by the hippo's feces. Other Traits Ability Scores - STR +3, DEX +0, LUK +0, INT -3, WIS +1, CHA +0 Deific Boons - Animal Instincts Senses - Scent 5 ft, Nightvision Speed - 30 Land Speed, hippos cannot swim, but they can use their land speed to trot along the bottom of floor while submerged in water. Feats - Toughness, Freedom of Movement Skills - +6 (+4 scent) +Perception, +8 +Athletics, +8 +Intimidation

entries.title: Big Cat
entries.content: A rustling in the bushes and a low growl are the last thing you hear before a giant cat pounces from the underbrush, directly onto your neck.
