entries.title: Automaton
entries.content: A humanoid robot stands a few heads taller than a man with oversized arms. You see it stop the thief that rushes out of the town square by launching one of its arms as a projectile and pinning them. Level 2 Large Construct Defense Traits | AC | 16 | | | ------------------------------------------------- | ------------------------- | ------------------------ | | HP | 40 | | | Poise | 25 | | | Saves | Fort +5, Refl +4, Will +2 | +4 vs Mind-Affecting | Resistance - Poison, Psychic, Force Offense Traits (DC 14) | Unarmed (P) | +5 | Melee or Thrown, +4 Grapple | | ----------------------------------------------------- | ---------- | ----------------------------------------------------- | | | 2d6+3 (10) | +2 zap Overcharged | Multiattack - Make 2 unarmed attacks Innate Techniques - Pin Down Rocket Fists - An automaton can launch its unarmed strikes as thrown attacks. They magnetize back immediately unless they are grabbed out of the air or they use their Pin Down technique. Special Attack: Rocket Punch - Make a thrown weapon attack using unarmed damage. On-hit make a drag attack. If the drag is successful, the creature is pulled adjacent. Special Attack: Power Fist - On-hit stagger your target for one round (fort negates). If after this attack is resolved the target is defeated, it flies 1,000 ft into the air and gently lands on the ground one minute later. With a low roof, the creature is launched less high, but for just as long. Overdrive zap Recharge - As a swift action, an automaton can enter overdrive, giving it the benefits of Static Field for 1 turn. On the following turn it must recharge, and reduces its speed to 15. If it ever uses the discharge option of static field, overdrive cannot be used again until rest. Weaknesses/Deep Lore Memo - When a duedette is Defeated, a bird erupts from their body that flies off to deliver a message to its superior of its demise. Can be easily intercepted with most forms of ranged attacks within 3 rounds. Past that it usually flies to high and too far for most creatures to catch it. Just Business - A duedette is forbidden from attacking creatures unprovoked, as they they were under the effects of Sanctuary. They are often permitted to use force when collecting debts, but will typically use it as a last resort. They can still fight in self-defense, however. Other Traits Ability Scores - STR +0, DEX +3, LUK +0, INT +1, WIS +3, CHA +0 Languages - Common. By listening to someone speak a language, an automaton can slowly learn how to speak a language over the course of 1 minute. Scanning Database - As a FRA, an automaton can make any kind of knowledge check using its engineering bonus, but with Disadvantage. Speed - 25 Land Speed Feats - Ricochet Toss, Point Blank Master Skills - +4 +Perception, +7 +Athletics, +8 Engineering

entries.title: Heckle Bot
entries.content: This robot never shuts up, constantly throwing insults at anything that it thinks can be offended. Level 3 Medium Construct Defense Traits | AC | 17 | | | ------------------------------------------------- | ------------------------- | ------------- | | HP | 56 | | | Poise | 32 | DRA 2 | | Saves | Fort +5, Refl +4, Will +5 | | Immune - Taunt Offense Traits (DC 15) | Clamp (C) | +5 | | | --------------------------------------------------- | --------- | --- | | | 2d6+2 (9) | | Multiattack - Make 1 Melee Attack and a Grapple Innate Techniques - Boasting Taunt, Brace Yourself, Elemental Barrier, Kidnap, Stubbed Toe 1/day - Mana Burn Constant - Mana Drain Mockery Psychic - Change the flavor of insults to be more painful than goading. Insult a creature within 30 ft to deal 3d8 (13) psychic damage and give them Disadvantage on their next attack roll (will halves damage and negates disadvantage). Rage Bait language - If the hecklebot succeeds on a +Monster Lore check against a creature, the creature has disadvantage on saves against the hecklebot's taunt effects. As a swift action, a hecklebot can throw insults at a creature within 30 feet, taunting them for 1 round (will negates). Weaknesses/Deep Lore Killing With Kindness - When complimented, requiring a swift action, a hecklebot needs to reprogram, typically only expecting rage in response to its harsh words. All taunt effects against it immediately end and it cannot taunt creatures on its next turn. This can only occur once a combat. Other Traits Ability Scores - STR +2, DEX +1, LUK +0, INT +3, WIS +0, CHA +0 Languages - Common, draconic, infernal, celestial, sylvan, primordial, aquan, necril, orc, giant Scanning Database - As a FRA, an automaton can make any kind of knowledge check using its engineering bonus, but with Disadvantage. Speed - 35 Land Speed Feats - Power Pose, Defensive Training Skills - +5 +Perception,+8 Engineering, +10 +Local, +8 +Planes, +8 +Linguistics

entries.title: monkey-gree-gree
entries.content: Crust Monkey Gree Gree Level 3 Large Construct \[boss] Defense Traits AC 19\ HP 40\ Poise 25\ Fort +5, Refl +4, Will +2 Resistance - Poison, Psychic, Force, Mind affecting +4 Offense Traits (DC 15) Unarmed (P) +5 (+4 grapple) (20/40)\ d10+3 (8) +d6 zap (overcharge) Multiattack - Make 2 unarmed attacks. Innate Techniques - Mad Monkeys Special Attack: Rocket Punch - Make a thrown weapon attack using unarmed damage. If successful, make a drag attack with no MA penalty. If the drag is successful, the creature is pulled adjacent. Aura: Stance Change - The Monkey Gree Gree swaps between three different faces: Monkey covering eyes, monkey covering ears and monkey covering mouth. It starts covering its ears, and swaps at the start of each turn after the first in a repeating loop (cover ears, cover eyes, then mouth). Each stance has an aura that radiates 60 ft with a different effect. Cover Ears - All creatures within the aura are deafened, giving them -4 perception.\ Cover Eyes - All creatures within the area are blinded.\ Cover Mouth - All creatures within the aura are silenced. The Gree Gree radiates this aura while hiding in plain sight. The Gree Gree is affected by its own aura.\ Weaknesses/Deep Lore Yes Master - When an automaton is programmed, it is done so while listening to a voice. By mimicking this voice with a DC 20 disguise check, you can give the automaton new orders. Can only be attempted once. With a DC 13 engineering check, an inactive automaton can be reprogrammed. Hand Needed - When an automaton uses its pin down technique, it loses access to the hand and its multi-attack on subsequent turns until the creature removes it and it magnetizes back. Other Traits Ability Scores - Str +3, Dex +0, Lucc +0, Int +2, Wis +0, Cha +0 Speed - 25 land Hide in Plain Sight Language Processing - By listening to someone speak a language, an automaton can slowly learn how to speak a language over the course of 1 minute. Feats - Point Blank Master Skills - +4 perception, +7 athletics, +8 engineering

entries.title: Dark Iron Enforcer
entries.content: A mechanical dwarf stomps towards intruders. Lava seems to course through its black metal chassis. It lets off some exhaust before speaking. "Present Identification." Level 4 Medium Construct Defense Traits | AC | 20 | | | ------------------------------------------------- | ------------------------- | ----------- | | HP | 60 | | | Poise | 31 | | | Saves | Fort +9, Refl +4, Will +4 | +4 vs charm | Resistance - Fire, Charm Offense Traits (DC 16) | Axe (S) | +7 | | ----------------------------------------------------- | ---------- | | | 2d8+3 (12) | | Unarmed (C) | +7 | | | d8+3 (7) | Multiattack - Make an axe attack and an unarmed attack. Innate Techniques - Sky Uppercut 1/day - Defensive Spin, Toe Smasher, Ups and Downs Constant - Quake, Technology Signature Skill - Boo, Big Shot Stoke Flames (fire) (Recharge)- As a standard action, an enforcer can unleash a gout of flames upon creatures in a 20 ft cone in front of it. All creatures take 4d6 (14) fire damage. Any creature that wears medium or heavy armor has it softened and melted, gaining Weakness 5 crush for 1 round (reflex halves dmg and negates weakness). Recharges after using exhaust. Exhaust (recharge) (Poison)- As a standard action, an enforcer can release a thick cloud of exhaust in a 10 ft radius around it that lingers for 1 round. This creates Total Concealment. Any non-construct creatures in the area take 4d6 (14) poison damage and are sickened 2 (fort halves dmg and sickened). Recharges after using stoke flames. Weaknesses/Deep Lore Viking - When an enforcer is made Wet, it disables its turbo thrusters and stoke flames abilities. Dark Iron Duty - Unless Taunted or attacked first by one directly, an enforcer cannot attack a Dwarf Other Traits Ability Scores - STR +3, DEX +0, LUK +0, INT +1, WIS +0, CHA +0 Turbo Thrusters - As a swift action, an enforcer can either increase their speed by 10, or take flight until the end of their turn. As an immediate action they can negate fall damage they take. For overland travel, a dark iron enforce need not sleep, and their thrusters allow them to fly long distances. They can travel 600 miles a day. Speed - 30 Feats - Skill Focus, Signature Skill, Unarmed Combatant Skills - +6 +Perception, +13 (adv) +Intimidation, +9 +Athletics, +7 +Local !ligma

entries.title: Glass Cannon
entries.content: A cannon made out of only the flimsiest of materials makes for an excellent one-dimensional all-out offensive weapon. Level 4 Medium Construct Defense Traits | AC | 14 | | | ------------------------------------------------- | ------------------------- | --- | | HP | 1 | | | Poise | 1 | | | Saves | Fort +4, Refl +9, Will +4 | | Force Field - The only thing protecting the glass cannon from instant destruction. Functions as a Divine Shield that automatically refreshes after not taking damage for 1 round. Offense Traits (DC 16) | Icicle Cannon (P) | +11 | long, +Siege Weapon | | ----------------------------------------------------------- | ---------- | --------------------------- | | | 2d8+3 (12) | | Multiattack - While its divine shield is active, make 2 cannon attacks. Innate Techniques - Gravity Bow, Called Shot Constant - Artillery Weaknesses/Deep Lore Meltdown - When subject to a Fire effect, a glass cannon is instantly destroyed, even if it had its forcefield up. Other Traits Ability Scores - STR +0, DEX +3, LUK +3, INT -, WIS +0, CHA +0 Languages - None Speed - 60 Land Speed, Ignores Difficult Terrain Feats - Bullseye, Point Blank Master Skills - +6 +Perception

entries.title: Haunted Golem
entries.content: You see a statue whose color has faded away with time. Suddenly your sword comes out of its sheathe and starts to swing at you on its own. Once you grab it out of the air, the statue animates and flings you into the air. Level 5 Large Construct/Undead Defense Traits | AC | 17 | | | ------------------------------------------------- | ------------------------- | --------------------- | | HP | 50 | | | Poise | 24 | 24 (possessed object) | | Saves | Fort +8, Refl +7, Will +5 | +4 vs charm | Incorporeal Offense Traits (DC 17) | Unarmed (C) | +8 | +8 Disarm, +4 MV | | ----------------------------------------------------- | ---------- | -------------------- | | | 2d6+3 (10) | | Innate Techniques (CL 7) - Icicle, Sky Uppercut (U3), Life Drinker, Never Lucky (U3), Chill Metal, Sanctuary, Barrier, Mud Buddy 1/day - Earth Shatter, Haunted, Fake Out, Foe Toss Possess Object Su Subtle Recharge - While hiding in plain sight, a haunted golem can covertly possess an item within 30 ft, giving the item a Hover speed of 30 and the ability to communicate telepathically. As a standard action, the golem can make a Melee Attack with the object, dealing either the weapon damage or its unarmed damage, whichever is higher. It can use the weapons special attacks, but otherwise no other types of special attacks. The possessed object has Poise equal to the haunted golems. When disarmed, the animating force in the weapon disappears and this ability ends. The creature that successfully disarmed the weapon can choose to wield the weapon. Protector - Whenever an allied Minion is damaged, a haunted golem can choose to move its speed to Threaten the enemy that did the damage. If they already threatened them, they can make a Simple Attack against them. Weaknesses/Deep Lore Protector of the Innocent - A haunted golem can intuitively tell when a creature is a minion. It will not do anything that could cause +HP damage to such creatures. Other Traits Ability Scores - STR +3, DEX +0, LUK +0, INT +1, WIS +0, CHA +0 Languages - Common Hide in Plain Sight - Any abandoned building/ancient ruins Speed - 30 Land Speed, 30 Hover Feats - Maneuver Versatility, Directed Disarm, MM Bullying Spell Skills - +7 +Perception, +7 +Deception

entries.title: Turbo Frogg
entries.content: A giant frog with a jetpack boosts across the battlefield without a care in the world. Level 5 Large Construct/Beast Defense Traits | AC | 20 | | | ------------------------------------------------- | ------------------------- | --------------- | | HP | 70 | | | Poise | 28 | | | Saves | Fort +8, Refl +9, Will +4 | +4 vs charm | Offense Traits (DC 17) | Tongue (C) | +8 | +2 TC, +4 Drag/Grapple, +4 MV, +30 ft of Reach during frogg's turn | | ---------------------------------------------------- | ---------- | ----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | | | 2d6+3 (10) | +Warpath | | Bite (C) | +8 | +2 TC | | | 2d8+3 (12) | +Warpath | Multiattack - Make a tongue and bite attack Innate Techniques - Laser Pointer, Crash, Pounce, Sprint 1/day - Plague of Frogs (U3), Hot Potato Constant - Unbreakable Advance, Warpath Turbo Charged Elusive - Whenever a turbo frogg takes the Core Rules, How to Play/Actions/Common Actions/Full Round Actions/Charge action, it can continue its movement up and over the creature. It can charge into the air. Special Attack: Electro Whip Zap - Make a tongue attack that deals an additional 2d8 (9) zap damage. The creature is Staggered On-hit (fort negates). Crash Landing - As a swift action, a turbo frogg can augment a Jump give it some extra oomph. When the frogg lands, it deals 20 Poise damage to creatures within 10 ft. Any poise broken creatures are Knocked Down. If it uses this ability when charging, it must end the movement of the charge where it lands. Weaknesses/Deep Lore Short Circuit (elusive)- When a turbo frogg takes any zap damage, their turbo thrusters activate, shooting them 15 ft in a random direction. Other Traits Ability Scores - STR +3, DEX +2, LUK +3, INT -2, WIS -1, CHA +0 Languages - Common (cannot speak) Hoppin' - A frogg can Jump as far and as high as its speed automatically. It can also jump as a free action. Amphibious Speed - 40 Land Speed Deific Boon - frog shape Feats - Maneuver Versatility, Matador, Tumbling Charge Skills - +6 +Perception, +10 +Athletics, +9 +Acrobatics

entries.title: Dwarven Centurion
entries.content: The pinnacle of dwarven engineering designed to deal death made in the image of its creators. It lumbers forward and gyrates its limbs with extreme precision. Level 6 Large Construct Defense Traits | AC | 19 | | | ------------------------------------------------- | -------------------------- | ------------------------ | | HP | 102 | | | Poise | 40 | | | Saves | Fort +10, Refl +8, Will +6 | +4 vs Mind-Affecting | Resistance - Slash, Pierce, Fire Superheated - Creatures have disadvantage on drag and grapple attacks against a centurion that has activated one of its Supercharge attacks unless they have resistance or immunity to fire/heat. Offense Traits (DC 18) | Bladed Arm (S) | +10 | | | ---------------------------------------------------------- | ---------- | ------------------------ | | | 2d8+4 (13) | +2d6 bleed | | Ballista Arm (P) | +6 | 300 ft range | | | 4d8 (18) | See below | Innate Techniques - Cripple, Suck, Endothermic Touch, Revenge, Ups and Downs Ballista Arm - Functions as the siege weapon, but it reloads automatically, requiring 1 round of downtime between shots. As a FRA, it can reload and fire its ballista (or fire then reload). Centurions come with 4 bolts loaded, requiring 2 turns of manual restocking (or aid from multiple allies) to get a new supply. Special Attack: Supercharge Recharge - Gain +4 to an attack roll. Recharges after using Letting off Steam. Letting off Steam (recharge) fire air water - Special: A dwarven centurion cannot use this ability until it has supercharged one of its other attacks. Let out a cone of superheated steam in a 30 ft cone or a 10 ft radius around it, dealing 6d8 (27) fire damage and inflicting sickened 2 (fort halves). Weaknesses/Deep Lore Weakness - Zap 3 Other Traits Ability Scores - STR +4, DEX +0, LUK +0, INT -1, WIS +0, CHA +0 Languages - Dwarven (cannot speak) Urban Defender - Ignores Difficult Terrain created by steep inclines, stairs and other terrain commonly found in urban environs. Speed - 30 Land Speed Feats - Combat Reflexes, Combat Step, Parting Shot Skills - +10 +Perception, +12 +Athletics

entries.title: Boom Reaver
entries.content: This titan of a construct descends upon you with jet thrusters, delivering a message from its employer before bringing down the axe. Level 7 Huge Construct Boss Defense Traits | AC | 23 | | | ------------------------------------------------- | -------------------------- | -------------------------------------------- | | HP | 180 | | | Poise | 36 | DRA 1 | | Saves | Fort +12, Refl +9, Will +7 | +4 vs Mind-Affecting and +Acrobatics | Resistance - Psychic Jet Boots - Immune to Fall Damage while not Dazed or Stunned Recovery Offense Traits (DC 20) | 2h Axe (S) | +12 | +2 Core Rules, How to Play/Actions/Common Actions/Full Round Actions/Charge, +4 MV, +4 Shove | | ---------------------------------------------------- | ---------- | ------------------------------------------------------------ | | | 2d8+3 (12) | | Multiattack - Make 2 melee attacks Innate Techniques - Sonic Boom (U4), Earth Shatter (U4), Lightning Strike (U3) 1/day - Crater (U4), Fore! (U4) Constant - Shockwave Magnets - As a swift action, the Boom Reaver can return its axe to its hand as long as it is within 60 ft. If it is possessed by another creature (reflex negates). Special Attack: Giga Impact Sonic - Make a Core Rules, How to Play/Actions/Common Actions/Full Round Actions/Charge attack that deals an additional 4d8 (18) sonic damage. The creature takes poise damage equal to the total damage of the attack. If they are poise broken, they are pushed back 20 ft. Before being knocked back, the Boom Reaver can use the second attack in its multiattack. Next Turn Shipping Companion Teleportation - The Boom Reaver has a special rune engraved on one of its hands that gives it direct access to factories used to create contructs. As a free action, as long as it does not already have 2 companions, it can mark a point on the ground for a construct of up to Level 3 to teleport to that position at the start of the Boom Reaver's next turn. When the creature appears, all creatures within 10 ft take 20 poise damage. If this poise breaks, inflict Knockdown. Displacer - The Boom Reaver gets a +4 bonus to shove attacks. When it shoves, the target is pushed back an additional 10 ft. Powerful Charge Boss Actions - Usable once each round Aftershock - Can only be used in response to a creature being knocked down within reach. The ground underneath them glows and the creature's speed is reduced to 0. A 5 ft radius around their space explodes after 1 round, launching any creature in the radius 30 ft in a random direction and setting them to 1 poise. Kinetic Reverberations - When the Boom Reaver takes slash, pierce, crush damage, it can store the kinetic energy from the blow and immediately release back on their attacker if they are within 30 ft. The attacker takes poise damage equal to the damage dealt to the Boom Reaver. If this breaks their poise, they are launched directly away from the Boom Reaver 20 ft. Rushdown - At the end of an enemy's turn, cast Telekinetic Charge on an ally. Weaknesses/Deep Lore Card Denied- While siphoned or anchored, the Boom Reaver loses access to its Next Day Shipping ability. Any in process shipments are still made, however. Speak With Manager - Boom Reavers are made for the protection of Lyra Corp assets. Complaints related to their services can be communicated directly to the programmer or the manager who assigned the Boom Reaver to the task, with the Boom Reaver serving as a conduit. It will only do this when it is not in combat. Other Traits Ability Scores - STR +3, DEX +2, LUK +0, INT +1, WIS +0, CHA +2 Languages - Common Senses - Nightvision Speed - 40 Land Speed, 30 Hover Turbo Thrusters - The boom reaver can jump as far and as high as its movement speed. A Word From My Employer - The boom reaver has the capacity to speak to directly to a specified creature it is bound to. That creature can also project its voice through the boom reaver to speak to others remotely. Feats - Tandem Charge, As One, Combat Reflexes, Sentinel, Skill Focus, Maneuver Versatility, Maneuver Master Skills - +9 +Perception, +12 (adv) +Athletics, +9 Engineering, +9 (adv) +Local

entries.title: Chrimbus Killer
entries.content: A weapon rolls in on treads made to appear as a malicious mechanical Santa. Its wide array of weapons rev up while lets out laugh as joyful as it is creepy. Level 7 Huge Construct Boss Defense Traits | AC | 21 | | | ------------------------------------------------- | -------------------------- | --- | | HP | 130 | | | Poise | 40 | | | Saves | Fort +7, Refl +10, Will +8 | | Keep It Cool - Whenever a Chrimbus Killer is affected by an effect that creates a great deal of water or ice, it creates a cloud of Fog in its space. This fog provides Partial Concealment until it disperses as a result of some other effect or wind. Recovery Intelligent Design - A Chrimbus Killer is immune to any damage or status effect inflicted by its own attacks (like getting caught in its missile AoE). Offense Traits (DC 20) | Arm Cannon (P) | +10 | Long | | -------------------------------------------------------- | --------- | -------- | | | d10+3 (8) | | Multiattack - Make 3 arm cannon attacks Innate Techniques - Laser Pointer 1/day - Rain of Arrows, Gravity Bow, Snatch, Self-Destruct Deathrattle - Fire all remaining missiles at random enemies within range. Bonus Points - Whenever a Chrimbus Killer moves, it automatically attempts to crush any creature it moves through. The target creature can choose to AoO or make a reflex save to avoid being crushed. The creature becomes Drained 7 (reflex negates). Any creatures it runs over get deposited behind it. Gift Grenade - As a swift action, a Chrimbus Killer can drop off a gift in an adjacent space. At the start of its next turn, or when a creature opens/interacts with it, it explodes violently, dealing 5d6 (17) crush and poise damage to all creatures within 5 ft. Boss Actions - Can do 2 of the following actions each turn. Fire the Missiles Recharge - When another creature ends their turn, fire a single missile at a creature within 60 ft. It deals 7d8 (28) fire damage in a 10 ft radius (reflex halves) and leaves flames on the ground that set creatures Ablaze if they end their turn inside of them. Chrimbus Killers come loaded with 5 missiles. Unload - When another creature ends their turn, the Chrimbus killer revs up its gun and sprays a 30 ft cone of bullets, dealing 7d8 (28) pierce damage (reflex halves). If a creature ends its turn in the radius, it takes this damage again. It cannot use its arm cannon attack on its next turn. Lock On - When a creature damages the Chrimbus Killer, it can choose to laser focus on it. It gets Advantage on attack rolls against it and the creature has disadvantage on saving throws against the Chrimbus Killer's offensive abilities for 1 round. Weaknesses/Deep Lore Turn Radius - A Chrimbus Killer cannot move in more than one direction at once. It must choose a direction and move directly in that direction, or diagonally towards that direction. No Brakes - When a Chrimbus Killer is subject to Forced Movement, it moves an additional 10 ft. Other Traits Ability Scores - STR +0, DEX +3, LUK +3, INT -1, WIS -1, CHA +0 Languages - Common, Sylvan Speed - 20 Land Speed, Ignores Difficult Terrain Feats - Snap Shot, Point Blank Master, Clustered Shots, Butterfly Sting Skills - +8 +Perception, +9 +Athletics, +12 +Stealth ![Santa-NK1phase2.gif|Mecha Santa boss, [Terraria]]

entries.title: Froggalier
entries.content: This frog snail hybrid slithers towards you. It screams "CHARGE" but you laugh at its speed. The power of the snail comes as a surprise to you as it slams into you. Level 1 Small Cryptid/Beast Defense Traits | AC | 14 | | | ------------------------------------------------- | ------------------------- | --- | | HP | 28 | | | Poise | 23 | | | Saves | Fort +4, Refl -1, Will +5 | | Sticky Hold - A froggalier cannot be separated from its snail mount. The creatures are considered 1 entity. Deific Boon - enlightened Shelly - A froggalier takes no penalty to AC when charging, and can charge through difficult terrain. Offense Traits (DC 13) | Ram (C) | +4 | | | ------------------------------------------------- | ---------- | --- | | | 2d8+3 (12) | | Multiattack - Ram and cast Metronome Innate Techniques - Metronome, Mage Hand 1/day - Power Overwhelming, Tentacles for Arms, Plague of Frogs Special Attack: Charge Forth elusive - Special: Can only be made as part of a charge Get +2 attack. You can move past the creature you attack. You can also turn during this charge. Allies that charge before your next turn get this benefit as well. Weaknesses/Deep Lore Nearsighted - The symbiotic relationship between the frogg and the snail mount muddies the senses of the frogg. They can only see creatures within 30 feet of them. Any attacks made outside of this range treat the froggalier as DD. Solvent - With an application of super solvent (requiring a successful attack roll or a grapple) a froggalier can be forcefully separated from its sticky mount. This kills the froggalier. Other Traits Ability Scores - STR +3, DEX -2, LUK +3, INT +0, WIS +1, CHA +1 Languages - Common, sylvan Speed - 20 Land Speed, Climb 20. Can climb along any solid surface, including smooth walls and ceilings. Senses - Scent (30 ft) Feats - Freedom of Movement Skills - +1 +Perception, +3 +Nature, +3 +Religion, +5 +Persuasion

entries.title: Wobblerr
entries.content: A strange blue creature stares at you. You try and tap it, causing it to wobble, before rebounding and smacking you backwards. Level 1 Medium Cryptid Defense Traits | AC | 12 | | | ------------------------------------------------- | ------------------------ | ----------------------------- | | HP | 38 | Regeneration 5 (necrotic) | | Poise | 32 | | | Saves | Fort +4, Refl 1, Will +3 | | Safeguard - A wobbler has advantage on any saving throw against an effect that deals no damage. Offense Traits (DC 13) Counter Su - As a standard action, a wobbler can ready itself for an incoming attack. When it takes damage, it can choose to rebound this damage back at its attacker and multiply it 1.5x. This can happen no matter the distance between them or the method of damage. If the attack did poise damage, the wobbler inflicts whatever combat maneuver was used against it. Indirect methods of damage, such as fall damage, damage over time like bleed, or environmental damage, do not proc counter. Encore (Su) - As a standard action a wobbler can clap and cheer at a creature within 60 ft who has taken a standard action in combat. That creature is forced to repeat whatever the last standard action then performed on their next turn (will reduces to repeat swift action). If the creature is unable to perform its last standard action, such as if the ability they used last is a recharge ability, they are effectively Dazed. Trapped (Su) - In order to leave a space within 5 feet of a wobbler, a creature must spend 20 ft of movement to push past its psychic field. Weaknesses/Deep Lore Staring(?) Contest - Wobblers have incredible endurance. They test it against one another constantly in what basically ends up being a staring contest. But instead of keeping their eyes open, they simply try to remain completely still. The first to break posture loses. They typically perform these contests in areas with hazards to increase the speed of the contest, such as in rivers, or unstable terrain. In game terms, creatures must roll a fortitude save. Subtract 20 from this result. If he number you get is greater that 0, subtract that number from your poise. First to get poise broken loses. Every fortitude save receives a cumulative -1 penalty from the last. Other Traits Ability Scores - STR +3, DEX +0, LUK +0, INT -2, WIS +0, CHA +0 Languages - Common (cannot speak) Speed - 15 Land Speed Senses - Darkvision Feats - Toughness Skills - +3 +Perception, +6 +Athletics

entries.title: Scavenger
entries.content: This humanoid creature has pale skin, long claws, sharp teeth and the whites of its eyes are a strange color. If feasts upon the rancid carrion left behind by a predator ages ago. Level 2 medium Cryptid/humanoid Defense Traits | AC | 15 | | | ------------------------------------------------- | ------------------------- | --- | | HP | 54 | | | Poise | 28 | | | Saves | Fort +8, Refl +5, Will +4 | | Immune - Drained, envenomed Resistance - Poison Rigor Mortis Recharge Healing - When a scavenger is killed, they do not truly die. They are exceptionally good at playing dead locking their limbs and slowing their bodily functions to almost inactive. A creature trained in +Medicine can identify when such a creature is not truly dead passively if their bonus is at least +6, or automatically if they attend to the "corpse" for whatever reason (looting, moving it etc). If the scavenger is not killed within the next minute, it rises back up and heals 10 hp. It must use its Spoiled Appetite ability to recharge this ability. Spoiled Appetite Healing - A scavenger relishes the opportunity to consume rotting viscera and bone. A creature must have been dead for at least 24 hours, or be Undead to be worthwhile to consume to a scavenger, though the longer the better. As a swift action against an adjacent corpse, a scavenger can consume such meat to heal 10 hp. If the creature is undead, they instead heal 15 hp. A small/medium creature can be the target of this ability once. Each size above medium allows an additional targeting. Offense Traits (DC 14) | Bite (P) | +5 | +4 Undead | | -------------------------------------------------- | ---------- | ------------- | | | 2d8+3 (12) | | | Claw (S) | +5 | +4 Undead | | | 2d8+3 (12) | | Multiattack - Make 2 melee attacks Innate Techniques - Reckless Charge, Frenzied Leap, Lick 1/day - Silencing Strike Frenzy Recharge - Consuming a delicious meal energizes a scavenger like nothing else. As a swift action, a scavenger can enter frenzy for 1 round, giving them Resistance to all damage and an additional 30 speed. Recharges when they use their spoiled appetite ability. They also get access to the following special attack. Eviscerate - Cause the target to bleed d6 On-hit Favored Enemy - Scavengers get a +4 bonus to attack against Undead Weaknesses/Deep Lore Addiction - Scavengers love the taste of undead creatures over all else. Once combat starts, they are persistently taunted by any present undead, even those not participating in the combat. Friends Not Food - The scavenger was originally created by a Boner alchemist and it is baked into the scavenger that boners are friends, not food. They cannot willingly harm boners. If forced to do so, they get an additional save against the effect to negate these commands. Other Traits Ability Scores - STR +3, DEX +3, LUK +0, INT -1, WIS +0, CHA +0 Languages - Common, necril Speed - 50 Land Speed Senses - Scent (10 ft), Nightvision Feats - Fleet Footwork Skills - +8 (+4 scent) +Perception, +8 (+2 scent, +4 tracking undead) +Survival, +7 +Athletics, +7 +Acrobatics

entries.title: Minotaur
entries.content: Humanoid in shape with cloven hooves and the head of a bull, this creature assesses you before mindlessly charging at your red mage. Level 3 Large Humanoid/Cryptid Defense Traits | AC | 18 | | | ------------------------------------------------- | ------------------------- | -------- | | HP | 50 | | | Poise | 26 | DR 2 | | Saves | Fort +8, Refl +2, Will +4 | | Offense Traits (DC 16) | Gore (P) | +6 | +2 Seeing Red, +2 charge, +4 Topple | | ---------------------------------------------------------- | ---------- | --------------------------------------- | | | 2d8+3 (12) | | | 2h Axe (S) | +6 | +2 Seeing Red, +2 charge | | | 2d8+3 (12) | | | Earthbending (C) | +6 | 30 ft, +2 Seeing Red, +2 charge | | | 3d8+3 (16) | | Multiattack - When making a charge attack, make a gore attack and another Weapon Attack Innate Techniques - Colossal Smash, Sprint 1/day - Ferocious Howl Constant - Unbreakable Advance Powerful Charge Seeing Red - Against a creature that is significantly red, or is Bleeding a minotaur gains +2 Attack Trample - 4d6 (14) crush and Knockdown (reflex halves dmg and negates knockdown) Summon Ally - 1 Loot Goblin (only while in a dungeon or maze) Special Attack: All In, cleave, lacerate Weaknesses/Deep Lore A-MAZE-ing - Minotaurs are exceptionally good at not getting lost. Once upon a time. they built elaborate mazes between their settlements and the outside world. Most lie in ruin. A minotaur always knows how to get to any point in their maze quickly, and can even physically change the maze with earth bending to create shortcuts. Their tremorsense increases to include any area that can be considered a "maze." Can't Look Away - Minotaurs have Disadvantage on saves against effects that taunt originating from creatures who are significantly red or bleeding. Other Traits Ability Scores - STR +3, DEX -1, LUK +0, INT +2, WIS +1, CHA +1 Languages - Common, primordial, sylvan Speed - 25 Land Speed Senses - Tremorsense (60 ft, +Maze) Earthbending - As the earthbender feature of the same name Feats - Weapon Focus (Slash), Skill Focus Skills - +6 +Perception, +11 (adv) Engineering, +8 (adv) +Athletics, +6 +Insight

entries.title: Drider Mage
entries.content: A spider descends from the ceiling slowly on a string of silk. As it reaches the brightness of your torchlight, you see a dark elf from the waist up emerging from where the spider's head would be. Level 5 Large Cryptid/Humanoid Defense Traits | AC | 19 | | | ------------------------------------------------- | ------------------------- | --- | | HP | 60 | | | Poise | 24 | | | Saves | Fort +5, Refl +8, Will +9 | | Web Walker - A drider cannot be Entangled by webs. they ignore the Difficult Terrain of webs. Ambrosia (Healing) - Heal 17 Offense Traits (DC 17) | Light Blade (S) | +8 | | | --------------------------------------------------------- | -------------- | -------------- | | | d6+3+2 zap (8) | | | Bite (P) | | 5 ft Reach | | | d6+3+2 zap (8 | +Venom | Multiattack - Make a melee Weapon Attack and cast a cantrip or activate Static Field. Innate Techniques (CL 7 Zap) - Gust, Shocking Grasp, Grasp of the Undying, True Strike, Dimensional Anchor 1/day - Lightning Bolt, In Orbit, Dispel Magic Constant - Static Field Sticky Situation - Weaknesses/Deep Lore Miracle of Life - Other Traits Ability Scores - STR +0, DEX +3, LUK +0, INT +2, WIS +2, CHA +0 Languages - Elven, Common, Infernal, Sign Language, Morse Code Darkvision Silk Jump - By placing a web on a surface, a drider can avoid Fall Damage when they Jump. They can also suspend themselves in midair, and climb back up this thread to return to their original starting point. This thread can be cut with any slash or pierce damage. Speed - 40, 40 climb. Can climb along any surface. Deific Boons - From my cold dead head, Feats - Ambrosia, Combat Step, Divine Interference, MM Elemental Shift Skills - +9 +Perception, +10 +Stealth, +10 +Acrobatics, +9 Arcana, +9 +Religion, +9 +Insight, +7 +Deception

entries.title: Cryptids
entries.content: Cryptid is the category that any creature that doesn't fit in elsewhere goes. They often are a mix of or resemble another creature type, but don't fit in with them neatly.

entries.title: Dragonfly
entries.content: A shining insect floats through the air around its draconic ally, enchanting its scales with protective wards. It flicks its tongue at you as you fail to harm it and its allies. Level 1 Tiny Dragon/Fey Defense Traits | AC | 15 | | | ------------------------------------------------- | --------------------------------------- | --- | | HP | 26 | | | Poise | 19 | | | Saves | Fort -1, Refl +7 (Evasion), Will +1 | | Perfect Flight - Dragonflies do not automatically become clumsy 1 on hit while flying. They can take flight as a free action. Scaly Sigils - As a standard action, give an adjacent allied draconic creature a divine shield and become drained 4. This cannot be used on dragonflies. This divine shield lasts until lost or until the dragonfly gets more than 30 ft away from the dragon it used it on. Offense Traits (DC 13) | Elemental Blast (???) | +5 | 30 ft | | --------------------------------------------------------------- | ---------- | ----- | | | 2d6+4 (11) | | Innate Techniques - Noble Sacrifice 1/day - Bouncing Blast, Sanctuary, Boasting Taunt Elemental Blast - Functions as the bender feature of the same name. The damage type is different from dragonfly to dragonfly. It matches the damage type of the breath weapon of last dragon it used its scaley sigil feature on. If the dragons breath weapon is weird, this defaults to fire. Weaknesses/Deep Lore Tasty Snack - A dragonfly presented with another flying bug it could reasonably eat, such as a butterfly or a locust, it can be distracted by its tantalizing aroma. If a creature lets such a creature loose in the middle of a battle, a dragonfly must roll a DC 13 will save or spend its entire next turn flying to catch it, ending its turn at the space it catches its prey. Other Traits Ability Scores - STR -2, DEX +4, LUK +1, INT -2, WIS +0, CHA +1 Languages - Draconic (cannot speak) Nightvision Treasure Sense - Dragonflies possess scent (5 ft). It is only usable on detecting the value of objects. They are automatically are able to discern the value of something with a quick sniff. Particularly valuable things such as rare magic items may have extremely powerful and wide scents as strongly scented creatures and objects normally would. Attached - Whenever a dragonfly uses its Scaly Sigils feature, that creature immediately gains the benefits of the dragonfly's teamwork feats, as though they had practiced together. Speed - 50 Fly, 50 Hover Feats - As One, Bonded Mind Skills - +3 (+4 scent) +Perception, +7 +Acrobatics, +7 +Stealth

entries.title: Whelp
entries.content: The whelp represents an underdeveloped member of a draconic species, before it gets many of the cool features typical of adult dragons. Many are frozen at this stage in development, unable to grow. Level 1 Small Dragon Defense Traits | AC | 17 | | | ------------------------------------------------- | ------------------------- | ------------------------ | | HP | 28 | | | Poise | 22 | DR 1 (+5 Power Pose) | | Saves | Fort +3, Refl +4, Will +1 | (+4 from Persevere) | Steady Flight - Dragons cap at clumsy 2 while flying. Resistance - Matches Breath Weapon type (typically fire). Offense Traits (DC 13) | Bite (P) | +4 | | | -------------------------------------------------- | ---------- | --- | | | 2d6+3 (10) | | | Claw (S) | +4 | | | | 2d6+3 (10) | | Multiattack - Make a claw and bite attack Innate Techniques - Magic Missile 1/day - Magic Dart, Avenging Wrath, Backstab Constant - Persevere Breath Weapon (Recharge) - 3d6 (10) elemental (reflex halves). Can target a 30 ft line or a 15 ft cone. Must deal damage with a bite attack to recharge this ability. Tail Swipe - As a swift action while grounded, a dragon can make a quick turnaround and swipe at foes it threatens with its tail. Creatures take (10+LvL) poise damage (reflex halves). Poise broken creatures are knocked down. Can only be used on creatures of up to medium size. Weaknesses/Deep Lore Imprint - The first non-whelp creature a whelp encounters after it hatches that doesn't attack it, it believes to be its parent. If multiple creatures are present the order of operations is… Its mother>creatures it heard often while it was in its egg>its own kind>reptiles>females>random Other Traits Ability Scores - STR +0, DEX +3, LUK +3, INT +0, WIS +0, CHA +1 Languages - Draconic Senses - Darkvision, Echolocation (30 ft), Scent (10 ft) Dragon Flight - Dragons can take flight without using a swift action, but they must land by the end of their turn if they do so. Speed - 30 Land Speed, 30 Fly Feats - Pack Tactics, Power Pose Skills - +3 (+4 scent) +Perception, +6 +Acrobatics, +6 +Stealth, +3 +Deception

entries.title: Shrubbagazzor
entries.content: The shrubbagazzor is a creature born of magical manipulation of topiary. These creatures can also be born in response to the mistreatment of the natural world to fight against corruption. Level 3 Large Dragon Defense Traits | AC | 19 | | | ------------------------------------------------- | -------------------------- | ------------------------------ | | HP | 44 | | | Poise | 28 | DRA 2 | | Saves | Fort +8, Refl +3, Will +10 | adv vs fear | Steady Flight - Dragons cap at clumsy 2 while flying. Stomper - Dragons do not provoke AoOs from leaving a threatened square against creatures that are knocked down. Take Root healing recharge- At the start of its turn as a swift action while ground borne, a shrubbagazzor can cause roots to shoot out of its legs and burrow into the ground, allowing it to take the total defense action and reduce its speed to 0 this turn. If it remains rooted (aka it doesn't move or isn't forcefully moved). It also regains 10 +HP at the start of its next turn. This ability can only be used once a minute. Resistance - Poison Offense Traits (DC 15) | Bite (P) | +6 | +4 MV, +4 Dirty Trick | | -------------------------------------------------- | ---------- | --------------------------------------------------- | | | 2d8+3 (12) | +Jitters | | Claw (S) | +6 | +4 MV | | | 2d8+3 (12) | | Multiattack - Make a claw and bite attack Innate Techniques - Nourish, Tree Shape, Pack Attack 1/day - Wall of Sheep, Fetch, Ants in your Pants, Power of the Sun, Inheritor's Smite Constant - Courage New Life Companion - If a shrubbagazzor casts a Mother Earth spell on a space where there is vegetation growing, it also causes a whelp(agazzor) to grow from it, unless it has already animated 2 of them. Compared to a normal whelp, it has the plant creature type, poison resistance and the Shrubbagazing ability, but no breath weapon. Breath Weapon Recharge - Against plant creatures, the rose breath heals 6d6 (21) hp. Against non plants it fascinates for 1 round (will negates). Creatures with scent have disadvantage on this save. 40 ft cone To recharge this effect, a shrubbagazzor must restore hp from its Take Root ability. Tail Swipe - As a swift action while grounded, a dragon can make a quick turnaround and swipe at foes it threatens with its tail. Creatures take (10+LvL) poise damage (reflex halves). Poise broken creatures are knocked down. Jitters - The fragrant breath of a shrubbagazzor is a powerful energizer with an equally powerful crash. On hit on a bite attack, a creature gains +10 speed on their next turn, but become fatigued immediately after. A creature can only be affect by this once each day. Wing Buffet - As an immediate action when taking flight or landing, a dragon can flap its wings rapidly to whip up a whirlwind. Creatures within 20 ft are knocked down (fort negates). A creature can choose to forgo its save, automatically getting knocked down, to make an AoO if the dragon is within reach. Weaknesses/Deep Lore Stop and Smell the Flowers - While in tree shape, a shrubbagazor can have flowery vines growing along its trunk and branches. Creatures with scent always get a perception check to notice something odd about the tree. If they fail the perception check, they are affected as per the Jitters ability. Other Traits Ability Scores - STR +3, DEX +0, LUK +0, INT +0, WIS +3, CHA +2 Languages - Draconic, common, sylvan, druidic Senses - Darkvision, Echolocation (30 ft), Scent (10 ft) Shrubbagazing - A shrubbagazor can see through greenery like shrubs, leaves and whatnot as though they were transparent. It also gives this ability to whelps it summons. Dragon Flight - Dragons can take flight without using a swift action, but they must land by the end of their turn if they do so. When allowed to fly long distances high in the sky, a dragon can fly 600 miles a day, or 1000 if it fatigues itself. It can continue moving this fast for consecutive days by becoming drained 5 each day it does. Deific Boons - Spirit Animal, The Forest has Ears, Nature's Vigilance Speed - 30 Land Speed, 30 Fly Feats - Perfect Positioning, Divine Interference, Maneuver Versatility, Maneuver Master Skills - +8 (+4 scent) +Perception, +10 +Insight, +7 +Nature, +8 +Athletics

entries.title: Bog Creeper
entries.content: A pestilent creature with creatures jittering around just underneath its scales, this dragon has bad intentions and worse breath. These creatures prefer the semi aquatic slurry of swamps to the air. Level 7 Large Dragon/Undead Boss Defense Traits | AC | 23 | +2 Bone Cage | | ------------------------------------------------- | -------------------------- | ------------ | | HP | 150 | | | Poise | 30 | | | Saves | Fort +12, Refl +8, Will +7 | | Steady Flight - Dragons cap at clumsy 2 while flying. Stomper - Dragons do not provoke AoOs from leaving a threatened square against creatures that are knocked down. Immune - Poison, Disease Resistance - Necrotic Reformation - Instead of being defeated, a bog creeper immediately dissipates into 8 bone beetles that all appear 30 ft away from where it's defeated. These beetles act on the bog creepers turn. If any remain at the end of the bog creepers next turn (including any it summoned previously), the beetles coalesce and the bog creeper reforms at the point closest to all the beetles that remain with (# of beetles x10) hp. If the beetles are defeated in time, the bog creeper still reforms, but it is defeated. Carrion Consumption Healing recharge - Bog creepers often keep decaying and diseased creatures inside of its lair for later consumption. As a swift action, a bog creeper can consume such a creature to gain 20 temp hp and recharge its breath weapon. Offense Traits (DC 20) | Bite (P) | +10 | | | -------------------------------------------------- | ---------- | --- | | | 2d8+3 (12) | | | Claw (S) | +10 | | | | 2d8+3 (12) | | Multiattack - Make 2 melee attacks Innate Techniques - Blight (U4), Bone Cage, Unholy Frenzy, Corpse Transposition, Fake Out 1/day - Corpse Explosion, Gloom Bolt (U6), Warcry, Pulverize Breath Weapon Recharge poison - Unleash a wave of putrid and caustic liquid. 60 ft cone, 10d6 (35) poison damage and applying sickened 1 (reflex halves dmg and negates sickened). In its wake, it leaves behind hazardous waste that deals 2d6 (7) poison damage to any creatures that end their turn in the area. Recharges after using reformation/carrion consumption. Bad Breath poison - For 1 round after using its breath weapon, or carrion consumption, a bog creeper emits a horrific stench. Creatures it bites during this time become sickened 2. Any creature within 10 ft of the bog creeper during this time is made sickened 1 (fort negates). Creatures with the scent ability have disadvantage on this save. Tail Swipe - As a swift action while grounded, a dragon can make a quick turnaround and swipe at foes it threatens with its tail. Creatures take (10+LvL) poise damage (reflex halves). Poise broken creatures are knocked down. Wing Buffet - As an immediate action when taking flight or landing, a dragon can flap its wings rapidly to whip up a whirlwind. Creatures within 20 ft are knocked down (fort negates). A creature can choose to forgo its save, automatically getting knocked down, to make an AoO if the dragon is within reach. Beetlemancy - All Bone Beetles are considered companions that do not consume any companion slots. Bog creepers cannot summon bone beetles when they have 2 other non-beetle companions. Boss Actions - Usable 2/turn Back and Better than Ever - After using Reformation, use Breath Weapon. Freedom! (recharge) companion - After taking damage, summon a Bone Beetle. Usable 1/turn. Catch a Whiff - Breathe (not the weapon, just stank breath) on a creature within reach to inflict sickened 1 (fort negates). Weaknesses/Deep Lore Rotten Heart - A bog creepers heart can be destroyed to give a weapon the elemental weapon enchantment (poison) without consuming an enchantment slot. If destroyed with a staff, it instead gains the benefits of an elemental staff (poison). Take a Shower - If subject to cleaning materials as a standard action, such as soap, a bog creeper is drained 3. If the creature has a relevant +Profession skill, this drain is increased to half of the skill roll. Other Traits Ability Scores - STR +3, DEX +1, LUK +0, INT +1, WIS +0, CHA +3 Languages - Draconic, common, necril Senses - Darkvision, Echolocation (30 ft), Scent (10 ft) Dragon Flight - Dragons can take flight without using a swift action, but they must land by the end of their turn if they do so. When allowed to fly long distances high in the sky, a dragon can fly 600 miles a day, or 1000 if it exhausts itself. It can continue moving this fast for consecutive days by becoming drained 5 each day it does. Signature Skill - stalker, dampen presence Speed - 35 Land Speed, 35 Fly Feats - Feint Partner, Skill Focus, Signature Skill, Skills - +9 (+4 scent/echolocation) +Perception, +12 (adv) +Deception, +10 (adv) +Stealth

entries.title: Bone Beetle
entries.content: Level 3 Small Beast/Undead Defense Traits | AC | 16 | | | ------------------------------------------------- | ------------------------- | --- | | HP | 50 | | | Poise | 23 | | | Saves | Fort +2, Refl +7, Will +3 | | Offense Traits (DC 14) | Bite (P) | +5 | +3 companion, +4 MV, +4 vs Sickened | | -------------------------------------------------- | --------- | ----------------------------------------------------------------- | | | 2d6+2 (9) | +Gnaw Away | Gnaw Away - On-hit the bone beetle will tear off skin from its target, inflicting drained 1. Weaknesses/Deep Lore Weakness - Crush 5 Other Traits Ability Scores - STR -1, DEX +2, LUK -1, INT -3, WIS +0, CHA +0 Speed - 40 Land Speed, 40 Fly Feats - Linebacker, Maneuver Versatility, Feint Partner Skills - +4 +Perception

entries.title: Brood Mother
entries.content: An enormous female dragon covered in spines and (often) babies. Brood mothers are one of the most popular dragons to hunt since they provide a steady supply of eggs for poachers to sell as pets. Level 9 Huge Dragon Defense Traits | AC | 25 | | | ------------------------------------------------- | ---------------------------- | ------------- | | HP | 92 | | | Poise | 34 | DRA 2 | | Saves | Fort +12, Refl +10, Will +12 | | Steady Flight - Dragons cap at clumsy 2 while flying. Stomper - Dragons do not provoke AoOs from leaving a threatened square against creatures that are knocked down. Under My Wing - Whelps within reach of the brood mother get +3 AC. Immune - Fire Offense Traits (DC 21) | Bite (P) | +6 | | | -------------------------------------------------- | ---------- | --- | | | 2d8+3 (12) | | | Claw (S) | +6 | | | | 2d8+3 (12) | | Multiattack - Make a claw and bite attack Innate Techniques - Avenging Wrath, Flash Forward, Fire Blast (U5), Earth Shatter 1/day - Rainbow Beam Brood companion fire - A brood mother is capable of creating magical flaming eggs that hatch into whelps and assist her in battle. By default she always has 2 fiery flying friends around her. When a whelp is defeated, it explodes in fire in a 10 ft radius, dealing 4d6 (14) fire dmg (reflex halves). Whelps are immune to these explosions. Whelps share all the brood mother's teamwork feats. As a free action once per turn, she can spit out 2 flaming eggs at any position within 30 ft of her. These eggs have 10 hp, fire resistance and automatically get hit by any attacks/fail any saves. If at the start of the brood mother's turn she does not have 2 whelps on her side, enough eggs hatch to make 2. Breath Weapon Recharge fire- 10d6 (35) fire and sets ablaze (reflex halves and negates ablaze) 300 ft line, 15 ft wide In order to use this breath weapon, the brood mother must first take a deep breath on a previous turn (free action). She cannot take a deep breath on the same turn she uses her breath weapon. Tail Swipe - As a swift action while grounded, a dragon can make a quick turnaround and swipe at foes it threatens with its tail. Creatures take (10+LvL) poise damage (reflex halves). Poise broken creatures are knocked down. Wing Buffet - As an immediate action when taking flight or landing, a dragon can flap its wings rapidly to whip up a whirlwind. Creatures within 20 ft are knocked down (fort negates). A creature can choose to forgo its save, automatically getting knocked down, to make an AoO if the dragon is within reach. Weaknesses/Deep Lore Nesting - Brood mothers are rarely seen with their actual children nearby. Due to their propensity to generate frequent of offspring (by dragon standards anyway), they are often hunted to have their eggs sold off to rich foodies and exotic pet owners. As such, they nest deep within volcanic caverns, usually requiring swimming through lava to reach in the first place. Markets for such eggs pay 1,000g for each one. Protect - A creature that steals or threatens one of her real eggs/children taunts the brood mother until her spawn are relinquished. While taunted in this way, she is persistently under the effect of an Inferno Sphere that her whelps/eggs are immune to. She gets advantage on all attacks against them, and the creature has disadvantage on any saving throws made against the brood mother's abilities. Other Traits Ability Scores - STR +3, DEX +0, LUK +0, INT +0, WIS +1, CHA +3 Languages - Draconic, common, primordial Senses - Darkvision, Echolocation (30 ft), Scent (10 ft) Dragon Flight - Dragons can take flight without using a swift action, but they must land by the end of their turn if they do so. When allowed to fly long distances high in the sky, a dragon can fly 600 miles a day, or 1000 if it fatigues itself. It can continue moving this fast for consecutive days by becoming drained 5 each day it does. Deific Boons - Roar of Dragons, Undercover Speed - 30 Land Speed, 60 Fly Feats - Escape Route, Gang Up, Pack Tactics, As One, Combat Reflexes, Sentinel Skills - +12 (+4 scent) +Perception, +12 +Insight, +12 +Medicine, +11 Arcana, +11 +Local, +14 +Athletics

entries.title: Wildfire Wyrm
entries.content: This long serpentine dragon looks like it's made of wood, with a raging fire boiling underneath the gaps in its scales. It's face is decorated with a flaming beard, mustache and eyebrows with bony horns. Level 12 Huge Dragon/Plant Boss Defense Traits | AC | 29 | | | ------------------------------------------------- | ---------------------------- | ------------- | | HP | 260 | | | Poise | 44 | DRA 2 | | Saves | Fort +16, Refl +18, Will +13 | | Steady Flight - Dragons cap at clumsy 2 while flying. Stomper - Dragons do not provoke AoOs from leaving a threatened square against creatures that are knocked down. Under My Wing - Whelps within reach of the brood mother get +3 AC. Immune - Fire Take Root healing recharge- At the start of its turn as a swift action while ground borne, a wyrm can cause roots to shoot out of its legs and burrow into the ground, allowing it to take the total defense action and reduce its speed to 0 this turn. If it remains rooted (aka it doesn't move or isn't forcefully moved). It also regains 20 +HP at the start of its next turn. This ability can only be used once a minute. Offense Traits (DC 25) | Bite (P) | +16 | | | -------------------------------------------------- | ---------- | -------------------------------------- | | | 2d8+4 (13) | | | Tail (C) | +16 | +4 Topple, +Free topple On-hit | | | 2d8+4 (13) | | Multiattack - Make a tail and bite attack, but not against the same creature Innate Techniques - 1/day - Seismic Setup earth - As a swift action, a wildfire dragon can slam the ground with its tail near a creature within reach, causing the earth to crack like a seesaw, sending the creature into the air and causing them to land 30 ft away from their starting position knocked down (reflex reduces to 10 poise damage). Breath Weapon Recharge fire- 12d6 (41) fire (reflex halves) 60 ft cone. Any greenery in the cone is instantly turned to ash. Trees and other wooden objects are scorched. If an object is already scorched, it turns into ash. The breath weapon recharges when the dragon uses its Timber feature. Scale Shatter (fire) - Every time a wildfire dragon drops to a threshold noted in "wooden scales," many scales break rank and explode, embedding themselves in nearby surfaces (and creatures). Creatures within reach take 4d6 (14) pierce damage and creatures within 30 ft take 2d6 (7) pierce damage (reflex halves). Creatures that fail their save have scales embedded in their skin, dealing 2d6 (7) fire damage at the start of their turns. The scales can be removed automatically as a swift action, or as a free action if the creature possesses the +Medicine skill. Fissure (earth) - If a wildfire dragon senses a creature with its tremorsense while burrowed underground, it can appear suddenly and violently. It shatters the ground within 30ft of the position it exits the ground from. This ability only functions on unsuspecting creatures. When using it, creatures get a perception check (DC 22) to notice. Those that fail to notice are entangled and silenced while crushed inside cracks in the earth and unable to breathe. If a creature rolls at least 32 on perception, they can push an unsuspecting ally to safety, treating them as though they noticed the fissures. This does not require an action and is intended to be used as an opener in place of stealth. Tail Swipe - As a swift action while grounded, a dragon can make a quick turnaround and swipe at foes it threatens with its tail. Creatures take (10+LvL) poise damage (reflex halves). Poise broken creatures are knocked down. Wing Buffet - As an immediate action when taking flight or landing, a dragon can flap its wings rapidly to whip up a whirlwind. Creatures within 20 ft are knocked down (fort negates). A creature can choose to forgo its save, automatically getting knocked down, to make an AoO if the dragon is within reach. Some kind of ash combo with breath weapon? Boss Actions - Usable 1/turn Timber (recharge) - At the end of another creature's turn, take a bite out of a tree and cause it to fall in a direction of the wildfire dragon's choosing. This essentially functions as a line attack whose distance depends on the size of the tree. All creatures in this line take 4d6 (14) crush damage, are knocked down, Held Down and entangled (reflex halves dmg and negates status). The knockdown condition cannot be removed until the entangled is removed first. Creatures can attempt to Breakout as a standard action. Dealing 30 damage to the tree is also enough to free a creature, or 15 slash or fire damage. Cannot be used in consecutive turns (chew your food!). Begone! - Use Seismic Setup at the end of another creatures turn. Pin - Usable when a creature moves adjacent to a wall while within reach. The dragon makes a melee attack that deals tail damage and entangles the creature on hit. This entangle lasts until the wildfire dragon is no longer threatening the creature or it is poise broken. Friction - If a creature within reach is currently entangled, a wildfire dragon can end the condition by dragging the length of its entire body against the creature rapidly. This automatically sunders the creatures armor (or shield if the creature chooses), deals 4d6 (14) slashing and 4d6 (14) fire damage and inflicts a unique T3 affliction. - Affliction: Melted - Creature's equipment has melted together with its skin. Things such as clothing, weapons, armor/shields cannot be removed. Creature is Drained equal to their current missing +HP. Creature cannot recover from this drain until the equipment is separated and the affliction is removed. - Cure - Deep soak in cool clean water for several hours a day for a week while under the supervision of a creature trained in +Medicine. No strenuous activity for the duration. Weaknesses/Deep Lore Boss Mechanic: Wooden Scales - A wildfire dragon has surprisingly soft and vulnerable scales, so it has developed a unique form of protection. It uses its magic to form a wooden scale exoskeleton on the outside of its body. As the wildfire dragon fights, it gives off heat which burns away these scales, leaving itself vulnerable, but giving it greater mobility. When a wildfire dragon reaches 210 hp, and every time it loses 50 more (160, 110, 60, 10), a layer of the scales explodes violently, activating Scale Shatter, decreasing the dragon's AC by 1, but increasing its speeds by 10. Healing past this threshold does not allow them to regain this AC or use scale shatter again. From the Ashes - Wildfire dragons must consume the ashen remains of creatures in order to replenish the protection from their wooden scales. They need a steady supply of ashes with their usual meals over the course of a week to regain 1 layer of scales. They typically bury themselves somewhere near their supply. Other Traits Ability Scores - STR +4, DEX +4, LUK +0, INT +1, WIS +1, CHA +0 Languages - Draconic, common, primordial Senses - Darkvision, Echolocation (30 ft), Scent (10 ft) Dragon Flight - Dragons can take flight without using a swift action, but they must land by the end of their turn if they do so. When allowed to fly long distances high in the sky, a dragon can fly 600 miles a day, or 1000 if it fatigues itself. It can continue moving this fast for consecutive days by becoming drained 5 each day it does. Deific Boons - Roar of Dragons, Undercover Speed - 30 Land Speed, 30 Fly, 30 Burrow (+10 to all speeds per scale shatter) Feats - Blind Fight, Skills - +15 (+4 scent/echolocation) +Perception, +15 +Insight, +18 +Athletics

entries.title: Deadbeat Djinn
entries.content: You pick up a bottle, disappointed that its empty only for a creature to pop out. This stinky humanoid has no legs, only a twisting tempest that hovers above the ground connected to the bottle. Level 1 Medium Elemental Defense Traits | AC | 15 | | | ------------------------------------------------- | ------------------------- | --- | | HP | 26 | | | Poise | 26 | | | Saves | Fort +3, Refl +1, Will +3 | | Offense Traits (DC 12) | Scimitar (S) | +5 | One and Done, Thrown | | ------------------------------------------------------ | --------- | ---------------------------- | | | d10+2 (7) | | Multiattack - Make 2 Thrown attacks with scimitars Innate Techniques (CL 4) - Imagination, Weapon Toss, Silencing Strike, Fraudgg, Time Shudder, Star Fall, Stunt Double Stench Aura Toxin - A deadbeat djinn has a natural musk. Creatures within 5 ft of the djinn are made sickened 2 until they leave this aura. Creatures with scent have this penalty while within 20 ft. Weaknesses/Deep Lore Lucky Contestant Curse - A deadbeat djinn lives in bottles of discarded alcoholic beverages, revealing itself and its horrid stench only when someone attempts to drink from this unusual looking empty vessel. If someone is unlucky enough to encounter this creature, it is now cursed to live with this creature for 1 week. It will inject itself into social situations with its outdated and offensive opinions, it will ruin the mood at gatherings and it will steal all of your alcoholic beverages. While this curse persists, you cannot escape the djinn, and its vessel is always in your pocket, bag or conveniently between you and your drink. If you use its wishtidigitation feature, you can decrease the amount of time they follow you around for by 1 minute. If you spend downtime thinking about random wishes to make, you decrease the duration of the curse by (int+1) days. Wishtidigitation - A deadbeat djinn is a master of only the most obscure magics, functioning in ways that defy all reason. They are able to perform great feats, but only when the end result would not be particularly beneficial, only interesting, quirky or mundane. If the object or information in question can be considered useful, the deadbeat djinn cannot perform it. If you wish for your date's favorite flower, then the deadbeat djinn can teleport you A flower, but not the one you are looking for. They can teleport the flower from another planet, but it looks like another flower on the current planet and has no medicinal properties. The more impressively mundane the better. Trivia on dead commoners, the number of breaths you've taken during your life, the number of grains of sand in the world compared to planets in the multiverse are all just one "I wish..." away. Other Traits Ability Scores - STR +2, DEX +0, LUK -1, INT +3, WIS +0, CHA +0 Languages - All Speed - 30 hover Feats - Ricochet Toss, Bonded Weapon Skills - +3 +Perception, +6 all +Knowledge

Feel free to add your own! Jade Idol - As a free action, a jade delight can summon a jade totem in an unoccupied space within 30 ft. This totem can be used as the origin point for any Melee Attack or Spell Attack for any allied elemental. They start at medium size, and increase in size if the previous is not destroyed (usually with Brute Force or mining, +2 DC per size above medium). Topaz - Naturally Invisible while in the radius of an effect with the light tag. This effect ends when it does anything other than move or use the following ability. Can spend a FRA to channel energy to heal all allies within 60 ft 5 hp, or a single creature within range 10 hp (Healing). ETC - Gain a Breath Weapon. Deals 3d8+3 (16) poison damage and applies sickened 1 (reflex halves damage and negates sicken). 30 ft cone. Weaknesses/Deep Lore Miner Issue - As a standard action, a creature can roll a +Profession (miner) check while a miner's delight is within their natural reach. They deal True Damage to the creature equal to half their profession roll. Level 6 Medium Elemental Defense Traits | AC | 20 | | | ------------------------------------------------- | ------------------------- | --- | | HP | 79 | | | Poise | 27 | | | Saves | Fort +8, Refl +8, Will +8 | | Resistance - Fire Amorphous - +13 Never Alone - Cannot be flanked Soggy recharge - As an instant action, a cheezette can absorb a water effect, as though it was dispelled. It can do this once an hour. Offense Traits (DC 18) | Dangerously Cheesy (Fire) | +8 | | | ------------------------------------------------------------------- | ---------- | ------- | | | 4d8+3 (21) | +Sizzle | Innate Techniques (CL 8) - Blazing Palm, Flame Cleanse Me, Gooball, Splatter, Dispel Magic 1/day - Mirror Images, To the Rescue!, Blazing Beam, Backfire Sizzle fire - Some of the hot cheese is left behind by a cheezettes touch. It is applied on hit, and functions exactly as ablaze. Sisters in Arms - Cheezette functions more like a two-headed creature, so it still only gets 1 turn, but the two assist each other in combat. At start of turn, gain 1 benefit for 1 round. - Gain Advantage on Attack - Gain advantage on skill checks - Gain advantage on saving throws - Gain advantage on CL checks Dangerously Cheesy (fire) - When creature melee attacks a cheezete, the weapon they use to attack gets stuck unless they succeed on brute force to pull it out. If they fail and they don’t let go of the weapon, it is heated up and they take 3d6 (10) fire damage. It can wield any weapons that are left behind in it. If the attack is a projectile weapon, they can spit it back out at their enemy, making an attack with the same range as an immediate action. Summon Ally (Recharge) - 1 Big Cat (cheetah variant). Only 1 can be summoned at once. Weaknesses/Deep Lore Topping Overload - For every weapon stuck inside of it past the first, a cheezette has its speed lowered by 5. It can remove a stuck weapon from itself as a swift action. Stuck ammunition does not lower its speed. entries.title: (7) Jad
entries.content: This is gonna hurt... Level 10 Massive Elemental boss Unique Defense Traits | AC | 26 | +4 Molten Shield | | ------------------------------------------------- | ---------------------------- | ---------------- | | HP | 240 | | | Poise | 55 | | | Saves | Fort +17, Refl +12, Will +12 | | Resistance - Fire Amorphous - +14 Molten Shield - Jad is immune to any effect that decreases his speed or limits his actions. Gain +4 AC and resistance to all damage. Cannot fall below half +HP while this shield persists. Massive - Jad's movement is Elusive against creatures that are medium or smaller Offense Traits (DC 24) | Bite (P) | +18 | | | -------------------------------------------------- | ----------------------------------------------------------------- | ------------------- | | | 2d20+6 (no average (:) | +Overwhelming Force | Here Comes Jad! - Jad falls from the ceiling of the battlefield and crashes into the center of the arena. All creatures inside are instantly set to 1 poise. Overwhelming Force - On-hit Jad's bite deals an equal amount of poise damage. If this poise breaks, the creature is tossed 60 feet directly away from him. Fire Storm fire persistent - Jad causes many pillars of flame to appear around the battlefield, functioning as the Pillar of Flame technique with the following changes: It appears underneath each enemy in combat, regardless of distance. They do not require concentration and they persist until Jad is defeated. They deal 2d20 damage (reflex halves). Boss Sequence - The Jad fight is unique in that it is intended to have "phases." It is intended to be an incredibly long and difficult fight where the turns proceed as follows. 1st Turn - Jad unleashes a powerful volley of attacks as a standard action (see below). 2nd Turn - Jad gets real hungry and moves towards the nearest source of lava, unleashing a fire storm and summoning a sequence of enemies reminiscent of previous waves. 3rd Turn - Jad recharges his assault and loses his molten shield for 1 round while he guzzles some nearby molten rocks (standard action). This sequence will repeat itself until Jad gets below 120 HP. Below Half - Jad will summon 4 (6) Servant of Jad. In 4 random directions 30 ft away from him that act after the current turn. Jad's Assault Volcanic Surge - The lava surrounding the battlefield closes in, covering the outer 10 ft of the battlefield. Pick a random direction. A 30 ft line in the ground extending from this outer edge cracks and fills with lava under the nearest creature, hitting them with partial submersion in lava (reflex negates). Crush earth - Jad slams the ground and causes a stalactite to crack on the ceiling above each enemy, leaving a bunch of rocks on the ground beneath as an indicator for what is to come. At the end of the creature's next turn, a huge (size category, as if it were a huge creature) falls down upon them, dealing 2d20 crush damage. Creatures within 20 ft of the impact zone (except Jad) take 2d20 poise damage, knocking down any that are poise broken. This effect also leaves behind a stalactite that alters the terrain, providing cover and a place to get high ground, and a place to escape the lava if it rises high enough. Time's Ticking (fire) - Jad spits a fire blast that slowly moves towards the nearest creature. At the end of the creatures next turn, it homes in on their location and explodes in their space, dealing 2d20 fire damage in a 20 ft AoE (reflex halves). If the creature somehow becomes untargetable, the next closest enemy is chosen instead. Earthquake - All enemies take 2d20 poise damage. Poise broken creatures are Knocked Down. Weaknesses/Deep Lore Wet Weakness - Takes a -4 penalty on saves against effects with the water tag. Any attacks with the water tag have advantage against this creature. Clumsy Titan - Jad cannot make AoOs against creatures of medium size or smaller. Focus - Jad will prefer targeting creatures without a form of cover with his attacks. Other Traits Ability Scores - STR +6, DEX +0, LUK +0, INT -2, WIS +0, CHA +2 Languages - Primordial (cannot speak) Lava Walker - Jad can move through lava, even if it is liquid, without harm to itself. Ignores difficult terrain that is lava-based. Speed - 40 Land Speed Feats - Pack Tactics, Skill Focus Skills - +12 (adv) +Perception, +26 (adv) +Athletics
