entries.title: Censorship Fairy
entries.content: You coax a beautiful fey creature out of the water, only for rays of sun to censor their naked body. You curse, only to find your words replaced with a loud sharp noise that makes your ears bleed. Level 0 Tiny Fey Defense Traits | AC | 12 | | | ------------------------------------------------- | ------------------------- | ----------- | | HP | 20 | | | Poise | 15 | | | Saves | Fort -2, Refl +3, Will +3 | Evasion | Natural Invisibility Su - While censoring, a censorship fairy is affected as per invisibility Offense Traits (DC 12) Innate Techniques - Prestidigitation, Living Roots, Acid Splash 1/day - Fairy Dust Censor (Su) - Whenever a creature says a curse word, or something particularly vulgar, a censorship fairy can cause a black box to appear over their mouth, and substitute the word with a loud beeping sound as an immediate action. The target creature's word cannot be heard, and they take d10 (5) sonic damage. This breaks invisibility. Weaknesses/Deep Lore Recruitment - A censorship fairy is willing to join the service of particular beautiful creatures. If they are currently employed, this typically requires an opposed +Persuasion roll. Other Traits Ability Scores - STR -2, DEX +1, LUK +3, INT +0, WIS +3, CHA +0 Languages - All Censorship (Su) concentration figment - Censorship fairies specialize in a very particular type of censorship: That of protecting the nudey bits of naked creatures. They can create incredibly detailed and fine-tuned illusions, but only to cover up erogenous zones in incredibly tasteful ways, such as a bunch of leaves in the wind, a ray of light or something more elaborate and unusual. They can censor such things by creating the figment as a standard action, and maintaining it as a free action. They need to stay within 30 feet of the creature they censor to maintain the illusion. Speed - 15 Land Speed, 35 Hover that can be used to fly during their turn Signature Skill - dampen presence, stalker Feats - Signature Skill Skills - +5 +Perception, +5 (+10 Invisibility) +Stealth

entries.title: G'nome
entries.content: A pointed hat and thick white hair leading into a beard and mustache grace the head of this short creature. One of its allies tosses it across the battlefield and reveals the hat isn't for show. Level 1 Small Fey Defense Traits | AC | 16 | | | ------------------------------------------------- | ------------------------- | --- | | HP | 30 | | | Poise | 26 | | | Saves | Fort +3, Refl +3, Will +2 | | Stick the Landing - When a gnome falls, they can choose to stick into the ground with their pointed hats to negate the fall damage as an instant action. Their speed is reduced by 20 and they cannot use their pointy feature for 1 round. Offense Traits (DC 13) | Battle Ladder (C) | +3 | +4 MV, +4 GU, +2 disarm/dirty trick/drag | | ----------------------------------------------------------- | ---------- | ------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | | | 2d8+2 (11) | | Innate Techniques - Boasting Taunt 1/day - Backstab, Pocket Sand, Kidnap Snatch and Grab - After using +Sleight of Hand to steal something, or disarming an opponent, all of the gnomes actions become elusive against the target of their theft. Pointy elusive - When a creature falls onto a space or jumps into a space within 10 ft of a gnome, the gnome can move to the space directly where the creature will land and use its pointed hat to damage them as an immediate action. The creature takes 2d6 (7) pierce damage and is made clumsy 1. They can choose any space adjacent to the gnome to land. Sharp Aim - A gnome can spend a standard action to prepare and assume the position. Any other allied creature of at least medium size can throw a gnome in the position as though they were a thrown weapon with +2 attack (as though the gnome was aiding them), dealing 5d6 (17) piercing damage. If a gnome is poise broken while assuming the position, its stance falters and it can no longer be thrown. Weaknesses/Deep Lore Gnome Toss - Gnomes are surprisingly aerodynamic and light. After grappling a gnome, you can choose to end your grapple to toss the gnome (+Athletics roll) feet in a direction of your choosing. This also happens if you successfully shove, with the athletics roll determining the extra distance moved. This happens instead of the usual effect of grapple and in addition to the effects of a shove. One of a Kind - Gnomes love to steal things that normally come as a set, but only 1 of them, such as a pair of socks or several wheels on a vehicle. When presented with the opportunity to pilfer such an object, a gnome will do everything in their power to steal it. This could mean invading a home after hours, staking out a vehicle etc. Other Traits Ability Scores - STR +2, DEX +2, LUK +2, INT +0, WIS -1, CHA +2 Languages - Sylvan, Common Equipment - 1 Battle Ladder (2h weapon) Garden Gnome Su poly, true - Gnomes can transform into a lawn ornament that resembles a gnome. In this form, they can only move while not observed, unless the observers are gnomes. This effectively functions as Hide in Plain Sight. Speed - 40 Land Speed Deific Boons - enlightened Feats - Maneuver Master, Maneuver Versatility, Gang Up Skills - +2 +Perception, +5 +Stealth, +7 +Sleight of Hand, +5 +Deception, +3 +Cryptozoology

entries.title: Yummi
entries.content: A magical cat floats around in the air riding on a sizable tome. It taunts you as it empowers the creature it is tethered to. Level 3 Fey/Beast Defense Traits | AC | 13 | | | ------------------------------------------------- | ------------------------- | --- | | HP | 28 | | | Poise | 16 | | | Saves | Fort -2, Refl +3, Will +6 | | Parasite Su - As a swift action, a detached yummi can attach to a creature within 10 feet, gently floating around them at a fixed distance. It acts in tandem with the creature in initiative, using its actions interchangeably with theirs. It cannot be subject to any effects and is Untargetable, even to AoE effects, while attached. If the attached creature is defeated, yummi is stunned for 1 round and detaches. A yummi can detach as a standard action, popping out in an adjacent space. While attached, a yummi cannot be detected if the creature it is attached to cant be detected, such as if it is Invisible or is using Hide in Plain Sight. Offense Traits (DC 15) Innate Techniques - Magic Missile Weaknesses/Deep Lore Just a Myth - When a yummi is defeated, it gets sucked into its magic book permanently. With a DC 25 arcana check it can be summoned again with a 1 minute ritual at 1 hp. If the book is destroyed, the yummi is killed. A caster can cast any spells the yummi can innately cast using this book when it is not summoned. Helpless Baby - A yummi is always considered a Minion. Other Traits Ability Scores - STR -3, DEX +0, LUK +1, INT +3, WIS +1, CHA +1 Languages - Sylvan, Common, Elven, Primordial, Infernal, Celestial, Goblin, Fairy Flare, Morse Code, Draconic, Manganese Vigilant - Creatures the yummi is attached to aren't surprised if the yummi isn't Magic Book (Su) - By reading the magic book that it rides on, a yummi can confer a magic benefit upon itself, or an attached creature while using its parasite feature. It cannot grant the same benefit in subsequent turns. - Zoomies - Increase Land Speed by 10 ft and grant 4 Temp HP for 1 round. - E Healing - Heal 8 +HP - Swift Strike - Make a simple attack Nightvision Speed - 20, 30 Hover (on book) Deific Boons - Animal Instincts Feats - Skill Focus, Signature Skill (perception) Skills - +8 (adv) +Perception, +12 Arcana, +8 +Nature, +10 +History, +8 +Planes, +6 +Persuasion, +8 +Linguistics

entries.title: High Roller
entries.content: This tiny fairy carries a pack filled with dice. It regularly rolls these dice during the battle, growing more powerful the higher it rolls. Level 4 Tiny Fey Defense Traits | AC | 18 | | | ------------------------------------------------- | ------------------------- | --- | | HP | 52 | | | Poise | 17 | | | Saves | Fort +3, Refl +7, Will +5 | | Blessing of Frogg Saran - High rollers receive a unique blessing from their patron deity. Whenever they take damage type (fire, crush, force), there is a 1 in 6 chance they gain resistance to that damage type for that day, including the instance of damage they just took. Once they fail to gain resistance to a damage type, they don't roll it again for the rest of that day. Keep on rolling baby. Offense Traits (DC 16) | Rapier (P) | +7 | Chink Magnet, Dueling | | ---------------------------------------------------- | -------- | ----------------------------- | | | d6+3 (6) | +Lucky Break | Multiattack - Feint and make a melee attack Innate Techniques - Mage Hand, Ventriloquism 1/day - Fairy Dust, Memory Lapse, WonkaS, Bladed Dash Lucky Break - A high roller gets its name from its ability to pull wins out of its ass from seemingly nowhere. Whenever it rolls a d6 and rolls a 6, it rolls an additional d6. This can happen infinitely. Loaded Dice recharge - By blowing on a standard dice (a d6) and throwing it up to 30 feet, a high roller can cause it to explode. Deals a number of d6s of force damage equal to the end result of the loaded dice (reflex halves). This explosion is a 10 ft radius, with its center point on the ground where the dice lands. For example, i roll a 4 on the d6. This ability does 4d6 damage. Because of lucky break, this can potentially do infinite damage. If the high roller initially rolls a 6 when determining how many damage dice there are, this ability immediately recharges. Weaknesses/Deep Lore "Nature Spirit" - A high roller has the ability to transform into a particular butterfly. They are typically exceptionally colorful and sparkly. They can speak while transformed. They use this form to tempt unsuspecting creatures into playing their silly games of chance. 99% of Gamblers - A high roller is always enticed by games of chance, the higher the risk the better. They respect the results of such gambles, even to their own detriment. As such, high rollers are typically in crippling debt. Only 1% of them are filthy rich. Rich high rollers typically have expensive gear upgrades at their disposal. Other Traits Ability Scores - STR -1, DEX +3, LUK +3, INT +0, WIS -1, CHA +0 Languages - Sylvan, Common Speed - 30, 30 hover Fairy Dust - High rollers have bags of their own natural dust they keep at their hip. It takes a week to gather enough dust to fill a bag. This bag can be used to conjure a single item into existence worth 100 gold or less from the bag. The item is real, but causes the fairy dust to disappear. Deific Boons - enlightened Feats - Antagonize, Scapegoat Skills - +5 +Perception, +9 +Stealth, +8 (+2 feint) +Deception, +11 +Profession (gambling)

entries.title: Satyr
entries.content: A mischievous pan flute playing creature with a humanoid torso and furry hooved legs prances about. Their magic music twists the nature of any magic affecting them with random results. Level 4 Medium Fey Defense Traits | AC | 18 | | | ------------------------------------------------- | ------------------------- | --- | | HP | 52 | | | Poise | 25 | | | Saves | Fort +4, Refl +6, Will +5 | | Offense Traits (DC 16) | Satyr Shortbow (P) | +6 | Reload, BE | | ------------------------------------------------------------ | --------- | ---------------------------- | | | 2d6+2 (9) | | Multiattack - Make 2 ranged attacks Innate Techniques (CL 5) - Reincarnate, Tentacles for Arms, Fraudgg, Flying Arrow (U2), WonkaS, Boasting Taunt 1/day - Mind Maze, Laser Pointer, Pin Down Special Attack: Fairy Fire Su - Make a shortbow attack that deals an additional 2d6 (7) dmg. Target becomes glowing on-hit. Arrow can be removed as a standard action. Windpipes of Whimsy (Su) concentration - Satyrs regularly go out of their way to deface the temples of other gods and receive favor from Frogg Saran by doing so. They can play their pan flute as a swift action gaining the benefits of the Oh my Frogg technique. The satyr is talented enough to play without use of its hands. Weaknesses/Deep Lore Horny - When presented with the opportunity to get physical with attractive young people, satyr are just about willing to give up any previous allegiances. Such creatures need only offer their bodies to do so. Other Traits Ability Scores - STR +0, DEX +2, LUK +3, INT +1, WIS -1, CHA +1 Languages - Sylvan, Common Equipment - 1x short bow, 1x pan flute Deific Boons - enlightened Feats - Rapid Reload, Lightning Reload, Suppressing Fire, Bullseye, Spotter Skills - +5 +Perception, +7 +Deception, +7 +Profession (wind instruments), +7 +Local, +7 +Nature, +7 +Cryptozoology

entries.title: coven-magic
entries.content: Crust Coven Magic

entries.title: Hag
entries.content: The once innocent looking old lady transforms before your eyes into a wicked monster with plant-like growths and magic fetishes covering its body. Her cackle makes your skin crawl. Level 5 Large Fey Boss Defense Traits | AC | 20 | | | ------------------------------------------------- | -------------------------- | ------------------ | | HP | 120 | Magic Resist 3 | | Poise | 26 | | | Saves | Fort +7, Refl +5, Will +10 | | Offense Traits (DC 19) | Claws (S) | +7 | | | --------------------------------------------------- | --------- | --- | | | 2d6+2 (8) | | Innate Techniques (CL 10) - Mage Hand, Detect Pregnancy, Ventriloquism, Imagination, Dispel Magic, Violent Accident, Poison the Well, Grasp of the Undying, Acid Splash, Icicle 1/day - Persistent Pranksters, Pollinate (U3), Gutwrench, Animate Dead, Corpse Explosion, Drain Soul Constant - Spectral Hand Quicken Spell - Any cantrip Summon Ally (recharge) companion - Summon 2 fey that are level 2 or lower. They share the hag's teamwork feats. Boss Actions - Usable 1/turn Scatter Clones Su elusive figment recharge - At the end of the hags turn, she can cause 4 exact copies of herself appear in adjacent spaces. She and her copies immediately scatter in every direction. A creature can detect hidden presence to tell which one is the real one with a DC 25 perception check. However, if the clones live until the start of the hag's next turn, they also get 30 ft of movement and a standard action to cast a cantrip. They continue to do so every turn they survive. The clones automatically disappear when they are Poise broken, take +HP damage or fail a saving throw. Deep Digs psychic mind-affecting - This can only target an enemy at the start of their turn. Against a creature it has successfully rolled a +Knowledge check against, a hag can unbury some deep insecurity. The creature takes 3d6 (10) psychic damage and has Disadvantage on its next attack roll (will halves dmg and negates disadvantage). Imposter Imposter (Su) (figment) (recharge) teleportation - At the end of another creature's turn, a hag can teleport within 10 ft of a that creature if they are within 60 feet. When she does, the target creature also teleports to another location within 10 feet of their original position and the hag copies their appearance exactly. Using this on enemies tends to fall through if they know each other well enough, so hags prefer to choose neutral parties, such as creatures they capture. If they aren't participating in combat because they are a noncombatant, she can use this ability at the end of any other creature's turn to target this noncombatant. Weaknesses/Deep Lore Dealer - Hags typically do not like to engage in combat, and instead prefer to cooperate with other sentient creatures. This is to get something out of them rather than the goodness of their heart. As such, a hag will employ any method in her arsenal: threatening loved ones, offering valuable items or information, healing unique ailments, threatening retaliation etc. Coven - Hags typically exist in familial trios called covens. They don't live with their sisters, but they tend to have some method to regularly visit them. This could mean they live somewhere close by, they travel there via the feywilds, whatever. They keep in regular contact and come to each other's aid when needed. When gathered, hags possess the ability to perform magical rituals. This allows them to cast spells beyond what they are normally capable of. The most notable of which is a type of resurrection usable on the sisters. This happens automatically when the hag dies, and she appears within a special predesignated location in one of the homes of her sisters. When this happens, the hag is drained 10. More info about covens, such as how to stop the resurrection, can be found in subsection, which requires more direct research for players to find than just a successful knowledge check. Other Traits Ability Scores - STR +2, DEX +0, LUK +0, INT +3, WIS +3, CHA +3 Languages - All Deific Boons - party animal, mistrust, devil's in the details Signature Skill - lore dump, nerd Speed - 30 Land Speed Feats - Skill Focus, Signature Skill, MM Bouncing Spell, Cooperative Casting Skills - +10 +Perception, +10 +Insight, +10 +Deception, +10 +Persuasion, +12 +Disguise, +12 (adv) +Local, +10 +Nature, +10 Arcana, +10 +Religion, +10 +History, +10 +Cryptozoology

entries.title: Crystal Muncher
entries.content: A large purple creature that has gems embedded all around its body munches on some rocks, greedily shoving everything within reach into its mouth. Level 7 Huge Fey Defense Traits | AC | 21 | | | ------------------------------------------------- | -------------------------- | -------------------------------------- | | HP | 76 | | | Poise | 30 | | | Saves | Fort +11, Refl +6, Will +9 | +4 blurred vision, blind, mind reading | Resistance - Blinded, Blurred Vision, enigma Crystal Shield - A crystal muncher cannot be flanked. Offense Traits (DC 19) | Unarmed (C) | +13 | | | ----------------------------------------------------- | ---------- | ---------------- | | | 2d8+3 (12) | +Mage Slayer | Innate Techniques - Body Slam (U4), Dispelling Strike, Vulnerability 1/day - Shine, Focus Punch, Pulverize, Crystalline Explosion, Hammer Fist Constant - Life Drinker (U4) Quicken Spell - Crystalline Explosion Trample - 2d8+3 (12) damage. Instead of inflicting Knockdown, the crystal muncher can Swallow any creatures it tramples. Swallowed creatures are drained 3 immediately and at the end of their turns. Hide and Seek - A crystal muncher can consume a 5 ft cube of solid rock, crystal, metal etc as a swift action. It typically does this to remove Cover. Weaknesses/Deep Lore I Like Money - A Crystal Muncher's only true allegiance is to shiny rocks. If presented with the opportunity to munch on a particularly expensive rock/metal (at least 1,000g value) then it will spend a standard action gobbling it up. Presenting such an item is a standard action, and must be done within 30 ft. Once it consumes an item, it cannot be tempted by another for 2 rounds. Other Traits Ability Scores - STR +4, DEX -1, LUK +2, INT +0, WIS +0, CHA +0 Languages - Common, Sylvan Senses - Scent 10 ft (can only be used to smell something's value) Signature Skill - inception Speed - 25 Land Speed Feats - MM Latent Curse, Mage Slayer, Skill Focus, Signature Skill Skills - +7 +Perception, +13 +Athletics, +9 (adv) +Deception, +9 Arcana, +11 +Nature !From fusionmon|400

entries.title: Spelling Bee
entries.content: This creature is relatively humanoid, but with many bee features flies through the air with a swarm of bees. It is very well spoken and gets irritated when you use incorrect grammar or vocabulary. Level 7 Medium Fey Defense Traits | AC | 21 | | | ------------------------------------------------- | ------------------------- | --------------- | | HP | 76 | | | Poise | 30 | | | Saves | Fort +7, Refl +9, Will +9 | +4 Confused | Protect - A spelling bee can use an immediate action to command their bees to protect them, giving a +4 bonus to AC or a reflex save. Offense Traits (DC 19) | Stinger (P) | +9 | | | ----------------------------------------------------- | --------- | ------------------------------- | | | 2d6+2 (9) | +Stagger for 1 round, Venom | Innate Techniques (CL 11) - Nap Time, Splatter, Pollinate (U3), Ants in your Pants 1/day - Floristry, Leech Seed, Chimpanzee Bolt, Solar Power Venom Quiz language - As a swift action, quiz a creature within 30 ft on the spelling of a particular word. Spelling bees usually start off with simpler words then move on to more complicated words, unless things look particularly dire. A player can choose to spell it out OR have their character roll +Linguistics (nat 20 always succeeds). "Easy" DC 14 - Thoughtful, influential, equipment, syndrome, claimant, intuitively Medium DC 20 - Eusociality, splendiferous, separate, etymology Hard DC 26 - Platitudinous, antennae, silhouette, acquiesce Incorrect responses or cheaters cause the spelling bees swarm to home in on the creature, dealing 10 pierce damage and staggering them for 1 round (fort negates stagger). If multiple spelling bees are present, they give one the floor and don't interrupt with their own questions if one has already been asked this round. Weaknesses/Deep Lore Student Becomes Master - When quizzed, creatures trained in +Linguistics can make an opposed roll as an immediate action to trip up the spelling bee and have her own swarm sting her. She is immune to the poison however. Mix and Match - Spelling bees love to combine bees with other creatures. By bringing a creature back to their hive, they can transform them to get a new yellow and black coat of paint. For bestiary creatures, this can result in interesting fusions like the Bee Rex. If a player offers to be part of the bee's experiments, they can choose to undergo a 1 week transformation. Over this week they transform to more closely resemble a bee with additional limbs, black and yellow fuzzyness and antennae. They also get one of the following boons and banes.... Boons - 1 - You can produce honey. You can produce a few ounces of honey each day, as long as you are not going hungry and have a steady supply of flowers. This honey takes about an hour of work and 24 hours of waiting for the moisture to evaporate to be ready for consumption. 2 - You get functional wings, giving you the glide quality. 3 - You get functional antennae, giving you Scent 5 ft. 4 - You get a stinger attack with d8 base damage that applies venom on hit. Banes - 1 - You become an herbivore, only able to eat sweet foods like fruit/honey as well as nectar/pollen. 2 - You become a little too fuzzy, making you better adapted for cold climates. In warm climates you are persistently exhausted without some cooling method. 3 - You are protective of your kin. You cannot harm bees unless they attack you first. The first time one of your allies is defeated in combat, you are taunted by the creature that defeated them. 4 - Your additional bee limbs throw you off balance. You take a -2 penalty to all DEX based skill checks and ability checks. Other Traits Ability Scores - STR +0, DEX +2, LUK +1, INT +3, WIS +0, CHA +2 Languages - All Senses - Scent 5 ft Signature Skill - secret-ish, read lips, cliff's notes Speed - 25 Land Speed, 40 hover, 40 fly Feats - Signature Skill, Lead by Example, Photographic Memory, Toughness Skills - +9 (+4 scent) +Perception, +11 +Persuasion, +12 +Profession (dance/sing), +12 +Nature, +12 Arcana, +16 +Linguistics, +11 +Acrobatics

entries.title: Tramp
entries.content: You open a book and read some magic words only for a short fiend to pop out. It makes some suggestive comments and would love to spend more time with you. Level 0 Small Fiend lewd (minion) Defense Traits | AC | 12 | | | ------------------------------------------------- | ------------------------- | ------------ | | HP | 20 | | | Poise | 16 | | | Saves | Fort -2, Refl +1, Will +4 | +4 charm | Offense Traits (DC 12) | Gore (P) | +1 | | | -------------------------------------------------- | -------- | --- | | | d6+1 (4) | | Innate Techniques (CL 2) - Charming Gaze, Dance With Me!, Icicle Go Fuck Yourself Su recharge - A tome imp can create a duplicate of itself to demonstrate its debauchery, giving it advantage on any checks to seduce. Until one is attacked, both are considered real, and can cast spells, attack etc, but does not give additional actions or movement. While a duplicate is active, either one getting targeted with an attack, spell or other harmful effect causes it to disappear. Tramp Stamp (Su) curse - By performing sexual acts with a tramp, it can inflict a curse on its partner that slowly turns it into a tramp (will negates). Keep a running tally starting at 15. Every day that goes by while abstaining from sexual activity subtracts 1 from this value. Every time the creature partakes in a sexual act it adds 3. Each additional sex act performed with tramps instead adds 7. Reaching 0 removes the curse. Reaching 30 transforms the creature into a tramp. Weaknesses/Deep Lore Weakness - Holy 3 Well-Read - By poise breaking a tramp with a hard cover book in hand, you can seal it inside of the book until someone reads it or the book is destroyed. This is why they are often called "tome imps." Certain types of magical books can keep tramps trapped inside indefinitely. With +Planes or +Religion proficiency and 100g of Holy Water or purified salts, you can bless the book to turn it into a prison for the tramp. They can only leave the book by having their name called while the book is open. When they are outside of the book, they cannot interact with the book, except to enter it again. They cannot move more than 60 ft away from the book, getting dragged along with it if it happens to move. They are compelled to follow the orders of whoever summoned them, unless they are obviously harmful, though they are under no compulsion in how they enact their orders, and often use it as an excuse to tempt their masters. A tramp can be freed if the book is destroyed, or by muttering an incantation written in the books sealing magic. Other Traits Ability Scores - STR -2, DEX +1, LUK +3, INT +2, WIS +1, CHA +2 Languages - Common, Infernal, Celestial, Elven Speed - 30 Land Speed Deific Boons - party animal Feats - MM Coax Skills - +3 +Perception, +4 +Stealth, +4 +Local, +4 +Planes, +4 +Profession (dance), +6 +Deception

entries.title: Imp
entries.content: This ornery fiend is quick to change its mood, always looking for guidance from one of its superiors. Once they give the signal, they ready their claws and light themselves ablaze. Level 1 Small Fiend (minion) Defense Traits | AC | 16 | | | ------------------------------------------------- | ------------------------- | -------------------------------- | | HP | 30 | | | Poise | 21 | | | Saves | Fort +1, Refl +5, Will +1 | +4 Toxin, venom | Resistance - Fire, Poison Tactical Retreat - An imp can Disengage as a swift action. Offense Traits (DC 13+2) | Claw (S) | +3 | +2 while ablaze | | -------------------------------------------------- | --------- | ------------------- | | | 2d6+2 (9) | | Multiattack - Make a shove attack against an ally with a +20 bonus. Make a claw attack or cast Blazing Palm (if ablaze). Blaze It fire - While ablaze, an imp gets a +2 bonus to attack rolls and the DCs of its offense traits. They can also innately cast Blazing Palm. They can ignite themselves or douse the fire on themselves as a swift action. Ride and/or Die - An imp can mount a willing ally as a swift action. While mounted, the ally also automatically "benefits" from its Blaze It feature and gets a +10 bonus to its speed. An imp can also mount an enemy by poise breaking them. If the imp is ablaze, so to becomes its new mount. ¿Quires? poison recharge - While under the effects of Blaze It, an imp can expel a large quantity of toxic gas at a creature adjacent to it, dealing 3d6 (10) poison damage and applying sickened 1 (fort halves damage and negates sicken). Camouflage Weaknesses/Deep Lore Weakness - Holy 3 Cowardly - When it falls below 10 hp, an imp runs for its life, begs for mercy or attempts to otherwise evade death at all costs. With a successful intimidation check, you can get an imp as a companion until this ability procs again. Certain circumstances can prevent this ability from proccing. Other Traits Ability Scores - STR +0, DEX +2, LUK +2, INT -1, WIS +0, CHA -1 Languages - Common, Infernal Speed - 40 Land Speed Signature Skill - stalker, dampen presence Feats - Signature Skill Skills - +3 +Perception, +7 +Stealth, +2 +Deception, +2 +Planes

entries.title: Blessing
entries.content: This androgynous fiend wraps itself around an ally before worming its tongue into their mouth and locking lips. You see two more emerge from a pool of blood and crawl towards you, licking their lips. Level 2 Medium Fiend (lewd, monster) Defense Traits | AC | 16 | +2 Tangled | | ------------------------------------------------- | ------------------------- | ------------------------------------ | | HP | 40 | | | Poise | 27 | DR 5 Poise while Tangled | | Saves | Fort +1, Refl +5, Will +1 | +4 Toxin, venom | Offense Traits (DC 14) | Grapple | +11 | +4 MV, +2 charge | | ------------------------------------------------- | --- | ---------------------------------------------- | | | | +Tongue Action, +Share the Love | Innate Techniques - Sprint, Reckless Charge Tongue Action - While grappled by a blessing, a creature is also silenced. Share the Love - Whenever a blessed maintains a grapple or services a creature, they inflict a T3 affliction (will negates). The Master's Blessing - Creature experiences the aches and pains of the world much more vividly as their bones grind against each other and each breath too warm, too cold or too wet. They gain weakness 2 to all damage. During this time they will be visited in the night by The Master. Without some form of outside assistance to protect their mind from dream intruders, they must roll a DC 24 will save or be unable to resist the temptations of this silent dream visitor and partake of her flesh. Interacting with The Master temporarily abates the pain, and the debilitating status of this affliction, for 24 hours. After passing the save, the creature can choose to ignore the master on their own without assistance. But if the creature cannot resist for 3 nights in a row, they are then completely unable to resist at all on their own and begin to rapidly transform into a Blessing over the next 3 days. Cure - Only by meeting with and resisting the temptation of The Master can the affliction begin to fade. Slowly your body acclimates back to your painful existence. After 1 week of standard downtime resting activities, and avoiding all temptations of the flesh, the affliction is removed. A creature cannot receive this affliction more than once. Weaknesses/Deep Lore Weakness - Holy 3 Service - Those blessed by The Master exist to serve. If a creature presents an erogenous zone to the blessed, they will spend their entire turn moving towards that creature and clamor to appease them. Said creature is exposed to the Master's Blessing feature. If the creature rejects the blessing (aka passes the save), then that particular blessed ignores further presentations Other Traits Ability Scores - STR +3, DEX +0, LUK +0, INT -, WIS +1, CHA +0 Senses - Sightless Blindsense (Echolocation, Scent 60 ft) Speed - 40 Land Speed Feats - Maneuver Versatility, Tangled Skills - +5 (+4 +Blindsense) +Perception, +7 +Athletics

entries.title: Hell Hound
entries.content: A red and black dog the size of a man crunches some bones in its mouth. When it realizes you are in the room, it howls to alert its master. Level 2 Medium Fiend/Beast (monster) Defense Traits | AC | 15 | | | ------------------------------------------------- | ------------------------- | --------------------- | | HP | 44 | | | Poise | 27 | DR 2 | | Saves | Fort +7, Refl +4, Will +2 | Stalwart | Resistance - Fire Offense Traits (DC 14) | Bite (P) | +5 | | | -------------------------------------------------- | ---------- | --- | | | 2d8+3 (12) | | Multiattack - Make a melee attack and a drag attack Innate Techniques - Jaws of the Beast, Pack Attack, Noble Sacrifice, Kidnap Pack Tactics - A hellhound gains and grants flanking as long as it threatens the same enemy as an another ally. Howl elusive - As a standard action, a hellhound can howl to have allies converge on its position. They can move 20 feet closer to it as an instant action. A creature can only benefit from the howl speed boost once per turn. Breath Weapon fire - 30 ft cone, 3d6 fire damage and set ablaze (reflex halves and negates ablaze). Weaknesses/Deep Lore Weakness - Holy 3 Give a Dog a Bone - Hellhounds always prefer attacking any kind of boney creatures, such as skeletons. When not already supplied with such, a creature can toss a sizable bone within 30 feet of one, functioning like the Fetch technique. Burning Heart - A hellhound has a red-hot stone at its core. If carefully handled and placed inside of a vessel, such as a piece of jewelry, it enhances fire magic. Such an item increases the CL of fire spells by 1. It must be removed completely from the body and submerged in water for the stone to cool. Extracting the stone requires a DC 17 +Survival check, and you have disadvantage on the check without specialized tools, such as those a blacksmith uses, or fire resistance. Failing shatters the stone, rendering it worthless. Other Traits Ability Scores - STR +3, DEX +2, LUK +0, INT -3, WIS +0, CHA +0 Senses - Scent 10 feet Speed - 40 Land Speed Feats - Butterfly Sting Skills - +4 (+4 +Blindsense) +Perception, +9 +Athletics

entries.title: Pamper
entries.content: A pleasant creature as far as fiends go. These quadrupeds cling to their master and fulfill their every desire. Today master desires your death, so they rush to tear you to pieces. Level 2 Medium Fiend (minion) Defense Traits | AC | 17 | | | ------------------------------------------------- | ------------------------- | --------------- | | HP | 40 | | | Poise | 24 | | | Saves | Fort +5, Refl +5, Will +4 | +4 vs charm | Offense Traits (DC 14) | Bite (P) | +5 | +Master | | -------------------------------------------------- | ---------- | ------- | | | 2d6+3 (10) | | | Claw (S) | +5 | +Master | | | 2d6+3 (10) | | Multiattack - Make 2 melee attacks. If currently taunted by master's killer, make 3. Innate Techniques - Bestial Claw, Kidnap 1/day - Pack Attack, Sanctuary Master - A pamper grows attached to creatures, preferring fiends or fiendish creatures, particularly the charismatic ones. They can give themselves the companion tag, receiving all the benefits. They get a +8 bonus to +Perception and +Survival checks to locate or track their master. If enough time passes without seeing their master, or they find their master dead, they start a quest to find a new master. Protect elusive - When master takes damage, spend an immediate action to dash to their side at lightning speed. Move 50 ft closer to master and make an AoO against the attacking creature along the way. Special Attack: Pounce - By taking a jump action before attacking, roll a grapple attack with advantage. If the pamper succeeds, the creature is also knocked down as long as they are grappled. Weaknesses/Deep Lore Weakness - Holy 3 Pet Pal - Pampers tend to act more like animals than they do intelligent creatures. While they might not be the brightest, they still possess the ability to reason and speak. They only speak unless they absolutely have to, preferring nonverbal vocalizations. They will also speak if they believe they are alone with their master. They can roll +Disguise to appear as an unusual animal, though any creature with +Planes proficiency automatically sees through this ruse. Vengeance - If their master is defeated or slain in the presence of the pamper, the pamper is taunted by the creature that killed/defeated their master. This taunt does not end until the pamper is slain, the target is slain, their master is healed or the pamper is otherwise unable to detect/attack the target. If they evade vengeance, the pamper gains the +8 bonus to perception/survival usually reserved for hunting down their master against this killer for 1 week. Other Traits Ability Scores - STR +1, DEX +3, LUK +0, INT -1, WIS +2, CHA +0 Languages - Common, Infernal Senses - Scent (10 ft) Improved Leap - Can jump as high as its long jump (20 ft) Signature Skill - quick scan, one eye open, advanced tracking Speed - 50 Land Speed, 50 Climb, 50 swim Feats - Signature Skill x2 Skills - +6 (+4 +Blindsense, +8 master) +Perception, +6 (+2 track, +8 master) +Perception, +7 +Stealth, +7 +Acrobatics, +4 +Disguise

entries.title: Meathook
entries.content: This fiery fiend has chains that clatter as it swings itself across the battlefield. It laughs and squeals with joy as it slaps you and your allies around and digs its spiked jaw into your face. Level 3 Medium Fiend (minion) Defense Traits | AC | 17 | | | ------------------------------------------------- | ------------------------- | --- | | HP | 50 | | | Poise | 28 | | | Saves | Fort +4, Refl +8, Will +3 | | Resistance - Fire Fired Up - A meathook takes no penalty to AC while ablaze Offense Traits (DC 15) | Chains (C+Fire) | +6 | 10 ft reach, +4 drag | | --------------------------------------------------------- | ------------------- | -------------------------------- | | | d10+3+d10 fire (14) | | | Gore (P) | +6 | +Master | | | 2d8+3 (12) | +d6 bleed, +Embed? | Multiattack - Make a drag attack and another melee attack Innate Techniques - Headbutt, Boiling Blood Constrict - D8 fire Embed - Immediately after dragging a creature, if the meathook uses a gore attack, the creature and the meathook are entangled on hit. While entangled in this way, both the meathook and the attached creature are blinded to everybody but each other as they are stuck face to face. If the creature separates themselves, they immediately take bleed damage again. The bleed cannot be healed while the creature remains embedded. Embedded creatures are automatically crit by Concealed Carry. Special Attack: Concealed Carry - The meathook has the ability to fire grapeshot projectiles from its mouth. Functions as a blunderbuss attack (2d6+3, 15 ft cone). Because of the subtle nature of the attack, creatures are DD unless they succeed on a DC 16 +Perception check. Infernal Chains - A meathook can increase the range of her chains to 30 ft during her turn. When she does so, they become ranged attacks. On-hit she can drag herself to a creature elusive. Weaknesses/Deep Lore Washed Up - When wet or frosty, a meathooks chain attacks deal no fire damage (including with its constrict). At the end of a meathooks turn, they lose the wet or frosty condition. Other Traits Ability Scores - STR +1, DEX +3, LUK +2, INT +0, WIS +0, CHA +0 Languages - Common, Infernal Chain Swing - A meathook can also attach their chains to terrain, essentially allowing her to fly in any terrain with suitable places to wrap her chains around. She must still end her turns clinging to a wall or standing on solid ground. Speed - 40 Land Speed Feats - Blind Fight, Point Blank Master Skills - +5 +Perception, +10 +Acrobatics, +5 +Planes, +8 +Sleight of Hand

entries.title: Twinkubus
entries.content: An androgynous creature with tiny horns lies in bed with some brutish looking men. It gives the order to take care of you and they do so with great pleasure, an unnatural fervor to their compliance. Level 3 Medium Fiend (mastermind, lewd) Defense Traits | AC | 18 | | | ------------------------------------------------- | ------------------------- | --- | | HP | 38 | | | Poise | 22 | | | Saves | Fort +4, Refl +4, Will +6 | | Enigma - Advantage on saves against effects that read your thoughts or otherwise pry into your mind. Offense Traits (DC 15) | Slap (C) | +4 | | | -------------------------------------------------- | -------- | ---------- | | | d6+1 (4) | +Charm | Innate Techniques (CL 7) - Charming Gaze, Feign Defeat, Stage Fright 1/day - Command, Tongue Twister, Power Overwhelming Constant - Tongues Power Bottom - During sexual acts, a twinkubus can use the technique Order 66, but (will negates). If the twinkubus summoned the creature, he can spend a swift action to activate order 66 instead of a standard action. Flirty Feint - While in combat, a twinkubus can feint a creature as a swift action and impose 1 of the following penalties. * Impose Disadvantage on will saves against the twinkubus' abilities this turn * The creature is DD to ALL creatures until the start of the twinkubus' next turn Summon Ally - 2 of any LvL 2 or lower fiend. Can also summon a single creature under the influence of their Power Bottom ability. If the creature is a higher level than the twinkubus, they are summoned with half their max +HP. Any creature summoned automatically gets the Take the Hit feat. Weaknesses/Deep Lore Tucking Technique - A twinkubus is androgynous and can pretend to be female, even during sexual encounters (DC 26 +Perception to notice). After such an encounter, they can reveal their true gender destroying the psyche of heterosexual males and homosexual females. This inflicts a unique T2 Affliction. Shattered Sexuality - You are paranoid of all interactions, thinking about what kind of hardware the creatures you interact with has under the hood. Any and all opposed skill checks you make with disadvantage. If a creature flirts with you, attempts to seduce you or even so much as compliments you, you become drained 1. You can only suffer this drain 1/minute. Cure - Enter a relationship, or continue one, spending 1 week with your partner(s), culminating in intercourse, reaffirming your tastes. Profane Loan - After a sexual encounter with a twinkubus, you temporarily get a +1 bonus to all d20 rolls for 24 hours. After this buff runs out, you acquire a unique T2 affliction. Touch Starvation - Take a -1 penalty to all d20 rolls and have disadvantage on saves vs a twinkubus' abilities. Cure - Have intercourse with a twinkubus, or deprive yourself of intimate contact for 1 week. Other Traits Ability Scores - STR +1, DEX +1, LUK +1, INT +3, WIS +1, CHA +3 Languages - Common, Infernal, Tongues Telepathy Almost Humanoid - A twinkubus only has the smallest of details that it is a demon, such as small horns and a spaded tail. It can disguise itself as a hellion without any adjustments, or get rid of these minor details to appear human. In either case, creatures proficient in planes, or that initiate sexual contact with a twinkubus can roll a DC 28 perception check to see through their ruse. Psuedo-Intellectualism - A twinkubus can roll +Deception in place of any knowledge skill to roll monster lore. This does not actually tell them anything, but rather convinces another creature of something using "facts" and "logic." Deific Boons - party animal, mistrust Signature Skill - inception Speed - 30 Land Speed Feats - Skill Focus, Signature Skill, Take the Hit Skills - +6 +Perception, +6 +Acrobatics, +8 +Planes, +8 +Local, +6 +Sleight of Hand, +12 (adv) +Deception, +12 (adv) +Persuasion

entries.title: Dust Devil
entries.content: A tempest of dust makes its way towards you, the shadowy demonic figure within commanding and riding the winds. Level 4 Medium Fiend (Monster) Defense Traits | AC | 15 | | | ------------------------------------------------- | ------------------------- | --- | | HP | 68 | | | Poise | 29 | | | Saves | Fort +8, Refl +7, Will +5 | | Resistance - +4 Air Amorphous - +15 Offense Traits (DC 16) | Claws (S) | +7 | +4 MV | | -------------------------------------------------------- | ---------- | ------------------------------- | | | 2d8+3 (12) | | | Airbending (C) | +7 | 30 ft, +2 BE | | | 3d8+3 (16) | | Multiattack - Make 2 claw attacks. Innate Techniques - Kidnap, Pocket Sand 1/week - Wasteland Dust Storm Aura Air Recharge - Requires a standard action to activate, lasting 1 minute. 20 ft radius. Blinds all non-Fiends for 1 turn (fort reduces to blurred vision). Recharges after 10 minutes. Choking Debris (recharge) (air) - Target a creature within a Dust Storm Aura. Exhale a large quantity of dust in their direction and force it down their throat. They take 2d6 (7) slashing damage and are sickened 1 immediately (fort negates). At the end of their next 3 turns they take this damage and are sickened 1 again, then make a new save to end the effect. Special Attack: Vacuum Wave (air) - Target a creature within 30 feet with a drag attack. If successful, drag them adjacent and deal claw damage. Weaknesses/Deep Lore Dry Spell - If a dust devil takes damage from or fails a save against a water effect, it is also sickened 1. Submerging it in water or exposing it to many gallons has the same effect. Weakness - 3 Holy Other Traits Ability Scores - STR +3, DEX +2, LUK +0, INT -3, WIS +0, CHA +0 Languages - Understands, but cannot speak, infernal Speed - 30 Land Speed Airbending - As the Airbending Style of the same name Feats - Maneuver Versatility, Bullseye Skills - +6 +Perception

entries.title: Hell Knight
entries.content: You hear a loud crashing sound coming from the church only to find two hulking fiends wrestling. When they slam into one another, they release a powerful shockwave that hurts your ears. Level 5 Large Fiend (Monster) Defense Traits | AC | 17 | | | ------------------------------------------------- | -------------------------- | --- | | HP | 80 | | | Poise | 35 | | | Saves | Fort +10, Refl +6, Will +5 | | Resistance - Poison, Sonic My Zone - Hell knights have resistance to any damage that originates from a creature that is not within their natural reach. Offense Traits (DC 17) | Claws (S) | +8 | +8 Shove, +4 MV | | -------------------------------------------------------- | ---------- | --------------------------------------------- | | | 2d8+3 (12) | | Multiattack - Make 2 claw attacks. Innate Techniques - Sonic Boom, Thunder Stomp, Training Arc, Awesome Blow 1/day - Fracture Constant - Shockwave Rend - 2 claws, 2d6 (7) sonic. Plow Through - Creatures shoved by a Hell Knight are pushed 30 feet instead of 20. If they impact a solid surface, they take 2d6+5 (12) crush damage. Shoving a creature into another creature does this damage to both. These numbers already include the Hell Knight's Linebacker feat. Explosive Entry - In place of making attacks at the end of a Core Rules, How to Play/Actions/Common Actions/Full Round Actions/Charge, when it spends its action to break a barrier or when it willingly takes fall damage (still a standard action), the hell knights slam erupts in concussive force. Creatures within reach take 6d6 (21) sonic damage and are staggered for 1 round (fort halves damage and negates stagger). Weaknesses/Deep Lore Bloodlust - Once fighting begins, a hell knight must use its standard action to attack or otherwise attempt to inflict damage/CC. It takes 3 psychic damage every turn it does not. Weakness - 3 Holy Other Traits Ability Scores - STR +3, DEX +1, LUK +0, INT -3, WIS +0, CHA +0 Languages - Understands, but cannot speak, infernal Improved Leap - Can jump as high as its long jump (20 ft) Speed - 40 Land Speed Smashing - 1/turn as a free action, roll an +Athletics check for Brute Force. Feats - Freedom of Movement, Diehard, Maneuver Versatility, Linebacker Skills - +7 +Perception, +18 +Athletics, +10 +Intimidation

entries.title: Sudokuu
entries.content: A crazy demon laughs as it rides into battle on a unicycle, exploding out of a building every so often, blazing a trail wherever it goes. Level 5 Medium Fiend (Anomaly) Defense Traits | AC | 17 | | | ------------------------------------------------- | ------------------------- | ---------------------------- | | HP | 90 | Regeneration 5 (wet) | | Poise | 28 | | | Saves | Fort +7, Refl +7, Will +5 | | Resistance - Fire Offense Traits (DC 17+4) | Firebending (Fire) | +9 | +8 Shove, +4 MV | | ------------------------------------------------------------ | ---------- | --------------------------------------------- | | | 2d8+3 (12) | | | Claw (S) | +7 | | | | 2d6+2 (9) | | Innate Techniques (CL 8) - Fire Blast Honorable Sudoku - If a sudoku defeats itself with its fireblast technique, all saves made against it have disadvantage. It never gets a save against its own fireblast, but also can't be knocked down from it. Fireblast's DC is 4 higher when it includes itself in the blast (already including the bonus from its Callous Casting feat). Return the Favor - Creatures that make AoOs against a sudoku provoke AoOs from it. Weaknesses/Deep Lore Rise from Ashes - It is a sudokus eternal torment that they must die again and again. After a sudoku is defeated it turns into a pile of smoldering ash. The ash stays warm for 24 hours, then the sudoku rises again, immediately exploding in a fireblast. If it is inside of a container too small for it to fit, it bursts out. Only by dousing the ashes in holy water can it be permanently slain. Wet Blanket - While wet, a sudoku's fireblast DC is decreased by 2 and its regeneration is disabled. While mostly submerged in water, it cannot use fireblast or fire bending. KYS NOW - With a DC 25 +Persuasion check as a standard action, a creature can convince a sudoku to end it all immediately. Sudoku prefer to perform some kind of dramatic and explosive finish, but if no such option is available, they jump off of cliffs, snap their necks etc, immediately killing them. Weakness - 3 holy, 3 frost Other Traits Ability Scores - STR +0, DEX +2, LUK +3, INT +2, WIS -1, CHA -2 Languages - Infernal, common Unicycle - A sudoku's preferred method of transportation is a unicycle with a spiked wheel. It can travel at a speed of 50 while riding on it. If poise broken, it falls off of it in addition to any other effects and must spend a swift action to remount. Speed - 30 Land Speed Feats - Callous Casting, Toughness, Matador Skills - +6 +Perception, +9 arcana, +9 +Acrobatics

entries.title: The Flexicutioner
entries.content: An enormous fiendish woman sits on her throne of bones, holding a man in her bloody claws. She crushes him into a pulp and her demonic minions cheer, then sighs and gestures for you to approach next. Level 5 Huge Fiend Unique Boss (Anomaly) Defense Traits | AC | 16 | | | ------------------------------------------------- | -------------------------- | ----------------------------------------------------- | | HP | 160 | | | Poise | 40 | DR 5 (+5 PP), DR 10 vs disarm | | Saves | Fort +11, Refl +5, Will +8 | | Resistance - Sonic Recovery Offense Traits (DC 18) | 1h Axe (S) | +11 | thrown, One and Done, +4 MV, +4 ALL combat maneuvers | | ----------------------------------------------------------- | ---------- | ------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------ | | | d10+4 (9) | | | Tail (C+P) | +9 | +4 MV, +4 ALL combat maneuvers | | | 2d6+4 (9) | | | Heavy Object (C?) | +9 | Thrown | | | 2d8+4 (13) | | Multiattack - Make a Melee Attack and a combat maneuver Innate Techniques (CL 4) - Foe Toss, Colossal Smash, Charming Gaze Flexicution - Fang reaches down and crushes a creature with a free hand. This forces the creature within reach to roll an opposed brute force check. If they win, she provokes an AoO. If she wins, the creature is crushed, dealing 4d6 (14) crush damage and inflicting drained 7. If this defeats the creature, she can immediately foe toss them. Breath Weapon fire recharge - Fang unlocks her jaw and unleashes a torrent of concentrated flames in a 60 ft line. Affected creatures take 6d6 (21) fire damage and are set ablaze (reflex halves dmg and negates ablaze). Rift Slasher concentration persistent recharge necrotic - Fang slices the ground with her axe and temporarily opens up a rift to The Underworld. A 15 ft line in front of her cracks open and becomes inaccessible, shunting all creatures in the AoE to the side. Damned souls reach for any creatures within 15 feet to pull them into the rift. They roll a +13 drag attempt immediately against all creatures within 15 feet, and passively when a creature walks into range. All poise broken creatures are partially dragged into the rift before getting ejected, taking 6d6 (21) necrotic damage. The rift persists for 1 minute and has a 1 minute cooldown. Special Attack - execution Summon Ally - 2 Pampers Boss Actions - Actions are usable 1/turn as an instant action with specific triggers. Mwah - When a creature ends their turn within reach, cast charming gaze Out of My Way - At the end of a creatures turn, roll +Athletics instead of +Acrobatics as though trying to avoid AoO. If Fang succeeds, she tosses the creature aside 10 feet and they are knocked down for 1 round. Cut in Half - At the end of Fang's turn, she holds her axe over her shoulder and turns her body. At the start of her next turn, she attacks all creatures within her reach with a +4 bonus. Weaknesses/Deep Lore Choice Attire - Whenever Fang is subject to an effect with the air tag, she always spends her immediate action to keep herself covered up. If she is unable to, she is shaken 1. Flattery - By rolling a DC 20 +Persuasion or +Deception check as a standard action, a creature can fluster fang, causing her to be charmed by them. Succeeding on a second DC 25 check gives her the time out condition, provided there are no obvious signs of continued aggression or betrayal. Any Fiends of her Level or lower automatically stop fighting at her request. Higher level fiends require persuasion from her. Hey Mom - By expending prayer, Fang can communicate directly with her mother, Caldera, the Cataclysm, as though upcasting sending. Other Traits Ability Scores - STR +4, DEX +0, LUK +0, INT -1, WIS +3, CHA +1 Languages - Infernal, common Speed - 30 Land Speed Deific Boons - huge, weight for it, perfectly huge Feats - Bonded Weapon, Maneuver Versatility, Maneuver Master, Power Pose Skills - +10 +Perception, +18 (adv, +2 huge) +Athletics, +8 +Persuasion

entries.title: Demon Prince(ss)
entries.content: A well-dressed fiend carries itself with a great sense of importance as it struts across the battlefield. With a bow it introduces itself to you, and then invites its friends to join. Level 6 Medium Fiend (Mastermind) Defense Traits | AC | 19 | | | ------------------------------------------------- | -------------------------- | --- | | HP | 56 | | | Poise | 25 | | | Saves | Fort +7, Refl +8, Will +10 | | Resistance - Fire Double Team Recharge Sp - A prince can use Stunt Double as a swift action. Usable 1/minute. Offense Traits (DC 18) | Claws (S) | +8 | | --------------------------------------------------- | ---------- | | | 2d8+2 (11) | Innate Techniques (CL 10) - Dance With Me!, Venomous Promise, The Hand that Feeds, Spell Blade 1/day - Fire and Brimstone, Hellfire, Backfire, Mirror Images Now its a Party! Companion Summon Ally - When a prince uses its Fire and Brimstone technique, he can also bring forth to one allied fiend (LvL 4 or lower) from the The Demon Realm to fight by his side while he concentrates on it. He can extend the duration by 1 round or change the direction of the technique as a standard action. Each round at the start of the prince's turn if he is still concentrating on the spell, then he can summon another fiend. Weaknesses/Deep Lore How Dare You! - By spitting out an insult at a prince (swift action) you can taunt him for 1 round. Subsequent attempts to do so require an appropriate skill check, opposed by the prince's will save. Weakness - 3 Holy Other Traits Ability Scores - STR +1, DEX +2, LUK +0, INT +2, WIS +2, CHA +3 Languages - Infernal, Common, Elven, Celestial Deific Boons - party animal, mistrust Feats - Skill Focus, Feint Partner, Escape Route Skills - +10 +Perception, +10 (adv) +Insight, +13 (adv) +Deception, +10 +Planes, +10 +Local, +10 +Stealth, +10 +Acrobatics, +10 +Linguistics, +13 +Profession (dance, sing, harp, guitar)

entries.title: Vrock
entries.content: A sinister looking bird of roughly humanoid shape sheds spores as it scratches its feathers with its claws. As it picks itself with its beak, it notices you and lets out a unholy sound that sounds like a mix of hundreds screaming. Level 6 Large Fiend (Minion) Defense Traits | AC | 21 | | | ------------------------------------------------- | -------------------------- | --- | | HP | 68 | | | Poise | 27 | | | Saves | Fort +7, Refl +9, Will +10 | | Resistance - Zap, Sonic Offense Traits (DC 18) | Talon (S) | +9 | Only usable while flying, +4 drag, +4 MV | | --------------------------------------------------- | ---------- | ------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | | | 2d8+3 (12) | | | Peck (P) | +9 | +4 MV | | | 2d8+3 (12) | | Multiattack - Make 2 talon attacks. Innate Techniques (CL 7) - Finger Gun, Rend Vision 1/day - Most Annoying Sound, Garrote Constant - Lightning Rod Static Charge Zap - Vrocks can dance as a standard action in order to build up 1 stack of static electricity. When multiple rocks are in the same combat, they all share the same static counter. This visibly manifests itself as crackling electricity that jumps from vrock to vrock, as though they were adept Firebenders. At any point a vrock can spend 1 point of this static to cast Lightning Bolt. By spending an additional point of static they increase the DC and CL by 4. By spending more static charges, they can keep increasing these numbers infinitely. Static can only be charged in combat and not while a vrock Hides. Scream Scavenger Sonic Mind-Affecting Recharge - A vrock can make their scream sound like any creature they have eaten. As a standard action, they can scream with all of their voices at once. Non-Fiends within 20 ft of the vrock take 2d6 (7) sonic damage and are dazed (will negates daze). A vrock must eat another creature with a voice to recharge this ability. Spread the Rot - When a creature makes a Melee Attack against a vrock, it releases a puff of rotten feathers littered with insects and fungus in their face. The creature becomes Sickened 1 (fort negates). This effect can only trigger once each turn, regardless of how many attacks the creature makes. At the end of the creatures turn it is sickened by this ability, it becomes Drained equal to its sicken penalty. As a standard action, a vrock an affect all creatures within its natural reach with this ability. Weaknesses/Deep Lore Take a Bath - If a vrock is made Wet, then it becomes Sickened 1. If it is somehow thoroughly cleaned, they become sickened 2. While sickened in this way, a vrock cannot Spread the Rot. Weakness - 6 holy Other Traits Ability Scores - STR +1, DEX +3, LUK +0, INT +1, WIS +2, CHA +0 Languages - Infernal, Common, Celestial Senses - Nightvision, Scent (10 ft, 300 ft carrion) Deific Boons - from my cold dead head Speed - 30, 45 Fly Feats - Maneuver Versatility, Sniper, Maneuver Master Skills - +10 +Perception, +11 +Acrobatics, +11 +Stealth +9 +Cryptozoology, +9 +Athletics !366

entries.title: Dreadlord
entries.content: A humanoid shaped fiend with horns, wings and intricate armor carries itself with a degree of confidence. As it looms over you, you feel all hope fade. Level 8 Large Fiend (Anomaly) Defense Traits | AC | 23 | | | ------------------------------------------------- | -------------------------- | ------- | | HP | 116 | Regen 5 | | Poise | 36 | | | Saves | Fort +13, Refl +8, Will +8 | | Resistance - Zap Regeneration - Does not function while a creature exists in its Aura of Dread Offense Traits (DC 20+2 Callous) | Claw (S) | +11 | | -------------------------------------------------- | ----- | | | 2d6+3 | Innate Techniques (CL 12+1 force) - Dispel Magic, Drain Soul 1/day - Great Gavel, Storm Step, Rushing Winds Constant - Heavens' Vengeance Aura of Dread Aura - Creatures within the natural reach of a dreadlord (other than dreadlords) take a -2 penalty to saving throws. Force Punch Sp Force - As a standard action, a dreadlord can make a melee Spell Attack. It deals 5d8+13 (35) force damage. A creature is pushed a distance away from the dreadlord equal to the damage dealt by the attack (rounded down to nearest 5). Weaknesses/Deep Lore Square Up - The first time a creature willingly abandons its weapons while within the Aura of Dread, the dreadlord will spend their next turn attacking them, unless they are already taunted. Weakness - 5 holy Other Traits Ability Scores - STR +3, DEX +0, LUK +1, INT +3, WIS +0, CHA -1 Languages - All common/racial languages, Morse Code, Necril Gear - Elemental Staff (force), Medium Armor Senses - Nightvision, Scent (10 ft, 300 ft carrion) Speed - 30, 30 Fly Feats - MM Bouncing Spell, MM Sniping, Callous Casting, Ghostbuster Skills - +10 +Perception, +13 +Athletics, +13 Arcana, +13 +Planes, +13 +Religion, +13 +Linguistics ![{3CF5222C-5E05-4E7D-8D8F-A09660B28792}.png|Despicable Dreadlord, by Konstantin Turovec for [Hearthstone]]

entries.title: Succubus
entries.content: A feminine fiend with curves in all the right places snaps her whip-like tail at friend and foe alike, all thankful for such a thoughtful blessing. Level 9 Medium Fiend (Mastermind) Defense Traits | AC | 23 | | | ------------------------------------------------- | --------------------------- | ------------ | | HP | 74 | | | Poise | 27 | | | Saves | Fort +9, Refl +12, Will +13 | +4 Charm | Sweet Essence Healing - Whenever a succubus inflicts drain or Horny, they heal 5 hp. Offense Traits (DC 21) | Tail Whip (S) | +12 | (+10 reach during turn, +8 grapple, +2 drag, +2 topple) | | ------------------------------------------------------- | ---------- | ----------------------------------------------------------------------- | | | d6+3 (6) | +1 Horny stack | | Stomp (C) | +12 | +12 (only usable against Knocked Down targets) | | | 3d8+3 (16) | +1 Horny stack | Multiattack - Make 2 Whip attacks. One against an ally and one against an enemy. Innate Techniques (CL 12) - Unnatural Lust, Detect Thots, Detect Thoughts, Charming Gaze, Command, Teleblock 1/day - Domination, Feign Defeat, Order 66, The Hand that Feeds Constant - Sympathy, Sense Motives Horny Jail Lewd - On-hit for its attacks, whenever a creature fails a save against one of its spells or when a creature is Charmed by a succubus, it gains a stack of Horny. Horny stacks intensify physical sensation, functioning as Weakness equal to horny value. The succubus' DC increases by a value equal to the horny stacks on a particular creature. Upon becoming horny 3, a creature becomes Staggered for 1 round. Upon becoming horny 6, the creature gets put in Time Out. These stacks disappear once every succubus is defeated. Constrict - Drain 6 Kiss Su - As part of maintaining a Grapple, or as a melee attack, a succubus can kiss an enemy to make them Charmed for 1 round and add one stack of Horny. Sudden Betrayal- The succubus can use +Disguise in place of +Stealth to Surprise, or if the combat has already started, to inflict DD on the turn of the reveal. Only functions once per combat, and only if the succubus started the combat while using a Polymorph effect. Whipped Into Shape - A succubus' tail functions as a Whip. A succubus can use this whip against allies that are a lower LvL than it. Instead of the usual effects, it allows them to count as the succubus' Companion and grant the creature their teamwork feats. This companionship lasts until they whip another creature while they already have 2 companions. They can AoO allies that move within their natural reach. Weaknesses/Deep Lore Rejection Vulnerability - Whenever a succubus fails a CHA Ability Check or Skill Check, they take 1 psychic damage. A creature can automatically do 1+CHA damage to the succubus by insulting their appearance, taste in hats etc (standard). Weakness - 3 holy Other Traits Ability Scores - STR +0, DEX +3, LUK +2, INT +2, WIS +2, CHA +4 Languages - Telepathy, Infernal, Common, Celestial Polymorph (Su) poly, true - A succubus can match the appearance of any playable race. Transforming into a creature is a standard action, but returning to its true form is a free action. Senses - Nightvision Tail - The succubus has a prehensile tail that can finely manipulate objects up to 15 ft away. Profane Gift (Su) Scrying - As part of an intimate encounter. You can confer a gift of +1 to an ability score (max +3) on up to 3 willing creatures. Gifted creatures also receive other effects. - You can telepathically communicate with these creatures over any distance. - They are permanently charmed by you, which cannot be removed without first removing the gift. - You can scry these creatures. - You form a Summoning Contract with these creatures. You can summon one as a standard action. This portion of the ability has the companion tag. Deific Boons - party animal, mistrust, devil's in the details Speed - 30 Feats - Spell Ambush, Feint Partner, Gang Up, Skill Focus, Perfect Positioning, Take the Hit Skills - +13 +Perception, +17 +Insight, +20 (adv) +Disguise, +20 +Persuasion, +20 (adv) +Deception, +14 +Local, +14 +Planes, +14 +History, +14 Arcana !Art by me!|511

entries.title: README
entries.content: Slur'Nesh Level 10 Medium Fiend \[unique] \[boss] \[mastermind] Defense Traits AC 25\ HP 80\ Poise 24\ Fort +10, Refl +10, Will +15 Resistance - +4 vs mind-affecting Offense Traits (DC 22) Touch +10\ Charms indefinitely. If in combat, reduces to 1 round. Innate Techniques - Corpse Transposition, Darting Duplicate, Overstimulate, Tongue Twister\ 1/day - Nightmare, Domination, Order 66 Power Word N (Su) \[mind affecting] \[language] \[recharge] \[subtle] - Slur'Nesh can utter a heinous word, and have it echo from the mouth of a creature within 60 feet. This word carries such vulgarity that it causes another creature within 30 ft of the original target to fly into a blind and senseless rage at the original target. They are taunted by the creature for 3 rounds, and use their deadliest most powerful abilities to kill them as quickly as possible. If the creature is a minion to Slur'Nesh, or if the secondary target has been subjugated by the race/people of the primary target, this has no save. If they are not they have disadvantage but (will negates). If the creatures have been allies for an extended amount of time, are family etc, then they do not make the save at disadvantage. Essentially, target 1 creature to taunt another with the power of racism. The two creatures relationship and races affect how difficult it is to resist. Summon Ally \[companion] - 1 Living Forge Cycle of Hate -  Weaknesses/Deep Lore Superior Polymorph - Any successful checks to transform Slur'Nesh back into its true form instead deals 20 true damage to it. True Form - Slur'Nesh avoids its true form at all costs. If it drops to 0 hp, all remaining damage is ignored and it transforms into its true form. It cannot use its polymorph ability again until it rests. It cannot use any abilities listed in this form while in its true forms, and gets a new set of abilities and stats. Other Traits Ability Scores - Str +0, Dex +0, Lucc +2, Int +3, Wis +3, Cha +3 Hierarchy - When Slur'Nesh is in a settlement of intelligent creatures, it can intuitively tell certain aspects of the social hierarchy. It knows which types of people are subjugated, as well as who does the subjugation, and can generally tell how extreme said subjugation is. If the settlement is generally egalitarian, then it can intuit 3 specific creatures who break this mold. Shape Change \[poly, true] - Any humanoid, including any racial features. Can be used as a free action 1/turn. Rumormonger - Slur'Nesh can attempt to spread a rumor throughout a small town or larger settlement by attempting a deception check. The difficulty depends on the size, ranging from 10 for a small town to 30 for a metropolis, though this difficulty may be modified if the population as a whole tends towards skepticism or insanity. It takes a week for a rumor to spread. If it succeeds by 5 or more, the time is reduced to 3 days. If it fails by 5 or more, the rumor mutates into a competing theory. Can only have 1 rumor brewing at once. Feats - SF Deception, SS Deception, SF Insight, SS Insight Skills - +15 perception, +19 (adv) insight, +19 (adv) deception, +19 disguise, +15 local, +15 planes, +15 history, +15 linguistics Envy from Fullmetal Alchemist by Jinwu

entries.title: Balrogue
entries.content: You call for your squire only to realize he is nowhere to be seen. One by one your allies seem to disappear until you are the only one that remains. The last thing you remember is a fiery whip wrapping around you as you are dragged into the shadows. Level 11 Medium Fiend (Mastermind) Defense Traits | AC | 25 | | | ------------------------------------------------- | ---------------------------- | --- | | HP | 108 | | | Poise | 32 | | | Saves | Fort +12, Refl +17, Will +14 | | Resistance - Fire Evasion Offense Traits (DC 23) | Whip (fire) | +15 | (+8 drag, +2 topple, +4 MV) | | ----------------------------------------------------- | -------- | ------------------------------------------------------------- | | | d6+4 (7) | +Last Whisper | Multiattack - Make a Whip attack and a Drag attack Innate Techniques (CL 14) - Achilles Heel, Assassinate, Bullet Time, Kidnap, Twist the Knife (U5), Dark Lure, Living Shadow, Invisibility, Dead Silent 1/day - Alpha Strike, Adrenaline Burst, Illusory Wall, Mirage Cunning Action - A balrogue has an additional standard action that it can use to do anything that is not an attack, casting a spell or using a Su ability, unless it is to do the following... - Into the Shadows - A balrogue can drag a creature up to 60 ft, depositing them anywhere within Reach of their whip. Last Whisper - Against a DD target, a balrogue's whip attacks silence for 1 round On-hit or whenever the it successfully drags a creature. Signature Skill - stalker, dampen presence Weaknesses/Deep Lore Weakness - 5 holy Other Traits Ability Scores - STR +1, DEX +4, LUK +2, INT +3, Wis +3, Cha +0 Languages - All non-secret Speed - 50 Equipment - Whip (with elemental (fire) magic weapon property) Darkvision Fade Away - A balrogue can use Blur as an innate technique if it has already used all of its other actions. A balrogue can take 10 on stealth, resulting in a 34 for most balrogues. Deific Boons - from my cold dead head, born in darkness, shadow blending Feats - Sniper, Fleet Footwork, Signature Skill, Maneuver Versatility Skills - +19 (+4 Invisibility, adv) +Stealth, +17 to all other non-+Knowledge skills. +17 +Local, +17 +Planes

entries.title: Chained Oblivion
entries.content: A sigil of a name in infernal floats in the air. The burning text intensifies as you approach before it unleashes an enormous fiend with a hankering for destruction. Level 11 Huge Fiend (Anomaly) Defense Traits | AC | 27 (heavy armor) | | | ------------------------------------------------- | ---------------------------- | ----------------------- | | HP | 130 | | | Poise | 35 | DRA 1 | | Saves | Fort +15, Refl +11, Will +15 | +4 Charm/possession | Resistance - Psychic Iron Will Offense Traits (DC 23) Claws (S) +15 (+4 MV) 2d8+4 (13) +Drain Life Multiattack - Make 2 claw attacks. Innate Techniques - Disintegration, Pulverize, Unga Bunga, Awesome Blow 1/day - Elemental Shield, Roar of the Cataclysm, Shackles of Pain, Vile Vigor, Asphyxiate Constant - Long Arms Awaken Fire Necrotic - When the Chained Oblivion are awakened, they explode violently. All creatures within 60 ft take 5d6 (17) fire damage and 5d6 (17) necrotic damage (reflex halves). Drain Life - The chained oblivion's claw attacks drain a creatures life. On-hit the creature is Drained 2. Convergence (fire) (necrotic) Su - As a swift action, the chained oblivion can set in motion a process that will all but guarantee the destruction of their enemies. The chained oblivion chooses 2 unoccupied points within 60 ft on the ground within 60 ft of each other. In one point a fiery orb appears. In the other, one covered in bone. At the start of each the chained oblivion's turn, the orbs move 10 ft closer to each other, dealing 5d6 (17) fire or necrotic damage (depending on which orb) to any creature they bump into. When the orbs are adjacent to each other, they fuse and begin to fly around the battlefield. Each turn the orbs move in a line 40 feet, dealing 5d6 (17) fire damage and 5d6 (17) necrotic damage (reflex halves) to any creature they pass through. This effect persists until the chained oblivion is defeated. With an +Athletics check, a Dispel effect or any other reasonable effect to prevent the movement of these orbs, their convergence can be halted for one round. This delaying effect happens instead of being completely removed by dispel or completely destroyed through athletics. When the chained oblivion is defeated, the orbs disappear. Signature Skill - Boo (SS), Big Shot (SS) Weaknesses/Deep Lore Infernal Seal - The Chained Oblivion are a set of fiends that were too powerful to control and too numerous to rule without endless fighting between factions. As such, the Demon Lords decided to seal them away for later use. Powerful creatures that serve the interests of demon lords can summon these creatures in their dormant state, where they appear in some type of magical seal. This sealed form varies from case to case, but is usually large and unwieldy, weighing several tons and is big enough to contained the chained oblivion. This seal can be broken away with a specific password (which can gleaned through a DC 40 +Planes check), the destruction of a particular object used as part of the seal, or a DC 40 Brute Force check. Even when the seal is broken, it still takes 2 full rounds (about 12 seconds) for the creature to awaken, during which it is in Stasis. At the end of the second round, they unleash a devastating burst, activating their Awaken ability and ending their turn. With a +Planes check, you can slow or halt this process as a standard action while adjacent to the seal. A result of 30 or higher slows the process by 1 round and a result of 40 stops it completely and prevents its reactivation for 1 minute. Defeat - The chained oblivion cannot die from being Defeated. While defeated, they are Invincible. After 1 minute of being defeated, they return back to being in stasis, as per the Infernal Seal feature. During this minute, creatures can determine what the methods are for unsealing the creature again. Other Traits Ability Scores - STR +4, DEX +0, LUK +0, INT +0, Wis +2, Cha -1 Languages - Infernal, Common Equipment - Heavy Armor Deific Boons - Huge, Huge XL Feats - Maneuver Versatility, Divine Interference, Signature Skill, Fearsome Finisher, Skill Focus, Demolitionist Skills - +15 +Perception, +15 (adv) +Intimidation, +13 +History, +13 +Planes +17 (+2 huge, +4 Demolitionist) +Athletics ![{4273741D-2A3C-400E-90CF-ACC0943279F7}.png|Imprisoned Antaen by Rafael Zanchetin, for [Hearthstone>)]]
