entries.title: Balor
entries.content: A sigil of a name in infernal floats in the air. The burning text intensifies as you approach before it unleashes an enormous fiend with a hankering for destruction. Level 14 Large Fiend (Mastermind) Boss Defense Traits | AC | 32 (medium armor) | | | ------------------------------------------------- | ---------------------------- | ------------------------------ | | HP | 200 | | | Poise | 40 | DRA 5, DR 10 vs Disarm | | Saves | Fort +18, Refl +16, Will +18 | +4 Fear | Immunity - Fire Recovery Bleed for Master Su Healing - At the start of the balor's turn, all of its allies within 60 feet without the defeated condition take 5 True Damage and the balor heals for this amount. Offense Traits (DC 27) | Flame Whip (S+Fire) | +19 | +10 Reach during turn, +8 drag, +4 MV, +2 Topple | | ------------------------------------------------------------- | ----------------- | ------------------------------------------------------------ | | | d6+5 (8) +10 fire | +Taste Fear | | Serrated Sword (S) | +19 | bleed (drain 10) | | | d6+5 (8) | +Taste Fear | Multiattack - The balor makes 2 attacks. It dual wields its flame Whip and serrated sword. Innate Techniques (CL 15) - Bladed Decimation, Telekinetic Thrust, Backfire 1/day - Calamity, Wall of Fire, Fire Blast, Warcry, Extreme Speed, Armed Robbery, Constant - Taste Fear Quicken Spell - Telekinetic Thrust Destruction Unbound (Su) Recharge - 1/day when a balor falls below half hp, a chain reaction is triggered inside of it, with an obvious glow to any who can see it. At the start of its next turn, it unleashes a devastating blast that deals 30 true damage to every creature (other than the balor) within 30 ft, and knocking them back 30 ft (reflex haves damage and negates the knockback). Cruel Critical - When a balor crits, the creature becomes Shaken 2. Doomed - Enemy minions in combat against a balor are shaken 4. Head Collector - When a balor melee attacks a defeated creature or lowers a creature's +HP to zero with an attack, and they become defeated, they are decapitated. This can only be done if it deals slash damage. Equal Opportunity Execution - A balor can use its Fearsome Finisher feat when it defeats or kills one of its allies. Boss Actions: Usable twice per turn - Get Over Here! - Usable at the end of a creature's turn after it exits the balor's Threaten range. A balor can use its Telekinetic Thrust technique to pull an enemy adjacent to it (up to 30 ft), but it deals no damage. - Get Back Up! - Whenever an ally that isn't the balor is Knocked Down or is forcefully moved from its space, it can use its Telekinetic Thrust technique to prevent them from moving or getting knocked over. - Roar - Usable at the end of a creature's turn if it damaged the balor that turn. If they are within 60 ft, it can Demoralize them. If successful, the creature becomes shaken 2. If the creature was already shaken 4, they become Frightened. - Worthless Minion (healing) - At the end of a creature's turn (including its own) a balor can execute an ally that is below 20 +HP or that is a minion to it. When it does so, it heals the creatures remaining hp, or half that if it is a minion. Weaknesses/Deep Lore Holy Tincture - When the balor drops below half hp and its Destruction Unbound ability triggers, if before its next turn it is exposed to Holy Water, the damage of its explosion changes to holy and it damages the balor (with no saving throw). Weakness - Weakness 5 Holy Other Traits Ability Scores - STR +5, DEX +2, LUK +1, INT +1, Wis +2, Cha +0 Languages - Infernal, Common Senses - Nightvision Signature Skill - one eye open, quick scan Equipment - Heavy Armor, Serrated Sword, Flame Whip, Medium Armor Speed - 40, 40 Fly Roar (recharge) - A balor can affect its voice as per Amplify at will. As a standard action, a balor can let out a roar that echoes miles. In this roar contains a few words, typically something like "converge on me" or "mission complete, retreat" that all Fiends can intuit its meaning. Usable once an hour. Deific Boons - Huge, from my cold dead head Feats - Callous Casting, Signature Skill, Skill Focus, Dual-Wielding Versatility, Maneuver Versatility, Fearsome Finisher, Bonded Weapon Skills - +18 +Perception, +18 +Insight, +20 (adv, +4 FF) +Intimidation, +20 (adv, +2 huge) +Athletics, +17 +Planes !Pathfinder 2nd Edition Balor Demon|670

entries.title: Ogre
entries.content: This large green humanoid has curious ears that poke out of the top of its head almost like antennae. You have the displeasure of smelling its breath as it speaks to you, singing your eyebrows. Level 3 Large Giant Defense Traits | AC | 16 | | | ------------------------------------------------- | ------------------------- | --- | | HP | 44 | | | Poise | 28 | | | Saves | Fort +8, Refl +2, Will +3 | | Resistance - Poison Stench persistent - Creatures within the natural reach of an ogre becomes sickened 1 (fort negates). Offense Traits (DC 15) | Unarmed (C) | +6 | +4 MV, +4 GU | | ----------------------------------------------------- | ---------- | --------------------------------------------------- | | | 2d6+3 (10) | +Giant Slam | | Onion (C) | +6 | Thrown, +2 BE | | | 3d6+3 (13) | Sickened 2 | Multiattack - Make 2 melee attacks Innate Techniques - Foe Toss, Gut Check, Body Slam 1/day - Spartan Kick Constant - Artillery Better Out than In poison recharge - As a free action (1/combat) an ogre can create a cloud of toxic gas in a 10ft radius around them. Creatures inside are staggered and take 3 poison damage and are sickened 1 each round inside until they leave. This effect persists for 1 round after they leave (fort negates). Ogres are immune to this ability. Special Attack: Slam - Shove a creature in any direction with a +4 bonus. If it impacts a solid surface, it takes unarmed damage. It is dazed for one round (fort negates). Giant Slam - Many giants deal 10 poise damage on-hit to any creature smaller than them when they make melee attacks. If this breaks poise, the creature is either shoved or knocked down, giants choice. Summon Ally - DONKEH. It has -1 INT and knows common. Weaknesses/Deep Lore Bath Time - An ogre that becomes wet loses its stench ability. Other Traits Ability Scores - STR +3, DEX -1, LUK +0, INT +0, WIS +0, CHA +4 Languages - Common, giant Gear - A bag full of succulent onions. Only slightly rotten for flavor. Speed - 30 Feats - Bullseye, Maneuver Versatility, Gang Up, Suppressing Fire Skills - +5 +Perception, +8 +Athletics, +9 +Intimidation, +9 +Deception, +5 +Nature

entries.title: Frost Giant Draugr
entries.content: Once full of spirit, this giant warrior now only feels hate and despair. It lurches towards you, its eyes filled with hunger. Level 4 Large Giant/Undead Defense Traits | AC | 20 (heavy armor) | | | ------------------------------------------------- | -------------------------- | ------------- | | HP | 60 | | | Poise | 31 | DRA 1 | | Saves | Fort +10, Refl +3, Will +4 | | Resistance - Cold Offense Traits (DC 16) | 2h Sword (C) | +8 | +4 MV | | ------------------------------------------------------ | ---------- | ------------------------------- | | | 2d8+4 (13) | +Bleeding | Multiattack - Make a combat maneuver and a Melee Attack Innate Techniques (CL 4) - Cripple, Spartan Kick 1/day - Frost Armor, Blight Constant - Ice Skating (self) Grasp of the Starved Su - A frost giant draugr can plunge one of its hands into the ground as a standard action and attempt to grapple a creature within 60 ft as its hand pops out from beneath them. If successful, it deals 2d8 (9) necrotic damage and the giant pulls them back to it and swallows them. It can only use this while standing in snow/ice against foes standing in snow/ice. Swallow - Drained 3 Protect recharge elusive - A frost giant draugr chooses a creature to be its charge for 24 hours. Whenever that creature takes damage, the draugr can move 25 feet closer to that enemy as an immediate action. If the enemy is within reach, make an AoO as per Sentinel. Choosing a charge is 1/day and the movement is usable 1/turn. Weaknesses/Deep Lore Team Spirit? - A frost giant remembers its home team, and refuses to fight anyone it recognizes as a member or fan of its home team, even while charmed, possessed or otherwise controlled. Other Traits Ability Scores - STR +4, DEX -1, LUK +0, INT -1, WIS +0, CHA +0 Languages - Common, giant, primordial Speed - 25 Feats - Maneuver Versatility, Sentinel Skills - +6 +Perception, +10 +Athletics, +5 +Stealth

entries.title: Frost Giant
entries.content: This reclusive blue giant lives almost exclusively in the frozen corners of the world where the ice never melts. It wields a weapon with a peculiar shape to it and moves along snow and ice with startling speed. Level 4 Large Giant Defense Traits | AC | 20 (heavy armor) | | | ------------------------------------------------- | -------------------------- | ------------- | | HP | 60 | | | Poise | 33 | DRA 1 | | Saves | Fort +10, Refl +5, Will +4 | | Resistance - Cold Interception - As an immediate action when they wield a hockey stick, a frost giant can parry a Ranged Attack. They can even do this against siege weapons. Offense Traits (DC 16) | Hockey Stick (C) | +8 | +2 Disarm, Knockdown, Shove, +4 GU, +4 MV | | ---------------------------------------------------------- | ---------- | ---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | | | 2d8+4 (13) | | | Puck (C) | +8 | Thrown, +Siege Weapon+4 GU, +4 MV | | | 4d6+4 (18) | | Multiattack - Make a combat maneuver and a Melee Attack Innate Techniques - Cripple, Spartan Kick, Fore!, Put Me In!, Dive, Bull Rush 1/day - Flying Arrow, Frost Armor Constant - Ice Skating (self) Hockey - Frost giants always carry around a piece of solid ice that can be used as a puck for a game of hockey, or as a deadly weapon when needed. - Frost giants can create these pucks as a standard action. Pucks cannot exist within 300 ft of another, as per the rules of the game (Su). - A frost giant can use a puck attack if it controls a puck. It can control it a free action whenever a puck isn't already controlled and within it's natural reach while it wields a hockey stick. - A frost giant can pass the puck to an ally as a swift action - A puck cannot be taken without first Poise breaking a giant that controls one. When a creatures loses control of of a puck, it flies a random direction 20 ft. Swallow - Drained 3 Weaknesses/Deep Lore Team Spirit - Frost giants live in tight enclaves and are wildly fascinated with their home team. They take great pride in their abilities and readily accept challengers. They are even willing to accept challenges from people who do not play hockey, with the goal of the challengers usually to score a single goal on the team of giants. Teams are always played with an equal number of players, usually with at least 4, and players aren't allowed to deal any +HP damage to others. Other than that, there are no hard fast rules. The goal of the players is to pass a wide stretch with a puck. Because the frost giants are exceedingly good at bullying players, they will likely need to think of clever ways to disarm, distract or otherwise get the giants out of the picture. They will also need to maneuver across ice (see Frosty Terrain). Giants are pretty big, so their rinks are over 400 ft wide from goal to goal. A start from the center requires 200 ft for the puck to travel. Other Traits Ability Scores - STR +4, DEX +1, LUK +0, INT +0, WIS +0, CHA +1 Languages - Common, giant, primordial Speed - 35, 45 ice skating Feats - Ricochet Toss, Point Blank Master, Maneuver Versatility, Sentinel, Escape Route, Gang Up Skills - +6 +Perception, +10 +Athletics, +6 +Nature, +8 +Profession (hockey)

entries.title: Frost Giant Spirit

Any other ally within 60 ft can gain this benefit with the same restrictions as long as they speak the giant language. Song of Sorrow - Frost giant spirits are only formed as a result of being killed in battle and left to rot. As such, their songs they chant regularly are filled with great tragedy. Other Traits Ability Scores - STR -1, DEX +3, LUK +0, INT +3, WIS +0, CHA +0 Languages - Common, giant, primordial Speed - 30 Hover Feats - Skill Focus, MM Bouncing Spell Skills - +6 (adv) +Perception, +8 (adv) +Intimidation

entries.title: Molten Giant
entries.content: The metal this giant wears glows red hot. The weapon glows even hotter. When you draw your weapons it only seems to put fuel on the fire that is their hair. Level 5 Large Giant/Elemental Defense Traits | AC | 21 (heavy armor) | | | ------------------------------------------------- | --------------------------- | ------------- | | HP | 70 | | | Poise | 32 | DRA 1 | | Saves | Fort +9, Refl +4, Will +9 | +4 Earth | Resistance - Fire Super Hot - Any creatures without fire resistance that maintains prolonged contact (such as drag or grapple) with a molten giant take 4 fire damage and are sickened 2 from the intense pain. Offense Traits (DC 17) | 2h Flail (C+Fire) | +11 | +4 MV | | -------------------------------------------------------------- | -------------- | -------------------------------- | | | 2d8+4+d6 (16) | +Giant Slam, +5 Weapon Focus | | Molten Rock (C+Fire) | +11 | Thrown | | | 2d6+4+2d6 (18) | +3d6 (10) Lava | Multiattack - Make 2 melee attacks Innate Techniques - Awesome Blow, Foe Toss 1/day - Colossal Smash, Earth Shatter Constant - Shockwave Heat of Battle - At the start of a molten giants turn, it generates 2 Heat. Whenever a molten giant takes fire damage, it gains heat stacks equal to how much fire damage it took (after resistance). As a swift action, a molten giant can superheat its weapon, adding its heat stacks as additional fire damage on-hit, after which the heat dissipates until used again. Molten Rock fire - By spending 2 heat as a standard action and with 2 hands free, a molten giant can dig its fingers into a stone surface and melt it enough to give it something to throw, or it can super heat a nearby rock of adequate size. The rock is a thrown weapon that deals 2d6+4 (11) crush and 2d6 (7) fire damage. The creature also has molten rock cling to them, dealing 3d6 (10) damage at the start of their turn. This damage decreases by another d6 each turn. If the creature is cleaned or otherwise cooled then the damage stops. Giant Slam - Many giants deal 10 poise damage on-hit to any creature smaller than them when they make melee attacks. If this breaks poise, the creature is either shoved or knocked down, giants choice. Special Attacks - Wild Strike, Tenderize Weaknesses/Deep Lore Extinguish - Whenever a molten giant is made wet while they have heat, they negate it and instead lose all heat stacks. They also cannot gain heat for 1 round. Other Traits Ability Scores - STR +4, DEX -1, LUK +0, INT +1, WIS +2, CHA +0 Languages - Common, giant, primordial Easy Fix - If a piece of metal equipment a molten giant wields becomes damaged, they can spend 1 Heat as a swift action to fix it. Equipment - 1x Heavy Armor, 1x 2h flail, Speed - 35 Land Speed Feats - Skill Focus, Pet, Weapon Focus (crush), Maneuver Versatility Skills - +9 +Perception, +9 (adv) +Insight, +9 +Survival, +8 +Nature, +11 +Athletics, +13 +Profession (blacksmithing)

entries.title: Cloud Giant
entries.content: A cloud flies by at rapid speeds and knocks you to your feet. Level 10 Huge Giant Defense Traits | AC | 25 (med armor) | | | ------------------------------------------------- | ---------------------------- | --- | | HP | 100 | | | Poise | 36 | | | Saves | Fort +16, Refl +11, Will +10 | | Resistance - Zap, Air Offense Traits (DC 22) | Great Hammer (C) | +14 | | | ---------------------------------------------------------- | ----------- | --------------------- | | | 4d8+4 (22) | +17 Bonk, +Blown Away | | Air Bending (C) | +14 | | | | 6d10+4 (37) | +Blown Away | Multiattack - Make 2 melee attacks. Innate Techniques - Shocking Grasp, Spirit Animal, Storm Stomp, Amplify, Storm Step, Fore!, Foe Toss, Colossal Smash, 1/day - Lightning Bolt (U5), Snowscape, Wind Wall, Thunderlord's Decree Blown Away - On-hit make a creature Clumsy 1. Whenever a cloud giant make a creature clumsy, they are pushed 10 ft directly away from it. During a multiattack, this movement does not happen until after all attacks are resolved, unless the giant so wishes it otherwise. Nimbus Companion - A Cloud Giant employs the service of a loyal Cloucat. These cats are trained from birth for combat and have adjusted stats. While mounted on the cloucat... - The giant gains a Fly speed equal to the cloucat's. When it becomes Clumsy 3 or greater, it is automatically dismounted and the clumsy stacks are removed. - Whenever the cloud giant uses a technique that involves movement, the cloucat moves with it and ignores its negative effects as though it were the user. Cannonball air - By falling 30 ft and landing on the ground after jumping into the air or flying, you can cannonball as a standard action. Creatures within 20 ft of you take your airbending blast damage and are knocked to the edge of the AoE (fort halves damage and negates pushback). Until the start of your next turn, get +4 AC vs ranged attacks. Swallow - Drain 5 Weaknesses/Deep Lore Other Traits Ability Scores - STR +4, DEX +1, LUK +0, INT +1, WIS +1, CHA +1 Languages - Common, Giant, Primordial Storm Sight - Can partially see through fog clouds, icy mist, etc. Creatures that would normally have total concealment because of such conditions, instead have partial concealment. Deific Boons - Eye in the Sky Bingus: Speed - 35 Land Speed, 35 Hover Feats - Combat Reflexes, Sentinel, Spell Ambush, Escape Route, Bonk, MM Bullying Spell Skills - +13 +Perception, +13 +Survival, +13 +Stealth, +13 +Local, +13 +Nature, +19 +Athletics

entries.title: Bingus Mingus
entries.content: A muscular two-headed giant laughs maniacally as it waves its arms around wildly, smashing the ground with its club and shooting rays of shadowy energy from the other. Level 12 Huge Giant Boss, Unique Defense Traits | AC | 27 | | | ------------------------------------------------- | ---------------------------- | ----------------------------------------------- | | HP | 240 | | | Poise | 41 | | | Saves | Fort +15, Refl +11, Will +16 | adv fort Mingus, adv will Mingus | Recovery Mingus: Nudge Elusive - As an immediate action, nudge BM 5 ft. If this puts BM out of the area of an AoE effect, gain advantage on the save. Mind and Body - Bingus has Advantage on fortitude saves. Mingus has advantage on will saves. These bonuses only apply to single target effects. Wake Up Doofus - Bingus and Mingus can use the Aid action on each other as a swift action. Offense Traits (DC 25) | Bingus: Club (C) | +18 | +4 MV | | ---------------------------------------------------------- | --------- | ------------------------------- | | | d10+4 (9) | +Bloody Assault | Bingus: Multiattack - Make 2 melee attacks. Mingus: Innate Techniques (CL 17) - Gloom Bolt, Hot Potato, WonkaS, Confuse Ray 1/day - Singularity, Shackles of Pain, Tastier Rainbow, Self-Destruct Bingus: Innate Techniques - Walk Around, Colossal Smash, Dispelling Strike (U6) 1/day - Bladed Decimation, Armed Robbery Bingus: Tip the Balance Earth Recharge - Bingus smashes the ground in front of him, causing it to flip up into the air like a loose floorboard, flinging foes towards him. Target a 60 ft cone. Creatures within the area take 10d6 (35) crush damage, are flung adjacent to him and knocked down (fort halves damage and turns movement/knockdown to 30 poise damage). Bingus: Bloody Assault - Inflict 4d6 (14) bleed On-hit with melee attacks. Boss Actions: Usable once per turn. - Mingus: Gloom Bolt - At the start of a creature's turn, fire a Gloom Bolt at them. If they are Blinded by the technique, it only lasts until the end of their turn. - Mingus: Hot Potato (recharge) - When a creature enters BM's reach, cast Hot Potato. - Bingus: Weaknesses/Deep Lore Two Heads are Better than One - Bingus and Mingus are two parts of the same creature. Usually one head controls most of the body (Bingus), while the other controls one arm (Mingus) and can only nudge the rest of the body. Bingus' and Mingus' abilities are marked to tell who can do what. They each have their own actions and their own individual positions in initiative. They collectively still only get one boss action. Whenever a they are subject to a single target effect that inflicts a status condition, the attacker must choose to target either Bingus or Mingus. No This Way! - If Bingus and Mingus are Taunted by two different creatures at the same time, they are Entangled as they fight for who to go for. This also happens when either is Dominated or otherwise robbed of their autonomy. Anomaly - BM is a herald of god of chaos Frogg Saran. His mere presence is enough to reshape the world around him. At the start of combat, a Mario Kart-esque random effect shows up above him, adding a new set of rules to the combat. Whenever ANY creature fails to follow the rules set by the random anomaly, a frog falls from the sky onto their head. Roll a d4 to decide which one triggers. A creature is forced to follow the rules on any turn they are confused, even if they don't understand them. People with GBP for Frogg always understand the rules. - The Number 3 - A creature can only move in increments of 3 spaces (aka 15 ft). If at the end of their turn, they moved a number of spaces not divisible by 3, they are Confused until the end of their next turn. Creatures that don't move at all are exempt from Frogg's wrath. To slightly aid in understanding, moving creatures hear a croak every 3 spaces they move. - Rainbow - A creature must use a different Special Attack, Spell or Su ability each turn. If they ever repeat the same turn, or something very similar (GM discretion), they are Confused until the end of their next turn. - WonkaS Game - At the end of a creature's turn, they must shout out and point to a creature they can see saying "I choose you" or something to that effect. They then teleport and swap places with them, automatically removing the Grounded condition from any targets involved. If they do not choose a creature to swap places with, they are confused until the end of their next turn. - Other Traits Ability Scores - STR +4, DEX -1, LUK +3, INT +3, WIS -2, CHA +0 Languages - Common, Giant Senses - Darkvision Deific Boons - frog shape, born again believer, forbidden knowledge Bingus: Speed - 30 Feats - Combat Reflexes, Sentinel, Maneuver Versatility, Butterfly Sting - MM Elemental Shift, Skills - +12 (adv) +Perception, +18 +Athletics, +17 Arcana, +17 +Planes, +17 +Cryptozoology, +17 +Religion ![Pasted image 20251011012428.png|Cho'Gall, Twilight Chieftan, by Alex Horley Orlandelli for [Hearthstone]]

entries.title: loot-goblin
entries.content: Loot Goblin Level 0 Small Goblin Defense Traits AC 13\ HP 20\ Poise 18\ Fort -1, Refl +5, Will +0 Flee! - A loot goblin's movement is \[elusive] while its hp is below 10. Offense Traits (DC 12) Unarmed (C) +4 (+4 disarm)\ d8+3 (7) Innate Techniques - 1/day - Break Free Claimed - A loot goblin can use disarm to take anything on a creatures person that is readily accessible, such as potions, weapons, magic items etc. When a loot goblin disarms a creature, it can grab hold of the item and immediately stow it away in its loot pouch. Weaknesses/Deep Lore Shinies - A creature can wave around a valuable object to tantalize a loot goblin. A valuable enough object taunts the goblin, forcing it to steal the item (will negates). The object must have at least a 1k gold value. If it is worth at least 10k, the goblin gets no save. A pouch of gold is satisfactory as well. Once presented, the item can be the target of the loot goblin's "claimed" ability. Other Traits Ability Scores - Str -1, Dex +3, Lucc +3, Int +0, Wis +0, Cha +0 Night Vision Speed - 40 Loot Scent - A loot goblin has the ability to smell the value of anything in its possession or adjacent to it. Objects with more than 1k gold of value can be smelled from 30 ft, and more than 10k can be sniffed out from hundreds of feet away. Loot Enthusiast - When fleeing after scoring some loot, a loot goblin can carry up to 100lbs of loot, despite its small size. Once the adrenaline wears off after the coast is clear, they must drag anything heavier than 50 lbs. Gear - 1 durable and elastic sack Feats - Fleet Footwork Skills - +3 perception, +6 acrobatics, +6 stealth, +6 sleight of hand

A creature can only be affected by this 1/day, no matter how many guards shout. Weaknesses/Deep Lore Only Human - Guards have lives outside of protecting a town. As such they will typically not engage against creatures they successfully identify are level 6 or higher without someone else of a similar level commanding them. entries.title: Murderous Chieftan
entries.content: This orc has numerous battle scars and a dangerous aura that can be felt with their gaze. They don't command their allies to fight on sight, but you can tell one wrong move can change that. Level 3 medium Orc Boss Defense Traits | AC | 14 | | | ------------------------------------------------- | ------------------------- | --------------- | | HP | 56 | +50 Temp HP | | Poise | 30 | | | Saves | Fort +8, Refl +6, Will +4 | +4 Fear | Ferocity Naked Courage - When not wearing armor, an orc chieftain gets Resistance fear, pierce, slash and crush damage. Offense Traits (DC 15) | Greataxe (S) | +6 | +4 MV, +2 Combat Maneuvers, +2 Topple, +2 Vengeful | | --------------------------------------------------------------- | ---------- | -------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | | | 2d8+3 (12) | | | Crossbow (P) | +6 | Long, Reload | | | 2d6+3 (10) | | | Misc Heavy Object (C) | +6 | Thrown | | | 2d8+3 (12) | | | Bola (C) | +6 | Thrown | | | d6+3 (6) | | Multiattack - Make a Melee Attack and a combat maneuver attack Innate Techniques - Break Free 1/day - Head On, War Paint Vengeful - The chieftain gets a +2 bonus to attack against any creature that has damaged them since the end of their last turn. Special Attack: Frenzied Strikes - The chieftain grows noticeably more jittery as their muscles bulge, their mouth froths and their eyes roll into the back of their head. A chieftain makes an amount of simple melee attacks attacks equal to (1+the number of turns they have made a frenzied strike this combat), stacking up to 4 attacks at once. They must use these attacks against different creatures. Gaze Fear - Inflicts Shaken 1. Boss Actions - A chieftain can take one of the following actions each turn. It cannot use these actions during Mak'Gora. Sound the Horn Healing - When combat begins, the chieftain blows their war horn. If there are at least 3 other allied orcs in the nearby area, the chieftain is rallied, giving them 50 Temp HP. Let the battle begin! Finish Him - At any point a chieftain can command an ally to make a Melee Attack against a Defeated creature. Chieftain's Command Recharge Healing - Snap an ally within 30 ft out from an effect that causes the frightened or fascinated conditions. Feast (healing) Recharge - The chieftain drives their fist into a defeated or dead enemy's chest, then pulls out and consumes their heart. The chieftain and all allies that can see them gain 10 temp hp. This kills the enemy. Dishonorable COWARDS - Can only be used in response to a creature breaking the rules of Mak'Gora. Immediately use the Warcry technique, and its radius increases to 40 ft. Weaknesses/Deep Lore Mak'Gora - Chieftain of an orc tribe is an ever shifting position, and a chief must be ready to defend their position. If you challenge the chieftain to a duel, they may be willing to partake depending on the circumstances. Even if they don't the pressure of their peers may goad them into it (will negates). If they pass their save they make up an excuse to placate their band of warriors. The murderous chieftain and the members of their tribe fight with honor to the death, and will not aid each other using magic, items etc, before or during the fight. It is expected the opponent does the same. Breaking the rules in the middle of the battle, such as to cast a spell on an ally, causes the duel to break down and a chaotic battle ensues. A war band will respect the results of the battle, and bow to whoever is the victor. Other Traits Ability Scores - STR +3, DEX +3, LUK +0, INT -1, WIS +1, CHA +1 Languages - Common, Orcish Equipment - 1 Greataxe, 1 Crossbow, 1 Bola, War Horn Deific Boons - huge Speed - 55 Land Speed Feats - Maneuver Versatility, Maneuver Master, Fearsome Finisher, Escape Route Skills - +6 +Perception, +6 +Survival, +8 (+2 huge) +Athletics, +10 +Intimidation

entries.title: Falcaptain
entries.content: A bird with gold and blue feathers dashes around the battlefield back and forth, evading attacks with ease. After a flurry of blows, he stops to give a salute while saying "Show me your moves!" Level 9 Medium Beastfolk Defense Traits | AC | 24 | | | ------------------------------------------------- | --------------------------- | -------------------------------- | | HP | 110 | | | Poise | 35 | DR 2 (+5 PP) | | Saves | Fort +12, Refl +14, Will +9 | | Dash Dance - In combat a falcaptain is persistently moving. At the end of its turn, it chooses an adjacent space. It is treated as though it occupies its current space, or the space it chose for all effects, whichever is more advantageous. To affect him with an ability, it must be able to reach/affect both spaces. Falcaptain's movement while strafing is elusive. Offense Traits (DC 21) | Unarmed (C) | +12 | +4 MV | | ----------------------------------------------------- | ---------- | ------------------------------- | | | 2d6+3 (10) | | Multiattack - Make 3 unarmed attacks Innate Techniques - Clothesline, Wake-Up Slap, Fired Up, Burst of Speed 1/day - Rend Vision, Peacocking Constant - Unbreakable Advance Combo Starter - When a falcaptain successfully grapples a creature, he can choose to immediately throw them airborne 15 ft into the air. They maintain the entangled condition until the end of the falcaptains next turn and become clumsy 1. This remains the case no matter what happens to the falcaptain in the meantime. A creature can take the aid action to automatically grab their ally out of the air if they can reach (typically only for large creatures), removing these penalties. Special Attack: Falcon Punch Fire - Deal an additional 2d6 (7) fire damage and launch target 30 ft directly away from the falcaptain on hit. The target is also set ablaze on hit. Special Attack: Knee of Justice Zap - Special: This attack can only be used against an airborne target. Deal an additional 4d6 (14) zap damage and launch target 30 ft directly away from the falcaptain on hit. Special Attack: Stomp - Special: This attack can only be used against an airborne target or a Knocked Down target. Deal an extra 2d6 (7) crush damage and immediately send the target to the ground, knocking them down. This special attack recharges On-hit and can be used multiple times a turn. Down Taunt Healing - By spending a standard action to say his catch phrase, falcaptain can recharge all of his special attacks and gain 10 temp hp. If he successfully hits a creature with Falcon Punch, he can take an immediate action to down taunt, ending his turn. Weaknesses/Deep Lore Clipn't - Falcaptain must use a special attack each round of combat. If he fails to do so and doesn’t down taunt, he is shaken 1. If this happens and he becomes shaken 4 or higher, he gains the Time Out condition until he rests. Other Traits Ability Scores - STR +3, DEX +3, LUK +0, INT -1, WIS +1, CHA +1 Languages - Common, Sylvan Athleticism - Falcaptain can jump twice each round as a free action, even combining them in midair. Speed - 45 Land Speed, Glide Feats - Maneuver Versatility, Maneuver Master, Power Pose, Blind Fight, Skill Focus Skills - +11 (adv) +Perception, +16 (adv) +Acrobatics

entries.title: Humanoids
entries.content: Humanoids often have class levels, but these ones don't.

entries.title: jizzle
entries.content: Jizzle Level 0 Small Ooze Defense Traits AC 11\ HP 20\ Poise 21\ Fort +5, Refl +1, Will +1 Resistance - Poison Indestructible - A jizzle cannot be killed, only temporarily abated. When defeated, it shrinks down to the size of a mouse. By placing a defeated jizzle into a vessel, you can remove it as a threat. Immune - Blinded, Blurred Vision Amorphous - +12 Offense Traits (DC 12) Psuedpod (poison) +3 (+4 drag)\ 2d6+2 (9) Extract Fluids - As a standard action, the jizzle can feast on a creature within reach's bodily fluids. The creature takes 5 true damage and is fatigued (fort negates). Proper hydration and 10 minutes of rest can get rid of this fatigue. Weaknesses/Deep Lore So That's Why it's Called That... - With a DC 11 alchemy check and 1 hour, you can add the secret ingredient to a captured jizzle. With it, you can create a new jizzle physick. Curiosity - Jizzles when they aren't hungry will attempt to mimic the appearance of friendly creatures around them. Other Traits Ability Scores - Str +2, Dex +0, Lucc +0, Int -, Wis +0, Cha +0 Sightless Blindsense - Tremor 60 ft Compression Speed - Swim 30, Climb 30 that is usable on any solid surface Skills - +2 (+8) perception, +4 athletics By 水太

entries.title: primordial-ooze
entries.content: Primordial Ooze Level 3 Large Ooze \[boss] Defense Traits AC 15\ HP 100\ Poise 26\ Fort +8, Refl +5, Will +3 Color Change - A primordial ooze is immune all damage. After being affected by a unique type of elemental damage (see new life), it instead becomes resistant to damage for 1 round. When it loses this damage immunity, it takes half damage from the ability used. Once it takes damage from 7 unique elemental types, it loses this quality until it rests. Amorphous - +12 Immune - Blinded, Blurred Vision Offense Traits (DC 15) Psuedpod (poison) +6 (+4 MI)\ 2d6+3 (10) Multiattack - Make 2 psuedopod attacks Innate Techniques (CL 4) - Avenging Wrath, Sanctuary, Noble Sacrifice\ 1/day - Taste the Rainbow, Nauseating Trail, Lightning Strike, Chill Metal Boiyoiyoing - 1/turn, a primordial ooze can deal 20 poise damage to a creature by moving adjacent to it. Engulf Weaknesses/Deep Lore Leftovers - A DC 23 alchemy check allows safe storage of the potent oils required for the Rebirth spell. New Life - Primordial oozes are made of creatures that are yet to be. The first time a primordial ooze takes elemental damage of new type, the primordial ooze summons a (up to) level 3 creature of a specific type in an adjacent space, depending on damage type. The creature is never hostile towards the ooze. Fire - Fiend\ Necrotic - Undead\ Psychic - Aberration\ Force - Cryptid\ Zap - Construct\ Frost - Elemental\ Poison - Beast\ Holy - Celestial\ Sonic - Fey Other Traits Ability Scores - Str +3, Dex +2, Lucc +0, Int -, Wis +0, Cha +0 Sightless Blindsense - Tremor 60 ft Feats - Maneuver Initiate, Maneuver Master Skills - +5 perception, +8 athletics, +7 stealth

entries.title: Living Ice
entries.content: A pile of icicles on the ground springs to life, stretching all over the place to pierce you with its sharpened frosty body. Level 4 Large Ooze Defense Traits | AC | 18 | | | ------------------------------------------------- | ------------------------- | ---------------- | | HP | 78 | | | Poise | 27 | DR 5 Solid | | Saves | Fort +6, Refl +9, Will +4 | +4 Water | Amorphous - +15 Resistance - Frost, Water Immune - Blinded, Blurred Vision Offense Traits (DC 16) | Pseudopod (C) | +7 | | | -------------------------------------------------------------------- | ---------- | ---------------------- | | | 2d6+3 (10) | | | Psuedopod (P) [solid] | +7 | | | | 2d8+3 (12) | | | Waterbending (frost/crush) | +7 | 30 ft range | | | 4d8+3 (21) | +Frosty On-hit | Multiattack - The living ice shrinks and causes many spikes to rapidly expand around its body. Make 3 pseudopod attacks attacks with a -4 penalty. If they all target different creatures, remove the penalty. Innate Techniques - Body Slam, Lick (U2), Denied 1/day - Ice Lance Icy Hold Frost - Creature within 30 ft is made frosty. They are also take the living ice's waterbending damage as frost damage and are entangled (reflex halves dmg and negates entangled). Breakout as a swift action. 1 fire damage frees the creature automatically. Can also be used on medium-sized objects. Path of Ice - Whenever living ice moves, it leaves behind a trail of ice. Most creatures that step onto an icy space are knocked down (+Acrobatics or reflex negates). Living ice and other creatures accustomed to ice can traverse safely. Weaknesses/Deep Lore Form Shift - Living ice can swap between a solid and liquid form, generally preferring it's solid ice form. If a creature does fire damage to it while it is solid, it becomes liquid. If a creature uses frost damage on it when it is liquid, it becomes solid. Various other stats change depending on form, as noted in italics. Dealing crush damage to it while it is knocked down also turns it into its liquid form. If not under duress, it can change forms as a free action 1/turn. While in combat, it can't transform back into its solid form for 1 round after forcefully converted to its liquid form. Other Traits Ability Scores - STR +2, DEX +3, LUK +0, INT -, WIS +0, CHA +0 Languages - None Speed - 40 Land Speed, 40 Climb, 40 Swim (+20 to all speeds while submerged in liquid) Senses - Sightless Blindsense (Tremorsense 60 ft) Hide in Plain Sight - Any snowy or arctic terrain. 1 creature that fails to notice the living ice when it uses its hide in plain sight ability to ambush is automatically knocked down when attacked. Signature Skill - stalker, dampen presence Feats - Power Pose, Signature Skill Skills - +6 +Perception, +9 +Stealth

entries.title: Dragon Blood
entries.content: The life blood drained from the corpses of many dragon animated. It seeks to drain the life of anything it can engulf. Level 6 Massive Ooze/Undead Boss Defense Traits | AC | 20 | | | ------------------------------------------------- | -------------------------- | --- | | HP | 180 | | | Poise | 50 | | | Saves | Fort +10, Refl +5, Will +8 | | Amorphous - +15 Recovery Resistance - Necrotic, Poison Immune - Blinded, Blurred Vision, Toxin, Venom Offense Traits (DC 19) | Pseudopod (main) (P) | +12 | +4 MV | | -------------------------------------------------------------- | ---------- | ------------------------------- | | | 2d8+4 (13) | +Festering Blood | | Pseudopod (lesser) | +10 | +4 MV | | | 2d6+4 (11) | +Festering Blood | Innate Techniques - Dog Pile, Lick (U3), Corrupting Mist, Cure Wounds 1/day - Drain Soul, Unga Bunga, Colossal Smash, Awesome Blow Engulf - Every turn engulfed, the trapped creature takes 10 necrotic damage. Hydragon Blood - Dragon blood functions as though it were several different creatures all attached to each other. This should be represented through 1 massive sized creature token as the head, and then 3 smaller medium sized tokens that each represent one of its ravenous pseudopods. They still all act on the same turn, but each of the pseudopods gets its own simple attack each round. All of the lesser "heads" must remain within 30 ft of the main head. The lesser heads threaten, but dragon blood can only make a single AoO against a creature, regardless of how many heads threaten a single creature. Similarly, AoE effects cannot double (or quadruple) up on damage when targeting multiple heads. Effects like MV that can only be used 1/rnd are still the case etc. Life Blood - Whenever dragon blood is subject to a healing effect, the main head makes an AoO against a random creature it threatens. Festering Blood - Functions as bleeding for 2d6 damage. While bleeding, all healing is halved, and other half is redirected to the dragon blood. The DC to remove the bleed is 22. Boss Actions - Usable 1/turn Get 'em Boss recharge - At the end of another creature's turn, cast Cure Wounds, only targeting self. Recharges after Life Blood procs from another source of healing. Get 'em Goons - At the end of another creature's turn, cast Dog Pile. Blood Buddy companion - At the end of its turn, dragon blood can create a particularly slimy mud buddy. Its +HP is reduced to 45. Belch - Immediately after engulfing a creature, emit a gross sounding stream of air at a creature you threaten. Inflicts sickened 2 (fort halves.) Blood Curdling Scream recharge - Usable immediately when taking damage while below half hp. Use the Roar technique, but it originates from main and lesser heads all at the same time (damage doesn't double up). Weaknesses/Deep Lore Indigestion - Dragon blood is much better at digesting the meat than anything else. It can be convinced, very forcefully, to eat anything. Objects roughly the size of a person are not particularly effective due to the large size of the ooze. An object must be roughly 10x10x10 ft before it has any noticeable effects. Such objects when thrust/dropped into the bloods amorphous body deal 10 true damage. Extremely large objects, like a large shipping vessel, deal 20 true damage and completely occupy one of dragon blood's lesser heads. Assimilation - Whenever dragon blood randomly targets an enemy, it instinctually attacks any Dragons first. Other Traits Ability Scores - STR +4, DEX -1, LUK +0, INT -2, WIS +0, CHA +0 Languages - Understands draconic Speed - 35 Land Speed, 35 Climb, 45 Swim Senses - Sightless Blindsense (Tremorsense 120 ft, Scent 30 ft) Feats - Maneuver Versatility, Combat Reflexes, Freedom of Movement Skills - +8 (+8) +Perception, +16 +Athletics

entries.title: mockstump
entries.content: Mockstump Level 3 Small Plant Defense Traits AC 19\ HP 44\ Poise 27\ Fort +5, Refl +3, Will +7 Comedy Gold \[healing] - If a creature spends a standard action to roll a perform (comedy) check within 30 ft of the mockstump, they can grant the mockstump temp hp equal to half their roll. This functions with all mockstumps in range. Offense Traits (DC 15) Bonk (C) +7\ d10+2 (7) Innate Techniques - Tree Shape, Mushroom Dance, Pants\ 1/day - Nourish, WonkaS, Headbutt, Sanctuary Wall of Wood (Su) \[concentration] - A mockstump can create a wall of trees that resemble larger versions of itself placed side by side in a long line. The wall is 30 feet high and 30 feet wide. This wall cannot be damaged, but a creature can attempt to break it or tell it a joke as a swift action. They can also attempt to climb it as a swift action, but the stumps are constantly laughing, making it require an athletics check to climb. The DC for breaking, comedying or climbing the wall is 20. If broken or told a good joke, the wall disappears entirely. If the creature fails this check, they are immediately effected by the mockstumps Hoo Hoo! ability. If the failure results in a natural 1 roll, the creature fails so spectacularly the wall disappears (though it is still ridiculed by the stumps). Hoo Hoo! \[psychic] \[recharge] - Whenever a mockstump passes a saving throw, a creature misses an attack against it or fails an opposed skill check, a mockstump can laugh at them to cause them distress if they are within 60 ft. They take 2d6 (7) psychic damage and are shaken 1 (will halves dmg and negates shaken). This can only happen once a turn, unless performed as a result of a creature failing to best the wood wall. Speak for the Trees (Su) - A mockstump can spend a swift action to cause an inanimate tree (including one from its own wall) to slam down on a creature within 10 feet of it. This functions as its own bonk attack, and can also be used to shove, bully or knock down. Weaknesses/Deep Lore Weakness - Fire 2 Other Traits Ability Scores - Str +2, Dex +0, Lucc +3, Int -2, Wis +2, Cha +0 Speed - 30 Mascot - A mockstump always grants a +2 bonus to skill checks when using the aid action. Feats - Fleet Footwork, Blind Fight Skills - +7 perception, +7 survival Mockstump by BasedBinkie

entries.title: Thorn Spitter
entries.content: A flower springs to life and starts rapidly spitting thorns at you and your allies coated in a dangerous looking liquid. Level 3 Small Plant Defense Traits | AC | 17 | | | ------------------------------------------------- | ------------------------- | --- | | HP | 50 | | | Poise | 24 | | | Saves | Fort +3, Refl +9, Will +3 | | Resistance - Poison Offense Traits (DC 15) | Thorn (P) | +7 | (30 ft, no second range increment | | --------------------------------------------------- | -------- | ------------------------------------------------------- | | | d8+4 (8) | | Multiattack - 2 thorn attacks Innate Techniques - Pin Down Thorn in my Side - Thorn attacks stick inside of creatures On-hit. A creature takes damage equal to the number of thorns stuck in them at the start of their turn. A thorn can be removed automatically with either a swift action or the aid action, dealing 1 damage. All thorns can be removed simultaneously with a DC 17 first aid, dealing no damage. Special Attack: Toxin poison - A thorn spitter can coat its thorns in annoying poisons, dealing an additional d8 (4) poison damage. It can apply one of the following. This special attack can be used once for each poison. - Irritation - Apply sickened 2 on hit. - Sleepy - Creature is made drowsy. If it does not take damage within 1 round, it falls asleep. Thorn spitters will not attack creatures still drowsy from sleep toxin. - Paralytic - Inflict a Venom that worsens as time passes. Creature is staggered for 1 round (fort negates). If the creature failed the first save, they make another save at the end of the turn they are staggered. They are dazed on the turn after (fort negates). This daze lasts until they pass a save (each at end of turn). Weaknesses/Deep Lore Weakness - 3 fire, 3 slash Other Traits Ability Scores - STR +0, DEX +4, LUK +0, INT -, WIS +0, CHA +0 Languages - None +Blindsense - Tremorsense 60 ft Speed - 5 Feats - Snap Shot, Point Blank Master, Combat Reflexes Skills - +5 +Perception, +9 +Stealth

entries.title: Treant
entries.content: A tree springs to life, creaking and bending its branches like arms and legs. Its leaves resemble wispy beards and lengthy heads of hair. Level 3 Huge Plant Defense Traits | AC | 19 | | | ------------------------------------------------- | ------------------------- | --------------- | | HP | 50 | FH 1 (sunlight) | | Poise | 29 | DR 12 (rooted) | | Saves | Fort +8, Refl +2, Will +6 | | Take Root - A treant can entangle itself as a swift action to reset its Poise and gain DR 12 vs poise damage for 1 round. Photosynthesis - FH 1 in sunlight Offense Traits (DC 15) | Unarmed (C) | +6 | +4 MV | | -------------------------------------------------------- | ---------- | ------------------------------- | | | 2d8+3 (12) | | | Razor Leaf (S) | +6 | Short | | | 2d6+3 (10) | | Multiattack - Make 2 attacks, either melee attacks or razor leaf attacks. Innate Techniques (CL 4) - Wild Growth, Steal Years, Reckless Charge, Bull Rush 1/day - Toe Smasher, Bush, Brace Yourself, Focus Punch Summon Ally - Any Level 1 Beasts Deathrattle - The treant falls, targeting a 30 ft line. Creatures in the area take 4d6 (14) crush and poise damage (reflex halves). Creatures poise broken by this are Knocked Down and Held Down until they can Breakout from underneath the tree. Weaknesses/Deep Lore Weakness - 3 fire The Forest Speaks - The bending and creaking of a treant is a subtle form of language it uses to communicate with others of its kind. A creature trained in +Linguistics can interpret the meaning of this communication with a DC 15 skill check. These conversations tend to happen very slowly over hours, however. Other Traits Ability Scores - STR +3, DEX -1, LUK +0, INT +1, WIS +3, CHA +0 Languages - Sylvan, Druidic, Common, Ent Greensight Speed - 25 Hide in Plain Sight - Forested terrain only. Deific Boons - friend of the forest, The Forest has Ears Feats - Divine Interference, Maneuver Versatility Skills - +8 +Perception, +8 +Insight, +5 +Nature, +5 +History +8 +Athletics

entries.title: Shambling Shroom
entries.content: A hunchback with fungus growing all over its back limps around, leaving toxic debris in the air wherever it goes. It its hand it holds a handful of sinister looking mushrooms. Level 4 Large Plant/Undead Defense Traits | AC | 20 | | | ------------------------------------------------- | ------------------------------------------------- | ------- | | HP | 68 | Regen 3 | | Poise | 30 | | | Saves | Fort +9 (stalwart), Refl +3, Will +4 | | Resistance - Poison, Toxin, Venom, Necrotic Regeneration - Stopped by Bright Light Offense Traits (DC 16) | Corrupting Touch (necrotic) | +7 | | | --------------------------------------------------------------------- | -------- | --------------------- | | | 3d6 (10) | +drain 3 | | Unarmed (C) | +7 | | | | d8+3 (7) | | Innate Techniques - Mana Scorch, Tentacles for Arms, Grasp of the Undying 1/day - Colossal Smash, Ants in your Pants, Mind Maze Constant - Mana Drain Spores Abound Necrotic Persistent - A shambling shroom's greatest calling is to plant mushrooms wherever it goes. To do so, it is always on the move, leaving behind spores that linger in spaces it moves through for 1 minute before settling. As a swift action, a shambling shroom can shake its body to cause them to erupt around it, leaving behind a cloud within its natural reach. Whenever a creature moves into a space occupied by these spores, they take root and start quickly growing. Undead creatures are unharmed by this process. Any other creature takes 2d6 (7) necrotic damage and is Sickened 1 (fort halves dmg and negates sicken). The sickened condition is considered Permanent Status until the creature washes, burns, or uses first aid to get them off or steps in sunlight. Weaknesses/Deep Lore Sunlight Bane - When in natural sunlight, a shroom shambler can't use its spores abound or corrupting touch abilities. Other Traits Ability Scores - STR +3, DEX -1, LUK +0, INT +0, WIS +0, CHA -1 Languages - Common, Sylvan, Necril Speed - 25 Feats - Back to the Wall, Diehard Skills - +6 +Perception, +9 +Athletics, +6 +Nature, +6 Alchemy

entries.title: Devil Shroom
entries.content: This mushroom has arms with clawed hands and horns that jut from its mushroom cap. It seems to ride a vortex of flame around and its more than happy to share. Level 9 Plant/Fiend Defense Traits | AC | 23 | | | ------------------------------------------------- | --------------------------- | --- | | HP | 74 | | | Poise | 28 | | | Saves | Fort +9, Refl +12, Will +12 | | Immunity - Fire Offense Traits (DC 21) | Claw (S) | +12 | | | ------------------------------------------------------------- | ---------- | --------------------------------- | | | d6+3 (6) | | | Plasma Skull (Fire) | +12 | | | | d6+13 (16) | +glowing and bone chilled | Innate Techniques (CL 13+3 fire) - Ice Lance, Bouncing Blast, Finger Gun 1/day - Self-Destruct, Life Drinker Constant(?) - Plasma Blade Blaze It - All elemental damage the devil shroom deals is automatically converted to fire damage. Trailblazer - All spaces a devil shroom moves through with its hover speed are set on fire. Creatures moving through such spaces are set Ablaze (reflex negates). Special Snowflake - A devil shroom benefits from the empowered version of its Ice Lance technique against creatures that are either Glowing or ablaze. Devouring Flames - When using its Plasma Blade attack, a devil shroom can choose to inflict Bone Chilled instead of glowing. Weaknesses/Deep Lore Brakes - When Wet, the devil shroom cannot use its hover speed. Sealed Soul - A devil shroom gains the Soul Cage Medium ability but it can be used as an instant action at no cost. It manifests itself as a the flaming skull it uses to make attacks with its Plasma Blade. If that technique is ever dispelled, or the devil shroom ceases Concentration, the soul is freed and the devil shroom must capture a new soul to use its Plasma Blade. The soul may choose to linger briefly to thank its rescuers and curse its warden. Other Traits Ability Scores - STR +0, DEX +3, LUK +1, INT +3, WIS +1, CHA -1 Languages - Sylvan, Common, Elven Nightvision Speed - 20, 45 Hover Signature Skill - stalker, dampen presence Deific Boons - spider climb Feats - MM Sniping, Bullseye, Skill Focus, Signature Skill, Point Blank Master Skills - +12 +Perception, +16 (adv) +Stealth, +13 Arcana, +13 +Planes

entries.title: Mushemoth
entries.content: The terrain comes to life underneath your feet as a log covered with mushrooms peels from the forest floor. Nearby some large mushrooms angle upwards to reveal a pair of glowing eyes amid the fungus. Level 9 Huge Plant/Giant Defense Traits | AC | 24 | | | ------------------------------------------------- | --------------------------- | --- | | HP | 92 | | | Poise | 32 | | | Saves | Fort +15, Refl +8, Will +12 | | Swell Recharge - By immersing itself in water, a mushemoth can absorb water as a free action. For 1 round, any creatures striking with a Melee Attack are targeted with a Bully attack from a water spray. This can also happen if the mushemoth becomes Wet. Offense Traits (DC 21) | Unarmed (C) | +13 | | | ----------------------------------------------------- | ---------- | ------------------------------- | | | 2d8+4 (13) | | | Vine (C) | +13 | 25 ft reach during its turn | | | d6+4 (17) | | Multiattack - 2 Attacks Innate Techniques (CL 11) - Living Roots, Stubbed Toe, Decomposition, Life Transfer, Colossal Smash, Awesome Blow 1/day - String Shot, Vile Vigor, Roundhouse Constant - Vine Whip Artillery Mage - 15 ft Quicken Spell - Living Roots, Stubbed Toe, Decomposition Carve Earth Earth - A mushemoth can dig its arms into dirt, sand, mud or clay, scooping them together or turning 180 degrees. - Scoop Together - Creatures within 15 ft in a semicircle take 9d6 (31) crush damage and an equal amount of Poise damage (reflex halves). Poise broken creatures are dragged in front of the mushemoth and Entangled. Creature can Breakout as a standard action. This also creates a wall 15 ft long, 15 ft high and 10 ft wide of loose material. - Scoop Around - Creatures within 15 ft take 9d6 (31) crush damage and an equal amount of Poise damage (reflex halves). Poise broken creatures are dragged 180 degrees around the mushemoth and Entangled. Creature can Breakout as a standard action. This also creates a wall 15 ft long, 10 ft high and 5 ft long of loose material on opposite sides of the mushemoth. Special Attack: Sand Attack Earth - Scrape up dirt and fling it at a specific creature or at many. - Specific - Make a Ranged Dirty Trick with a +8 bonus against a creature within 60 ft. Instead of the usual effect, it blinds the creature. The creature must somehow wash the dirt from their eyes to remove this condition. - Many - Make a Dirty Trick that targets creatures in a 20 ft cone. Instead of the usual effect, it blinds the creature. The creature must somehow wash the dirt from their eyes to remove this condition. Seeds Abound Healing Recharge - A mushemoth can scatter its seeds over all creatures within its natural reach. They immediately take root (fort negates). At the start of a seeded creatures turn, it is Drained 5 and if the mushemoth is within 60 ft, it restores 5 hp. These seeds die when the mushemoth is defeated, when exposed to direct sunlight or with first aid. This ability cannot affect Beasts and is twice as effective against Undead. Weaknesses/Deep Lore Furry Sympathizer - A mushemoth has Disadvantage on attacks made against Beasts. When in combat, they prioritize all other foes if possible. Other Traits Ability Scores - STR +4, DEX -1, LUK +2, INT +0, WIS +3, CHA -1 Languages - Sylvan, Druidic, Giant Nightvision +Blindsense - Tremorsense (30 ft) Speed - 25 Hide in Plain Sight - Forested/mushroom terrain only. Deific Boons - friend of the forest, The Forest has Ears, Feats - Maneuver Master, Sentinel, Combat Reflexes, Unarmed Combatant, Weapon Focus (crush) Skills - +14 +Perception, +14 +Medicine, +11 +Nature, +15 +Athletics ![mushhemoth.jpg|[ Kevin McKenna]]

entries.title: Procrastinating Spirit
entries.content: The ghost of a creature hangs its head as it whispers to itself, its words self-defeating. Grabbing its attention only seems to set it off. Level 0 Medium Undead Defense Traits | AC | 13 | | | ------------------------------------------------- | ------------------------- | --- | | HP | 20 | | | Poise | 21 | | | Saves | Fort -1, Refl +5, Will +0 | | Incorporeal Flyby Attack - After successfully landing an attack or draining hope from a creature, a spirits movement is elusive from its target. Offense Traits (DC 12) | Ghost Touch (Cold or Necrotic) | +3 | | | ------------------------------------------------------------------------ | ------- | --- | | | 2d6 (7) | | Innate Techniques - Icicle, Grasp of the Undying Drain Hope Su - Plague a creature within reach with regret and sorrow of a spirit yet to pass. The creature takes 2d6 (7) psychic damage and is shaken 1 (will halves dmg and negates shaken) Weaknesses/Deep Lore Fear the Styx - Procrastinating spirits fear the finality of their deaths, and seek to delay their journey into the Underworld whenever possible. Exposure to water from the river Styx immediately destroys a procrastinating spirit and sends it to the Underworld. Other Traits Ability Scores - STR -1, DEX +3, LUK +0, INT +0, WIS +0, CHA +0 Languages - Common Darkvision Speed - 30 Hover. During the spirits turn it can be used to fly Feats - Bullseye Skills - +2 +Perception

entries.title: Skeleton
entries.content: A pile of bones animates and rattles at you menacingly, wielding whatever was on hand to dispatch you. Level 0 Medium Undead Defense Traits | AC | 13 (light armor) | | | ------------------------------------------------- | ------------------------- | --- | | HP | 25 | | | Poise | 20 | | | Saves | Fort +4, Refl +2, Will +0 | | Resistance - Necrotic, pierce +Shield Actions (if not wielding spear) Offense Traits (DC 12) | Claws (S) | +2 | | | --------------------------------------------------------- | --------- | ------------------ | | | 2d6+2 (9) | -2 Disarm | | Rusty Spear (P) | +2 | Improved Reach | | | 2d8+2 (9) | | Weaknesses/Deep Lore Disarmed - When a skeleton is disarmed, one of its arms falls off instead of the usual effect. Its claw damage is reduced by two with only one arm. It cannot use claw attacks with no arms. A skeleton can pick up and reattach one of its arms as a standard action. Weakness - Crush 2 Other Traits Ability Scores - STR +2, DEX +2, LUK +0, INT -, WIS +0, CHA +0 Old Habits - Skeletons are often created from the bones left behind from failed adventures. They can be found wandering around dungeons looking for loot. If any such loot exists in the form of a weapon/armor and is accessible inside of the dungeon, replace the skeletons equipment with the loot. Gear - 1x light armor, 1x heavy shield, 1x rusty spear Hide in Plain Sight Nightvision Spookyness - +2 Speed - 40 Land Speed Feats - Toughness Skills - +2 +Perception

entries.title: Zombie
entries.content: A walking corpse shambles towards you. Its soulless gaze meets yours and you can feel its hunger. Level 0 Medium Undead Defense Traits | AC | 16 | | | ------------------------------------------------- | ------------------------- | --- | | HP | 25 | | | Poise | 25 | | | Saves | Fort +5, Refl -1, Will +0 | | Resistance - Necrotic, crush Undead Fortitude - The first time this creature would defeated, it is instead reduced to 1 hp unless it is defeated by holy damage or a crit. Offense Traits (DC 12) | Bite (P) | +3 | | | -------------------------------------------------- | --------------------- | ---------- | | | d8+3+d8 necrotic (12) | +Infection | Infection - Creatures that take damage from the zombie are inflicted with a T1 affliction (fort negates). Boils - A bunch of puss filled boils slowly form on your skin. At the end of each day, you become drained 5. If this kills the creature by reducing its max hp to 0 or the creature otherwise dies while this infection affects them, they rise as a zombie a few minutes later. Cure - Application of holy water to the affected area immediately dissolves the boils, removing the affliction, but not any drain it inflicted. entries.title: Body Bagger
entries.content: An undead beastfolk with a good nose sniffs around the graveyard. It starts to dig, throwing whatever it finds inside of its sack before it picks up your scent and smiles. Level 1 Medium Undead/Beast Defense Traits | AC | 15 | | | ------------------------------------------------- | ------------------------- | --- | | HP | 30 | | | Poise | 28 | | | Saves | Fort +4, Refl +5, Will +2 | | Resistance - Necrotic Offense Traits (DC 14) | Body Bag (C) | +4 | +2 Drag, +4 MV | | ------------------------------------------------------ | ---------- | -------------------------------------------- | | | 2d8+3 (12) | | | Bite (P) | +4 | +4 MV | | | 2d6+3 (10) | | Multiattack - Make a drag attack and a Melee Attack. Innate Techniques (CL 0) - Kidnap 1/day - Blight Bag 'em and Drag 'em - When a body bagger succeeds on a drag attempt, they snatch the creature up in their durable leather body bag. The creature is entangled while encased in this bag. They can escape by successfully disarming the body bagger. Anything else that could reasonably separate trapped creatures from the bag can also free them. If a body bagger has something already in its bag, it cannot put another creature inside. Body baggers can empty their bag as a swift action. Bag - A body bagger typically has their bag full of corpses to loot for their masters. When full of bodies, they can use it as a weapon. When there is a living creature inside of the bag, they get access to the following Special Attack. Special Attack: Bag 'em and Whack 'em - Make a Melee Attack with the body bag. The target and the creature in the bag take damage from this attack. Weaknesses/Deep Lore Even Trade - Body baggers can be convinced to leave a creature alone for bits and pieces of recently deceased creatures. They won't accept rotten or skeletal remains. Any deal made with such items gives a creature advantage on skill checks with them. Weakness - Slash 2 Other Traits Ability Scores - STR +3, DEX +2, LUK +0, INT -1, WIS +1, CHA +0 Senses - Scent (10 ft), Nightvision Speed - 35 Land Speed Feats - Maneuver Versatility Skills - +4 (+4 scent) +Perception, +6 (+2 tracking) +Survival, +6 +Athletics, +5 +Stealth

entries.title: River Spirit
entries.content: A soft spoken young woman swims around a lake, offering you a spot amongst those having fun inside. The glamer removed once you hop in, you see a pile of corpses reaching to grab you. Level 1 Medium Undead/Fey Boss Defense Traits | AC | 16 | | | ------------------------------------------------- | ------------------------- | --- | | HP | 52 | | | Poise | 25 | | | Saves | Fort +0, Refl +3, Will +3 | | Resistance - Necrotic Recovery Bound - River spirits are bound to the body of water they perished in and cannot be forcefully removed from it. They also cannot willingly leave it. Washed Away recharge elusive - As a swift action, a river spirit can cause a wave of water to push itself or an ally within 30 ft. It pushes up to 30 feet in a straight line. Beneath the Waves - As long as its attacker is not immersed in water, gains resistance to ALL damage from said creature. Offense Traits (DC 14) | Caress | +3 | | | ---------------------------------------------------------- | -------------------- | ------------- | | | Drain 6 | +Fatigued | | Waterbending (C) | +5 | 30 ft range | | | 2d6+2 (9) | | Innate Techniques (CL 5) - Pollinate, Bone Shaker, Decomposition 1/day - Mind Crush, Wild Growth, Blight, Unnatural Lust Pool Party glamer - A river spirit is surrounded by the victims it kills. From outside of the water the victims and the spirit appear as though they have taken on aquatic forms and are enjoying life beneath the surface of the water. For creatures that are inside of the water, the skeletal victims reach for them and pull them under. The victims cannot be attacked. They deal 17 poise damage each turn persistent. If poise broken, the creature is grappled. A creature can only hold their breath for half the usual time (typically 2 rounds, or 4 with +3 STR) and starts drowning if they are grappled for too long. Boss Actions - Usable 1/turn Jig is Up (recharge) companion- Once the jig is up and creatures realize the true nature of the river spirit, she lets 2 skeletal victims walk free, functioning as skeletons. They gain the river spirit's +Teamwork Feats, a 40 ft swim speed and a +4 bonus to grapple attacks. Rattle 'em Boys - Command all companion skeletons to make a Simple Attack. Water's Caress elusive - Move up to 20 feet. If there is a grappled creature within reach during this movement, automatically land a caress attack. Weaknesses/Deep Lore Temptation charm - A river spirit has an aura that compels creature to approach the water's edge (will negates). Each turn a creature stands next to the water, they are unknowingly drained 4 as it caresses them. The effects of the drain are not apparent to onlookers or the creature until the river spirits hold is broken. Each turn a tempted creature may attempt a new will save to realize what is happening If creatures that are not tempted happen to pass a +Religion check, they automatically learn of this quality. Weakness - Crush 2 Other Traits Ability Scores - STR -1, DEX +2, LUK +2, INT +2, WIS +0, CHA +3 Nightvision Speed - 40 Swim Feats - Escape Route, Combat Step Skills - +3 +Perception, +10 +Deception, +5 +Local, +5 +Cryptozoology

entries.title: Chrimbus Spirit Snatcher
entries.content: An ghostly figure fades through the wall and starts snatching up objects around the room and throwing them into its bag. It's abdomen opens wide like a mouth and lets out a cackle before it escapes with its plunder. Level 3 Medium Undead Defense Traits | AC | 16 | | | ------------------------------------------------- | ------------------------- | --- | | HP | 44 | | | Poise | 23 | | | Saves | Fort +4, Refl +6, Will +5 | | Resistance - Necrotic Incorporeal Offense Traits (DC 14) | Disarmed Weapon (?) | +8 | | | ------------------------------------------------------------- | ---------- | ---------------- | | | ? | | | Bite (P) | +6 | +4 MI, +8 disarm | | | 2d6+3 (10) | | Innate Techniques (CL 3 +2 if stolen focus) - Denied, Hidden Power, Dispel Magic 1/day - Backstab Constant - Blur, Heart Seeker Signature Skill - Is...this your sword Rain On Your Parade (Water) - A spirit snatcher can cause water to fall upon creatures within 20 ft of it, making them Wet. Special Attack: Frostbite (Frost) - Make a bite attack that deals an additional 2d6 (7) cold damage On-hit the creature becomes Entangled, but can Breakout as a swift action. They can escape automatically as a standard action. Shadow Punch (Necrotic) - As a Special Attack against a DD target, a spirit snatcher can choose to plunge its free hand into a creature's body, dealing 4d6 (14) necrotic damage and making them fatigued On-hit. Weaknesses/Deep Lore Part of Me - A spirit snatcher's arcane bag is part of its body, and therefore cannot store anything particularly dangerous like pointy weapons or +Toxins. In particular, if a creature places a lump of coal in the bag (through SoH, poise breaking or as an attack), the spirit snatcher takes 10 psychic damage. Other Traits Ability Scores - STR +1, DEX +3, LUK -1, INT +0, WIS +0, CHA +0 Arcane Bag - Functions as the arcane pocket technique, but it takes up a hand and cannot be disarmed. entries.title: el-stitchisimo
entries.content: El Stitchisimo Level 4 Large Undead \[unique] Defense Traits AC 17\ HP 61\ Poise 26\ Fort +9, Refl +4, Will +6 Immune - Poison Resistance - Necrotic Order Up \[healing] \[recharge] - Stitches can launch food at his allies to heal them. He comes equipped with 1 meal for the living, and one snack for the dead. He can launch this food up to 30 feet as a swift action, and it heals 10 hp. Munchies \[healing] - Any time Stitches lands a bite attack or devours an enemy, he heals 5 hp. Offense Traits (DC 16) Ham Bat (C+P) +10\ d10+3 (8) Bite (P) +8\ 2d6+3 (10) Multiattack - 1 ham bat attack and 1 bite attack Innate Techniques - Blood for Blood\ 1/day - Ups and Downs, Jaws of the Beast Hook, Line and Sinker \[recharge] - Stitches can throw his hook at a creature within 30 ft. If he also spends his swift action to reach, its range increases to 60 ft. The creature is pulled adjacent to him and takes 4d6 (14) slashing damage (reflex halves dmg and negates pull). Devour \[recharge] - Stitches can consume an ally within reach to put them in stasis for 1 round, using the ability like this does not have the recharge tag. Stitches can also consume an enemy automatically for 1 round.  During this round, stitches can move around freely. At the end of Stitches' next turn, he can choose to deposit them in any adjacent space. Putrid Bile \[recharge] \[poison] \[persistent] - When Stitches dashes, he can choose to unleash a toxic concoction. For the remainder of his turn, any space he moves into and all adjacent spaces are coated in a toxic and corrosive goo. This area is treated as difficult terrain. Creatures within this area take 4d8 (18) poison damage (fort halves). Weaknesses/Deep Lore Hands Full - Stitches has multiple abilities that are tied to objects he wields. He cannot use his hook or his pan if he is disarmed, disabling hook, line and sinker and order up respectively. Hungry Hungry Stitches - By breaking Stitches' poise, a creature can shove an object into his mouth-like abdomen in addition to any other effects. Other Traits Ability Scores - Str +3, Dex +0, Lucc +0, Int -1, Wis +2, Cha +1 Nightvision Feats - Toughness, Unstoppable Skills - +8 perception, +8 profession (cook), +9 athletics, +5 alchemy Stitches, as he appears in Heroes of the Storm

entries.title: nameless-hoard
entries.content: Nameless Hoard Level 4 Massive Undead \[group] Defense Traits AC 14\ HP 68\ Poise -\ Fort +9, Refl +3, Will +4 Single Target Resistance - Resistance to any damage that only affects a single creature, most common with attack rolls. Certain attacks like cleave can ignore this resistance. Single target effects that deal no damage have no effect. Poise Immune - Cannot be poise broken Offense Traits (DC 16) Auto Attack (P) \ 2d6+3 (10) or 23 poise damage to attempt a grapple Multiattack - Special: Can only be used after feasting\ Make an auto attack to deal pierce damage and to grapple. Feast - The nameless hoard consumes all. It can move over a dead creature and rapidly tear it to shreds, including other undead creatures. Any round the hoard does this, they can move an additional 40 feet to continue the frenzy. They also get access to a multiattack during any turn they feast. Grasp of the Starved - Creatures grappled by a nameless hoard are automatically dragged into its center. The hoard takes no penalty to actions or movement. The grappled creature are carried along with the hoard and automatically take its auto attack each turn (including the turn they initiate the grapple). The hoard can move over the space of a knocked down creature. While the hoard does so, the creature cannot lose the knocked down condition. Infection - Creatures that take damage from the hoard are inflicted with a T1 affliction (fort negates). Boils - A bunch of puss filled boils slowly form on your skin. At the end of each day, you become drained 1. If this kills the creature by reducing its max hp to 0 or the creature otherwise dies while this infection affects them, they rise as a zombie a few minutes later. Cure - Application of holy water to the affected area immediately dissolves the boils, removing the affliction, but not any drain it inflicted. Weaknesses/Deep Lore AoE Vulnerability - Take 50% more damage from any AoE effect Weakness - Holy 3 Other Traits Ability Scores - Str +3, Dex -1, Lucc +0, Int -, Wis +0, Cha -1 Nightvision Skills - +7 perception Cannibalize, Hearthstone

Resistance - Necrotic Offense Traits (DC 17) Longbow (P) +10\ d8+3 (7) Peck (P) +8\ d8+3 (7) Multiattack - Make 3 ranged attacks Innate Techniques - Flying Arrow, Pin Down\ 1/day - Rain of Arrows, Silence Sigil of Vigilance (Su) \[sigil] - As a standard action, a gloom stalker can place a sigil on the ground at a point within 60 ft that it can see. When an enemy moves anywhere within 20 ft of the sigil, the gloom stalker reflexively shoots them, provided they have a ranged weapon with which to do so and can see the target. They get a +2 bonus to these reflexive attacks if made with high ground. Special Attack: Serrated Arrow - This arrow is embedded in a creature in such a way that it impedes movement, making a creature clumsy 1. Can be removed as a swift action. Special Attack: Silencing Strike Weaknesses/Deep Lore Weakness - Holy 3 Birdcalls - By rolling a disguise check (DC 20) as a swift action, you can fake bird calls to fascinate a gloom stalker that can hear them for 1 round. Can only be attempted once. You get advantage on this disguise check if you are hiding in a tree. Other Traits Ability Scores - Str +0, Dex +3, Lucc +0, Int +0, Wis +3, Cha +0 Nightvision Wall Cling - Extra limbs afford a gloom stalker the ability to shoot its bow while climbing, but it loses an attack from its multiattack if it does so Speed - 30 climb Equipment - 1 longbow, Light Armor Feats - Suppressing Fire, SF Perception, SS Perception Skills - +10 (adv) perception, +12 survival, +10 stealth, +12 sleight of hand, +10 acrobatics, +7 nature By Orfii

entries.title: life-drinker
entries.content: Life Drinker Level 6 Medium Undead Defense Traits AC 21 (medium armor)\ HP 103\ Poise 34 (DR 20 vs grapple)\ Fort +7, Refl +9, Will +8 Resistance - Necrotic Offense Traits (DC 18) Vampiric Touch (Necrotic) +9\ 3d4+6 (13) Innate Techniques - 1/day - Asphyxiate Grasp of the Undying - Any creature grappled by a life drinker or its hands takes 10 necrotic damage and is bone chilled for 1 turn. Can also be done as a maintain option. Handy (Su) - A life drainer is surrounded by the spectral hands of the dead, and can command them to grapple their enemies with a +10 bonus (applying effects of maneuver initiate and master as normal).  * Creatures that end their turn within reach of a life drainer are automatically subject to a grapple.  * A Life drainer can summon a hand that immediately grapples a creature as a swift action. * When a life drinker dies, a hand spawns in its space and adjacent to its killer, and both immediately attempt to grapple a creature The hands act autonomously and do not impede the movement of the life drinker. They threaten, cannot move and act on the life drinkers turn. A hand cannot be killed, but by making a shove or drag attack it can be shoved back into or pulled out of the ground automatically, causing them to disappear. Vampiric Touch - Instead of a traditional attack, a life drinker drains the life from its foes with melee touches, dealing necrotic damage and healing equal to damage dealt. Grasp of the Starved \[recharge] - Target a point within 30 ft. Make a grapple attack with a +14 bonus against all enemies within 10 ft of that point as numerous hands reach to grab them. Any creature grappled has a hand linger to maintain it while the rest recede. Weaknesses/Deep Lore Weakness - Holy 3 Other Traits Ability Scores - Str +1, Dex +3, Lucc +0, Int +2, Wis +0, Cha +2 Nightvision Retrieve \[concentration] - A life drinker can use its hands to remotely interact with objects it can see, even retrieving them and appearing back next to it. Doing anything other than grappling requires a standard action each round to concentrate on the actions of the hands. Equipment - Medium Armor Feats - Toughness, Maneuver Initiate, Maneuver Master Skills - +8 perception, +11 stealth, +13 sleight of hand, +10 religion, +10 local, +10 arcana, +10 cryptozoology Lost Exarch, Hearthstone

entries.title: Navigating the Bestiary
entries.content: The bestiary is arranged first by the creatures type. Some creatures have multiple types and I decided to put them in the camp they felt more at home in. Some creature types inherently have qualities, other just have a general vibe. Feel free to mix and match or add more! Some monsters will have tags listed at the top of their entry. There are general tags, and some specific to creature types. Below is a graph showcasing how many of each creature type exists and how many of the pages are crusty (meaning they are either incomplete or not formatted properly). This is mostly for me for organizational purposes | Creature Type | # of Creatures | # of Crusty Pages | | ----------------------------------------------------------- | ------------------------------------------------------------ | ------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | | Aberrations | 11 | 0 | | Beasts | 23 | 0 (maybe roadrunner?) | | Celestials | 2 | 0 | | Constructs | 11 | 1 | | Cryptids | 5 | 0 | | Dragons | 7 | 0 | | Elementals | 13 | 0 (the gems are a bit light on stuff to do, quartzite crusher in particular could use some sauce) | | Fey | 8 | 0 | | Fiends | 20 | 2 | | Giants | 7 | 0 | | Humanoids | 10 | 1 | | Inevitable | 0 | 0 | | Oozes | 3 | 2 | | Plants | 6 | 1 | | Undead | 11 | 7 | | TOTAL | 147 | 14 |

entries.title: Aquatic Combat
entries.content: This refers to combat that happens in and around water, but typically not completely submerged. As far as visibility is concerned, most of the time you can see to the bottom of lakes and rivers unless they are particularly murky. Oceans, seas and particularly wide lakes or rivers are the exception to this, where they can be deep enough that even in ideal conditions you can't see all the way down. When a creature not in water attacks another creature that is at least waist deep in water, they have Cover. Creatures not in water cannot attack creatures that are completely submerged if they are more than 5 feet deep.

entries.title: Drowning
entries.content: Most fit creatures can pretty handily hold their breath for 30 seconds while under duress (or 5 rounds). For every point of STR you can hold it for an additional round. Every round past this limit inflicts drain. The first round you drain 4, the second drain 8, 12 etc, causing you to rapidly die if you are unable to somehow breathe. With specific equipment, magic or training, or being aquatic/amphibious, you can increase or eliminate this limit entirely. The most common way is Gillweed.

entries.title: Underwater Combat
entries.content: Fighting underwater can make many fighting styles completely useless. Projectile weapons like guns can't fire at all. Thrown weapons don't function at all either. Bows and such have their range reduced to 10/20 ft. Melee combatants take a -4 penalty to Attack unless they use a piercing weapon. Many of these things have workarounds. Certain types of weapons can be specifically designed for underwater use. Aquatic creatures, amphibious creatures and creatures trained in +Athletics figure out methods to ignore the melee attacking penalty.

entries.title: Water Pressure
entries.content: Roughly every 30 ft you descend into water, you double the amount of pressure your body experiences. Going past 150 ft is usually impossible without being an aquatic creature, or using special equipment/magic. If you do not have such aid, then going deeper than 60 ft is usually a bad idea. In combat you don't have the luxury of slowly ascending to avoid the bends. If you ascend more than 5 feet a round after diving to this depth you become drained 4 and dazed (fort reduces to drained 2). The DC is 20+every additional 5 feet you moved (10 feet DC 21, 20 ft DC 23 etc).

entries.title: +Environmental Hazards
