entries.content: What happens when you fall in lava? What does altitude sickness do? Extreme heat? Geysers? There are all kinds of things that aren't exactly creatures. It would be a tragedy if you had to figure out what happens yourself. This section is filled with all kinds of things that can pose a threat to the squishy humanoids ill prepared for what mother earth has in store for them. Note: Many creatures you encounter will be adapted to the environment they are in. You should probably not bother having lava affect creatures that are made of fire or obsidian for instance. These are soft immunities that are not included in the creature's stat block and should be granted based on vibes.

entries.title: Fog
entries.content: Fog, mist, dust, smoke and other dense air particles with occasionally fill or pepper the battlefield, obscuring vision. The extent to which varies. I have simplified the condition by just naming it fog. Solid Fog - Solid fog is the most common form of fog created by creature's special abilities. Solid fog gives creatures beyond 5 ft Total Concealment. Even adjacent creatures still have Partial Concealment. Standard Fog - Standard fog is generally the more common environmental effect. Rather than a specific range, I will separate categories with specific penalties assigned to them. You can assign a distance, like 120 ft, or 60 or 30 on particularly foggy days. I will use 120 ft as my example. First Increment (0-120 ft) - The fog obscures vision, applying a -4 penalty to +Perception. Second Increment (125-240ft) - People can easily fade away in the fog. Grants Partial Concealment. Third Increment (245+ ft) - Nothing beyond this can be seen. Grants Total Concealment.

entries.title: Frosty Terrain
entries.content: Adventures bring creatures to all kinds of places, including those in the far northern reaches of the world. This should help you deal with things like snow and ice peppering or covering your battles. Snow Light snow usually not too cumbersome. To an average person it doesn't even submerge their shoes. Light snow only inflicts movement penalties on creatures who are small or smaller, who treat it as difficult terrain. Heavy Snow Heavy snow is very cumbersome. Usually at least a foot deep, heavy snow counts as difficult terrain unless you are huge or larger. Creatures who are small enough can Hide inside of snow by burrowing inside it. Ice See Slippery Surfaces

entries.title: Gravity
entries.content: Most of the time, gravity should not be much of an issue, but in particularly strange scenarios gravity can change to be more or less intense. This section should help you navigate such scenarios. Low Gravity Low gravity lets creatures move vertically a lot more freely. It no longer requires a swift action in order to take the Jump action and creatures can automatically jump as far and as high as a creature's Land Speed. Low gravity also negates Fall Damage. Whenever you force a creature to move in low gravity, such as when you Shove a creature, the distance you move them is increased by 10 feet. No Gravity Low gravity can be very complicated. Many features of the game assume gravity, y'know, exists. The combination of gravity and friction are the foundation of how we understand basic physics. In the absence of gravity, all momentum is practically infinite. If you push yourself, or a creature pushes you, off of a surface, you will continue to move in that direction until acted upon by another force. This can potentially lead to scenarios where creatures unintentionally float off into directions much further than they would like. Actions that require deliberate force, such as a Shove attack, also have an equal an opposite effect on the shover. All forced movement is increased by 20 ft. This forced movement becomes a Persistent effect that triggers each round without another force at play. All Land Speed is changed to 30 ft. If you move in a direction, you require anchors in order move in anything other than a straight line. Some locations may still allow you to use Climb speeds. Fly speeds require air in order to function and thus do not work in the vacuum of space. Zero gravity in aquatic scenarios typically won't have an impact unless it is activated near the water's surface, but otherwise, Swim speeds should function as normal. High Gravity High gravity restricts movement and affects how much you can lift. Creature's with low STR will be more impeded than others. All creatures with movement impeded by high gravity are Grounded. Tier 1 - Creatures with -1 STR or less have all of their speeds halved. All creatures can only lift/manipulate 50% as much. Tier 2 - Creatures with +1 STR or less have all their speeds halved. Creatures with -1 or less STR must roll a DC 10 STR Ability Check each round to move at all. All creatures can only lift/manipulate a 20% as much. Tier 3 - Creatures with +3 STR or less have all of their speeds halved. Creatures with -1 or less STR must roll a DC 15 STR ability check each round to move at all. Creatures with +1 or less must roll a DC 10 check. All creatures can only lift/manipulate 10% as much. The distance of all forced movement is halved unless the creature initiating it is unaffected by the speed penalty of the high gravity. Yes I understand this means muscular psionics are better at telekinesis in high gravity. Why don't YOU try and lift a staff when suddenly it weighs 10x as much. You can create more extreme scenarios by continuing to increase these numbers in the same linear fashion, but tier 3 is already crippling almost all creatures.

entries.title: Lava
entries.content: Molten rock and metal. Lava is most commonly found around volcanoes, but can also be found erupting from the ocean floor and flowing through deep underground caverns. Lava typically comes in 2 variations; semisolid and liquid. Both are considered persistent effects. Semisolid Lava - Semisolid lava feels a bit like chunky mud if it melted your toes. Semisolid lava is difficult terrain, and every turn you move through it, you take 4d6 (14) fire damage. Liquid Lava - Liquid lava is much more dangerous since it can pool into large enough quantities for you to get completely submerged in. Each round you are submerged in lava, you take 10d6 (35) fire damage and are drained 5. Partial submersion does 6d6 (21) fire and drains 2. If you survive that and manage to escape, lava still clings to you, burning you while it cools. This damage starts at 4d6 (14), then decreases by d6 each round until it ends. By taking cold damage, getting hit with a water effect or otherwise rapidly cooling or removing the clinging lava, you can avoid this damage over time. If you are only partially submerged, like if it is only waist deep, the initial damage is reduced to 6d6 (21) fire, and the cling damage starts at 3d6 (10) fire.

entries.title: Slippery Surfaces
entries.content: Ice, oil, wet floors and the lot. Slippery surface can cause a problem for clumsy individuals. By treating such surfaces as difficult terrain, any further penalties can be avoided. Attempting to move at full speed requires a DC 10 +Acrobatics check to Balance. Failure inflicts Knockdown. Attempting to Jump into or off of a slippery surface also requires a check (but no more than one check is needed per round). Some abilities create slippery surfaces. These use the creature's DC instead of the base 10 listed here. Any forced movement against a creature standing on a slippery surface is increased by 5 ft.

entries.title: Sticky Surfaces
entries.content: Tar, quicksand, webs, sap and the like. Sticky surface can cause problems for everybody, but particularly for those that are untrained in +Athletics. Moving into a sticky surface inflicts the Entangled condition unless the creature succeeds on a DC 10 check athletics check to breakout. This can only happen once a turn. The entangled condition can be removed with breakout as a swift action. Some abilities create sticky surfaces. These use the creature's DC instead of the base 10 listed here. They also may require a different action to escape. In any case, some things can reasonably free stuck creatures like slashing someone free from a web.

entries.title: Amorphous
entries.content: In order to crit the creature, you must exceed its AC by a value higher than 10. Example: Amorphous - +15

entries.title: Cat's Grace
entries.content: You fall gracefully, taking 1 damage per 10 ft you fall. You cannot be drained from falling.

entries.title: Evasion
entries.content: Take half damage from Reflex saving throws. When you pass your save, instead negate the effect entirely. Other versions of this ability exist for Fortitude saves (stalwart), and Will saves (iron will).

entries.title: Fast Healing
entries.content: Tags: Healing The creature gains specified amount of hp the start of their turn if they are not defeated. When the creature is defeated, this ability is disabled for 10 minutes (see prevent recovery for means to keep those pesky healers down). If a creature with FH is Bleeding, roll a luck check (DC 10) to see if the bleeding stops at the end of their turns.

entries.title: Ferocity
entries.content: When the creature is reduced to zero hit points, they are not defeated until the end of their next turn. On said turn they are staggered. If a creature with ferocity is healed before their next turn, the creature is not defeated at the end of its next turn, and this ability is not consumed, however they are still staggered. This ability can only activate once per minute. FH does not function while in ferocity. Many effects in the game have some type of bonus effect on defeat, such as the bonus special attacks granted by the Unarmed Combatant feat, Aran or the Feast technique. These effects trigger when you would have defeated an enemy, but they have ferocity. Effects that trigger when you are defeated still activate after the ferocity ends, like Diehard's retaliatory attack, or Caustic Surprise's after defeat effect. Note - The wording is key. If a creature uses its actions on its ferocity turn to heal itself, the ability is still consumed. They do still stave off defeat however.

entries.title: Incorporeal
entries.content: Incorporeal creatures have resistance to crush, slash and pierce damage. They cannot be targeted with a combat maneuver without special equipment or techniques. Incorporeal creatures universally have hover speeds. Incorporeal creatures can pass through physical objects, but cannot end their turn inside of one. This allows them to ignore cover bonuses and more easily hide.

entries.title: Magic Resist
entries.content: Referred to as MR. Functions just like DR, reducing damage taken by a flat value, but it only functions against Sp or Su abilities.

entries.title: Recovery
entries.content: At the start of the creature's turn, if creature is still suffering from an effect that affected it last turn, it ends the effect. Does not include Permanent Status or being held down.

entries.title: Regeneration
entries.content: Tags: Healing The creature regains a specified amount of +HP at the start of their turn and ends all bleed effects before taking damage. This healing is capable of restoring lost limbs over a few days, or instantly if the limb is reattached. If at the start of this creature's turn it's regeneration is not disabled.... - It loses the defeated condition - Restores HP equal to its regeneration value - Is staggered instead of dazed - Does not accumulate drain/Afflictions All forms of regeneration have some means to stop it (typically for 1 round), usually taking a specific type of damage. If a creature is defeated while its regeneration is disabled, it is unable to regenerate for 10 minutes (see prevent recovery for other means to keep those pesky healers down).

entries.title: Attach
entries.content: When the creature grapples another creature it instead of the target becomes Entangled, typically due to its smaller size. Creatures with the attached property typically have special abilities that can only be used while attached.

entries.title: Constrict
entries.content: When the creature succeeds on a grapple check, they automatically deal damage from a specified attack form. Every turn they maintain the grapple they also do this effect.

entries.title: Engulf
entries.content: When the creature succeeds on a grapple check they can engulf the target. The target gains the entombed condition. Usually the creature can take no action other than to use Breakout to escape. Allies can use athletics to pull you out or poise break the creature to set you free.

entries.title: Swallow
entries.content: After successfully grappling a creature, the creature can choose to swallow its prey if it is at least one size category larger. The prey maintains Entangled condition and the swallowing creature doesn't need to take any actions to maintain the grapple. The prey gains Total Cover and takes a specified amount of damage the turn it is swallowed immediately and at the end of the swallowed creatures turn thereafter. A creature cannot use its swallow quality while sickened 2 or higher. Becoming sickened 2 causes it to vomit any swallowed creatures. The prey escapes automatically when the swallowing creature is defeated. A creature can also reasonably swallow a creature it defeats with a bite or tongue attack.

entries.title: Artillery Mage
entries.content: A creature can augment the range of its spells. As a swift action, any spells listed range increases by a specified value. If no value is given, assume 10 ft.

entries.title: Aura
entries.content: Tags: Su A creature has the ability to generate an area of effect surrounding them. Most are passive, some require activation. All auras are persistent effects, meaning creature can only be affected by a negative aura once per round. Creatures are immune to the effects of their own auras.

entries.title: Breath Weapon
entries.content: Tags: Su The creature has the ability to use a supernatural area of effect attack. Common forms include lines and cones of elemental damage. Most breath weapons are recharge effects that have some condition to use them again. If a creature with a breath weapon is currently grappling a creature, they can choose to use their breath weapon instead of maintaining the grapple. The grapple ends and the newly freed creature does not receive a saving throw on the breath weapon.

entries.title: Deathrattle
entries.content: An effect happens when the creature is defeated or dies (but not both), requiring no action. This typically involves exploding or releasing toxic gases.

entries.title: Gaze
entries.content: Tags: Su Merely by looking at another creature nearby, they can inflict some kind of effect. The gaze automatically happen to all creatures within a specific range. Anybody combating a creature with a gaze attack has a few options to try and negate it, made when they first are affected by the gaze attack. * They can choose to close their eyes (instant action), giving themselves the blinded condition until the start of their next turn, making them immune gaze attacks. * Alternatively they can choose to avert their gaze, giving them blurred vision and advantage on saves against gaze attacks. Gaze attacks can be avoided entirely by looking at the creature through a proxy, like a reflective surface.

entries.title: Mad Hops
entries.content: Creature can Jump as high and as far as it can move automatically.

entries.title: Multiattack
entries.content: A creature can make multiple attacks with their standard action (or when they charge or something similar). There are often limitations to what they can use these attacks for and how many they can make.

entries.title: Powerful Charge
entries.content: The creature gets a +2 bonus to Attack during any turn they make a Core Rules, How to Play/Actions/Common Actions/Full Round Actions/Charge.

entries.title: Quicken Spell
entries.content: Creature can augment the cast time of specific spells. Techniques listed under this quality can be used as a swift action. If the spell is at-will, it can also be quickened at will.

entries.title: Rend
entries.content: Whenever the creature lands the listed attacks (including if the attack poise breaks the creature), they inflict an additional effect, usually damage. These attacks must happen on the same turn. Example Rend - Two claws (2d6)

entries.title: Summon Ally
entries.content: Tags: Companion, Su Summon a magical copy of a lesser creature. Can potentially summon multiple creature at once. Can also grant other bonuses to summoned creatures, in addition to the companion boost to attack.

entries.title: Trample
entries.content: Creature can move around through other creatures spaces. As a FRA a creature can move its speed+15, or double its speed in a straight line. It harms all creatures along its path, dealing damage and inflicting Knockdown. Creatures can choose to either AoO or make a reflex save for half damage and negate the knockdown.

entries.title: Venom
entries.content: A status effect typically inflicted with one of a creature's natural attacks. Functions as follows... Venom comes in 3 stages. In order to advance from one to the next, the afflicted creature must fail a Fortitude save. The venom continues until they creature passes a fortitude save. Some creatures may have different effects for their venom, but this is the assumed default if no other details are listed. | Stage | Effect | | ----------------------------------------------------- | --------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | | Stage 1 | Creature is sickened 1, which cannot be removed until the creature is no longer envenomed. 1 minute after the creature fails its first save, it must roll again or hit stage 2. | | Stage 2 | Creature is sickened 2, which cannot be removed until the creature is no longer envenomed. The creature also has Disadvantage on Skill Checks. 10 minutes after the creature fails its second save, it must roll again or hit stage 3. | | Stage 3 | Creature is sickened 3, which cannot be removed until the creature is no longer envenomed. The creature is Drained 10In stage 3, a creature must roll a save every 10 minutes. Each failure inflicts drained 5. | Note: If a creature is exposed to venom again while already suffering from such an effect, they get Disadvantage on their next save against envenomed progressing.

entries.title: +Blindsense
entries.content: This creature has extraordinary senses that allows it to sense the presence of creatures without sight. A creature with blind sense has +4 bonus on +Perception against a creature to which its blindsense applies. The creature automatically receives a perception check with no penalty when a creature enters this range, even while naturally asleep, and automatically wakes up if it senses something. A successful perception check indicates the creature has "locked on" and identified the space the creature is in. They gain Seeking against any creature "locked on." Once a creature with blind sense has "locked on" to a creature, it stays detected until it leaves the creature's blindsense range, or the creatures blindsense is disabled. A creature with blindsense can detect hidden presence as a free action once per turn, but only the affecting the range of its blind sense. Blind sense relies on a specific sense (mentioned in the ability), usually hearing or scent, but can also include things like detecting life (with undead creatures), or feeling tremors in the ground/water. Blindsense can circumstantially be negated, based on the source.

entries.title: Darkvision
entries.content: You can see in complete Darkness. Treat any form of darkness as Bright Light. Everything in darkness is grayscale.

entries.title: Echolocation
entries.content: Sound based. A creature that takes sonic damage has their echolocation disabled for one round.

entries.title: Greensight
entries.content: Tags: Su Can see through any form of vegetation. Creatures cannot use vegetation as a source of Cover to hide against a creature with greensight.

entries.title: Lifesense
entries.content: Tags: Su Functions against living creatures. Undead, constructs (and other similar not really alive) creatures cannot be detected with life sense.

entries.title: Nightvision
entries.content: You treat Dim Light as Bright Light. You treat Darkness as Dim Light. Everything in darkness is grayscale.

entries.title: Scent
entries.content: Uses a creature sense of smell. Aquatic creatures can only use this underwater. Amphibious creatures can use it in water or land. If you have an item with a creatures scent on it, you get a +2 bonus on survival checks to track them. Scents can be remembered similarly to how you can recognize someone's voice. Creatures with scent automatically get a +Perception check (vs +Disguise) against someone impersonating a creature they are very close to. A creature that is sickened cannot use scent.

entries.title: Sightless Blindsense
entries.content: In the absence of sight, blindsense is enhanced. The usual methods for preventing blindsense (sickened with super scent) from functioning impose Partial Concealment on all creatures that their blindsense cannot negate until the condition is removed. Creatures with sightless blindsense instead get a +8 bonus to +Perception when blindsense applies and its range is usually higher.

entries.title: Thoughtsense
entries.content: Tags: Su Functions against intelligent creatures. Mindless creatures cannot be detected with thought sense. A creature that is shaken cannot use thought sense.

entries.title: Tremorsense
entries.content: Vibration based. Aquatic creatures can use this underwater. Amphibious creatures can use it in water or land. Only functions if the creature (and any creatures it is attempting to detect) is in direct contact with a physical surface (or continuous body of water). A creature that is clumsy cannot use tremorsense.

entries.title: Amphibious
entries.content: Amphibious creatures can exist comfortably on land or underwater, including the ability to breathe underwater or the ability to hold their breath for long periods of time.

entries.title: Camouflage
entries.content: Creature can use stealth to Hide without Cover or Concealment. Cannot be used while being directly observed. May come with other conditions for use.

entries.title: Compression
entries.content: You are able to move through spaces designed for creatures 1 size smaller than you without treating it as difficult terrain. You are able to move through spaces designed for creatures 2 sizes smaller than you by treating it as difficult terrain

entries.title: Hide in Plain Sight
entries.content: Creature has the ability to hide from creatures while standing perfectly still by pretending to be an inanimate object or changing colors. If the creature is already being observed, or enemies have already been surprised by the creature using this ability recently, then it does not function. Typically creatures do not get a +Perception check to see a creature that is hiding in plain sight until they go out of their way to inspect the nearby area, or the hiding creature acts.
entries.path: Bestiary/Monster Qualities/Other Traits/Hide in Plain Sight.md

entries.title: Prehensile Limb
entries.content: Creature has a limb that can be used to finely manipulate objects, such as grasping, opening and moving. Prehensile limbs cannot be used to wield Equipment, like +Weapons or shields, unless otherwise noted.

entries.title: Shape Change
entries.content: Creature can transform themselves into another form (or back) as a standard action. The forms available are specified in the ability. All shape change abilities are Polymorph effects.

entries.title: Telepathy
entries.content: Tags: Su The creature can speak directly into the minds of other creatures it can see. A creature must have an intelligence score of at least -2 in order to be telepathically communicated with. A creature can telepathically project its thoughts to several creatures in the nearby vicinity, even if it cannot see them. Once a creature initiates a telepathic conversation, the other creature can communicate back using telepathy. The creature that initiated the telepathic conversation can choose to end it whenever they wish.

entries.title: True Speech
entries.content: Tags: Su This creatures words transcend all language. The creature can speak to anything with an INT score as though it shared a common language. Creatures with a low INT score are able to much better articulate their feelings as though they possessed the intellect of a simple humanoid, but their instincts, desires and attitude toward you remain largely the same.

entries.title: Boss
entries.content: These creatures are distinct in that they command a certain degree of authority or power. Bosses are not always unique enemies, but there is a overlap. Bosses have significant boosts to their action economy. They get a handful of other bonuses as well. +Monster Lore is particularly helpful against bosses. As part of a successful monster lore check, a character also learns any special mechanics of a boss creature, something more involved to defeat it. Think like a video game boss fight where you need to break its armor before you can get to the weak spot. Boss Qualities | Recovery | Bosses almost always have the Recovery defensive trait. | | ------------ | -------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | | Boss Actions | The boss can act out of turn in response to specific events, usually at the end of a player's (or their allies') turns. These actions should typically be low impact compared to its main turn, doing things like creating disruptive terrain, summoning minions etc.The intention is to make the boss feel a little bit more dynamic, like it's responding to the player's actions and acting accordingly. Try to avoid adding too much mobility or risk having this be an easy escape. | | Health | Bosses have massively increased health compared to creatures of their level. I typically multiply their health by 2. |

entries.title: Groups
entries.content: Packs of beasts, platoons of soldiers and swarms of insects are all groups of weaker enemies that have slightly different representation than a typical monster. Group type enemies have a few quirks Universal Traits | AoE Vulnerability | Takes 50% more dmg from any AoE effect. | | ------------------------ | ---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | | Single Target Resistance | Resistance to any damage that only affects a single creature, most common with attack rolls. Certain attacks like cleave can ignore this resistance, but you can only attack the swarm with such moves.Single target effects that deal no damage have no effect. | | Poise Immunity | Group enemies do not have poise and cannot be poise broken. | Other Traits | Distraction | When a creature takes damage from certain group enemies, they can be overwhelmed by the sheer number of creatures. They are staggered for one round (fort negates) | | ------------ | ----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | | Swarm Attack | Common with insects. A swarm does not threaten. These creatures cannot make attacks and must instead enter a creatures space to deal their damage automatically at the end of their turn. This allows them to damage multiple creatures if they are close together with a big enough swarm. | | Auto Attack | Does threaten. Any attacks they make automatically hit. If they choose to combat maneuver, they deal 20+LvL poise damage. |

entries.title: Unique
entries.content: These creatures are one of a kind. They usually have more complicated game plans to their tactics and have a very distinct identity that is separate from other creatures of the same type. Often they are intended to be a singular specific creature. The +Monster Lore DC is 5 higher for these creatures.

entries.title: Bestiary Basics
entries.content: The bestiary is separated into 3 main groups: Monster qualities, the monsters themselves and environmental hazards. Monster qualities are not always exclusive to monsters, but they are much more common on them. Many qualities that appear frequently are not described in bestiary entries and are instead described here in order to save time/space.

entries.title: +Achievement
entries.content: Achievements are archetypal tasks that provide some kind of tangible benefit after completion. You do not need to opt into an achievement, instead they can be claimed retroactively after accomplishing a task.

entries.title: Backstabber
entries.content: +Achievement Requirement - Murder 3 people you once considered allies, or who at thought you were an ally (before you turned on them). If you murder someone in a position of great power, like a powerful guild leader or king, it counts as 3. Reward - You have a 6th sense for when people trust you. By observing how they react to your presence in a brief interaction (such as an introduction), you can tell whether they trust you, are indifferent towards you or if they distrust you.

entries.title: Battle Scars
entries.content: +Achievement Requirement - Get defeated by a Crit and survive in 3 different encounters. Or get crit 3 times in the same combat and survive. Reward - Su The scars you get from battle are deeply entrenched in your memory. Your memories of combats you have encountered cannot be magically removed. By touching them, you can perfectly recall past events, as long as they happened in the thick of combat. Other creatures can touch your scars to share in the experience of the memory
entries.path: Classes/+Class Details/Progression Outside of Class/Achievements/Battle Scars.md

entries.title: Critical Pet
entries.content: +Achievement Requirement - Use stealth to initiate combat on a creature by patting its head 3 times. This pat can have no mechanical effect such as delivering a spell or attacking. Reward - You unlock the "Pet" skill. It uses your stealth bonus. If you sneak up on a creature and pet their head (again, no additional mechanical effect), you establish dominance, giving you a +2 bonus to skills for the subsequent social or combat encounter. If a creature within reach is smaller than you, you can pet them as a free action 1/turn for no mechanical benefit (no save).
entries.path: Classes/+Class Details/Progression Outside of Class/Achievements/Critical Pet.md

entries.title: Ascendance
entries.content: Most classes stop gaining features at level 11, but every class universally has access to ascendance, which is granted at level 12. After which the content train stops. Ascendances are meant to cater to a fantasy where after a character's journey has gone on long enough, they not only acquire power, but also often prestige. Ascendances can often be summed up in a single title like Immortal, Master or Demigod.

entries.title: Demigod
entries.content: You are well known and beloved/feared throughout the land through your deeds and people worship the ground you walk on. You defeated a demigod previously and absorbed their power. \ Through some means you have literally ascended to godhood. Gain the following benefits… - Deific Boons - Take T1 Deific Boons from another god, or make one up. You and creatures that choose you as their god gain this benefit. By achieving even greater (story/world related) feats, you can acquire greater boons. - Emissary - Form a Summoning Contract with your chosen one. The creature must be level 10 or lower. You can telepathically communicate with this creature over any distance. - Prayers Answered - You can Innately Cast Sending upcast to 3rd level so long as it is to communicate with to any of your followers. - Deific Aspect - You can project yourself anywhere you have seen, as though you had been summoned via summoning contract. This requires a 1 minute cast time with a very obvious physical phenomenon occurring to indicate your imminent arrival, such as storm clouds, whirlpool, magic circle (something appropriate for you) during the cast time. You do not need to know the location if your followers pray for your assistance. You are not drained if you gain summoning sickness. Otherwise this has a cooldown of 1 day.

entries.title: Immortal
entries.content: You have harnessed the power of a powerful artifact. You have received the blessing of a god. You have unlocked forbidden magics. Through some means, you have managed to figure out how to prevent aging for yourself. Gain the following benefits… - Timeless - You cannot die of old age, nor can you be forced to magically age. You can alter your age to appear as a younger or older version of yourself, going anything from your prime to an elderly form. Changing requires a standard action and represents your new True Form.\ - Optional - You no longer need to drink, eat, sleep, breathe or anything else tied to your biology that requires your continued operation. You must still must only do light physical/mental activity in order to rest. You can still do any of these things if you so choose. - Ex Machina - Whenever you are slain, you automatically resurrect 1 day later at a pre-designated significant location, such as your hometown or primary place of worship. This ability goes on cool down for 1 month. If you die during this time, you stay dead.

entries.title: Legendary Weapon
entries.content: You have a special weapon that you use to vanquish your enemies. It's name, legendary. It's purpose, divine. This indestructible weapon will be passed down from champion to champion for all of eternity. (if dual wielding is your thing, no issue in making it twinned weapons) Gain the following benefits… - Bonus Feat - Gain the Bonded Weapon feat, applying only to your legendary weapon(s). - Indestructible - This weapons destruction can only be brought about through a series of complex rituals, requiring the intervention of deities or some other incredibly powerful force of destruction, determined upon ascension. - Intelligent - It may or may not be able to communicate, but it can at least understand language and perceive the world around it. Its bonded wielder can receive knowledge from it through language or psychic impressions conveying the information. It has a +20 knowledge bonus concerning monsters of a specific type (plants, fiends etc.). Gain Bane against this creature type. - Unique Property - Each legendary weapon is unique and has something that aids and defeating its favored foe. Some examples below. | Name | Effect | | ------------------ | ------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | | Destined Death | Creatures slain by this weapon are turned to ash and their souls are absorbed by the weapon. They have no afterlife and cannot be brought back from the dead unless the weapon is destroyed. | | Like Butter | This weapon can destroy almost anything, cutting through/crushing stone and metal with ease. It has the "transmundane" level of toughness (see Breaking Objects). | | Detected | This weapon shutters when in the presence of a specific creature type within 100 ft, even through Polymorph effects and Total Cover. The wielder gets an innate sense of the direction to any such creatures. | | Special Attack | Something like Elden Ring's weapons somber weapons/ash of war. Get creative. |
entries.path: Classes/+Class Details/Progression Outside of Class/Ascendance/Legendary Weapon.md

entries.title: Master
entries.content: Through dumb luck, large quantities of practical experience or intensive research, you have reached a level of mastery demanding of respect from all mortals, and perhaps even those greater than mortal. Gain the following benefits… - TECHNIQUE - Create your own school of techniques and make 6 techniques, one for each level. You automatically have all of them memorized without spending memory. - Mana Cheating - You restore 1 mana at the start of each turn. - Signature Technique - You can innately cast a level 3 or lower technique you could normally cast. Its DC increases by 2. If you aren't up to the task of making a bunch of content, instead gain this boon... TECHNIQUE? - Immediately gain 3 technique points and 3 memory slots.

entries.title: Progression Outside of Class
entries.content: Just so its all in one place. Boons/Banes - Gained at level one as a result of having positive or negative luck. Feats - Gained at level 1, and every odd level after. Technique Points - Gain one that can be used on any school of techniques at level 2, and every even level after. Ascendance - Unique template applied at level 12. +Achievement - Can be gained at any time, even retroactively, when certain conditions are met, varying based on the achievement Fall Damage
entries.path: Classes/+Class Details/Progression Outside of Class/Progression Outside of Class.md

entries.title: Character Creation
entries.content: Note: Read main character status! It is essential for character creation. There are several things for you to decide when deciding what you're writing down on your character sheet. Start with a theme, aesthetic, fighting style, or a particular character type you want to mimic from popular media. These are in order of necessity for actually playing the game, but not necessarily in the order that is most convenient to any individual's workflow. Also here is a helpful link for what a basic character sheet should include. | | | | | --- | ----------------------------- | ------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | | 1 | +Class | Classes tend to be very specifically tailored to certain themes, often through the use of archetypes (subclasses).Some classes have choices pre-built in, like what type of techniques you know, a martial artist's fighting style or a necromancer's starting minion. | | 2 | Race | Races come with a variety of benefits. Primary race features help define what role they generally fit, while secondary features are generally more interchangeable. Human is kind of the "default" which is generally good for all characters and isn't very difficult to roleplay. | | 3 | Ability Scores | All ability scores do something on their own, without considering effects from classes. Different classes tend to get different mileage from each choice. | | 4 | Skills | Skills generally determine the type of things that your character does that are less specific. Rogues and ninjas might be known for stealth, but anybody can take the skill. Most skills are tailored towards out of combat interactions, but some, like athletics, stealth, and acrobatics, have a variety of applications. | | 5 | Feats | Feats are customization options that usually give static bonuses, new options in combat, and generally serve to tailor your character towards a specific play style. | | 6 | +Backgrounds | This section helps inform what your character was up to before they became an adventurer and provides you with unique sets of starting gear. | | 7 | Equipment | Decide what type of weapons, armor, and equipment you want. All characters start off with 1,000g to spend as they wish. | | 8 | Backstory | Think about what your character wants, the kinds of tie-ins they have to your world and the kinds of tragic events that happened that led to them brooding in corners of bars. Writing backstories can be fun, but you shouldn't feel obligated to write a specific number of pages or wordcount like it's some silly school assignment. How much you put into table top is how much you get out of it. The most important part of character design is making sure you, your GM and your players are all interested, or at the very least not annoyed, by your character. | | 9 | +Character Creation | This page allows you to decide details about your character if you are uncertain about how big or small, old or young you want your character to be. |
entries.path: Classes/+Class Details/Quick Character Creation Guide/Character Creation.md

entries.title: Class Descriptions and Completion Status
entries.content: This section primarily exists to give you a starting point to see what you're interested in. Because this is early access, not all the classes are done - (C) denotes that the class is complete, often with a variety of options. - (P) denotes the class is playable, but lacking in variety or later levels are sparse - (ND) denotes the class is still in not done, and is largely unplayable | Name/Status | Description | | ------------------------- | -------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | | Alchemist (P) | The scientist class. One of the more versatile classes that allows you to specialize in different types of fighting. Specialize in poisons, bombs, physical augmentations and more! Every single alchemist is completely unique thanks to their scientific discoveries. | | Beast Trainer (P) | The class name says it all: Form a close bond with a special animal uniquely trained to fight by your side. Beast companions can be tailored to fit very specific niches, or you can make a really big dino that chomps people. Also comes with a side of nature magic. | | Blood Hunter (P) | It's definitely not Bloodborne. A gunman with a crimson tint. You can sacrifice your life to continuously fire your firearm for massive damage. You fight to the last breath and hunt those that escape the bite of your bullets. | | Barbarian (C) | The true bruiser class. The barbarian is designed to run straight into the middle of the battlefield all alone as their optimal strategy. They specialize in taking and dealing damage and equal measure, as well as disrupting the battlefield with combat maneuvers and raw strength, often in ridiculous ways. | | Bard (C) | The cheerleader class. That's a bit of an exaggeration, but the bard is primarily concerned with supporting their team by inspiring them with tunes. Bards are at home in the middle of their team where they can provide the most benefit. They often excel in social situations. Their performances assure that whatever hijinks your party gets up to goes down in history. | | Bender (C) | The elemental class. Based largely off of the "Avatar: The Last Airbender" and "Legend of Korra" anime. Based off of what element you choose, you will get a variety of abilities tailored to manipulating that element. Airbenders are mobile with the ability to glide and eventually fly. Firebenders specialize in dealing damage. Earthbenders are tough to keep down and bend the element with the most utility. Waterbenders are the best generalists with decent durability, healing and CC. | | Druid (C) | The nature mage class. Focus on a particular element of nature, like beasts, plants, insects or fungi. This choice informs the nature of many of your class features. The druid excels in off-road adventures to the uninhabited corners of the world. | | Fiend Shifter (C) | The demon class. Tailor yourself towards one specific type of fiend as they are depicted in folklore and popular media. Destroyers do just that and grow to monstrous sizes with extra demon parts to get the job done. Succ shifters primarily deal in deals, using the power of persuasion to lock people into promises as well as lock enemies in their embrace. There are more, but I'll leave it at that for brevity. | | Fighter (C) | Do they even really need a description? Your standard warrior class whose primary asset is their mastery over their equipment. Archetypes give variety in your game plan whether its zone control with Dark Knight, dueling with Swashbuckler or horde slaying with Aran. | | Classes/Hero/Hero (C) | A class based off of the silliness encountered in the Oldschool Runescape MMORPG. It is very flexible, with an emphasis on adding tons of new abilities that interact with skills, similar to the Signature Skill feat. | | Hunter (ND) | A simple martial class meant to emulate the vague ideas surrounding whatever a "Ranger" is in DnD. They use ranged weapons, excel at tracking and...other things I'm sure eventually. | | Healer (C) | The true support class. Provides the invaluable resource of healing, whether it means +HP, poisons or whatever other strange ailments that befall adventures. | | Jester (C) | Bard felt too general for me, so they're primarily musicians now. Jesters are kind of an in between for bard and rogue. Part martial, part mage, with tons of supernatural abilities that allow them to get into and out of trouble based being an Actor, Mime, or Prankster. | | Lunatic (C) | The wild magic class. A full blown magic caster class similar to 5e's wild magic sorcerer. This class is meant to be a complete joke as far as flavor text goes. If you want your character to be completely unpredictable and completely insane, this is the one for you. Praise Frogg Saran! | | Light Guardian (C) | Light guardians are duty-bound to protect the world from the seemingly endless onslaught of fiends and mages who would take advantage of others. A very simple martial support class centered around preventing and protecting against magic and Fiends. | | Martial Artist (C) | The unarmed class. Your primary asset is your body and your spirit. Martial artists require no equipment in order to be functional, replacing weapons and armor with years of rigorous training. This class covers everything from Wrestlers to Monks to Barroom Brawlers. | | Magus (ND) | The spell sword class. Not quite fighter not, quite wizard, the magus specializes in dealing damage with magic via their weapons. They get unique abilities that allow them to combine spells and attacks. | | Mana Addict (P) | Armor of Agathys, the class. When built around, the 5e spell is completely bonkers. I felt it necessary to give it its own class. Outside of that, they have unique ways to manipulate and steal mana that other classes down. Rare anti-mage technology. | | Maligner (C) | A class with a very specific theme. You no longer exist. Instead you are a set of interchangeable and replaceable personas. There is no "real you." Most of the maligner's abilities are centered around deceit through illusions, enchantments and transformations. | | Mechanic 1 (ND) | The mecha class. Has e variants: Siege Engineer, Classes/Mechanic/Pilot and Suitor. Siege engineers are the literal tanks of Nick Nacks. Pilots are the mecha classic where you hop into an oversized robot and use that for fighting, transportation etc. Suitors prefer a technologically advanced body suit (a la Metroid) that enhances their combat prowess and comes with all kinds of useful adaptations and gadgets. | | Mesmerist (C) | The hypnotist class. You have a very special pair of eyes that allow you to break the mental barriers of creatures just by staring at them. You cast spells that manipulate the minds of others and have a special set of psychic implants that you can use to create contingent effects like seeing through another creature's eyes or turning them into a sleeper cell. | | Necromancer (C) | The undead master class. Every enemy that you defeat you are able to transform into an allied skeleton or zombie. Your abilities allow you to better maintain these creatures and also makes them more powerful. Medium uses supernatural powers to interact with the dead. Death Knight gets into the thick of things with their minions and drains the blood of their enemies. Restless Spirit is practically immortal with the ability to return to life after death on a regular basis. | | Paladin (P) | The good boy class. Paladins are shielded by their faith in god as much as their armor. They always take the moral high ground and have mercy for other creatures. Some creatures though, like undead and demons, are beyond redemption, and must be eliminated by using their divine powers to smite them into oblivion. | | Pack Mule (ND) | What if a class was basically nothing but a pile of meat points that did no damage? This is more of a thought experiment at the moment to test the limits of the game. | | Psychic (C) | The psionic class. Your psychic powers allow you to manipulate objects with your mind, gaze into the minds of others and even manipulate their memories. Telekineticist specializes in the telekinesis, throwing objects (and creatures) around the battlefield. Spy that specializes in scrying: A type of advanced magical spying technique that allows you to hear and see things from great distances. Fate Spinner specializes reading and toying with the future. | | Rogue | The class that isn't afraid to use underhanded tactics. Rogue specialize in stealthy operations, are good with their hands and have high mobility. Rogues can be Masterminds, creating nebulous plans that fall into place precisely when needed. Rogues can also be common Thugs, specializing in bullying and intimidation. | | Shapeshifter (C) | The animal transformer class. The shapeshifter has the ability to take on any number of features from different types of creatures, but mostly animals. They can choose to go an anthropomorphic hybrid mode, growing claws and jaws werewolf style, or they can choose to completely transform into animals. The wide variety of features that a shapeshifter can use and the swiftness of the transformations allows them to adapt to the situation on the fly. | | Summoner (???) | This is mostly just a placeholder. I have plans...The pokemon trainer class. The summoner summons a unique creature called an eidolon that fights by their side. As the summoner grows more powerful, they are able to shape the course of the creatures evolution, allowing them to create completely unique abominations from other realms. Outside of the eidolon, they're also able to summon other weaker creatures to supplement their main damage source. | | Slayer (C) | The Doom class. Based largely off of the games "Doom: 2016" and "Doom Eternal." You are a one-man army with a deadly arsenal of unique guns, melee weapons and supernatural powers almost entirely dedicated to killing things. Even without these weapons, your fists are usually enough to get the job done.Also added an archetype called Parry Fiend based on Dark Ages. | | Virtuoso (C) | Another unique gun wielding class. Primarily based off of the league of legends character Jhin. The virtuoso has a spectacular flair to their combat, creating flashy displays out of their gunplay. They have supernatural abilities that allow them to murder people in a spectacular fashion. Fear the 4th shot. | | Wizard (P) | The magic caster with the biggest brain. Wizards possess the unique ability to memorize their spells on the fly. Given enough time for preparation, a skilled wizard can overcome any problem, including creating entirely new spells. A Chronomancer can go back in time to change events when they don't like the outcome. Financial wizards accumulate power as they accumulate wealth to an extreme degree. Destruction wizards are the most powerful mages around when it comes to defeating enemies. | | | |
entries.path: Classes/+Class Details/Quick Character Creation Guide/Class Descriptions and Completion Status.md

entries.title: Distributing Ability Scores
entries.content: Read Ability Scores in order to get a better understanding of the function of each ability score. Every character gets starting 6 points to invest in their ability scores as they wish. Every point you increase in ability score counts against this total. You can decrease an ability score to -1 in order to gain an additional point. The maximum points you can invest is 3. The minimum is -1. !Ability Scores.png
entries.path: Classes/+Class Details/Quick Character Creation Guide/Distributing Ability Scores.md

entries.title: Main Character Status
entries.content: Have you ever wanted to write an epic story for a character you made? How they survived a bloody conflict, went through incredibly rigorous training to become the best and brightest mage, was chosen by god or some other equally astonishing development. Then the game says "you die in one hit to a goblin." While not entirely unrealistic, it doesn't make for very satisfying gameplay to have your character feel like they are made out of paper and are one stray crit away from instant death. A game like DnD 5e remedies this by making KO'd a state your character falls in and out of multiple times as a result of "le nat 20," spammable low action healing and a lack of negative hit points. If you want the player characters to be invincible compared to monsters of a similar level, I say skip the pretense. Player characters are of a different breed and are hardened by their previous adventures, granting the following benefits… HP - Your hp is increased by 10. Defeat - You do not have a chance to instantly die when you fall unconscious, instead you receive a stacking debuff called doom (see Defeat). You also accumulate Afflictions. With this change, a measure of how worn down you are can be more easily tied to how much hp you have. There are obviously other factors, but you can't ever keep adventuring without hp. Mana is the general resource system tied to using special abilities, and it naturally recharges when not used. In general healing that keeps you at full HP all the time is intended to be more scarce, and usually come at the cost of reducing your max mana until you rest. In general, only PCs will have this buff. But BBEGs, legendary creatures and other major bosses intended for use at the end of major story beats could also receive a similar version of this buff. Then you can get the juicy "phase transition" threshold to give your big boss encounters more punch.
entries.path: Classes/+Class Details/Quick Character Creation Guide/Main Character Status.md

entries.title: +Class
entries.content: Classes are the fundamental basis from which you obtain abilities. Classes get a set of numerical bonuses to aid them. Every class has listed +HP, Skills, Memory, and at least one Save bonus. Stats can be flat front-loaded amounts, per-LvL bonuses, or both. Most characters will get their core abilities front-loaded at level 1. All classes get access to some type of techniques, which can be martial techniques or spells. Technique points granted through class usually have some form of restriction. Even levels are mostly dedicated to class features that directly increase the classes power. This includes gaining access to new combat abilities, increasing the power of old ones, and getting their own special attacks. Odd levels are mostly dedicated to getting the next tier of techniques. As such, class features are usually sparse. If any are included, they are usually minor increases in power or they are flavorful inclusions. You also get a feat every odd level. Most classes require selecting an Archetype at level 1, which grants more specific thematic abilities.

entries.title: Archetype
entries.content: A +Class Archetype is a specific branch of thematic abilities that are added to the original class. Unless otherwise stated, nothing is lost or replaced by selecting an Archetype.

entries.title: Bomberman
entries.content: Crust 1 - Ligma Gottem

entries.title: Plague
entries.content: Plague alchemists use their mastery over toxic substances to melt their enemies. They have incredible damage potential in drawn out fights without healers. 1 - Toxic to the Touch (Su) (Poison) Whenever a creature fails a saving throw against one of your spells that deals damage, or On-hit, it applies a stack of Toxic. For the next minute, they take poison damage equal to the stacks of toxic they have at the start of each of their turns. Toxic damage also applies an additional toxic stack each round, but it does not extend its duration. Toxic damage cannot kill creatures, it only defeats them if it drops them to 0. The toxic stacks persist while defeated, but do not accumulate further. This can be removed with first aid or by washing a creature clean, such as by being submerged in water. Any effect that removes Venom can also remove toxic stacks. You can deliver toxic to creatures without them noticing using +Sleight of Hand outside of combat. In such cases, the damage is delayed for a few moments for you to make some distance. 1 - Master Poisoner You gain Poison Jab as an innate technique. Whenever a creature fails a save against one of your toxins, they also gain a stack of toxic. 1 - Expunge (Su) As a standard action, remove all toxic stacks from all creatures within 60 ft of you. Creatures take 3 true damage for each stack of toxic they possesses. You can choose to either make it deadly or nonlethal. Nonlethal expunges can only defeat creatures, not kill them. When done out of combat, this ability has the Subtle tag. If you choose to lethally expunge outside of combat and it defeats a creature, they die Fortitude negates. 2 - Ambush When you successfully Hide from a creature and attack them or cast a spell on them, you can make an additional simple attack against them as a swift action. 4 - Vengeful Expunge When you are defeated, you can expunge as an immediate action. 6 - Innate Technique You gain Corrupting Mist as an innate technique, usable 1/minute. At level 10, you can upcast it.

entries.title: Potion Man
entries.content: Potion man, sitting at your stand trying to expand, but they taste so bland. You are a master of using potions to aid you allies. You can bottle your own techniques into potions to share them with allies. 1 - Extracts You can spend a point of alchemical inspiration to bottle your knowledge. Choose a single technique you possess with the buff or Prepared tags. You can give an ally access to this technique as though using the Administer Potion action. If it is a prepared technique, it lasts for 10 minutes. If it is a buff, it lasts its usual duration. Effects that are normally only target "self" are usable on others through extracts. Explosive Healing Healing potions you create using alchemical inspiration heal an additional 3 hp. You can throw a healing potion at an ally or a spot on the ground to have its healing juices wash over them, affecting a 5 ft radius around its impact zone. Mana potions also share this explosive quality, but restore no additional mana. 4 - Extract Injection Extracts you create with the prepared tag now last until rest. Your ally no longer needs to take a immediate action to consume the extracts.

entries.title: Alchemist Majors
entries.content: Making your own class features can be tough, both maintaining balance and a cohesive theme. This section should help provide guidelines for not only the types of things you should be thinking about studying for your alchemist, but also the power of the mechanical effects. In general, majors can be separated into a few categories. EnhancerPrimarily focused on adding attributes to creatures. Your creations are the next step in evolution or create entirely new types/fusions creatures.Examples: Shao Tucker (FMA)TransformerPrimarily focused on enhancing the self. These transformations come with great power, but also some kind of crippling flaw.Examples: Dr. Jekyll n Mr. Hyde, The Hulk, Armor-Bonded Souls (FMA)ManipulatorPrimarily focused on manipulating physical matter in the world. You turn objects into other things or focus on a specific type of chemical reaction, like starting fires or causing explosions.Examples: Most alchemists in FMA,Friend CrafterYou made 1 specific very special creature. It’s a homunculus, or sentient ooze or something more exotic.Examples: Flubber, Chaos (Sonic Adventure), Driders (DnD) 1 - Major The main thing that you should be focusing on for your major is thinking about a completely unique thing that your character specializes in. It should be specific and limited in scope. Idea Phase: "I specialize in transforming into animals" is a bit too vague and is too similar to the shapeshifter class. If we are on the cutting edge of new techniques, we want something that isn't just another class's features. "I specialize in grafting beast parts to people" is more specific, and falls more closely in line with what you imagine a fantasy scientist would do. For me at least, it's easy to imagine Frankenstein's monster type alchemist that has slowly replaced his body with more optimal animal parts to become a completely unique creature. Mechanics Phase: Sticking with the idea of grafting animal parts, this idea lends itself well to an enhancer type play style. You might gather the parts during your adventures by purchasing them or killing the beasts yourself, but gaining benefits requires work you do ahead of time. Crafting rules are elsewhere, where you can find how long it takes, how much it cost and how difficult it should be. Right now we are concerned with the mechanics. What should a grafted part do? Considering you are fusing parts onto yourself you could give yourself noteworthy parts from other animals to gain permanent abilities: Dog nose for scent, bull horns for a natural attack, fins and gills for aquatic adventures. Considering you're replacing your humanoid parts, there might also be some drawbacks. Lion claws aren't the best for grasping things like opposable thumbs are, for example. An ability like this can open up opportunities for limitless power as long as you keep pouring resources into it, so it should also have some hard limitation. Limiting yourself to only mixing and matching 3 parts for example. Considering the mechanical effect is similar, perhaps use shapeshifter's point system as a basis for how powerful the individual parts can be. 6 - Thesis Thesis marks the point where few have developed their research to this point. This should be the point where you start bending rules and creating increasingly ridiculous items with your craft. Continuing with the idea of grafting animal parts, this is where you might add a second tier to your crafting. No longer are you just replacing parts, but you can add completely new ones, doing things like turning people into centaurs of all kinds of creatures, giving yourself an additional limb, maybe an extra beast head. Up the limit of how many parts you can give a creature, so long as they are willing to accept tier 2 augmentations. 11 - Grand Discovery Grand discovery marks the point where only the best, brightest and most insane push their research to the boundaries of what should be possible. This is the point where you should be breaking rules and doing things once only thought imaginary. By combining different animal parts from dead creatures, you can create entirely new creatures. You can make alterations to yourself to make you completely unrecognizable from when you began. Once upon a time you were a man. Now you are a massive spider from the waist down allowing you to climb any surface with ease, your torso is covered in thick chitin to protect your vitals, your head is covered in beady eyes to allow you to see in all directions.

entries.title: Boomkin
entries.content: 1 - Major - Friend Crafter (companion) You have created a fragile companion meant to run in, tank some hits, and go out in a blaze of glory. Use beast tamer's beast companion as a baseline for what your creature should be with the following changes… - It can be a plant, ooze, humanoid, fey, or cryptid. - It gains 2 initial adaptation points and gains none from leveling. - It's base HP is 10+4*LVL. - It's intelligence is -2 and it can speak a language of your choosing, but not very well - It loses the evasion, scent, lost companions and one liner features of beast companion It gains the following special ability... Kaboom (fire/Poison) - Using your standard action, or when it is defeated, your creation explodes violently. Deals (10+3*lvl) fire or poison damage (chosen when exploding) in a 10 ft radius to ALL creatures (reflex halves). - You have evasion vs your creation's explosions. - You can recreate your companion by spending 3 rounds crafting, good as new. It maintains its memories and harbors no ill will towards you for its repeated destruction. 6 - Thesis You grow accustomed to your research blowing up into your face. You make it blow up in new ways, adding more variety. When your creation explodes, you can choose one of the following explosion types. Earthshatter earth - Damage changes to crush. Groundborne creatures are knocked down (reflex negates) Bigger Boom (fire/poison) - AoE increases to 20 ft radius Cold Snap frost - Damage changes to frost. Creatures are frosty (reflex negates). - You are immune to your creation's explosions - Recreating your companion takes 2 rounds 11 - Grand Discovery T

entries.title: Mutant
entries.content: 1 - Major - Enhancer poly, chimeric Your research is a bit self-centered. You were the only person who was willing to be a test subject for your research. Unfortunately it seems as though your research has developed a mind of its own, and in order to access its power, you need to share your body. In this particular example, your mutation enhances your physical prowess, but only when you let out this inner beast. This works very much like pointing a dog at an enemy, then unchaining them. As a free action, unleash the beast, gaining the following benefits - +Athletics and +Acrobatics get a +4 bonus - The unarmed combatant feat with a Multiattack of 2 attacks - Grow to large size - Increase Land Speed by 10 And the following drawbacks - Any turn you do not attack or destroy something during your turn, you take 3 true damage - Disadvantage on saves vs taunt - After being out of combat for 1 minute, you must roll a will save against your class DC. If you fail, you attack the nearest creature. To end your rampage, you must consume an item you craft with Alchemical Inspiration. If you run out mana, your rampage lasts until you are defeated or you rest. 6 - Thesis You start to learn more about your instinctual side, and learn to cooperate better with it. You've achieved greater mastery over your physical augmentations and creative ways to use them. Gain the following additional benefits when you transform - You are constantly under the effects of the Shockwave technique - You gain a 1st level martial Innate Technique, usable at will - Remove concentration and skill restriction debuff 11 - Grand Discovery You have mastered your transformation. You gain the benefits of it constantly and maintain full mental acuity, removing all drawbacks. And other stuff...It is a capstone after all

entries.title: Nekomancer
entries.content: 1 - Major - Enhancer (poly, chimeric) Your feline fascination drives your research forward. You can distill the essence of cats to be complimentary with humanoids. You can use alchemy to create "catgirl potions" for 50g with the following effects. - Nightvision - Claw attack (d8 slash) - Reduce fall damage to 1 per 10 ft fallen. You cannot become drained from falling. - Compression - The ability to speak with cats - Cosmetic changes such as cat eyes, ears, tail, whiskers, some fur The effects of the potion last 1 hour. You have Advantage on any skill checks against any creature affected by one of your nekomancy potions. 6 - Thesis Your nekomancy potions last 24 hours. When you make a nekomancy potion, you can choose to add one of the following benefits… Cat Form (poly, true) - Gain the ability to assume the form of any type of cat and back. Transforming is a standard action. Your size changes as appropriate. Your claw damage increases to 2d6 while transformed. You can speak while in cat form. Ideal Form (poly, true) - Gain the ability to assume the form of an attractive female human with secondary cat features and back. Transforming is a standard action. Your size changes as appropriate. You get advantage on purrsuasion while transformed. 11 - Grand Discovery

entries.title: Purifier
entries.content: 1 - Major: Purification You fix and heal, but not in the traditional sense. Your area of expertise is in reversing the irreversible. It is not all too uncommon to find individuals who are victims of a forced Reincarnate from Frogg Saran or that are being changed by some kind of alien mutation or profane corruption. You aim to turn back time to where the creature was healthy whole and themselves again...eventually. Using 100g of materials and 1 minute of time... - You can fashion a prosthetic limb or organ fitted to a specific creature's needs. When inserted or attached, the organ assumes normal functionality, albeit with some getting used to. Limbs inflict clumsy 1 and organs inflict sickened 1. These debuffs are considered Permanent Status and persist for 1 week. At the end of the week, the limbs are mostly indistinguishable from the originals, leaving only connecting scar tissue. - You can also create a foil to the effects of a Styx Serum, or other effects that remove memories or sense of self. A year comes back to them for each day that follows them drinking your memory potion. - Anything reasonably close to these abilities in terms of power/effect 6 - Thesis Using a laboratory, 1,000g of materials and 1 minute of time... - Limbs/organs you replace do not apply debuffs - You can reverse the effects of a Reincarnate spell, returning the creature to its previous form. - If a creature is corrupted but not yet completely consumed by some malicious force, such as a creature part way through transforming into a fiend, you can reverse the direction of this corruption. Over time after administering your solution, the creature reverts back to its old self, assuming it does not continue to actively corrupt further. - Anything reasonably close to these abilities in terms of power/effect What counts as "corruption" is intentionally left vague, because alchemists are supposed to break rules, not be bound by them. In fantasy, any number of things can "corrupt" for greater power or as a consequence of associating with evil. You can counter the GM Magic if you manage to capture corrupted allies or save otherwise unwilling enemies. 11 - Grand Discovery Using a laboratory, 10,000g, 1 rare materials and 1 minute of time you can restore a creature back from nearly anything short of death. A rare material is again, intentionally vague, because its not an item that exists in the game, but is something that should exist in the world. These ingredients should be extracted from creatures that have incredible restorative properties, are immortal or something similar. - A phoenix feather - A shard from an ancient monolith - Blood from a Celestial or a powerful troll By using a laboratory, 100,000g, 2 different rare materials and 2 weeks of active labor, you can create a clone of a creature you have a piece of (such as a blood sample, hair or a limb). This clone requires a full year to gestate before it grows into an adult. The clone is inert until the original dies. When they do, they wake up in the body of the clone a few minutes later. Their max +HP is permanently reduced by 10 and they are Drained 20. Roll on the Resurrection Chart. Clones cannot be reborn as clones. A creature trained in +Medicine that is giving treatment to a clone can discern the strangeness of this body with a DC equal to 10+The alchemist's Alchemy bonus.

entries.title: Alchemist
entries.content: Alchemist is a hybrid class with the potential to branch in many directions depending on the desired play style. Using the power of SCIENCE you can create friends, enhance your friends, yourself or you can manipulate the world around you in a variety of creative ways. | Basic Statistics | | | ------------------------ | ---------------- | | +HP | 20(+8 per Level) | | Poise | +6 | | Skills | 6 | | Saves | +2 Fortitude | | Memory | 3+ | 1 - Technique You have Magical Specialization and Martial Specialization You gain two technique points at level 1, then another two points every 2 levels after. These technique points can be used on the alchemy tree. Choose another tech tree. They can also be used on that tree. 1 - Alchemaster You get the Alchemy skill for free and a +4 bonus to alchemy checks. The Save DC of the items you create, even ones without alchemical inspiration, increase to your save DC (if it is higher). 1 - Alchemical Inspiration Mana Burn You can make AND drink, administer, activate or throw an alchemical item as a standard action. You can use this feature with items that have a craft DC of 10+(your alchemy bonus) automatically. Anything higher requires a roll. Failures consume materials. 1 - Toxin Enthusiast You have Resistance to poison damage, being envenomed and +Toxins. 1 - Major Every alchemist is defined by their (often nonsensical) specialization. In order to make you, the player, feel like you are on the cutting edge of research, you can break the usual rules of the game and create entirely new class features with a specific focus. This can include things like researching how to gain additional limbs or genetically engineered catgirls. More details in the Alchemist Majors section. 6 - Thesis Upgrade! Abilities granted by your major are improved, loosening the restrictions. 11 - Grand Discovery Abilities granted by your major are perfected, eliminating all but the most extreme restrictions.

entries.title: Avalancher
entries.content: An avalancher is a warrior whose preferred home is amongst the clouds. Unfortunately most have to settle for mountains, trees or other tall structures. An avalancher fights by descending upon their foes with their signature attack: The Plunging Attack. 1 - Special Attack: Plunging Attack recharge Note: You can only plunging attack a creature after taking the jump action from an elevation higher than your original target. Fall upon your foe, making a melee attack with Advantage. Deal an additional 3 damage for every 10 feet you fall (capped at 3*LvL). You deal an equal amount of poise damage. If this drops a creatures poise to zero, they are knocked down. On-hit or on a Graze, you negate all fall damage you would normally take. You cannot use this attack in consecutive rounds, but otherwise at will. Rage Powers Numbers are level requirements 2 - Yodeling - You can choose to effect your voice as per the Amplify technique at will. - You can choose to make your plunging attack deal sonic damage. 2 - Mountaineer You are unaffected by the oxygen-sparse environments typical of high elevation, and of the toxic gas environments typical of volcanic environments. You can freely use both hands to use equipment while climbing, and you gain an additional 40 speed you can only use for climbing.

entries.title: Beast Totem
entries.content: Embrace your wild side. Beast totem barbarians tend to be furrier and feathery-er than most. 1 - Totem Feature - Beast Buddy companion You gain the beast trainer's beast companion class feature. You are limited to choosing the strong type companion. Your companion starts off with 2 adaptation points, and gains no more as it levels. Its Level is your level-1. (yes you can be level 0) Rage Powers Numbers are level requirements 2 - Bestial Movement Su You and your beast companion gain an additional 40 speed each round that can only be used to climb or swim. As a swift action, you can sprout wings to gain the glide feature for 1 round. 2 - Druidic Training You get knowledge nature proficiency with a +4 bonus. You can use it while raging. You also know the druidic secret language. 2 - Blood Scent (Su) Gain blindsense (scent, 10 ft). You and your beast companion gain seeking against any creature that does not have full hp. 2 - Spirit Animal (Su) poly, true Choose a specific animal you can transform into and back from as a swift action. Gain a single natural attack that deals 2d6 damage and 3 points worth of abilities from the Polymorph Listfeature that only apply when in spirit animal form. Once chosen, these features cannot be altered. Your appearance is slightly unnatural for the type of creature you transform into, typically being larger and more dangerous looking with glowing eyes or strange fur patterns. Your companion can also transform into this form as an instant action when you do. 2 - Beast Speech (Su) You can speak with beasts as though you shared a common language. They are able to much better articulate their feelings as though they possessed the intellect of a simple humanoid, but their instincts, desires and attitude toward you remain largely the same. 4 - Fetch When your beast companion successfully performs a drag maneuver, it gains an additional 30 speed. It must be able to end this movement with the drag target in a space you threaten if it uses this bonus speed. 6 - Twice as Angry Whenever you Rage, your beast companion can Rage as an instant action, gaining the benefits of your Rage.

entries.title: Bronze Totem (Bronzebarian)
entries.content: An intellectual and cultured individual at heart, a bronzebarian applies a surprising amount of creativity to their techniques and fighting styles. Using the most advanced caveman strategies, bronzebarians set themselves apart from other barbarians. 1 - Totem Feature - Brute Force You can use your muscles to make up for your lack of intellect. As long as you have a negative intelligence score, you add your strength to int-based skills instead of intelligence. You can automatically tell when a creature has a negative intelligence score just by looking at them. You may use INT-based skills while raging. Rage Powers Numbers are level requirements 2 - Two-Handed Fist By taking one hand and wrapping it around the opposite wrist, you figure out how to two-hand your fists. When using unarmed strike, the barbarian can use both hands, effectively treating it as a 2h weapon. Gain unarmed combatant as a bonus feat and increase fist base damage to 2d8. 2 - Bronze Speech You can speak with any creature that has a negative intelligence score as though sharing a common language.
