Some examples of what you can accomplish… | Die # | Result | | ----- | ---------------------------------------------------------------------------- | | 5 | Get a group of animals to agree with you by making some kind of vocalization | | 10 | Get a bird to poop on someone while outdoors | | 20 | A vendor that sells pets rolls in town | If you fail the check then nothing happens, potentially making you look like an idiot depending on the setup. 6 - Lolcat Gain a new option with Nature Strikes You summon a cat/kitten to a spot you can see within 100 ft. The cat immediately starts doing something silly or adorable, like eating a cheeseburger or existing with a silly outfit. Creatures within 30ft of this cat are fascinated for 1 minute (will negates). Creatures that are minions to you get no save. Creature doesn't need to be a cat. Can easily be some other silly animal. Cats just have a high concentration of silly. 11 - King of Beasts You get an honorary crown, befitting of a king of beasts. All beasts treat you with the reverence usually only afforded to royalty by their subjects and servants. Unless a beast is under direct control from another creature, or a higher level than you, it will not harm you or otherwise impair you. Nature Strike can be used every other round and commanding large groups of beasts using Band of Beasts can be performed every day. If you stay in the same roundabout area, such as a forest or a valley, all your beast subjects will come to you when your kingdom comes under siege. This could mean the destruction of habitat, hunting on a large scale or particularly ominous creatures moving in. They can give you detailed descriptions about unnatural phenomenon or strange creatures as well as their general location.

entries.title: Hive
entries.content: Your body has transformed to be a suitable home for a specific type of insect. Your symbiosis with this hive gives you the ability to command them. You protect them, they protect you. 1 - Hive Mind - +2 vs any save with the mind-affecting tag. - Immunity to group type enemies' Distraction ability - Resistance to Swarm Attack and Auto Attack damage 1 - Territory Mark a location as your territory by allowing your hive to build structures there over the course of a few days. This location is a 100ft radius, centered on a point. Your territory gives the following benefits - You have regeneration 1 in your territory - You have +4 +Perception in your territory - Your hive watches over the area constantly, using your perception. If they detect something, you know what they see as though you had seen it yourself, even if you are not there (Su). 1 - Hive Body Parts of your body are constantly shifting and moving, making it difficult to pinpoint where your weak points are. You have the amorphous quality, which means to crit you, the enemy needs to roll your AC+12. You can command your hive to attack any creature within your natural reach as a standard action, automatically dealing (2d6+CL) pierce damage. 2 - Hive Consumption poly, true Become the hive. As a standard action, turn the entirety of your body/gear into a large swarm, gaining the following features. | Name | Effect | | --------------------- | ------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | | AoE vulnerability | Take 50% more damage from any AoE effect | | Single Target Defense | Resistance to any damage that only affects a single creature, most common with attack rolls. Certain attacks like cleave can ignore this resistance, but must only target the swarm. Single target effects that deal no damage have no effect. | | Poise Immunity | Cannot be poise broken | | Swarm Attack | Does not threaten. These creatures cannot make attacks and must instead enter a creatures space to deal their damage automatically at the end of their turn. This allows them to damage multiple creatures if they are close together with a big enough swarm. Doesn't affect allies. | | Fly | 40 | You can speak but you are unable to wield a staff in hive form, making you unable to cast spells. You can revert back to your original form as a standard action. You can also choose to host ground borne insects, such as ants. You lose the fly speed and gain a land and climb speed of 40. Your swarm attack damage increases to 3d6. 2 - Eye of the Swarm Any hive of insects under your control also protects your allies. While within the swarm, you allies are treated as though they have Cover. 4 - Bug Bite When you attack with hive body, you can attack all creatures within your natural reach. Your hive attack applies sickened 1. 4 - Expanded Territory The size of your territory expands to a 1000 ft radius. 6 - Distraction Your hive attack staggers for 1 round (fort negates). 8 - Hive Separation companion You can lose your hive mind and hive body features and have your hive separate from your body as a standard action. Treat it as an insect swarm. It cannot be more than 100 ft away from you, or else it immediately returns. If it is ever defeated, it returns to you and this ability is disabled until you rest. While reunited with you, your swarm has regen 1. If you share a space with your swarm, you can choose to absorb it as an instant action. Your hive is always half your level. 10 - Home Defense You always have a bonus hive defending your territory, functioning exactly as hive separation, but it cannot leave your territory. Your territory increases to a 1 mile radius.

entries.title: Mushroomancer
entries.content: 1 - Fungus Amungus Infect willing, Sleeping or defeated creatures with parasitic spores as a standard action. You can also inflict this upon a creature you have skin to skin contact with as a standard action. This infection deals no damage and has no noticeable side effects or visual symptoms at all. While a creature is infected, you get a +2 bonus on skill checks made against it. You also know the direction they are from you. You can infect up to 5 people at once, or more by spending Memory. Infecting any additional creatures forces you to decide to free another. You can remove an infection as a free action. This infection can be identified and removed with first aid against your DC. 1 - Power Up Su You don't just grow mushrooms, you become the mushroom. Every day you can grow 3 mushrooms on your body that contain magic within them that is unleashed when plucked and eaten. The magic is chosen when they are plucked. Plucking and eating/feeding is a single swift action. You can choose to grow new mushrooms with new powers on the following days, but growing new ones drains the magic from the old ones. Mushrooms can be preemptively plucked and handed out to allies so they don’t need to be next to you to consume. In addition to spreading your Fungus Amungus infection, the mushrooms can have the following effects… | Mush Type | Effect | | --------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | -------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | | Mega Mush buff | Grow to huge size (1 minute) | | Mini Mush (buff) | Shrink to tiny size (1 hour) | | Metal Mush (buff) | Gain DRA 2 and Resistance to Poise damage. Decrease speed by 15. -10 to +Stealth and Grounded. (1 minute) | | Rainbow Mush mind-affecting | Creature is fascinated for 1 hour. When interrupted during this hour, they naturally return to being fascinated after 1 minute. | | Double Mush figment | An illusory copy of you appears adjacent. You can give it a simple task and it pretends to do it to the best of its ability. It cannot interact with physical objects. Lasts 1 hour or until harmed. | | Royal Mush glamer | Transforms creature into princess version of itself, giving it an air of nobility. Any situation that could benefit from being of a privileged class grants you advantage on skill checks, such as getting into an exclusive party, seeing real royalty etc. Lasts until rest. | | Return to Fungus poly, true Dismissible | You transform into inanimate fungus. You can transform into a very large stalk, or as many smaller fungi grouped together as appropriate. You maintain you senses, but can take no actions or move other than to revert to your original form. Lasts until rest or until harmed. | 1 - Mush Touch (Su) By touching a surface, you can cause a tiny toadstool to sprout at that location. In less than ideal environments, they quickly perish. You can choose to leave behind a trail of these tiny toadstools behind when you while you walk. 2 - Resonant Spores You can telepathically communicate to any creatures infected by your spores. 2 - Puffed Up (Su) 1/minute when you fall below half hp, you release a cloud of toxic gas within a 10 ft radius. While within the gas, creatures not infected by your spores are sickened 1 and have blurred vision. These effects persist for 1 round after leaving the cloud. 3 - Shiny light As a swift action, you can cause your creations to glow with bio luminescence. Any creatures you have infected with spores as well as any mushrooms you create with Power Up or Mush Touch glow with a Bright Light in a 5ft radius. This isn't particularly useful in bright places, but in darkness it can light the way. Glowing creatures take a -5 penalty to +Stealth and cannot use Darkness to Hide. 4 - Decomposition (Su) Recharge You can cause dead or decaying organic matter to be overtaken by fungus and accelerate the decomposition process. This can be used to target things like corpses, fallen trees and leaves, or even wooden doors. This typically has the effect of damaging the structural integrity of the object. For something like a small wooden barrier, it only takes a few rounds. For something much larger like a wall or an enormous fallen tree, it can take minutes. This provides sustenance to you, and thus limits its usability. Decomposing something about your size like a door is more like a snack, but a particularly large object like an ornate wooden staircase is like eating a feast all on your own. You may need to wait until you are hungry again to fully decompose such objects. 4 - Mush Men (Su) Mana Burn scrying By spending 1 mana, you can animate a tiny mushroom to have little legs and eyes. They perish if attacked, have a speed of 30 and use your perception/stealth scores. You can give them telepathic orders, but they can do little more than keep watch or wander in general directions. They will mentally alert you (waking you up) if you they see something unusual, like an intruder, or something dangerous/scary. You can teach them a specific creature if you wanted them to find someone or something in specific. If they touch a creature, they can transfer your infection. This is usually only possible against incapacitated, friendly or willing creatures. Against an unwilling creature, you must succeed on a melee attack against a DD creature (uses your attack bonus). You can scry through the mushmen. You cannot recover the mana as long as the mushman persists, even if you rest. If the mushman returns to you safely, you recover the mana burn. If it dies, you must rest to restore it. You can willingly dismiss a mushman. 5 - Absorb When you get the wet condition, you instead increase 1 size category. Further applications of wet apply normally. You shrink back to normal size after you rest or with exposure to direct sunlight for a few minutes. 6 - Power Up+ (Su) You can create an additional Power Up each day. Gain additional options. | Mush Type | Effect | | ------------------------------------------------------------------------ | --------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | | Magic Mushroom healing | You can temporarily abate the negative effects of a T1 affliction for 1 hour. | | Higher Plane | Creature gains the time out condition until rest. | 8 - Green Demon (Su) (recharge) poison Target a creature more than 30 feet away from. A dangerous and toxic mushroom pops out of your hand and relentlessly follows them for 1 minute. It flies at a speed of 30 at the end of each of your turns, moving in a direct line towards its target. This can lead to the mushroom getting "caught" on solid terrain. If it ever enters the space of the target creature, or if another enemy enters its space, it is immediately infected. The creature then takes d6 per LvL poison damage (fort halves) and is dazed for 1 round. 10 - 1-Up (Su) (healing) (recharge) Make a mushroom that you can feed to a creature that has died in the last minute to restore them to 1 hp. They become drained 10. Usable 1/month.

entries.title: Druid
entries.content: Druids focus on the supernatural side of nature: with the ability to commune with it as though it were a person. They choose a specific aspect of nature to focus on, such as plants or animals. The abilities granted to them by Mother Earth allow them to interact and manipulate these things to a sometimes comical degree. | +HP (specialist) | 20(+6 per level) | | ------------------------ | ---------------- | | Poise | 5 | | Skills | 4 | | Saves | Will | | Memory | 2+(2\*lvl) | 1 - Technique Specialization (Magical) You have Magical Specialization You gain 2 technique points at level 1, then another 2 points every 2 levels after. These technique points can be used on the Mother Earth, Lunar or Shroomlette tree. 1 - Druidic You learn the secret language, druidic (see Linguistics). In text form it can only be seen by other creatures that know druidic. You can essentially write secret messages by touching your finger to any natural object (typically boulders and trees) and writing. Any message written in this way can be erased by a creature that knows druidic with ease. 1 - Nature Tongue You can speak with animate plants and Beasts as though you share a common language. They are able to much better articulate their feelings as though they possessed the intellect of a simple humanoid, but their instincts, desires and attitude toward you remain largely the same. You can also interact with inanimate plants, but these creatures only have the most basic of ideas, only able to give single word answers like yes/no or a direction. 1 - Nature Stride When traveling through natural terrain that restricts your movement, such as thick snow or dense jungle, your speed is not affected. All allies that can see you also gain this benefit. After not seeing you for a few minutes, your allies lose this benefit. 1 - Wild Empathy Gain the Wild Empathy feat. Its usable on any creatures you can speak to with nature tongue, allowing you to influence plants. You need not roll against inanimate plants, but their influence is incredibly limited, only able to help by shaping their growth for instance. If multiple druids try to influence the same plant, they roll to establish dominance permanently. 1 - Skill Get +Nature as a skill with a +4 bonus. 1 - Deific Boons You start play with 10 GBP for the Earth Mother, unless your WIS is -1 or lower.

entries.title: Destroyer
entries.content: Destroyer shifters are the most monstrous of all fiend shifters. They specialize in destruction, feats of strength and striking fear in the hearts of their enemies. Destroyer abilities are inspired by fiendish, animalistic and monstrous creatures with little more than the desire to kill. 1 - Techniques Your have Martial Specialization. Your technique trees are Barbarism and Cataclysm. 1 - First Seal: Fiendish Frenzy Su When you break the first seal, you increase to large size and increase your Land Speed by 10. If there isn't enough room for you to grow, you can roll +Athletics as an instant action to make room. When you transform you can willingly choose to give yourself the taunted condition against one enemy that you can see. You can also choose to immediately take the Focus action against them. You get +4 to d20s (attack, skills, saves etc) against enemies that taunt you. You can choose to automatically fail any effects that taunt. Whenever you use this self-inflicted taunt and you do not attack or otherwise attempt to cause harm to the creature that you are taunted by, you take 3 psychic damage and the taunt ends. 6 - Second Seal: Bloodlust (Su) When you break the second seal, you increase to huge size. As part of the action to break the seal, and every turn after as a swift action, you can dash, as long as you are taunted. If the creature you are taunted by is defeated, leaves combat, is devoured, or you otherwise aren't taunted, you can choose to be taunted by another creature as an instant action. 11 - Final Seal: Monstrosity (Su) fear When you break the final seal, you increase up to massive size. Any creature you are taunted by must make a will save or be frightened for as long as you are taunted by them (will negates). * Fiendish Growths Numbers indicate level requirements (10 total) 1 - Terror to Behold You can demoralize creatures up to 60 ft away. When you break the seal, you gain a +4 bonus to intimidation and can demoralize a creature as part of doing so. You gain the fearsome finisher feat 1 - Cacophonous Clap sonic You gain the rend monster ability (2 claws). Deals 2d6 (7) sonic damage. As a standard action, or at the end of a Core Rules, How to Play/Actions/Common Actions/Full Round Actions/Charge, deal 2d8+lvl (9+lvl) sonic damage to all creatures within your natural reach and stagger all creatures for one round (fort halves damage and negates stagger). 1 - Tongue You gain an extra long and dexterous tongue capable of grasping and finely manipulating objects as well as your hands. You can also use it to make attacks with +10 reach during your turn (deals crush damage). Gain a special attack Grasping Tongue: - Kidnap your foe. If the attack lands, make an additional drag attack. 2 - Taste Test (Su) Whenever you land a tongue attack, or just lick someone, you learn a tidbit of information about the creature. This information is one of the few things: A random piece of personal information (such as a love interest), something that weighs heavy on the creature's mind (such as upcoming important events) or the creature itself (as per one section of monster lore). If you've already learned about the creature through monster lore (even from an ally or previously licking the creature) you can instead learn about another category. 4 - Destructive Tendencies Your claws are considered to be of adamantine toughness for the purposes of Breaking Objects. You get a +4 bonus to athletics checks to break things. 4 - Path of Ruin Whenever you take the Core Rules, How to Play/Actions/Common Actions/Full Round Actions/Charge action, you automatically destroy obstacles, Cover and difficult terrain that is of solid or lesser toughness. Anything harder you get a free attempt to break it. You can charge directly into the air and are immune to fall damage any round you charge. You have resistance to all damage when you charge. 4 - Devour You gain the swallow monster quality. Deals 3d8 (13) poison damage. You get DRA 3 vs swallowed creatures. You can also swallow creatures you successfully drag with your grasping tongue special attack instead of dragging them. 4 - The Succ (sonic) air Once per minute you can inhale with such sudden force as to evacuate the air in its proximity, causing a sudden wave of air pressure from the implosion. All creatures and loose objects within 30 feet of the you take (2d6+d6/odd lvl) sonic damage and are pulled adjacent to you (fort halves damage and negates pull).\ Swallowed creatures are also affected and automatically fail their save. 10 - Endless Abyss (Su) You become a pure destructive force of the universe to such a degree that you can manifest the Endless Abyss. This takes the form of a black hole that is integrated into your body somehow (replaces eye, in the back of the throat, or something more exotic like the dark star skins in league) and is immediately recognized as dangerous by most. You can choose to annihilate objects that you comes into contact with, absorbing them into the abyss, as long as you are larger than them. You can concentrate for 1 minute to allow the Abyss to consume a defeated creature within reach. Add 1 to your soul counter. For every soul consumed, you can regurgitate an annihilated object or creature. Regurgitated creatures and objects are in the same state they were in when they were annihilated. 10 - Eater of Souls (Su) healing Teleportation overheal Whenever a creature within reach is defeated, you consume a piece of its soul and recover 10 hp. If they are within 60 ft of you, you can teleport adjacent to them as an immediate action to get a nibble of that soul. You can only munch a specific creature's soul 1/day.

entries.title: Succ
entries.content: Succ shifters are the schemers of the fiend shifters. They know when you've been naughty.\ Succ shifters have altered base stats. | HP (mixed) | 20(+8 per level) | | ---------- | ---------------- | | Poise | 6 | | Skills | 6 | | Save | Fort | | Memory | 3+2*LvL | 1 - Technique You have Magical Specialization. Your technique trees are Shira, Brazzeria (T) and Psionics. You get an additional technique point at this level and every odd level after. 1 - First Seal: Hugs When you break the first seal, creatures are much less resistant to your gentle caress. When you Grapple a creature, you do not need to spend any actions to maintain the grapple and your speed is reduced to 15 instead of 0, allowing you to carry along your new friend. Your claws give you a +4 bonus to grapple attacks while your hands are empty. You gain the Maneuver Versatility feat. 6 - Second Seal: And Kisses Su healing Overheal charm When you break the second seal, You gain constrict. Initiating a grapple and every turn you maintain it thereafter inflicts drained 5 on your target and you heal 5 hp. Whenever you succ a creature like this, the creature is also charmed for 1 minute (will negates). You can apply either or both effects with a normal kiss. 11 - Final Seal: ? When you break the final seal, * Fiendish Growths Numbers indicate level requirements (8 total) 1 - Charmer (charm) Sp companion subtle As a swift action, you can attempt to charm a creature within 30 ft permanently (will negates). If you do not want to take companion slots, it functions as the spell charming gaze. These creatures do not gain the companion attack bonus. 1 - Dealbreaker (subtle) curse scrying Whenever a creature makes a promise (verbally, written, etc) to you, you can unknowingly curse them (will negates). Whenever the cursed creature breaks that promise, you know. As a standard action, you can scry on them. You can do this indefinitely. You can remove this curse at any time as an instant action. If the creature breaks the promise, it must apologize to you and provide some form of penance to remove the curse. 1 - Cuddle Gain the Tangled feat. Whenever you rest with another creature(s, max three others), you and the creature(s) gain (2\*lvl) temp hp, lasting until they are lost. 1 - Chant Your CL increases by 1. By decreasing your speed by 30 and chanting in a fiendish tongue, you can increase the CL and DC of the effect of spells you cast this round by 1. A creature can perform the aid action to increase your CL by 1 if they also chant in a fiendish tongue. They need not be in reach, you only need to hear them. 1 - Cleavage Cache (Su) You gain an extra dimensional space as a part of your body. You can access it by reaching into your chest if you're feelin lewd, or through something more exotic like a literal drawer integrated into your body (a la Ava's Demon). This space has an unlimited quantity of volume, allowing you to put and pull out comically large objects, but anything stored within counts against your carry weight. Gain 100 carryweight. A creature can only remain inside for 1 minute before being forcefully ejected. It cannot enter again until you rest. Other creatures can access your cache while you are defeated, dead or asleep (with sleight of hand). Though it may require a lot of searching to find the desired object. 1 - Succ Insight (Su) poly, true You are constantly under the effects of Detect Thots. It does not have the concentration tag. By making skin to skin contact with a creature (or whatever you/they have that replaces skin), you can learn one piece of information. Prolonged contact gives you multiple chances to gather more information. - One kink or fetish the target has - The name and appearance of the creature the target is most attracted to - The name and appearance of the creature the target most recently did something lewd with You can transform to match the appearance of a creature you learn about from this ability as a standard action, even if it isn't humanoid. If you successfully +Disguise yourself as this creature, you have advantage on all Skill Check made concerning this creature. Depending on the target's familiarity with the creature, it may be difficult to disguise as them, or benefit from the transformation at all if their relationship soured post intercourse. 4 - Handwave (Su) (concentration) You can telekinetically manipulate objects within 60 ft, limited by your carry weight. You can hold a large quantity of smaller/lighter objects if they are all close together. These are essentially treated as an additional limb that requires concentration to use. 8 - Profane Tribute (Sp) Gain a +1 bonus to one of your ability scores (max +3). You can confer this benefit on up to 3 willing creatures. Gifted creatures also receive other effects. - You can use Telepathy to communicate with these creatures over any distance. - They are permanently charmed by you, which cannot be removed without first removing the gift. - As a standard action, you can scry on these creatures. You can do this indefinitely. This portion of the ability has the scrying tag - You form a Summoning Contract with these creatures. You can summon one as a standard action. You can only have 1 summoned at any given time. This portion of the ability has the \[companion] tag, but only when the creature is summoned.

entries.title: Unchained
entries.content: Unchained shifters are all about inflicting as much pain upon themselves and others as possible. They take their favorite parts of other creatures and add it to themselves. 1 - Techniques You have Martial Specialization. Your technique trees are Barbarism and Techniques/Martial Techniques/Pupper/Pupper. 1 - First Seal: Shackled Su When you break the first seal, 4 spiked chains burst from your back that you can make attacks with (deals crush damage). They function as additional opposable limbs that can be used to hold objects, climb walls, grapple creatures etc. The chains have an additional 5 feet of reach during your turn. 6 - Second Seal: Share my Pain (Su) When you break the second seal, your chains now have an additional 10 ft of reach during your turn. You can innately cast Shackles of Pain as a swift action, taking up one of your shackles while the effect persists. Instead of its usual range, it has a range of reach. It also has an altered effect. It does not reduce any of the damage you take, but instead causes chained creatures to take half the damage you take as true damage. 11 - Final Seal: Generosity (Su) [[fear] When you break the final seal, your chains now have an additional 15 ft of reach during your turn. Your share my pain feature can target up to 4 creatures within reach at once. * Fiendish Growths Numbers indicate level requirements (9 total) 1 - Infernal Shackle (Su) By spending 1 mana when you land an attack against a creature with your chains or claws, you can shackle them. One of your chains pops out of your body and stays put at the point they were at when you attacked them. At the end of their next turn, they are pulled back to that position and your chain returns to you. The chain immediately breaks if the creature gets more than 60 feet away, or if someone uses athletics to break it as a standard action. In any case, escaped chains reappear attached to you. 1 - Bits and Pieces After a battle, you can peruse the bodies of your defeated foes for pieces of them you like, such as their eyes, teeth, feathers, scales etc. You can surgically remove them with your claws and add them to yourself after 1 minute, either replacing that part of your body or you somehow make room. Increase your max hp by 1 each piece. The parts need to be fresh, so waiting for more than 5 minutes ruins their potency. Unchained shifters are a bit picky and will only add pieces of creatures of their level or higher (though they won't retroactively remove pieces as they level up). You can have a maximum of (3+3*LvL) pieces. 1 - Return the Favor Gain combat reflexes as a bonus feat. Whenever you provoke an AoO, the attacking creature provokes an AoO from you. The act of provoking isn't enough and the creature can simply opt to not attack you to deprive you of your AoO. 1 - Flesh Wound healing When you are Defeated, you only become Drained 2. When recovering from being defeated, you are Staggered instead of Dazed. Whenever you take damage, keep a running tally of how much damage you have taken. After 1 minute of not taking damage, restore half of this value as hp. 1 - Masochism You can attack yourself, dealing damage as normal, requiring no attack roll. You gain +2 attack and +10 speed for 1 minute after doing so. If an ally attacks you, or includes you in the AoE of an effect that deals damage, you also get this bonus. Any damage you take during your turn has 100% effectiveness against creatures shackled by share my pain. 1 - Battered and Bruised While at or below 50% hp, you have +2 AC, +2 to all DCs you inflict and +2 to all saves. 4 - Sadism On-hit your attacks apply a stacking debuff (similar to shaken/sickened, max 4) called pain. Pain gives a creature weakness 1 to all damage types. Any healing effect removes all pain stacks. 6 - Sharing is Caring (Sp) You can use your share my pain feature on allies. Instead of its usual effect, it causes them to take half damage, redirecting the mitigated damage back to you. You can sever these chains as a free action. 8 - Shadenfreude (Su) (healing) Whenever an enemy within 60 ft of you fails a skill check, fails to even graze with an attack or fails a saving throw, you restore 5 hp.

entries.title: Youkai
entries.content: Youkai shifters have a powerful being sealed inside of them. As their relationship deepens, so too does their power. Youkai abilities are inspired by Japanese folklore. 1 - Imprisonment By concentrating, you can enter your own mind, allowing you to communicate directly with your youkai spirit in its prison. Youkai spirits tend to be powerful fiends unwillingly sealed inside of creatures, either to harness their power, or seal it away. 1 - Techniques You have Martial Specialization Your technique trees are Protector and Scoundrel. 1 - First Seal: Spirit Guide When you break the first seal, you gain FH 1 while below half hp. When you roll a natural 1, your youkai spirit intervenes, allowing you to Reroll it as an immediate action. You can break the first (second and final) seal as an immediate action to activate this reroll ability. 6 - Second Seal: Spirit Cooperation When you break the second seal, your FH increases to 2 while below half hp, and you gain FH 1 when at or above half hp. Even when you are deprived actions (such as being staggered, dazed or defeated), your youkai spirit can take any of the actions listed in the below abilities. - Spirit Siege - Spirit Guide - Spirit Guardian - Spirit Simulacrum 11 - Final Seal: Quickened Spirit When you break the final seal, your FH turns into regeneration, converted back to FH by poison or fire damage. Through you and your youkai spirit acting in tandem, you gain an additional swift action each turn. * Fiendish Growths Numbers indicate level requirements (8 total) 1 - Blood Claws Su Note: If you are not missing hp or are not bleeding, you cannot use this ability. By taking 1 True Damage for each attack, you can make a Thrown claw attack by digging your claws into your wound and throwing blades of blood at an enemy. 1 - Vigilance Your youkai spirit is always watching over you, even when you do not manifest it. You get a +4 bonus +Perception and Signature Skill for profession. You cannot be surprised by enemies ambushing you. 1 - Spirit Guardian In combat your spirit actively participates to defend you. You cannot be flanked. Your spirit can possess your body and move with a 30 ft hover speed and automatically spend your standard action to disengage, even if you lack those actions from being dazed for example. When you are defeated, creatures must still beat your AC or poise break you to attack/move you. 1 - Spirit Snatcher (Su) Healing When you grapple a creature, your spirit also grabs a piece of their soul, making them fatigued. If you are not poise broken by the start of your next turn, your spirit devours this piece of soul. The creature you grappled becomes exhausted and drained 5. You regain (the creature's LvL) HP. A creature can only be affected by spirit snatcher 1/day. The creature remains Exhausted for as long as they have the drained condition. 1 - Spirit Knowledge You can tap into the wisdom of the powerful spirit sealed inside of you. You get +History and +Insight as bonus skills, each with a +4 bonus. You can offer yourself up to the spirit, causing you to be Drained 5. This allows you to Reroll a Skill Check. 4 - Spirit Simulacrum (Su) Figment Fear As a standard action, you can "free" your youkai spirit. At least that's how everyone around you interprets it. With a dramatic flourish, like an explosion, lightning crashing or something similarly flashy, your youkai spirit emerges from you to give your enemies a scare. - All enemies within 60 ft are Frightened (will reduces to shaken 1) for 1 round. A creature that passes itself cannot be frightened or shaken by this ability for 1 hour. Even when revealed to be fake, the hysteria induced status remains. - You get a +10 bonus to coercion and can do so untrained. This bonus applies to uses out of combat, and to creatures you defeat in combat after using this ability. 8 - Third Eye You grow a third eye, giving you the Innate Technique Glimpse of Truth 1/day. As a swift action, you can fire a laser beam from this eye. Roll a d4 to see what you get, then choose a target. Make an Thrown attack. It deals 3d6 (10) damage with a random type and bonus effect. | 1 - Smoke | Fire damage. Creature has Blurred Vision On-hit for 1 round. | | ---------- | ------------------------------------------------------------------------ | | 2 - Blood | Poison damage. You heal equal to damage dealt. | | 3 - Shadow | Necrotic damage. Creature is Bone Chilled on-hit for 1 round. | | 4 - Ice | Frost damage. Creature is Frosty on hit. | You can decide which eye beam you would like to use, but doing so closes your third eye, disabling this ability for 1 minute. You cannot use this ability while blinded or while you have blurred vision, but you also negate any other penalties from such status. 10 - Spirit Siege Your youkai spirit plays a much more active role in combat. It is capable of dashing around the battlefield before quickly returning to defend you once again. As a swift action, limited to once each round, you can command your spirit to do one of the following actions. | Action | Effect | | ------ | --------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | | Crush | Melee attack a creature within 30 feet. It is Dazed for 1 turn on hit with no other effect (fort reduces to stagger) | | Block | You gain Improved Cover against 1 specific creature you can see. Spaces moving towards you are considered difficult terrain for the creature. | | Slice | Make a simple attack against the creature within 30 ft using your claw damage. | Transform Turn into a high level fiend?

entries.title: Fiend Shifter
entries.content: A Fiend Shifter is someone who has stolen the secrets to the powers of Fiends, slowly letting this power overtake them. They only grow into a more terrifying monster capable of combating even the most terrifying the fiendish realms have to offer with the very same power they wield. | +HP (martial) | 20(+10 per level) | | ------------------------ | ----------------- | | Poise | 8 | | Skills | 4 | | Saves | Fort | | Memory | 3+lvl | 1 - Techniques Your technique specialization varies by Archetype. You gain 1 technique point this level and 2 levels after. 1 - The First Seal You add fiend to your type, in addition to any others you possess. As a free action, deal 1 true damage to yourself to break the first seal. This gives you bonuses based off your archetype. This lasts for 1 minute, or until you exit combat (or something similarly strenuous), whichever is longer. 1 - Shifter Claws You possess claws on your hands that you can use to make natural attacks. They deal 2d8 slashing damage if your hands are empty, or d10 if they are not. Any other natural attacks that you gain through this class share this damage, but often with a different damage type. These attacks can be used interchangeably with claws. Your claws share the properties of a single weapon in your possession, such as magic enchantment, a magic focus' ability to cast spells, or special attacks. You can bond to a new weapon with a 1 minute ritual requiring concentration and contact with the weapon. 1 - Multiattack 2 claw attacks. 1 - Fiendish Growth Gain a fiendish growth from your chosen archetype, or the universal list. Get another one at level 2, 4, 6, 8 and 10. 1 - One of Us Su poly, true The physical transformation into a fiend shifter is a dramatic one that can draw unnecessary attention. You gain a specific form reminiscent of your original form that you can revert back to as a standard action. Breaking the seal or using a feature gained through fiendish growth ends this disguise. 1- Skill You get +Planes as a bonus skill with a +4 bonus. 1 - Deific Boons You start play with 10 GBP for any gods that are Demon Lords, unless your WIS is -1 or lower. 2 - Telepathy (Su) You can speak directly into the minds of other creatures you can see. A creature must have an intelligence score of at least -2 in order to be telepathically communicated with. A creature can telepathically project its thoughts to several creatures in the nearby vicinity, even if it cannot see them. Once a creature initiates a telepathic conversation, the other creature can communicate back using telepathy. The creature that initiated the telepathic conversation can choose to end it whenever they wish. 4 - Hell and Back Sp recharge You gain Hell and Back as an Innate Technique, usable 1/day. 6 - Second Seal (Su) You break the second seal, improving the ability that you gained at first level. 8 - Shadow Realm (Su) You can banish a willing or helpless creature to The Demon Realm. The creature is unable to return through any means other than a naturally existing portal. The Fiend Shifter can telepathically communicate with banished creatures. They can also stun themselves to observe them, as though floating overhead. 11 - Final Seal (Su) You break the final seal, perfecting the ability that you gained at first level.

entries.title: Universal Fiendish Growths
entries.content: The numbers next to the abilities are level requirements (4 total so far) Any archetype can select these options. 1 - Horny Gain 2 special attacks, usable with a new gore attack (deals pierce damage). Knockdown: On-hit make an additional topple attack. Knockback: On-hit make an additional shove attack. 4 - Shape Change Su poly, true You can change your appearance to that of any small or medium race. Can change back as a standard action, or as a swift action when you break the seal. 6 - Bloody Hellfire (Su) persistent fire Gain a breath weapon. Unleash a 30 ft cone or 60 ft line of flames that persists until the start of your next turn. Creatures within the area, or that later move into it take (2d6+d6/odd lvl) fire damage (reflex halves). This ability ignores fire resistance. A creature damaged also starts Bleeding for d6. Recharged by consuming an organ of a freshly killed race, or through rest. 6 - Crush Resolve Once each turn you can Demoralize a creature On-hit with a Melee Attack. 10 - Death From Above Grow a pair of wings that grant you a Fly speed equal to your land speed.

entries.title: Aran
entries.content: Fighter Archetype. Cleave through waves of enemies eventually gaining the strength to spin through entire armies. Aran is the premier AoE DPS martial. Spin to win! 1 - Smash Swing Gain weapon focus (slash) as a bonus feat. You can use great cleave at will. When you cleave, it is referred to as Smash Swing. 1 - Combat Step (Elusive) If you defeat at least one enemy during Smash Swing, you may activate Combat Step as a swift action to move an additional 10 feet without spending your movement. Cleave normally requires you stand still to attack all foes in range, but movement using combat step ignores this, allowing you to continue your cleave as you step through combat. You still can't continue the endless cleave with your normal movement. At level 6, Combat Step is always a free action. 2 - Combo After defeating an enemy with a melee attack, you get another Superior Attack that must be used before the end of your turn (doesn't work off-turn). Whenever you defeat another creature past the first in a round, get you an additional Combat Step that can be used as a free action. 4 - Hoard Breaker - Your melee attacks ignore damage resistance to single target attacks from group type enemies. - Your cleave attacks against group enemies always Crit On-hit. - You cannot be flanked 6 - Combo Drain (Healing) Defeating an enemy heals 3 hp. 8 - Aero Swing If you Smash Swing while in the air, you may spend all of your remaining movement to Fly in place until the start of your next turn. You may use this to fly using your Combat Steps, should you defeat enemies in the air. You cannot be made clumsy while using aero swing.

entries.title: Basic Bitch Fighter
entries.content: Fighter Archetype. For when you really don't want to think about complicated class features. 1 - Superior Stats You gain a bonus feat every level. Every time you gain a technique point, you can choose to instead turn it into a feat. You get access to the following feats, each of which can be taken any number of times. Ability Increase - Increase an Ability Score by +1 (up to +3) Super Strength - Increase STR by +1 (up to +5). Super Armor - Gain +1 AC (up to +3). Power Stance - Gain +3 Poise. Paladin Cosplay - Get a +1 bonus to Saves (up to +3). Superior Genetics - Get an additional major feature from your race.

entries.title: Bloody Finger
entries.content: Fighter Archetype. Suddenly end fights with the power cause creatures to explode into bloody chunks long after you cut them with your blade. Gank people from across the realm who dare intrude upon your territory. ![Bloody Finger.png|by [Kero|412]] 1 - Bleed Out Any piercing or slashing weapon you wield gains the bleed property. On-hit with a bleed weapon (even if it does no damage), keep a running tally of how many times the creature was hit. This number is referred to as "bleed stacks" and is separate from other bleed effects. Crits apply 2 bleed stacks. Bleed stacks are visible on the creatures body and last until you rest. Even if a creature cannot bleed, it can still gain bleed stacks. 1 - Hemorrhage (Su) Sheathe your weapon(s) as a standard action. Creatures that have bleed stacks (no matter the distance) lose all stacks and take 2d6 (7) True Damage. Creatures with 5+ stacks take 8d6 (28) true damage. Creatures with 10+ stacks take 16d6 (56) damage. This damage continues to double every 5 stacks infinitely. 1 - Bloodied Finger One of the bloody finger's fingers is always a crimson red. It can be used to write in a bloody ink. 1 - Weeb Training You can dual wield one handed weapons using dex or strength as long as the weapon has a Japanese name. If you wield a Japanese weapon in two hands, it applies 2 bleed stacks on hit, or 4 on a crit. 2 - Special Attack: Delayed Doom This attack deals 0 apparent damage. Roll damage anyway and take note of it. The next time you use hemorrhage, any creature you struck with delayed doom takes all of this stored damage. You can use this special attack at will. 4 - Invasion (Sigil) You can inscribe your name, or a symbol of significance to you on a surface using your bloodied finger. When a creature triggers the sigil, you get a glimpse of the surrounding area briefly. Anytime it is triggered, you can choose to crawl out of the sigil as though you were using a Summoning Contract to summon yourself, causing the sigil to disappear. You can stay summoned this way for 5 minutes before you return to your body. You cannot use this feature while you have summoning sickness. If you create a sigil while you already have one, the old one disappears. 6 - Rivers of Blood (Su) As a standard action, slash the air rapidly, causing many bloody projectiles to fly out in a 30 ft cone in front of you. Creatures in the area take 6d6 (21) slash or pierce damage and get 4 bleed stacks (reflex halves damage and bleed stacks). This ability recharges when you use your hemorrhage ability against a creature with 5 or more bleed stacks. 8 - Blood Scent Gain Scent (120 ft) and Seeking against wounded creatures. 11 - Lord of Blood Whenever a creature takes bleed damage within 30 ft of you, they gain a bleed stack.

entries.title: Dark Knight
entries.content: Fighter Archetype. Stand stalwart in the face of danger, an immovable obstacle that your opponents must fight tooth and nail to merely walk past, let alone survive. Outside of combat your massive skill bonuses can allow you to sense what others don't, and sniff out liars. Based on the Maplestory class of the same name. 1 - Evil Eye (Su) You can manifest an ominous looking eye that floats around you, enhancing your senses and giving you a palpable aura of menace. You get proficiency in +Intimidation and +Insight. You get a (4+½ LvL) bonus to +Perception, insight and intimidation. You also add this bonus to your will save against Demoralize. You can choose to make this eye disappear 1/turn as a free action, but you lose the bonuses to perception and intimidation. 1 - No Escape All effects that grant elusive movement do not function against you while Evil Eye is active. Creatures can tell you still threaten to attack them in such cases. If a creature tries to move around you using avoid AoO while wield a reach weapon, you add a bonus to your reflex save equal to the perception bonus granted by Evil Eye. 1 - Hyper Body You get a +4 bonus to Fortitude saves. Your starting HP increases by 10. Each level after you gain an addition 4 hp. 2 - Stalwart You take half damage from fortitude saves. When you pass a fort save, it deals no damage and has no additional effect. 4 - Polearm Mastery You can make an infinite number of AoOs, and you have a +4 to Attack with AoOs. 6 - Despair When a creature ends its turn within your reach, it becomes shaken 1 (Will negates). Minions are automatically shaken. 8 - Special Attack: Impale (Su) You can leave behind a phantom weapon inside of your target on-hit. The creature is is unable to leave your natural reach and is Anchored. This condition only ends if you are disarmed, dazed, stunned, you or the target are moved out of reach or you are defeated. You can use this special attack an infinite number of times, but if you impale a second creature, the first is freed. 11 - Hyper Army As a standard action, you can inspire all allies that can see you, giving them (10+4xLvL of creature) additional current and maximum hp until they rest, or until you are defeated.

entries.title: Full Attacker
entries.content: Fighter Archetype. Feeling nostalgic about 3e, 3.5e or PF1e? Well here's me doing my best to recreate that experience in a slightly less heavy handed way than BB fighter. The full attacker is the fightiest guy, all about gaining insane amounts of movement speed and then using that speed to gain more actions. Woe to those who dare to stand in your face. 1 - Who's Next? If you defeat an enemy and don't have enough speed to reach a new enemy, you can move until you threaten the next closest enemy, unless your speed is 0. 1 - Full Attack Any turn you spend your standard action to make an attack, can make an additional MELEE superior attack a turn by sacrificing 45 speed once per turn. You cannot use techniques or special attacks after full attacking. If you use a special attack or technique during your turn, you cannot full attack. 1 - Fervor of Battle During combat, your speed increases by 15 this level and another 5 every level after. You must always end your movement with an enemy in reach. If you ever end your turn without an enemy in reach for any reason, you become clumsy 1. 2 - Action Surge Each one can be used in place of a superior attack and they all share a global cooldown, only able to be used 1/round. You cannot sacrifice superior attacks gained from baby slayer to use these abilities. - 5 Steps Ahead (elusive) - Prepare for an incoming attack. While preparing you can trigger this ability as an immediate action, moving up to 30 feet to the opposite side of a creature that affects you with a harmful effect. You get a +4 bonus to AC and saves against this effect. - Random Bullshit Go - Throw an item that isn't a weapon. Improvised weapons can still be used. If you throw a harmful item, such as a toxin, it applies on hit (then forcing saving throws as normal). If you throw a helpful item, such as a potions, its effects function as normal, ignoring any broken vials that may get involved. If for whatever reason an effect requires multiple rounds to take effect, that is still the case for this ability. - Cunning-ish Action - You can disengage, Jump, aid, focus or make a +Combat Maneuver. - Brute Force - Roll a Brute Force check. 11 - Fullest Attack When you full attack, you can sacrifice an additional 45 speed to make an additional attack with the same restrictions.

entries.title: Iron Knuckle
entries.content: Fighter Archetype. Your armor is a second skin to you. Your armor is also your first skin. The iron knuckle has 2 "phases" to their battles. The first is a walking tank with multiple layers of armor, durable and stubborn until you get to the squishy single layer of plate armor underneath. The second is a nimble warrior that gets a boost of adrenaline, feeling light as a feather just after their outer shell is cracked open. Iron knuckles are practically invincible to being attacked head on while layered up, and must be ganged up on. Mages will find a much easier time breaking the outer layer. !Iron Knuckle, as it appears in the LoZ series|312 1 - Metallurgy You are fused with a set of standard metal plate armor. You can enhance this armor with new metals and enchantments with a more involved crafting process. You are permanently treated as though you are wearing heavy armor. It is part of you and cannot be separated without causing permanent life-threatening damage to you. Because this armor is a part of you, it being destroyed hurts you. Whenever your armor is destroyed/degraded you are Drained 10. You add construct to your creature types. You gain proficiency in the profession (blacksmith) skill. 1 - Layer Up You can choose to put on an additional layer of heavy armor over the first, providing you with the following benefits after 1 minute of preparation. - 18+LvL AC (instead of the 16+LvL given by normal heavy armor) - DRA 2 (instead of the 1 given by normal heavy armor) - 20 Poise - You benefit from the Quake technique constantly While layered up you also take a -10 penalty to your speed and cannot fly or hover. Enemies can remove layer up by dealing damage that drops you below half hp. It takes 3 rounds to remove layer up manually. It requires a set of heavy armor in order to use layer up. When an enemy removes layer up, you cannot use that particular set of heavy armor to layer up again until you repair it. Be sure to pick it up after the battle! 1 - Light as a Feather After carrying the weight of incredibly heavy metal armor during the heat of battle, to have the burden removed suddenly fills you with new vigor. When an enemy removes layer up from you, you become light as a feather, gaining the following benefits until combat ends. - You gain (10+2*LvL) temp hp (healing), potentially preventing you from dying from single instances of damage. - Your speed and jump distance increases by 10 until combat ends (or you layer up again) - You are affected by the Unbreakable Advance technique until combat ends (or you layer up again). - You gain access to the sudden strike special attack until combat ends. Special Attack: Sudden Strike - Advance 10 ft towards an enemy (elusive) and make a melee attack. On-hit the target is pushed back 5 feet and knocked down. Deals an additional (d6 per odd LvL) damage. 2 - Bully You gain the Power Pose feat. When you use it, it deals 15 poise damage instead of 10. If you are layered up then you can use it as an instant action 1/turn. You can use power pose against AoOs an unlimited number of times as an instant action. You can choose if you want to leave a creature at 1 poise, or poise break them with this ability. If a creature is poise broken by this ability then they are made clumsy 2, or you can push them 10 ft back. While light as a feather, when a creature misses you with an AoO, or you successfully use acrobatics to evade AoOs, you can make a bully attack as an instant action. 4 - Bull Rush If you are layered up, when you shove a creature, you can move with them to increase the shove distance by your remaining movement. While light as a feather, you gain powerful charge. 6 - Adrenaline If you are layered up then you are immune to fatigue. You gain the innate technique adrenaline burst while light as a feather. 8 - Invincible You are immune to attack rolls made by minions while layered up. Any minion that attacks you is instead set to 1 poise automatically as it bounces off of your metal body. This is treated as a miss. While light as a feather, you gain resistance to damage from failed saving throws (aka you always take half damage, but if you fail, you still suffer any additional effects). 10 - Back Massage Whenever a creature misses or grazes you, they are knocked back 10 ft. If you also poise break them with Power Pose as part of the same attack, you can push them 20 ft. 11 - Special Attack: Iron Wave Strike at a foe, dealing an additional 6d6 (21) damage. If you are layered up, gain temp hp and poise equal to the total damage dealt. If light as a feather, also immediately layer up again until this temp hp is lost. This poise recovery cannot exceed your poise maximum.

entries.title: Kazekenshin
entries.content: Fighter Archetype. Wind Swordsman are focused on being annoying and slippery with an incredibly powerful signature attack: Steel Tempest. Their abilities are centered around mobility to get into a better position to use this attack. 1 - Steel Tempest Su Air Once per round when you land a melee attack against a creature, gain a stack of gathering storm. With two stacks of gathering storm, you can use the following special attack. This special attack can be used any number of times in a combat. Your attack whips up a whirlwind, targeting all enemies in a 60 ft line. Compare your attack roll to the ac of all creatures in the line. Enemies are Staggered On-hit for one round and take an additional d6/odd lvl damage. You cannot gain gathering storm stacks in the same turn you use steel tempest. 1 - Flow Keep a running tally of how far you move. Every time you move 60 ft, you gain a protective barrier that gives you DRA (3+1/2xLvL) for the next instance of damage you take. 1 - Sweeping Blade (Elusive) Once per enemy per round, unless your speed is 0 or you are unable to make attacks, you can choose to dash in a straight line through a enemy within reach as a free action, appearing directly on the opposite side of the creature adjacent to them. This movement doesn't count against your movement each round. Any movement around a creature you target with this ability is also elusive for this turn. You can combine sweeping blade with steel tempest, creating a new unique effect. You can either attack a 60 ft line as usual, or you can choose to attack a 10 ft radius around you at your destination. 2 - Wind Wall (Sp) (recharge) You can innately cast Wind Wall. You can use wind wall as an immediate action, but its size is decreased to a 15x15 square, its duration changes to 1 round and its range decreases to reach. It does not impede you or your allies' movement or attacks. The penalty to ranged attacks also applies to the DC of any ability that must physically pass through the wall. It would not apply to a spell like charming gaze, but it would apply to an effect like bestial claw. You can only use this ability again after 1 minute passes. 4 -Ride the Wind You can use sweeping blade against a single target you attack with steel tempest, appearing on the opposite side of them from your current position. 6 - Already Dead (Teleportation) As an immediate action, you can teleport adjacent to any creature you can see within 30 feet that gets inflicted with a debilitating status effect (such stagger, poisoned, sickened) and make a simple attack against them. This ability must be used in response to the status being inflicted, it cannot be used against a creature that merely happened to be status'd. 8 - Last Breath Creatures hit by steel tempest are dazed for one round (fort reduces to stagger). 11 - Storm of Steel Steel tempest's line attack increases in width. Instead of a 60 ft line that is 5 ft wide, it is 15 ft wide, essentially targeting all spaces adjacent to the line. When combined with sweeping blade, the AoE increases to a 20 ft radius around you at your destination.

entries.title: Standard Fighter
entries.content: Fighter Archetype. Rally your allies around a point and fight together. Your standard is an annoying attack that you can to enhance allies on the battlefield. All of your class features are centered around rallying around and the manipulation of this standard. 1 - Martial Standard (Su) (Force) As a swift action, summon a flag from the sky and have it slam down onto an unoccupied space within 30 ft of you. Enemies within 5 ft of it take (1+LvL) Force damage. You can choose to automatically pick up your flag by moving into its space, recharging this ability. A creature can knock over your flag by rolling athletics as a swift action (DC 10+your athletics bonus), causing it to disappear and recharging this ability. The flag automatically disappears after 1 minute. 1 - Flag Aura While within 60 ft of your flag, your allies share your teamwork feats. Your flag counts as an ally to you for the purposes of fulfilling teamwork feat conditions. Gain a teamwork feat at lvl 1, 4 and 8. 1 - Secret Language: Semaphore You speak the language of flag twirling. When communicating, you do it letter by letter, limiting its use to simple messages unless you spend a very long time. Your flag bears the mark of a country, guild or religion of significance. Members of the countries army or officials of the country, important members of your religion or guild etc. intuitively understand your messages. 2 - Bullying Blow Before you make any attacks on your turn, you can decide to forfeit the ability to make combat maneuvers until the start of your next turn. When you do so, attacks deal poise damage equal to the hp damage they deal until the start of your next turn. 4 - Slap Sense (Healing) Once per round in place of an attack, you can slap some sense into a creature suffering from a Mind-Affecting effect or is Asleep. They take 1 True Damage and get an additional save against an effect of your choosing. If the effect had no DC, use the creature/entities offense trait DC/class DC. 4 - Capture the Flag Make a charge attack targeting your flag from any distance, as long as you can see it and there is a direct path. Any creature in your path is automatically pushed out of your way on either side of you (your choice). You can melee attack any creature within reach once during this charge. You can choose to superior attack during the charge as well, allowing you to strike one particular creature twice. 6 - Bolster You can use the aid action as a swift action. If your flag is within your natural reach when you use this feature, you use the aid action to give the same bonus to all creatures in your flag's aura. 6 - Swag Flag (recharge) If you summon your flag and a creature is defeated by it, you can summon it again at a new location as a free action. Usable 1/turn. 8 - Golden Aegis (Su) (Recharge) When you summon your flag, and at the start of each of your turns, choose an ally to grant a divine shield. Can only happen 1/turn, regardless how many times you summon your flag. 11 - Supreme Standard (Su) When you summon your flag, you can choose to activate this ability or not for its duration. The outer radius of your flags aura has a visible barrier around it. Creatures can pass through it freely, but attacks, spells, offense traits, thrown objects, weather effects and plenty of other things cannot. Any AoE effects are halted along the barriers edge. Even persistent ones like darkness or wall of fire are cut off. Whichever side of the barrier had more of the effect or it center is where it persists. In general, creatures on opposite sides of the barrier have line of sight, but do not have Line of Effect. You can choose to decrease the size of the aura to 30 ft as part of summoning it.

entries.title: Swashbuckler
entries.content: Fighter Archetype. Swashbuckler are the best duelists around, with their signature parry and riposte ability giving them an edge over virtually all martial combatants, especially in melee. Their swift action is incredibly valuable, and can be spent in a number of ways that are powerful in their own ways. 1 - Parry and Riposte With a light weapon in your main hand and a buckler in your offhand, you can parry at any time. If you choose to use an unarmed strike or natural attack, you need a buckler in your off hand and a completely free main hand. Parry functions in 2 different ways. Prepare to Parry - You can choose to forgo any number of attacks during your turn. Until the start of your next turn you can parry as an instant action a number of times equal to the number of attacks you decided to forego. If you successfully negate an attack, you can riposte and make a melee attack against the creature as an instant action. You get Advantage on any attacks made to parry or riposte when you prepare to parry. Reactive Parry - You can attempt to parry an attack without preparing by spending 1 mana. If you successfully negate an attack, you can riposte make a melee attack against the creature as an immediate action. If your foe attacked with a ranged attack, or with a weapon that has the reach property, you can only retaliate if they are within your reach. You can parry without a buckler, but take a -4 penalty to attack when you parry. 1 - Heavy Mettle When wearing light or no armor, and wielding a single light weapon, get the following benefits. - +13+LvL AC - (4+½ LvL) bonus damage with light weapons 1 - Practiced Duelist You gain +Deception as a skill. You get a +4 bonus on deception checks to feint, and an equal bonus on +Insight checks to resist feint. You can increase the duration of feint to last until the start of your next turn by spending 1 mana. You gain It's Time to Duel! as an innate technique. 1 - Book 'o' Piracy You speak the language pirates. While not a secret language, it is incredibly difficult to understand despite it only being a modified version of common. A creature must be trained in linguistics to see past the many layers of slang that typify pirate lingo. 2 - Charmed Life The first time you use luck to add to a d20 roll each round, it can be done as an instant action. By spending 2 mana, you can reroll a save as an immediate action. 2 - Dodging Panache elusive When you are hit or grazed by an attack, spend 1 mana as an immediate action to gain a +4 bonus to AC against a that attack and move 5 ft. 4 - Superior Feint As a standard action, automatically feint a creature. It is feinted for all allies as well until the end of your next turn. A creature you use superior feint against becomes immune to it for 1 minute. 6 - Focus You can take the focus action as a swift action. While you focus, you also get a +4 bonus to... - AoOs made against this creature - Reflex saves against the creature's +Acrobatics to avoid AoO - +Perception against this creature 8 - Special Attack: Targeted Strike By spending 2 mana, choose a specific body part to target to gain a bonus effect On-hit (fort negates). Special: This special attack can be used more than once a combat by spending more mana. | Body Part | Effect | | --------- | -------------------------------------------------- | | Head | The target is Confused for 1 round | | Legs | The target is Knocked Down | | Wings | The target is grounded for 1 round | | Neck | The target bleeds for 2d8 (9) damage | | Torso | The target is Staggered for 1 round | 11 - Child's Play Reactive parry requires no mana. When you riposte after a reactive parry, it is an instant action instead of an immediate action.

entries.title: Fighter
entries.content: Men-at-arms clad in steel that use various fighting styles to achieve the same goal: destroy as many enemies as possible using overwhelming martial prowess. ![deadly arsenal.jpg|[Deadly Arsenal, Hearthstone|512]] | Basic Statistics | | | ----------------------- | ---------------- | | +HP | +10/LvL | | Poise | +8 | | Skills | 2 | | Saves | +2 Fortitude | | Memory | 3+LvL | 1 - Techniques You have martial specialization. You gain 1 technique point at level 1, then another point every 2 levels after. These technique points can be used on any martial tree. 1 - Superior Attack Once per any turn where you're capable of taking the attack action, you can make a simple attack as a free action, or as an additional simple attack if you do take the attack action. 1 - Weapon Training You have +2 to Attack with weapon attacks, natural attacks and unarmed strikes. You have DR 5 vs disarm. 1 - Armor Training Armors requires 2 less STR to use. You ignore the +Stealth penalty to heavy armor. 1 - Physical Training Gain athletics as a skill with a +4 bonus. You can Breakout an ally as a swift action, regardless of the action it usually took to breakout. 2 - Bravery You have resistance to fear effects. 2 - Always Ready When combat starts, if you are below full hp, heal to full HP. 2 - Baby Slayer When an enemy is a minion, you automatically know just by looking at them. Gain an additional superior attack that can only be used against minion enemies. You are immune to being grazed by minions 4 - Smash From Air Gain Combat Reflexes as a bonus feat. If you already have it, gain a bonus feat. You can use an attack of opportunity to swat a ranged attack out of the air that targets either you or an ally within reach. Roll an attack roll. If your attack roll matches or exceeds theirs, the attack is negated. You take a -4 attack penalty to attacks opposing siege weapons. 6 - Striking Your weapon attacks, natural attacks and unarmed attacks gain additional damage equal to their damage dice. Examples: 2h weapon @1=2d8, @6=4d8 Light Weapon @1=d6, @6=2d6 8 - Weapon Spirit As a standard action you can enchant 2 different +Weapons (including unarmed attacks and natural attacks) you hold with any single quality from the magic weapon properties section, except Bane. You can enchant 2 different weapons, and give them different enchants. You can enchant new weapons, but the old ones lose their enchantments. Weapons cannot have typically more than 1 enchantment, unless they have multiple enchantment slots.

entries.title: Mom GBP
entries.content: TierDescription/benefitTier 1(1k+ GBP)You are on moms good side. She loves you very much.Get +1 attack and CL while with momTier 2(10k+ GBP)You would die for mom. You inspire others to join your family.Get +2 attack or CL while with mom.Tier 3(100k GBP)Mom gives you a gold star. Certified favorite.Get +3 attack or CL while with mom. Methods of acquiring GBP are listed below... GiftsMom does not accept gold as gifts. Gifts must be personalized and thoughtful. Regardless of cost, a gift gives 100 GBP. You cannot give gifts more often than 1/week, resetting at the end of each weekend.MVPAt the end of each encounter, Mom awards someone an MVP award. They gain 100 GBP. Encounters can include non combat scenarios as well (at DM discretion).Very Good!Whenever a family member rolls a natural 20, they get 10 GBP. Randomly rolling dice to fish for bonus points is discouraged.

entries.title: Mom
entries.content: Crust Moms exist to support their team outside of battle just as much as they do inside the battle. 1 - Emotional Support healing Mom can remove any tier 1 affliction with 1 hour of coddling. You gain a unique T1 affliction (see Afflictions for more details). Other moms cannot remove this affliction with emotional support. | Name | Effect | Removal Method | | --------- | ------------------------------------ | -------------- | | Mom'd out | Cannot use emotional support feature | Standard | 1 - Hug Su When you initiate a grapple on an enemy, they are pacified for 1 round instead of the usual effects. By spending 1 mana as a standard action, you can grapple an ally to put them in stasis for 1 round and remove all shaken stacks. 1 -Good Boy Points Mom says she doesn't play favorites, but we all know that’s a lie. Everybody must fight for Mom's favor. Mom keeps track of all the good things her children (everybody) do. This functions like God Benediction Points (see gods) with 3 tiers of favor. More details in the GBP section. 1 - Call Me T1 good boys can innately use the upcast version of sending 1/day, but only to contact mom. 1 - Professional Mom You get a unique +Profession proficiency called Mom with a +4 bonus. You can use to to perform any type of mom-y activities, such as daycare services for children. Tangential/stereotypical features of momming, such as cooking, cleaning and other such things can use this skill, but do not receive the +4 bonus. 2 - Lost and Found recharge Mom has a 6th sense for objects of significance, and often randomly picks them up for safekeeping. You can procure a specific item a creature once owned that someone mentions they misplaced, so long as you have been to a location it could be reasonably located.\ Ex. "Oh you guys went into Old Man's Cave? I remember going there once. Lost my wedding ring there." "This wouldn't happen to be it would it?" You can't use it to proactively search for something, like a secret entrance, or a sacred artifact, unless an ancient resurrected hero is missing their sword and it was last seen in a place you’ve been (and it wasn't closely guarded). This doesn't let you retrieve items that are in someone else's possession and the DM can choose to deny particularly unlikely circumstances. Recharges every Monday. 4 - Mommy Energy You exude a palpable aura of love. Creature's find it difficult to harm you. Creatures who attempt to harm you must redirect their aggression for the remainder of their turn (will negates). This does not consume the action, but it does force them to alter it, such as attacking another creature, or excluding you from an AoE. Once a creature passes their save, or you deal damage, mom is on the menu and this aura is gone. 6 - Support+ Mom can remove Tier 2 afflictions with emotional support. 11 - Support++ Mom can remove Tier 3 afflictions with emotional support.

entries.title: Divine Protector
entries.content: Crust Divine protectors are incredibly frustrating healers that not only excel at healing wounds, but in preventing them altogether through the use of divine shields. Replaces a single technique point you get at level 1. Your lay on hands loses the overheal tag and cannot grant temp hp. 1 - Technique You gain a technique point in the protector tree at this level and every 2 levels after. 1 - Divine Shield (Su) Gain a Divine Shield that naturally reforms after not taking damage for 1 minute. As a standard action you can refresh your divine shield. As a standard action, you can give an ally within reach a divine shield. These shields last 10 minutes. 1 - Blaze of Glory (Su) recharge When you are defeated, you go out in a blaze of glory. All your allies within 60 ft (minus yourself) are immediately granted a divine shield. This can only happen once a minute. 6 - Front Lines While you have a divine shield, your actions are elusive. 10 - Rally Movement of allies (except you) within ??? is elusive. 11 - Divine Army If you spend 1 mana per creature affected, creatures you heal with holy arrow or channel energy gain a divine shield.

entries.title: Explorer
entries.content: Explorers are the generalist healers with a handful of utility abilities that assist with travel. They promote treating creatures with mercy whenever possible, and in the most extreme examples, can make death impossible. Their aura is powerful enough that existing near them makes you safer. 1 - Aura of Mercy (Su) Gain a 15ft aura in which creatures cannot gain stacks of Doom. Enemies also do not die when defeated in this aura. This aura is suppressed when you are defeated, or activated/deactivated as a free action on your turn. This aura has a distinct, personalized visual component that interferes with stealth. 1 - Lucky Break You add your Luck as a bonus to to the +HP you regain all healing effects you use on yourself or allies, except for effects that heal over time, like regeneration or golden shower. You can add luck to a roll an ally makes as an immediate action if they are within your aura of mercy. You still cannot add luck twice (ie you both add luck to the roll). 1 - Compassion (Su) mind-affecting When you use Channel Energy, enemies in range that were aggressors/not acting in self-defense are pacified for 5 rounds (will negates). They get a new save at the end of their turns if you or your allies take aggressive actions versus their allies, and it ends immediately if you or your allies take aggressive actions towards them. If there are no enemies left, initiative ends, and this ability can be extended for up to 10 minutes as long as you try resolving the conflict peacefully. 2 - Cure (Su) healing When you use healing touch, creatures get a new save against any lingering effects that would normally give additional saves (such as poison and some fear effects). Does not apply to permanent status like curses or drain. 2 - Endure Elements (Su) Creatures that touch your aura of mercy receive a blessing for 1 hour. This blessing allows them to exist inside of otherwise hazardous environments without harm. Blessed creatures ignore environmental effects like toxic volcanic gas, thin air at extreme heights, extremes in temperature, the vacuum of space or the pressure/lack of air in underwater environments. This does not make you immune to hazardous weather, such as hail, windstorms, lightning, quicksand etc. Only passive effects that prevent you from existing. 4 - Mystic Door (Su) Teleportation When you rest in a settlement of at least 100 people, you can mark one of the doorways, portals, or similar entrance in the place you rested as your Mystic Doorstep. Until you rest again, as long as you are within 10 miles of the place you slept, you can conjure a Mystic Door that links to the Mystic Doorstep for one minute. It is the same size as the entry you marked. It takes one minute to cast. Any creature can take the portal back simply by walking through it, but it is one-way. Once the Mystic Door disappears, so does your Mystic Doorstep. This effect does not require line of sight. 4 - Dispel (Su) recharge As a standard action, touch a creature within reach. On an ally, one harmful spell or Supernatural effect immediately ends, unless it is Permanent Status. This has the (healing) tag. On an enemy, this functions as Dispel Magic. For cursed items, this allows the safe removal of the item, but requires a caster level check. This has a 10 minute unique cooldown. 6 - Blessing of Restoration (Su) (healing) You recover from all stacks of drain every time you rest, regardless of conditions. As a standard action, you can touch a creature to absorb (LvL) stacks of drain from them and you become drained an equal amount. A creature can only be affected by this once each week. 8 - Elemental Protection (Su) Gain resistance to a non-physical damage type. Allies in your aura also gain this. You can change what you're resistant to by spending 10 minutes adjusting your magic. 10 - Angel Ray (Su) Your Holy Arrow has its range increased by 10 ft. 10 - Expanded Mercy Your Aura of Mercy has its radius increase to 25 ft. 11 - Benediction (Su) Creatures you heal with channel energy benefit from your Dispel ability (ignoring the cooldown), and can make a simple attack as an instant action.

entries.title: Symbiote
entries.content: You body is filled with foreign microscopic organisms that have taken a liking to their new home, and do their best to keep their host alive. You have developed a unique codependency with these creatures which manifests in strange organic growths on your body that give you a host of alien powers. Symbiote trades consistent healing with burst healing and higher disruption. Removes holy arrow. 1 - Enhanced Arm One of your arms comes with extra attachments, often including enhanced musculature, tentacles, claws and such. This hand cannot wield equipment like staves, weapons, shields etc. You gain a natural attack that does 2d8 damage (crush, pierce or slash, chosen on character creation). You get a +2 bonus when using this attack, increased to +8 when using combat maneuvers. 1 - Infection When you use your enhanced arm attack, infect enemies for 3 turns on hit or when you successfully poise break them. Infected enemies have any FH disabled. Also disables regeneration if it can be removed by a specific damage type (like fire). You have +2 to attack with your enhanced arm against infected creatures.\ Infection is removed automatically by any \[healing] effect or by administering first aid.\ Allies you use healing touch on also get infected. Channel energy applies infection to all enemies and allies. Enemies can remove this infection with a +medicine check as a standard action. 1 - Patient Zero (healing) You have FH (1+⅓ lvl). By using healing touch, or touching an ally within reach as a free action, you can infect them to give them this FH for 10 minutes. 1 - Rapid Growth (healing) (necrotic) (recharge) If a creature has gotten a share of your infection, you can spend a swift action to apply healing touch to all infected creatures. Allies regain hp and enemies take the same amount as necrotic damage and are staggered for 1 round (fort negates stagger). This ability removes the infection.
