Cannot be used in consecutive rounds. 2 - Organic Length Your reach with your enhanced arm increases by 5 ft during your turn. This reach increases by another 5 ft every even level (@4 +10 reach, @12 +30 reach). Size does not affect your reach with this limb. 4 - Swipe By spending 2 mana, you can target a (reach) ft cone. Creatures within take your arm damage, are infected and are knocked back to the end of the cone (reflex negates). Ex. At this level it is a 15 ft cone. @12 it is a 35 ft cone. 4 - Massive Shove Whenever you shove a creature, it is pushed to the edge of your reach in a straight line, then an additional 10 ft. Any other bonuses to shove distance also apply. Creatures impacting terrain are staggered for 1 round (fort negates). 6 - Siphon (persistent) (concentration) Spend 3 mana to dig your enhanced arm into the ground and have it emerge in a space within reach. A 10 ft radius around this point is consumed by your organic growth, turning it into difficult terrain, even to hovering creatures. Enemies that start their turn within this area or move into it take your enhanced arm damage. Enemies within the area are siphoned until they leave. You can choose to maintain this ability indefinitely at no additional cost. If you are moved from your position or poise broken, this effect ends. This effect must be used through a solid surface. You need line of sight, but do not need line of effect. 10 - Congo Line Whenever you shove a creature, all creatures in a line behind them are shoved as well (reflex negates). 11 - Terminal Enemies are infected until the effect is removed manually.\ Rapid growth doesn't end infections.

entries.title: Healer
entries.content: Healers are mages that combine natural talent, a breadth of experience, and a compassion for life that results in abilities that lift spirits and mend wounds, both literally and metaphorically. A healer's mere presence commands a high degree of respect, as even a single healer behind the scenes can turn the tides of a battle. For this reason, healers are often involuntarily pulled into conflicts that do not concern them, as every government, army, church, and adventuring group seeks out their abilities, and every enemy of said groups seek to eliminate them. | +HP (specialist) | 20(+6 per level) | | ------------------------ | ---------------- | | Poise | 5 | | Skills | 4 | | Saves | Will | | Memory | 2+2\*lvl | 1 - Technique You have Magical Specialization. You gain 2 technique points at level 1, then another point every 2 levels after. These technique points can be used on the Brazzeria (T) and goomshroom trees. 1 - Healing Touch Sp Healing Overheal Touch a creature to heal (3+CL) hp. If you Mana Burn 1, you may touch yourself as a swift action. Temp hp granted by this ability lasts 10 minutes. 1 - Channel (Sp) (healing) (Overheal) Recharge You can unleash a pulse of energy at your maximum power, healing all allies within 60ft for (5+2*CL) hp. 1 - Holy Arrow (Healing) Sp Holy As a standard action, conjure a magic bow and make a ranged attack with 20ft range. The target takes holy damage equal to Healing Touch. If this attack passes through allies, apply healing touch to them. Holy arrow also heals yourself. This can be used through a staff. This ranged attack counts as being made from a bow. 1 - Skill You are proficient in the +Medicine skill and get a +4 bonus to it. 1 - Innate Casting You can innately cast death ward 1 - Deific Boons You start play with 10 GBP for any gods that are not Demon Lords, or are otherwise overtly evil, unless your WIS is -1 or lower.

entries.title: Hero
entries.content: The Hero is someone who is born for adventure. They have an incredibly diverse skill set to accomplish their goals. The quests of the world seem to gravitate towards them, making sure they are never idle. | +HP (mixed) | 20(+8 per level) | | ------------------------ | ----------------- | | Poise | 8 | | Skills | 0...you'll see... | | Saves | Reflex | | Memory | 3+lvl | 1 - Combat Style Choose one of the following 3 options to specialize in... Melee - You gain 10 hp per level instead of 8. Gain martial specialization. Choose a martial technique tree. You gain a technique point in it at level 1 and every odd level after. You also gain a Multiattack. Multiattack - Make 2 melee attacks. Ranged - Gain martial specialization. Choose a martial technique tree. You gain a technique point in it at level 1 and every odd level after. You also gain a multiattack. Multiattack - Make 2 ranged attacks. Magic - Gain magical specialization. Choose 2 magical technique trees. You gain a technique point them at level 1 and every odd level after. Increase your memory by 2. 1 - Home Teleport (Su) (Teleportation) (recharge) By spending a week in a specific location, such as a house, clearing, or guild hall, you can designate it as home. By spending 1 minute concentrating on a ritual, you can teleport yourself and up to 8 creatures in physical contact with you back home. Requires 1 week to recharge. If the ritual is interrupted, this ability does not require recharge. Does not require line of sight. 1 - All Skill Gain 3 skill proficiencies. You gain the Advantage on skill checks you are proficient in. Every level you also gain a skill proficiency from the list below. Each of these is associated with a particular Runescape skill. You must be proficient in a skill in order to gain its skill unlocks. At this level and levels 3, 5, 7, 9 an 11 gain a skill unlock from the combat skills. At this level and levels 2, 4, 6, 8 and 10. gain a skill unlock from the utility skills. Skills: +Profession (Arson, wood cutting, mining, fishing, cooking, farming), +Knowledge (+Religion, Arcana, Engineering, Alchemy), +Survival, +Acrobatics 1 - Quest! Heroes are called to adventure, but there are a handful of quests that are rites of passage for true heroes. More information can be found in the quest subsection. 1 - Ditto Every hero proudly presents their finest victory in the form of a pet ditto: An ooze creature that can mimic the appearance of a creature that the hero has defeated in battle. It functions as a familiar. It knows a number of transformations equal to your level. If you choose to teach it a new form and it has no more slots, it forgets a previous one permanently. New transformations must at least match the level of its highest known transformation. It starts play only knowing 1 transformation of a level 0 creature. A ditto can never transform into a creatures with class levels. Your ditto becomes a level 0 copy, losing all defense traits (outside of base stats), offense traits (outside of its attacks) and feats. Its stats are adjusted to be a level 0 creature, effectively subtracting its LvL from things like AC, saves, poise etc. Its hp is 20. Its size is always tiny. It can only communicate in methods the creature it transforms into could. If it aids you in combat, it gains the companion tag. You can also use your familiar to do a handful of other useful things. Spying (scrying) - You can sense through your companion at will. Assistant - Your companion can use the aid action to assist you for any skill checks you are proficient in. To My Side (teleportation) - You can concentrate for 3 rounds to teleport your companion to your side. 2 - Gear Swap You can choose to also swap any worn equipment, such as armor or magic items, when you take the swap gear action. 6 - Combat Style+ Gain a new upgrade based on the combat style you chose at level 1, or choose a different combat style you did not choose at level 1. If you choose a different level 1 combat style, its boons are retroactive, granting you technique points/+HP you would have gained. Melee - As a swift action, you can enter one of 3 stances as a buff. They last until you gain another buff and you can swap between them whenever you like. Aggressive - Deal 10 poise damage On-hit with melee attacks, or 20 with 2h weapons. Accurate - Get a +2 bonus to attack with melee attacks. Defensive - Get a +2 bonus to AC Ranged - As a swift action, you can enter one of 3 stances as a buff. They last until you gain another buff and you can swap between them whenever you like. Long Range - Increase your range by 15 on all ranged attacks. Accurate - Get a +2 bonus to attack with ranged attacks. Rapid - You can make a Simple Attack as a swift action. Magic - As a swift action, you can enter one of 3 stances as a buff. They last until you gain another buff and you can swap between them whenever you like. Long Range - The listed range of your spells increases by 10. Accurate - Get a +2 bonus to attack with spell attacks Rapid - You gain the Quicken Spell trait, usable with any cantrip, but it costs 2 Mana.

entries.title: Hero's Quest
entries.content: Crust The quests for heroes are not always set in stone, you can feel free to come up with new ones, but these are things that exist inside of Oldschool Runescape (the game this class is based off of). Dragon Slayer - Tzhaar Champion - Monkey Business -

entries.title: Runescape Skills
entries.content: For clarity's sake... Everything written in Green is a skill. Everything written in Red is a combat talent you can unlock under that skill. Everything written in Blue is a utility talent you can unlock under that skill. Abilities that are preceded by a number have a level requirement. Prayer (Knowledge Religion) - Protection Prayers Su - Gain an additional point of prayer and 5 GBP in a god of your choice, or Frogg Saran if your WIS is -1 or lower. When you would harmed by an attack or fail a saving throw, you can choose to negate the attack or pass the save by spending prayer as an immediate action. - Piety (Su) - Gain an additional point of prayer and 5 GBP in a god of your choice, or Frogg Saran if your WIS is -1 or lower. By spending prayer as a swift action, you can give yourself a +2 bonus to all d20 rolls until the start of your next turn. Magic (Knowledge Arcana) - Magical Specialization - Gain magical specialization. - (6) Magic! - Gain access to the telekinetic lift and Alchemize techniques as 1/day innate techniques. - (5) Antimagic - Gain access to the teleblock and superstitious techniques as 1/day innate techniques. Herblore (Knowledge Alchemy) - Quick Brewski - You can craft an alchemical item for 10% of its original price with 1 hour of work 1/day. You must still be able to meet its craft DC. You must still be able to provide odd specific ingredients, such as the tongue in a Tongues Potion. - (5) Chug - You can drink a potions once per turn as a free action. Fishing (Profession Fisher) - Reel 'em In - You can use a fishing rod to finely manipulate objects. You can manipulate anything you could normally carry, replacing any Thievery check with profession (fisher). Range 30 ft. - Patience - You get a +2 bonus to attack when you attack as part of a Prepare action. - (4) Fish are Friends (Su) - Using the power of fishing, you can convert aquatic creatures into friends. By catching a specific type of fish with a DC 30 profession (fisher) check, you can get a bonus to an +2 associated skill check when using the fish as a tool, as though they successfully used the assist other action. Unique fish might be able to assist in other ways, this is meant to apply to anything, within reason. This fish transcends its fishy existence to survive outside of water to assist you. It cannot be attacked, but it can be stolen. This kills the fish. A few examples of weird fish upgrades below… Hammerhead - Smithing, construction, you name it Pufferfish (or other poisonous) - For use in creating poisons Monkfish - Good for meditation Archerfish - You can trip with your ranged attacks Sharks - Underwater tracking Bass - Good for dropping sick beats Seahorse - Makes for a good underwater mount Woodcutting (Profession Lumberjack) - One With Nature (Sp) - You gain tree shape as an at will innate technique. You can speak with plants. - Axe Man - You gain a climb speed equal to your land speed. If you wield an axe, this speed doubles. - Treebane - While wielding an axe, you can make a profession (lumberjack) check to cut down a tree within your reach without even swinging at it as a standard action. DC 10 for medium sized trees, then 8 higher per size above medium (18 large, 26 huge, 34 massive). The tree immediately transforms into a pile of logs when you use treebane. Sentient trees are immune to treebane. Whenever you use profession (lumberjack) to make money, you're essentially Taking 20. Cooking (Profession Chef) - Amazing Food healing - Turn mundane food into AMAZING FOOD. It takes you 1 hour of work and 100g of supplies to cook 5 pieces. They heal 10% hp when eaten on as a swift action, or double that if "savored" as a standard action. If you make more food, the pieces of food made previously perish, but they otherwise remain fresh forever. - Tick Eating healing - When you take hit point damage that would defeat you, you can eat a piece of AMAZING FOOD as an immediate action, causing you to instead be set to 10% hp. Must have amazing food to use. - Feast (healing) - Mining (Profession Miner) - Shaft Mining - As a standard action, you can mine a 5x5x5 ft cube, effectively allowing you to burrow underground. You can only use this ability on unworked stone/earth etc. You must wield a pickaxe to do so. Whenever you use profession (miner) to make money, you're essentially Taking 20. - Break In - You can use +Profession (miner) as a substitute for +Athletics to break things with Brute Force or Breakout as long as you wield a pickaxe. You get a +4 bonus to do so. - (8) Vibrations (Su) - As a standard action, you can slam a pickaxe into the ground to gain +Blindsense (Tremorsense 30 ft) for 1 round. This allows you to see the general shape of things like buildings and caves, even through walls and floors. Construction (Knowledge Engineering) - Bridge the Gap (Su) - While you wield a hammer, you have a “bridge speed” equal to your Land Speedthat lets you quickly nail together planks and walk along them. This bridge is solid enough for creatures to walk on and lasts until the end of your turn, or the start of your next turn if you want your allies to follow. Costs 2g per foot bridged (30ft=60g). You can continue the bridge indefinitely, even as previous sections fall, effectively ignoring gravity, but you must at least start attached to something. - Affordable Housing - With an hour of work, you can build a makeshift house with natural materials like dirt, wood and stone. You can remove T1 Afflictions with general cures by resting in such a house. - (3) Comfort - By creating a home worth at least 2k gold, you can get a boost by resting in this home. You and any allies get temp HP equal to half your skill roll. By spending the gold again, you can make home improvements to improve roll again to gain more temp hp. You can spend 40k gold to take 20 and maximize your comfort. Hunter (Survival) - Speak to Me (Su) - You gain the Wild Empathy feat. You are under the effects of Speak with Animals. - (3) Box Trap - With 10 minutes of preparation you can set a box trap for a specific creature, or a type of creature. You leave a trail of desirable stuff to a box with a stick propping it up. The creature makes a perception check (DC 15+your +Survival bonus). If they succeed then they notice the unusual and untrustworthy setup. If they fail they are trapped and Entombed in the box for one minute. Creatures with 0 or more INT get advantage on their perception check, and can escape the box using any means at their disposal. Athletics DC to Breakout is equal to the perception check to notice. Agility (Acrobatics) - Boost - You can dash as a swift action. You can dash as a free action, but it makes you fatigued. - Endurance Training - +10 Land Speed. - Parkour - You can jump as a free action 1/turn. When you jump, you ignore fall damage from 60 ft of falling. When you take fall damage, you take 1 damage per 10 ft you fall. - (4) Shortcut (Su) - You can squeeze through the tightest of spaces and the smallest of cracks. Even with only a centimeter of space, you can slide through it. You can even comfortably sit in spaces like these indefinitely. You can't effectively navigate while squished in this way, making it only usable to get through very thin gaps, like doors and windows. Fire Making (Profession Arson) - Fireproof - You gain resistance to fire damage. You take no damage or penalty to AC while ablaze. You can snap your fingers to spontaneously combust as a free action. The fire is cosmetic and provides light, but no heat. - Meltation - You gain the Melt Firebending Style ability. If you don't have the elemental blast feature, your blast damage is (d6 at level 1, and d6 higher every odd level after). - Pyrotechnics - You gain a technique point in the pyromancy tree. You can select this boon multiple times. - Dubstep Ignite recharge - When a creature survives one of your attacks with 5 or less hp, you know. You can spend an instant action to deal 5 true damage to them. Usable 1/minute. - (5) Solves Everything Sp - You gain fire blast as a 1/day innate technique. You increase its range to 120 ft. At level 9 this is usable at will.

entries.title: +Hex Maniac
entries.content: Hex maniacs are are mages that primarily utilize curse effects and alchemy in order to differentiate themselves from other mages. They can turn parts of creatures into useful and unusual potions or poisons, and they can inflict all kinds of colorful permanent debuffs on creatures. | +HP (specialist) | 20(+6 per level) | | ------------------------ | ---------------- | | Poise | 5 | | Skills | 2 | | Saves | Will | | Memory | 2+(2*LvL) | 1 - Techniques You have Magical Specialization. You gain 2 technique points at level 1, then another 2 point every 2 levels after. These technique points can be used on the Corruption and Alchemy trees. 1 - Curse Mastery - You get the arcana skill with a +4 bonus. You can use arcana instead of medicine to identify curses. - You can prepare any technique with the curse tag, as long as you would normally be able to prepare techniques of that level (ie you can prepare Power of the Sun if you are level 3). - You gain resistance to curse effects. - Whenever you pass a save against a curse effect, you can redirect it back at the creature that it inflicted it upon you, who must save against their own curse. This costs an amount of mana equal to what was spent on the technique, or (1/2 the creature's LvL) for non-technique curses. 1 - Cackle Su With an insult, sinister laugh or something to that effect, you can unnerve your foes. As a swift action, you can increase the DC or CL of the next spell you cast with the curse tag. If your target is a minion, the creature gets no saving throw at all against cackle-enhanced curses. 1 - Hex Sp You get access to unique curses, called hexes. Using a hex requires a standard action. All hexes have the curse tag, 30 ft range and cannot target the same creature more than once (ever). You get two right now! Assurance subtle - You can ask creatures to perform a task, including a time frame of 1 minute, 1 hour, 1 day, or 1 week. If they agree, they become cursed (will negates). When the time frame specified passes, the curse's debilitating effects activate. The creature gets disadvantage on all saving throws and skill checks. The curse can only be removed by completing the task, or if you release them from the curse by wiping off the mark that appears. Confidant charm - You curse a creature to trust you (will negates). While cursed, they are willing to divulge their deepest secrets to you while in private, as though you were a close friend, family member, lover or religious authority. It is a simple matter to convince cursed individuals into having these conversations, and also to steer the direction in one you find desirable. A third party must insult the caster in your presence, you insult the cursed individual, or suitable evidence is presented to the cursed individual that you shared their secrets are ways to remove the curse. 1 - Cauldron You get the alchemy skill, with a +4 bonus. You can use alchemy instead of +Survival in order to extract resources. You can also turn a mundane piece of cooking equipment into a site for a magical ritual. This requires at least 2 hours of cooking before you can produce anything of value. You can only use items harvested using the Extract Resources function of survival in a cauldron concoction. Each potion requires you spend at least 1 harvested resource and Crystallized Mana equal to the LvL of the creature involved in making the potion. You start off with the ability to make two concoctions... Shapeshifter Potion Sp glamer - You can create a potion that, when consumed, visibly transforms the consumer into the creature type used in the potions ingredients. You don't transform into an exact copy of the creature, but can be mistaken for a family member. You gain none of its abilities, size or speed, though other creatures will still treat you as though you were the creature's size. Effectively though you are standing in the center of a giant illusion that bends to your will. The potion lasts CL hours, but can be ended early by the consumer by vomiting the potion out. Ligma Potion - 2 - Gifts subtle Instead of casting a curse on a creature directly, you can instead infuse a handheld item with a curse. If a creature accepts your gift, then they are automatically afflicted with the curse with no saving throw. This usually makes it very obvious the source of the curse if the effects are immediate, like Death Mark, but the gifts become much more useful for spells like Forest's Curse. In order for the cursed item to take effect, it needs to be accepted willingly in an exchange or otherwise, and cannot be simply slipped into someone's pocket. Once accepted, the cursed item continues affecting the creature forever, until it is destroyed. Even the usual methods of removing the curse are ineffective while the gift persists, and the gift cannot be regifted. Regardless of the type of item it is, the magic within it can be safely removed by burning it in a fire. The DC to identify curse is 5 higher than its usual DC in order to figure out the cursed item plays a role. 2 - Jinxes and Juices Choose a new feature from the list of hexes or cauldron concoctions. 11 -

entries.title: Hexes
entries.content: Animorph poly, baleful - You curse a creature to slowly transform into a common animal, inflicting drain 6 (will negates transformation). While cursed in this way, parts of their body start growing patches of fur or scales. They grow new limbs and their body hunches over and changes proportions. It is a painful process. Every day they become drained 2 and cannot recover from being drained naturally. After 1 week, they transform completely into a small animal, like a lizard, monkey or rat. You decide what this animal is when casting. To remove the curse, the cursed creature must eat a meal with some of this common animals meat in it. This applies at any stage of the curse, even while transformed into an animal completely. Note - The reason why the spell specifies common animals is so that way you don't choose something really specific and basically extinct like "Florida Panther." It doesn't need to be common to the region you're in necessarily, but it does need to be a general category of animal. Fleshwarp glamer - You curse a creature to appear as a grotesque monster to all but themselves (will negates). You maintain roughly the same shape as before, but you have crimson veins running down fleshy growths all of your body. You always appear to be slick with body fluids. Your facial features are misplaced on your body and misshapen. Your voice sounds like your throat is thick with phlegm, with an odd pitch to it, etc. Creatures in your presence are grossed out, only getting more extreme when they speak to you or physically interact with you. Nearly all will consider you some type of weird monster, ostracizing you from most civilizations. In order to remove this curse, you must have consensual intercourse.

entries.title: Actor
entries.content: An actor is not merely a performer. These jesters use their voice and appearance to cause confusion in all but the most perceptive bunch. Throw your voice and rapidly change who you are on a dime to make people think they are interacting with many people. Actors can pull all kinds of ridiculous stunts to get them into and out of trouble. 1 - Everyone, Everywhere, All at Once (Su) Your proficiency in disguise is unmatched. You can make a disguise as a swift action. Not only that, but you can be two people at once! This typically only functions with a singular person. You can change appearances and positions such that as long as both of your disguised personas are not visible at once, you can effectively inhabit both of them. In such scenarios you typically cause people to keep turning around to face whoever is speaking. 1 - Throw Voice You are constantly under the effects of Ventriloquism does not have the buff tag. You can have your voice originate from a place you cannot see, such as around a corner or as a muffled voice inside of a chest. 1 - Professional With proper venues, you can use disguise to make money as thought using a profession. Talents Gain one each even level. Numbers next to the name are level requirements. 2 - I Yield, I Yield! You can roll deception as an immediate action when you fall below (10+2\*lvl) hp to pretend as though you have lost the will to fight. The inflicting creature or their leader within 30 ft roll opposed insight. If you succeed, they ignore you until rejoin the fight. When you do, the first creature you attack is DD. 2 - Flesh Wound/Deadly Wound You can exaggerate how painful something was, or make it seem as though something did not hurt at all. Roll deception or disguise (opposed by perception, insight or medicine) and you can play off a deadly wound as though it was nothing, even if it defeated you. In such a case you are still unable to perform actions, but your speed while defeated is not reduced. You can also do this when you take a relatively insignificant amount of damage to try and garner sympathy, or to convince a third party the attacker was trying to kill you etc. 2 - Statuesque You can use disguise to appear as a statue. While a statue, you cannot move or perform any actions, and otherwise functions as hide in plain sight. 2 - Traditional Theater You can stir up a commotion to gather attention, only to hit your audience with some of the most mind-numbingly boring or awful performances they have every seen. When you begin your performance, decide whether you want to make it boring or cringe to decide its effects. Boring performances put audiences to sleep and cringe ones make them run to the hills (will negates). The creature with the highest will save is the one to roll a collective save for the audience. They are the brave audience member willing to break the audience's silence and boo you off the stage, snapping the rest of the audience out of it. 4 - Sorry, Not Sorry You can harm or inconvenience someone without consequence. By first feigning assistance with a particular task, you can use it as an excuse to destroy items, spill beverages and overall show off how incompetent you can be. Creatures roll insight/perception (or perhaps even profession!) opposed to your disguise or deception. If you succeed, they are upset, but there are no immediate repercussions and you are just a waiter who fades into the background. Failure indicates a more intimate grilling and consequences appropriate for your misdeeds. You can attempt to do this repeatedly to the same person, but you have disadvantage on your skill checks. 4 - Auction House You can make the description of an item so tantalizing, so incredible that they must have it. During conversation, you can gloss up a particular item. You don't even need to possess the item, but it does need to exist. You can roll deception to exaggerate its quality, value or even function. If the creature fails its insight check, it immediately runs off to go find this item, or tries to get it from you. With this feature, you can essentially use deception as a profession skill to generate money. However you will damage your reputation by doing so.

entries.title: Mime
entries.content: You have to feel it to believe it. Mimes are exceptionally good at making the precise hand and body movements to convince others that what their gestures imply exist really do. So much so that they won't believe it when they actually do! 1 - Pantomime (Su) (Concentration) (Mana Burn) You can create objects out of thin air. Said objects must relatively simple and fit within a 10x10x10 ft cube. You can mime a flat wall, but you can't make a rat maze. You can mime a long rope, but you cannot mime a pulley system. Miming requires a standard action. Outside of these limitations, you can do anything. Let your imagination run wild as you mime staircases, simple tools and all kinds of other things into existence at your convenience, and your enemies' inconvenience. 1 - Practiced Routines (Su) A mime has a few routines that require significantly less strain in order to manifest. These specific uses of pantomime do not have the \[mana burn] tag, but are more limited in scope. Each use costs 1 mana. You gain the "build a wall" practiced routine and another of your choosing at level 1, 2, 4, 6, 8 and 10. - Build A Wall - You cause a flat 10x10 invisible wall to appear perpendicular to the direction you gesture. A creature cannot pass through it without a successful will save. Once a creature succeeds, all others make the save with advantage. If the initial creature fails, all others have disadvantage until one passes. - Omnitool - You can pantomime a mundane tool such as a fishing rod, a pick axe or a hammer. It only functions while in your hands, but otherwise functions as expected. - Lasso - You can pantomime rope, tying it around objects and using it as a lasso to hook around distant objects. There is no limit to how long you can make it, but it must be one continuous string. Other creatures can use the rope if they see you pantomiming it. - Binoculars - You put your hands over your eyes to enhance your vision. You can see distances hundreds of feet away with much greater detail. - Finger Gun - You can point at someone with your thumb in the air and move your thumb down to touch your index finger to "fire" at them. The creature takes 2d8+CL (9+CL) psychic damage (will halves). You can "upcast" this ability as though it were a spell, adding an additional d8 (4) of damage per additional mana spent. 1 - Speechless You can cast spells without verbal components. 2 - Charades (mind-affecting) You can use your pantomiming skills to cause creatures to say things they might not normally say out loud. In neutral and friendly scenarios, you can start a game of charades. Choose one creature in particular to be your victim, then create an elaborate pantomime that causes them to say something in specific (will negates). This could be something silly like "Colonel Mustard in the library with a candle stick" or something more nefarious like "those disgusting green immigrants." Pass or fail, when you initiate this goading charade, the game ends as a result of awkwardness or everybody else getting discouraged at your weird pantomime. The response is generally limited to a short sentence. Creatures subject to this ability cannot be subject to it again until you rest. 4 - Silent Theater You gain Dead Silent and silencing strike as innate techniques. By spending 2 mana, you can cast silence as a swift action on yourself.

entries.title: Prankster
entries.content: Pranksters are the most ridiculous type of jesters. They are capable of doing things people typically might expect from cartoons. Taking your eyes off of them for just a second is a sure fire way to let them get away. They use incredible feats of acrobatics and sleight of hand in order to troll their enemies. 1 - Pranked! As a swift action, you can perform a prank. What this entails varies based on what kind of prank you choose. - Slipping and Sliding - Pull out your banana peels and oil slicks. Toss one into a creature's space or into an empty space. It persists until cleaned up. The creature you tossed the prank at, and any creatures that later move into this space are knocked down (reflex negates). You can have one such slippery trap set up at a time. If you create a new one, the old one falls apart. - Realistic Replicas - As long as nobody (other than allies) have direct line of sight of you, you can manufacture a dangerous and silly prank. You cover up a door, you hide a switch or you paint a cave into a wall with exceptional speed. A creature must roll perception against your sleight of hand or they don't realize the deception. They are immediately subject to a dirty trick, as though you had poise broken them, and they take 2d6+lvl (7+lvl) damage of an appropriate type (such as crush as they run into a wall). You can have one such replica set up at a time. If you create a new one, the old one falls apart. - Lemon Chaser - Throw some fuel on the fire to bring the pain. In this case, the fuel is citrus juice and the fire is an opponent within reach. If the creature is not damaged, this does nothing. If the creature is missing, hp, the creature becomes sickened 1 from the intense pain (fort negates). If they are bleeding, they instead become sickened 2 (fort halves). - Flower Power recharge - You activate a pocket ornament, allowing you to spray an unsuspecting foe. You make a creature within reach wet. Usable 1/combat. 1 - Uncontainable While you are trapped inside of a room, or a cage, cell etc, you automatically escape the moment someone doesn't keep an eye on your for at least one full round (or 6 seconds). You can never tell people how because a magician never reveals his secrets. 1 - Kinesthetic Learner Instead of disguise and deception, you gain proficiency and a +4 bonus with +Acrobatics and +Sleight of Hand. Talents Gain one each even level. Numbers next to the name are level requirements. 2 - Juggling By wielding nothing you can wield everything. As long as you have your hands empty, you can toss your equipment up into the air such that you can effectively wield 3 different items at a time, provided each one could normally be used with one hand. You start with 3 and can add an additional item to the mix every even level past 2. You can also choose include random objects on your person, or that you pick up. Creatures cannot take these items from you. If you are ever stunned or dazed, you stop juggling, and you can choose what items you still hold in hand. If a creature poise breaks you, instead of any of the usual effects, they can choose to stop you from juggling, and prevent you from grabbing one item in particular. You can catch up to 2 other items. 2 - Balancing Act (Recharge) As a swift action, or an instant action when you land a Melee Attack or poise break a creature, you can roll acrobatics to jump directly onto a creature, balancing on their shoulders, back etc (reflex negates). When you jump on and at the start of your turn while you are balancing on a creature, they are made clumsy 1. You can be removed by poise breaking, being knocked down or anything that forcefully moves you from your position. Any AoE effect that targets you also targets the creature you balance on. You can only attempt balancing act once each round. 2 - Make them Disappear! Given 3 rounds of setup, you can plan an elaborate ruse. Using mirrors, conveniently camouflaged hidey holes and hidden compartments in boxes, you can appear to make creatures completely disappear. The creature with the highest perception bonus in a group of onlookers rolls perception opposed by your sleight of hand. If you succeed, then the creature you choose can slip away undetected for the next minute. After the minute is up, people catch on, realize it isn't a joke etc and start the search...If they care. 2 - Vanishing Act (Su) Whenever you take damage, you have a 33% chance to disappear from sight in a cloud of smoke, deflating or something equally unusual, rendering you invisible until the start of your next turn. I recommend making effects like this a high roll. AKA a 5 or 6 on a d6. 2 - All Hot Air (Su) Take a deep breath, stick your thumb in your mouth and blow on it as a swift action. Doing so causes your body to expand in delightfully silly ways. - Belly - Inflate yourself. Just like a balloon. This gives you a 10 ft fly speed as long as you hold your breath and perform no actions other than movement. - Brain - Big brain time. You gain a +2 bonus to the next intelligence based check you make this round. - Hand - Increase the size of your hand. Your next unarmed strike this turn adds (d6/odd level) damage. - Assets - Attempt to charm a creature (will negates). They have a +4 bonus on the save if they wouldn't normally be attracted to you due to sexual orientation (or lack thereof for something like a robot). In combat the charmed creature gets a new save every turn. Out of combat the charm lasts 10 minutes, and once it wears off, the creature cannot be charmed again until rest. 4 - Squashed (Su) You gain a special attack. Flatten - Make a melee attack to smush a creature against the ground with such force that they are practically rendered two-dimensional. The creature has their speed reduced by 15 and their reach reduced to 5 ft. This lasts until an ally uses the aid action on them, or the creature spends a standard action. The damage of this attack is changed to bludgeoning. It deals your LvL in additional damage. You can use this special attack on your allies or yourself in order to slip underneath things like doors. You can much more easily hide underneath furniture etc. Allies take no damage from this attack and can exit their smushed state as a swift action.

entries.title: Jester
entries.content: Jesters are masters of misdirection. Their methodology varies, but the goal is always the same: Confound and deceive. Some choose to do so with prestidigitation, drawing the eyes of creatures where they want, and more importantly, away from where they don't. Others use their voices and costumes to have a 3 way conversation with only themselves and their poor victim. One should always keep their eyes on a jester and their goals, for the moment they don't, they might just disappear. | +HP (mixed) | 20(+8 per level) | | ------------------------ | ---------------- | | Poise | 6 | | Skills | 4 | | Saves | Will | | Memory | 2+(2\*lvl) | 1 - Techniques You have martial specialization and magical specialization. You gain 2 technique points at level 1, then another point every 2 levels after. These technique points can be used on the scoundrel and moogen trees. 1 - Master of Deception You get proficiency in the disguise and deception skills, with a +4 bonus. You also gain the Signature Skill feat for disguise. You start play with a disguise kit. 1 - Fight Dirty As a swift action, you can make a special type of attack against an enemy. - Mockery psychic - Deal (d4+LvL) psychic damage to a creature within 30 ft and give them disadvantage on their next attack roll (will halves dmg and negates disadvantage). If you can personalize the insult to an extent the GM determines it digs deep, the DC is 2 higher. - Disarm - Roll a disarm attack. In additional to its usual effects, roll Simple Attack damage, unless a technique already does so. - Trick - Roll a dirty trick attack. In additional to its usual effects, roll Simple Attack damage, unless a technique already does so. 1 - Cutting Words (recharge) Roast your enemies, causing them to falter when it matters most. As an immediate action, you can force a creature within 30 ft to subtract a d6 to a d20 roll (minus luck checks). At level 6 this increases to d8. At level 10 this improves to d10. This ability can be used once per minute. You can ignore the recharge time and use it again if you mana burn 2. 1 - Jack of All Trades Add (1+½ LvL) to all skills that you lack proficiency in. You can make checks for all skills as though you were proficient. You also have the unique ability to perform any skill in unconventional (and sometimes nonsensical) ways. This could mean using strange tools, using your feet instead of your hands and adding superfluous steps for added flare 2 - Hidden Props Su After a minute of scheming with an item in direct contact with you, you can make sure that this item is always there when you need it. This item must consume 2 or less inventory slots. You can only have one hidden prop at the ready at a time. You procure these items by pulling them out of hidden hatches, around corners or from behind someone's ear. If it is a handheld object (like a trumpet or a sword), this requires no action. Anything larger requires a swift action. These items effectively do not exist as long as you have them as hidden props. They cannot be stolen from you and they do not exist in a specific place. They exist wherever it is most convenient for the jester at any given moment, making other creatures unable to interact with them if you believe it inconvenient. If you are killed, the items fall out of your pocket. Nonlethal methods of retrieving these items against the jester's will require charms, curses and the like. 2 - Audience Plant (recharge) There is a secret network of jesters, their assistants and their fans that all work to aid each other. As long as you are in a situation where there are others present but not currently in sight (such as a person in a nearby shop, or guards in the adjacent room), you can give a signal to have them act. Depending on the type of signal, you can have them act differently. - Cause a Commotion - The plant will cause a ruckus by shouting, playing music and being obnoxious. At the very least, it will momentarily draw the attention of creatures so you can hide (or something similar). - Vouch For Me - A random member of the crowd cheers for you, or a prison guard tells their coworker to lay off you a bit. This isn't enough to get you out of situations, but will likely give people pause before inflicting any form of harsh judgement. - Oops? - The plant will "accidentally" do something, like opening a window in the way of someone chasing you, or unlocking a door for you to let you inside. In order to recharge this ability, you have to return the favor in some way to the person that assisted you after the situation resolves itself. Usually if you remain in the area, they will come to find you. 4 - Status Quo (Su) Spend 1 minute familiarizing yourself with an area that comprises around a 40ft cube. You and your allies can interact with an environment, including moving things, painting things, destroying things etc. Then as a standard action while nobody is looking, you can cause it to revert back to its original state, sans minor details like you stealing items from a safe, or adding a few innocuous items. You can do this even if you are not present. You can intuitively tell when nobody is looking and you can use this ability. 6 - You Called? (Su) Whenever someone asks about a you, wondering where you are or what you're up to, you can decide to be doing something near them. As long as the creature in question is within 1 mile of you, can appear before them within 3 rounds, or about 20 seconds. The circumstances you arrive are typically quite natural. You can combine this with other abilities to do things you shouldn't be able to get away with, like the Prankster Uncontainable ability.

entries.title: Universal Talents
entries.content: 2 - It's Just a Prank Bro You can say anything you want to people, as long as its a joke. NPCs will not react negatively to anything you say, even if you call them a whore, a failure or a poopoo head. All you have to do is add a qualifying statement like "just kidding" or integrate it into a joke and all is forgiven. 2 - You Fool! You get a +10 bonus to skill checks and ability checks whenever you can reasonable use your incompetence as a reason for why you did something, like +Deception to say "you didn't mean it" or +Athletics to "accidentally" break something. Creatures other than your allies need to be present for you to gain this bonus. 2 - Fooling Around Whether its talking nonsense after failing a +Monster Lore check or being overly dramatic about falling after failing to Balance, you can turn it around. Whenever you miss a creature with an Attack or fail a Skill Check/Ability Check, you can make it seem like it was all part of your plan to get a quick laugh out of creatures. As an immediate action, grant an ally witnessing your failure Advantage on their next d20 roll within the next minute. You can also pretend to fail at something to grant this advantage. No matter the case, creatures other than your allies must be present to witness the tomfoolery and it always must be silly and dramatic, aka, something that will draw attention. Note: If you are not under duress, if there is no tension, you cannot fool around. This should not be read as "standard action grant advantage to every roll ever."

entries.title: Lunatic
entries.content: Trust in the power of Frogg Saran. He's playing multi-dimensional 4D chess on a cosmic scale the likes of which you cannot comprehend. You need only to enact his will and you will be rewarded accordingly. To the moon! The lunatic has a spell list filled with spells that have very inconsistent results. On top of that, every time a lunatic casts a spell, there is a chance that he receives a blessing from Frogg in the form of wild magic, further adding more unpredictable variables to their spell casting. | +HP (specialist) | 20(+6 per level) | | ------------------------ | ---------------- | | Poise | 5 | | Skills | 2 | | Saves | Will | | Memory | 3+lvl | 1 - Spell Preferences Lunatics simultaneously have access to every single spell school, and do not prepare spells. Instead they can choose any spell to memorize permanently whenever they gain a memory slot. Additional technique points gained through natural progression or otherwise grant additional memory slots. @Lvl 1 they can only select level 1 techniques. @Lvl 3 they can select up to level 2 techniques etc. In addition, they get 6 +Cantrips and always memorize all Frogg Saran (T) techniques of spell levels they can cast. 1 - Innate Casting You can Innately Cast Metronome. 1 - Madness? By embracing his will you have little need for your own. Instead of taking a penalty to your will saves based on your negative WIS, you gain a bonus (-2 wis=+2 will). Instead of gaining a bonus to your will save based off of your positive wisdom, you gain a penalty (+2 wis=-2 will). Decrease your wisdom by 1. Gain 10 Frogg Saran GBP. 1 - Inner Machinations Psychic You gain resistance to psychic damage. Whenever a creature attempts to read your thoughts, it fails. When a creature attempts to read your thoughts or deals psychic damage to you, they take 2d6+lvl (7+lvl) psychic damage. 1 - Suffering From Success Whenever you roll a natural 1 on a D20, gain a luck dice. The first time you are harmed by one of your own spells in a combat, gain a luck dice. If you use anything to Reroll a natural one, or have advantage and don't roll multiple 1s, you do not gain a luck dice. You can have an unlimited number of luck dice. With many saved up you hum/glow with power. At the start of each day if you have zero luck dice, gain one. 1 - Wild Magic Whenever you cast a (non cantrip) spell, roll a DC 5 luck check. If you fail, roll on the wild magic table for a bonus effect. Every time you pass this luck check, increase the DC by 2. When wild magic triggers, reset the DC back to 5. Triggering wild magic is the only way to reduce the DC of this luck check. The effect always triggers after the casting of the spell has been resolved. 2 - Lucky Number Roll a d8+1. This number permanently becomes your lucky number. Whenever you roll this number on a d20, you gain a luck dice and you treat the result as 10 higher. 2 - Frog Friend Companion You are blessed! Gain the Pet feat with the following stipulations. - Your pet must always be a frog or frog-like creature. - It only has 2 adaptation points, but automatically gets the tongue ability. - Whenever your frog rolls a 5 or lower on an Attack Roll, it triggers Wild Magic, affecting the target of the attack instead of itself. This does not reset your wild magic. - It can shrink to tiny size. Changing to tiny or increasing in size back to the usual as a swift action. - Its base stats are changed to -1 WIS and +1 LUK - It has the enlightened deific boon instead of Animal Instincts, putting its WIS to -2. 2 - Refund recharge Every even level you can replace one of your spells known with another spell. By donating 1,000 gold to a frog, you can instantly replace a spell known 1/day. 4 - Certified Expert You have advantage on skill checks and serendipity checks as long as nobody is watching you. 6 - Wilder Magic (recharge) Su As an immediate action, spend a luck dice to force a creature casting a spell within 30 feet of you to roll on the wild magic table. Usable 1/minute without spending a luck dice. 8 - Crazy Talk You say a lot of weird things, but sometimes trapped in the madness is a grain of truth. Once per game session, you can will practically anything to be true, though how true it is varies. First you make your intention to the DM to be known. "I'm good friends with the royalty in this town." "There is a secret entrance into the base through this cave." "There's someone on the other side of this door" right before you blow it up. How true it becomes depends on what the bronze senses are tingling over. Being friends with royalty is unlikely, so its much more likely you're friends with the banished prince. There may just be a secret entrance through the cave, but the tunnel has since collapsed, only allowing the smallest of creatures inside. Someone being directly on the other side of a door is context based, but in the right scenario could possibly catch your enemies off guard.

entries.title: Wild Magic
entries.content: When you trigger wild magic, first roll a d4 to determine which chart you roll on. 1 is red, 2 is yellow, 3 is purple and 4 is green. After that, roll d20s until you get a result on the chart. Hopefully eventually there will be more than 20! | Dice Roll | Name | Effect | | --------- | ------------------------------------------------------------------------- | ----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | | 1 | Worst Nightmare | A figment of one of the caster's (no doubt numerous) fears appears somewhere within 30 feet of them (GM decides where) and spooks you, causing you to be frightened until it is proven to not be real. | | 2 | Disarmed | Your dominant arm falls off. Grab off the ground and reattach as a standard action or 30 ft of movement. | | 3 | Stop Right There Criminal Scum! | You cannot willingly move from your current position until the end of your next turn while you are harassed by the fun police. | | 4 | Baby Mode | Turn into a baby version of yourself until you spend a standard action making baby noises. This changes none of your statistics. You are affected as per Sanctuary but it lasts for 1 minute. You can choose to stay a baby for as long as you desire. | | 5 | Magic Hot Potato | Burst into painful rainbow flames, taking d6 (3) force damage immediately and another d6 (3) at the start of each of your turns until you stop, drop and roll (30 feet of movement), or succeed on a melee Spell Attack to pass it on to another creature. That creature can pass it along or get rid of it with the same methods. Ends after creature it is attached to is defeated. | | 6 | Invisible Spray | Your pants turn Invisible until dispelled or properly washed. If not your pants, whatever covers your underwear. You cannot escape the embarrassment unless you are naked. | | 7 | Grass Type | Become a potted plant until the start of your next turn. Functions as Tree Shape, but you're a wee potted plant. | | 8 | You Died | Horribly. Then you respawn in a new body in the same state you were in moments ago, but with one less health. Roll on the Resurrection Chart after combat. | | 9 | Turbo Thrusters | Roll a d4. 1=North, 2=East, 3=South, 4=West. Move 60 feet in that direction. | | Dice Roll | Name | Effect | | --------- | ------------------------------------------------------------- | --------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | | 1 | The Box | You are surrounded by a semi-opaque forcefield on all sides (including underneath) until the end of your next turn, essentially trapping and protecting you. | | 2 | Achoo | The force of the magic cast causes you to sneeze. This magic-infused sneeze vaults you in the opposite from the direction you were facing (10*LvL of spell slot expended) feet elusive. If you impact a solid surface along your path, take d6 (3) crush damage. If you cast a spell that doesn’t have a specific target direction (such as a self only spell) you are always vaulted north. | | 3 | Embiggen | +1 size category until you wake up the next day (stacks). | | 4 | Ensmollen | -1 size category until you wake up the next day (stacks). | | 5 | Incoming | A shadow appears over your current space (or something like that). A very heavy object falls on that space by the end of your next turn dealing 10d6 (35) crush damage to whatever it lands on. | | 6 | LEARN! | A book with a topic relevant to what you're currently doing falls on your head. You become Drained 1 but potentially gain insight into your current task, giving you a +4 bonus to an associated Skill Check. | | 7 | Wet | You're Wet now. This kills the viking. | | 8 | The Answer | Your current health changes to 42. If you don't have that much max hp, then you temporarily overheal to this value. | | 9 | Swap | The target of your spell swaps places with you. If the effect is AoE, swap with a randomly chosen creature affected. If the target is yourself or doesn't effect a creature, this effect is stored until you cast a spell that does. | | 10 | Divinity Gaming | You and all creatures within 10 feet of you are set ablaze. | | 11 | Lost in the Sauce | A barrel of sauce appears in your space. It grants you Cover but you must take spend 30 movement to exit the barrel. You become wet. | | 12 | Mix Up | Swap places with an ally (determined randomly among all allies). | | Dice Roll | Name | Effect | | --------- | -------------------------------------------------------------------- | -------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | | 1 | So Handsome | You grow a THICK handlebar mustache until you wake up the next day. | | 2 | Thank Mr Skeltal | Your skeleton briefly hops out of your body and plays the trumpet, as per Mr Skeltal. | | 3 | Genderbend | If male, turn into a HOT BABE version of yourself until you wake up the next day. If female, turn into a HOT DUDE version of yourself until you wake up the next day. | | 4 | Blessing of Frogg-Saran | You gain a frog friend that kills itself in some spectacular way by the end of the day. | | 5 | Blue Attack | You’re blue now. That’s my attack. Must be dispelled to return to your normal colors. | | 6 | Big Head Mode | Your head increases in size to be comically large until you wake up the next day as per the Big Head Mode technique. | | 7 | Second Amendment | Gain bear arms until you wake up the next day for no mechanical benefit. They're fuzzy I guess. | | 8 | Morality | A tiny angel and devil version of yourself float over your shoulders and start backseat gaming or judging the morality of your actions. Nobody else can see them. | | 9 | Signature Spell | An arcane mark of your choosing appears somewhere on the target of your spell’s body. | | Dice Roll | Name | Effect | | --------- | ------------------------------------------------------------------- | ------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | | 1 | Foxdie | A friendly fox with 1 hp appears in the space next to you, it taunts enemies within 30 ft. | | 2 | Spaghetti | Gain the effects of long arms until you wake up the next day. | | 3 | Dark Souls Roll | Move 5 feet elusive | | 4 | Laser Eyes | +4 circumstance bonus to +Intimidation for the rest of the day. | | 5 | Buy One, Get One Free | This spell doesn’t consume mana. Roll again, if you get a 5, cast the spell again. This can happen indefinitely. | | 6 | Rainbow Mode | You can change your skin color like a chameleon until you wake up the next day, giving you a +4 circumstance on +Stealth checks. | | 7 | Tentacular | You gain (spell lvl) natural attacks that all attack the nearest enemy within your natural reach (d6 base damage). If there is no enemy within range, they flail around all spooky like and disappear immediately. | | 8 | Detect Thoughts | Tune into the surface thoughts of a creature of your choosing that you can see as per Detect Thoughts | | 9 | Disco Nunu | A disco ball appears above your head. Bronzelords fear this technique. Enemies with a negative INT are frightened of you until the start of your next turn. | | 10 | It's a Lie | A delicious cake appears in your hands, or at your feet if your hands are occupied. | | 11 | Cheat Code | A scroll of the spell you just cast appears in your hand, or at your feet if your hands are occupied. | | 12 | Fuck Gravity | Gain a hover speed until you rest. | | 13 | Trolling | Gain regeneration equal to the mana cost of the spell for one minute. | | 14 | Za Warudo | The universe (minus you) gains stasis until the end of your next turn as you freeze time. | | 15 | Medic | Two monkey medics with a stretcher appear at a location of your choosing to evacuate a defeated creature. They move 60 feet each round elusive. If no creatures are defeated, they instead use the carry action on an ally of your choosing. Taking damage causes them to disappear |

entries.title: Dimensional Warrior
entries.content: A dimensional warrior's specialty is mobility through blatantly overpowered abilities that allow them to teleport on a dime and strike opponents through magic proxies. 1 - Echo Su By spending 1 mana as a swift action, you can cause a colorful copy of yourself made of pure mana to appear in a space you can see within 30 ft. You can make a Natural Attack, Unarmed Attack or Weapon Attack from the position of this copy instead of your own. This copy threatens as you would and consumes your AoOs. Echos cannot provide flanking. If you create an echo while another exists, the old one disappears. 1 - Warp Speed (Su) Teleportation Mana Burn You gain a "warp" speed, allowing you to teleport instead of walking, swimming etc. Your warp speed is equal to your Land Speed. You mana burn any turn you use it. 1 - Me Too! (Su) When an opponent uses a Teleportation effect within your reach, you can teleport with them as an immediate action. If they are out of your reach, you can use a standard action to open a portal to follow them. 2 - Talents Gain one at level 2, 4, 6, 8 and 10. Numbers indicate level requirements Talents 2 - Playing With Portals (Su) (teleportation) Concentration By spending 1 mana as a swift action, you can place a portal on an adjacent roughly flat surface and have a linked portal open up along another such surface within 60 ft of you that you can see. the portal is about 5 ft tall and half as wide. Creatures cannot pass through this portal, but anything else can. This could mean arrows, spells or precious cargo. 2 - Harm's Way Out (Su) (teleportation) After an ally takes damage within 60 ft of you, you can choose to spend 1 mana as an immediate action to swap places with them.

entries.title: Thunderlord
entries.content: A thunderlord's specialty is disruption. Knocking people around with rushing wind and cacophonous attacks that completely ignore the poise system. 1 - Blown Away Whenever you deal zap damage, sonic damage or crush damage from an air effect, you push the creature you damage 10 ft directly away from you. If the effect already pushed the creature, it pushes them an additional 10 ft. 1 - AS: Reverberating Strike sonic Deal an additional d6+CL (3+CL) thunder damage and energize your foe On-hit for 1 round. They take an additional 2d6 (7) sonic damage if they move of their own volition, no matter the method. 2 - Talents Gain one at level 2, 4, 6, 8 and 10. Numbers indicate level requirements 11 - Avatar Form Su buff recharge As a swift action, conjure a larger version of yourself made entirely of crackling storm clouds to linger over your head. - Your natural reach increases to 20 ft during your turn. - Blown Away doubles in power to knock people 20 feet away from you - Whenever you make a Melee Attack during this time, your avatar causes an immense shockwave at the point of impact. Creatures within 20 ft of the original target are knocked 20 ft away from the target 20 ft and are knocked down (fort negates knockdown). Talents 2 - AS: Wind Strike air Strike an enemy, dealing no additional damage, but instead surrounding in a tempest that cuts into them as they move for one round. They take d8 (4) slashing damage for every 5 feet they move, no matter the method. Despite not dealing any damage, this attack still procs Blown Away On-hit. 2 - Reverberations (air) Sp At the start of your turn as a swift action, you can spend 1 mana to unleash powerful winds upon creatures within 10 ft of you, dealing 2d6 (7) crush damage (fort negates). 2 - Thunderous Smite (Sp) (sonic) If you spend 1 mana as an immediate action when you take damage, you can blast your attacker with a sonic blast if they are within 30 ft. They take 2d6+CL (7+CL) sonic damage (fort halves).

entries.title: Arcane Strikes
entries.content: Note: All arcane strikes are treated as spells. Level 1 Spells | Name | Dmg | Bonus Effect | | ----------------------------------------------------------------- | ----------------------------------- | ---------------------------------------------------- | | Electrocute Zap | 2d6+CL (7+CL) zap | Creature is Staggered for 1 round (fort negates) | | Melt Poison | 2d6+CL (7+CL) poison | | | Ignite Fire | (CL) fire | Set creature Ablaze | | | | | | | | | Level 2 Spells | Name | Dmg | Bonus Effect | | -------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | ---------------------- | ---------------------------------------- | | Vampiric Strike Necrotic Healing | 2d6+CL (7+CL) necrotic | Heal equal to necrotic damage dealt. | Level 3 Spells | Name | Dmg | Bonus Effect | | ----------------------------------------------------------------------- | -------------------- | ------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | | Lava Cling (fire) | 3d6+CL (10+CL) fire | Deals the fire damage again at the end of he creatures turn. | | Fighting Spirit Force | 3d6+CL (10+CL) force | For the next minute, a ghostly intangible version of yourself follows the target relentlessly. You can make melee attacks as though you threatened the creature until the spell ends, though you cannot make AoOs against it using this spirit. The spell ends automatically if you don't have line of sight to the target for more than 1 round. | Level 4 Spells | Name | Dmg | Bonus Effect | | ---------------------------------------------------------------- | ------------------- | -------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | | Molten Wave (fire) | 4d6+CL (14+CL) fire | A 30 ft line of bubbling magma erupts from the ground using the space of your target as its origin. Creatures other than the primary target in the line take the arcane strikes damage (reflex halves). Creatures that move into the area also take the arcane strikes damage.This is a Persistent Concentration effect. | Level 5 Spells Level 6 Spells | Name | Dmg | Bonus Effect | | ------------------------------------------------------------------ | ------------------- | -------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | | Black Blade Holy | 6d6+CL (21+CL) holy | For the purposes of how this attack interacts with Resistance and Weakness, it deals holy damage. But in actuality, the spells damage and the weapon damage combine to drain an enemy equal to the damage dealt.This is a Curse effect that can be removed through the application of Holy Water.While the curse persists, the drain cannot be recovered. If the target dies, your weapon absorbs its soul, denoted visually. It cannot return to life until your weapon is destroyed or its soul is otherwise freed. |

entries.title: Magus
entries.content: Crust What if sword wizard? The magus specializes in dishing out sword swings and spells in equal measure, learning to combine them in unique ways. The magus has the ability to conjure magic blades out of thin air to augment his fighting style, or supercharge his weapon with magic to unleash devastating single hits. The magus is one of the most versatile in their ability to destroy their enemies. | +HP (mixed) | 20(+8 per level) | | ------------------------ | ---------------- | | Poise | 6 | | Skills | 2 | | Saves | Fort | | Memory | 2+(2\*lvl) | 1 - Techniques You have Martial Specialization and Magical Specialization. You gain 3 technique points at level 1, then another 2 points every 2 levels after. You can spend these technique points however you see fit. 1 - Multiattack Make a melee attack and cast a spell. 1 - Enchant Weapon Su As a swift action, spend 1 mana to enhance 2 different +Weapons (including natural attacks and unarmed attacks) with a magic weapon property for 1 minute, except Bane. 1 - Arcane Strike Sp You get access to a unique list of spells. Each of these spells requires a melee attack to deliver. Each of these abilities still requires memory. Access scales just like any technique tree, granting you new abilities every odd level. When you multiattack and use an arcane strike, you can apply it to your weapon, essentially adding the effect of an arcane strike to a weapon attack. This can be used with spell combat to cast an arcane strike and make 2 attacks. 2 - Mana Well Gain an additional point of Mana this level and at levels 6 and 10. 2 - Burned into my Brain mana burn When you prepare techniques at the start of each day, you can choose to mana burn any amount and gain additional memory for each mana you burn.

entries.title: Monk Vows
entries.content: Monk vows cannot be broken, even while under the influence of charm effects, possession etc. Monks have trained their bodies to react to certain things by instinct, making things like being unconscious irrelevant. Vow of Silence Restriction - You cannot speak. You have trained your body to such an extent that it does not even remember how to make sound, even when you are in pain. Certain actions that require communication may become difficult, such as persuading people, without creative ways to circumvent these restrictions. Benefit - You get +Linguistics as a bonus skill and sign language as a bonus language. You also gain the Psychic (C) class's Psychic Casting feature. Vow of Fasting Restriction - You refuse to ingest anything. Even if you were to eat or drink something, your body would reject it, causing you to vomit. This means you are unable to consume helpful potions, such Jizzle Physicks. Benefit - You do not need to eat or drink. You only need peaceful meditation for 1 minute for nourishment. After such meditation, you heal to your max hp and gain 10 temp hp that persists until lost. Vow of Detachment Restriction - You refuse to get close to others. Literally. The touch of other intelligent creatures makes you reflexively recoil. You cannot initiate anything that requires prolonged contact, such as a grapple, a handshake or a hug. If such a creature tries to initiate prolonged contact, they must poise break you. Benefit - At the start of your turn, you automatically escape a creatures unwanted touch. Vow of Celibacy Restriction - You cannot return romantic feelings. You have completely shut of the part of you that feels romantic attachment to the point where even pretending feels unnatural and uncomfortable. You cannot perform acts of physical intimacy such as intercourse, kissing or other culturally significant acts typically reserved for lovers. You can't even pretend, giving you a -10 penalty on any skill checks related to any romance-related tasks. Benefit - You get a +2 bonus on saves against charm effects. You are immune to effects with the Lewd tag.

entries.title: Monk
entries.content: A monk is an unarmed fighter that chooses to not only hone their body into a perfect weapon, but also their spirit. Be it through the exploration of the self or the deprivation of worldly pleasures, these warriors unlock spiritual powers that work in tandem with their highly mobile, up close and personal fighting style. The monk has a fuzzy core identity, but makes up for it with significant versatility. 1 - First Strike: Dazing Fist Your first unarmed attack you land on a creature has a bonus On-hit effect. This effect can only be applied to a creature 1/day. Creature is Dazed for 1 round on hit (fort negates). 1 - Monk Vow You have taken a vow, depriving yourself of something others might consider core to existence. Your unusual lifestyle comes with a unique benefit befitting of your dedication. 1 - Perfect Balance You gain the +Acrobatics skill, and a +4 bonus. You can stand on even the thinnest and weakest of surfaces without falling over and without breaking the surface. This can include flimsy bridges/branches, rope, or even wet napkins. 1 - Meditate Su Draw in the energy of the universe and steel your mind as a FRA. You regain (1+¼) your level mana, remove all shaken stacks and take the total defense action. 1 - Monk Talents At level 1, gain one of the following talents. At level 2, 4, 6, 8 and 10, gain an additional talent. Numbers indicate level requirements. 1 - Run Like the Wind Mana Burn Recharge Spend 1 mana as a free action to Dash. Usable 1/round. 1 - Elemental Fist (Su) Spend 1 mana as a free action to cause your unarmed attacks to deal entirely damage of an elemental type for 1 round, chosen between fire, frost or zap. One of your unarmed attacks during this round can be made as a Thrown attack. At level 6, whenever you activate elemental fist, your Flurry attack deals full damage. Techniques and special attacks that add additional damage also turn into this damage type. 1 - Light Steps (Su) concentration You can start concentrating on this ability as a free action. As long as you only move during your turn and take no other action, you can walk along any flat continuous surface. This can include things that aren't even otherwise solid such as water or lava. It can also include things such as walls or ceilings. You can use this ability for only part of your turn to get to a location, but if you attack, cast a spell etc, then you need a surface you would normally be able to stand on or you plummet. 1 - Well of Wisdom Monks have a way of thinking about the world unlike any other. By simplifying complex truths into easy to understand proverbs, you can give your allies a unique perspective. 1/day you can dispense your knowledge as a standard action to a creature within reach. When the proverb finally clicks, you give your ally a Reroll on a skill check of their choosing. If they do not use it by the time they rest, the wisdom is lost. If you can think of an actual proverb that applies to the situation at hand, the reroll is made with advantage. 1 - Energy of the Universe Healing Upgrade your Meditate ability so it also restores (2+LvL) HP. 4 - Deep Breath At the start of your turn if you are shaken, confused, frightened or under some type of charm/possession effect, you can choose to immediately end all of those effects as a swift action by spending 1 mana. 4 - Healing Hand (Su) healing Knock bones into place and relax muscles with a quick jab. Spend 1 mana to touch a creature and heal (6+1/2 LvL) hp. Can be done as a swift and a standard action. 4 - Special Technique Sp Choose a spell that is 1 level lower than your highest tier of technique known (level 1 technique at this level, and 1 higher every 2 levels after). You permanently memorize this spell without spending memory and can use this spell without a Staff Component. Every even level, you can choose another technique to replace your previous one. 6 - Special Attack: Ki Block On-hit with an unarmed strike apply Siphoned for 1 round (fort negates). Alternatively you can disable a creatures limb for 1 round. Targeted wings disable flight, legs decrease Land Speed by 15 and arms render them unusable, etc (fort negates). You can spend 3 mana to use this special attack more than once a combat.

entries.title: Wrestler
entries.content: Wrestlers specialize in grappling their foes and doing with them as they please: pinning them to the ground, tossing them into the air and bending them into pretzels. 1 - Grapplemancer Gain the Maneuver Versatility and Tangled feats. The tangled feat's bonuses last until the start of your turn whenever you succeed on a grapple, regardless of if you maintain the grapple. 1 - Wrestling Moves Wrestlers are defined by their unique moves, centered around grappling. Wrestling moves involve you initiating a grapple, and then immediately ending it in order to gain a bonus effect and deal your unarmed strike damage, unless otherwise noted. All wrestling moves are special attacks that have a global cooldown, preventing them from being used multiple times in one turn and on subsequent turns (use 1 turn, don’t use next turn). If the target is knocked down, staggered or cannot take actions as a result of some other Status, the wrestling move is considered a finisher, increasing its DC by 2. Gain 2, then an additional one at level 4, 6, 8 and 10. See subsection for selection. 2 - Crowd Favorite Whenever a combat you take part in has spectators, enough to be considered a "crowd," you gain a single Reroll for that combat. 2 - Tap Out If a wrestler successfully grapples a creature for three turns (sequence being initiate turn one, maintain turn 2, tap out turn 3), he can force the creature to tap out or be knocked out. If the creature refuses to tap out, they are instantly knocked unconscious, drop to 0 hp and are drained 2. Creatures that tap out receive the time out condition for 24 hours. If you can initiate a grapple outside of your turn, you must still take 3 full turns to force someone to tap out. 4 - Flex By spending an immediate action to pose and flex, you can increase your next strength-based skill check by 4. 4 - From the Top Ropes Whenever the wrestler initiates a wrestling move from a position where he jumps down onto his foe at least 10 feet, he treats the move as a finisher and he does not take fall damage. The wrestler cannot use flight or a high jump height to count as “jumping down” onto his foe. 6 - Signature Move Give one of your wrestling moves a special name. It's DC increases by 2. 8 - Countermeasures When a creature fails to break your poise with a combat maneuver, you can bully them as an instant action as long as it was with a melee attack. 11 - The Wall You cannot be unwillingly moved from your space. If the attack had other effects, they still apply.

entries.title: Wrestling Moves
entries.content: | Name | Effect | | ---------------- | ----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | | Chokeslam | You grab your opponent by the throat, lift them up and slam them into a nearby solid object to knock the wind out of them. They are staggered and silenced for one round (fortitude negates silence). | | Bear Hug | You wrap your arms around your opponent, then squeeze, shake and thrash around. You make them clumsy 2 (fort negates). Any grapple you initiate with a bear hug deals your unarmed strike damage every turn you begin or maintain it (but only once a round). This counts as and does not stack with constrictBear hug does not end your grapple. | | Yeet | You lift your opponent into the air and throw them (regardless of weight). You can throw an opponent up to 20 feet. They are knocked down (fort negates). With maneuver master, this movement also provokes AoOs. | | Backbreaker Drop | You hold your opponent on your shoulders, take a knee and bring them down onto it in one swift motion. Your opponent is knocked down for 2 rounds (fort reduces 1 round). | | Twister | You lift your opponent over your head and rapidly spin them around like you're making them into a pizza. Your opponent is sickened 2 for one minute. They also have blurred vision for as long as they are sickened (fort negates). | | Suplex | You twist the foe such that his head is hanging above the ground then slam them down onto the ground. Your opponent is confused (fort adds 2 to confusion rolls). | | Giant Swing | You lift the foe up by their legs and swing them around into another target. Make a Melee Attack against another enemy in your natural reach. If your attack is successful, they both take the same damage. If you miss/graze the secondary target, the initial target takes unarmed damage with no additional effect.
