If you successfully land the attack, both targets are dazed for one round (fort negates). | | Patrol | Special: This move can exclusively be used when you Prepare the action.You wait and watch to protect your friends. If a creature you are aware of attacks an ally within 30 feet of you, immediately lunge at them and grapple them elusive. If you succeed, the creature cannot attack anybody but you, and you take the attack instead of your ally.Patrol does not end your grapple. | | Showstopper | Special: Must be initiated as part of a Core Rules, How to Play/Actions/Common Actions/Full Round Actions/Charge.Pick up your enemy as you run at them, lift them in the air carrying them up to what your movement allows and cause a shockwave from the impact of slamming them into the ground. The initial target and creatures within 15 feet of the impact are staggered for 1 round (fort negates). | | Huracanrana | Special: You can jump as high/far as your speed allows to initiate this moveYou jump onto your opponent's shoulders and wrap your legs around their head, then twist or flip to fling them to the ground. A flying opponent is thrown to the ground and cushions the wrestlers fall, taking appropriate fall damage. They are grounded for 1 minute (fort reduces to 1 round). | | Seismic Toss | Pick up your enemy and throw them very high into the sky. They are treated as through they are 100 ft in the air during their next turn. They do not take fall damage. |

entries.title: Barroom Brawler
entries.content: To some, drinking isn't just a something they do at parties, its a lifestyle. Barroom Brawlers have developed an unusual fighting style that combines the unnatural movements while drunk with the pain numbing effects of alcohol to be as annoying as possible to take down. 1 - Drinking Buddy In a neutral scenario (aka one where you aren't actively being attacked), you can goad someone into a drinking contest with you (will negates). If you've already challenged someone, it won't work again. If you are in a location where alcohol is sold, the DC is 2 higher. 1 - Keg Whether you carry it around, sling it onto your back, you have a large source of basic booze you keep with you at all times. To you it weighs nothing, but to anybody else it weighs 200 lbs full and 20 empty. Your keg is enough to last you a week before you must return to civilization and craft a refill. With an empty keg, you cannot chug. If you spend 3 days crafting, you can fill your keg with moonshine. 1 - Chug recharge healing Take a swig to grant yourself (4+½ lvl) temp hp. You can take up to 3 drinks in one turn to increase the temp hp you gain by the same amount. You can drink as a standard action, swift action, and by spending 30 ft of movement. Any turn you chug, you only have access to one of your hands while the other is used to shotgun your drink from the keg. Chug has a unique form of cooldown. See tip below for more details. Note: You must use a form of alcohol processing in order to begin recharging chug, after which it is on cooldown for 1 round. A helpful way to remember if you can chug is to recall what you did last round. The sequence goes... 1. Chug 2. Processing 3. Nothing 4. Chug TL;DR - If you chugged or processed the alcohol last turn, you cannot chug this turn. 1 - Alcohol Processing At any point the turn after using chug, you can do one of the following actions. You must do one of the following to begin recharging chug. Chug is on cooldown for 1 round after using alcohol processing. Rest and Digest (healing) - As a free action, heal equal to your remaining temp hp from chug. If you have no temp from chug remaining, you can still use this ability, but it provides no healing and only recharges chug. Belch sonic - As a standard action, burp in the face of a creature within reach. They take 5 sonic damage and become staggered for 1 round (fort negates stagger). Vomit poison - As a standard action, release the contents of your stomach onto a creature within reach. They takes (2d6+d6 @3 and every odd LvL after) poison damage and are sickened 1 (reflex halves dmg and negates sicken). Exotic Flavor - As a free action, appreciate the fine details of an exotic beverage you've obtained via the Taste Test quest. Gain its benefits until you chug again. 1 - Seeing Double Gain the Blind Fight feat. 1 - Quest! Taste Test Standard booze can only get you so far. By traveling around the world and finding different types of alcohol, you can gain new bonuses from alcohol processing. These bonuses apply until you chug again. These beverages can usually be obtained from different cultures, races or master craftsman. Some examples… | Type | Benefit | | -------------- | -------------------------------------------------------------- | | Mind Bomb | +2 to INT skills | | Bloody Brandy | +2 to melee damage against damaged creatures | | Gob-Gin | Your movement is Elusive | | Sunset Sake | Give yourself Nightvision | | Seriozna Vodka | Take 1 True Damage and gain Resistance to frost | | Bourbon Brace | +2 to fortitude saves | 2 - Staggering Fortitude recharge As an instant action, you can cause all damage you take to be delayed until the end of your next turn. Recharges after using chug. 2 - Poison Resistance You gain resistance to poison damage, toxins and Venom. 2 - Stalwart You take half damage from fortitude saves. When you pass a fort save, it deals no damage and has no additional effect. 4 - Improvise, Adapt, Overcome When you use an improvised weapon, you can give yourself +2 attack, but the item breaks and becomes unusable after. 4 - Barrel Roll (recharge) elusive Jump onto your keg and roll around on it as a standard action. Every time you move adjacent to an enemy this turn, shove it 10 feet and gain an additional 10 speed. You cannot affect the same creature multiple times with barrel roll. 6 - Happy Hour (recharge) 1/day when you chug, allies within reach gain the same temp hp you do. 8 - Woozy Fist (elusive) (recharge) As an instant action, you can move 5 feet. If you use this in response to being attacked, a creature can move to follow you if they have the movement available to keep you in range, otherwise the attack automatically misses. Recharges when you chug. Note: This might not seem like much, but consider this. As an instant action you can step out the AoE of effects, like a line attack. You can instantly move behind cover in response to a ranged attack. You can retreat into the warm embrace of your shield brother for protection. Instant movement in a turn-based game is better than you might initially suspect! 11 - Drinking Problem Chug no longer requires alcohol processing in order to recharge, allowing you to use it every other turn. Basically all you ask yourself is "did I chug last turn?" If you did, no chug. If you didn't, chug away!

entries.title: Marauder
entries.content: A Marauder runs down and disrupts vulnerable targets using tether abilities, breaking ranks and exposing weaknesses for their team while diving in and out of the battle. | +HP (martial) | 20(+10 per level) | | ------------------------ | ----------------- | | Poise | 8 | | Skills | 1 | | Saves | Reflex | | Memory | 3+lvl | (Same stats as base martial artist, but less skills) 1 - Body Tether Su You have magically conjured ropes or chains, long hair, a silk spinneret, or some other flavored abstraction that you can use in place of rope or chains. You can only use your body tether in one way at a time; as soon as you do something else with it or stop touching it, the previous one ceases to exist. For example, you can tie someone up with your body tether, or use Swing Line with it, but it only lasts until you let go. This restriction can be ignored by simply using a real tether from your inventory, such as rope. This tether can be as long as your movement speed. Body Tether requires an empty hand to use in combat. Body Tether counts as an unarmed strike. 1 - Scorpion's Grip You can increase the range of your drag or bully maneuvers to 30ft. This makes it a thrown ranged attack If you succeed at the drag, the effect changes to moving the target adjacent to you. 1 - Swing Line When you Jump, you can also attach a tether to something you can target within a distance equal to your remaining movement speed. This functions exactly as if a rope was tied to the point, with you holding the end, and can be used as such. This tether only lasts as long as you are using it (such as for the purposes of other creatures climbing it) unless you use rope or a similar tether from your inventory or the environment. 1 - Displacement Focus Gain the Maneuver Master feat. Whenever you inflict forced movement... - You can move the target 10ft farther. - You may move 10ft, which doesn't count against your total movement and is elusive against any targets that you inflicted forced movement on this turn. 1 - Skill Gain a +4 bonus and proficiency in +Athletics, +Acrobatics and +Stealth 2 - Lifeline Elusive Target an ally within 30ft. Using both your and an ally's immediate action, you can pull them to your side - assuming you can lift them, and there are no impassable barriers. You can also use this to grab an object or unconscious target with your Immediate Action. 2 - Weave You can Dash and Hide with the same Swift Action. 4 - Speed Infusion (Su) Grant a touched ally +1 Attack of Opportunity per round (similar to and stacking with Combat Reflexes) for 10 minutes. Up to 3+CHA allies can benefit at the same time. This effect ends when you are defeated. You gain this benefit passively. 4 - The Leash (Su) A creature restrained by you with a tether is pacified as long as you're holding the leash, and are forced to follow your movement orders. 11 - Chain Grab Your excess poise damage from poise breaking an enemy rolls over instead of the enemy poise resetting back to full. Weave You can Dash and Hide with the same Swift Action.

entries.title: Merciful Monk
entries.content: Merciful monks have transcended the need to deal any damage at all, optimizing their fighting style to turn an enemies force against them and aid allies who are in danger. 1 - First Strike: Pacify Your first unarmed strike you land on a creature has a bonus On-hit effect. This effect can only be applied to a creature 1/day. Pacify a creature for 1 round on hit. 1 - Monk Vow: Vow of Mercy You have taken a vow to never directly harm another creature. You cannot deal +HP damage with attacks, spells, items etc (or effects that cause similar results like inflicting drained). You can still make attacks in order to inflict other effects (see Fighting Styles or the above first strike). Despite dealing no damage, you attacks still apply, On-hit effects, unless the effect is damage. Your unique approach to life gives you a +2 bonus to attack, the +Persuasion skill and a +4 bonus to persuasion. 1 - Ki Su Gain a (3+lvl) additional Mana Reserves. All abilities marked with Ki cost 1 mana. 1 - Disturb the Balance You can parry without a buckler. When you successfully parry, you can make a bully attack against the foe that attacked you, as long as they are within 30 ft of you. 2 - Ki: Life Link (Su) This ability has a different effect depending on if you target an enemy or an ally. You can only tether one creature at a time. Enemy - A visible tether appears between you and a target within 30 ft, bonding you together for 3 rounds (will negates). As long as this creature is within 60 ft of you, any damage that it deals to creatures that aren't you is halved and the creature takes half the damage. If the creature gets more than 60 ft away from you, the effect ends. Ally mana burn - A visible tether appears between you and a willing ally within 30 ft, bonding you together indefinitely. As long as this creature is within 100 ft of you, it gains 15 speed. Other allies that pass through the tether gain an additional 15 speed for 1 round. This tethers bonus can be inflicted on creatures by moving around them and having the tether pass through them. 4 - Redirect Force When an attack misses or grazes you and there is another creatures threatening you, you can redirect the attack to any other creature that threatens you as an immediate action, automatically hitting. If you have the Monkey Fighting Style, you cannot "double redirect" an attack when riding an opponent and using Redirect force. That would be double damage and completely obnoxious. 6 - Special Attack: Ki Block On hit apply Siphoned for 1 round (fort negates). Alternatively you can disable a creatures limb for 1 round. Targeted wings disable flight, legs decrease speed by 15 and arms render them unusable etc (fort negates). 6 - Ki: Your Skills Are Inferior (Su) As a standard or swift action, taunt an enemy. If they land an attack against you or break your poise, the taunt ends. If successful, you can parry as part of the same action by spending an additional 1 mana. 8 - Ki: Safeguard (Su) elusive healing recharge As a swift action, you can dash up to 30 feet to appear adjacent to an ally. You grant yourself and your ally 10 temp hp. You restore 10 poise for you and your ally. You can parry attacks directed at your safeguarded ally until the start of your next turn, provided they stay within reach. This ability cannot be used in successive turns. 11 - Pressure Points (Su) Whenever you land an unarmed attack against a creature, it takes a stacking -1 penalty to attack.

entries.title: striker
entries.content: Crust Striker You dribble around a ball constantly using anything but your arms, using it to accentuate your unique fighting style using it. Using all types of kicks, bumps and headbutts, you slam this ball repeatedly into your foes until they drop. 1 - Ball is Life You use mostly your feet to kick around a ball. You can kick it to make a thrown weapon attack \[elusive] against a creature (deals unarmed damage) and have it immediately bounce back to your feet. In order to steal your ball, a creature must somehow catch it mid-attack, or disarm you.\ If a creature ever grabs the ball with their hands/arms/opposable limb of choice, they immediately take 2d6+lvl (7+lvl) true damage, and the same damage at the end of each turn they continue to hold it. 1 - Penalty Shot If you do not move before attacking, you can make a ball attack at a creature within 40 ft at no penalty. 1 - Kicking Circles If you move through every space adjacent to a creature in a circle around them (usually provoking in the process), you feint them. 2 - Conditioning Gain endurance as a bonus feat.

entries.title: Strongest Under the Heavens
entries.content: Crust You know the greatest secrets of them all that the masters refuse to teach in other schools teaching martial arts. Your ability to manipulate "energy" 1 - Attacks - Poison hand pressure point nonsense - No touch knockout - Psychic powers to block/overpower people - The ability to generate photos of the creature being defeated through distracting them - Preemptive strike on people will to fight, based sole-y on reputation alone - "Miracle Kneel" The ability to feign defeat to get out of danger, or lul the enemy into a false sense of security that allies can exploit - Taunting - Seemingly immune to all status effects, but really you just delay them Skills - Extreme talent, but only in contests against others - Use deception for knowledge by asking leading questions - Fame and fortune disproportionate of actual abilities. Hijacking the narrative - Money from renown -

entries.title: The Boss
entries.content: Being the boss is all about getting into the middle of the fight, taking and dealing blows in equal measure and using their enemies as their weapons. 1 - One-Two Punch Every second unarmed strike you make a turn gets Advantage. 1 - Getting Serious While below half hp, gain FH 1 and access to haymaker. Special Attack: Haymaker Su force healing recharge Gain (10+2*LvL) as temp hp until the start of next turn. Deal the same value in force damage in a 30-foot cone (reflex halves). You can also make a unarmed attack against a creature to have the cone erupt from them, also damaging them with the cone. On-hit the creature takes a -2 penalty to their save against haymaker. Can only be used 1/minute. 1 - Send It Whenever you provoke an AoO, get a +2 bonus to attack until the end of your turn. 2 - Now Kiss During your turn, if you land an unarmed attack or poise break two creatures that are within reach, you slam them together, dazing them for 1 round (fort negates). If you are already grappling a creature, you only need to land an attack or poise break 1 other creature within reach. If you are grappling two creatures, this happens automatically. Pass or fail, once a creature is affected by this ability, it is immune for 1 hour. 2 - Meat Shield When grappling a creature, you can cause the grappled creature to be the target of an attack roll instead of you as an immediate action. 4 - Toss Foes You know the barbarism technique Foe Toss and automatically memorize it without consuming memory. 4 - Bossing Around You know the magical technique Command and automatically memorize it without consuming memory. You can use it without a staff. 6 - Fisticuffs (Su) Years of fighting hand to hand has given you insight into the things that make creatures tick merely by exchanging blows. Whenever you land or are hit by an unarmed strike, you can spend 1 mana as an instant action to glean information through contact. You cannot choose what you glean, rather it gives you insight into buried feelings, lifelong goals, insecurities and things of that sort. Whatever is most relevant to the creature is what you learn. 8 - Star KO If one of your unarmed attacks defeats a foe, you can choose to launch them d100 miles in a random direction. This does not cause them additional harm, only puts them far away from you, unless the location they land is inherently hazardous. 10 - Special Attack: Stratosphere Slam Perform an unarmed strike and uppercut your foe into the stratosphere (fort negates). While airborne, the target is considered thousands of feet away from the battlefield and is entangled. At the end of your next turn they land in the space they were launched from, taking 20 crush damage from falling and are knocked down. 11 - Special Attack: Showstopper You gain the wrestling move of the same name (see Wrestling Moves for more details). It can also be a finisher. The initial target and creatures in the impact zone instead take 7d6+lvl (24+lvl) crush damage and are staggered for 1 round (fort halves damage and negates stagger). Can only be used 1/minute.

entries.title: Fighting Styles
entries.content: Snake Style - When a creature misses you with an attack, you can move 10 ft directly closer to it (elusive). If this movement puts you within your natural reach, it Provokes. This AoO must be an unarmed strike. If the attacker is already within your natural reach, you cannot move. - Snake Sidewind - When you would be hit or grazed by an attack, you can spend an immediate action to parry the attack. This makes it count as a miss for snake style if successful. Cerberus Style - You cannot be flanked. When you would normally be flanked, gain a flanking bonus against the creatures threatening you. - Your movement is Elusive as long as you do not leave an enemy's threatened zone - Cerberus Snare - When you hit a foe with an unarmed strike, its speed is reduced by 15, it is Anchored and Grounded for 1 round On-hit. Demon Style - You get Powerful Charge. You also get a +2 bonus to damage when you Core Rules, How to Play/Actions/Common Actions/Full Round Actions/Charge attack. - You can turn up to 180 degrees once during your charge. - Tenderize - Whenever you succeed on a drag or shove attempt, deal 10 crush damage if you move them at least 10 ft. Grabbing Style - Get a +2 bonus to Grapple attacks. You only have -15 speed while grappling a creature. - Grabbing Everything: When you make a grapple attack, or maintain a grapple, you can make a grapple attack or maintain a grapple on another creature. Jabbing Style - Whenever you make an Unarmed Attack, you can move 5 ft (Elusive) after the attack, so long as you still Threaten the creature you initially attacked. This movement is in addition to your standard movement. - Flurry of Blows - When you make an unarmed strike, you can choose to deal half damage to instead make 2 against the same target. Each has all of the original effects of the attack, such as bonus effects granted by techniques or On-hit effects, but the effect can only apply once, even if both attacks land (ie only 1 saving throw). The bonus Flurry attack already does half damage and is incompatible with this feature. This effect is generally intended to increase consistency, with a few interesting quirks. Dragon Style - You get a (1+1/2 LvL) bonus to +Athletics. - Dragon Rampage - Your attacks are so violent that you create reverberating shockwaves, or you have a draconic aspect that strikes with a delay. Your first attack each round strikes again in a 5 ft AoE around your target's spaces at the start of your next turn, dealing its damage and applying On-hit effects again. One roll is made against all creatures. This only happens if you are still within reach of the targeted spaces at the start of your next turn. These spaces are targeted whether the original creature is still there or not. Monkey Style - You gain a Climb speed and a "crawl" speed equal to your Land Speed. - You can remove the Knockdown condition as a free action. - Monkey Madness - On-hit with an unarmed strike or after you break a creature's Poise, you can start riding them as an instant action. You follow their movement automatically until you are knocked off by poise breaking or otherwise. You have Cover while riding. If an attack grazes you, it instead targets your ride at full effectiveness, as though it had hit them. You cannot ride creatures smaller than you (unless you have Unconventional Mount). Other ideas - Your unarmed attacks add 2*STR to damage. - Special Attack: Dragon Roar Su Sonic - Make an unarmed attack against a creature. It also unleashes a devastating roar that erupts from them. Creatures in a 30 ft cone take 2d6+2LvL (7+2LvL) sonic damage (fort halves). On-hit the DC for the initial creature is increased by 2.

entries.title: Martial Artist
entries.content: Many warriors fight with weapons or spells, but some choose to make their own body the ultimate weapon. Through grit, asceticism or flashy techniques, a martial artist's combat prowess shines without the aid of arms or armor. | +HP (martial) | 20(+10 per level) | | ------------------------ | ----------------- | | Poise | 8 | | Skills | 4 | | Saves | Reflex | | Memory | 3+lvl | 1 - Techniques You have Martial Specialization You gain 1 technique points at level 1, then another point every 2 levels after. These technique points can be used on the unarmed or barbarism trees. 1 - Feat Gain the Unarmed Combatant feat. 1 - Unarmored Defense You are considered to be wearing medium Armor for the purposes of determining your AC. Gain 6 poise. Functions only while not wearing armor and not wielding a shield. 1 - Multiattack Make 2 attacks, one of which must be an Unarmed Attack. 1 - Flurry Make a simple unarmed attack as a swift action. It does half damage. 1 - Fitness +5 Land Speed per level. You can use the Jump action 1/turn as a free action. You can jump an unlimited number of times when you dash. You can dash as as swift action. Functions only while not wearing armor and not wielding a shield. 1 - Fighting Style Choose one from the subsection

entries.title: Pilot
entries.content: Pilots hop into their mechs and drive them around. 1 - Mech Basics Mechs must be one size category larger than you to use. In order for another creature to use your mech, they must roll a DC (20+your engineering bonus) to use it. Otherwise it is a very fancy hunk of metal to them. You are effectively treated as mounted on your mech while driving it. Any damage it takes depletes your hp and any effects targeting the mech target you (such as charming gaze). Any attacks it makes use your stats, etc. It is not a separate creature, but an extension of yourself. The only exceptions are - Use the AC value of your mech while mounted in it. This can alter your Poise as well. - You use the listed Carry Weight of the mech. You do not count against this weight, but your equipment and items do. - Does not gain additional speed based on your DEX unless otherwise stated. 1 - Emergency Exit At any point, you can eject from your mech as an instant action and appear in its space. Remounting requires a swift action, but cannot be done in the same turn you exit. Emergency exit needs to be manually recharged as a standard action while mounted to use again. 1 - Weapon of Choice Gain the pincers, sawblade or pea shooter augment as a bonus augment. 1 - Mech Types Choose One | Mech Types | Speed | AC | Carrying Capacity | Bonus Features | | ------------ | ----- | ---------- | ----------------- | -------------------------------------------- | | Speedster | 40 | 14+LvL | 100 lbs | Gains additional speed based on your DEX | | All Arounder | 30 | 15+LvL | 2k lbs | | Augments Mobility | Name | Description | | -------------------------------------------------------------------------- | ----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | | Turbo Thrusters Recharge | As a swift action, gain +30 speed and a +2 bonus to bully, shove and topple until the end of your turn. Gain a Fly speed until you stop moving. Decrease fall distance 60 ft for the purposes of determining fall damage. Cannot be used in consecutive rounds. | | Hovercraft | Your mech gains a Hover speed equal to its Land Speed. | | Offroad | Your mech gains a Swim and Climb speed equal to its land speed. | Attacks | Name | Description | | ----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | ---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | | Pea Shooter | Your mech has weapons that rapidly shoot ranged attacks at a short range.d6 pierce. 30 ft rangeMultiattack: Fire 3 projectiles. | | Pincers | You mech comes with 2 natural attacks built into it that excel at crushing and grabbing.2d6 crush. Increases to +2 CM (drag, grab)Multiattack: Make 2 pincer attacksConstrict - 2d6 crush | | Sawblade | You mech comes with 2 natural weapon(s) built into it that excel at ripping and tearing.2d6 slash. BleedMultiattack: Make 2 sawblade attacksSpecial: While you are charged, you rev up the sawblades. You get a +4 bonus to reflex saves vs attempts to avoid AoO, and a +4 bonus to Attack when making AoOs. | | Artillery (charge) | You can fire a siege projectile with 120 ft range. Make a Ranged Attack with a +2 bonus to deal (2d6+2*LvL) crush or pierce dmg. | | Charge Forth (charge) | Move twice your speed in a straight line. All creatures you move through with this ability take (2d6+2*LvL) crush damage and are knocked down (reflex halves dmg and negates knockdown). | | Death Laser (recharge) Zap or Force | Target a 60 ft line. All creatures inside take (2d6+2*LvL) zap or force damage (your choosing). | Defense | Name | Description | | ---- | ----------- | | | | Utility | Name | Description | | -------------------------------------------------------------------------- | -------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | | Clown Technology | Up to a dozen allies can also mount your mech to slip inside of an extradimensional space. It has a appropriate seating and a screen where inhabitants can see what you see.While inside they cannot interact with the outside world. If you take damage, all creatures inside are immediately ejected into adjacent spaced and staggered for 1 round. | | Cloaking Device Recharge | You have Camouflage if you move 15 ft or less while out of combat. 1/minute, you can manually activate cloaking device as a swift action for 1 round to move at full speed. | | Cruising Speed | While not under duress, increase all mech speeds to 50mph. | | Filtration | Your mech creates its own self-sustaining air supply from within, allowing you exist in locations without air and evade toxic gasses. |

entries.title: Siege Engineer
entries.content: Siege Engineers use a specialized type of vehicle that excels in destruction and defense at the cost of mobility and utility. 1 - Mech Basics Mechs must be one size category larger than you to use. In order for another creature to use your mech, they must roll a DC (20+your engineering bonus) to use it. Otherwise it is a very fancy hunk of metal to them. You are effectively treated as mounted on your mech while driving it. Any damage it takes depletes your hp and any effects targeting the mech target you (such as charming gaze). Any attacks it makes use your stats, etc. It is not a separate creature, but an extension of yourself. The only exception to this is you use the AC value of your mech while mounted in it. 1 - Emergency Exit At any point, you can eject from your mech as an instant action and appear in its space. Remounting requires a swift action, but cannot be done in the same turn you exit. Emergency exit needs to be manually recharged as a standard action while mounted to use again. 1 - Parabola Your attacks always ignore Cover thanks to the angle you can fire them at. 1 - Weapon of Choice Gain the shield action 1 - Mech Type | Mech Types | Speed | AC | Carrying Capacity | Bonus Features | | ---------- | ----- | ------ | ----------------- | -------------- | | Tank | 20 | 16+LvL | 2k lbs | DRA 1 | Augments Mobility | Name | Description | | ---------- | ------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | | Blitzkrieg | You ignore difficult terrain and automatically destroy any barrier of average or lesser toughness (see Breaking Objects) simply by moving through it. You get a +8 bonus on +Athletics checks to break through barriers of of solid or greater toughness. This check is made with Advantage if you have fired an attack at the barrier. | Attacks | Name | Description | | ------------------------------------------------------- | -------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | | Fireworks | Your mech has weapons that shoot flaming projectiles that explode on impact.2d10 fire, 120 ft rangeSpecial: Targets a 5 ft AoE, using a single attack roll.Fireworks Show Recharge: You can fire your attacks into the air each round, causing colorful explosions to amuse creatures. Outside of combat this fascinates minions. For non-minions, (will negates). | | Cannon | Your mech has a cannon affixed to it.2d10 crush, 120 ft rangeSpecial: You can target a space on the ground, or a wall to automatically Graze creatures within 10 ft of it.High Impact: | | Stopping Power | When you attack a creature with Cover, or Improved Cover, you destroy the cover after the attack is resolved. | Defense | Name | Description | | ---- | ----------- | | | | Utility | Name | Description | | -------------------------------------------------------- | ------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | | Filtration | Your mech creates its own self-sustaining air supply from within, allowing you exist in locations without air and evade toxic gasses. |

entries.title: Suitor
entries.content: Suitors wear their mech like it was a fancy suit of armor. 1 - Suit Basics Your mech is essentially a piece of full body armor shaped like you that you must enter to operate. It is form fitting and is operated by you physically moving your limbs. Once you put it on, you can activate it and deactivate it as an instant action 1/turn, causing it to either disappear or reappear. While it is deactivated, it leaves behind large distinct tattoos that look like wires, power sources or something similar behind. Usually because of this feature, there isn't much reason to get out of the mech outside of repairs. In order for another creature to use your mech, they must roll a DC (20+your engineering bonus) to use it. Otherwise it is a very fancy hunk of metal to them. Because your mech is a suit of armor, you can decide what type of armor it mimics (light, medium, heavy), provided you have the required strength. 1 - Handy Many offensive suit augments require hands to utilize. Unlike a pilot who can get a more complex control scheme to activate additional limbs, you cannot use extra limbs to control the additional augments. If you take more than 2 hand augments, then you need to swap between or remove them using the Swap Item action. Hands consumed by augments can be used to hold/grab things, but cannot wield weapons/shields or perform fine manipulation. Ex. You can drag an ally to safety but you can't feed them a potion. You can open a door, but you cant lockpick it. 1 - Weapon of Choice Gain a hand augment. Augments Hand | Name | Description | | ---------------------------------------------------------- | --------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | | Beam Cannon | You have a cannon attached to your arm capable of firing bursts of elemental energy.D8. 30 ft rangeMultiattack: Fire 2 cannon shotsSpecial: As a swift action, you can change the damage of your cannon to an elemental type permanently (limited to frost, fire, holy, necrotic, poison, zap). | | Flamethrower | You get a flamethrower attached to your arm. Your attacks target a 15 ft cone in front of you with fire. They count as Thrown attacks.2d6 fire.Special: By spending your charge, you can set your foes Ablaze On-hit. | | Power Fist | You have a special mechanism on your knuckles that gives your unarmed attacks some extra punch. Gain Unarmed Combatant as a bonus feat.2d10 crush. +8 CM (topple, shove)Special: Your Shove attacks push an extra 10 feet and you get a +4 bonus on +Athletics. | Mobility | Name | Description | | -------------------------------------------------------------- | ---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | | Enhanced Agility | You gain the Martial Artist fitness feature. Only usable with light armor. | | Space Jump | You can jump as far and as high as your speed. You can jump multiple times any turn you take the jump action. | | Grapple Hook | Whether it is attached to your hand, or a separate device, you have a projectile weapon that is connected to you that you can fire up to 60 ft as a swift action. It is not powerful enough to quickly pull you to your destination, but can do so slowly. If you fire it at a surface that is angled roughly 45 degrees upward, you can use it to travel 60 ft in the direction you hooked.Can also be used to make Drag attacks, pull objects to you and anything else that could reasonably be done with a grapple hook. If a target is within 30 ft, you can Grapple them. | Attacks | Name | Description | | ----------------------------------------------------------------------------- | ----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | | Oil Slick Recharge | As a swift action, target a point within 30 ft. Cover that space, and all spaces within 10 ft in a thick black liquid. This area is very flammable and treated as difficult terrain. Cannot be used in consecutive turns.If ignited, sets creatures inside ablaze (reflex negates) and removes the difficult terrain. Fire persists for 1 round. | | Turbo Fist (charge) Elusive | Move 60 feet in a straight line. All creatures you move through with this ability take (2d6+d6/odd lvl) crush damage and are knocked down (reflex halves dmg and negates knockdown). The DC increases by 2 for the first creature you hit.You can choose to end your charge when you hit the very first creature to instead inflict Dazed for 1 turn (reflex halves dmg and negates daze). It does not receive the +2 boost to the DC. | | Overheat (charge) Fire | Release an enormous amount of heat to creatures nearby. Gain an aura that causes creatures within 10 ft of you to be set ablaze (reflex negates). Lasts 3 turns. While this effect is active, if you take at least 5 fire damage, extend its duration by 1 round. | | Charge Beam (charge) | Special: Only usable with Beam Cannon Spend your charge to increase weapon damage for a single shot changes to (2d6+d6/odd LvL). Also get +30 range and a bonus On-hit effect based on the element you currently have selected for a single attack. Holy - Opponent emits Bright Light, they take a -10 penalty to +Stealth and attacks against them gain SeekingNecrotic - Foe is Bone Chilled until after they receive their next Healing effect.Fire - Set foe AblazeFrost - Foe becomes FrostyPoison - Foe is Sickened 2Zap - | Defense | Name | Description | | --------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | ------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | | Overshield Healing Recharge | Gain (20+3*LvL) Temp HP as a FRA. While you have the shield, you cannot move, but are Unstoppable. You are still unstoppable against effects that break the shield. You can deactivate this shield as a free action, losing the temp hp but allowing you to act again. Usable 1/minute. | | Evasion | Gain Evasion (light armor only). | Utility | Name | Description | | -------------------------------------------------------------------------- | --------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | | Cloaking Device Recharge | You have Camouflage if you move 15 ft or less while out of combat. 1/minute, you can manually activate cloaking device as a swift action for 1 round to move at full speed. | | Filtration | Your mech creates its own self-sustaining air supply from within, allowing you exist in locations without air and evade toxic gasses. | | Morph Ball | As a swift action, transform into a tiny armored ball. While in ball form, your movement is elusive, but you can take no other actions other than move. You can only see to a range of 30 ft. Cannot be used while Knocked Down. You can end morph ball as a free action. |

entries.title: Mechanic 1
entries.content: Mechanics are engineers that put their heart and soul into their gadgets and gizmos, preferring one specific creation that they pilot into battle. | Basic Statistics | | | ----------------------- | ------------------------------------- | | HP | +10/LV | | ACC | +2 | | Skills | 4 | | Saves | Fortitude | | Mana | 2+1/LV | | Other | +4 and Proficiency in Engineering | 1 - Techniques You gain 2 Technique Points at level 1, then another point every 2 levels after. These Technique Points can be used on any tree, though Spells require a way to give your Mech the ability to qualify as a Staff component. 1 - Greatest Creation Mechanics start off with a specific type of mech, determined by archetype. The variety tends to come from how integrated it is into the mechanics themselves, such as a vehicle they pilot or a suit that augments their bodies. When you are Defeated, this mech becomes unusable and requires repairing. This requires a (30-Engineering roll) minutes (minimum 1) and appropriate tools. If you ever lose this mech, you can recreate it in (30-engineering roll) days (minimum 1) and 200g of materials like metals and leather seats. Unless otherwise noted, all class features only function while mounted on/in mech. 1 - Supercharge Mechs have many powerful abilities that require the mechanic to gather power as a Free Action, allowing them to use one of the Supercharge abilities on their next turn. This always has some kind of distinct audio and visual quality, such as revving engines, glowing chassis, or steam erupting from its nooks. You automatically fail Stealth checks when Supercharging, and creatures get +20 Perception to notice you when Supercharging. While Supercharging, the Mechanic cannot move. They can choose to end the Supercharge and start it again at a later point in their turn to avoid this. Because Supercharging is an aggressive action that is easily noticed, it will almost always result in combat starting when the action is taken, making it infeasible to use a Supercharge ability on the first round. You cannot Supercharge and use a Supercharge ability on the same turn. You must alternate turns between Supercharging and using a move that consumes the effect. 1 - Augment Gain 4 Augments, two of which use the Charge feature. Augments are customizable features that can give you new abilities, add additional features to your mech's limbs and enhance your physical abilities. Gain an additional Augment each even level. Some augments have level restrictions denoted by a number in (parenthesis). 2 - Lock On You can choose a foe within 60 ft of as a Swift Action to hone in on. Your attacks have Seeking and +2 ACC against this foe until you dismisses this ability (a Free Action). You automatically know the position of the creature, even if they turn Invisible or gain Total Cover. This ability does not negate cover for the purposes of attacking this foe, but you are still aware of the foe's position until it moves at least 120 ft away from you. You only Lock On to one creature at a time.

entries.title: Mechanic
entries.content: Crust Mechanics are engineers that put their heart and soul into their gadgets and gizmos, preferring one specific creation that they pilot into battle. They come in 2 main variants: pilots and suitors. Pilots are your typical mecha-style characters that hop into a larger robot and steer it around. Suitors have something more form fitting that augments their movement and fighting. | +HP (mixed) | 20(+8 per level) | | ------------------------ | ---------------- | | Poise | 6 | | Skills | 4 | | Saves | Will | | Memory | 3+lvl | 1 - Techniques You have Martial Specialization. You gain 2 technique points at level 1, then another point every 2 levels after. These technique points can be used on any martial technique tree. 1 - Greatest Creation Mechanics start off with a specific type of mech, determined by archetype. The variety tends to come from how integrated it is into the mechanics themselves, such as a vehicle they pilot or a suit that augments themselves. When you are defeated, this mech becomes unusable and requires repairing. This requires a (30-engineering roll) minutes (minimum 1) and appropriate tools. If you ever lose this mech, you can recreate it in (30-engineering roll) days (minimum 1) and 200g of materials like metals and comfy leather seats. Unless otherwise noted, all class features only function while mounted on/in mech. 1 - Charge Mechs have many powerful abilities that require the mechanic to gather power. This always has some kind of distinct audio and visual quality, such as revving engines, glowing chassis, steam erupting from every nook and cranny etc. You automatically fail +Stealth checks when charging, and creatures get a +20 bonus to +Perception to notice you when charging. Rolling initiative resets your charge, requiring you to charge again. This mechanic (teehee) is designed to make it so you cannot use charge attacks on turn 1 of combat. Period. You can't use it as a surprise attack, preemptively charge and then enter combat etc. Hopefully this is airtight, but just in case, don't let players do this. Charging is a free action, but you cannot use a charge move in the same turn that you charge. Vise versa is also true; you cannot charge right after using a charge move. This usually makes every odd turn (1, 3, 5) a charge turn, and every even turn a charge attack turn, provided you have the actions and don't spend them doing other things. The terminology is a bit weird but there is a distinction between the class feature Charge and the general tag recharge. These 2 things do not interact. The mechanic has many of both types of abilities and neither interact with each others cool downs. Abilities that require charge that are attack rolls count as special attacks. 1 - Skill You get Engineering as a bonus skill and a +4 bonus to it. Doesn't require mech. 1 - Augment Gain 2 augments, one of which uses the charge feature. Augments are customizable features that can give you new abilities, add additional features to your mech's limbs and enhance your physical abilities. Gain an additional augment each even level. Some augments have level restrictions denoted by a number in (parenthesis). 1 - Feat Gain Point Blank Master. 1 - Scan As a standard action, closely examine an object or creature within 30 ft to get a +4 bonus on +Knowledge checks related to it. 2 - Lock On You can choose a foe within 60 ft of as a swift action to hone in on a foe. Your attacks have seeking and you get a +2 bonus to attack against this foe until you dismisses this ability (free action). You automatically know the position of the creature, even if they turn Invisible or gain Total Cover. You can automatically lock on to a creature you scan, regardless if you succeed on the knowledge check. This ability does not negate cover for the purposes of attacking this foe, but you are still aware of the foe's position until it moves at least 300 ft away from you. You can lock on to only one creature at a time.

entries.title: Pilot
entries.content: A Pilot's weapon of choice is a Mech, a transforming war machine that is ridden and loaded with weapons to deal maximum damage. | Basic Statistics | | | ----------------------- | --- | | HP | | | ACC | | | Skills | | | Saves | | | Mana | | | Other | | 1 - Mech Basics Mechs must be one size category larger than you to use. In order for another creature to use your mech, they must roll a DC (20+your engineering bonus) every round. You are treated as Mounted on your mech, but it uses your statistics. Any damage it takes depletes your HP and any effects targeting the mech target you (such as Charming Gaze). It is not a separate creature, but an extension of yourself. It needs repair when you are Defeated while mounted, but in exchange, your Defeat condition changes. As long as you remain inside, you are treated as being at 1 HP, will stop being Defeated after one minute of not taking damage, and may then exit safely to start repairs. If you take enough damage to die outright, the Mech explodes, killing you with it. While piloting, your own arms are occupied. You use the Mech's arms instead. By default, your mech has two arms and two legs. Its arms can perform Unarmed attacks, but also Pincer Natural Weapons. Pincer: 2d6 Crush Damage, AS 2, Close Natural Weapon. The Mech may change what its arms are at the start of its turn, similarly and simultaneously to Switching Weapons. Its arms are part of its body, and thus cannot be Disarmed like a regular weapon can. A Mech weighs about a ton. You can build spare sets of parts ahead of time (in the time it would normally take to make a Mech) to quickly replace a lost Mech in the time it would normally take to repair one. 1 - Emergency Exit Recharge. At any point, you can eject from your mech as an Instant Action and appear in its space. Remounting requires a Swift Action. Augments Supercharge Augments | Name | Description | | -------------------- | ----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | | Siege Mode | Turn an arm into a Gatling Gun.d8 Pierce Damage, AS 3, Short Range, Trigger Weapon.Siege Mode: Supercharge to Entangle yourself for one round to gain +3 AS on the Gatling Gun, and increase all ranged attacks to Long Range. You can remain Entangled on subsequent rounds to maintain the bonus without needing to Supercharge again.Being displaced ends Siege Mode prematurely. | | Arcane Amplification | Turn an arm into a Staff.d8 Crush Damage, AS 3, 1h Bludgeon Staff Weapon.Arcane Amplification: Supercharge to grant a Spell cast with the Staff +2 CL. | | Rocket Boost | Add a jetpack and shock absorbers to the Mech, negating Fall Damage. Rocket Boost: Supercharge to Jump as a Free Action. This does not count against your movement. Vertical jumps get their full distance. |

entries.title: Puppet Master
entries.content: This archetype replaces psychic implants 2 - Puppeteer companion concentration You can take control of the body of a creature that gets defeated within 30 ft of you as an instant action if they are the target of your hypnotic stare, or you can redirect your stare as an immediate action if they aren't (will negates). When you take control, they gain (10+2\*lvl) temp hp and you control them as the Domination spell until this temp hp is depleted, you no longer have line of sight to your puppet, you are unable to concentrate or 1 minute passes. The creature acts on your turn.

entries.title: Mesmerist
entries.content: Mesmerists always have eye-catching irises, tattoos, masks or baubles to place more emphasis on their eyes. Through these special eyes, a mesmerist can see a creatures insecurities, or generate them where they otherwise wouldn’t exist, eroding a creatures will to make them more susceptible to their spells, skills and more. | +HP (specialist) | 20(+6 per level) | | ------------------------ | ---------------- | | Poise | 5 | | Skills | 2 | | Saves | Will | | Memory | 2+(2\*lvl) | 1 - Techniques You have Magical Specialization You gain 2 technique points at level 1, then another 2 points every 2 levels after. These technique points can be used on the Moogen and Shira trees. Characters with magical specialization gain points in wild card equal to their highest tier of magic technique and cantrips. 1 - Hypnotic Stare Su You can focus your gaze on a creature within 30 ft as a swift action to apply a -2 penalty to their will saves. This lasts until the creature leaves this area, you are defeated or until you focus your gaze on another creature. This ability does not require the creature to meet your gaze. You can't use hypnotic stare while blinded. If facing a creature with a gaze attack, you must not avert your gaze, even partially, if you want to use your hypnotic stare against it. 1 - Copycat (Su) companion You have an eye for technique. Whenever a creature within 30 ft that you can see performs a technique, you can choose to memorize it as an instant action, provided it does not exceed the highest level technique you can cast. It takes up a companion slot while memorized instead of memory. You get the companion boost to attacks made with this technique. 1 - Staring Contest You get advantage on saves against gaze attacks. 1 - Skill Gain +Deception as a bonus skill with a +4 bonus. 1 - Psychic Casting You don't need verbal components to cast spells, but to cast at all you need a still mind. You cannot cast spells while frightened, dominated or confused. 2 - Psychic Implants (Su) contingent subtle mana burn Psychic implants are effects that linger in a creature's mind, from simple suggestions to something more sinister. You can spend one minute to insert a psychic implant as a standard action against a creature within 30 ft. At any point thereafter, you can activate the implant as an instant action. You mana burn while it is implanted, but when the implant ends, so to does the mana burn. Gain a psychic implant this level and another one at levels 4, 6, 8 and 10. 2 - Painful Stare (Su) mind-affecting recharge You can infuse your stare with figments that cause creatures to experience things that do not exist. Whenever a creature affected by your hypnotic stare takes +HP damage, you can apply the effect of one of your painful stares as an instant action. Usable once each turn. When you gain a psychic implant, you can choose to instead gain a choice from the list of painful stares. 2 - Bold Stare (Su) Creatures affected by your hypnotic stare take additional penalties. Choose one this level and at every four levels thereafter. All penalties apply simultaneously. | Name | Effect | | --------------------- | --------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | | Stupor | -2 to skill checks | | Nearsigheted | Creature cannot see beyond 30 ft, except for your eyes | | Sluggish | -2 to reflex saves | | Maimed | -15 speed | | Surrounded | Attacks made against target creature always benefit from flanking. Creature cannot flank. | | Mindless Manipulation | Your effects with the Mind-Affecting tag can affect mindless creatures you stare at, but your hypnotic stare's penalty to will saves doesn't apply. | | Diplomatic | Get +2 bonus to +Persuasion against creatures you stare at | | Sapped | -2 to creature's DC | | Eyes Locked | Creature has Blurred Vision against all creatures other than you | 4 - Aura Sight (Su) You can detect creatures based off of an aura that you can physically see, even when the creature would otherwise be concealed. Gain a sight based blindsense (30 ft) while not using hypnotic stare. While using hypnotic stare, the creature cannot hide from you, even behind total cover. You see their aura through invisibility, fog etc. This ceases to function while you have blurred vision or you are blinded. 6 - X-Ray Vision (Su) concentration As a standard action, you can touch a surface to see through it. You must spend a standard action each turn to maintain this sight, You can see through 1 ft of most substances, but only 1 inch of metal. You cannot see through surfaces with a thin sheet of lead in it. Some things, like piles, count as 1 continuous object. 8 - Me First (Su) When you use copycat, you can choose to instantly use the technique they use by increasing its mana cost by 1. You lose any actions you spend on your next turn. If you can identify a spell before it is used (with arcana), it occurs before they use it. 11 - Distant Stare (Su) The range of hypnotic stare increases to 60 ft.

entries.title: Painful Stares
entries.content: Effects with a number require a specific level. 2 - Staring Daggers Your stare is painful in the most literal sense. When triggered, the creature takes an additional d4+CL (2+CL) pierce damage. 2 - Stink Eye Your stare fills the creatures nostrils with a powerful stench. When triggered, the creature becomes sickened 1. This cannot cause a creature to become higher than sickened 1. 2 - Bloodthirst Your stare causes the creature to believe they are hemophiliac. The immediately begin bleeding for d6 damage. 6 - Ice Cold Stare frost The creature believes ice creeps across its body. When triggered, the creature becomes frosty. 10 - Dirty Look Your gaze holds a deep unsatisfied hunger for the creature so powerful it forces them to avert their gaze from you. When triggered, they are DD against you specifically until the end of your next turn.

entries.title: Psychic Implants
entries.content: Effects with a number require a specific level. All of these effects have the contingent, subtle and companion tags. They are all (Su) abilities. Unlike other contingent effect, there is no visual indicator. 2 - Suggestion When you inflict the implant, you give the creature a simple suggestion and a trigger. Something is simple when it requires little effort, isn't dangerous and only requires a few minutes. This won't make a creature act against its ethos, but can make them do something unusual for them. "Don't lock the door on your way out. Vouge for me when I speak to the elder." Etc You can also leave the implant's suggestion blank, and make a suggestion later by verbally making it. 2 - Innocuous Visitor When you activate the implant, you cause the creature to not notice you and your allies while you exist nearby for 10 minutes. The creature treats your actions as normal as long as they are not violent and does not remember what you and your allies did during this time. 2 - Witness scrying When you activate the implant, you can sense through the creature for up to 10 minutes. You see through its eyes, hear with its ears etc using all of its senses from their PoV. You are effectively stunned while you do this and can choose to redirect your senses back to your own body if you take damage or are otherwise harmed. 2 - Mind over Matter healing When you activate the implant, the implanted creature gains 5+(your level\*3) temp hp. This forces them to push past their bodies usual limits, so it also inflicts drained 1. 2 - Advanced Darkness Dark What you activate this implant, the creature suddenly finds themselves in a cloud of darkness that they cannot escape. This darkness lasts one minute. Does not work against creatures with darkvision. 8 - Imposter glamer When you activate this implant, you can choose another creature that the implanted creature can see and have them appear to transform into a more to fearsome version of themselves. They stay the same size, but you can make them appear more fiendish, undead, they grow a bunch of tentacles, etc. Their exact appearance is up to you. The transformation lasts 1 minute. Reactions may vary from directly attacking the creature to causing a scene. No matter what though, the creature is automatically shaken 2 during the transformation. 10 - Fake Death When you activate this implant, you can have the creature appear to die to all others. The creature actually turns invisible, as per the spell. Creatures get a will save to disbelieve the illusory corpse when they interact with it.

entries.title: Death Knight
entries.content: For when casting isn't as important to you and you want to get in there and slap some fools with your boners. This variation of the class has a lot more survivability and is geared towards being a raid boss. | +HP (mixed) | 20(+8 per level) | | ------------------------ | ---------------- | | Poise | 6 | | Skills | 4 | | Saves | Fort | | Memory | 3+lvl | 1 - Techniques You have Martial Specialization and Magical Specialization. You gain an additional technique point at level 1, then another point every 2 levels after. The technique points can be used on any martial tree or the Vampirism tree. 1 - Vengeful Spirit Su teleportation When an undead you control within 30 ft of you is defeated, you can teleport to its space as an immediate action (with larger creatures choose which space), and its remains teleport to your previous position. You can also make a Simple Attack. 1 - Bathe in Blood (Su) healing Whenever a creature within 30 ft of you takes bleed damage, you heal 3 hp. 1 - Special Attack: Brow Slicer Strike just above a foes eyes to cause them to take d6 (4) bleed On-hit. Causes blurred vision for as long as they are bleeding. 1 - Death Strike Whenever you strike a creature, you drain blood from its wounds. If the creature is bleeding, it immediately takes bleed damage and extends the duration by 1 round (if it has one). In the case where the attack itself inflicts bleed, this ability does not proc unless the target was already bleeding. 2 - Boiling Blood (Su) Spend 1 mana to choose 3 creatures within 30 ft of you. Their blood melts out of their veins and start bleeding for 2d6 (7) damage (fort negates). 4 - Rally Servant (Su) By spending 2 mana as a swift action, you can command an undead you control to attack a foe you made a melee attack against this turn. 6 - Special Attack: Heart Strike You cause a creature to bleed internally, making it harder to heal. On-hit inflict a bleed effect that deals 2d6 (7) damage. The DC to remove the bleed is 5 higher. 6 - Deathrattle (Su) Undead you control gain a Deathrattle. When an undead creature you control is defeated, it attempts to cling to nearby foes with its undying breath. An enemy (of your choosing) within its natural reach is entangled (reflex reduces to 15 Poise damage). Creatures can Breakout as a swift action. 8 - Death Grip (Su) necrotic Spend 4 mana to dash 30 feet without provoking (even into the air) and make a grapple check with a +4 bonus. If you succeed, you carry the creature down into the ground and slam them violently. They take 5d8+CL (22+CL) necrotic damage, appropriate Fall Damage and the grapple ends. 8 - Touch of Corruption (Su) On-hit with a Weapon Attack, Natural Attack or Unarmed Attack, you can spend 2 mana to add touch of corruption as an additional effect (instant action). The creature is drained 10 (fort halves). You can also make touch of corruption as a Melee Attack with no other effects to remove the saving throw attached. 10 - Death Coil (Su) (necrotic) (healing) Recharge Spend 5 mana to unleash a skull of dark energy at a creature within 30 ft. The creature takes 6d8+CL (27+CL) necrotic damage (will halves). The skull tears a chunk of the creature's spirit and you can direct the skull at an ally (also with 30ft of you) to heal them for the damage dealt. Usable 1/minute. 11 - Plague Strike (Su) When you defeat a creature on your turn, you can use your Animate Dead feature as an immediate action, provided it is within your reach.

entries.title: Medium
entries.content: Mediums go all in on casting. They also have several unique abilities that give them insight into the dead. | +HP (specialist) | 20(+6 per level) | | ------------------------ | ---------------- | | Poise | 5 | | Skills | 2 | | Saves | Will | | Memory | 2+(2\*lvl) | 1 - Techniques You gain an additional 2 technique points at level 1, then another point every 2 levels after. The technique points can be spent on any magic tree. 1 - Skill You gain +Religion proficiency and a +4 bonus. 2 - Speak with Corpse Su Mana Burn For 10 minutes your eyes glow with a chilling green aura. During this time you can speak to corpses of creatures with intact heads within 5 feet of you, asking 5 questions (in total, not to each creature) before the power fades. The corpse knows only what it knew in life, including languages. Answers are usually brief or cryptic. The corpse is under no compulsion to offer a truthful answer if you are hostile to it or it recognizes you as an enemy. The corpse can’t learn new information, doesn’t comprehend anything that has happened since it died, and can’t speculate about future events. 2 - Closet Skeletons (Su) By placing an (almost complete) inanimate skeleton of a race in a creature's closet for 24 simultaneous hours, you can divine things from the skeleton afterwards. You can speak directly to the skeleton and it will tell you details about the creature. These are things that the creature is ashamed of, and goes out of its way to hide and lie about. The creature must be a current resident of the home you place the skeleton into. 4 - Through Death's Eyes (Su) scrying Touch a dead creature to perceive what happened during the last minute it was alive and conscious. Alternatively project your senses through one of the undead you control for 10 up to minutes. You can continue to give your minions orders while you observe. You can speak using your voice or a raspier one more appropriate for an undead creature. 6 - Spirit Medium (Su) concentration You can initiate contact with spirits of the dead that reside in the Underworld. To do so you need a few things... * An artifact of significance to the creature, such as a weapon, trinket or family picture. * The creature's full name * 1 minute of uninterrupted concentration If you meet the conditions, and the creature's spirit is not trapped in some way (including inside of a living body) then the spirit temporarily inhabits your body, up to 10 minutes. If the ritual fails, you know why, such as the artifact not being of enough significance, the creature being alive etc. You lose access to your body, only viewing the events as though a passenger in your own body, therefore requiring a 3rd party to do the interacting with the spirit. The creature speaks in its own voice, but otherwise moves and interacts with the physical world via your body. Whether or not a spirit is interested in speaking changes drastically from person to person. Some spirits will be happy to be able to speak with loved ones, and others will immediately leave at the sight of a past enemy. A +Persuasion check is usually in order before the spirit leaves. The spirit is mostly limited to only what they knew in life, and they are still able to fully articulate their thoughts as though they were of sound mind. Little information about the Mortal Realm passes into The Underworld, and less still reaches the ears of the average spirit. 8 - Soul Cage (Su) By spending 2 mana as an immediate action when a creature dies within 60 ft of you, you can choose to seal the creatures soul inside of a staff you wield. A tiny spectral cage forms at its tip with a miniature version of the creatures spirit inside. Until the the cage is opened with +Sleight of Hand, or you cage a new soul, the spirit is trapped inside of your staff. While trapped the spirit remains conscious and can perceive the world around it as it did in life. You can converse with the spirit, and you can silence it if it gets to loud. 11 - Overlord When you animate dead, you can choose to set its LvL to 3 and have it not consume a companion slot. You are allowed to give it a singular order at the time of creation that it fulfills until it is destroyed.

entries.title: Restless Spirit
entries.content: A restless spirit is a bruiser that is designed to get in there an whack some fools. Filled with supernatural abilities that allow you to cheat death. | +HP (mixed) | 20(+8 per level) | | ------------------------ | ---------------- | | Poise | 6 | | Skills | 4 | | Saves | Fort | | Memory | 3+lvl | 1 - Techniques You have Martial Specialization. You gain an additional technique point at level 1, then another point every 2 levels after. The technique points can be used on any martial tree. 1 - Drain Hope Su Recharge Healing Necrotic Overheal By spending 1 mana a swift action, make a Thrown attack. On-hit apply a tether linking you and the target creature. At the end of your turn it takes 2d6+CL (7+CL) necrotic damage and you heal an equal amount. This tether lasts 3 up to 3 rounds. If the creature moves more than 60 feet from you, the tether breaks. When you miss, or when the tether breaks, this ability cannot be used again on your next 2 turns. 1 - Death Defied (Su) (recharge) When you are defeated, you can instead choose not to be. Your body vanishes and transforms into an incorporeal and invincible spirit version of yourself, cleansing all non-permanent status. While in this form you can continue to float around the battlefield with a 30 ft hover speed. At the end of your 4rd turn defying death, you return to life at 50% of your max hp. While in this form you can move and speak, but you can only take 2 actions: simple melee attack, or drain hope. They have unique effects while defying death. Melee attacks reduce a creatures speed by 15 on hit, but deals no damage. Drain hope immediately recharges when you enter death defied, and has its resource cost removed. It does not deal damage or heal you, but for each round you drain, you return to life 1 turn sooner. After you use death defied, you acquire a unique Tier 3 Affliction and Drain Hope immediately recharges. Hades' Watchful Eye - You cannot use death defied. Cure - You must pay your respects to the god of death. This requires performing a 1 week ritual in a place of worship dedicated to Hades, or spending 1 week tidying up places where the dead are kept. 1 - Bonkers You can use the cleave Special Attack any number of times each combat. It is not limited to only slashing weapons. The first time you land a melee attack each round, gain a stack. When you have 2 stacks, your next weapon attack gets the following bonuses. You can choose not to unleash this attacks power when you attack to save it for a more suitable moment. - Get a +10 bonus to attack. - The attack echoes to nearby enemies. Targets all enemies within 10 ft of the original target. If the attack already hits multiple targets (like with cleave), then it cannot strike the same target twice. 2 - Wraith Walk (Su) teleportation You can let your spirit temporarily leave your body by spending 1 mana as a standard action. When you do, you immediately dash and become an Invincible Incorporeal spirit, similar to death defied. Your body remains in the position you used this ability from. At the end of your turn, your body teleports to the position of your spirit. 4 - Children of the Grave (Su) Curse Companion You can apply a curse to an enemy by spending 2 mana as a swift action. The curse has no effect until the creature is defeated or killed. When said condition is filled, its spirit briefly leaves its body and fights by your side. The spirit uses all of the creatures stats, but it can only use its multiattack, or make a single simple attack. It spawns with only 50% of its max +HP and disappears after 1 minute. The spirit persists even if the creature recovers from the defeated condition or death. A spirit can only spawn from a creature once each day. If you are not present when the spirit is summoned, you receive a mental alert and can immediately begin scrying and control the spirit. If you already have 2 companion slots filled because of your undead, one is put in stasis until the spirit leaves. The curse is removed by touching a gravestone and saying "F." 6 - Striking Your weapon attacks, natural attacks and unarmed attacks gain additional damage equal to their damage dice. Examples: 2h weapon @1=2d8, @6=4d8, @11=6d8 Light Weapon @1=d6, @6=2d6, @11=3d6 8 - Improved Wraith Walk You can use wraith walk as a swift action, and can maintain wraith form for up to 3 turns. Using it as a swift action increases its mana cost increases to 2. When you move directly through a creature, it becomes Sickened 2 (fort negates). 11 - Youuuuu

entries.title: Necromancer
entries.content: A necromancer's primary power is the ability to give new life to corpses, adding them to their squad of undead minions. Their favorite spells aid their minions, use them as ammunition or simply kill creatures. Their other abilities aid them in gathering information through their undead or dead creatures. Some necromancers choose to sit back and let their minions do the work, and others lead them into battle. Archetype determines base stats. 1 - Techniques You have Magical Specialization You gain 1 technique point at level 1, then another point every 2 levels after. These points must be used on the Necromancy tree. 1 - Animate Dead Sp companion Spend 1 mana to cast Animate Dead, but creatures are 1/2 your level instead of 1/3 (min 0). - You start off with a single creature that you used this ability on. The creature must have been level 1 or lower. - You can only control 2 undead, as per the companion system. Any in excess are automatically destroyed. - As you advance in level, so do your created undead. - You do not Mana Burn from your undead. 1 - Rejuvenation Su healing - With a 3 round ritual, you can cause an adjacent destroyed undead creature you created to reform at full hp. You can also use this on undead you haven't created, but the creature has its own autonomy and memory. Undead companions are also healed to full hp if adjacent to you. - You can perform either of these rituals on multiple undead creatures at once 1 - Deathwatch (Su) As a standard action, you can stare down a creature/object to find out a handful of details about them. - Alive, dead, neither (Undead, Constructs, object etc.) - If not dead, current and max hp. - If dead, how long have they been dead. - Determine if something is even a dead creature (such as a pile of ash). 1 - Languages Gain necril as a bonus language. 1 - Charnel Soldiers (Su) All of your controlled undead share your teamwork feats. 2 - Necrosis Gain resistance to necrotic damage. 6 - Command Undead (Su) Temporarily gain control over an undead creature. Functions as the Domination spell (will negates), but it can only target undead within 10 ft of you, it only lasts 3 rounds max and it does not share the spell's tags. Against minions and mindless undead that are not companions, this has no save. 8 - Blindsense Lifesense 10 ft. 10 - Advanced Rejuvenation (Su) Your rejuvenation activates on yourself automatically after you die, bringing you back to life at 50% hp in 3 rounds. Creatures that succeed on a DC 30 +Religion or Arcana check can either interrupt this ritual or banish the spirits automatically as a standard action while within reach of your corpse.

entries.title: Dashing Hero
entries.content: The ultimate knight in shining armor. Dash into battle with blinding speed to pluck your allies from danger while riding on your noble steed. Your enemies will regret ignoring you to attack innocent bystanders. 1 - Technique You gain a point in the Techniques/Martial Techniques/Pupper/Pupper tree this level and every two levels after. 1 - Innate Technique You gain Sanctuary as an innate technique, usable at will. As an instant action, you can use sanctuary when an innocent bystander would be directly targeted by an enemy by an offensive ability or attack. 1 - Noble Steed You gain the Pet feat. It also gains the Beast Trainer special training feature. Specifically the mount bonus. 1 - Special Attack: The Cavalry Arrives When you Core Rules, How to Play/Actions/Common Actions/Full Round Actions/Charge attack a creature, any ally or innocent noncombatant it threatens can immediately step away from that creature until they exit its reach. Their movement is elusive. You can use this special attack at will. 1 - Multiattack Make 2 attacks against a creature threatening an ally that isn't yourself or your noble steed. Make 3 attacks against a creature threatening an innocent noncombatant. You can also make this multiattack against a creature who targeted a creature with a harmful ability and you saved them with Heroic Dash or if you used Sanctuary on an innocent. 2 - Heroic Dash Recharge Elusive As an immediate action, you can move up to your speed, or your mounts speed if mounted, in response to an ally (other than you/mount) being subject to an attack or is caught in an AoE effect. You can move your ally as though taking the carry action as part of this movement. If this places the ally out of range of the attack or AoE of the effect, then they suffer no harm from it. This ability can be used once a minute. If you target an innocent non-combatant, it has no cooldown, and can be used while on cooldown from saving another creature. You can take the Carry action as a swift action. Note: Even if something does not directly harm an innocent bystander, doing something like torching a house, or doing some comically evil villain thing like activating a slow moving cage into lava could still qualify. 4 - My Hero You can take the Carry action as a free action. When you carry an innocent bystander, you immediately Dash. You must drop them off in a location they have Total Cover from all enemies. They gain a Divine Shield, are affected as per Sanctuary and do not become Clumsy. If they are defeated, you can use Lay on Hands as a free action on them.

entries.title: Inquisitor
entries.content: Inquisitors crush wills as hard as they crush skulls. By marking people for judgement, you can send them deep into the depths of despair. 1 - Skill Gain +Intimidation as a bonus skill with a +4 bonus. 1 - Judgement Su Recharge Mark an enemy within 30 feet for judgement as a swift action. Some type of glowing visual effect manifests on/around the creature while they are judged. All allies get a +2 Attack against this creature. The first time you make a Melee Attack against a marked enemy, you can attempt to Demoralize them as a free action On-hit. If successful, they are Frightened. When this enemy is defeated, this ability immediately recharges. If you are unable to defeat this creature within a minute, the mark of judgement disappears from your target and judgement recharges. 1 - Multiattack Make 2 attacks against a creature that is Shaken or Frightened. If your first attack inflicts one of these conditions, you can make another.
