entries.content: Masters of stealth, infiltration and execution. The rogue is "that guy" someone knows who's the man for the job. The rogue is the guy who knows people higher up in town as much as those in the underbelly of society. Out-skill and outsmart your foes with the power of planning, tactics and playing dirty. | +HP (mixed) | 20(+8 per level) | | ------------------------ | ---------------- | | Poise | 6 | | Skills | 8 | | Saves | Reflex | | Memory | 3+lvl | 1 - Techniques You have Martial Specialization You gain 1 technique point at level 1, then another point every 2 levels after. These technique points can be used on the scoundrel tree. 1 - Mobility Movement through occupied/threatened spaces is elusive unless you leave a creature's Threaten zone. At any point during your movement, you can move 10 feet back along the path you originally took. This movement doesn't count against your total movement and is always elusive. 1 - Serendipity Gain a +5 bonus to Serendipity checks. Whenever you roll on a random chart, you have advantage. If this roll is usually made in secret, you know both results and can choose between them regardless. 1 - Signature Skill Get the Signature Skill. Get an additional signature skill at level 6 and 10. 1 - Cunning Action Gain an additional standard action that cannot be used to attack, use Su abilities or cast spells. Attacks involve anything that directly negatively impacts an enemy, not just attack rolls. Specific class features allow you to do specific things with cunning action that you normally couldn't. 2 - Evasion Gain Evasion. 2 - Talented Rogue has a selection of special abilities unique to them. Gain one this level and at levels 4, 6, 8 and 10. 4 - Stroke of Luck (1+LUK) times per day, you can force a specific serendipitous event as a free action. Advantageous scenarios could be something like a cart of hay to catch your fall, or a bird smashing into a window to get a guard to investigate. Things with a DC 15 happen automatically. Anything higher requires a roll. 8 - Reliable Talent If you roll less than a 10 on a skill check, treat the final result as though you rolled a 10.

entries.title: Goober
entries.content: This archetype replaces all of the following class features from the base shifter class. Beast companion, nature stride and uncanny dodge. It also alters some class features as mentioned. 1 - Shapeshift Add the goober qualities to the list you can use this feature. 1 - Form of Goober Your base form permanently changes to that of a pool of semisolid ooze similar weight and volume to your original body. You count as your previous creature type(s) and ooze. While in ooze form, all of the items on your person float around in your jelly body. You cannot make attacks, cast spells, use items (including armor, weapons and shields) unless they are very simple like a doorknob or a button. As a semisolid, you can slide through and hide in thin crevices. Other than the qualities mentioned above, you otherwise function as normal. Moving at the same speed, being able to carry the same amount, being able to speak, having the same senses etc. As a swift action, you can abandon your amorphous qualities in order to assume the roundabout humanoid shape that you used to have, albeit with a much more gooey aesthetic. Whenever you shapeshift into other creatures, you maintain this gooey aesthetic. 1 - Wild Empathy Only functions against ooze creatures. 3 - Mitosis companion As a standard action, you can sacrifice half of your current and max hp to split into two copies of yourself. It functions exactly as you, expending your resources such as mana, limited uses per day items and abilities, consumable items etc. TLDR; This ability does not generate more resources. Your copy is a minion version of you (making it 1/3 your level). It is 1 size category smaller than you and uses the hp you sacrificed as its max hp. If you are ever defeated, the copy turns into an inert pile of goo. If the mini you perishes, you regain your max hp. 4 - Amorphous The number to crit you is AC+13 6 - Stretching Strikes Take no MA penalties this turn. Your reach increases to 30 ft for this attack. If you land this attack and another attack against a different creature without moving more than 30 ft away from the original target, you can pull these creatures towards each other, meeting at a midpoint between them. They take an 4d6 (14) crush damage. Both creatures are dazed for 1 round (fort negates daze).

entries.title: Spiderman
entries.content: Your access to spinnerets gives you incredible mobility. Alters shapeshift 1 - Shapeshift You get 1 less adaptation point. 1 - Clingy You get the climb and cling upgrades from the Polymorph List constantly. 1 - Spinneret You have a spinneret in each of your wrists. These highly specialized organs allow you to produce a silk that is incredibly strong, sticky and flexible. All class features surrounding webs involve using these spinnerets. All of them require at least one of your hands to be free, and some require both of them and are labeled as such. 1 - Special Attack: Web Shot Make a Thrown attack. On-hit the creature is entangled. They can roll to Breakout as a swift action, or tear their way out automatically.

entries.title: Polymorph List
entries.content: These points are often used through other features and are referred to as adaptation points. All creatures get access to the general list. Creatures like beast companions and all Shapeshifter archetypes also get the animal list. Everything beyond that is restricted to specific archetypes. General | Name | AP Cost | Description | | ----------- | ------- | -------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | | Burrow | 3 | Gain a Burrow speed of 10 | | Fly | 8 | Gain a fly speed equal to land speed | | Sprint | 1 | Spend a swift action to dash. If you do this again within a minute, you become fatigued. | | Climb/Swim | 1 | Gain a climb speed or a swim speed equal to your land speed. | | Speedy | 1+ | Increase land speed by 5 per AP spent. | | Mad Hops | 1 | Increase the distance the jump action brings you by 5 ft. You can jump vertically as high as you can jump horizontally. | | Venom | 2 | Choose a single natural attack. It applies envenomed on-hit. | | Charger | 1 | Gain the powerful charge quality. | | Constrict | 3 | You gain the constrict quality. | | Launch | 1 | You gain a Natural Attack that can only be used to make a Ranged Attack. It has d8 base damage and Short range. | | Reach | 2 | Increase your natural reach by 5 ft. | | Stampede | 2 | FRA move your speed+15, or double that in a straight line, moving through other creature's spaces. Any creature whose space you move through takes (natural attack dmg+LvL) and is knocked down.Creatures can choose to either AoO or make a reflex save to negate knockdown and halve damage. | | Manhandle | 1+ | Gain a bonus to +Combat Maneuver attacks equal to AP spent (max 4) | | Attack | 1 | Gain an additional option to make natural attacks with, such as a stinger that deals pierce damage. | | Big | 1 or 3 | Increase to large Size, or huge for 3 | | Smol | 0 | Decrease to small or tiny size | | Pest | 3 | Decrease to the size of a common fly. If you take +HP damage during this transformation, you revert back to your true form. | | Skill | 1+ | Gain a bonus to a skill equal to 1+AP spend (max 4 total bonus) chosen from the following skills. +Athletics, +Acrobatics, +Disguise, +Intimidation, +Insight, +Perception, +Survival,+Stealth | | Nightvision | 1 | Gain nightvision | | Prehensile | 1 | Gain a prehensile limb or your appendages (trunk/tail) become prehensile. | Animal | Name | AP Cost | Description | | ----------------- | ------- | ------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------ | | Glide | 1 | Gain the glide quality | | Cling | 1 | Your climb speed can be used on any solid surface, like roofs and slick walls | | Toboggan | 1 | Ignore slippery difficult terrain. Move at double speed by sliding on belly while on said terrain. | | Bonk/ Jolt/ Chill | 4 | Choose a natural attack. It causes the target to bestaggered for one round on-hit (fort negates). | | Bleed | 2 | Choose a natural attack. It gains the bleed weapon quality. | | Bother | 1 | Make noise to taunt an enemy within 30 feet (will negates). Lasts 1 minute, but the taunted creature gets a new save after each turn. | | Death Roll | 1 | As a grapple maintain option, you can inflict Knockdown onto a creature. They are Held Down until the grapple ends. | | Pointy | 1 | Creatures attacking you with non-reach melee attacks take (5+1/2 LV) piercing damage. This effect cannot happen more than once a turn. Damage resolved after attack completes. | | Stink Spray | 2 | As a standard action, make a ranged attack with Thrown range. It applies Sickened 2 on-hit. | | Tongue | 1 | You can make Drag attacks as melee attacks at range of 30 ft during your turn. | | Water Spray | 1 | You can make Topple or Shove attacks as ranged attacks at a range of 30 ft. | | Colorful | 1 | By concentrating, you can dance and present colorful patters to fascinate a creature within 30 ft of you (Will negates). Minions within 30 ft of you are also automatically fascinated. | | Blood Scent | 3 | The range of your scent increases to 100 feet against wounded creatures and you get +4 to+Perception and tracking against them. Gain Seeking against wounded creatures. | | Adapt | 1 | Choose a hostile environment to exist comfortably in (arctic, deep ocean, volcanic etc) | | Ant Haul | 1 | Increase Carry Weight by 100 | | Color Change | 1 | In any turn where you do not move, gain a +10 bonus to +Stealth. You can hide in plain sight, but not if you start hiding while observed. | | Echolocation | 3 | Gain +blindsense, Echolocation 30 ft. Gain weakness sonic 5 | | Grace | 1 | You cannot be knocked down or drained from falling. Fall damage is reduced to 1 for every 10 ft you fall. | | Turtle Up | 1 | The total defense action also gives DRA 4 | | Other | ? | Some animals are weird. If you’ve got an idea, work it out with your GM. | Plant | Name | AP Cost | Description | | -------------- | ------- | --------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | | Clover | 5 | Once per minute, Reroll a d20. | | Spores | 1 | As a standard action, erupt in spores. Choose either a 5 ft radius or a creature within 20 ft to fall Asleep (fort negates). Recharges after 1 round | | Roots | 1 | Get a +4 bonus to Poise and to saves against effects that move you from your position or knock you down. | | Photosynthesis | 1 | Gain FH 1 while in direct sunlight. | | Aroma | 1 | Smell nice. Every round a creature sniffs you, they remove one stack of sickened. You can sniff yourself. Sniffing you is a free action that a creature can do if you are within their natural reach. | Elemental | Earth Glide | 3 | Functions as Burrow but it works through stone and other similar tough earth. Your speed is 10. | | -------------------------------------- | --- | -------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | | Element Form poly, true | 1 | As a standard action, turn into a puddle, rock, fire or visible gas. If you are evaporated, drank,crushed, put out or inhaled, the transformation ends and you take 10 True Damage. | | Flow | 1 | Difficult terrain does not affect your movement speed. | | Let's Get Salty! | 3 | Taunt creatures On-hit with melee attacks for 1 round. | | Quake | 2 | As a Special Attack, you can Topple all creatures within 10 ft. You can use this special attack any number of times a combat. | | Wettest Water | 1 | Become a formless watery version of yourself that can slip through any space water can flow as per Liquefy. Fire you touch automatically goes out and you can attempt to dispel fire effects you touch (bonus lvl+5) | | Weatherman | 2 | You can see clearly through smoke, fog, heavy rain and other environmental conditions. | Goober | Stretch | 1 | Increase reach by 10 ft during your turn, or 30 ft if you do not make any attacks. | | ------- | --- | ------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------ | | Ditto | 4 | Assume the form of a specific creature you have seen and abandon your gooey aesthetic. Gives+2 +Disguise. Cannot be combined with skill ability. | | Anytool | 1 | Grow an additional limb in the shape of a specific tool, like hammer, crowbar, pitchfork etc. | | Osmosis | 1 | You gain Osmosis as an at-will Innate Technique. | | Snack | 1 | You can eat toxins and Venom. As a standard action, roll attack against the effect's DC. If successful, the effect is removed and you heal 5 hp. If you fail, it is not to your tastes and you cannot try to eat it again. | | Slimed | | Whenever you land a Natural Attack/unarmed strike against a creature, its speed is reduced to zero as it is stuck to you. You lose access to this natural attack until the creature is unstuck. The creature can automatically escape as a swift action.If you are forcefully moved away, this effect also ends. |

entries.title: Shapeshifter
entries.content: Unleash the power of the wilds. Shapeshifters are incredibly versatile, capable of adapting to any situation on a dime with their shapeshifting ability, though they typically focus on tearing their enemies to shreds with their claws. | +HP (martial) | 20(+10 per level) | | ------------------------ | ----------------- | | Poise | 8 | | Skills | 4 | | Saves | Fort | | Memory | 3+lvl | 1 - Techniques You have Martial Specialization You gain 1 technique points at level 1, then another point every 2 levels after. These technique points can be used on the Beastmode tree. 1 - Shapeshift Su poly, true or poly, chimeric As a swift action, spend 1 mana gain a series of ANY benefits from the polymorph list. Each of these qualities has a point value associated with them. You have (3+LvL) points to choose abilities with. These points are often used through other features and are referred to as adaptation points. The effects last an hour and are permanent @6. All benefits are replaced the next time you use it. While under the effects of a poly, baleful effect, you can use shapeshift to immediately end it and still benefit from shapeshift as normal. See subsection for all abilities. You have access to all the charts, unless it shares the name with an archetype. 1 - Shifter Claws You possess claws on your hands that you can sheathe and unsheathe as an instant action. This natural attack deals 2d8 slashing damage while your hands are empty, or d10 if they are not. Typically while shapeshifted (using the poly, true version) you can't wield weapons, shields etc so the damage is always 2d8. Any other natural attacks that you gain through this class shares this damage, but often with a different damage type. These "claws" persist through shapeshift, transforming into another form of attack if the creature doesn't have a similar pair of limbs to adapt to (rhino, squid). Your claws share the properties of a single weapon in your position, such as magic enchantment, a magic focuses ability to cast spells, or special attacks. You can bond to a new weapon with a 1 minute ritual requiring concentration and contact with the weapon. 1 - Multiattack Gain a Multiattack. Make two attacks, one of which must be with your shifter claws. 1 - Feat Gain the wild empathy feat. 1 - Druidic Aside from being a secret language, druidic can only be read by other creatures that know druidic. You can essentially write secret messages by touching your finger to any natural object (typically boulders and trees) and writing. Any message written in this way can be erased by a creature that knows druidic with ease. 1 - Deific Boons You start play with 10 GBP for the Earth Mother, unless your WIS is -1 or lower. 2 - Beast Speech (Su) You can speak with Beasts as though you shared a common language. They are able to much better articulate their feelings as though they possessed the intellect of a simple humanoid, but their instincts, desires and attitude toward you remain largely the same. 3 - Beast Companion You gain the Pet feat. 4 - Uncanny Dodge You cannot be DD unless a creature feints you or you are otherwise Helpless. 4 - Animal Instinct Mana Burn You can get an overall hunch of a creature or terrain by picking up on things others might miss, like sudden changes in the wind, the absence of animal sounds or subtle scents. When used against a creature, you learn whether the creature is interested in taking advantage of you, interested in helping you, or not particularly interested in you. You don't learn about their plans, only their relation to you. If the creature is a beast, you also learn if it is polymorphed. When you use it on a terrain, its mostly focused on your immediate vicinity or a place you plan to go, such as a cave mouth or the front gate of a town. You learn if the place is dangerous, a safe haven, or neutral. You don't learn why it's dangerous, you just get a bad feeling. If the terrain is natural, you automatically see through any illusions in your direct line of sight. That 'aint natural! Something is "natural" when it is on the mortal plan and it isn't made by sentient creatures. Things like towns and dungeons usually aren't natural. 6 - Wild Heart healing When you use shapeshift, you gain 5 temp hp. This increases to 10 temp hp if you use it to true polymorph into a creature. As a swift action, you can spend 1 mana to gain 5 temp hp. 11 - Fluid Form Your form is fluid and ever changing. You no longer have a true form, and everything you transform into is considered your new "true form." This makes it so creatures cannot forcefully end your polymorph effects with things like Dispel Magic or True Form. You no longer spend mana when using shapeshift as a swift action. You can spend 1 to shapeshift as a free action. You can still only shapeshift once a turn.

entries.title: Shouts
entries.content: Shouts have level requirements listed next to them. 1 - Unrelenting Force Sonic Your shout manifests as an incredibly loud shockwave that launches your enemies away from you. Deal 2d6 (+d6 @3 and each odd LvL after) sonic damage to creatures in a 30 ft line in front of you and launch them back 5 ft per d6 you roll (fort halves damage and converts knockback into 20 Poise damage). 1 - Elemental Breath Persistent Your shout manifests as a Breath Weapon of an elemental damage type. Choose between Fire, Frost, Zap and Poison. Deal 2d8 (+d8 @3 and each odd LvL after) damage of that type in a 60 ft line or a 30 ft cone (reflex halves). The ground beneath where you shouted is suffused with this element for 3 rounds. A creature that moves into or ends their turn in this area takes 10 damage. Creatures that were struck by the elemental breath do not take this persistent damage unless they end their turns in the area. 1 - Spirit World Become Incorporeal for 5 rounds. If you take any action other than movement, you lose your incorporeal status. 1 - Whirlwind Sprint Elusive Your shout manifests as a violent wind that carries you forward. Move 60+(5 per level) ft in a straight line. Creatures in this line or adjacent to it are set to 1 Poise. 1 - Dragonrend Automatically inflict the Grounded condition on creatures in a 100 ft line for 1 minute. If they are currently airborne, they plummet, provoking AoOs and taking Fall Damage. Dragons, as well as any dragon adjacent creatures, are also made Clumsy 2. 4 - Storm Caller Zap Mimic the effects of Call Lightning Storm, but it is not concentration. When you initially shout, and as a free action on each subsequent turn, you can call down a bolt of lightning. After you call down 2+(1/2 LvL) bolts of lightning, this effect ends. 4 - Bend Will Charm Companion Charm a creature within 100 ft that is a Minion to you for 24 hours. When you say the first syllable to this shout, any targetable creature is highlighted in your vision. If none are within range, then you stop the shout before it activates. You lose the action, but the shout does not recharge. While a creature is charmed in this way, they are willing to perform menial tasks for you, so long as they are not inherently dangerous (ie excavate this volcano). If you are in the heat of battle, they will fight by your side. 8 - Slow Time Functions as the Slow Time technique.

entries.title: Skyman
entries.content: The Skyman is a class defined by the signature ability to speak the tongue of dragons and manifest incredibly powerful effects from their words. There's also a healthy amount of jank. It is based on a Bethesda game after all. | +HP (mixed) | 20(+8 per level) | | ------------------------ | ---------------- | | Poise | 8 | | Skills | 6 | | Saves | Fort | | Memory | 3+lvl | 1 - Techniques Choose either Martial Specialization or Magical Specialization. Then choose a single technique tree within your specialization. Gain a Technique Point in that tree at this level and every odd level after. 1 - Shout Su Recharge You learn draconic and ancient draconic as a bonus languages. You can spend 1 mana as a swift action in order to shout. The effects of which vary based on the shout. Pick a shout from the Shouts page at this level, and at level 2, 4, 6, 8 and 10. You can shout once per minute and cannot do so while Silenced. Some shouts may have additional restrictions. 1 - Blessing of the Mountain Goat The skyman is adept at maneuvering vertical terrain. You can comfortably Balance on the smallest of ledges and walk on and up practically vertical walls. This is essentially like having a Climb speed equal to your Land Speed, but you need not use your hands. 1 - Blessing Your birth was accompanied by the prominence of a particular constellation. Choose a blessing. Blessing of the Lovers - Get a +2 bonus to all skill checks. Blessing of the Steed - Get +1,000 Carry Weight. You can take the Swap Item action as a free action once each turn, even if it is not the start of your turn. Blessing of the Mana Well - You get 1 MR. When this ability reduces damage, recover 1 mana. 1 - Talented Choose a talent from the talent list. Gain another at level 2, 4, 6, 8 and 10.

entries.title: Talents
entries.content: Abilities that are preceded by a number have a level requirement. Combat Talents Potion Chugger - You can drink any potions as a free action 1/turn. Staggering Shield Recharge - Your shield attacks stagger On-hit (fort negates). A creature can only be subject to this effect once a minute. Ker-plink - When you take the Shields Up action against a Ranged Weapon Attack, you (or the target you shield) negate its damage entirely. This only functions for 1 attack with shields up and doesn't work against siege weapons. Bowl 'em Over - You gain the Trample ability while you wield a shield, dealing 2d6+LvL crush damage. Paralyzed - As an instant action when you would take Damage or suffer a status effect, you can instead choose to stun yourself until the end of your next turn. Face Tank Healing - Whenever you provoke an AoO, gain 10 Temp HP. Just Your Imagination - Gain the Sniper feat. If you successfully snipe an enemy to ambush and the enemy fails their +Perception check, combat does not start. Buy One, Get One Free - Gain the Bonded Weapon feat. If you bind to a light or one-handed weapon, you get an exact copy of that weapon, including all materials, magic weapon properties, etc. One of them is always considered the original, and if it is taken from you for longer than 1 minute, the copy disappears. Gift of Giving - You can use Thievery to place a Toxin on an unaware creature's person somewhere (the skyman is very creative. Not even creatures without pockets are safe). At any point before the creature realizes it’s been planted and disposes of it, the Skyman may activate the poison as a free action, as though fulfilling whatever the usual delivery method is. (10) Bucket Su - You can Mount a common bucket, giving you a Fly speed equal to your land speed. If you are ever Poise broken, or otherwise separated from your bucket, you plummet. (4) Horse Technology (Su) Teleportation - Gain the Pet feat, and the creature must be a horse. With a whistle (free action), you can teleport this horse by your side. If this horse dies, it is banished to the horse dimension and you cannot summon it again until you rest. Your horse also shares your Blessing of the Mountain Goat feature. (4) Combat Caster Sp - Choose an elemental Damage tag. You permanently memorize level 1 spell with that elemental damage tag. Gain a Multiattack: Cast a level 1 spell with your chosen elemental damage tag and make a Melee Attack. Combat Medic Sp - You permanently memorize level 1 spell with the Healing tag. Gain a Multiattack: Cast a level 1 spell with the healing tag and make a Melee Attack. Martial Prowess - Gain a Multiattack: Make 2 attacks. (4) Housecarl Companion - Gain the assistance of a basic companion. They are a human fighter with no archetype, no alternate racial features and only combat feats. - Their Level is your level-3 - They have starting stats of STR +3, DEX +1, LUK +0, INT +0, WIS +0, CHA +0 If your housecarl dies, you must pay either their family or their employer 5,000g before you can acquire a new one. Utility Talents Courier - You have a loyal squad of three couriers that always appear at your beck and call. They can carry up to 10 pounds and will always reach their destination within one day. These couriers know the safest routes and generally refuse to go to particularly dangerous locations. If killed, get a letter from a replacement telling you about the last one’s death within 24 hours and all of your couriers take a 2 week vacation. Fast Travel (teleportation) (recharge) - 1/week you can teleport to any Teleportation Circles. This ritual costs 200g of a rare white chalk and 1 minute to perform. You can bring up to 8 other creatures, provided they are no larger than large size. Roaming Alchemist - You can find alchemy crafting materials in all manner of places. While travelling out and about in the world, you can generate materials as though using your Alchemy skill as a profession, but it is only half as effective. Master Enchanter - You gain an additional skill proficiency that must be spent on a crafting +Profession. You gain an additional underwear slot that can function as an additional item slot for any type of item, but it cannot be medium armor or heavy armor. Just Checking - You can freely check a creature's pockets, bags etc to see what they are carrying on their person without scrutiny. Stealing it still requires Thievery. I Belong Here - All creatures of your level or lower treat you as though you belong where you are. You can walk into people's homes uninvited, and for no reason. You can exist within a royal palace like you lived there. NPCs do the mental gymnastics required to justify you existing wherever you are. There are some upper limits when it comes to interacting with NPCs, like sleeping in their beds. You can do anything you like, so long as it does not break any major laws, like theft, assault etc. Even still, if nobody notices you do these things, who can say it was you? Ragdoll - You can appear dead as a standard action, rendering yourself Helpless, but still aware of your surroundings. Only a +Medicine check (DC 20+LvL) can determine that you’re faking it. When you “die,” you can set a condition (I hear the word bazinga) or a duration to awaken. Speech 100 - Gain the +Persuasion skill. You can use it to Gather Information in d4 minutes. You can automatically end a conversation with an NPC with no repercussions, as though the conversation had ended naturally. In addition, you can initiate conversations with any NPC, provided they aren't already in the middle of a conversation or in combat. No other situations are valid for denying conversation with you. For whatever reason, you seem as though you have the qualifications and social standing to interact with just about anyone. Creatures do not treat you well necessarily, but they will hear what you have to say. Once the conversation comes to a natural end, you cannot force your way into a conversation for another 24 hours. Crust Fast Travel? You can make it to any destination in the world in 3 days flat. The location you decided in cannot be a specific latitude and longitude coordinate and must therefore have some kind of name or significance, such as a settlement or a natural wonder. If the location of this place is widely unknown, and your group is not an exception, you cannot travel to it.

entries.title: Parry Fiend
entries.content: The Parry Fiend trades many of the abilities granted by the original slayer class to pivot the class from an unrelenting aggressor to a more defensive play style. Instead of killing everything that moves, a sentinel focuses more on disruption and setting up combos. The class primarily revolves around parrying enemy attacks. This archetype replaces features in the base class rather than adding them.Removes: Glory Kill, Dash, Shredder, Feats, Blood Punch 1 - Energy Su You no longer have glory kill, so that method of regaining energy is removed. Instead you regain 2 energy when you successfully parry an attack. 1 - Sawblade Shield You start play with and can craft a circular shield with spinning blades inside of it with 8 hours of work and 100g. This is treated as a tower shield and a 1h weapon that can deal slash or crush damage and has the CM (shove) property. If this shield is destroyed, it repairs itself after 1 round. In the hands of a Sentinel, the shield also additional properties. It has the thrown property and 2 enchantment slots, one of which is the Returning enchantment, but the returning can be used as an instant action. You can reasonably augment existing shields to also make them better suit your tastes, giving them the above properties. 1 - Omniparry Any offensive ability that deals damage to you, you can. Roll an attack roll as an immediate action. Against opposing attack rolls, if you match their roll, you negate it. Against anything else if you match the DC of the ability, you negate it entirely (at least for you in the case of AoE effects). When you successfully parry any ability, you rupture your attacker and any other enemies within 10 ft of them, making them clumsy 2. But how do I determine my attack bonus for parrying? You use what your melee attack bonus would be against the target. If you are currently flanking the target, add flanking. If your shield has the Bane enchantment against their creature type, add bane. 1 - Energy: Onslaught elusive As a free action, spend 2 energy to dash directly adjacent to a creature within 30 ft and crash into them, releasing a powerful shockwave. The target creature and all enemies within 10 ft are staggered (fort negates). Instead of a shockwave, you can instead choose to shove the target. 1 - Sawed Off When you make a thrown attack with your shield, it embeds itself into a creature on hit, entangling them. If at the end of the creatures turn it is still entangled by this, it takes your shield attack damage again. If you choose to use the returning property of your shield to parry, shield action etc, this entangle ends. When a creature is a minion, you know. You do not need to make attack rolls with your shield against minions. They are automatically crit by your shield attacks. If you defeat a minion with a shield attack, it can bounce to another minion within 20 ft. 1 - Feats You gain the Shield Focus, Sentinel and Point Blank Master feats. 2 - Shield Runes (Su) Inscribe a rune upon your shield to give it additional powers that activate when you successfully parry. You can only activate 1 rune when you parry. Choose one at this level and another at level 6 and 10, - Quaking Rune - All ruptured creatures are also staggered. - Vengeful Rune - Your next onslaught against a ruptured creature costs no energy. If you are within range, you can use onslaught as an instant action. - Celestial Rune - All ruptured creatures are smited by divine power, taking 2d6+lvl (7+lvl) holy damage. - Cursed Rune - All ruptured creatures gain weakness 2 to all damage for 1 minute. Ligma Rune - All ruptured creatures contract ligma.

entries.title: Slayer
entries.content: The Slayer revels in combat. He is hard to kill, mobile and has an arsenal unlike any other. He is powerful from a range, but his enemies shudder in fear when he is even close due to his ability to swiftly finish any fights he started with glorious displays of power and gore. With a handful of supernatural abilities, firepower and superhuman strength, the Slayer is a force to be reckoned with. | +HP (brute) | 20(+12 per level) | | ------------------------ | ----------------- | | Poise | 10 | | Skills | 2 | | Saves | Fort | | Memory | 1+(1/2 lvl) | 1 - Techniques You have Martial Specialization. You gain 0 technique points, not usable in any martial tree. You won't need them. 1 - Versatility You can ignore the 2h property of any weapon, but the base damage decreases to d8 1 - Superhuman * You add STR instead of DEX to attack/dmg for all weapons created by Energy (bigger str means bigger gun) * +10 Land Speed * Gain the +Athletics skill with a +4 bonus * +2 attack, increasing to +4 against fiends. * You can automatically jump as high and as far as you can move. * You are immune to fall damage. 1 - Feats You gain the unarmed combatant and Point Blank Master feats. 1 - Glory Kill healing overheal elusive You can sense weakness in your enemies. Whenever a creature you can see is at or below an hp total equal to (9+lvl), you know. When a creature within 30 feet is at this threshold, you can spend an immediate action to dash with incredible speeds to reach it (in a straight line, even if it is airborne, if you can't fly you fall) and make an unarmed strike against it. On-hit it instantly defeats the creature. If the creature isn't a Minion, you regain 1 mana and you heal (3+LvL) hp. If you so choose, though is completely unnecessary, you can brutally and spectacularly execute whatever creature you just targeted with this ability and they die...Horribly (fort negates). Killing a creature in such a way always results in some amazing feat of strength, agility and a disturbing creativity in the Slayer’s brutality. If this threshold happens to be equal to or larger than the health pool possessed by the creature, you can still use this ability even though they have taken no damage. 1 - Old Friend You can manifest a powerful double barrel shotgun with a creative attachment. Manifesting the gun is the same as the Swap Item action. Merely holding the gun does not require mana, but each shot requires 1 mana, completely ignoring the reloading process. Other abilities also cost 1 mana unless otherwise stated. The weapon stats are as follows… DMG - 2d8 P Properties - 2h Range - Functions like a blunderbuss Meat Hook - You can fire a grappling hook at a surface or creature within 30 ft to drag yourself to it as a standard action. If you target a creature, you must make a ranged attack. On-hit the target is set to 1 Poise. When you arrive adjacent to it, you can fire the shotgun or make an unarmed strike against the creature. Meat hook does not require energy. 1 - Shredder This ability functions as Old Friend, but instead of a shotgun you manifest an axe guitar with the following stats… DMG - 2d8 S Properties - 2h Shred - Gain proficiency in +Profession (guitar). Gain a +4 bonus when using the shredder. Riff of Rage - Give all allies within 60 ft +2 attack for 1 round (standard action) Special Attack: Finale - Declare an attack as a finale. If the attack ends the combat, future finale attacks gain +2 damage permanently. If it does not end the combat, lose 1 damage (minimum 0). A savvy foe can tell how many stacks you have. Note: Finale only stacks in actual combat, not training. You can never passively gain stacks. 1 - Boost (elusive) By spending 1 mana as a free action, move 10 feet, even directly into the air. Can be used multiple times to cover great distances. 2 - Blood Punch Spend 2 mana to augment an unarmed strike that staggers the creature On-hit (fort negates). On-hit, enemies within 10 feet of the original target automatically take the attacks damage. This ability can be used in tandem with techniques. 3 - Heavy Assault This ability functions as Old Friend, but instead of a shotgun you manifest a rifle with the following stats… DMG - 2d8 P Properties - 2h Range - Long Zoom - As a FRA, make a single shot with 300 ft range and +2 attack. 3 - Heavy Impact When you fall at least 20 ft, you release a shockwave in a 10 ft radius around you. If you fell at least 50 ft, the shockwave is a 20 ft radius. The effect this has depends on the target and their HP threshold. Against enemy minions, it sets them to your Glory Kill threshold. If they are already within the Glory Kill threshold, they are defeated. Against anything else (including allies), it deals 10 poise damage. 4 - Grenade Recharge As a free action during movement, but only once per turn, spend 3 mana to throw a supercharged ball of energy that explodes on impact on the ground, affecting all creatures within 10 ft of the space it lands in. You have 3 types of grenades - Fire - Set creatures ablaze (reflex negates). - Frag - Deal (3d6+LvL) crush damage and poise damage. If this breaks poise, the creature is Knocked Down (reflex halves damage). - Frost - Put creatures in stasis for 1 round (reflex reduces to frosty). 5 - Hammer This ability functions as Old Friend, but instead of a shotgun you manifest a hammer with the following stats… DMG - 2d8 C Properties - 2h Horny Jail - As a standard action, automatically bonk a creature within reach to make them not horny for 1 hour. Immediately dispels any Lewd effects. Hammer Down recharge - 1/combat, strike the ground as a standard action to deal your (d6+d6/odd level) crush dmg and make creatures clumsy 2 within 20 ft (fort halves dmg). Plunging Attack - If you start your turn above a creature and are not flying/pseudo-flying, you can make a plunging attack. Plunging attack adds 1 damage per 10 feet you fall (max +20 dmg). Plunging attack can also apply to Hammer Down. 6 - Striking Your weapon attacks, natural attacks and unarmed attacks gain additional damage equal to their damage dice. Gain this benefit again at level 10. 2h weapon @1=2d8, @6=4d8, @10=6d8 Light Weapon @1=d6, @6=2d6, @10=3d6 7 - Ballista This ability functions as Old Friend, but instead of a shotgun you manifest a hand held ballista with the following stats… DMG - 2d8 P Properties - 2h, +Siege Weapon Range - 120 ft (no second range increment) Special Attack: GET OVER HERE! - On-hit make a drag attack with a +4 bonus. If successful they are pulled adjacent to you, regardless of distance. Siege Mode (recharge) - 1/combat as a FRA charge and release a horizontal slashing energy projectile. Creatures in a 100ft line that is 15 ft wide take (5d8+lvl) slashing damage and start bleeding for 2d8 damage (reflex halves damage and negates bleed). You can reduce the charge time, making this only require a standard action, but the line is only 5 ft wide. 9 - Sword This ability functions as Old Friend, but instead of a shotgun you manifest a sword with the following stats… DMG - 2d8 S Properties - 2h, Special Attack: determination Melt - As a standard action, cut a hole in a surface of solid or lesser toughness large enough for you to fit through with ease. Special Attack: Sever - On-hit sever an limb from a creature, making them Drained 10 On-hit and severing a limb (fort negates severing limb). - Arm - Creature is unable to use arm - Leg - Creatures Land Speed is reduced by 15. Clumsy 1 permanently. - Wing - Creature is unable to fly. Other limbs can be selected, disabling natural attacks like tentacles and such. These are just the most common examples. 11 - Carnage You gain combat reflexes as a bonus feat. You can use glory kill an instant action by consuming an AoO (essentially 3 times a round unless you can AoO more somehow, or 4 if you spend an immediate action). You can only attempt to glory kill a particular creature once per round.

entries.title: Chronomancer
entries.content: Chronomancers manipulate time and space. 1 - Cutting Edge of Time You must select God Mode as your technique tree used with Cutting Edge. 1 - Alternate Timeline Recharge Whenever you roll a Skill Check, reality branches into two possible outcomes for you. While not in combat, you can rewind to the moment just before you rolled this check and Reroll to try and reach the alternate timeline. You can also do this if you simply say or do something that you wish to undo, such as triggering an alarm. You can only do this for rolls/events that happened within the last minute. If you drain 10, you can ignore the recharge tag. At level 11, this no longer has the recharge tag, but you can still only rewind a particular roll/event once. 1 - Skill Gain the +History skill with a +4 bonus. Arcane Discoveries (Chronomancer) 2 - Venerable Mage Your apparent age is always as though you were incredibly old, regardless of how old you actually are. Get rekt lmao 6 - Accelerate Whenever you affect an ally with a beneficial spell, like Cure Wounds or Astral Infusion, you can spend 3 mana as an instant action to also affect them with the Fast Forward spell. You can only affect 1 ally with beneficial AoE effects. 10 - Contingency Contingent Mana Burn You can ignore the usual restriction placed on effects with the contingent tag and have 2 active at once. They cannot have the same trigger (or triggers that are effectively the same like "Ragnar inhales" and "Ragnar exhales"). The timelines briefly converge, giving you access to another version of yourself that comes to your aid under a very specific condition. By spending 10 minutes scanning the multiverse, you can come up with a very specific set of instructions for yourself when the time is right. This essentially acts as a Prepare action that you set well in advance, but it can only be used to cast a Spell of up to level 3. While this spell is contingent, you are mana burned equal to its level. This contingency manifests as a unique timepiece that must exist in a visible position on the caster, such as on the wrist or around the neck. If it is not visible, then the contingency cannot take effect. 10 - Chronoshift recharge As an immediate action, you can spend 6 mana to cause a defeated ally within 60 ft to go back in time to just before they were defeated. You put them in stasis until the start of their next turn. They are then set to (CL) +HP and treated as though they had not been defeated. This can even be used on a creature that has been killed outright if it happened within the last round.

entries.title: Controller
entries.content: Crust Controller 1 - With 2 Hands! While Arcane Discoveries (Controller) Unless otherwise stated, arcane discoveries for 2 - T

entries.title: Destruction
entries.content: Destruction wizards look to win battles by unleashing enormous amounts of damage, usually to large groups of foes. 1 - With 2 Hands! While wielding a Staff Component in each hand, you increase your CL by an additional 1 for spells that deal damage. This increases to 2 for spells of your school specialization. Arcane Discoveries (destruction) Unless otherwise stated, arcane discoveries for destruction wizards only function on spells that deal damage. 2 - Artillery Mage You can spend mana to increase the range of a spell you cast. For each point of mana you spend, you can increase the spells range by 10. Spells with a range of reach gain (reach+10\*mana spent) range. If the spell is a melee attack, it turns into a ranged attack. Does not function for spells without a listed range. 2 - Precision Strike Whenever you cast a spell that has a listed AoE, your allies within that AoE are not targeted by the negative effects of the spell (including you!). Woe to the destruction wizard who believes he has outwitted Frogg Saran by evading the many aspects of his spells that backfire. You can still use the techniques, but casting a Frogg spell demands unforeseen consequences. If you do not play along, you will make him your enemy. 2 - Threatening Magic When you wield a staff component, you threaten, regardless of if it is a weapon. In place of simple attacks when a creature provokes an AoO, you can cast +Cantrips.

entries.title: Financial
entries.content: Financial wizards look to win battles far before they are ever fought using an enormous amount of preparation 1 - Silver Spoon You have a contact in the financial wizard's guild. This could be a teacher, banker or parent with connections or that works for it. You start play with an additional 1,000g worth of mundane items. Whenever you sell items, you automatically get 60% of the items value instead of 50%. 1 - Money is Power Su The more decorated you are in your wealth, the more powerful you become. As long as you are covered in jewelry, fine clothing and other fancy mundane items worth at least (1000*LvL), your CL is increased by 1. Whenever you take Damage, you can choose to destroy (1000*LvL) worth of items in you are wearing in order to negate it as an immediate action. Gold pieces themselves cannot be destroyed using this. Arcane Discoveries (Financial Wizardry) 2 - Arcane Synergy Whenever you would gain any kind of bonus to arcana checks outside of this class, such as from the aid action, or a bonus from the facilities at your disposal, you increase that bonus by +2 for each unique source. A unique source means something that is different from the previous ones. For example, your two financial wizard brothers would not be different sources. Those same two brothers and a dragon are 2 sources. 2 brothers, a dragon and a laboratory are 3 sources. Basically there needs to be a variety of sources of inspiration for arcane synergy to function. Each individual can still lend aid, giving a bonus depending on their proficiency. In that same example, your two brothers would collectively give a +6 bonus (2 proficient+2 proficient+2 synergy). The dragon, less versed in magic, lacks the arcana skill and gives a +3 bonus. The lab you are doing research in normally gives +2, but with synergy gives +4. So your bonus changes from +7 to +13 thanks to arcane synergy with this setup. 2 - Bling Get an additional magic item slot that can be used for baubles.

entries.title: Rainbow Mage
entries.content: Rainbow Mages are masters of all magic. They get access to far more magic at once than any other caster. 1 - Mage's Paradise You get an additional technique point each level, but it must be used on a tree you have no points in. Arcane Discoveries (Rainbow Magic) 2 - Combo If you case a spell from a tree you have not cast this combat, gain +1 CL. When you cast a spell that you have cast this combat, you lose this CL boost. The boost depletes before determining the results of the spell (ie I cast Fire Blast for a +1 CL boost, then next turn I cast it again. I do not get the CL boost for the second cast.) 2 - Spice of Life If you case a spell from a tree you have not cast this combat, reduce its mana cost by 1. 6 - Rainbow Magic Get access to Taste the Rainbow as an Innate Technique. When you hit level 10, get access to Tastier Rainbow and Rainbow Beam as 1/day innate techniques.

entries.title: Universal Arcane Discoveries
entries.content: Can be taken by any type of wizard. 2 - Elemental Mage Whenever you cast a spell with an elemental damage tag, like Fire or Frost, you can choose to gain Resistance to that damage type as an immediate action. Whenever you take damage from an effect with such a tag, you can choose to gain resistance to that damage type after the effect is resolved as an immediate action. Either effect only lasts 1 minute, and is replaced the next time you gain resistance to an element through this feature.

entries.title: Wizard
entries.content: If there are any mortals that can be considered masters of magic, it can be wizards. Each one a prodigy in their own right, pushing the boundaries of what is possible with mana. Wizards are on the cutting edge of new magical items, rituals and spells, creating new ones and enhancing old classics to fit their particular approach to magic. | +HP (specialist) | 20(+6 per level) | | ------------------------ | ---------------- | | Poise | 5 | | Skills | 2 | | Saves | Will | | Memory | 3+(2\*lvl) | 1 - Techniques You have Magical Specialization. You gain 2 technique points at level 1, then another 2 points every 2 levels after. These technique points can be used on any magical technique tree. 1 - Cutting Edge Choose a magical technique tree. This tree is the field of magic you specialize in. You get a +8 bonus to arcana when dealing with spells of this tree. You gain a technique point in this tree, and an additional technique point each odd level, all must be dedicated to this specific technique tree. Every time you gain a technique point in this tree, you can make some magic happen. You can choose one of 3 paths, depending on how much effort you are willing to put forth... 1. You can create your own completely original spell for this spell school. This can be a bit of a daunting task, requiring a degree of trust between game master and player. Once a spell is created, the balancing does not stop there, and should be adjusted as needed to not disrupt the game too much. 2. Alter an existing spell. Have you ever looked at a spell and wished it was just a little bit different? Perhaps you wanted a boneless Bone Shaker spell, or a snow woman instead of a Techniques/Magical Techniques/Cryomancy/level-1/Snow Man. These changes are encouraged to alter not only the flavor text, but also the mechanics. Perhaps our boneless-shaker allows you to create the boneless world you desire, tearing out a creatures skeleton leaving them a clumsy blob person. Perhaps a snow woman is more affectionate preferring to get up close and personal, grabbing creatures and keeping them frosty. In any case, the power of the altered spell should be pretty close to the original, and should serve a similar type of purpose. You could also choose to upgrade a lower level spell. Say you thought Fire Blast wasn't explosive enough. Make a 5th level version that explodes again at the end of your next turn, forcing creatures who failed the initial save to eat another one without some extra speed or aid from allies. 3. If you aren't confident in your ability, or just don't have the time. Gain +1 memory. 1 - Skill You are proficient in the Arcana skill with a +4 bonus. 1 - Neuroplasticity You can choose to leave up to 3 of your memory slots unused. By spending 1 minute, you can choose to prepare a spell in that unused memory slot. If you mana burn 1, you can fill an empty slot as a free action. 1 - Familiar Choose a level 0 fiend, celestial or fey, or create a beast that is automatically tiny using the Beast Trainer's Beast Companion feature. It's level is always 0. If it aids you in combat, it gains the companion tag. You can also use your familiar to do a handful of other useful things. Spying scrying - You can sense through your companion at will. Assistant - Your companion can use the Aid action to assist you for any skill checks. To My Side teleportation - You can concentrate for 3 rounds to teleport your companion to your side. 2 - Mana Wells Your mana pool is deeper than most. Add 1 to your mana pool at this level, and levels 4, 6, 8 and 10. 2 - Arcane Discovery The remainder of this class is full of arcane discoveries. These features are customizable abilities that you gain at level 2, 4, 6, 8, and 10. Each archetype comes with its own.

entries.title: Blood Hunter
entries.content: The first Blood Hunters were unique types of gunmen who, much like vampires, were obsessed with blood. Their research has led them down a divergent path where their unique affinity for manipulating life forces is well-suited for combat. If it bleeds, they can kill it. | +HP (martial) | 20(+10 per level) | | ------------------------ | ----------------- | | Poise | 8 | | Skills | 4 | | Saves | Reflex | | Memory | 3+lvl | 1 - Techniques You have Martial Specialization and Magical Specialization. You gain 2 technique points at level 1, then another point every 2 levels after. These technique points can be used on the marksman, Vampirism and scoundrel tree. 1 - Multiattack Make two attacks, one of which must be a Gun attack. 1 - Blood Price Su You can reload your gun as an instant action by taking 1 true damage. 1 - Vengeful (Su) healing overheal Whenever you take at least 3 damage, the next time you deal damage back to an enemy, you heal 3 hp. 1 - Exploit Weakness You can parry with a gun. Your parry attacks have a +2 bonus. In addition to parry's usual effects, when you successfully parry, you also deal the guns damage and the enemy's turn immediately ends. 1 - Life Force (Su) By spending a swift action, you can examine a creature, living or not, and tell how much +HP remaining it has. 1 - Skill You gain +Survival as a bonus skill, with a +4 bonus. 1 - Feat Gain Point Blank Master as a bonus feat. 2 - Last Stand recharge When you are Defeated, you can instead feign injury. To all others it looks like you have been defeated, but you do not gain the defeated condition until the end of your next turn. During your next turn you are knocked down, but you can make a single firearm attack. If you are healed before you make the attack, this ability is immediately recharged. 2 - Blood Tax (Su) When you land a gun attack, spend 1 mana to also inflict d6 bleed On-hit. You can instead take 1 true damage to apply this effect. 2 - Blood Hunt You get a +4 bonus on +Survival checks to track creatures your allies have injured within the last week. This bonus increases to +8 your allies inflicted bleed damage on them during the encounter. 4 - First Blood You have advantage on attack rolls until you deal damage in a combat.

entries.title: Hunter
entries.content: Crust Hunters are marksman trained to hunt down a specific type of creature with impunity. They specialize in using weapons that are otherwise inconvenient for most creatures to use as their main weapon, such as crossbows and firearms. | +HP (martial) | 20(+10 per level) | | ------------------------ | ----------------- | | Poise | 8 | | Skills | 2 | | Saves | Reflex | | Memory | 3+lvl | 1 - Techniques You have Martial Specialization. You gain 2 technique points at level 1, then another point every 2 levels after. These technique points can be used on the marksman and Techniques/Martial Techniques/Pupper/Pupper trees. 1 - Multiattack Gain a Multiattack. Make two attacks, one of which must be a ranged attack. 1 - Feats You gain rapid reload and lightning reload as bonus feats. 1 - Skill You gain +Survival as a bonus skill, with a +4 bonus. 1 - Special Attack: Hunter's Mark Make a carefully aimed ranged attack at a creature, granting it Seeking. On-hit, you mark them. You can only have 1 creature marked at a time. You get a +4 bonus to survival checks against a marked creature. Choose one additional effect your hunters mark has while the mark persists. Reveal - Creature glows slightly, giving it a visible colorful aura. Creature takes a -10 penalty to +Stealth and and all attacks against it have Seeking. Taunt - The creature you strike is taunted by you until it damages you, upon which the mark is removed. As an immediate action when a creature makes an attack against a marked creature, you can transform that attack into a crit. Using this function of Hunter's Mark causes the ability to be unusable until you rest. Otherwise this special attack can be used at will.

entries.title: Light Guardian
entries.content: The Light Guardians are an organization of people that make sure mages don't tear the world asunder. They also do their best to keep the planes separate from each other, in particular the demon realm. Their incredibly powerful signature ability, light binding, suppresses magic in all of its forms, leveling the playing field. | +HP (martial) | 20(+10 per level) | | ------------------------ | ----------------- | | Poise | 8 | | Skills | 2 | | Saves | Will | | Memory | 3+lvl | 1 - Techniques You have Martial Specialization You gain 2 technique points at level 1, then another point every 2 levels after. These technique points can be used on the Arc and Protector tree. 1 - Special Attack: Light Binding Su Holy Make a weapon attack, Natural Attack or unarmed attack and deal your LvL in additional holy damage. If this attack is melee, gain +2 attack. The enemy is Siphoned On-hit until the end of your next turn (will negates). You can use this special attack at will. Light binding can also be used to permanently close portals to other Planes automatically. Particularly large and powerful rituals may involve a longer process than just a singular attack. Light binding functions as Dispelling Strike against magical effects, but it deals holy damage. 1 -Bouncing Light (Su) Whenever you use light binding, your attack bounces using ghostly shining afterimages, affecting another target using the same attack roll. It bounces to an additional target at level 6 and 10. Functions even if the attack is melee. Each additional target needs to be within 20 ft of the last. Additional targets can be ongoing magical effects as well as creatures. 1 - Spell Resistance (Su) As an immediate action, shield an ally you can see (including yourself), giving them resistance to damage against a spell or supernatural (Su) effect. You intuitively know what this ability can and can't apply to. 1 - Skill Gain Arcana and +Planes as bonus skills with a +4 bonus 2 - Channel Energy (Su) healing overheal recharge You can unleash a pulse of energy at your maximum power, healing all allies within 60ft for (5+2*lvl) hp. 4 - Hell and Back (recharge) You can innately cast the spell Hell and Back. 8 - Banishment (Su) When Fiends fail their save against Light Binding and you are not in The Demon Realm, you choose can send it to the demon realm as an instant action.

entries.title: Maligner
entries.content: Maligners are master manipulators that worm their way into positions of power through deceit and cunning. They use enchantments to force cooperation, illusions to misdirect, lies to spread rumors, and polymorphs to imitate. While there are Maligners that start off benevolent, they most often find themselves engaging in morally abhorrent behavior while impersonating enemies just to keep their stories straight. Note: This class is a pretty odd fit for this game, mostly because it has basically zero combat related abilities as part of the class. The role a Maligner will play in a party will probably be akin to that of a rogue. They are much more impressive out of combat than in combat. | +HP (specialist) | 20(+6 per level) | | ------------------------ | ---------------- | | Poise | 5 | | Skills | 4 | | Saves | Will | | Memory | 2+(2\*lvl) | 1 - Techniques You gain 3 technique points at level 1, then another 2 points every 2 levels after. These technique points can be used on the Shira, Moogen and one other tree, chosen at character creation. 1 - Faceless Monster Su poly, true companion As a standard action, you can assume an alternate persona of your creation. You fabricate a backstory, connections, race, appearance, clothes, voice, the works - this alternate form is completely unique. When you have an alternate persona, you have no "true" form. Your alternate persona has all of your abilities and statistics, but you can decide to swap up to 2 of your skills for any others, and your race may be different. If these changes cause you to no longer qualify for a feat or class feature upon assuming the other form, then you lose access as normal. It takes a week of work to fabricate a new form, and a week to "kill off" an old form. These personas consume your Companion slots, and you may have more than one if you have enough slots. You start off with one alternate persona. 1 - Skill Gain +Deception and +Disguise as bonus skills, each with a +4 bonus. 1 - Catechize Language-Dependent Upon engaging in conversation with a creature for at least one minute, you can call out one detail of its appearance, recent activities (within a day), or mannerisms as a free action. You instantly learn its mental state and intentions as they relate to the detail, within the scope of the totality of information you have on the creature (will negates). Essentially, you correctly guess why a creature did or is doing something. Part of this ability is speaking your guess to the creature, and judging your accuracy off of its response; if it passes its save, you guess wrong, may look silly, and can no longer use this ability on the same creature until you rest. You may substitute the DC with Insight, and the target may substitute its Will save with Deception. 1 - Deific Boons You start play with 10 GBP for Shira, the She-Devil and Moogen, The Murderer. Unless your WIS is -1 or lower. 2 - Rumor Trails (Su) concentration As a free action, name a rumor. You gain a sixth sense for who has heard or spread this rumor. If you see the people they told or heard it from, they appear to glow white or red to you, respectively. If you interact with the creature, you can see a literal trail of ethereal energy to the people they either heard the rumor from, or who they told the rumor. Using this ability again negates the previous use. 4 - Face Stealer (Su) (poly, true) You can amputate a helpless, willing, or recently deceased (within an hour) race's face, and spend 500g and 8 hours of labor to convert it into a Maligner's Mask attuned to that specific creature. Most creatures will die or go blind upon having their face amputated without immediate medical attention. When you wear this mask, you partially transform into that creature, which counts as a disguise and grants a +4 bonus skill checks to appear as that specific creature. Putting it on is a full round action, but removing it is a Swapping Gear action. It does not change your clothes or equipment. Other creatures may wear these masks. While you possess a mask, you can use Faceless Monster to create a persona of a person whose mask you possess. If the persona you steal still lives, or is absent for long periods of time, it can cause problems. 6 - Rumormonger You can attempt to spread a rumor throughout a small town or larger settlement by attempting a deception check. The difficulty depends on the size, ranging from 10 for a small town to 30 for a metropolis, though this difficulty may be modified if the population as a whole tends towards skepticism or insanity. It takes a week for a rumor to spread. If you succeed by 5 or more, the time is reduced to 3 days. If you fail by 5 or more, the rumor mutates into a competing theory. Each of your personas may only have one rumor brewing at a time. 8 - All Friends Here (Su) After a minute of concentration, you can enter a state of calm where you deepen your connection with your collection of masks. You can communicate with the fragments of the persons soul left behind within. The masks appear to speak to you, and you can inquire things about these people as though you were a neutral party. As such, they are usually willing to tell you some things, but hesitant for others, usually requiring skill checks. Once you try and pry into something secret and fail, the information is lost forever.  The masks are unable to form new memories, and therefore always treat you as they would a stranger, no matter what you ask.

entries.title: Mana Addict
entries.content: Crust A mana addict is an antimage who is well and truly addicted to the power of mana flowing through them. They get various abilities that function differently depending on how much mana they have. | +HP (mixed) | 20(+8 per level) | | ------------------------ | ---------------- | | Poise | 6 | | Skills | 4 | | Saves | Will | | Memory | 3+lvl | 1 - Techniques You have Martial Specialization You gain 2 technique points at level 1, then another 2 points every 2 levels after. These technique points can be used on the Arc and Pupper trees. 1 - Mana Wells You have a mana pool vaster than even that of most practiced mages. You gain an additional point of mana this level and every even level after. All your points of mana reserves are instead converted into maximum mana, unless they are tied to specific items. 1 - Mana Barrier healing force You can manifest a shimmering barrier of volatile energy that protects you and snaps at those who disturb it. As a standard action, you can grant yourself temp hp equal to your current mana. Keep note of this value. Whenever a creature deals +HP damage to you, they also take this value as force damage, regardless of how much of the temp hp you actually have left. Mana barrier lasts indefinitely. Anything that decreases the temp hp you gain when you initially use this ability, like being Bone Chilled, also reduces the counterattack damage. 1 - Manamateurs Sp Upcast Whenever you take +HP damage, you can spend mana as an immediate action to reduce the damage. You can spend 1 mana to reduce the damage of an effect on you by 3. If you decrease the damage of an effect to zero, it still activates the force damage counterattack from Mana Barrier. At level 3 and every odd level after, you can increase the mana you spend by 1. For each additional mana spent, reduce the damage you take from an effect by 3. 1 - Innate Casting You can innately cast the Mana Ramping cantrip. 1 - Taste the Mana Whenever you inflict mana burn, even when inflicting it on yourself, you restore 1 mana. You can also use the mana burn you inflict on another creature to cure your own at a 1 to 1 ratio. As a standard action, you can cause an adjacent helpless creature to mana burn 2, but you are drained 3 for every mana burn you cure. 1 - Skill Gain Arcana as a bonus skill with a +4 bonus. 2 - Babble You can spend 1 mana to speak the magic words of similar spells while another creature casts a spell to confuse them while they cast (will negates). This functions as the Tongue Twister technique with a few key differences: You can only use this as an immediate action to a creature casting a spell. If the creature fails its save, it only suffers the effects of tongue twister for the spell it is casting. If it has already spent any actions that tongue twister would normally consume, they are instead staggered until the end of their next turn. 2 - Mania While at or above half mana, you have a +10 bonus to your Land Speed, Advantage on +Perception and Immunity to fatigue. 2 - Depression (mana burn) While below half mana, but not out of mana, you gain the following special attack usable an unlimited number of times per combat. Special Attack: Mana Leech - On-hit, steal (½ lvl) mana from the target and they mana burn an equal amount. When you are completely out of mana, you become fatigued, but you gain resistance to damage that originates from an effect than can be dispelled (ie Su/Sp effects). You recover 1 mana at the start of your turn. 4 - Backlash You gain Psionic Backlash as an at-will Innate Technique. When you use it, the creature is mana burned 2 (will halves). 4 -

entries.title: Pack Mule
entries.content: Crust Pack mule is designed to give you the authentic DnD martial experience where you experience just how much of a "team game" it is by being a useless blob of defensive stats. You are almost completely reliant on your team to defeat your enemies. You scale incredibly poorly offensively, but you scale incredibly well defensively. Note: This class was intentionally designed obtusely in order to try and recreate the feeling of playing an unoptimized martial character in 5e/PF1e. Nick Nacks was intentionally designed from the ground up to make this kind of thing hard to happen by accident. So many techniques have baseline effectiveness that I had to use very heavy-handed methods to achieve the desired result. You will need to find out creative ways to be useful outside of what you're used to. Good Luck! ![pathfinder bingo.png|A silly meme related to why I made this class.[ I even made a video about this.|600]] | +HP | 20(+20 per level) | | ---------- | ------------------ | | Poise | 20 | | Skills | 4 | | +Saving Throw | Fort, Refl, Will | | Memory | 4+(2*LvL) | 1 - Techniques You have Martial Specialization You gain a technique point at level 1, and another every 2 levels after. These points can be used on the Protector tree. 1 - Weenie This class fundamentally alters how many aspects of your character scales offensively. * Your DC is 7+LvL instead of 12+LvL (even when using Alchemical Items made by others) * Whenever you deal +HP or Poise damage, that value is cut in half. This uniquely also affects True Damage. These effects are also applied to companions. 1 - Battered and Bruised This class also fundamentally changes how many aspects of your character scales defensively. - You have Regeneration equal to your LvL. When you are defeated, this is disabled for 10 minutes, or until you lose the condition. - You gain Resistance to being Drained (cutting any drain inflicted upon you in half) - You get a +10 bonus to luck checks to determine the type of affliction you acquire when you are defeated. - You have Recovery 1 - Maximum Lift - Your Carry Weight is multiplied by itself. Calculate it as normal and then do (carry weight)^2 - You get 10 additional inventory slots. - +Athletics proficiency with a +4 bonus. - +4 bonus to all STR-based ability checks 1 - Team Game - You can take the Carry action as a swift action. When you carry a defeated creature, they are protected from all harmful effects. - You can take the Aid action as a swift action. - You gain a teamwork feat. 2 - Talents Gain one at this level and levels 4, 6, 8 and 10. Numbers next to abilities are level requirements. 2 - Secret Compartment You get +Sleight of Hand as a bonus skill with a +4 bonus. You can choose a single item no larger that 1 inventory slot to get an additional +8 bonus when you roll sleight of hand to conceal item. It requires a standard action to retrieve something from your Secret Compartment. 2 - Extra Pockets - +1,000 carry weight - +10 inventory slots - +2 to STR-based ability checks You can select this talent multiple times. 2 - Planning Ahead Gain the Brilliant Planner feat. The amount of gold you can invest in it is (1,000*LvL) 2 - Action Surge recharge As a free action, gain a standard action. Usable 1/minute. 2 - Versatility Gain a +Multiclass feat and the Signature Skill feat. 2 - Esoteric Knowledge (recharge) Once per game session you can roll any Skill Check. It ignores any bonuses you posses, such as from Ability Scores, racial/class bonuses, or skill proficiencies. You instead roll with a bonus of (2xLvL). Other bonuses such as Invoking Deities, a Bard's inspiration or LUK can still be added after the fact. 2 - Magic Initiate You gain Magical Specialization.

entries.title: Virtuoso
entries.content: Death should never be quick. It should be an opera. The virtuoso is a creature of cadence and cruelty. All of their abilities are as flashy as they are deadly. They primarily focus on dealing damage from range using a special type of firearm. Anybody familiar with a virtuoso fears the power behind the fourth shot of this gun. | +HP (mixed) | 20(+8 per level) | | ------------------------ | ---------------- | | Poise | 6 | | Skills | 4 | | Saves | Reflex | | Memory | 2+(2*LvL) | 1 - Techniques You have Martial Specialization and Magical Specialization. You gain 2 technique points at level 1, then another 2 point every 2 levels after. These technique points can be used on the scoundrel, Moogen and marksman trees. 1 - Cadence Su You can transform a single standard pistol into a special weapon just by holding it in your hands. * You need to reload it after making 4 shots instead of 1 * You can only fire once per round, making you incompatible with some multi-shot abilities (such as Snap Shot) * You get +2 to attack when you attack using this gun. * Bullets fired out of combat do not consume ammo. * The 4th shot gets +10 to attack and deals 2x damage on a crit instead of 1.5x. * Reloading always requires a standard action, even it feats, techniques and such say otherwise. 1 - Climax When you Crit, gain +2 AC, +20 Land Speed and Elusive movement until the start of your next turn. 1 - Flowering Trap (Su) As a swift action, place a trap in an unoccupied space that quickly camouflages with the surrounding terrain and blends into the terrain (DC 20+LvL +Perception to notice). If an enemy steps on a trapped space, it blossoms. Blossomed traps create an area of Bright Light and Difficult Terrain in a 5 ft radius around its space until the end of the triggering creatures turn. At the end of the turn of the triggering creature, it explodes, dealing (2d6+d6/odd lvl) pierce damage to the area made into difficult terrain. You can have 2 traps out at once at any given time. If you place another while 2 are out, one of your choosing disappears. You are mentally alerted when a trap is triggered, and which one. You gain +4 perception against any creature that has triggered a trap since you last rested. Whenever you defeat a creature, you can choose to trigger a flowering trap beneath them that explodes at the start of your next turn. This trap doesn't count against your total. 1 - Spotlight (Su) light By spending 1 mana as swift action, you can target a creature within 60 ft to have a spotlight focus on for 1 minute. The light around the spotlight artificially dims for dramatic effect (but no mechanical effect). That creature takes a -10 penalty on +Stealth and is persistently in an area of Bright Light. The creature also has Advantage on +Profession checks to "put on a performance". While a spotlight is active, you can increase the volume of your voice to echo over even large crowds. 1 - Behind the Mask (Su) You have an alternate ego that forms while masked, with its own name. Magical means to detect things treat you as two different creatures (such as Caught Red-Handed). If a creature takes off your mask, you can spend 1 mana as an instant action to cause the mask they remove to be a temporary copy that lasts 1 minute, revealing another mask underneath. +2 to +Disguise while masked. While your face is obscured, such as with a mask or in darkness, you can make your voice sound like anyone you have heard. 1 - Finale You gain a unique special attack. Finale - Deal an additional 1 damage with a pistol attack. If this ends the combat, permanently increase the damage of finale by 1. If it does not end the combat, decrease the damage of finale by 1 (minimum 1). You cannot stack finale in mock combats, training, or passively during down time hunting wolves etc. 1 - Deific Boons You gain 10 GBP for Moogen, The Murderer, unless your WIS is -1 or lower. 2 - Deadly Flourish (Su) By spending 2 mana as swift action, you can target a 60 ft line. The very first enemy within this line is entangled for 1 round (reflex negates). If a flowering trap you can see is triggered, you can activate this as an immediate action against a creature within the radius of the blossoming trap. 2 - The Crowd Goes Wild! Sp You can innately cast the Ghost Sound cantrip as a free action, but only to make the sounds typical of an audience, such as clapping, laughter, gasping etc. You can cast this as an instant action when you successfully end a combat with finale. 2 - Armor Penetration Your pistol gets a variety of new benefits. - Shots deal True Damage. - They ignore Cover, and Improved Cover, but not Total Cover. - They cannot miss and always Graze, even if they are deflected via something like parry or Block. 3 - Perfect Execution When you kill a creature with your pistol, you can choreograph how their blood splatters. You can cause it to form a pool of a specific shape, with enough precision to write in text, but usually no more than 2 sentences. You can also cause it to make patterns on the deceased's skin. 4 - Curtains Close (Su) When you defeat a creature, you can spend 2 mana as an instant action to cause a pair of curtains to appear and close in between you and your victim directly adjacent to you. These curtains are 20 ft tall and 20 ft wide. It lasts for 1 minute, or until they are opened. You can open the curtains as a free action from any distance, and an enemy can open the curtains as a standard action if they are adjacent. It is not difficult for any creature to simply move past the curtains by going through or under without opening them. 5 - Eulogy (Su) When you kill someone, you can write a eulogy for them, provided you have the means to physically write one. You can drop this eulogy off on the corpse, the creature's previous home or simply a nearby settlement. This eulogy naturally multiplies, finding itself in newspaper stands, cafes, waiting rooms etc. The less important the creature, the less it spreads. This results in the eulogy being pretty common knowledge for well known people, and the talk of the town for beloved or reviled creatures. You can choose to write or draw anything on this eulogy, but cannot attach any kind of magical spells/traps to it. It must be concise enough to fit on a single standard page. 6 - Red Carpet (Su) By spending 1 mana as a free action, you can roll out the red carpet for 1 minute. This carpet is 10 ft wide and 60 ft long. It must be placed on a mostly flat, though not necessarily solid, surface. It can also be placed on any surface where carpet is commonly found (such as a staircase). It can be placed on the surface of still(ish) water. It can be used as a bridge to cross a chasm, provided the gap is mostly level. While walking on this carpet, creatures ignore difficult terrain. If you make another carpet, the previous one disappears. 7 - Theater of the Mind (Su) Mind-Affecting You can extend your hand towards a creature and offer to show them the world (or something to that effect). If they willingly take your hand, they are automatically Fascinated for up to 5 minutes. During this time you completely obscure their senses from the outside world and take them on a magical journey of your imagining with catchy songs and pretty visuals straight out of a Disney movie. To the outside world it merely looks like you are holding hands with the creature. You can even decide to move the creature around as though you were going for a walk, or dancing at a party.
