Creatures with blindsense (scent) take a -2 penalty to their save. Regardless if the creature saves, survival attempts using scent get a +2 bonus until the creature washes up.
entries.path: Equipment and Economics/Equipment Types/Alchemical Items/Thrown Concoctions/Stink Oil.md

entries.title: Divorce Potion
entries.content: 2,600g (DC 30) It's so easy to fall in love, but much harder to climb out of it. The divorce potion negates the effects of the love potion. It uses many of the same ingredients but requires organic matter harvested from the creature who the creature is in love with. The effects of the potion are instant. The creature is fully aware of everything they did while under the effects of the love potion, but views it as they would have from the day just before they drank it.  Divorce potions are most commonly administered to fix accidental love potion consumption. Due to the higher DC, they are much less common.
entries.path: Equipment and Economics/Equipment Types/Alchemical Items/Toxins/Love Potion/Divorce Potion.md

entries.title: Love Potion
entries.content: 800g (DC 20) By extracting the juices of a dead love bug, the complex process of creating a love potion can begin. Potion is what everybody calls it, but its function is more in line with a toxin. Care must be exercised when using love potions, for love is not an exact science. When a creature consumes a love potion, they become charmed and deeply romantically interested in the first person they see for 8 hours. At the end of this duration, the creature loses the charmed condition. It then makes a will save (DC 20 negates) to resist the effects of the potion or the romantic feelings become permanent. In the event where the creature first seen is not romantically compatible, it instead forms a close platonic bond.
entries.path: Equipment and Economics/Equipment Types/Alchemical Items/Toxins/Love Potion/Love Potion.md

entries.title: +Toxins
entries.content: Toxins are harmful substances that usually require getting into the body of your target, either through injury or ingestion. Most of them inflict some type of debilitating status. An injury toxin can be applied to your weapon as a standard action, and typically evaporated within a few minutes. Applies its effect on-hit.

entries.title: Styx Serum
entries.content: 2,400g (DC 27) Using a sample of water from the river Styx and some brain matter, you can create a concoction that can wipe a creature's memories of the recent past. It looks a bit foggier than water and tastes like stagnant water. The Styx serum is incredibly powerful when ingested. It wipes out the memories of the last 24 hours, including drinking the serum. Particularly important events, such as a first kiss, a loved one dying or an unexpected betrayal cannot be erased completely from the spirit by the Styx serum. Such events are instead interpreted as gut feelings, fated connections, love at first sight or deja vu,
entries.path: Equipment and Economics/Equipment Types/Alchemical Items/Toxins/Styx Serum.md

entries.title: Alchemical Items
entries.content: ⚗️ Alchemical Items Come in several different categories based on their typical use case. The format includes the items name, the DC to craft it and its cost to purchase. I put alchemical items into different price brackets. One for relatively minor and commonplace items another for powerful items, and the last for things requiring incredibly rare reagents.  Item DC refers to craft DC, which determines the price. The save DC is typically a base amount (or the class DC of the Alchemist class). If you want to purchase items with a higher DC, adjust the price accordingly. Cheap - DC^2-80. Then rounded up to nearest 10. Common - 100+(DC+5)^2. Then rounded up to nearest 100 Expensive - 1000+(DC+10)^2. Then round up to nearest 100
entries.path: Equipment and Economics/Equipment Types/Alchemical Items/Alchemical Items.md

entries.title: +Backgrounds
entries.content: Background helps to determine what your character was doing before they got sucked up into an adventure, other than acquiring the skills necessary to go out into the dangerous world of course. Backgrounds typically give assets with a specific focus.
entries.path: Equipment and Economics/Equipment Types/Approximating Golds Value/Background/+Backgrounds.md

entries.title: Businessman
entries.content: You are one of owners of a large business, or the primary owner of a small business. Choose a specific profession, craft or performance skill that you are proficient in, or that could be reasonably related. You have access to a location that you can perform all of the necessary tasks of your trade. You make 10% more money when you work here. You have to return to this business for at least twice each month for upkeep. If you have a method of long distance communication good enough to have lengthy back and forths, such as a good postal system or magic, you can also upkeep as long as you send such messages 1/week. If you don't you are replaced or the business begins to decline.
entries.path: Equipment and Economics/Equipment Types/Approximating Golds Value/Background/Businessman.md

entries.title: Family Man
entries.content: You are the co-owner of a house somewhere in the world, usually at least on the tangent of civilization. You lived with either your immediate family, like parents and siblings, or you made a family of your own with a spouse and children. You own a meager house in a specific place, and a handful of family members to come home to after an adventure. Your house is always considered a suitable place to rest in order to cure general afflictions. Your family generally understands you won't always be back home for dinner, but going more than a month without contact will usually cause them unease, and may eventually cause them to move.
entries.path: Equipment and Economics/Equipment Types/Approximating Golds Value/Background/Family Man.md

entries.title: Noble
entries.content: You come from an upper ring of society. Your name might not carry the same weight everywhere you go, but your gold pouch certainly does. You get a bonus 1,000 starting gold that you can spend as you wish. Your family also gives you an allowance every month of 500g. For campaigns with a faster or slower pace, such as if there are frequent time skips, consider changing how often the allowance comes in. If you are far from home, it can take up to a week for your allowance to arrive. They can only ever be picked up from settlements.
entries.path: Equipment and Economics/Equipment Types/Approximating Golds Value/Background/Noble.md

entries.title: Tradesman
entries.content: You worked in a job that required a specialized form of training, such as an alchemist, a doctor or a blacksmith. Over the years of your work you have accumulated a few tools of the trade.  Select one of the following presets, or make one that's similar.  Doctor - You start off with a medicine kit, a dose of troll oil and 3 doses of bloodblock. Alchemist - Start off with a masterwork alchemist kit. You start off with a Love Potion and an Alchemist Fire. Craftsman (weapons/armor) - Start off with a set of combat ready equipment. You can start off with... -Heavy armor and a weapon -Medium armor, 3 melee weapons, a ranged weapon and a shield -Light armor, 6 light melee weapons, 2 ranged weapons and a shield You also have masterwork tools for crafting weapons/armor.
entries.path: Equipment and Economics/Equipment Types/Approximating Golds Value/Background/Tradesman.md

entries.title: Wanderer
entries.content: You go wherever the road takes you. You might have had a home once, but for one reason or another you had to or wanted to leave. You start play with a standard rock. Very basically, rocks are autonomous, mass produced transportation and shipping vehicles. Most significant places in civilization will have service stations and parking available.
entries.path: Equipment and Economics/Equipment Types/Approximating Golds Value/Background/Wanderer.md

entries.title: Economics
entries.content: This section is dedicated to determining income, typically through the use of a skill, like profession, perform or alchemy. All income is based around a formula which calculates a yearly salary (y) based on your skill bonus (x). y=5000+500(10+x^2) Salary assumes you you work for 5 days a week, for 50 weeks a year. Divide this value by 250 to figure our your daily income. Most jobs tend to have weekly payouts. The chart below does this formula for you for most expected values. Anything lower than a +0 bonus to the task at hand makes a 5k salary, 20g daily and 100g a week. Skill Bonus (x) If you don't mind sacrificing convenience for realism, the following rules also exist (since multiplying the value every time you buy/sell can get tedious). The above formula assumes a prosperous nation with a lot of economic opportunity and a population with thousands of people per city. In poorer and less populous regions, consider using the following multiplier for all goods and services Developing - Multiply all values by .75 Poor - Multiply all values by .5 It also assumes the work provides value. If you are a butcher in a society of vegans, you will struggle to find work. Apply a multiplier in such circumstances. Service/Goods is Almost Useless - Multiply all values by .1 High Supply/Low Demand - Multiply all values by .5 Low Supply/High Demand, Novel Service - Multiply all values by 1.2
entries.path: Equipment and Economics/Equipment Types/Approximating Golds Value/Economics/Economics.md

entries.title: Mana Donation
entries.content: By going to specialized facilities, or by going to churches with gilded altars, a creature can donate mana. When you donate, you mana burn an equal amount. A creature can safely donate an amount of mana equal to their (max-1). There are usually safeguards against donating your max mana, but less savory individuals don't mind. Mana burning your max mana gives you a T3 affliction: mana starvation. This means that most common people are only able to donate 1 mana each day. Children typically do not possess enough mana to safely donate. Because mana is the source of most creature's power, creatures with class levels tend not to donate mana or risk being burned out during a late night ambush. Specialized facilities and gilded altars are incredibly expensive, typically costing 100s of thousands of gold to craft. This makes it so facilities for mana donation tend to be rather sparse, only located in sizable settlements with magical and religious influence. When mana is extracted, it is turned into a mana crystal. Mana crystals are versatile and can change between states of matter, becoming either liquid or solid for ease of storage. Each point of mana can earn 4g in a mana extraction facility. In a church, it earns 5 GBP and food/lodging for the day. Accumulation of Mana Most religious services happen in the evening after work, so that way people may spend their mana as they wish during the day, and donate it at night if they wish. With large generous populations this can mean that you can amass large quantities of mana rather quickly. Because most people can only donate 1 mana, you can almost 1:1 translate a population of donors to daily mana generation. Not everybody who donates has commoner tier mana, but at the same time, not everybody can always donate! So the 1:1 ratio is generally the go-to method of determining mana generation over time. Again, not all places have the correct facilities, so this is often available in villages.
entries.path: Equipment and Economics/Equipment Types/Approximating Golds Value/Economics/Mana Donation.md

entries.title: Populations
entries.content: Settlements receive classifications based on population. More populous settlements tend to condense more wealth, have more facilities and are more cosmopolitan. Certain types of services can be expected in larger settlements, but are unheard of in smaller ones. The settlements of Nack are generally less populous than those in our world, with even the biggest of cities having only hundreds of thousands of people. Hamlet - Village - 1,000+ people City - 10,000+ people Metropolis - 100k+ people Some general assumptions about the population... * About 10% of the population is assumed to be over level 0, with a skew towards early levels. * Most regions have a dominant religion, with at around half the population favoring them. Metropolises break this mold by having more variety, while hamlets tend to have a much higher skew of 1 religion.
entries.path: Equipment and Economics/Equipment Types/Approximating Golds Value/Economics/Populations.md

entries.title: Approximating Gold's Value
entries.content: As a general rule, I have loosely tied the value of gold to the USD. Average laborers like farmers can be expected to make ~10-20k gold every year, with the amount increasing with more sophisticated trades such as smiths or merchants. Gold pieces are small coins and are virtually weightless due to the minting process performed by the financial wizards. Another benefit of this minting is it causes gold pieces to naturally stack into specific values (5, 10, 20, 100, 1000). Someone can easily hold 100g in their pockets, 1000g in a dedicated pouch. Anything more and they are bulging at the seams and would be better off with a bank. Roundabout CostDescriptionCheap1 - 100 goldCheap items are purchased several times throughout the day typically. Includes things like food, camping supplies, common alchemy goods, lodging and such. On the low end you might get a piece of candy, winter clothes or a dagger. On the high end you could get a specialized tool like a fishing rod, a great sword or a steel padlock.Common100 - 1kCommon items are generally purchased less often than cheap ones by most. Includes things like heavy armor, low impact magic items and exceptional alchemical goods. On the low end you get something like a furnace, a med kit or a cape of ever flowing. On the high end you get something like troll oil, or super goo.Expensive1k - 10kExpensive items must be saved up for my most. These include transportation vehicles, the average magic item and goods made with rare metals. On the low end you get something like a magic weapon. On the high end you get something like a cannon, a sailing ship or a meager home.Rare10k - 100kRare items are usually reserved for the rich, or require loans, savings or groups to purchase. Things like sizable houses, battleships, and masterfully crafted itemsLegendary100k+Reserved almost exclusively for powerful magic items and large scale projects.
entries.path: Equipment and Economics/Equipment Types/Approximating Golds Value/Approximating Gold's Value.md

entries.title: +Shield Actions
entries.content: Shields give access to a unique set of actions. See subsections for more details. Shield actions can be used against a +Siege Weapon, but it breaks the shield, rendering it unusable until repaired.
entries.path: Equipment and Economics/Equipment Types/Armor and Shield Rules/Shields/+Shield Actions.md

entries.title: +Shield
entries.content: - Shields give you poise and additional combat options called shield actions. - Shields also double up as basic 1h weapons with d8 base damage. - If you do not meet the STR requirement, you cannot use shield actions | | Poise | Weight | Str Requirement | SQs | | ---------------------------------------------------------- | ----- | ------ | --------------- | ---------- | | Heavy Shield | 5 | 15 | +1 | CM (Shove) | | Tower Shield | 10 | 40 | +3 | CM (Shove) | Note: If you un-equip a shield, you immediately subtract the bonus poise it grants (min 1). Special: If you wield a shield in two hands, you increase your effective STR by two for determining if you can wield it.
entries.path: Equipment and Economics/Equipment Types/Armor and Shield Rules/Shields/+Shield.md

entries.title: Blast Shield
entries.content: Shield Types: Tower Action: Immediate When you fail a reflex save, gain resistance to the damage taken.
entries.path: Equipment and Economics/Equipment Types/Armor and Shield Rules/Shields/Blast Shield.md

entries.title: Block
entries.content: Shield Types: Heavy Shield, Tower Shield Action: Immediate Decrease the bonus to an incoming Attack by 2. If it is a graze, it instead misses completely. Can be used on attacks targeting allies within natural reach. Multiple creatures can block the same attack.
entries.path: Equipment and Economics/Equipment Types/Armor and Shield Rules/Shields/Block.md

entries.title: Shields Up
entries.content: Shield Types: Heavy Shield, Tower Shield Action: Immediate Gain Resistance to any damage originating from a Ranged Attack roll until the end of the current creature's turn. Can be used on attacks targeting allies within natural reach instead.
entries.path: Equipment and Economics/Equipment Types/Armor and Shield Rules/Shields/Shields Up.md

entries.title: Tank Up
entries.content: Shield Types: Tower Action: 15 ft of movement - Plant your shield to gain Total Cover from one particular direction. Essentially defends you from all attacks originating from a cone in that direction. A creature within 5 feet of you behind your shield gains Cover, but only from a direct line. On subsequent turns reduce your movement by 15 to maintain this. - You can choose to attack from behind the shield, but it gives cover instead of total cover. - Using other +Shield Actions ends tank up. - You can only use tank up once per turn, forcing you to stick to a particular direction. - You cannot tank up in the same turn you Core Rules, How to Play/Actions/Common Actions/Full Round Actions/Charge.
entries.path: Equipment and Economics/Equipment Types/Armor and Shield Rules/Shields/Tank Up.md

entries.title: Armor
entries.content: - Gain Poise equal to the AC the armor gives. - Heavy armor imposes a -2 penalty to stealth checks. - Dex column decides how much DEX you get to add to AC. If it has no value, your dex doesn’t add or subtract from your AC. | | AC | Dex | Weight | Str Requirement | SQs | | ---------------------------------------------------- | --- | --------- | ------ | --------------- | ----- | | Light | 1 | Limitless | 10 | | | | Medium | 4 | 1 | 25 | +1 | | | Heavy | 6 | - | 50 | +3 | DRA 1 | Note: If you do not meet the armor's strength requirement, your speed is reduced by 30 (minimum 5).
entries.path: Equipment and Economics/Equipment Types/Armor and Shield Rules/Armor.md

entries.title: Buckler
entries.content: Buckler doesn't count as a shield and doesn't provide any passive defensive bonuses. Instead you get access to the parry ability. You can still interact with objects with a buckler on hand, but bucklers cannot be used if the hand is occupied. Parry (standard) - Prepare yourself for an incoming attack. When you are attacked, you can choose whether or not to parry it. If you do, roll an attack as an instant action. If your attack roll is higher, the attack is negated and the enemy takes Poise damage equal to your attack roll. You can decide to parry an attack after the result of the roll is revealed. You cannot parry siege weapons.
entries.path: Equipment and Economics/Equipment Types/Armor and Shield Rules/Buckler.md

entries.title: +Downtime
entries.content: Players are expected to go on adventures, but adventures don't always happen back to back. What happens in between? Campaign Pacing Because downtime can often lead to long periods of time passing by relative to how long adventures take, its worth considering how these activities fit into the pace of your campaign. Most of the math regarding activities that happen over time is measured in weeks, so that will be the basic assumption in most things listed here. If your campaign has a months or years of time skips, players suddenly having 10s of thousands of gold from +Profession can give them access to powerful items earlier than expected. On the flip side, if the structure of your campaign has rapid fire adventures with no downtime at all, players might not get the chance to do any of these activities, and how you award gold/items should be adjusted accordingly. If you don't want to adjust these systems, then don't use them. Downtime Things like earnings from profession are generally measured in weeks. Using the +Medicine skill to provide long term care can a long time, depending on how wounded the individual is. Using these types of activities as a framework, I would also like to add other types of activities that you can fill your time with if you aren't a skilled laborer. Over time I will add more things here. This is what I have in mind. I will also include how repeatable I think they should be and, how long they should take and other details. Acquiring entirely new Skills - ~3 months at more or less depending on the skill - Not repeatable or limited - Requires teacher, the skill of which can change how quickly you learn Learning a language - For working proficiency, maybe 2 weeks. - For mastery, ~4 weeks Creating a new technique - Few months for a low level technique, many months for a high level technique - Large quantities of research materials, including money and a physical location fit for the task - Some form of inspiration, be it lost arts, copying something you fought, or meeting a very specific need Research - Time varies wildly based on how esoteric the knowledge is and where your sources of information are coming from. - A clear goal, like "discover how Bane the dragon keeps coming back to life after we kill her" - Large quantities of research materials, including either the money to purchase them or a physical location fit for the task (library, church, knowledgeable oracle) Crafting Items - Using the +Profession skill or Alchemy, create things - Varies wildly based on price - Some type of raw material related to what you are trying to create - Mana (see Mana Donation) - Other crafting details TBD, but these are the basics Retraining - Change one of your feats -

entries.title: Crystallized Mana
entries.content: Mana is an naturally occurring, invisible and hard to use resource akin to air. Crystallized mana is mana condensed into a pure and physically tangible form. Crystallized mana is usually formed by using special rituals to extract mana from creatures, inflicting mana burn on them. This is usually referred to as Mana Donation. Crystallized mana is a resource that is used in crafting magic items (located in the gear upgrades section). It is also often a resource sought out for by larger societies in order to participate in large scale magic infrastructure like teleportation circles, or to perform powerful rituals, usually to justify types of GM Magic for story elements.

entries.title: Cloud
entries.content: 1,500g As an instant action, you can cause a solid cloud to manifest underneath your feet. The cloud lasts until the end of your next turn. You can use it to negate Fall Damage, or to give yourself a boost, allowing you to jump twice with a single Jump action. This has a 1 minute cooldown.

entries.title: Amulet of the Damned
entries.content: 6,000g Tags: Something to help the Barrows brothers rest, if only temporarily. You deal full damage to Incorporeal creatures. When you deal damage to an incorporeal creature, they are anchored for 1 round. Part of the following +Unique Item Sets - Blighted Set - Tainted Set - Corrupted Set - Defiled Set - Infested Set - Wretched Set
entries.path: Equipment and Economics/Equipment Types/Gear Upgrades/Baubles/Pricing/2k - 10k/Amulet of the Damned.md

entries.title: Bandage
entries.content: 5,500g This badge is meant to resemble bandages, but when the wearer is afflicted with a bleed effect, you can choose to spend 1 mana to automatically negates it. This has a 1 minute cooldown.

entries.title: Faded Scale of Ao Shin
entries.content: 4,000g Sometimes bestowed upon the followers of Ao Shin the Storm Dragon as a gift, but this one has lost its luster. For a single scale it is still quite large, taking up most of the hand if placed on an empty palm. Rubbing it slightly causes this gold and blue scale to crackle with electricity. Spend 1 mana to gain access to the following special attack, usable at-will. Lightning Strike - Electrify your next melee attack. On-hit the electricity arcs to another target within 15 ft. All secondary targets take the entirety of the attacks damage as lightning damage (reflex halves). They do not suffer any of its on-hit effects. You choose the targets. Quest - Receive the favor of the Ao Shin to improve this item. Granting the following benefits. You can remove the special attack tag from lightning strike, allowing you to combine it with the effect with other special attacks. When you do so, you increase the amount of mana you spend from 1 to 6. When you make special attacks that target multiple creatures (like cleave, or Kazekenshin's steel tempest), the additional lightning bolt strikes cannot target the same creatures as the attack roll(s) made in the special attack.
entries.path: Equipment and Economics/Equipment Types/Gear Upgrades/Baubles/Pricing/2k - 10k/Faded Scale of Ao Shin.md

entries.title: Peace
entries.content: 5,000g This badge is a symbol of peace. If you perform no action on your turn in combat, you can spend 1 mana to be affected as per Sanctuary until the start of your next turn. This has a 1 minute cooldown.

entries.title: Target
entries.content: 5,000g This badge literally puts a target on you. Any ranged attacks directed at allies within 5 ft of you are instead directed at you.

entries.title: Bull Goat
entries.content: 20,000g This tiny ram icon can be affixed to any piece of medium or heavy Armor, increasing your Poise by 5.
entries.path: Equipment and Economics/Equipment Types/Gear Upgrades/Baubles/Pricing/10k - 25k/Bull Goat.md

entries.title: +Baubles
entries.content: Baubles are little badges, icons, talismans and whatnot that you affix to your person, providing you with a passive bonus. You can have two baubles.

entries.title: Diving Suit
entries.content: 2,500g+ Tags: - Can count as non-armor, light armor, medium armor or heavy armor, increasing the price accordingly. The diving suit comes in a wide variety of styles and sizes, accommodating for all types of armor users. The diving suit is an air-tight suit designed for underwater use. It allows the wearer to breathe underwater for 30 minutes before they need to resurface and recharge. They also ignore penalties for Underwater Combat.
entries.path: Equipment and Economics/Equipment Types/Gear Upgrades/Body and Shields/Body Slot (no armor)/Pricing/2k - 10k/Diving Suit.md

entries.title: Goomsayer Robe
entries.content: 3,000g Tags: Su, Glamer This robe has very long sleeves and a hood that casts a shadow over the face. It has colors that match those of a Goo Dragon. Typically only worn by Goomsayers (agents of the Shroomlette) and their allies. Goomsayer robes allow any creature of medium size or smaller to obscure their appearance completely, appearing as a generic robed figure, provided they wear the robe with the hood pulled over their face. Even bright lights are unable to pierce this hood. It droops over the legs, and even conceals other features that could be revealed through traditional clothing, like horns or tails. For creatures that aren't humanoid, it allows them to appear as though they were humanoid shaped. Effectively turning them into a robed figure. The robe allows the user to manipulate objects as though they had hands. If the wearer does have hands, they do not appear visible even when you peek into the sleeves. Held objects appear attached to the robe. If a creature really wants to reveal who you are, they can either do it covertly through +Sleight of Hand, or overtly through poise breaking you, revealing your head under the hood.
entries.path: Equipment and Economics/Equipment Types/Gear Upgrades/Body and Shields/Body Slot (no armor)/Pricing/2k - 10k/Goomsayer Robe.md

entries.title: Twin Shade Robe
entries.content: 3,000g Tags: Su, Recharge Ghosts decorate this otherwise dark colorless robe. When not looking directly at it, they seem to shift to and fro. As a swift action, you can spend 1 mana to release the Incorporeal ghosts inside of this robe. They move 100 feet each round towards the nearest non-allied creatures that are at least Level 0. When they make contact with a creature, they disappear and return to the robe. If the ghosts travelled at least 30 feet, creatures touched have their speed reduced by 15 and become Glowing for 1 round. Regardless of distance, you see a bright glowing outline of the creature for 1 minute, even through solid terrain. Once used, it takes the ghosts 10 minutes to recharge their spookyness.
entries.path: Equipment and Economics/Equipment Types/Gear Upgrades/Body and Shields/Body Slot (no armor)/Pricing/2k - 10k/Twin Shade Robe.md

entries.title: Barrows Armor (heavy)
entries.content: 15,000g Tags: - Is heavy armor Dark metallic armor that changes its appearance if you possess a Barrows set bonus. You gain resistance to pierce, slash and crush damage made from ranged attacks. Part of the following +Unique Item Sets - Corrupted Set - Defiled Set - Infested Set - Wretched Set
entries.path: Equipment and Economics/Equipment Types/Gear Upgrades/Body and Shields/Heavy Armor/Pricing/10k - 25k/Barrows Armor (heavy).md

entries.title: Giganite Armor
entries.content: 185,000g Tags: - Is heavy armor This completely black metal is does not look special, but even the strongest struggle to move it. - Giganite plate weighs 500 lbs, limiting its use to only exceptionally strong creatures. It also lowers your Land Speed by 5. You cannot Fly or Hover while wearing giganite. - You are immune to forced movement, unless it inflicted by the environment (ie gravity or a pitfall). - Increases your Poise by 30 instead of the standard 6 for heavy armor.
entries.path: Equipment and Economics/Equipment Types/Gear Upgrades/Body and Shields/Heavy Armor/Pricing/100k+/Giganite Armor.md

entries.title: Heart of the Wild
entries.content: 125,000g Tags: Healing Is heavy armor This armor is made of wood, but hard as metal. It has vegetation that grows on it that connects with the wearer, suffusing it with life. - Calculate your +HP as though you were 2 Levels higher. - Gain Regeneration 5. This can only be stopped if your hp is brought to 0, and only for 10 minutes. Creatures can visibly see your rapid recovery. Limbs replaced by this regeneration more closely resemble plant life.
entries.path: Equipment and Economics/Equipment Types/Gear Upgrades/Body and Shields/Heavy Armor/Pricing/100k+/Heart of the Wild.md

entries.title: Cybertron Armor
entries.content: 9,000g Tags: Light Is light armor This armor is lined with all manner of complex but lightweight circuitry. Colorful energy scintillates across the nodes that pepper this armor. - Whenever you Dash, you also Disengage. - During any turn you dash, you can choose to spend 1 mana to leave behind a solid wall of light that blocks Line of Effect, but not line of sight. This wall lasts until the start of your next turn. - Whenever you are affected by a Zap effect (including a friendly one), you can dash as an immediate action.
entries.path: Equipment and Economics/Equipment Types/Gear Upgrades/Body and Shields/Light Armor/Pricing/2k - 10k/Cybertron Armor.md

entries.title: Sand Cloak
entries.content: 6,000g Tags: Is light armor A leathery cloak with an abrasive texture. You can take the Hide action while being directly observed if you activate this item. Whenever you take the hide action, you can choose to spend 1 mana to make a 5 ft radius cloud of dust appears around you functioning as Solid Fog. This dust fades away at the end of your next turn.
entries.path: Equipment and Economics/Equipment Types/Gear Upgrades/Body and Shields/Light Armor/Pricing/2k - 10k/Sand Cloak.md

entries.title: Barrows Armor (light)
entries.content: 15,000g Tags: - Is light armor Dark leather/cloth armor that changes its appearance if you possess a Barrows set bonus. Gives a +10 bonus to the range of ranged attacks. Uniquely this also applies to the range of spells that have spell attacks. Part of the following +Unique Item Sets - Blighted Set - Tainted Set
entries.path: Equipment and Economics/Equipment Types/Gear Upgrades/Body and Shields/Light Armor/Pricing/10k - 25k/Barrows Armor (light).md

entries.title: Crystal Armor
entries.content: 11,000g Tags: Is light armor Elven armor not valued for its protection, but instead for its synergy with traditional elven weapons. When using a Crystal Bow, Crystal Halberd or similar type of custom elven crystal weapon, get a +2 bonus to attack.
entries.path: Equipment and Economics/Equipment Types/Gear Upgrades/Body and Shields/Light Armor/Pricing/10k - 25k/Crystal Armor.md

entries.title: Kinetic Absorption Armor
entries.content: 11,000g Tags: Is light armor This skin tight body suit doesn't seem like it protects much, but getting hit while wearing it sure feels great! Increase your Land Speed by based on how much +HP you are missing. - If you are missing any hp, increase by 10. - If you are missing at least half your hp, increase by 20. - If you at or below 10 hp, increase by 30.
entries.path: Equipment and Economics/Equipment Types/Gear Upgrades/Body and Shields/Light Armor/Pricing/10k - 25k/Kinetic Absorption Armor.md

entries.title: Jingle Armor
entries.content: Crust 9,000g Tags: Su Is medium armor This chainmail armor is made of solid gold, silver or some other incredibly valuable mineral. Moving around with it on makes you jingle, as thought your pockets were filled with coins. - You can store an infinite quantity of gold, silver or other valuable minerals inside of this armor's extradimensional space. It cannot contain any other kind of item. By turning it upside down (even while worn), all of its contents slowly poor out. You can easily retrieve its contents from the extradimensional space when you wear it. - For every 1,000g of value stored inside, you increase the distance you
entries.path: Equipment and Economics/Equipment Types/Gear Upgrades/Body and Shields/Medium Armor/Pricing/2k - 10k/Jingle Armor.md

entries.title: Frost Barrier Armor
entries.content: 20,000g Tags: Frost, Su Is medium armor This cool blue armor insulates the wearer from the biting cold aura it emanates. After 1 minute of not taking damage, the wearer of the armor is affected by the Frost Armor spell, but it lasts until you take +HP damage. When the barrier breaks, the creature that broke it becomes Frosty. Additionally, any time you take damage from a foe within your natural reach, you can choose spend 2 mana to freeze yourself and your enemy as an immediate action, inflicting the entombed condition (reflex negates). The ice negates the next instance of damage the creature takes and the entombed condition ends.
entries.path: Equipment and Economics/Equipment Types/Gear Upgrades/Body and Shields/Medium Armor/Pricing/10k - 25k/Frost Barrier Armor.md

entries.title: Thornmail
entries.content: 15,000g Tags: - Is medium armor This breastplate is decorated with thorny iconography. Just looking at it hurts a litte. Whenever you take +HP damage from an enemy, the creature that dealt the damage feels your pain and takes 3 True Damage.
entries.path: Equipment and Economics/Equipment Types/Gear Upgrades/Body and Shields/Medium Armor/Pricing/10k - 25k/Thornmail.md

entries.title: Sunfire Armor
entries.content: 60,000g Tags: Fire, Light, Persistent Is medium armor This breastplate comes with a cape. Both pulse with light, which intensifies in the heat of battle. You emit Bright Light at a range of 40 ft, and Dim Light for 40 feet past that. Whenever you roll initiative, the armor automatically ignites for the duration of combat, or until you manually turn it off as a swift action. The flames lick all enemies within your natural reach, dealing 2+(1/2 LvL) fire damage each round during your turn.
entries.path: Equipment and Economics/Equipment Types/Gear Upgrades/Body and Shields/Medium Armor/Pricing/25k - 100k/Sunfire Armor.md

entries.content: 55,000g Tags: - This enormous metal shield is plated with shinier metals, but on in inside is made of nigh indestructible metal using forging techniques perfected by generations of dwarven smiths. You can use any shield actions against siege weapons without your shield breaking. The shield cannot be broken through conventional means, nor does it rust.
entries.path: Equipment and Economics/Equipment Types/Gear Upgrades/Body and Shields/Tower Shields/Pricing/25k - 100k/Dwarven Aegis.md

Increase your Land Speed by 30. You lose this bonus speed if you... - Take or deal any damage - Target an enemy with or fail a save against a non-damaging ability

entries.path: Equipment and Economics/Equipment Types/Gear Upgrades/Footwear/Pricing/25k - 100k/Giganite Boots.md

entries.path: Equipment and Economics/Equipment Types/Gear Upgrades/Footwear/Pricing/25k - 100k/Ninja Tabi.md

entries.title: Barrows Gloves
entries.content: 4,000g Tags: Teleportation A set of gloves that makes a weapon wielded by the Barrows brothers feel like an extension of your own body. You gain DR 10 vs disarm attacks. If you possess a set bonus, you can teleport a disarmed weapon back to your hand as a free action. Part of the following +Unique Item Sets - Blighted Set - Tainted Set - Corrupted Set - Defiled Set - Infested Set - Wretched Set
entries.path: Equipment and Economics/Equipment Types/Gear Upgrades/Gloves/Pricing/2k - 10k/Barrows Gloves.md

entries.title: Free Man's Chains
entries.content: 33,000g Tags: Su These translucent, almost ethereal chains were originally designed by escaped prisoners who refused to allow themselves to be recaptured by wandering wardens. Now anybody with a vendetta against a specific creature can benefit from them. To activate any of the Free Man's Chains abilities, you must first conduct a ritual to bind yourself to a specific creature. This ritual requires 10 minutes of concentration where you require the creature's name or alias and at least a basic idea of their appearance. Once you perform this ritual, you cannot perform it again until you Defeat, kill or capture the creature you are bound to. Against this creature you gain DRA 3. Whenever you would be Stunned or Dazed by this creature, you are instead Staggered. If the creature you are bound to every becomes incarcerated, dies or some other form of extreme circumstance, the chains react. You aren't aware of what happened, but you are aware that you can perform the ritual again.
entries.path: Equipment and Economics/Equipment Types/Gear Upgrades/Gloves/Pricing/25k - 100k/Free Man's Chains.md

entries.title: Eye Patch
entries.content: 1,000g A fuzzy patch to place over your eye that wraps around the head, lined with material to block even the harshest light and the pocket-est sand. You take a -2 penalty to +Perception. When you are afflicted with the Blurred Vision or Blinded condition, you can move the patch over to to the other eye and ignore the condition. Certain effects that don't directly target the eyes, like Curse of Blindness make an eyepatch useless. Note: You need 2 eyes for this to work. You can buy normal eye patches to cover wounded/missing eyes for 1gp.
entries.path: Equipment and Economics/Equipment Types/Gear Upgrades/Headwear/Pricing/1gp - 2k/Eye Patch.md

entries.title: Face Mask
entries.content: 1,000g This is a black market item. This mask is a unique item that can only be created by a Maligner. It looks as thought the skin on someone's face was removed with careful precision and perfectly preserved. When you wear this mask, you partially transform into that creature, which counts as a +Disguise and grants a +4 bonus skill checks to appear as that specific creature. Putting it on is a FRA, but removing it is a Swap Item action. It does not change your clothes or equipment. It is virtually impossible to acquire masks of a specific creature, considering they are usually harvested from fresh corpses. Masks that are in circulation are typically of deceased individuals. Masks of particularly famous, influential, beautiful or rare creatures are often much more expensive. Even more so if the creature still breathes.
entries.path: Equipment and Economics/Equipment Types/Gear Upgrades/Headwear/Pricing/1gp - 2k/Face Mask.md

entries.title: Full Helmet
entries.content: 1,000g Counts as heavy armor. A full helmet completely covers the head, including the face, leaving only slits or holes for vision/breathing. Many are decorated with plumage or cosmetic royal/animal iconography. Full helmets block your peripheral vision, giving a -2 penalty to +Perception. Gives a +1 bonus to Fortitude saves.
entries.path: Equipment and Economics/Equipment Types/Gear Upgrades/Headwear/Pricing/1gp - 2k/Full Helmet.md

Also allows the wearer's speech to be understood underwater by other creatures wearing shelmets or that speak Aquan (see Languages).

entries.content: 4,000g Tags: - Counts as heavy armor. A dark metallic helmet that changes its appearance if you possess a Barrows set bonus. - Gives a +1 bonus to Fortitude saves. - Gives +2 Poise Part of the following +Unique Item Sets - Corrupted Set - Defiled Set - Infested Set - Wretched Set
entries.path: Equipment and Economics/Equipment Types/Gear Upgrades/Headwear/Pricing/2k - 10k/Barrows Helmet.md

Part of the following +Unique Item Sets - Blighted Set - Tainted Set
entries.path: Equipment and Economics/Equipment Types/Gear Upgrades/Headwear/Pricing/2k - 10k/Barrows Hood.md

entries.title: Scouter
entries.content: 10,000g Tags: - A device designed to be attached to the side of the head, with a colorful visor over one eye. It makes some rapid fire beeping noises at the press of a button. Stare at a creature and click a button as a swift action to see their maximum +HP. - This can only be done 1/rnd - Takes changes in max HP into account like drain.

entries.title: Spark of Light
entries.content: 4,000g Tags: Holy, Light, Recharge, Persistent, Su Counts as medium armor. This helmet constantly emits light, as a Torch. When you take damage, you can choose to spend 1 mana to turn off this light as an instant action to burst in a burning radiance, causing shining orbs to litter the nearest space occupied by your attacker, where it then continues to emit light. Creatures other than you that move into this space take 5 Holy damage. This is a Persistent effect. You must move into this space to collect the orbs to restore the light on the helm. If you fail to do so, the helm becomes inert and must be recharged through exposure to sunlight for 7 days.
entries.path: Equipment and Economics/Equipment Types/Gear Upgrades/Headwear/Pricing/2k - 10k/Spark of Light.md

entries.title: +Magic Weapon Properties
entries.content: These properties can be attached to most body slot items. A weapon can typically only be enchanted with one of these properties. Weapons with additional enchantment slots can get more than 1. Such enchantments cost 5,000 gold.
entries.path: Equipment and Economics/Equipment Types/Gear Upgrades/Magic Armor Properties/+Magic Weapon Properties.md

entries.title: Arachnid
entries.content: Tags: Recharge, Su, poly, chimeric By spending 1 mana as a swift action, you can cause long spider legs to pop out of your back. You can use these legs to Climb using your Land Speed without using your hands or legs, allowing you to freely swing weapons, cast spells etc while climbing. These legs last one minute, and cannot be summoned again for 1 minute after they retract.
entries.path: Equipment and Economics/Equipment Types/Gear Upgrades/Magic Armor Properties/Arachnid.md

entries.title: Catalogue
entries.content: Tags: Su Whenever the wearer takes damage, a mark is left behind on the armor or on the wearer's skin showing the attacker's name, if it has one. If the wearer takes damage from a few dozen creatures, the names start to overlap and become illegible. If the attacker is wearing a +Disguise, then the wearer must make a +Perception check with a DC of (10+attacker's disguise bonus). Failure indicates the name is illegible.
entries.path: Equipment and Economics/Equipment Types/Gear Upgrades/Magic Armor Properties/Catalogue.md

entries.title: Glamered
entries.content: Tags: Su, Glamer This armor enchantment allows the wearer to feed outfits to the armor. When you do, you can spend a standard action to change the armor to appear as that particular outfit, or back again. When you feed a new outfit to the armor, the old outfit pops out. You can also turn the armor upside down while it is not being worn to shake the outfit out. An outfit is generally considered to contain footwear, legwear and some type of body wear. Can also include things like hats, gloves, glasses etc. You can even feed it suits of armor to match uniform of some local guards for instance.
entries.path: Equipment and Economics/Equipment Types/Gear Upgrades/Magic Armor Properties/Glamered.md

entries.title: Sanctum
entries.content: Tags: Su, Teleportation You can rub a symbol on the armor in a specific pattern as a standard action in order to get sucked into the armor, which clatters to the ground. You appear in a tiny extradimensional space with breathable air, but little else. Objects separated from your person in this space fade away into nothing within a few seconds. You cannot perceive anything in the outside world while in this space and you can exist here indefinitely, though you will eventually get hungry/thirsty. By holding up a finger and making the same motions to activate the armor, you can reappear wherever the armor is. If the armor is in a space you can't fit, you appear in the nearest space where you can. This also happens if the armor is destroyed or dispelled. You do not need to see your destination to use this effect.
entries.path: Equipment and Economics/Equipment Types/Gear Upgrades/Magic Armor Properties/Sanctum.md

entries.title: +Magic Weapon Properties
entries.content: These properties can be attached to most weapons. Classes like Fighter and Magus can add these properties to their weapon temporarily. A weapon can typically only be enchanted with one of these properties. Weapons with additional enchantment slots can get more than 1. Such enchantments cost 5,000 gold. | Name | Effect | | ------------------ | ------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | | Bane | +2 att/dmg vs a specific creature type (Plants, Giants, etc). Your AC is 2 lower when you bane a creature type you possess. | | Benevolent | Increase the bonus of the aid action by +2- Only usable while in combat - Only usable while wielding this weapon - Doesn't stack with itself (ie multiple benevolent aids only give +4 on the first aid, then +2 for each one after) | | Diving | This weapon doesn't have reduced effectiveness during Aquatic Combat. | | Elemental | Weapon can swap between its usual damage type(s) and a specific type of elemental damage (determined on creation) as a free action 1/turn. | | Fortuitous | Once per round, when the wielder of a fortuitous weapon hits with a simple weapon attack as an AoO, they can make a second simple weapon attack of opportunity with this weapon against that foe at a –4 penalty.Doesn't consume one of your bonus AoOs from something like Combat Reflexes. | | Ghost Touch | Weapon treats incorporeal creatures as corporeal and disables their ability to move through solid objects On-hit for 1 round. | | Grounding | Attacks inflict grounded for 1 round on-hit (fort negates) | | Invigorating | When you defeat a creature with this weapon, you lose the Fatigued condition. If you are not fatigued, get +10 speed this turn. | | Locking | On-hit you can lock the weapon in place, entangling the creature struck (reflex negates). While entangling a creature, you cannot use the weapon. | | Mimicing | Attacks made with this weapon On-hit against a creature allows the wielder to copy one of the creatures elemental resistance types for one round. You can only have one copied resistance at a time. On hit the wielder has an intuitive sense the creature is resistant to, allowing them to choose.The resistance wears off after 1 minute. | | Anchored | On-hit apply anchored for 1 round (will negates). | | Radiant | The wielder can activate the weapon to use Radiance as an Innate Technique once per day, anchored to the weapon. | | Returning | As a free action, you can call your weapon back to your hand. If it is within 60 feet of you it returns instantly. Anything beyond requires 3 rounds. If there are any physical barriers preventing its return, this does not work.If a creature possesses the weapon (reflex negates). | | Seeking | Weapon attacks ignore Partial Concealment, Total Concealment and other forms of miss chance created by the environment or magic. | | Sticky | DR 10 vs ALL disarm, not just against the enchanted item | | Transformative | As a swift action you can change your weapon into another type of weapon listed on the Weapon Examples page. | | Vicious | +2 dmg. On-hit the wielder takes 2 True Damage. |
entries.path: Equipment and Economics/Equipment Types/Gear Upgrades/Magic Weapon Properties/+Magic Weapon Properties.md

entries.title: Bane
entries.content: +2 att/dmg vs a specific creature type (Plants, Giants, etc). Your AC is 2 lower when you bane a creature type you possess.
entries.path: Equipment and Economics/Equipment Types/Gear Upgrades/Magic Weapon Properties/Bane.md

entries.title: Benevolent
entries.content: Increase the bonus of the aid action by +2 - Only usable while in combat - Only usable while wielding this weapon - Doesn't stack with itself (ie multiple benevolent aids only give +4 on the first aid, then +2 for each one after)
entries.path: Equipment and Economics/Equipment Types/Gear Upgrades/Magic Weapon Properties/Benevolent.md

entries.title: Diving
entries.content: This weapon doesn't have reduced effectiveness during Aquatic Combat.
entries.path: Equipment and Economics/Equipment Types/Gear Upgrades/Magic Weapon Properties/Diving.md

entries.title: Elemental
entries.content: Weapon can swap between its usual damage type(s) and a specific type of elemental damage (determined on creation) as a free action 1/turn.
entries.path: Equipment and Economics/Equipment Types/Gear Upgrades/Magic Weapon Properties/Elemental.md

entries.title: Fortuitous
entries.content: Tags: Recharge Once per round, when the wielder of a fortuitous weapon hits with a simple weapon attack as an AoO, they can make a second simple weapon attack of opportunity with this weapon against that foe at a –4 penalty. Doesn't consume one of your bonus AoOs from something like Combat Reflexes.
entries.path: Equipment and Economics/Equipment Types/Gear Upgrades/Magic Weapon Properties/Fortuitous.md

entries.title: Ghost Touch
entries.content: Weapon treats incorporeal creatures as corporeal and disables their ability to move through solid objects On-hit for 1 round.
entries.path: Equipment and Economics/Equipment Types/Gear Upgrades/Magic Weapon Properties/Ghost Touch.md

entries.title: Grounding
entries.content: Attacks inflict grounded for 1 round on-hit (fort negates)
entries.path: Equipment and Economics/Equipment Types/Gear Upgrades/Magic Weapon Properties/Grounding.md

entries.title: Invigorating
entries.content: When you defeat a creature with this weapon, you lose the Fatigued condition. If you are not fatigued, get +10 speed this turn.
entries.path: Equipment and Economics/Equipment Types/Gear Upgrades/Magic Weapon Properties/Invigorating.md

entries.title: Locking
entries.content: On-hit you can lock the weapon in place, entangling the creature struck (reflex negates). While entangling a creature, you cannot use the weapon.
entries.path: Equipment and Economics/Equipment Types/Gear Upgrades/Magic Weapon Properties/Locking.md

entries.title: Mimicing
entries.content: Attacks made with this weapon On-hit against a creature allows the wielder to copy one of the creatures elemental resistance types for one round. You can only have one copied resistance at a time. On hit the wielder has an intuitive sense the creature is resistant to, allowing them to choose. The resistance wears off after 1 minute.
entries.path: Equipment and Economics/Equipment Types/Gear Upgrades/Magic Weapon Properties/Mimicing.md

entries.title: Phase Locking
entries.content: On-hit apply anchored for 1 round (will negates).
entries.path: Equipment and Economics/Equipment Types/Gear Upgrades/Magic Weapon Properties/Phase Locking.md

entries.title: Radiant
entries.content: The wielder can activate the weapon to use Radiance as an Innate Technique once per day, anchored to the weapon.
entries.path: Equipment and Economics/Equipment Types/Gear Upgrades/Magic Weapon Properties/Radiant.md

entries.title: Returning
entries.content: As a free action, you can call your weapon back to your hand. If it is within 60 feet of you it returns instantly. Anything beyond requires 3 rounds. If there are any physical barriers preventing its return, this does not work. If a creature possesses the weapon (reflex negates).
entries.path: Equipment and Economics/Equipment Types/Gear Upgrades/Magic Weapon Properties/Returning.md

entries.title: Seeking
entries.content: Weapon attacks ignore Partial Concealment, Total Concealment and other forms of miss chance created by the environment or magic.
entries.path: Equipment and Economics/Equipment Types/Gear Upgrades/Magic Weapon Properties/Seeking.md

entries.title: Sticky
entries.content: DR 10 vs ALL disarm, not just against the enchanted item
entries.path: Equipment and Economics/Equipment Types/Gear Upgrades/Magic Weapon Properties/Sticky.md

entries.title: Transformative
entries.content: As a swift action you can change your weapon into another type of weapon listed on the Weapon Examples page.
entries.path: Equipment and Economics/Equipment Types/Gear Upgrades/Magic Weapon Properties/Transformative.md

entries.title: Vicious
entries.content: +2 dmg. On-hit the wielder takes 2 True Damage.
entries.path: Equipment and Economics/Equipment Types/Gear Upgrades/Magic Weapon Properties/Vicious.md

entries.title: Tracking Device
entries.content: 1,500g This tiny device is about the size of a peanut and resonates with psionic energy. You can attune to it by concentrating on it while in contact with it for 1 minute, provided you don't have negative intelligence. More importantly, it can fit inside of a creature's body. With engineering or medicine proficiency, you can implant this device into a creature. The process is painful and obvious, so it can usually only be done on Helpless creatures. While implanted, you can know the general direction and distance of any tracking device you have attuned to. Each tracking device has a slightly different signal, allowing you to tell which one is which, provided you have the mental capacity to do so. You can be attuned to (1+INT) tracking devices at once. If the creature ever receives medical attention, a DC 20 +Medicine check also reveals a strange bump, or an unusual ache that indicates where the tracking device is, and it can be removed.
entries.path: Equipment and Economics/Equipment Types/Gear Upgrades/Slotless Items/Pricing/1gp - 2k/Tracking Device.md

entries.title: Weapon Tattoo
entries.content: 2,000g An elaborate tattoo that depicts a sword embedded in the ground. By making the motion of pulling it out, the tattoo disappears from the body and appears in your hands. You can get a tattoo that functions like a magical sheathe for a single weapon, a ranged weapon with ammunition, or 2 light weapons. The tattoo must be visible in order to function, and it must be at least the size of your hand. This can cause some complications if you wanted to keep the weapon hidden by putting it on your ass but you are a heavy armor user. The tattoo is not bound to a single weapon, and will change to more closely depict whatever weapon is stored within. In all other ways, it functions as though it was your hip, allowing you to freely take the Swap Item action with any weapon(s) within. A creature can peel your skin off to remove the tattoo, causing the weapon to fall out.
entries.path: Equipment and Economics/Equipment Types/Gear Upgrades/Slotless Items/Pricing/1gp - 2k/Weapon Tattoo.md

entries.title: Apple of Eternal Sleep
entries.content: 6,000g What a tasty looking apple! It looks and tastes like a normal apple. Creatures with the +Nature skill can roll a DC 16 check to realize this apple's unusual nature. A creature with Scent can detect it is unusual as well. A creature who eats even a single bite of this apple will fall asleep almost immediately, absorbing the magic from the apple. While asleep, the creature is frozen in time, cannot age or die of thirst/hunger or even lack of air. The creature cannot be woken up unless kissed by someone with royal blood.
entries.path: Equipment and Economics/Equipment Types/Gear Upgrades/Slotless Items/Pricing/2k - 10k/Apple of Eternal Sleep.md

entries.title: Banner of Doom
entries.content: 7,000g Tags: Fear This flag is inscribed with symbols of Demon Lords, prominent criminal organizations or evil mages. Those that exist within 30 ft of the banner while it is planted (a standard action) hear whispers from it, causing them to become shaken 1, unless they are allied with the group the banner's iconography showcases. This has no effect on creatures that are already shaken. If the creature was not already shaken when it entered the area and it leaves the area, the shaken the banner inflicts immediately dissipates as the whispers vanish. A creature can roll a DC 15 +Athletics check to push the banner over and disable its effect.
entries.path: Equipment and Economics/Equipment Types/Gear Upgrades/Slotless Items/Pricing/2k - 10k/Banner of Doom.md

entries.title: Boat Bottle
entries.content: 4,000g Tags: - A relatively mundane looking bottle with a few scribbles on it. This bottle can be activated by spending a minute in direct contact with a boat you own. After this minute the boat shrinks down in size to fit inside the bottle and is placed in stasis. Any creatures on board are deposited on the ground/in the water where the boat was. You then specify a password/phrase to activate the boat. When you do so, the boat pops out of the boat and splashes into the nearest body of water. If none are present, nothing happens. Once a ship is freed from the bottle, it turns to dust.
entries.path: Equipment and Economics/Equipment Types/Gear Upgrades/Slotless Items/Pricing/2k - 10k/Boat Bottle.md

entries.title: Brain Vat
entries.content: 5,000g A floating brain in a jar catches your eye. Before you get a chance to ask about it, a voice comes from it asking "what are you lookin' at?" The brain vat is the method of capture of choice for particularly cruel alchemists. In order to create a brain vat, the vessel must be prepared long before the brain itself is extracted. This means you must craft the item in advance, only adding the brain as the final component. To safely extract the brain from a creature, it must be alive or freshly killed. You must roll a DC 10 first aid check, otherwise the brain might have some defects. When you successfully extract a brain, you can safely store it away on your shelves. The vat itself comes with an apparatus that allows the brain to "hear" the world around it, and one that allows it to respond back in its original voice. Both features have a switch that allow you to turn them off and on, letting you mute, deafen or both. Requires swapping out the brain juice for new fresh ingredients every 6 months or so. Forgetting leads to a very insane brain.
entries.path: Equipment and Economics/Equipment Types/Gear Upgrades/Slotless Items/Pricing/2k - 10k/Brain Vat.md

entries.title: Mini Me
entries.content: 4,000g Tags: Su, Scrying, Recharge *This hand-held plushie seems incredibly lifelike. These plushies closely resemble a creature. It can only be made using the hair of the original. For hairless creatures, something equivalent like scales, feathers etc works. Once the plushie is complete, the person it is a copy of must be present for the final part of the item's creation where they welcome the mini into the world. By squeezing the chest of the plushie a creature can send a mental "ping" to the original. The original can then close their eyes to see, hear (smell, etc) through the mini me. While doing so, they can speak through the mini me. This communication can be established any number of times each day and can last indefinitely.
entries.path: Equipment and Economics/Equipment Types/Gear Upgrades/Slotless Items/Pricing/2k - 10k/Mini Me.md

entries.title: Nullifying Nail
entries.content: 12,000g An oversized nail made of adamantine, separated from its brothers and sisters, seems a bit overpriced for just a single nail. By spending 3 mana as a standard action, you can roll an Brute Force athletics check to impale this nail into a solid surface. You make this check with Advantage if you wield any type of hammer. While embedded, ALL creatures within a 30 ft radius of the space you embedded the nail in have a chance their spells Fizzle. Every time they cast a spell, they must make a Caster Level check, rolling the CL of the spell against a DC of (10+your LvL). Any creature can remove the nail as a standard action by beating your athletics roll.
entries.path: Equipment and Economics/Equipment Types/Gear Upgrades/Slotless Items/Pricing/10k - 25k/Nullifying Nail.md

entries.title: Summoning Contract
entries.content: Price Varies, See Details Below Summoning contracts are written in order for a creature to provide aid to another without following them around all the time. There are always 2 parties involved in a contract: the summoner and the summoned. Typically it is up to the summoner to convince the summoned to sign the contract, as the magic does not work with unwilling creatures. It is possible to find summoning contracts with a signature from a summoned creature, but not a summoner. Contracts are often temporary, being lifted after a certain number of summons, or after a specific task is complete. When the summoned creature signs the contract in blood, so to must the summoner. The summoned creature takes up a companion slot while summoned. 1/day as a FRA, you can summon the contracted creature. Creatures summoned in this way always have a pseudo-ethereal translucent quality to them that is easily identifiable (DC 10 arcana). A summon typically only lasts a minute, but can last up to an hour if the summoned creature accepts summoning sickness. The creature also gains summoning sickness if it drops to 0 hp while summoned. Summoning Sickness - A specific type of ailment that occurs as a result of a summoned creature pushing themselves too hard while summoned from a summoning contract, usually by sticking around for too long or being defeated. The creature is drained 2 and cannot be summoned for two weeks, even if the drain is removed. In most aspects, a summoned creature functions exactly as they would if you were fighting by their side in their actual body, with a few key differences. As a magical effect, they can be dispelled (DC 12+summoned creatures lvl). They have all of their gear and any resources that they expend (such as potions) while summoned are still expended when they return to their body. Summoned creatures only have 50% of their max hp. To the summoned creature, when you attempt to summon them, they receive a mental message from their summoner no longer than 2 sentences. They can choose to deny being summoned if they pass a will save (your class DC). If they succeed, they can send 2 sentences back. Otherwise, they enter a trance like state and their spirit leaves their body and appears next to you. If their body takes damage, or is otherwise harmed, then their spirit immediately winks out of existence. A summoned creature can dismiss itself as a standard action. A creature can only be summoned once per day. The more powerful a creature is, the more expensive it is to bind them to a summoning contract. 1,000*(1+summoned creatures LvL)^2 | Creatures Level | Contract Cost | | --------------- | ------------- | | 1 | 4,000 | | 2 | 9,000 | | 3 | 16,000 | | … | | | 12 | 169,000 | If the contract has a limit on uses, adjust price as below. | # of Summons | Price Adjustment | | ------------ | ---------------- | | 3 | -50% | | 1 | -66% | If the contract has stipulations that limit when the creature can be summoned, halve the cost again. (only summon vs fiends) Ex. Level 1 creature, limited use cases, 3 times. 1,000g.
entries.path: Equipment and Economics/Equipment Types/Gear Upgrades/Slotless Items/Summoning Contract.md

entries.title: Seal, Standard
entries.content: 500g A seal is a vague title given to an item of significance to channel magic power through. For more information see Staff Component Seals are typically attuned to a specific type of magic, such as Brazzeria (T) or Pyromancy, allowing you to cast spells of that tree with +1 CL.
entries.path: Equipment and Economics/Equipment Types/Gear Upgrades/Spell Focus Items/Seals/Pricing 1/1gp - 2k/Seal, Standard.md

entries.title: Lantern of Tangled Souls
entries.content: 4,000g Tags: Su, Light, Elusive This seal has an obvious supernatural element thanks to the oddly colored light it emanates. On closer inspection, the light source appears to be spectral creatures floating around inside. Gives off light as a Torch. Can be extinguished or lit as a free action 1/turn. While lit, you can spend 1 mana as a swift action to project the lanterns light to a space within 30 feet you can see. It lingers there for 1 round, the spirits reaching out towards allied creatures. If an ally reaches over and touches the origin of the light, they become Incorporeal and are immediately dragged adjacent to you, placing them on the side of you they were originally. When they arrive, they become corporeal again. If you ever move more than 30 feet away from the projected light, it retracts back to the lantern.
entries.path: Equipment and Economics/Equipment Types/Gear Upgrades/Spell Focus Items/Seals/Pricing 1/2k - 10k/Lantern of Tangled Souls.md

entries.title: Amplifying Staff
entries.content: 2,000g Favored by bards, this light staff shimmers in the light. If you hold it up to your mouth, it doubles the volume of any vocalizations. By spending a swift action to increase the mana cost of any ability with the healing tag by 1 (or adding a mana cost of 1), you can increase its range by 10 feet, or its radius by 5 ft. Manathirst 12 The range/radius extension can be done as a free action. Manathirst 22 The range/radius extension no longer requires mana. It is a 1h crush weapon. Also comes in a battle staff and light variant, like most staves.
entries.path: Equipment and Economics/Equipment Types/Gear Upgrades/Spell Focus Items/Staves/Pricing/1gp - 2k/Amplifying Staff.md

entries.title: Elemental Staff
entries.content: 3,000g This staff has a mana crystal affixed to its end, shimmering with a color that indicates the type of element it favors. All spells with the elemental tag shared by the staff get +1 CL. 1/day it can reduce the mana cost of such a spell by 1. Manathirst 12 1/day you can spend 1 mana to change the elemental damage type of the spell to the stave's elemental affinity. The standard staff is a 1h crush weapon. Also comes in a battle staff and light variant, like most staves.
entries.path: Equipment and Economics/Equipment Types/Gear Upgrades/Spell Focus Items/Staves/Pricing/2k - 10k/Elemental Staff.md

entries.title: Likk'em
entries.content: 2,300g This staff gets a +1 bonus to CL when casting spells from the Frogg Saran (T) technique tree This staff has a smug frog carved into the tip of it. Its eyeballs are wheels with numbers inscribed on them from 0 to 9. Whenever you defeat a creature with a melee attack using this staff, or a spell cast using this staff, the staffs eyes start spinning, stopping when they reach how much damage you overkilled the target. If you did exactly enough damage to defeat the creature and the eyes stop on zero, it plays a frog croaking sound and you gain a Luck Dice. By spending a luck dice, you can innately cast Lucky Star The standard staff is a 1h crush weapon.
entries.path: Equipment and Economics/Equipment Types/Gear Upgrades/Spell Focus Items/Staves/Pricing/2k - 10k/Likk'em.md

entries.title: Blighted Staff
entries.content: 12,000g The Blighted drained the strength of his enemies. This 2h battle staff causes any status effect you inflict with a spell to cure your own. The staff can cure any effect you inflict or the following (once per spell)... - Shaken 1 - Clumsy 1 - Sickened 1 - Blurred Vision Note - Any status you inflict means you have to make a creature suffer that particular status. For example if you are frosty, you can cast Ice Beam, and if you land it, you remove the frosty. The creature must actually suffer the status effect, so it does not function if it is immune. Part of the following +Unique Item Sets - Blighted Set
entries.path: Equipment and Economics/Equipment Types/Gear Upgrades/Spell Focus Items/Staves/Pricing/10k - 25k/Blighted Staff.md

entries.title: Wand, Standard
entries.content: 2,000g This piece of enchanted wood is infused with mana from those adept at magic casting, allowing you to channel their experience through it. While wielding a wand, you can increase the CL of a spell you cast by 1 as a swift action. A wand comes with 1 cantrip that you can Innately Cast while you wield it.
entries.path: Equipment and Economics/Equipment Types/Gear Upgrades/Spell Focus Items/Wands/Pricing/1gp - 2k/Wand, Standard.md

entries.title: Wand, Enhanced
entries.content: 5,000g Functions as a Wand, Standard, but as follows. An enhanced wand, in additional to casting a cantrip, allows you to Innately Cast a tier 1 technique 1/day.
entries.path: Equipment and Economics/Equipment Types/Gear Upgrades/Spell Focus Items/Wands/Pricing/2k - 10k/Wand, Enhanced.md

entries.title: +Unique Item Sets
entries.content: Item sets are groups of items that must be combined in order to gain a powerful bonus effect when used in tandem with each other.
entries.path: Equipment and Economics/Equipment Types/Gear Upgrades/Unique Item Sets/+Unique Item Sets.md

entries.title: Blighted Set
entries.content: Items Required Bauble - Amulet of the Damned Footwear - Barrows Boots Gloves - Barrows Gloves Headwear - Barrows Helmet Armor - Barrows Armor (light) 2h weapon - Blighted Staff Set Bonus: Blighted Aura Whenever you deal +HP damage or inflict drain on a creature with a spell, they become sickened 1.
entries.path: Equipment and Economics/Equipment Types/Gear Upgrades/Unique Item Sets/Blighted Set.md

entries.title: Corrupted Set
entries.content: Items Required Bauble - Amulet of the Damned Footwear - Barrows Boots Gloves - Barrows Gloves Headwear - Barrows Helmet Armor - Barrows Armor (heavy) 2h weapon - Corrupted Hammers Set Bonus: Corruption You gain rend. Armor Crunch - 2 hammer attacks. Target becomes Clumsy 1.
entries.path: Equipment and Economics/Equipment Types/Gear Upgrades/Unique Item Sets/Corrupted Set.md

entries.title: Defiled Set
entries.content: Items Required Bauble - Amulet of the Damned Footwear - Barrows Boots Gloves - Barrows Gloves Headwear - Barrows Helmet Armor - Barrows Armor (heavy) 2h weapon - Defiled Flail Set Bonus: Defiler Every time you make a Melee Attack with the defiled flail, you gain a stack of defile. At 2 stacks, you can decide to make your next melee attack with the flail automatically hit, requiring no roll.
entries.path: Equipment and Economics/Equipment Types/Gear Upgrades/Unique Item Sets/Defiled Set.md

entries.title: Infested Set
entries.content: Items Required Bauble - Amulet of the Damned Footwear - Barrows Boots Gloves - Barrows Gloves Headwear - Barrows Helmet Armor - Barrows Armor (heavy) 2h weapon - Infested Spear Set Bonus: Infest Tags: healing On-hit restore +HP equal to the damage bonus you currently have from the Infested Spear.
entries.path: Equipment and Economics/Equipment Types/Gear Upgrades/Unique Item Sets/Infested Set.md

entries.title: Tainted Set
entries.content: Items Required Bauble - Amulet of the Damned Footwear - Barrows Boots Gloves - Barrows Gloves Headwear - Barrows Helmet Armor - Barrows Armor (light) 2h weapon - Tainted Crossbow Set Bonus: Tainted Shot You can make a simple attack with the Tainted Crossbow as a swift action. If you make 4 attacks with the crossbow in one turn, you can reload it as a free action.
entries.path: Equipment and Economics/Equipment Types/Gear Upgrades/Unique Item Sets/Tainted Set.md

entries.title: Wretched Set
entries.content: Items Required Bauble - Amulet of the Damned Footwear - Barrows Boots Gloves - Barrows Gloves Headwear - Barrows Helmet Armor - Barrows Armor (heavy) 2h weapon - Wretched Axe Set Bonus: Wretched Strength You get a +1 bonus to damage with the wretched axe weapon attacks for every 10 +HP you are missing. For the purpose of this set bonus, drain is considered missing hp.
entries.path: Equipment and Economics/Equipment Types/Gear Upgrades/Unique Item Sets/Wretched Set.md

entries.title: Garish Gavel
entries.content: 1,900g This hammer is bejeweled all along its head and has a finish to its wooden handle that makes it sparkle. When you strike it against a solid surface, it is exceptionally loud, even with only a light tap. Everyone can't help but look upon he who slams the gavel. Special Attack: ORDER - Must be directed at a solid object instead of a creature. Taunt all enemies within 30 ft for 1 round. The hammer is a 1h crush weapon the One and Done and Dueling properties.
entries.path: Equipment and Economics/Equipment Types/Gear Upgrades/Unique Weapons/1h Weapons/Pricing/1gp - 2k/Garish Gavel.md

entries.title: Benevolent Blade
entries.content: 2,200g *This silver sword has a hilt that shines a radiant gold. This weapon is used to non-lethally dispatch creatures when defeating them since it has no chance of potentially killing them. When you defeat a creature with this weapon, you can spend 1 mana to give them the Time Out condition. Special Attack: Lifesteal Healing On hit heal (2+LvL). Manathirst 12 Special Attack: Benevolence (healing) Strike at an ally as the blade transforms into a warming radiance. It grants the attacks damage as temp hp and has no harmful on hit effects. Manathirst 22 You can use the Lifesteal and Benevolence special attacks at-will. The sword is a 1h slash or pierce weapon with the one and done property.
entries.path: Equipment and Economics/Equipment Types/Gear Upgrades/Unique Weapons/1h Weapons/Pricing/2k - 10k/Benevolent Blade.md

entries.title: Fiery War Axe
entries.content: 5000g *Favored by veteran Fighters, this axe is the color of obsidian, with a blade that looks white hot. When pointing the weapon at any creature that is considered a minion to you, it ignites in flames. Against any such foe, the axe deals an additional d10 fire damage. The axe is a 1h slash weapon with the One and Done and CM (topple) property.
entries.path: Equipment and Economics/Equipment Types/Gear Upgrades/Unique Weapons/1h Weapons/Pricing/2k - 10k/Fiery War Axe.md

entries.title: Icebreaker
entries.content: 2,500g *This mace is a cool blue color and, when wielded, the air around is visibly chilled as though it was exhaling on a cold day. It excels at crushing ice, and can easily smash through any type of icy barrier less than 1ft thick. Anything thicker becomes something of a mining operation. If a creature is frosty, or otherwise mostly made of ice, the mace does an additional 5 damage and makes it clumsy 1 on-hit. It has the following special attack... Rime Time - Make a swipe at the air and target a 30 ft cone. All creatures in the area take 2d6+LvL (7+lvl) cold damage and are made frosty (reflex halves and negates frosty). The mace is a 1h crush weapon with the CM (Shove) and One and Done properties.
entries.path: Equipment and Economics/Equipment Types/Gear Upgrades/Unique Weapons/1h Weapons/Pricing/2k - 10k/Icebreaker.md

entries.title: Impulsive Hammer
entries.content: 7,500g *This hammer is eager to smash, and prefers to be swung overhead, resisting other types of swinging motions. When the wielder lands a Crit, the hammer releases a shockwave. The creature you attacked and any enemy within 10 ft are automatically Knocked Down. The hammer is a 1h crush weapon with the CM (topple) and One and Done properties. Note: I'm normally all for changing weapons around to suit your needs, but this weapon should absolutely not be allowed to made as a light weapon with the Chink Magnet property. That would be dangerously cheesy.
entries.path: Equipment and Economics/Equipment Types/Gear Upgrades/Unique Weapons/1h Weapons/Pricing/2k - 10k/Impulsive Hammer.md

entries.title: Quaking Hammer
entries.content: 4,000g *This hammer makes the ground shake when it strikes the ground. When a creature disarms you of this weapon, it smashes into the ground at their feet, letting you make a topple attack as an instant action. Get the following special attack... Fissure Earth - Strike the ground to attack all creatures in a 30 ft line. Compare your attack to every creature's AC in the AoE. If you spend 2 mana, you can make the 30 ft line 15 ft wide. Manathirst 12 Fissure deals an additional 2d6 (7) damage and always does the enhanced version. Manathirst 22 Fissure deals Poise damage equal to damage, inflicting Knockdown on poise break. The hammer is a 1h crush weapon with the CM (topple) and One and Done properties.
entries.path: Equipment and Economics/Equipment Types/Gear Upgrades/Unique Weapons/1h Weapons/Pricing/2k - 10k/Quaking Hammer.md

entries.title: Scrappy Swordfish
entries.content: 8,000g *This fish was subject to a magical ritual akin to taxidermy after all of the useful parts were removed. The fish is a bit small for its kind, but it is certainly as sharp. It remains rigid even when clashing with metal swords. 1/day you can say a brief prayer to Mother Earth as a swift action and the fish briefly springs back to life. It immediately readies to parry (as per Buckler), allowing you to decide which attack to deflect during the next round of combat. If you worship Mother Earth, you can use a point of prayer to parry with this weapon. The fish is a 1h pierce weapon with the Strings Attached and One and Done properties. Despite being gutted and undersized, the fish is still rather heavy and weighs 20 lbs. Special: You use +Nature to craft this item instead of +Profession.
entries.path: Equipment and Economics/Equipment Types/Gear Upgrades/Unique Weapons/1h Weapons/Pricing/2k - 10k/Scrappy Swordfish.md

entries.title: Wheel of Momentum
entries.content: 9,000g *This strange item is a large shield-like weapon with glowing blades along its edges that constantly circle the outside of the weapon. Because of the weapons motion, it is ill-fitted to be used for defense. Every time the user misses or grazes an attack roll with it, the blades circle faster and glow more vibrantly, giving the wielder +1 Attack that stacks with each miss. Once you land an attack, the speed resets back to default, removing the attack bonus. This stacking effect cannot be used outside of combat. It is a 1h slash weapon with the One and Done and Attached property.
