entries.path: Equipment and Economics/Equipment Types/Gear Upgrades/Unique Weapons/1h Weapons/Pricing/2k - 10k/Wheel of Momentum.md

entries.title: Orcish Hammer
entries.content: 22,000g This hammer is infused with the power of the wind itself by orcish shamans. It feels practically weightless while you swing it through air, despite its otherwise unwieldy size. Its wielder can innately cast Storm Step 1/day. The wielder also gains this special attack... Windfury - Attack twice in rapid succession, applying DR only once. The hammer is a 1h crush weapon with the Strings Attached and One and Done quality.
entries.path: Equipment and Economics/Equipment Types/Gear Upgrades/Unique Weapons/1h Weapons/Pricing/10k - 25k/Orcish Hammer.md

entries.title: Greater Sword
entries.content: 1,000g An oversized blade with swings so clumsy it would give any self-respecting blacksmith a heart attack. This sword's swings are always going to hurt someone which each swing. If you Graze or hit a foe with a weapon, it deals an additional 1 damage. If you miss, you become Clumsy 1. The sword is a 2h slash weapon with the determination special attack and the Versatile (crush) quality.
entries.path: Equipment and Economics/Equipment Types/Gear Upgrades/Unique Weapons/2h Weapons/Pricing/1gp - 2k/Greater Sword.md

entries.title: Ogre Club
entries.content: 1,000g This club has metal plates at its end, each face inscribed with magic sigils to enhance this heavy weapons power. Unfortunately, ogres' propensity for randomness creates a different definition of power than most. While the you are threatening multiple enemies, you get a +2 bonus to Attack, but you decide which enemy you attack randomly among all creatures you threaten. The club is a 2h crush weapon with bewildering blow special attack and the CM (shove) quality.
entries.path: Equipment and Economics/Equipment Types/Gear Upgrades/Unique Weapons/2h Weapons/Pricing/1gp - 2k/Ogre Club.md

entries.title: Crystal Halberd
entries.content: 5,000g This elven halberd is practically weightless and can be swung with incredible speed. The crystal halberd is so lightweight that it is practically weightless. Wielders must use DEX to attack with it, or risk breaking it. This does not decrease its base damage. If you can speak elven, you can whisper to the weapon to unlock the its true potential with the following special attack. Rapid Strike - Make two attacks against the same creature, the second with a -4 penalty. It has improved reach quality and the outmaneuver special attack.
entries.path: Equipment and Economics/Equipment Types/Gear Upgrades/Unique Weapons/2h Weapons/Pricing/2k - 10k/Crystal Halberd.md

entries.title: Heavy Metal
entries.content: 6,000g This oversized guitar is made almost entirely out of iron with chrome plating. This weapon weighs 50 lbs and is hilariously impractical as an instrument. In order to even properly play the guitar, the wielder must possess at least +1 STR. It has the following special attack... Instrumental Destruction Sonic Whack someone with Heavy Metal so hard it breaks and becomes unusable, dealing an additional 2d6 (7) sonic damage. The mana cost of the next technique you use this turn is decreased by your STR score. You must Mana Burn 1 as a FRA to repair it so it can be played/wielded again. The guitar functions as a large hammer-esque 2h crush weapon with the meteor swing special attack and the CM (topple) quality.
entries.path: Equipment and Economics/Equipment Types/Gear Upgrades/Unique Weapons/2h Weapons/Pricing/2k - 10k/Heavy Metal.md

entries.title: Muramana
entries.content: 4,000g Tags: - This spear has a blue glowing sheen to it when held by a creature. The more mana the creature has, the more brightly it shines. Creatures with incredibly high mana cause the spear to ooze liquid mana. As long as you have not spent any mana this turn, you get a bonus to damage based on your current mana. For every 5 mana you currently have, you deal an additional 1 damage. It is a 2h pierce weapon with the Reach and Strings Attached properties.
entries.path: Equipment and Economics/Equipment Types/Gear Upgrades/Unique Weapons/2h Weapons/Pricing/2k - 10k/Muramana.md

entries.title: Rusty Sword
entries.content: 4,000g This curved iron sword looks as though the black iron exterior could peel off at any moment and is covered with patches of rust. The sword has a -2 penalty to damage. When used to cut down creatures that can bleed, it changes. The iron in the blood repairs the weapon and then some. On-hit the rust fades and the blade sharpens, adding +1 damage, up to 4 times for a total of +2 bonus damage. The sword is a 2h slash weapon that has the Bleed Weapon and CM (dirty trick) property.
entries.path: Equipment and Economics/Equipment Types/Gear Upgrades/Unique Weapons/2h Weapons/Pricing/2k - 10k/Rusty Sword.md

entries.title: Sticky Spear
entries.content: 7,000g This long, pointed piece of wood is covered in a sticky sap. The wielder cannot change weapons using Swap Item without first spending a standard action to attach it to another object and pulling it off. However the spear is very fickle. Whenever you disarm a creature, it leaves you behind and hops directly into your target's open hand. It is a 2h pierce weapon with the Reach, CM (disarm) and Attached properties. Special: You use Alchemy or +Nature to craft this item instead of +Profession.
entries.path: Equipment and Economics/Equipment Types/Gear Upgrades/Unique Weapons/2h Weapons/Pricing/2k - 10k/Sticky Spear.md

entries.title: Corrupted Hammers
entries.content: 12,000g The Corrupted is relentless. His repeated strikes debilitate foes. On-hit against a creature who is Clumsy, reduce the creature's speed. The speed reduction is 5 per stack of clumsy they possess. This takes into account if the attack itself inflicts clumsy (ie concussive strike). This penalty lasts until the clumsy is removed. They are a pair of light crush weapons with the thrown weapon property and the hack n smash special attack. Part of the following +Unique Item Sets - Corrupted Set Note - The intention behind this weapon's effect is that the speed penalty will not stack with itself after hitting multiple times. It's more you apply a unique status that slows by 5 per clumsy stack. Ex. Frost giant is clumsy 2. I bonk him with hammers once and they get -10 speed. Hitting him 20 more times doesn't decrease his speed any more until I make him clumsier.
entries.path: Equipment and Economics/Equipment Types/Gear Upgrades/Unique Weapons/2h Weapons/Pricing/10k - 25k/Corrupted Hammers.md

entries.title: Defiled Flail
entries.content: 12,000g The Defiled specialized in cracking through an enemy's defenses. His greatest delight was to prove even the most durable warriors could be crushed. - Ignore all Resistance to your melee attacks made with the flail. - Ignore all DR on your melee attacks made with the flail. The Defiled Flail has 2 enchantment slots It is a 2h crush weapon with the improved reach property and the wild strike special attack. Part of the following +Unique Item Sets - Defiled Set
entries.path: Equipment and Economics/Equipment Types/Gear Upgrades/Unique Weapons/2h Weapons/Pricing/10k - 25k/Defiled Flail.md

entries.title: Infested Spear
entries.content: 12,000g The original Dark Knight, The Infested does not feel alive unless he wades into the heart of the battle. - You get a bonus to Melee Attack damage equal to the number of enemies you threaten. - Your speed increases by 10 whenever you provoke an AoO The Infested Spear has 2 enchantment slots It is a 2h pierce weapon with the improved reach property and the lifesteal special attack. Part of the following +Unique Item Sets - Infested Set
entries.path: Equipment and Economics/Equipment Types/Gear Upgrades/Unique Weapons/2h Weapons/Pricing/10k - 25k/Infested Spear.md

entries.title: Soul Eater
entries.content: 22,000g This black greatsword has a red mana crystal in the shape of an eye affixed to its hilt. Any who hold the blade in their hands can feel its blood lust. While wielding this blade, the wielder has Weakness 2 to all damage, but reduces the mana cost of all techniques by 1. This effect cannot prevent Mana Burn. The wielder gets a +2 bonus to Attack with this weapon. The Soul Eater has 3 enchantment slots It is a 2h slash weapon with the Bleed Weapon and CM (disarm) properties.
entries.path: Equipment and Economics/Equipment Types/Gear Upgrades/Unique Weapons/2h Weapons/Pricing/10k - 25k/Soul Eater.md

entries.title: The Dimensional Anchor
entries.content: 20,000g A literal anchor, clearly for a smaller vessel, that weighs 50 lbs. - The anchor passively alerts whoever holds it whenever teleportation occurs within 100 ft of it. It also alerts the holder when they are within 100 ft of a portal. This detection functions through solid barriers, but not Lead. - Whenever you are subject to a Teleportation effect against your will, you can slam the anchor into the ground as an immediate action to negate it. - Enemies the anchor strikes are Anchored On-hit for 1 minute. The anchor is a 2h crush weapon with the magic crash special attack and the CM (drag) quality.
entries.path: Equipment and Economics/Equipment Types/Gear Upgrades/Unique Weapons/2h Weapons/Pricing/10k - 25k/The Dimensional Anchor.md

entries.title: Wretched Axe
entries.content: 12,000g The Wretched axe demands blood, caring not for whose it is. - The axe has the Vicious quality without consuming an enchantment slot, and the damage you and your target take is increased to 4. This damage can never defeat you. The Wretched Axe has 2 enchantment slots It is a 2h slash weapon with the oversized property and the execution and all in special attacks. Part of the following +Unique Item Sets - Wretched Set
entries.path: Equipment and Economics/Equipment Types/Gear Upgrades/Unique Weapons/2h Weapons/Pricing/10k - 25k/Wretched Axe.md

entries.title: Ogrelord's Whip
entries.content: 1,800g Unlike the standard Whip, this whip is capable of threatening its full reach off turn, but only your allies. When you make an AoO against an ally, it always does 5 slashing damage and increases their speed by 20 this round. You also gain a charge, up to 3. Each charge you can spend as an free action to increase the bonus of your next attack by +2 (max +6). Charges are lost when you roll initiative.
entries.path: Equipment and Economics/Equipment Types/Gear Upgrades/Unique Weapons/Exotic Weapons/Pricing/1gp - 2k/Ogrelord's Whip.md

entries.title: Hunter's Rifle
entries.content: 6,000g This gun has a barrel that grows to comically large size at its tip. You get a +4 bonus on survival checks to track any creature you have hit or grazed with this weapon in the last week. Special Attack: My Brand - On-hit apply a brand to a creature, physically marking them. You and your companion creatures' attacks do an additional d6 damage until the end of your next turn. The rifle functions as a 2h Gun.
entries.path: Equipment and Economics/Equipment Types/Gear Upgrades/Unique Weapons/Exotic Weapons/Pricing/2k - 10k/Hunter's Rifle.md

entries.title: Ray Gun
entries.content: 11,000g This pistol looks like a child's toy, made of colorful plastic with superfluous details. Pulling the trigger reveals otherwise. It blasts rays of pure energy, dealing force damage instead of a pistol's usual damage. It does not need to be reloaded, but it can only fire 5 times per day. Special Attack: Pack a Punch Creatures within 5 ft of the original target take 2d6 (7) force damage and poise damage. If this poise breaks a creature, it is Knocked Down. Functions as a 1h Gun. Special: You use Engineering to craft this item instead of +Profession.
entries.path: Equipment and Economics/Equipment Types/Gear Upgrades/Unique Weapons/Exotic Weapons/Pricing/10k - 25k/Ray Gun.md

entries.title: Demon's Grasp
entries.content: 1,800g This green metallic gauntlet has its fingers honed at the tip like claws. Imprinted on the back of its "hand" is inscribed a slur written in infernal against fiends. It is exceptionally good at capturing the attention of fiends. When attacking with fiendish allies, they are all taunted by the target of your attack On-hit. Fiends you Grapple are automatically Taunted by you. When fighting a fiend, every attack with this weapon taunts on-hit for 1 round (will negates). Allied fiends get a +2 bonus to Attack against any creature you attack with Demons Grasp for 1 turn. The gauntlet is a light slash weapon with the Attached and CM (grapple) qualities.
entries.path: Equipment and Economics/Equipment Types/Gear Upgrades/Unique Weapons/Light Weapons/Pricing/1gp - 2k/Demon's Grasp.md

entries.title: Anal Probe
entries.content: 3,000g This baton looks unimpressive until you press a button on its side to coat it in a strange liquid and electrify it as a swift action. It can remain electrified for 1 minute before it must recharge for 5. When you thrust it, it seems to magnetize to the target's orifices. You must hold it in 2 hands to keep it electrified. It is a 1h light club when not electrified. When electrified it instead deals 2d6 zap dmg. It also gains a special attack. Special Attack: Terrestrial Exploration Shove the probe in whatever hole the creature is most uncomfortable with. They are clumsy 2 on-hit. Special: You can use Engineering to craft this item instead of +Profession.
entries.path: Equipment and Economics/Equipment Types/Gear Upgrades/Unique Weapons/Light Weapons/Pricing/2k - 10k/Anal Probe.md

entries.title: Eye of Moogen
entries.content: 4,000g This knife is molded after the weapons typically wielded by Moogen, The Murderer. A black blade whose hilt bears a resemblance to The Eye of Moogen. - By closing one of your eyes, you can use the knife as a proxy for your own vision, allowing you to peek around corners using the blade. - The knife seems to enjoy cutting into flesh and can be used to administer first aid. - Against a creature you administer first aid, or On-hit, you getting a brief image in your head of the last creature the target killed, if any. The knife is a light pierce weapon with the Chink Magnet and Versatile (slash) property.
entries.path: Equipment and Economics/Equipment Types/Gear Upgrades/Unique Weapons/Light Weapons/Pricing/2k - 10k/Eye of Moogen.md

entries.title: Nosey Knife
entries.content: 4,000g *This knife makes the wielder feel uneasy, like it knows what its thinking. Whenever you Crit a DD creature, the knife randomly chooses a technique the creature knows to memorize, up to your highest tier technique known. While you wield the knife, you also have that technique memorized without spending memory. The knife has a will of its own, and will memorize new techniques if you keep critting whether you want it to or not. The knife is a light pierce weapon with the Conceal and Chink Magnet property.
entries.path: Equipment and Economics/Equipment Types/Gear Upgrades/Unique Weapons/Light Weapons/Pricing/2k - 10k/Nosey Knife.md

entries.title: Overflowing Dagger
entries.content: 9,000g The dagger seems uninteresting unless placed in the hands of a skilled mage, where its true power is revealed. Whenever you cast a Spell, this dagger is empowered. It gains a bonus to damage equal to (the spell's CL)-(your LvL). This damage bonus is stored until you make an attack with the dagger, which then vanishes at end of your next turn. The knife is a light pierce weapon with the Conceal and Chink Magnet property.
entries.path: Equipment and Economics/Equipment Types/Gear Upgrades/Unique Weapons/Light Weapons/Pricing/2k - 10k/Overflowing Dagger.md

entries.title: Boring Blade
entries.content: 200,000g This mundane sword shows its true power only when the user does not use any fancy shmancy techniques with it. If fact, the sword appears to "activate" glowing with power for a minute when using a technique, but counter intuitively, this is how the sword appears when it deactivates. As long as you have not used a technique in the last minute, it is treated as though it has every special weapon property seen in the +Weapons rules page, with the exception of reach and ranged weapon properties. It has conceal, bleed, one and done, etc. You can use every special attack that doesn't have a specific weapon requirement. Boring blade has 3 enchantment slots. The sword is a light pierce weapon with the Chink Magnet and the Attached property.
entries.path: Equipment and Economics/Equipment Types/Gear Upgrades/Unique Weapons/Light Weapons/Pricing/100k+/Boring Blade.md

entries.title: Crystal Bow
entries.content: 6,000g Initially one might question making a bow out of rocks, but the elves that make them put these magical bows to great use. Tags: Su, Force A crystal bow does not require any form of ammunition in order to fire. When the wielder pantomimes pulling an arrow, one forms of pure magic force. If you can speak elven, you can whisper to the weapon to unlock the its true potential with the following special attack. Crystal Shard - This attack deals true damage. It has long range and the volley special attack.
entries.path: Equipment and Economics/Equipment Types/Gear Upgrades/Unique Weapons/Ranged Weapons/Pricing/2k - 10k/Crystal Bow.md

entries.title: Tainted Crossbow
entries.content: 12,000g Against the Tainted, there is always a bolt midflight on its way to its target. The tainted crossbow is a 2h ranged weapon with the Reload property, but it removes the restriction of only firing once per round. It can be fired 4 times before it needs to be reloaded. It has long range and the rapid fire special attack. Part of the following +Unique Item Sets - Tainted Set
entries.path: Equipment and Economics/Equipment Types/Gear Upgrades/Unique Weapons/Ranged Weapons/Pricing/10k - 25k/Tainted Crossbow.md

entries.title: Tome of Knowledge (T1)
entries.content: 7,000g The tome is on the smaller side and decorated in accordance to what knowledge is contained within. While its pages are in view, you feel a cascade of new knowledge dance through your brain, only for it to disappear the moment you put it in your pocket. The tome of knowledge provides you with the knowledge to use any single level 1 technique, chosen when the tome is created. To use the knowledge within, you must study it every morning when you prepare your techniques, it it consumes 2 Memory slots instead of 1. You can only activate the technique when you wield the book in your hand.
entries.path: Equipment and Economics/Equipment Types/Gear Upgrades/Wieldables/Pricing/2k - 10k/Tome of Knowledge (T1).md

entries.title: Tome of Knowledge (T2)
entries.content: 14,000g The tome is on the smaller side and decorated in accordance to what knowledge is contained within. While its pages are in view, you feel a cascade of new knowledge dance through your brain, only for it to disappear the moment you put it in your pocket. The tome of knowledge provides you with the knowledge to use any single level 2 technique, chosen when the tome is created. To use the knowledge within, you must study it every morning when you prepare your techniques, it it consumes 3 Memory slots instead of 1. You can only activate the technique when you wield the book in your hand.
entries.path: Equipment and Economics/Equipment Types/Gear Upgrades/Wieldables/Pricing/10k - 25k/Tome of Knowledge (T2).md

entries.title: Wieldables
entries.content: This is gear that you hold in your hands, but doesn't neatly fall into the category of shield, weapon or spell focus. For now pickings are slim. More to be added as I get more inspiration.

entries.title: +Unique and Magic Weapons
entries.content: A category for things you make attacks with. Includes staves, swords and bows alike. I'm not opposed to changing some of the details of many of these weapons, like making them 2h weapons/light weapons. Or changing their qualities if you're a grapple focused build to give it CM (grapple) instead of one of its default properties. If you're crafting these items, you can change these details as you see fit. If you're purchasing these items, add +10% to the total cost.
entries.path: Equipment and Economics/Equipment Types/Gear Upgrades/+Unique and Magic Weapons.md

entries.title: Gear Upgrades
entries.content: Some magic items have a scaling component to them called Manathirst, growing more powerful when in the hands of someone who is more powerful. Manathirst features are unlocked when you have 12 and 22 max mana, typically kicking in at level 5 and 10. Magic Items come in two main categories: wielded items and worn items. Wielded Items: Weapons, staves and shields. These can be mixed and matched to your hearts content, as long as you have the hands to hold them. Worn Items: These items have dedicated slots. You can only have one worn item in each slot (minus baubles). See chart below for more details. | Type | Description | | --------------------- | -------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | | Armor | Covers the torso, shoulders and most of the arms and legs. Heavier armors require more STR but give more defense. Other types of armor forfeit any AC at all to give other benefits. | | Footwear | Covers the feet, ankles and sometimes shins. Mostly for mobility. | | Gloves | Covers the hand, wrists and sometimes forearms. Used for enhancing skills and offensive abilities. | | Headwear | Covers the head, and sometimes face or neck. Focused on protecting your head from dangerous gasses or loud noise. | | Baubles | Baubles are the etc category where you put trinkets, badges, belts, and the rest. You can have 2 baubles equipped at once. |
entries.path: Equipment and Economics/Equipment Types/Gear Upgrades/Gear Upgrades.md

entries.title: Pricing Gear Upgrades
entries.content: This section is mostly for people who are interested in making up new items, or are curious how I decided pricing. Gear prices are generally calculated by what level the creature is expected to obtain them. I took what I considered the average profession bonus of the a character at a given level and then used that to find the value of 4 weeks of work on the Economics page. I consider an average profession bonus to be 5+1.33(LvL). This estimation assumes that someone will have racial skill bonuses, Skill Focus and other etc additions from class/items/circumstance to eventually increase the skill, hence the 1.3x multiplier instead of 5+LvL. Using this formula, we can make a simple chart. | Level | Skill Bonus | Value Generation (4 weeks, approximated) | | ----- | ----------- | ---------------------------------------- | | 1 | 6 | 2,300 | | 2 | 7 | 2,800 | | 3 | 9 | 4,000 | | 4 | 10 | 4,800 | | 5 | 11 | 5,700 | | 6 | 13 | 7,600 | | 7 | 14 | 8,700 | | 8 | 15 | 9,800 | | 9 | 17 | 12,400 | | 10 | 18 | 13,800 | | 11 | 19 | 15,300 | | 12 | 21 | 18,500 | Now obviously we cant have every item cost this value. Legendary weapons crafted by level 1 guys in few months? Nah. We can take these values and multiply them depending on the rarity, power etc. This will create some overlap, as super powerful items for a level 2 character probably aren't that big a deal for a level 10 character. Multipliers can be 1.5x, 2x, 4x or even 10x, 50x for incredibly powerful or rare items. Cheaper items tend to be things that have limited uses each day, are relatively mundane or have a specific niche.
entries.path: Equipment and Economics/Equipment Types/Gear Upgrades/Pricing Gear Upgrades.md

entries.title: Rock Stats
entries.content: Level 0 Construct Defense Traits | AC | 10 | | | ----- | ------------------------- | --- | | HP | 20 | | | Poise | 15 | | | Saves | Fort +0, Refl +0, Will +0 | | Offense Traits (DC 10) None Weaknesses/Deep Lore Vehicle - Standard rocks are streamlined to be useful as transportation and shipping vehicles, but leave a lot to be desired in terms of their combat capabilities. If attacked, a rock is helpless to stop its attacker aside from running away. As such, rocks must be kept away from danger. Other Traits Ability Scores - STR +0, DEX +0, LUK +0, INT -, WIS +0, CHA +0 Speed - 80 Land Speed, increases to 45mph out of combat. In ideal conditions, such as well-maintained roads, a rock can travel about 1000 miles a day, assuming it travels through the night, or 600 assuming it doesn't. These speeds are halved when moving through areas without well maintained roads, and reduced to 1/4 if you are traveling through wilderness with unwieldy terrain, such as snowy, sandy, mountainous or forested biomes. Some rocks come with specific terrains in mind and ignore such penalties. Autonomous - A rock is programmed to move towards specific well known destinations, in cardinal directions, or to move the path of least resistance towards a set of coordinates. If the rider wishes, there is also a driver's seat that allows them to more finely control the rock's movement. The autonomous driving controls leave a lot to be desired for sleeping passengers. You can get some sleep, but you do not gain the benefits of rest unless you stop to sleep. Seating/Storage - Rocks have compartments that can be easily hidden when not in use, both for seating and for storage. Standard rocks have seating to comfortably accommodate for 20 medium creatures. Such seating is protected from the elements via a weak magical barrier, capable of stopping small insects, wind and rain, but not arrows and swords. They can carry up to 5000 lbs in a compartment that contains 30 inventory slots Hide in Plain Sight - Wherever rocks are common (most places), it requires a DC 25 +perception check to notice a rock is abnormal when it is not in use. A rock can hide any kinds of custom augmentations while camouflaged, including decorative additions like paint jobs.
entries.path: Equipment and Economics/Equipment Types/Goods and Services/Rocks/Rock Stats.md

entries.title: Rocks
entries.content: It's not a boulder, it's a rock! Rocks are the premier transportation and shipping vehicles in the world of Nack. They are incredibly fast, cheap and can plop down anywhere and look natural because they're rocks. Rocks are construct creatures that have their programming optimized for navigation and high speed transportation. More details in the stats subsection. Pricing A standard rock costs 1,500g. If you would only like to rent one, you are typically limited to predesignated paths, such as state roads. It costs 10g for short trips within 600 miles, 30g for something between 2-6 days and 80g+ for anything longer. Prices for augments are included below. Using augmented rocks multiplies adds 50g to the price to rent them, with particularly expensive ones being even more expensive or unavailable for most customers. If the rock is lost, destroyed or damaged, the owner/company that provided the service expects compensation for replacements/repairs. Augments All prices listed are in addition to the base 1,500g price. Offroad - 2,000g Capable of traversing average and less than ideal conditions at full speed. Some regions that require movement types not available to a rock may still impede its speed. Climbing/Swimming - 1,000g Rock is capable of using its full speed while climbing or swimming. Each sold separately. Armored Plating - 10,000g Adds +5 ac, +5 to all saving throws, +10 poise and +20 hp. Flight - 60,000g Rock is capable of using its full speed while flying.
entries.path: Equipment and Economics/Equipment Types/Goods and Services/Rocks/Rocks.md

entries.title: +Goods and Services
entries.content: In order to adventure, you need to eat, sleep and relax after a hard day of getting munched on by wolves. These things are going to steadily eat into your wallet. This section is dedicated to quantifying these basic necessities. That being said, not all restaurants and inns are created equal. Some more expensive ones may have access to special types of services, or provide you with connections to a specific type of clientele for an enormous price hike. | Types | Description | | ------------------------------------------------------------------------- | -------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | | Food/Water | It costs 25g a week to keep yourself fed. Certain features, like proficiency in the survival skill or the Hellion's dirt consumption can significantly decrease or eliminate this cost. 25 gold a week translates to \~110g a month or about \~1,300g a year. | | Lodging | It costs 100g a week to keep a roof over your head, provided you don't already have a house and need to acquire rooms at inns or pay farmers to sleep in barns. Sleeping out in the wilderness costs nothing, but can bring dangers like weather, monsters, thieves and the like. If you already own a house, this cost is decreased to 50g a week. This represents upkeep for your house in the wilderness or upkeep, taxes or loan repayments for your house in a city. 100 gold a week translates to \~440g a month or about \~5,200g a year.\ 50 gold a week translates to \~220g a month or about \~2,600g a year. | | Supplies | Adventuring/travel requires a lot of miscellaneous things, amounting to 25g a week. Adventuring requires many things. You need to keep your swords sharp, your fires lit, your tents and bedrolls without holes etc. You're also gonna need a lot of rope. What counts as "adventuring" doesn't necessarily require you be out in the wilderness and could just as easily involve you snooping around towns gathering info, harassing farmers and exploring haunted houses. As a benefit for paying for supplies, you need not write down many miscellaneous items on your character sheet as you or a party member are assumed to have such things. This usually means something like a hammer, rope, flint and steel etc. 25 gold a week translates to \~110g a month or about \~1,300g a year. | | Recovering from Afflictions | A night at the tavern is usually enough to wash away your woes, getting rid of all T1 afflictions. This usually involves you blowing a good amount of money all at once, costing 100g. General recovery can refer to visiting amusement parks or eating out at a nice restaurant. Such activities must always be performed at some type of "for leisure" location. It doesn't necessarily need to be in a settlement, so it could also include remote locations like hot springs in a mountain. Higher tiers of afflictions will likely require a specialist and a large degree of time. The cost for these services is tied to how long it takes to heal, using the Approximating Gold's Value chart to determine cost. Some afflictions require very specific treatment that might not be generally available. | | Transportation | Speed is usually the name of the game, but might not always be available. Because of the variety in transportation, it is its own subsection. A creature with 30 speed traveling on foot can travel 30 miles a day on a road, 24 miles a day in wilderness or 15 miles a day in dense/difficult terrain like snow, mountains and jungle. Judge overall travel speed based on the weakest link. |
entries.path: Equipment and Economics/Equipment Types/Goods and Services/+Goods and Services.md

entries.title: Gmail
entries.content: People in Nack do not have phones or radio, and therefore need to communicate with people somehow. A common method is the Sending spell, but that is often limited in nature, and requires specific magic knowledge and knowledge about who you contact. Enter Gobbo Mail (shortened to G-Mail), a company that comprises of (mostly) goblins that let you send letters to places all around the world. One can purchase their services for 20g at any number of locations around the globe. They exist in all metropolis and city size settlements, and many villages (see Populations). You can also purchase stamps for the same price that effectively are you preemptively purchasing services to skip any lines, or pay for the responses of people you send letters to. They have the gobbo guarantee that their letter will be delivered in 3 days or less anywhere on the planet. Almost anywhere. Particularly dangerous locations may require more money to convince the couriers, or such requests may be outright denied. The methods they use to travel such distances are closely guarded trade secrets.
entries.path: Equipment and Economics/Equipment Types/Goods and Services/Gmail.md

entries.title: Teleportation Circles
entries.content: A teleportation circle is an undertaking that requires a great deal of upkeep to maintain, with skillful mages on call for when things go wrong. Only the most rich and powerful nations have access to a teleportation circle, and typically only in their capital. The number of teleportation circles in a world shouldn't exceed a dozen. That being said, the process of teleportation between circles is usually an easy, painless and routine task. Teleportation between these circles costs 100g and typically only takes a few minutes of waiting in line. Certain restrictions may be in place during times of war and strife preventing teleportation while they last. In The World of Nack, the Brotherhood of Steel controls the circles.
entries.path: Equipment and Economics/Equipment Types/Goods and Services/Teleportation Circles.md

entries.title: Lead Architecture
entries.content: Lead structures are the most expensive, but most effective way of making sure an area is safe from scrying. This is done by lining the building materials with layers of lead during construction, or as a later add on. Scrying attempts inside of lead structures always fail. Even going near the structure causes scrying magic to be faulty, obscuring the senses. A singular room costs 25,000g An average home costs 60,000g Large or complex structures such as prisons, dungeons or palaces have a starting price of 150,000g, which can easily increase based off the scale of the project. As long as the building is intact, the risk of lead poisoning is basically zero. During times of strife though, the lead can poison the air, making it unsafe to breathe.
entries.path: Equipment and Economics/Equipment Types/Mundane Items/Lead/Lead Architecture.md

entries.title: Lead Container
entries.content: Lead containers are mostly made of other metals like iron, steel, or for the particularly paranoid, adamantine. There is a layer of lead that prevents divinations of the container entirely. To anybody scrying on the area, the container (and its content) is completely invisible. A small container for handheld items like books, the family jewels or light weapons costs 2,000g A medium container for sizable items like great weapons, or your rock collection costs 5,000g A larger container for very large items like humanoid bodies or golden toilets or costs 12,000g Special: Care must be taken when dealing with lead containers. There are methods of detecting lead and it usually indicates somebody has something to hide. In addition, if you place objects on a lead container, one could infer the location of something important since they would appear to be floating in midair while scrying. Lead containers are unsafe to carry around, and inflict lead poisoning on those that do for too long. Holding onto the container for a few days is enough to poison you.
entries.path: Equipment and Economics/Equipment Types/Mundane Items/Lead/Lead Container.md

entries.title: Lead Paint
entries.content: 100g per bucket Lead paint is the most convenient and cost effective way method of preventing scrying from would be spies over large areas. However it is a much more temporary solution. The high concentration of lead in the paint, as well as the additional binding agents, causes the paint to peel much more often and release a toxic gas that can cause problems over time. This requires a monthly cleaning and reapplication to maintain the anti-divination properties. A bucket of paint can cover the average sized room. Larger rooms with high roofs require many buckets. Buckets of paint are typically used for smaller private meeting rooms. Creatures that reside in rooms treated with lead paint are subject to lead poisoning within days of existing in it. If the room is poorly maintained, this can happen with only brief exposure.
entries.path: Equipment and Economics/Equipment Types/Mundane Items/Lead/Lead Paint.md

entries.title: Lead
entries.content: Price Varies Lead seems like an unusual item to catalogue, but it has unique properties that make it desirable despite its drawbacks. Lead has the unique property of interfering with scrying effects. Lead paint is the most convenient method of preventing scrying from would be spies, but it can also be used in other clever ways. For example if you have a treasured item you don't want a spy to see, you can use a metal box lined with lead to cause it to appear invisible to any \[scrying] effects. Particularly rich and paranoid creatures, like wizards an beholders hoard lead in order to cover their entire lairs in it. It only takes a very thin sheet of lead that mostly covers an area to veil it from these pesky divinations. There are various security measures available for sale. Lead Poisoning Lead is toxic to creatures, and using it to prevent scrying has drawbacks. When meeting the conditions for lead poisoning (as noted in the various products) the creature is immediately Drained 5 and has Disadvantage on skill checks until this drain is removed. Continued exposure increases this drained penalty until the creature dies.

entries.title: Dragon Metal
entries.content: 100,000g per item Dragon Metal is often referred to by what its made into rather than as a material more often than not; Something like "dragon plate armor" or "dragon great sword." It is made by combining a rare form of jewel, called dragonstone, with steel. When in the presence of a dragon, the dragonstone absorbs bits of expended mana over time and change color to match the color of the dragon's scales. The dragon metal that is created by this often superficially resembles the dragon whom the dragonstone absorbed its power. When dragon metal is made into a weapon, the weapon has a +2 flat bonus to damage. They also get a bonus special attack, selected from the attached list. When dragon metal is made into a set of medium or heavy armor, it gives resistance to an elemental damage type, based on the dragonstone used in its crafting, defaulting to fire. For light armor see dragonhide.
entries.path: Equipment and Economics/Equipment Types/Mundane Items/Materials/Dragon Metal/Dragon Metal.md

entries.title: Dragon Weapon Special Attacks
entries.content: Sever - This attack gains +2 attack. On-hit sever the connection a creature has to their deity. They cannot invoke deity nor can they use Prayer or Deific Boons until they Rest. Similar abilities that rely on divine power like Paladin smite are unaffected.
entries.path: Equipment and Economics/Equipment Types/Mundane Items/Materials/Dragon Metal/Dragon Weapon Special Attacks.md

entries.title: +Mundane Items
entries.content: Things that you can usually buy in many locations around the world with the correct vendor in town. Many have real life analogues.
entries.path: Equipment and Economics/Equipment Types/Mundane Items/+Mundane Items.md

entries.title: Caltrops
entries.content: 500g Caltrops are small pointy objects designed to harm creatures that walk through areas where they are deployed. Every space a creature moves through occupied by caltrops, they take 2 piercing damage and start Bleeding for 2 damage. A creature can avoid this damage by treating it as difficult terrain. 500g is the price attached to the package of caltrops that can be deployed immediately as a standard action. Treat it as a thrown weapon to determine the distance you can throw it. All spaces within 5 ft of its landing point are covered in these pointy bits. If deployed beforehand carefully over 1 minute, a similar package can cover a 10 ft radius. Caltrops can be swept up in the AoE of many effects, such as aqueous orb, causing them to be rendered ineffective.

entries.title: Holy Water
entries.content: 100g Holy water is an item that is regularly created by churches and sold to commoners as a form of protection against the profane. Many fiends and undead have an aversion to this substance, and can often cure diseases inflicted by them and prevent some of their supernatural abilities. The most common use of holy water is as a weapon. You can throw a small bottle of it as a thrown weapon. It deals 2d6 (7) holy damage on hit. Another use is to remove the bone chilled condition. This requires drinking it using the use potion for yourself or administer potion for else. Many uses of holy water require its direct application, such as on wounds inflicted by the profane, or directly on the remains of the profane to put their spirits to rest.
entries.path: Equipment and Economics/Equipment Types/Mundane Items/Holy Water.md

entries.title: Property
entries.content: Property is synonymous with land, and everything on it. Property can include facilities, homes or it can just look pretty. The price of property changes based on a few factors: Size, buildings, appearance, location and the overall state its in can influence its price. Small bits of land with little intrinsic value generally go pretty cheap. You can buy an acre of unworked "wild land" for 10,000g Premium land, such as places that could be good spots for attractions or farmland are a bit more expensive. You can buy an acre of premium land for 50,000g A small home that can comfortably fit a single person costs 10,000g An average home that can comfortably fit a family costs 25,000g A luxurious home, like a mansion with many rooms for family and guests, leisure rooms for toys and sports and large outdoor yards costs 100,000g Large or complex structures such as prisons, dungeons or palaces have a starting price of 250,000g, which can easily increase based off the contents.

entries.title: Tools
entries.content: 80g Tools typically come in sets catered to a specific task, such as fishing, lockpicking or medicine. Some tasks require the use of tools, others only give a penalty if you don't have tools. Tool kits weigh 10 lbs and require 2 inventory slots. Some tasks, such as smithing, require even more advanced tools you can't carry around like a forge.

entries.title: Torch
entries.content: 20g Torches are sticks wrapped with some type of cloth, chemical or vegetable matter that is flammable. Torches give off Bright Light in a 20 ft radius around them, and Dim Light 20 ft past that. They can stay lit for up to 2 hours. Lighting and dimming a torch repeatedly can significantly decrease the amount of time they stay lit. If a torch ends up getting wet, it becomes unusable. If a torch ends up clattering to the ground from being knocked over or disarmed, roll a DC 10 luck check to see if it goes out, using the luckiest fellow benefiting from it in the vicinity if it was not wielded by anybody in specific. Each torch requires 1 inventory slot.

entries.title: +Exotic Weapons
entries.content: Inherently function differently than other weapons and require a more nuanced description to describe their functionality. These weapons get no additional weapon points to customize with. Exotic weapons consumed inventory slots based on how many hands they require to wield, either 1 or 2.
entries.path: Equipment and Economics/Equipment Types/Weapons/Exotic Weapons/+Exotic Weapons.md

entries.title: Bola
entries.content: Bola - Crush, Ammunition, CM (Topple) (2 lbs, 20g) - Thrown weapon - Bolas can be used to topple from a range. - Bolas can only be used once per combat as they need to be untangled to be used properly

entries.title: Boomerang
entries.content: Boomerang - Light, Thrown (5 lbs, 500g) - Boomerangs return to the user when thrown automatically, allowing multiple attacks with a single thrown weapon. - Boomerangs curve, allowing you to hit opponents with total cover, so long as you aim for the correct space - Boomerangs do not return when caught with something like a goo dragon's sticky quality - Special Attack: Wait For It... - Throw a boomerang at an unusual angle, initially missing a target. At the start of your next turn make an attack against them, but they're DD.

entries.title: Gun
entries.content: Guns come in 3 variants: Pistol, rifle and blunderbuss Pistol - 2d6 pierce, Reload (5 lbs, 500g) - Short range - Requires an even more complex reloading process than crossbows. Their reload requires a FRA - Pistols are 1h weapons, but they cannot be used to dual wield - Also comes in a 2h rifle version that has the 2h property and does 2d8 dmg (10 lbs, 800g) Blunderbuss - 2d6 pierce, 2h, Reload (10 lbs, 800g) - Requires an even more complex reloading process than crossbows. Their reload requires a FRA - Simultaneously attacks every creature in a 15ft cone, comparing a single attack roll to all affected creatures' AC - Does not provoke AoOs from shooting in melee - Not compatible with special attacks or features that increase range (such as Artillery)

entries.title: Harpoon
entries.content: Harpoon - d10 pierce, 2h, Bleed (15 lbs, 500g) - Thrown weapon - Harpoons come with a particularly strong 40 ft rope attached, allowing you to drag the weapon back to you in case you miss (standard action). Holding onto this rope after you toss uses a hand. - Harpoons stick into the target. The bleed from this weapon cannot be stopped until after it is removed with Breakout as a swift action. The target also can't move farther than 40 ft away from you without forcing you somehow. - While embedded, you can make a drag attacks against the target without penalty from being at a range. The target can do the same against you.

entries.title: Lance
entries.content: Lance - Pierce, 2h, Reach (10 lbs, 500g) - While mounted, a lance can be wielded in one-hand while still benefiting from 2h damage. - Special Attack: Joust - When you make this attack while mounted and during a Core Rules, How to Play/Actions/Common Actions/Full Round Actions/Charge, your movement is elusive against your target. On-hit dismounts the target.

entries.title: Net
entries.content: Net - CM (Drag) (5 lbs, 100g) - Thrown weapon - Nets deal no damage, but instead inflict the entangled condition On-hit - The entangled condition can be removed via Breakout as a standard action, or automatically with a Melee Attack if wielding a slash weapon. - You still benefit from the CM (Drag) feature of the net as long as the creature is entangled. - You can use nets to make drag combat maneuvers against targets within 20 ft. When you drag with a net, you forfeit entangling your target - Nets can only be used once per combat since they must be refolded again to be effective

entries.title: Shield (W)
entries.content: Shield - d8 crush, 1h (15 lbs, 50g) - A large chunk of wood or metal with handles on one side to affix to your arm. Can be used to make shield actions. - Comes with the CM (shove) quality - Gives 5 poise - Also comes in a tower shield variant, giving additional shield actions and 10 poise. (40 lbs, 100g) - More details under shields page

entries.title: Staff
entries.content: Staff - d8 crush, 1h (5 lbs, 150g) - A well crafted stick either made with wood infused with magic, or has an object affixed to its end to channel magic power. Can be used to cast spells. - Also comes in a light variant (d6, 2 lbs , 120g) and a 2h battlestaff variant (2d6, 10 lbs, 200g) - When you wield 2 staves, or have a staff and some other Staff Component, you get a +1 boost to your CL

entries.title: Whip
entries.content: Whip - Slash, Light, CM (Drag), CM (Topple) (2lbs, 200g) - Whips can be used to attack with 10 ft of extra reach during your turn. You do not Threaten these spaces when it isn't your turn. - You can use the whip to grab objects and pull them adjacent to you, provided you would normally have been able to lift them.

entries.title: Base Special Attacks
entries.content: All weapons come with some of these special attacks, based on the damage type they deal, or their weapon type (ranged, melee, shield etc). Special attacks by default recharge after being out of combat for 1 minute. When used out of combat (for the utility), you can use them an unlimited number of times. You can swap weapons to gain more special attacks, but you cannot use special attacks with the same name more than once in the same combat. Most special attacks require a melee attack, unless the weapon is ranged. The ALL in these subcategories refer to only manufactured weapons, not natural attacks and unarmed attacks. All Slash Melee Special Attack: Lacerate - On the hits apply d6 bleed damage for 2 turns (fort negates) Special Attack: Cleave - Attack two separate creatures you threaten from your current position. All Crush melee Special Attack: Concussive Strike - On hit apply clumsy 1. Special Attack: Backbreaker - Deal 15 poise damage on-hit. All Pierce Melee Special Attack: Lunge - Increase your reach by 5 ft for this attack and all others made this turn Special Attack: Pierce Defense - Ignore DR. If your opponent uses an immediate or instant action in response to this attack, gain +4 attack. All Ranged Special Attack: Maim - On hit reduce the creature's speed by 15ft for one round (fort negates). Special Attack: Snipe - Ignore -2 penalty to attack for a long ranged shot All Shields Special Attack: Endure - Slam a target with your shield and continue to hold it up, focusing on them. Gain resistance to all damage from this target for 1 round, even if you miss. Special Attack: Shield Bash - Inflict clumsy 1 on hit.
entries.path: Equipment and Economics/Equipment Types/Weapons/Special Attacks/Base Special Attacks.md

entries.title: Unique Special Attacks
entries.content: This section is dedicated to helping you fill in the "special attack" weapon quality, or for giving unique weapons special abilities. See the Base Special Attacks section for the special attacks weapons get by default. All Melee Special Attack: Determination - Focus your conviction. You get a +4 bonus and Seeking to your next attack with this weapon by the end of your next turn. You can also choose to not attack at all with this special attack, granting your next attack with this weapon a +10 bonus instead. If you choose to delay this bonus, Determination does not consume your special attack next round. Special Attack: Stacking Strike - Make an attack. If this defeats the creature, stacking strike permanently gains 1 damage. Weapon specific, so you have to stack weapons individually. You cannot farm stacks passively and must gain them in combats you actually roll out. Special Attack: Bewildering Blow - On-hit creature is confused for one round (will negates). Special Attack: Magic Crash - Roll an attack roll in place of a Dispel check against a magical effect you can attack, including one on a creature. Special Attack: Meteor Swing - On hit your target is unable to make AoOs for 1 round. Special Attack: Ankle Slicer - On hit your target has -15 speed for 1 round. Special Attack: Lifesteal (Healing) - On hit heal (2+LvL). Special Attack: Throat Slicer - On hit silence a creature for one round (fort negates). Special Attack: All In - On hit instead of rolling for damage, you deal maximum damage. If you graze, you instead miss entirely. When you miss, you take 20 poise damage. Weapon Specific The above special attacks can be generalized to any type of weapon. Even the "slicer" special attacks could be achieved just as effectively if you crushed a throat or foot. In general I try to be lax with what weapons you give your character so mechanics to not interfere with flavor. However, the following weapons will specify a type of weapon you lock into, and are incompatible with each other. Special Attack: Volley - Fire many shots at once, targeting a 15 ft cone in front of you. Compare a single attack roll to all creatures in AoE. This requires a bow, which is a 2h ranged weapon without the reload property. Special Attack: Rapid Fire - Fire two shots in rapid succession at the same creature, each with a -2 penalty to attack. This requires a bow, which is a 2h ranged weapon without the reload property. Special Attack: Wild Strike - Swing around a flail without concern for the consequences. Deals full damage on a graze. If you miss, you instead treat the attack as if you did half damage to yourself. This requires a flail, which is a 1h or 2h melee crush weapon. Special Attack: Hack n Smash - If a creature takes a shield action in response to this attack, they are made clumsy 2 on hit. They cannot use shield actions until this clumsy is removed. This requires a hammer, which is a 1h or 2h melee crush weapon. Special Attack: Outmaneuver - While not being flanked, move 5 ft elusive and make a melee attack. This requires a polearm, which 2h melee reach weapon. Special Attack: Dig Deep - Embed your weapon into your foe punch it in further, making them clumsy 2. They can remove it as a standard action, or a swift action with +Medicine proficiency. You let go of the weapon as part of using this special attack. This requires a pick style weapon, which is a 1h or 2h melee pierce weapon. Special Attack: Execution - Finish them. If this attack defeats the target, they stay defeated for at least 1 minute unless someone administers first aid. This requires a heavy sword or axe style weapon, which is a 2h slashing weapon. Special Attack: Overrun - Move your opponent 5 ft in a direction that isn't towards you on hit. This requires a heavy ramming or hammer type weapon, which is a 2h crush weapon.
entries.path: Equipment and Economics/Equipment Types/Weapons/Special Attacks/Unique Special Attacks.md

entries.title: Added Special Attack
entries.content: Points: 1 Gain an additional special attack from the Unique Special Attacks list.
entries.path: Equipment and Economics/Equipment Types/Weapons/Weapon Qualities/+Qualities for All Weapons/Added Special Attack.md

entries.title: Dueling
entries.content: Points: 1 Gain +2 to feint when wielding this weapon
entries.path: Equipment and Economics/Equipment Types/Weapons/Weapon Qualities/+Qualities for All Weapons/Dueling.md

entries.title: Parrying
entries.content: Points: 1 Gives a +2 bonus to attack when you parry while wielding this weapon
entries.path: Equipment and Economics/Equipment Types/Weapons/Weapon Qualities/+Qualities for All Weapons/Parrying.md

entries.title: Strings Attached
entries.content: Points: 1 When disarmed, your weapon cannot go further than 5 ft from you. You can re-equip the weapon as a swift action (elusive).
entries.path: Equipment and Economics/Equipment Types/Weapons/Weapon Qualities/+Qualities for All Weapons/Strings Attached.md

entries.title: Ammunition Weapon
entries.content: Points: 2 Can be drawn as a free action an unlimited number of times per turn. Sold/carried in units of 50. Also grants the thrown property. You do not need to wield an ammunition weapon, and it can be thrown directly from the inventory as long as you have a free hand.
entries.path: Equipment and Economics/Equipment Types/Weapons/Weapon Qualities/Light Qualities/Ammunition Weapon.md

entries.title: Chink Magnet
entries.content: Points: 1 When not Dual Wielding, your attacks against DD targets automatically crit. Note: Some weapon qualities, like chink magnet, require you to not dual wield. You can still wield 2 weapons, but you only get the bonus during turns you only attack with one.
entries.path: Equipment and Economics/Equipment Types/Weapons/Weapon Qualities/Light Qualities/Chink Magnet.md

entries.title: Conceal
entries.content: Points: 1 +4 to conceal item. Drawing a weapon with the conceal property to use with +Sleight of Hand does not require a swift action
entries.path: Equipment and Economics/Equipment Types/Weapons/Weapon Qualities/Light Qualities/Conceal.md

entries.title: Light Weapon
entries.content: Points: 0 Can use DEX instead of STR when making an Attack Roll. Automatically comes with light weapons. unarmed attacks, natural attacks and spell attacks that don't involve weapons also have this property.
entries.path: Equipment and Economics/Equipment Types/Weapons/Weapon Qualities/Light Qualities/Light Weapon.md

entries.title: Thrown Weapon
entries.content: Points: 1 Weapon can be Thrown.
entries.path: Equipment and Economics/Equipment Types/Weapons/Weapon Qualities/Light Qualities/Thrown Weapon.md

entries.title: Bleed Weapon
entries.content: Points: 2 Applies d6 Bleeding damage On-hit for 1 round.
entries.path: Equipment and Economics/Equipment Types/Weapons/Weapon Qualities/Melee Qualities/Bleed Weapon.md

entries.title: CM
entries.content: Points: 1 Gain a +2 bonus a specific combat maneuver while you wield this weapon.
entries.path: Equipment and Economics/Equipment Types/Weapons/Weapon Qualities/Melee Qualities/CM.md

entries.title: Versatile
entries.content: Points: 1 The weapon can be used to deal an additional damage type. This also gives access to more Base Special Attacks.
entries.path: Equipment and Economics/Equipment Types/Weapons/Weapon Qualities/Melee Qualities/Versatile.md

entries.title: One and Done
entries.content: Points: 1 When not Dual Wielding, get +2 Attack and the damage dice of the weapon increases to d10. Note: Some weapon qualities, like One and Done, require you to not dual wield. You can still wield 2 weapons, but you only get the bonus during turns you only attack with one.
entries.path: Equipment and Economics/Equipment Types/Weapons/Weapon Qualities/One-Handed Qualities/One and Done.md

entries.title: Long Range
entries.content: Points: 2 Increases the range of the weapon to Long.
entries.path: Equipment and Economics/Equipment Types/Weapons/Weapon Qualities/Ranged Qualities/Long Range.md

entries.title: Reload
entries.content: Points: 0 Can only be fired once per turn. Requires a standard action to reload. Change dmg to 2d6
entries.path: Equipment and Economics/Equipment Types/Weapons/Weapon Qualities/Ranged Qualities/Reload.md

entries.title: Short Range
entries.content: Points: 0 Short is the default range for a ranged weapon.
entries.path: Equipment and Economics/Equipment Types/Weapons/Weapon Qualities/Ranged Qualities/Short Range.md

entries.title: Finesse
entries.content: Points: 1 This 2h weapon counts as a Light Weapon, allowing you to use DEX to attack and damage. Its base damage decreases from 2d8 to 2d6.
entries.path: Equipment and Economics/Equipment Types/Weapons/Weapon Qualities/Two-Handed Qualities/Finesse.md

entries.title: Improved Reach
entries.content: Points: 2 Gain 5 feet of reach with this weapon
entries.path: Equipment and Economics/Equipment Types/Weapons/Weapon Qualities/Two-Handed Qualities/Improved Reach.md

entries.title: Oversized
entries.content: Points: 1 Gives a +1 bonus to the weapons damage. Whenever you miss an attack (does not count graze), you become clumsy 1.
entries.path: Equipment and Economics/Equipment Types/Weapons/Weapon Qualities/Two-Handed Qualities/Oversized.md

entries.title: Attached
entries.content: Points: 1 Can only be used when attached to something else and you wield. Typically attached weapons are things like hook hands, shield/armor spikes and bayonets. gives DR 20 vs disarm.
entries.path: Equipment and Economics/Equipment Types/Weapons/Weapon Qualities/Unusual Qualities/Attached.md

entries.title: +Weapons
entries.content: See also Natural Attack and Unarmed Attack Weapons come in 4 main categories: light, 1-handed, 2-handed, ranged. The weapon type determines how many additional qualities it can have, its weight and its damage. I've made a handful of example weapons in the next page. Exotic weapons break the mold. All weapons are given points based on their type, they are all listed here for your convenience. | Type | Weight | DMG | Weapon Points | Inventory Slots | Description | | ------ | --------------------------------- | --- | ------------- | ------------------------------ | ------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------ | | Light | 2 | d6 | 2 | 1 | Can use DEX instead of STR. Automatically comes with light weapons. unarmed attacks, natural attacks and spell attacks that don't involve weapons also have this property. | | 1h | 5 | d8 | 2 | 1 | Can be used with one hand | | 2h | 10 | 2d8 | 3 | 2 | Must be wielded in two hands | | Ranged | 5 (1 per 10 pieces for ammuntion) | d8 | 2 | 2 | Can only be used to make ranged attacks. Require two-hands | ANY Weapon | Quality | Description | Points | | -------------------- | --------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | ------ | | Added Special Attack | Gain an additional special attack from the Unique Special Attacks list. | 1 | | Dueling | Gain +2 to feint when wielding this weapon | 1 | | Strings Attached | When disarmed, your weapon cannot go further than 5 ft from you. You can re-equip the weapon as a swift action elusive. | 1 | | Parrying | Gives a +2 bonus to attack when you parry while wielding this weapon. | 1 | Ranged Qualities | Quality | Description | Points | | ------- | ---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | ------- | | Short | Can be shot up to 40 ft, or 80 feet with -2 | Default | | Long | Can be shot up to 60 ft, or 120 with -2 | 2 | | Reload | Can only be fired once per turn. Requires a standard action to reload. Change dmg to 2d6 | 0 | ANY Melee | Quality | Description | Points | | --------- | ----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | ------ | | CM (X) | Does 2 additional poise damage with a specific combat maneuver when wielded. Also applies to bully attacks. | 1 | | Versatile | Your weapon can deal 2 different physical damage types, also giving it access to more special attacks. | 1 | | Bleed | Applies d6 bleed On-hit for 1 round | 2 | Light | Quality | Description | Points | | ------------ | -------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | ------ | | Thrown | Can be Thrown 20, or 40 feet with -2. | 1 | | Conceal | +4 to conceal item. Drawing a weapon with the conceal property to use with sleight of hand does not require a swift action | 1 | | Chink Magnet | When not Dual Wielding, your attacks against DD targets automatically crit.This benefit only applies when the weapon is used to make melee attacks | 1 | | Ammunition | Can be drawn as a free action an unlimited number of times per turn. Sold/carried in units of 50.Also grants the thrown property.You do not need to wield an ammunition weapon, and it can be thrown directly from the inventory as long as you have a free hand. | 2 | Note: Some weapon qualities, like chink magnet, require you to not dual wield. You can still wield 2 weapons, but you only get the bonus during turns you only attack with one. One-Handed | Quality | Description | Points | | ------------ | ------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | ------ | | One and Done | When not Dual Wielding, get +2 Attack and the damage dice increases to d10These benefits only apply when the weapon is used to make melee attacks | 1 | | Thrown | Can be Thrown 20, or 40 feet with -2. | 1 | Two-Handed | Quality | Description | Points | | -------------- | ------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | ------ | | Improved Reach | Gain 5 feet of reach with this weapon | 2 | | Finesse | This 2h weapon counts as a light weapon, allowing you to use DEX to attack and damage. Its base damage decreases from 2d8 to 2d6. | 1 | | Oversized | Gives a +1 bonus to the weapons damage. Whenever you miss an attack (does not count graze), you become clumsy 1. | 1 | Weird Some qualities feel out of place on general categories. Use as appropriate. | Quality | Description | Points | | -------- | ----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | ------ | | Attached | Can only be used when attached to something else and you wield.Typically attached weapons are things like hook hands, shield/armor spikes and bayonets.DR 20 vs disarm. | 1 |

entries.title: Improvised Weapons
entries.content: An improvised weapon can be anything relatively dangerous when thrown or swung. It could mean a stick, a rock, a chair or a shiv. Improvised weapons function as one of the following weapon types. Light (shiv)- D6 dmg, Light Weapon property 1h (stick)- d8 dmg 2h (chair)- 2d8 dmg Throwing (rock) - D6 dmg, Thrown After any turn you make an attack with an improvised weapon, roll a DC 10 Luck Check to see if it breaks.

entries.title: Inventory
entries.content: Separate from Carry Weight, which determines raw lifting ability, inventory represents how much stuff you can carry on your person. These represented by inventory slots. By default characters have 20 of (or more with high STR). Other modifiers, such as size can affect inventory, with each size over medium granting +4 slots and each size under subtracting 4. Every item from the biggest sword to the tiniest potion has an inventory slot stat, representing how annoying it is to carry around with a minimum of 1. Items do not consume inventory slots when you have them equipped, such as your armor. I think basic adventuring supplies, such sleeping bags, tents, rations for short journeys and such should not require inventory slots. Any such thing with abnormal qualities or bulk however should. Common pieces of equipment consume the following number of inventory slots... | Equipment | Slots | | ------------------------------------------------------ | ------------------------------------------------ | | Light/1h Weapons | 1 | | 2h weapons (including most range weapons) | 2 | | Light Armor | 1 | | Med/Heavy Armor | 2 | | Buckler | 1 | | Shields | 2 | | Most baubles, boots, gloves and headwear | 1 | | Potions, toxins and other alchemical items | 1 | | Staff Component | 1 (or 2 if also a 2h weapon) | | Other Items | | | 1 tiny creature | 1 | | 1 small creature | 2 | | 1 medium creature | ~5 | | 1 large creature | ~20 | | 1 huge creature | ~50 | When denoting items on your character sheet, you can do the following to visually help represent filled inventory slots. | Slots | Item | | ------------------------------------------------ | ----------------------------------------------- | | 1 | Spare greatsword | | 2 | * | | 3 | Spare heavy armor | | 4 | * | | 5 | Bola | | 6 | Bola | | 7 | Beeg rock | | 8 | * | | Etc. | * | If you prefer physical sheets, doing something similar like drawing a line down the numbered lines for each additional slot an item takes up works too.

entries.title: Jizzle Physicks
entries.content: Inventory Slots: 1 A core part of a life of adventure is taking damage, and your friendly neighborhood alchemists have you covered. As part of your starting gear (or a fee of 50g if you lose yours) you gain access to a flask of wondrous physick with a regenerating ooze creature inside of it called a jizzle. When called upon, your jizzle will do its best to repair your body. Functions as follows... | Action | Standard to drink yourself or to administer it to another creature | | ------ | ----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | | Effect | Heal to max hp after 3 turns.Stuns for 3 rounds as it snaps your bones back into place and clogs your veins before returning to its container. If you take hp damage or are poise broken during these rounds, you vomit it out immediately, providing no benefits. | | Quirks | Jizzle physicks are personalized to an extent. It requires a creature's blood, and a 1 hour alchemical procedure for a jizzle physick to acclimate to a new user, otherwise it does not heal. | Dev Note: It may seem like this cheapens the experience of adventuring a bit. That is because it does. Between Mana Burn and Afflictions and other Permanent Status, it isn't like there aren't methods that grind you down as you adventure. If I did not include the jizzle, then mana regeneration combined with spells like Cure Wounds and Paladin's Lay on Hands would be the substitute "stand around for a while and heal back to full hp." The Jizzle is just a simple way to circumvent the need to check the "someone on our team has unlimited healing" box.

entries.title: Prices+Weight of Common Equipment
entries.content: Characters start off with 1,000 gold. | Equipment Type | Weight (lbs) | Cost | Inventory Slots | | -------------- | --------------- | ------------ | --------------- | | Light Armor | 10 | 50g | 1 | | Medium Armor | 25 | 200g | 2 | | Heavy Armor | 50 | 800g | 2 | | Buckler | 5 | 20g | 1 | | Heavy Shield | 15 | 50g | 2 | | Tower Shield | 40 | 100g | 3 | | Light Weapon | 2 | 20g | 1 | | 1h Weapon | 5 | 50g | 1 | | 2h Weapon | 10 | 100g | 2 | | Ranged Weapon | 5 | 100g | 2 | | Ammo Weapon | 1 per 10 pieces | 1g per piece | 1 | | Gold pieces | 10 per 1000 | | 2 |

entries.title: Repairing Equipment
entries.content: While there aren't any explicit rules that allow you to break equipment, to maintain realism, some circumstances can cause your equipment to break. This could be using your sword to cut down a tree and lose its edge, or using a shield to block a +Siege Weapon. When equipment is broken, but not completely destroyed, it becomes unusable for its intended purpose. Any passive abilities (like a shield's Poise) or active abilities (like a sword's cleave) for broken equipment do not function. It requires 10% of the items value in appropriate materials and an appropriate skill for repairing said item (usually +Profession). This takes however long it would take someone with that skill to make that much money (see Economics for more details). When an item is destroyed, then it cannot be repaired through traditional means. If it can be repaired at all, it would require special crafting components and a skilled craftsman.

entries.title: The Black Market
entries.content: The black market is not a singular place, but rather a series of vaguely connected organizations that span most of the populated world. Mostly organized by criminals, the black market seeks to serve items that are generally not socially acceptable, or even legal, to sell. This could include things like slaves, blood, connections to Fiends and other such unscrupulous items. Anyone with the +Local skill is aware of the black market, but it generally takes a contact in order to get access to the black market. Street reputation goes a long way in determining your access. If you are known to bust criminals and hunt down bandits for local governments, direct access is typically impossible. Often times, the black market isn't available unless you are in a City, as per Populations. Though criminals can be convinced to use creative ways to move items where they are needed if the price is right.
entries.path: Equipment and Economics/Equipment Types/The Black Market.md

entries.title: Equipment
entries.content: This section of the website is dedicated to MONEY and what you're going to be spending it on. Because money makes the world go round, its also got some lore baked into it. See backgrounds, +Weapons and Prices+Weight of Common Equipment if you're here for character creation.

entries.title: Antagonize
entries.content: Requirements - You can taunt a creature for one round instead of making them shaken when you demoralize them. You can also use +Deception just like you would +Intimidation in order to achieve this effect. The opponent rolls a will save with a DC of (10+your deception bonus). You can continue this taunt by spending a swift action each turn, as long as you are still within 30 ft of your target.

entries.title: Scapegoat
entries.content: Requirements - Antagonize When you use the antagonize feat to taunt a creature, you can instead make it taunted by any ally the enemy can see.

entries.title: Directed Disarm
entries.content: Requirements - Whenever you disarm a creature, you can choose a space within 15 ft for the disarmed item to land.

entries.title: Linebacker
entries.content: Requirements - * You shove creatures 10 ft farther * If your foe impacts a solid surface or an object when you shove them, they break it if is of average or lesser toughness (see Breaking Objects. * If you shove a creature into an object or surface, they also take 5 damage (usually crush, adjust as appropriate). If the object or surface is particularly dangerous, like a weapon rack or a glass window, then the damage increases to 10. * If you shove a creature into another creature, they both take 5 crush damage.

entries.title: Maneuver Master
entries.content: Requirements - - Whenever poise break a creature, it provokes AoOs from all of your allies minus you. This can only happen once each round per ally. - You ignore the resistance to poise damage and immunity to being poise broken that huge and massive creatures possess.

entries.title: Maneuver Versatility
entries.content: Requirements - As an instant action 1/round, gain +4 to your next attack that is a +Combat Maneuver. You can replace simple attacks with combat maneuvers, unless it is an additional attack granted by Dual Wielding.

entries.title: Mine!
entries.content: Requirements - * When you disarm a creature, you can immediately wield the weapon you steal. If you don’t have a free hand, you can drop equipment to hold the disarmed gear. * You get a +2 bonus to Attack with weapons you have disarmed from your opponents this combat. * You get a +2 bonus to CL with a spell focus you have disarmed from your opponents this combat.

entries.title: Swift Grappler
entries.content: Requirements - Maintaining a grapple only requires one attack from your Multiattack or a swift action.

entries.title: Tangled
entries.content: Requirements - While grappling a creature, gain DR 5 vs poise damage and +2 AC.

entries.title: The Mountain
entries.content: Requirements - - Your Shove distance increases by 5 ft - You can make shove attacks as AoOs - You get a +8 bonus to Attack if you shove as an AoO - Whenever a creature fails to break your Poise with a combat maneuver, you can shove them as an AoO if they are within Reach - Whenever your poise is broken, your maximum poise increases by 10 for the remainder of the combat. This effect can stack.

entries.title: Bullseye
entries.content: Trade rate of fire/movement for increased accuracy and range. Requirements - Carefully line up a ranged attack and increase the action required to make it to a FRA. Ignore the Attack penalty for shooting in the second range increment. If your target is in the first increment, instead gain +2 attack. For effects that don't have range increments, such as spells, you can increase the action to cast to a FRA to gain the +2 bonus to attack.

entries.title: Clustered Shots
entries.content: You learn to strike at a foes weak spot after making an opening. Requirements - On-hit with a Ranged Attack, apply a unique Status to the foe. Your ranged attacks beyond the first that applied this status ignore DR and deal 2 bonus damage for the remainder of attacks this combat.

entries.title: Parting Shot
entries.content: Requirements - When you take the disengage action, you make a simple ranged attack.

entries.title: Point Blank Master
entries.content: Requirements - Dex +1 Your ranged attacks are elusive. Any Special Attack that specifies it can be used with a Melee Attack can now be used with a Ranged Attack if the target is within your natural reach. Some additional exceptions for what this ability can and can't be combined with can be made as appropriate.
entries.path: Feats/Combat Feats/Ranged Combat Feats/Point Blank Master.md

entries.title: Snap Shot
entries.content: Requirements - Point Blank Master You threaten spaces within your natural reach with ranged weapons.

entries.title: Sniper
entries.content: Requirements - +Stealth proficiency After making a ranged attack (or a ranged multiattack), you can hide as an immediate action, rolling stealth against only your target(s). If you succeed, they are unaware of your position, though can still assume your general cardinal direction.

entries.title: Suppressing Fire
entries.content: Requirements - As a standard action, you can prepare to cover an ally's movement using a ranged weapon you can make ranged attacks with. They are treated as though they took the disengage action on their next turn. When your ally leaves a threatened space, you can make a single Simple Attack against the threatening creature.

entries.title: Trick Shot
entries.content: Requirements - You can make combat maneuvers with ranged attacks at a -4 penalty. You are limited to bully, shove, topple, disarm.

entries.title: Lightning Reload
entries.content: Requirements - Rapid Reload Your hands are a blur while reloading, cranking back crossbows and loading bullets with such incredible dexterity it borders superhuman. Crossbows - You can spend 1 mana as an instant action. Reloading with your crossbow no longer requires any actions for 1 round. Guns - You can reload your gun as an instant action by spending 1 mana for each shot.

entries.title: Rapid Reload
entries.content: Requirements - A weapon with the reload property can be shot and reloaded as a standard action, or a FRA with a Gun. If the weapon isn't loaded, you can reload it then fire it as a standard action (or FRA with gun).

entries.title: Shield Focus
entries.content: Requirements - You can use a shield action as an instant action instead of an immediate action 1/round. Gain a new shield action, usable as an immediate action. Shield Crash - When a creature misses you or grazes you with a melee attack, you can inflict Knockdown (fort negates).

entries.title: The Wall
entries.content: Requirements - Shield Focus When you wield 2 shields * You can use the shield special attacks (shield bash/endure) any number of times per battle. * Gain a +2 bonus to AC against any enemy you attack with a shield for 1 round. * Tank Up does not cost any movement to activate and it does not reduce your speed, effectively allowing you to always tank up, but still only ever in 1 direction.

entries.title: +Combat Feats
