entries.content: A combat feat is only useful during combat, or is at least more skewed towards being useful during combat.

entries.title: Ambrosia
entries.content: Requirements - Wis +1 or a Deific Boon from Frogg Saran Tags: Healing You are gifted a pouch from your deity. At the end of each rest, if the pouch is empty, it is filled with food from your god. The ambrosia can be eaten using the same action as Use Potion on yourself or administer potion on another creature. It heals (7+2*LvL) and nourishes as a full meal.

entries.title: Back to the Wall
entries.content: Requirements - - You cannot be flanked as long as you are adjacent to a wall. - You are considered flanking a creature that is adjacent to a wall.

entries.title: Blind Fight
entries.content: Requirements - * You are not DD while blinded. * You have no Attack penalty when attacking a creature with Partial Concealment. * You only have -2 attack penalty when attacking a creature with Total Concealment.

entries.title: Bodyguard
entries.content: Requirements - - When an ally is moved out of your natural reach (via its own volition or not), you can move as an instant action so that it is again in your natural reach. - You can use the Get Down! action as an instant action. Any movement you take using this feat counts against your movement for your next turn. If you cannot reach your ally, you can move as close as you can get. You can position yourself in the most advantageous way around your ally, assuming you have the movement.

entries.title: Bonded Weapon
entries.content: Requirements - Tags: Su, Teleportation Gain the ability to perform a 10 minute ritual that allows you to bond with 2 specific weapons. - You gain DR 10 against disarm combat maneuvers made against these weapons - When Thrown, they automatically return to your hand after the attack is resolved if desired - If these weapons somehow manage to leave your possession otherwise, you can spend a swift action to have them return to your hand(s)

entries.title: Bonk
entries.content: Requirements - Level 3 You gain the following special attack, usable at-will with any melee 2h weapon. Can also be used with any Natural Attack with at least 2d8 as its damage dice. Vital Strike - Add 3 damage to your attack. This improves by an additional 2 every level after level 3. (Essentially (2xLvL)-3)

entries.title: Butterfly Sting
entries.content: Create an opening for an ally when you find an opening in the enemy's defense. Requirements - Whenever you Crit a creature, you can turn it into a regular hit instead. Choose another ally you can see. The next attack they land becomes a crit On-hit.

entries.title: Combat Reflexes
entries.content: Requirements - - Gain two additional AoOs. Opponents can tell you continue to threaten past the first AoO. - You can draw a weapon as part of an attack of opportunity if you have a free hand.

entries.title: Combat Step
entries.content: Requirements - DEX +2 Reduce your speed to 10 this turn to make your movement elusive. This speed cannot be boosted by any means. If your speed is 10 or lower, you cannot combat step.

entries.title: Defensive Training
entries.content: Requirements - Increase your poise by 5.

entries.title: Diehard
entries.content: Requirements - STR +1 * The first time per day your +HP would be reduced to 0, instead reduce it to 1. * When actually reduced to 0, you make make a simple attack immediately before you are defeated. This has a cooldown of 1 minute.

entries.title: Divine Interference
entries.content: Requirements - WIS +1 or a Deific Boon from Frogg Saran As an immediate action you can expend a use of prayer to Reroll an Attack directed against you or an ally within 30 ft.

entries.title: Dual-Wielding Versatility
entries.content: Requirements - * You can dual wield with any 1h weapon, not just light weapons. * When dual wielding, you can sacrifice your "off hand" attack to gain Advantage with your "main hand" attack. * You can also sacrifice your "off hand" attack to Feint a creature. You must use your "main hand" to attack the creature you feinted.

entries.title: Fan Club
entries.content: Requirements - Level 3, Cha +1 Tags: Companion You use your influence and charm to gain a group of people that appreciate you. They are often willing to perform minor tasks for you, stick their neck out for you, but not risk their life for you. They are located in one specific place at first, then they may branch as you gain more levels and influence. The size of your fan club varies based on your level. You multiply this value by your CHA. | Level | Fan Club Members | | ----- | ---------------- | | 3 | 3 | | 4 | 6 | | 5 | 10 | | 6 | 15 | | 7 | 22 | | 8 | 33 | | 9 | 50 | | 10 | 75 | | 11 | 120 | | 12 | 250 | One member of your fan club is your biggest fan. Your biggest fan is always a minion, other than that, there are no restrictions for what your biggest fan can be. When you gain this feat, you decide their +Class, race, stats etc. They count as a companion if they travel with you. Your biggest fan doesn't necessarily need to be a race with a class, but those that aren't should be acquired through story related encounters rather than chosen.

entries.title: Fearsome Finisher
entries.content: Requirements - When you defeat an enemy, gain a +4 bonus to +Intimidation checks for the rest of combat. Whenever you defeat a creature, you can Demoralize another creature as a free action. If they were already shaken, they instead are Frightened for 1 round This free demoralize attempt can only occur once each round.

entries.title: Fighter's Lament
entries.content: Requirements - When a creature teleports within your reach, it provokes an AoO. This includes if the creature teleports into your reach.

entries.title: Fleet Footwork
entries.content: Requirements - - Increase your land speed by 5ft. - Your movement is elusive against any target you have attacked this turn.

entries.title: Freedom of Movement
entries.content: Requirements - * You take no penalty to speed while staggered or frosty. This can reasonably extend to other minor temporary speed Status that aren't these exact status conditions (such as the speed penalty from the icicle cantrip). * If knocked down, your speed only decreases by 15. * If you would be unable to move at all, such as being stunned or entangled, your speed is instead 5. You bring any grapplers with you.

entries.title: Ghostbuster
entries.content: Requirements - +Religion Proficiency You treat incorporeal creatures as though they were corporeal.

entries.title: Innocuous Bending
entries.content: Requirements - Elemental Blast, +Sleight of Hand You can use sleight of hand's conceal item feature with your elemental blast. It does not require a swift action to use, but it only works once in a particular encounter.

entries.title: Mage Slayer
entries.content: Requirements - Arcana Proficiency The first time each round you land a melee attack against a creature that is concentrating on a Spell, the spell fizzles (fort negates). You can also make a ranged attack, but your DC decreases by 2.

entries.title: Matador
entries.content: Requirements - When a creature grazes you or misses you after moving up to you, you can force them to continue their movement 10 ft past you as an immediate action, but you cannot AoO them. If they impact a solid surface, they take 5 crush and Poise damage, or 10 if they Core Rules, How to Play/Actions/Common Actions/Full Round Actions/Charge.

entries.title: Power Pose
entries.content: Requirements - DR When you are damaged with an effect that your DR applies against, you can increase that DR by 5 as an immediate action. If the creature is within Reach, it takes 10 Poise damage.

entries.title: Ready for Anything
entries.content: Crust Requirements - - When you are surprised, you lose the DD condition when you take your turn -

entries.title: Ricochet Toss
entries.content: Requirements - - Whenever you make a thrown weapon attack, the weapon you throw returns back to your hand immediately, allowing you to continuously make attacks with the same weapon(s) in the same turn. - You also ignore the attack penalty from using thrown weapons in their second range increment. If a weapon's special effect requires being on the opponent to function (such as a Net), then you cannot ricochet them.

entries.title: Sentinel
entries.content: Requirements - * Whenever you land an AoO against a creature that attempts to move out of your threatened spaces, their movement is halted and their speed is reduced to zero for their turn * The first time a turn a creature you threaten damages an ally within your Reach (that isn't you), make an AoO against it * Sentinel SHOULD proc on things like - Any Attack Roll that deals damage, AoE damaging effects like Fire Blast, single target damaging effects like Magic Missile, high impact Persistent effects like Wall of Fire * Sentinel SHOULDn't proc on things like - Damage over time effects like Ablaze or Bleed, damage reflecting effects like Elemental Shield, low impact persistent effects like Self Immolation Note: Because the trigger to Sentinel is the same as some reactive features (like Avenging Wrath), you cannot use this feat and such abilities to attack a creature twice. Though nothing is stopping you if your ally is harmed more than once. See Immediate Action and Instant Action.

entries.title: Toughness
entries.content: Requirements - Increase your max +HP by 5+LvL.

entries.title: Tumbling Charge
entries.content: Requirements - When you Core Rules, How to Play/Actions/Common Actions/Full Round Actions/Charge, you can choose to charge through an enemy, rolling around them or running straight through them. This is elusive against the target of your charge. You can end your charge on the opposite side of the creature instead of ending it when you meet the creature. You must still have the movement to do so. You gain Flanking against a creature you tumbling charge for the rest of your turn.

entries.title: Unarmed Combatant
entries.content: Requirements - Even while unarmed you threaten with punches, kicks and elbows. Your unarmed strikes deal d8 dmg, or 2d6 if your hands are empty and intact. Gain 2 special attacks usable with unarmed strikes. Blunt Force Trauma - On-hit stagger your target for one round (fort negates). If this attack defeats the target, make a thrown weapon attack using the defeated creature as ammunition. Power Fist - On hit stagger your target for one round (fort negates). If this attack defeats the target, it flies 1,000 ft into the air and gently lands on the ground one minute later. With a low roof, the creature is launched less high, but for just as long.

entries.title: Unconventional Mount
entries.content: Requirements - You have adapted your riding style to accommodate for smaller creatures. You can mount creatures that are the same size as you. You can mount any willing creature, even those without the proper hardware to facilitate mounting.

entries.title: Weapon Focus
entries.content: Requirements - Choose one: slash, pierce or crush You can use the default special attacks of these weapon types (cleave/lacerate, lunge/pierce defense, backbreaker/concussive strike, snipe/maim) at-will. You also gain a new one depending on the type you chose, usable 1/combat. Slash: Great Cleave - Attack all creatures currently within reach. Pierce: Impale - Creature struck takes your base weapon damage if they move by the start of your next turn. Crush: Tenderize - stagger On-hit (fort negates) The default and newly acquired special attacks also add your LvL to damage. Special: If you have the unarmed combatant feat, then you can apply the effects of weapon focus (crush) to the special attacks granted by it instead of the standard crush attacks. If you have Shield Focus, you can choose to apply the effects of weapon focus (crush) to endure/shield bash.

entries.title: +General Feats
entries.content: General feats mostly assist with some kind of out of combat task, such as to increase the consistency of skills.

entries.title: Brilliant Planner
entries.content: Your efficient packing and planning pays dividends. Tags: Recharge Requirements - - You gain +5 inventory slots. By spending 1 hour in a settlement, 1,000 gold and any number of inventory slots, you can prepare nebulous "goods" for later retrieval. As a standard action you can root through your stuff to pull out ANY item that you could fit in the slots within the gold limit. You only "spend" money equal to the item's actual gold cost, meaning if you pull out a 2 gold slice of special goblin bread for a hungry child, you only need to spend 2 gold to replenish your brilliant plan next time you recharge this ability. As stated previously, it requires an hour of shopping in a settlement to recharge this ability. Particularly small settlements with low populations may limit the types of goods you can obtain (ie a banana in a remote northern fishing village).

entries.title: Convenient Companion
entries.content: Requirements - Tags: Su As a standard action, you can touch your companion creature to turn them into a tiny action figure version of themselves, provided they are willing. If left in figurine form for too long, your companion will free itself when it gets hungry, thirsty or lonely. As a standard action, you can toss this action figure up to 30 ft away to transform it back. If you are currently in combat, the companion can spring to life using its own standard action.

entries.title: Demolitionist
entries.content: Requirements - Gain a +4 bonus to athletics checks made to Breakout and Brute Force.

entries.title: Lead by Example
entries.content: Requirements - Whenever you perform a skill check you are proficient in, such as sneaking past someone or swimming across a raging river, anybody that follows your lead on their next turn gets a +10 bonus to the same check. If the task requires more than one turn for you to perform, your allies still get the bonus. If you fail the skill check, your allies do not get the bonus.

entries.title: Photographic Memory
entries.content: Requirements - +2 INT By spending a memory slot as a free action, you can memorize everything you are currently looking at, as though taking a photo. When looking back at this memory, you can comb it for further details, such as reading text or inspecting faces in a crowd. You can only memorize one such scene at a time. If you already have your memory slots full, you can forget something to use this feat.

entries.title: Signature Skill
entries.content: Requirements - You gain additional options in how to use a specific skill that you specialize it. For more information visit the Skills page and browse each skill. Features of skills that are denoted (SS) require signature skill.

entries.title: Skill Focus
entries.content: Requirements - Gain the following twice. Choosing either... A. * Choose one skill. You roll it with Advantage. * When you take 10 with this skill, you instead take 15. * If a feature calls for you to use 10+(skill bonus) as a DC for something, instead use 15+DC. B. Gain a Skill Proficiency.

entries.title: Taking Prisoners
entries.content: Requirements - Whenever your actions Defeat a creature, you instead inflict Time Out As a standard action against a defeated creature, you can perform a series of nonlethal blows in key places so they are put in time out, and to make it so they cannot lose the condition until they rest or someone performs a +Medicine check against them.

entries.title: Wild Empathy
entries.content: Requirements - Gain proficiency in +Persuasion, but only for use against Beasts. If you are already proficient in persuasion, gain a +2 bonus when using it against beasts. Base DC is 10+(creatures level). Add +2 to DC for things like the following: protecting young, is only beast adjacent (magic cow), abused by humanoids, starving, unfamiliar environment, mating season etc. Succeeding on a wild empathy check makes the creature friendly to you temporarily, allowing you to make simple requests. Beasts are dumb so their help is limited (which one of these guys hurt you, lead me to x). The most obvious use case is to inquire about strange things in the nearby area that have happened recently. Interacting with the same animal over time can give you bonuses or cause you to not need to make a check anymore.

entries.title: +Magic Feats
entries.content: Magic - Feats that specifically apply to spells. MM - Short for meta magic. A subsection of magic feats that allow you to apply general augments to spells by increasing the mana cost by 1. Most require intelligence. You can only apply 1 metamagic feat to a spell at once. If you apply this to an Innate Technique, then it requires a swift action in addition to its usual cast time (unless it is a swift, immediate or free action).

entries.title: Callous Casting
entries.content: Requirements - As a swift action, increase the DC of an instantaneous spell you cast this turn by 2 if you include any non-minion allies (including yourself) in its AoE. Special: If you have an ability to effectively not include allies in an AoE, then you do not get the bonus of this feat. If the ally is immune or has Resistance to the effect, then you do not get the bonus of this feat.

entries.title: Curse of Vengeance
entries.content: Requirements - When you are Defeated or killed, you can an effect with the Curse tag as an immediate action targeting the creature that defeated you. This effect must be able to be used as a standard action (or a lesser action, like swift/free).

entries.title: Magic Initiate
entries.content: Requirements - You gain access to 4 +Cantrips

entries.title: Matryoshkasting
entries.content: Requirements - Level 3 When you use a level 2 or higher Spell, the next spell of a lower level that you cast costs 1 less mana. This can chain together until you spend 0 mana on casting a spell. If a spell you cast is of the same level or a higher level than the one you cast last, the effect ends. For the purposes of this feat, a spell is considered of a higher level when you upcast it.

entries.title: MM Bouncing Spell
entries.content: Requirements - INT +3 Note: - Only a spell that targets a single creature can be a bouncing spell. - Uniquely, this feat can be applied as an immediate action and it costs 3 mana. Whenever a creature passes a save against your spell, or when you miss/Graze a Spell Attack, you can choose to target a new creature within 15 ft of the original target to be the new target. When this happens, the original target suffers no ill effects, such as half damage from a passed save or graze.

entries.title: MM Bullying Spell
entries.content: Requirements - INT +1 The spell does Poise damage equal to the +HP damage it deals. This costs 2 Mana. If the spell's target is "1 creature," this only costs 1 mana. Bullying spell cannot be applied to spells that already deal poise damage.

entries.title: MM Caster Minion
entries.content: Requirements - INT +2 While using a feature with the scrying tag through a Companion creature, you can use your actions and the creature's actions to have them be the origin point of your spells.

entries.title: MM Coax
entries.content: Requirements - INT +1 Your spells with the Mind-Affecting tag can affect Mindless creatures.

entries.title: MM Elemental Shift
entries.content: Requirements - INT +1 When you cast the spell that deals an elemental damage type, you can choose to transform it into a different element, choosing between fire, frost, zap and force.

entries.title: MM Explosion Anticipation
entries.content: Requirements - INT +0 You are immune to the effects of your own AoE spell, provided it does not have a listed duration. This does now allow you to evade damage from AoE spells that hurt you, like Self-Destruct or Hellfire.

entries.title: MM Focused Blast
entries.content: Requirements - INT +1 - When you cast a spell that deals damage and targets multiple creatures, choose a "primary target." The DC of the spell for that creature is 2 higher. - If the spell allows attacks against multiple creatures (such as Gloom Bolt), you also get a +2 bonus to your Spell Attack against this creature.

entries.title: MM Intensify
entries.content: Requirements - INT +1 Increase the CL of the spell by 2.

entries.title: MM Latent Curse
entries.content: Requirements - INT +0 Your spells with the curse tag can also be cast on items. The first creature to activate or put on this item is afflicted with the curse (save negates). If the save is successful, the curse is lifted. For items that aren't activated or worn (such as a rock) the creature must possess the item or at least 1 minute for the curse to take hold. With curses that allow for a creative uses, the parameters must be set at the time the spell is cast. Special: You can only curse three items at a time. When a curse afflicts a creature that plays with your protected items, it no longer counts against this total. If you cast another latent curse with 3 already active, you choose which item is no longer cursed. You have an innate sense when one of your latent curse spells is triggered.

entries.title: MM Lingering Spell
entries.content: Requirements - INT +1 Note: - Only an AoE spell without a duration can be a lingering spell. A lingering spell takes a spell like Fire Blast and lets the explosion last until the start of your next turn. In the case of fire blast, any creature that enters the explosion radius must immediately make a save against the effect. This adds the Concentration and Persistent tag to the spell.

entries.title: MM Persistence
entries.content: Requirements - INT +1 When you cast a spell that has recurring saves each turn (like an upcast Mind Crush), you can choose to give it the Concentration tag (if it doesn't already have it) in order to increase the DC of recurring saves by 2.

entries.title: MM Residual Damage
entries.content: Requirements - INT +2 When you cast a spell that deals damage that doesn't already have a duration, add a damage over time effect On-hit or on a failed save. The creature takes your CL in damage at the end of its turn. The type of damage matches the damage of the spell.

entries.title: MM Saviors Arrow
entries.content: Requirements - INT +1 - You can fire the effect of a spell with the healing tag using the range of a ranged weapon. If the healing spell can target multiple creatures, you can make as many attacks as you like, but cannot target an ally more than once. - You can also choose to target a space to be the origin point of your spell. - You can use ranged weapons to cast spells with the healing tag with this feat.

entries.title: MM Sniping
entries.content: Requirements - INT +1 Add +2 attack to spell attacks made with this spell.

entries.title: MM Tumultuous Spell
entries.content: Requirements - INT +1 Special: This feat cannot be applied to spells with the Persistent tag. It also cannot be applied to spells that already move creatures. Creatures are knocked in a random direction (I use d6 for hexes, with 1 being north and then going clockwise) a random distance (d6x5 ft) On-hit or when they fail a save against the spell.

entries.title: Spell Ambush
entries.content: Requirements - +Stealth Proficiency Your spell DC is 2 higher against creatures you successfully hide from using stealth (aka if they would normally be DD from hiding). This only applies to the spell's initial save. This does not apply to other ways to cause a target to be DD, such as if you blinded them or feint them. Within reason, some abilities that similarly can only be used on the opening turn of combat to deceive or fight dirty against an enemy can also use this ability (like the Succubus' Sudden Betrayal ability).

entries.title: Spell Sharing
entries.content: Requirements - - When you cast a single target spell with the buff tag, you can cast it on a companion creature and yourself, or all of your companion creatures (provided they are in range and valid targets). This functions even if the spell has a target of self. - If the spell does not have a listed range, its range for determining when you can buff your companions is reach.

entries.title: Threatening Illusion
entries.content: Requirements - When you create a figment effect that creates a creature (such as Imagination), it also threatens and provides Flanking as long as creatures have not saved to disbelieve. As a standard action, or as an AoO, you can have the figment attack a creature. The creature takes d8 per spell level psychic damage (will negates and creature disbelieves illusion).

entries.title: +Multiclass
entries.content: Multiclass - This feat allows you to borrow a feature from another class. Usually such features are secondary features of the class, or are there for flavor.

entries.title: Bending Initiate
entries.content: Requirements - You can use the Bender elemental blast feature. It is a Special Attack that is usable any number of times a combat. The element you choose cannot be changed.

entries.title: Ex-Guardian
entries.content: You used to be a light guardian a long time ago, or you dropped out of your training early. Requirements - You gain the Light Guardian Light Binding special attack. You can only use it once per combat. You do not gain any of the other class abilities that enhance it.

entries.title: Flurry
entries.content: You can mimic the Martial Artist ability to weave punches and kicks into their combos. Requirements - Unarmed Combatant You gained the Martial Artist's Flurry feature, but it costs 1 mana to use.

entries.title: Light Steps
entries.content: Requirements - Level 3 Tags: Concentration Light steps requires concentration, and you can start concentrating on this ability as a free action by spending 1 Mana. As long as you only move during your turn and take no other action, you can walk along any flat continuous surface. This can include things that aren't even otherwise solid such as water or lava. It can also include things such as walls or ceilings. You can use this ability for only part of your turn to get to a location, but if you attack, cast a spell etc, then you need a surface you would normally be able to stand on or you plummet.

entries.title: Pet
entries.content: Tags: Companion Requirement - Level 3, Cha +0 You get a Beast Companion. It is always a minion to you.

entries.title: Speak for the Dead
entries.content: Requirements - Level 3 Tags: Recharge, Su 1/day you can use the Medium subclass Speak With Corpse feature.

entries.title: Unarmored Defense
entries.content: Requirements - STR +0 You've trained your body in such a way to allow you to effectively respond to attacks by intercepting blows and dodging projectiles. Your AC while unarmored is 14+LvL. If you have at least +1 DEX, this number increases to 15+lvl. Gain 4 Poise.

entries.title: +ALL
entries.content: These racial feats can be taken by any race, provided they have the requisite racial trait. This is for traits that are shared between races.

entries.title: Strong Wings
entries.content: Requirements - Glide racial trait, LvL 9 Your wings grow in size. You are always capable of flight.

entries.title: Titanic Lift
entries.content: Requirements - Amazon, STR +3, LvL 5 Multiply your Carry Weight by 16. The following special attack can be used at will. Heavy Artillery - You can throw an object weighing at least 1 ton to deal 3d12+STR (16+STR) dmg, defaulting to crush dmg. This counts as a +Siege Weapon. As far as handedness is concerned, it requires 2 hands to toss a rock like this. You can lower the base damage of this attack to 3d8+STR (13+STR) to do it one-handed.

entries.title: Black Cat
entries.content: Tags: Su, Recharge Requirements - Beastfolk, Cat subrace Your fur is (or becomes) completely black. You can visibly see when a creature is lucky and adds it to their rolls. As an immediate action, you can negate another creature you can see that adds LUK to a d20 roll. 1/day you can inflict Disadvantage on a creature you can see as an immediate action.

entries.title: Run Down
entries.content: Requirements - Beastfolk When you take the Core Rules, How to Play/Actions/Common Actions/Full Round Actions/Charge action, you take no penalty to AC. If you have the shapeshift racial trait, you can change forms as a free action when you charge.

entries.title: Tailed Fox
entries.content: Requirements - Beastfolk, Fox bestial boon, Level 5 You gain an additional Technique Point in the tree you chose with your bestial boon.

entries.title: Arm-y
entries.content: Tags: Su, Scrying Requirements - Boner, Lend a Hand trait You can choose to start scrying your detached arm while its separated. Its speed increases to 20 ft.

entries.title: Deathbane's Touch
entries.content: Tags: Su Requirements - Boner As a swift action, you can infuse a weapon, unarmed strike or Natural Attack with vitality. The next Undead creature you strike this round becomes staggered On-hit for 1 round.

entries.title: Dragon Breath
entries.content: Requirements - Drakonid, Breath weapon racial feature, Level 7 The damage of your breath weapon increases to 5d8+LvL (reflex halves). You can fire it in either a 40 ft cone or an 80 ft line. In addition, it gains a bonus effect depending on what elemental damage type you chosen (reflex negates). - Necrotic - Inflicts bone chilled for 2 rounds - Fire - Foes are set ablaze - Frost - The area of the breath weapon becomes a slippery surface. You can pass over it without risk of getting knocked down - Zap - Inflicts staggered for 1 round - Poison - Inflicts sickened 2 - Force - Also has the teleportation tag. You can teleport to any point in the AoE of your breath weapon. - Holy - Does not damage allies. Grants all allies in the AoE a divine shield - Psychic - Foes are confused for 1 round - Sonic - Also gains the fear tag. Inflicts frightened for 1 round

entries.title: Mixed Scales
entries.content: Requirements - Drakonid, Resistance and/or Breath weapon racial feature You have a complicated family history or you received a blessing from a powerful dragon. You gain a Resistance to an elemental damage type. If you have the breath weapon racial trait, you can breathe this element.

entries.title: Giant Steps
entries.content: Requirements - Dwarf, Deep subrace While you are large or larger, your Land Speed increases by 10.

entries.title: Celestial Healing
entries.content: Tags: Su, Healing Requirements - Exalted - Fallen Your blood pouring over your body has the helpful side effect of healing your other wounds. Whenever you take damage from bleed, you get an additional save against an ongoing negative Status effect . You can cause yourself to start bleeding for 1 damage as a swift action. You can withhold this healing effect and give it to another within reach by letting your blood wash over them. This is a standard action.

entries.title: Channel Force
entries.content: Tags: Su, Elusive Requirements - Exalted - Fallen Whenever you affect an ally with a Healing effect, they can choose to move 5 ft closer to you. On-hit or when a creature fails a save against one of your spells that deals Holy damage, they are pushed 5 ft away from you (or 5 ft further if it already did).

entries.title: Light in the Darkness
entries.content: Tags: Su Requirements - Exalted - Fallen, Exalted only Your halo is particularly bright. Whenever Bright Light from your halo overlaps with a Dark effect, you automatically get a check to Dispel it, using your LvL as your CL.

entries.title: Blistering Feint
entries.content: Requirements - Geniekin, Firekin When you successfully Feint a creature, they take your LvL in fire damage.

entries.title: Race Mixing
entries.content: Requirements - Human, Must be taken at LvL 1 Gain a major feature of another race (including alternate features). You count as both races for all purposes. If you get mixed blood from another feature, you can still only be a mix of maximum 2 races.

entries.title: Bloody Vengeance
entries.content: Tags: Recharge Requirements - Orc, Vengeful When a non-Minion ally is defeated while in combat, fly into a rage as per Barbarian as an instant action. If you are already a barbarian, your bonus to strength increases by 1. You immediately mark the creature with your vengeful trait and become Taunted by them. This rage ends when the creature is defeated.

entries.title: Body Over Mind
entries.content: Tags: Healing Requirements - Orc, WIS +0 You use pain to snap yourself out of it. When you take damage, you can roll a DC 10 WIS check. If you succeed, you can immediately end a single Mind-Affecting effect on you.

entries.title: Ferocious
entries.content: Requirements - Orc, Ferocity You are no longer Staggered while in ferocity. Immediately Dash at the start of your turn while in ferocity.

entries.title: Unkillable
entries.content: Requirements - Orc You are no longer drained when you are Defeated. When you would otherwise die, you instead are drained 20, and in a coma until you recover this drain. It requires a creature with +Medicine proficiency to Assess the Wounded to realize you are still alive. Particularly destructive deaths, like guillotine or Disintegration do not function against you, leaving your head still attached and your atoms still...not a fine dust. If you are attacked further while in this coma, you die as normal, but any Healing effect used on you within a few minutes can bring you back to life.

entries.title: Racial Feats
entries.content: Racial - These feats expand on racial features or play more into the unique aspects of a race that would be unfitting for a race feature.

entries.title: As One
entries.content: Requirements - You can spend your movement during your allies' turns. You subtract this movement from the movement on your next turn. If you have already moved (such as when you go first in initiative), then your movement is limited by whatever remained at the end of that turn. Turn based games make movement awkward, but the idea is you are moving together, so other positional effects, such as escape route or +Shield Actions, can treat moving allies as within reach for the duration of the movement in circumstances where allies converge on a location or start in positions that are close to each other.

entries.title: Tandem Charge
entries.content: Requirements - As One During an ally's turn, if they make a Core Rules, How to Play/Actions/Common Actions/Full Round Actions/Charge attack, you can spend an immediate action to charge along with them and make a simple attack. You must charge attack the same creature they attack Your speed is zero on your next turn. Turn based games make movement awkward, but the idea is you are moving together, so other positional effects, such as escape route or shield actions, can treat moving allies as within reach for the duration of the movement in circumstances where allies converge on a location or start in positions that are close to each other.

entries.title: Bonded Mind
entries.content: Tags: Su Requirements - You can communicate using Telepathy with any ally as long as you can see them.

entries.title: Take the Hit
entries.content: Requirements - Bonded Mind As an immediate action, when an ally within 30 ft of you takes damage, you can choose to reduce the damage by half and then take that damage as true damage.

entries.title: +Teamwork Feats
entries.content: Note: A teamwork feat requires more than one creature to possess in order to function, usually providing some kind of synergistic effect. The wording of these feats often include "all allies," but it only applies to allies with the feat.

entries.title: Artillery Team
entries.content: Requirements - You can use the Aid action to reload an allies weapon with the Reload property (like crossbow or Gun). You can aid them preemptively to allow them to shoot more than once during their turn. Also siege weapons when I get to it.

entries.title: Cooperative Casting
entries.content: Requirements - The ability to cast spells You can use the Aid action on an ally within 30 ft to increase the CL ally by 2 for 1 round. When you do this, you can also spend 1 Mana to decrease the mana cost of their next spell by 1. If your level is higher than the creature you are assisting, the CL boost increases to 3.

entries.title: Escape Route
entries.content: Requirements - Leaving threatened spaces also threatened by your allies (other than you of course) is elusive.

entries.title: Feint Partner
entries.content: Requirements - If your ally Feints a creature, you can make an AoO against it if it is within Reach. It is DD for this attack.

entries.title: Gang Up
entries.content: Requirements - Gain a +4 bonus to attack with combat maneuvers if an ally has rolled an Attack against the creature you are attacking since your last turn.

entries.title: Harmonize
entries.content: Requirements - When you roll a Group Skill Checks with the +Profession skill to sing, play instruments or otherwise put on some kind of group performance (such as with sports), every ally proficient in a the skill beyond the first (ie you and another performer) gives a +4 bonus (max +12).

entries.title: Pack Tactics
entries.content: Requirements - As long as you are threatening the same creature as an ally, you gain Flanking regardless of position.

entries.title: Perfect Positioning
entries.content: Requirements - You can move into an allies space. When you do, they can move into yours as an instant action. This movement is elusive. While adjacent to an ally, you cannot be flanked.

entries.title: Shield Bros
entries.content: Requirements - Gain +2 AC when you are within the natural reach of an ally that wields a shield. This can increase up to +4 ac if 4 shield bros are involved. This basically functions as and does not stack with the Block action. Due to how spaces work on grids, having groups of 4 adjacent to each other can be difficult. So for groups, as long as each member is adjacent to another member, that should suffice for gaining this feats benefits.

entries.title: Spotter
entries.content: Requirements - - When you give the Aid action to grant a bonus to Attack to a creature firing a Ranged weapon or +Siege Weapon, the bonus increases to +4, but you can no longer have multiple creatures aid that creature to grant attack bonuses. - You also passively give allies (other than you) a +4 bonus when to +Perception checks when travelling with them, or when otherwise keeping watch together. This bonus doesn't stack with itself.

entries.title: Feat Functionality
entries.content: Feats are features in progression typically gained at every odd level. They allow your character to get bonuses to specific actions, perform entirely new actions and occasionally get general stat increases. Some feats have prerequisites, such as stat requirements or other feats. You must meet all of the requirements of a feat to take it. Feats come in a few categories. General - General feats mostly assist with some kind of out of combat task, such as things to increase the consistency of skills. Combat - A combat feat is only useful during combat, or is at least more skewed towards being useful during combat. Multiclass - This feat allows you to borrow a feature from another class. Usually such features are secondary features of the class, or are there for flavor. Teamwork - A teamwork feat requires more than one creature to possess in order to function, usually providing some kind of synergistic effect. The wording of these feats often include "all allies," but it only applies to allies with the feat. Magic - Feats that specifically apply to spells. MM - Short for meta magic. A subsection of magic feats that allow you to apply general augments to spells by spending an additional point of mana. Most require intelligence. Racial - These feats expand on racial features or play more into the unique aspects of a race that would be unfitting for a race feature.

entries.title: Anime Jesus
entries.content: Anime Jesus is the Anime god of order. He primarily concerns himself with the purging of corruption and evil in the realms beyond his own. The followers of AJ call themselves The Golden Order. Many that follow him mimic his powerful offensive magic in order to destroy Aberrations, fiends, undead and the mortals that serve/create them. Anime Jesus is not just concerned with the evil that exists on Nack, but also that which lies beyond the stars. A radioactive and corrupting force that permeates the galaxy known as ligmanium. It twists and transforms all it comes into contact with, eventually spreading between planets. Destroying this substance at its source is the prime directive of AJ and his followers. Anime Jesus himself is more well-known for his interest in mortal women. Many Exalted are born as a result with his intermingling with whatever and whoever happens to be around on his latest mission. When these children inevitably attempt to make contact to their father, the most common takeaway he gives to them is "Be fruitful and multiply." As a result, many of the children have large families to impress their father. As a result, this ideal is closely tied to the faith as a whole. Anime Jesus is a dark-skinned man with ample facial hair that shines gold. He is clad in golden armor and wears a rather odd pointed hat. His forehead bears a tattoo of the symbol of The Golden Order.

entries.title: T1 - Power of Prayer
entries.content: Tags: Su You can clasp your hands together and chant words of prayer out loud as a standard action in order to render your presence invisible to any Fiends, Undead or Aberrations. To them your speech is completely silent and you do not exist. They will even instinctually go out of their way to avoid bumping into you or including you in any AoE effect. You can continue to chant each round to maintain this effect indefinitely, but not after a creature has already noticed you. Allies can join you in your chanting in order to also gain this benefit. If you interact with objects that are very obvious, such as opening doors, throwing levers and picking up loot, the creature present with the highest will save makes a saving throw against your DC. Continuing to tamper with objects incurs more saves. Interacting directly with one of these creatures also blows your cover. Note: You cannot use this ability to Surprise an enemy.

entries.title: Ao Shin the Storm Dragon
entries.content: Ao Shin is an ancient dragon, having lived his youth in The Age of Dragons. Unlike most other dragons of his age, Ao Shin has rather lofty goals, varying from age to age, but usually involving getting revenge on those that have wronged him. In The Age of Darkness, this means elves, particularly those from The Land of Storms for abandoning him. He enjoys destroying the structures of mortals, but tends to only do so indirectly through his followers, though his followers can sometimes tempt him or anger him enough to let loose a tornado across the countryside. He is generally antagonistic towards other dragons, and hates Star Dragons and wish granting creatures with all of his heart. From what historians are able to gather, it seems that Ao Shin was meant to receive some type of blessing from the ancient dragons of his time, but another dragon wished upon a star and ascended to become the first star dragon in his stead. Ao Shin is pompous, spiteful and very narcissistic. Unlike many deities, he is more than happy to show himself to mortals on a regular basis. He does not bend to the wills of mortals and has a set schedule when he can be spoken to. Such meetings usually involve offerings. Fruit is his favorite. Ao Shin himself is an enormous dark blue dragon, hundreds of feet long with a majestic white beard. His scales crackle with lightning and where he resides the wind is always strong. He seems to have crafted himself a custom set of highly ornamental faded golden armor that protects the top half of his body.
entries.path: Gods/Gods/Demigods/Ao Shin the Storm Dragon/Ao Shin the Storm Dragon.md

entries.title: T1 - Air Pressure
entries.content: Tags: Su, Air You can call upon Ao Shin's destructive power for the purpose of the Breaking Objects and Breakout functions of athletics. You can use your +Religion or Arcana bonus in place of +Athletics for these purposes at a range of 30 ft. No matter the skill, you get a +4 bonus. During particularly stormy, rainy or windy weather, you instead use Prayer get a +8 bonus to this check, and can destroy wide areas like fields and small structures like houses.
entries.path: Gods/Gods/Demigods/Ao Shin the Storm Dragon/T1 - Air Pressure.md

entries.title: T1 - Cloud Gazing
entries.content: Tags: Su, Air, Water You can manipulate the heavens such that clouds form in a shape of your choosing. If you choose to shape them into text, you can write up to two simple sentences. If you choose something that isn't text, it is usually limited to a single entity, symbol or object. This message can also show up in a more dramatic fashion, with flashing lightning illuminating your cloud canvas. This guarantees all creatures see it. You can also try to hide it from onlookers, weather provided. Other creatures with Ao Shin GBP always notice it. Depending on the weather conditions, creatures may see it. If you are in a desert for instance, clouds will stand out. But in cloudier environs, you can roll a luck check to only deliver your message to desirable creatures.
entries.path: Gods/Gods/Demigods/Ao Shin the Storm Dragon/T1 - Cloud Gazing.md

entries.title: Artemis
entries.content: Artemis is the goddess of the moon, redemption and secrets. Artemis has a deep respect for community. "Love thy neighbor" is a nice-sounding statement, but the followers of Artemis live by this mantra. It is something that has always restricted a wide-reaching adoption of Artemis, but she insists that communities do not grow too large to where you no longer know everybody in your neighborhood. This even informs the city structures where Artemis is popular: Districts are limited to populations of about 200 in order to keep people integrated into their communities. The clergy and most devout followers of Artemis are sworn to absolute secrecy and cannot act on any information people tell them in confidence. Confession is a common method for people to deal with their problems, and a common method followers of Artemis use to solve problems around the world. The greatest failings that people keep close to them often cause the greatest problems. Without a proper diagnosis for a problem, healing cannot begin. As such, Eternal are often the most devout followers of Artemis, despite most of their communities existing in the Feywilds. Artemis once again walks amongst mortals. Her physical form is that of a air Geniekin. She is nearly 10 feet tall, has light blue skin and silver eyes. Her hair and her numerous face and body tattoos forming patterns across her skin are a milky golden yellow. She wears a simple robe that matches this golden yellow aesthetic. Her medium length hair always seems to flow in the wind away from her face, no matter the direction she faces. Note - Artemis once had a domain within Elysium, but has since fallen out of favor with the rise of the Brazzeria. Maybe one day she can reclaim her spot among the other Heavenly Deities.

entries.title: Moongaloids vs Lunartics
entries.content: Artemis vs Brazzeria Artemis is the goddess of the moon, redemption and secrets. It might seem strange to have two different good deities with a similar focus, but that is because Brazzeria has stolen Artemis' spot in the pantheon of Nack. The battle for relevance between herself and Brazzeria is what defines Artemis in the modern era, and unfortunately she is fighting a losing battle. They each have a slur they use to refer to each other. Brazzerians are referred to as "Moongaloids" and the followers of Artemis are called "Lunartics." The Slow Descent The tipping point for Brazzeria was during The Age of Magic, when a paladin of Artemis, Lance Crownguard, was tempted by a family of fiendish babes created by a demon lord. Brazzeria would disguise herself as Artemis and visit Lance to sway his thoughts and opinions during his adventure. His experience, his influence, his book, and later his conversion to becoming a Brazzerian would have far-reaching and sweeping impacts on the attitudes people had towards demons and creatures who resembled them like Hellion. This is where Brazzeria stepped in. The hellion of the world served as the perfect vessel for her ascension to greater heights. Where Brazzeria's influence spread, Artemis' waned. Nowadays only small communities or the people within the Scorched Plains still worship Artemis. Brazzerians need to struggle in order to exist in societies they aren't already established thanks to their ideas surrounding intimacy and their close relationship with Fiends. Their extremely liberal approach can often butt heads with the traditional practices of wherever they find themselves. Their communities can feel suffocated by such conditions. Despite this, the active conversion of an entirely unique cohort, that being fiends, has payed off in the long run. "The moongaloids are moving the world closer and closer to degenerate dystopia" is a common sentiment that is not without merit. With institutions like the Pleasure Parlor slowing creeping up in relevance, its clear that Brazzerian influence is on the rise and is not stopping any time soon.

entries.title: T1 - Confession
entries.content: Tags: Su You cannot use +deception to convince truth. You cannot tell half truths. If you do not want to speak the truth, your only option is to remain silent. You gain a marking, usually on your hand, that showcases your belief in Artemis. Any creature trained in +Religion can automatically tell its significance. If you place your mark against a creature, it also cannot deceive as you can't. You also cannot reveal any secrets told to you in confidence by other creatures. There is no phrase that needs to preface these statements, only sentiment. You can inherently tell when a creature tells you something they believe is a secret that needs to be kept. Even methods like detect thoughts or intrusive thoughts cannot reveal these secrets. While your mark is in contact with another creature, they can intuit that their secrets are safe with you. Any secrets you tell them while in contact, they also cannot share, even after you separate from them.

entries.title: T1 - Crawling Sins
entries.content: Tags: Su You regularly suffer from vivid nightmares where you experience the hardship that you have caused. You experience the financial troubles of those you have swindled, the relationship troubles your infidelity has cause etc. Only through rectifying your actions, seeking forgiveness from those you have wronged can you assuage the dreams. Against a willing or Helpless creature, you can also cause them to experience this same condition, manifesting as a unique Affliction. You must be somewhat aware of a specific sin the creature has committed, and the nightmares they experience are related to this event. Not used to this condition, the creature is unable to properly benefit from Rest. Once they repent for this sin, the nightmares stop and they can rest easy, hopefully with them having learned their lesson.

entries.title: T2 - X to Doubt
entries.content: Tags: Su The greatest lies are those we tell ourselves. As an instant action, you can gaze upon a creature to see if they have confidence in what they stand for and what they say. You can only do this in response to statements with moral weight to them like "all hellion are the same" or "I'm concerned for the safety of the children." You reveal any doubt they have about themselves, their past actions or their beliefs (will negates). When this ability is successful, you learn one of the following things about the creature, depending on what is most relevant in this particular scenario. 1 - The creature is possessed, Dominated or otherwise magically influenced by some outside force that forces them to think or act a certain way. 2 - The creature is actively deceiving others for social capital or other nefarious schemes 3 - The creature is ashamed of themselves, and doesn't believe their own words or actions, or only does so superficially

entries.title: Chrimbus Spirit
entries.content: See Santa for lore.

entries.title: T1 - Gift of Giving
entries.content: Tags: - You have an innate sense of what would make a meaningful gift to a creature just by looking at them. When you get in trouble for relatively minor misdeeds, you can offer the creature a personalized gift to smooth things over.

entries.title: Clairvoyance (G)
entries.content: Clairvoyance is the goddess of greed and Giants. More specifically, cloud giants. She is one of the last living first generation of The Titans, and as such, has lived for over 1000 years. She resides in the tallest mountain in the treacherous north of The Land of Frost, Cumulus Mountain. There she hoards wealth from the surrounding humanoid civilizations that she has accumulated over the centuries. She is extremely ambitious and antagonistic, commanding her daughters to harass travelers all throughout the region. Clairvoyance is a greedy deity, primarily concerned with the enrichment of herself and her daughters. They hunt for sport, and do not discriminate in what they hunt, many even preferring the taste of humanoids over other prey. Because of how isolationist this clique of cloud giants is, not much is known about them. Mysteriously, they are all females, which are similar to Amazon, but they lack the necessary males to aid in the creation of offspring. If the stories are to be believed, then the Clairvoyance's mother, the titan matriarch Thea, was able to create Amazon after the loss of her husband through her tears. Clairvoyance and her daughters' unique ability to see long distances through clouds makes intelligence gather operations almost guaranteed to fail. Only a rare few have ever spoken to her and lived to tell the tale, even fewer outside of battle. Clairvoyance herself stands several times taller than her daughters, who already stretched up to 20 ft tall. Her skin is a light blue, with completely white hair, eyes and tattoos covering her body in intricate patterns. She chooses to wear a tight toga, adorned with all kinds of trinkets she has created. She takes great pride in the jewelry she has stolen from others and melted down into a shape more befitting of her stature. Her favorite is a bejeweled staff with a crystalline ball in it that contains a raging storm inside of it. The motto often repeated by her followers is "Anything worthwhile in life must be fought for." Note - Unlike other gods, Clairvoyance is a very simple god when it comes to earning her favor: You can simply buy Clairvoyance GBP, with her requiring more money each point. Without the Blade of Avarice boon, you must manually deliver it to her or her daughters in The Land of Frost. For every 20k gold you offer her, you gain 1 GBP. This can also be given in the form of Gear Upgrades and other valuable materials.

entries.title: T1 - Blade of Avarice
entries.content: Tags: Su, teleportation You see people in black in white; the haves and have nots. You can tell just by looking at a creature that they are more or less wealthy than you based on this black (less wealthy) or white (more wealthy) aura. By ritually sacrificing people with wealth attached to them, you can transfer that wealth directly to Clairvoyance (G), teleporting their body (and any valuables sacrificed) directly to her. You must transfer wealth in increments of at least 1,000g, with any value in between rounded down to the nearest 1,000. Every 20k you "donate," you get 1 GBP.

entries.title: T2 - Eye in the Sky
entries.content: Tags: Su, Scrying You can project your sense of sight and hearing to any location you have line of sight to, initiating as a standard action, then maintaining it for a few minutes. You can move the location you view as though you were walking around in the area. You are still unable to view anything you do not have line of sight to from your original position (ie you cannot see around a giant pillar by projecting yourself to the side behind it). This basically makes eye in the sky function as extremely powerful senses of sight and hearing that require active concentration to maintain. While you do so, your body is vulnerable and stunned. When you stop scrying, you are drained 2. With good environmental conditions, that means you can potentially see incredibly far distances. Clouds do not obscure your line of sight with this ability. Depending on your height relative to the area around you. Here's a handy chart that should give you a basic idea of how far you can see with specific elevations. | Elevation (relative) | Distance to Horizon | | ------------------------------------------------------------------ | ----------------------------------------------------------------- | | 1 ft | 1 mile | | 100 ft | 12 miles | | 1000 ft | 38 miles | | 1 mile | 90 miles | | 6 miles | 220 miles | 6 miles was chosen as the final threshold since that's around the average most commercial airlines fly, and isn't far off from the tallest mountain peaks IRL. One mile further and you are encroaching upon the stratosphere, which is where common atmospheric conditions we're used to on the surface get weird (extremely low air density making it hard to breathe, wild variations in temperature, high UV radiation, ie not recommended to go higher).

entries.title: Gralth the Inferno
entries.content: Gralth is the god of dragons. He is over 1000 years old, and having lived even before the beginning of The Age of Dragons, he still remembers a time where dragons once roamed Nack freely. He makes his home hundreds of miles underground inside of the planet's mantle underneath a colossal volcano in the Land of Fire. The land surrounding this volcano is his domain. It is called the Domain of Dragons, which is a place where most other types of creatures are not allowed to roam. How most people know him is as the progenitor of the Drakonid race; his attempt at creating a bridge between his kind and humanoids, or via his children that share his name and seek to earn his approval. Gralth the Inferno is largely a deity only concerned with his own kind. Dragon slaying is often seen as a sport, a necessity or even a rite of passage that can earn a creature accolades. This disgusts Gralth, and he does everything in his power to curb humanoids and the like of the ability to do so. His boons allow dragons to exist more safely in the world. When he makes an appearance on the surface, it is usually in efforts to protect dragons on their journey to retreat to his domain. As such, he is reviled by many governments and organizations in the Land of Fire and has an enormous bounty on his head. Gralth is roughly humanoid in shape and nearly a 100 feet tall. His scales are a reddish orange color. He has head of flaming hair flowing from between his horns. Those that have witnessed him fight and lived to tell the tale say he fights like a Martial Artist, preferring to throw hands over chomping and clawing at people. Many do not know it, but he is the one who initially created Dragon Style (see Fighting Styles). The motto often repeated by his followers is "Live and let live, until we must fight to survive."

entries.title: T1 - Roar of Dragons
entries.content: Tags: Su Language-Dependent You learn draconic, the language of dragons. If you already knew it, learn a new language. As a free action when you speak, you can increase the volume of your voice so it is amplified several times. This mainly increases the distance it can be heard and doesn't cause any immediate damage or negative effects to those nearby. As a FRA, you can let out a draconic roar that echoes for miles. This roar contains a message, as per the limitations of the Sending technique, but its range is limited and it can only be understood by creatures that have this boon or are Dragons. This part of the boon has the recharge tag.
entries.path: Gods/Gods/Demigods/Gralth the Inferno/T1 - Roar of Dragons.md

entries.title: T2 - Undercover
entries.content: Tags: Su, Polymorph This boon functions differently depending on if the creature is a dragon, or if they are not a dragon. Creatures kind of in between (like Drakonid) choose whatever they are farther from to transform into. If you are not a dragon... This boon gives you the ability to transform into a specific draconic form, chosen when you gain this boon. You cannot use any abilities dependent on your original form (ie a long frog tongue). - You gain a fly speed equal to your land speed - You gain the dragon's Overland Flight feature. - Your size is (up to) large in draconic form. - While in draconic form, you can use a breath weapon, dealing 2d8+(2*LvL) damage of a specific elemental type (chosen when you gain this boon) in either a 30 ft cone or a 60 ft line. This breath weapon has the recharge tag, and the tag of the elemental damage type you chose. You can then choose between one of the following draconic subtypes, chosen when you gain this ability. - True Dragon poly, true - You are quadrupedal. You gain a bite and claw Natural Attack which do 2d8 pierce and slash damage respectively. You lose access to your equipment, but still gain any passive bonuses from it (see poly, true) for more details. - Protodragon poly, chimeric - You are bipedal. You do not lose access to your equipment. You can swap forms as a standard action. You can enter draconic form as part of the same action as using your breath weapon. If you are a dragon... This boon gives you the ability to transform into a specific humanoid form, chosen when you gain this boon. While in this form, you lose any abilities dependent on your physical form (like Tail Swipe). You are constantly under the effect of Tongues. You can swap forms as a free action 1/round.

entries.title: Shroomlette
entries.content: The Shroomlette is the shroomkin god of happiness, rest and fertility. One of the few gods that exists in the realm of mortals. Her domain is a garden filled with all kinds of oddities on the clouds above a mushroom forest. Forever resting in a shiny cradle, she waits to be tucked in by a visitor. The Garden is commonly used as a place of mediation since all forms of violence are forbidden within the domain of the Shroomlette. The Shroomlette is an entity that represents the bond between gooms, a breed of squishy draconic creatures, and shroomkin, a race of people with close ties to fey creatures and mushrooms. These two entities live in tandem with each other with one main goal: increase the quantity of happiness in the world. The tactics they use to do so is to subdue creatures that decrease happiness and physically change the world around them to make it a better place to live. Their motto is "Whatever makes everyone happy."

entries.title: T1 - Form of Baby
entries.content: Tags: Su, poly, true You can transform into a tiny baby version of yourself. You are unable to attack, cast spells, speak etc. Your movement is reduced to a crawl of 10ft. You can transform into a baby or revert to your original form as a standard action.

entries.title: T2 - Bubble Blowing Double Baby
entries.content: Tags: Sp You can Innately Cast Bubble at will. It does not have the concentration tag. You can make the bubbles into any shape you desire. Whenever you blow any type of bubble, you can carefully shape it to make bubble art.

entries.title: T1 - Trailblazer
entries.content: Tags: Su You have advantage on navigation checks. When you are travelling long distances, you and your allies' speed are never slowed by environmental conditions (including Rocks). This does not give you any specific adaptation to the region you travel to. You must still have the ability to survive deep oceans and high mountain peaks if you want to visit such places. To a lesser extent, Tourists are known to travel to weird locations without much reason other than to explore. In neutral scenarios, you can convince others to let you pass or tolerate your presence temporarily. This usually does not work for people that have a specific agenda, like guards in places of interest.

entries.title: Taka the Tourist
entries.content: Taka the Tourist is an anime that has made it her life's mission to try and make the world feel a bit more interconnected. She does this by insisting her followers go out and see more of the world, as much as they can. She and her followers create little outposts called Tourist outposts, and are the closest thing thing that Tourism has to temples. Tourists, as one might expect, are often nomadic and do not have countries, +Races or even many settlements where Tourism is the dominant religion. There is one location in particular that Tourists hold above all others: Auroras. It is a tiny settlement far to the north in the icy mountainous region of The Land of Storms. Aside from the low temperature and the treacherous journey to get there, one would be hard pressed to find a more idyllic natural beauty in the world. It is one of the few places in the Land of Storms that is not plagued by terrible weather. This eye of the storm is blessed with clear skies, an ocean view and brilliant lights filling the sky at night. Every Tourist is expected to make the pilgrimage to Auroras at least once in their life, and Taka will often accompany groups of people who seek this location. Taka herself is a dark-haired bright eyed Anime woman who prefers style over function. She likes stylish jackets and stylized art on her clothing. She wields an ancient looking broken sword as a handicap to keep fights interesting, since her relatively average looking appearance causes many to not realize the dangers that fighting her brings. Only against worthy opponents does the sword actually reveal its true form. Her motto is "Together apart, together in your heart."

entries.title: T1 - Spider Climb
entries.content: Tags: - The spider queens blessings bring you closer to her. - You gain a Climb speed equal to your Land Speed. It can be used along any surface. - You can cling to walls efficiently enough to allow full use of both your hands while climbing. - You ignore web-based Difficult Terrain and cannot be Entangled by webs.

entries.title: T1 - Technically Legal
entries.content: Tags: - The spider queen constantly whispers to you, teaching you ways to break rule systems and get away with it. By considering a particular act, you can think of ways to construe ways to legally justify said act, obfuscate the legality by pleading to the emotions of others or convincing others that the law is of little importance. When doing these things you are treated as though you have Skill Proficiency in +Linguistics and +Deception, each with a +4 bonus. This also functions for social rules, not just written law.

entries.title: The Spider Queen
entries.content: The Spider Queen is an entity that is worshipped almost exclusively by dark elves and other intelligent spider creatures. The original Spider Queen blessed a particularly brave Elf that delved into her lair. The original now passed, the title of Spider Queen is passed down through the dark elf Shi'Sara royal family. The Spider Queens have always encouraged competition. Even at a young age, children are encouraged to race their siblings when very young, and surpass their siblings in martial or magical prowess as they age. The only thing that matters to the subjects of the Queen is to make the most money, draw in the most followers, have the most powerful armies and make the most ground-breaking discoveries. Being talents is one way to achieve these goals, but eliminating the competition entirely isn't something that is discouraged. All spider queens are adept at Cryomancy, and this manifests in their skin turning from a dull gray to a light blue. This blessing granted by the original Spider Queen comes in the form a spider body from the waist down. The Spider Queen will often have other unusual traits for an elf, like a mouth that can open particularly wide, incredibly large size and multiple sets of eyes.

entries.title: T1 - Wishing Well
entries.content: Tags: Su You can use this ability by making a wish and dropping a sum of money into a well, or water sources that are or are soon to be moving underground. Your wish along with your money will work its way back into the world in a way that will aid the creature you wish for at some point in the near future. This could be a special delivery, a discount on a purchase they planned to make or an item they uncover in the ground. Ulule decides how best to use these funds to assist in your goal, and pays more attention to the intent behind the wish than the exact wording when deciding what to do.

entries.title: Ulule
entries.content: Ulule is a relatively niche revered figure of earthkin Geniekin. She fell in love with a mortal and wished to make a home for her new family. She found a giant beautiful tree and infused it with her own life. It turned the tree into a mix of wood, metal and stone. It later became known as the Iron Pine. This enormous tree and its roots served as the center point for these people in the heart of the Flame Empire. Her followers are generally upstanding people, albeit they keep themselves secluded away from others. Many that worship Ulule also worship the Earth Mother. The empire did not take too kindly to what they viewed as squatters and plotted to kill her. They succeeded, but the geniekin still remain to this day, cloistered in their Iron Pine, protected by its seemingly indestructible umbrella of protection that branches deep underground. The Iron Pine uniquely has a mind of its own, and its said that Ulule's spirit still resides within it. She selectively allows creatures like Earthbenders to manipulate its shape to better suit their needs. Ulule is a genie composed mostly of marble and gemstones that adorn her face. Great care is taken towards statues of her, which are particularly lifelike given she herself was made of stone. The motto often repeated by her followers is "What goes around comes around."

entries.title: +Demigods
entries.content: There exist a great many lesser or local deities that are much less popular, and therefore do not follow the same kind of rules as the other gods. They are still more mortal than divine, and are free to exert their lesser influence directly in the mortal realm. There are even rules on how a player character can become one under Ascendance. These demon dukes, demigods and other lesser deities can be killed like any man. They do still have abilities similar to gods, such as the ability to grant boons, and project their deific aspect, however their ability to perform miracles is limited. Demigods must often fight tooth and nail to not fade into irrelevance once the next generation of mortals forgets them for their deeds.

entries.title: Caldera, the Cataclysm
entries.content: The Cataclysm is the most outwardly terrifying, hostile and destructive force known to mankind. She is concerned with little more than raw physical strength, twisting this ideology to its absolute extremes and eliminating any and all unable to survive the hostility and violence so common among her and her minions. As such, she is very often held in high regard by eugenicists, anarchists and the unhinged that just want to watch the world burn. One of the few things that the followers of the Cataclysm will always hold in high regard is contests of raw physical strength. Their method of testing your might is typically an arm wrestling contest. This is often used as a last ditch effort by people to save their own skins when faced with overwhelming odds of death. On rare occasions, her followers will kidnap specimens they deem worthy enough. They will be dragged into the demon realm to have all manner of alchemical, magical and genetic experiments run on them to create a creature that is perfectly huge. Caldera herself is a massive muscular woman clad in metal armor who typically stands over 20 feet tall. Her body is covered in all manner of bony spikes, horns and scales. Along her back are dorsal plates that run down her massive tail, giving her stance a forward lean, allowing her to loom over and gaze upon lesser beings with her many eyes.
entries.path: Gods/Gods/Demon Lords/Caldera, The Cataclysm/Caldera, the Cataclysm.md

entries.title: Daughters of the Cataclysm
entries.content: The Cataclysm is absolutely obsessed with raw physical strength, and constantly throws herself and her followers into situations where they can test their might. This manifests in a particularly strange way, where she sees herself as the perfect being, and so therefore her offspring are the only ones that really have a chance of every topping her feats of strength. The exact circumstances of their creation are unknown, but they typically end up as large muscular demons, and are always women. Unfortunately for these ladies, mom's standards couldn't be higher: They must become stronger than their mother. Since all of them fall short of this goal, she abandons them, giving them some kind of leadership role in her demonic armies and only ever interacting with them sparingly. The only training she ever gives them is physical, so they are usually unprepared for this role.
entries.path: Gods/Gods/Demon Lords/Caldera, The Cataclysm/Daughters of the Cataclysm.md

entries.title: T1 - Huge
entries.content: Tags: - - Any thing that gives you additional Carry Weight in increments of 100, such as strength and racial features, gives you 1000 lbs of carry weight instead. - You can throw heavy objects, dealing 2d8+STR dmg (requires 1 free hand). - You get a +2 bonus to +Athletics to Breakout, Brute Force and to Arm Wrestling for each tier of boon you have in The Cataclysm (ie T1 +2, T2 +4 etc) - Your STR is treated as 2 higher for how you are affected by High Gravity - You can tell a creatures STR score just by looking at it

entries.title: T2 - Huge XL
entries.content: Tags: Your Size increases by 1 category. You can swap between this increased size and your previous size as a swift action. If you already have a feature that increases your size, like the Amazon Embiggen feature, it instead increases your size by an additional category.
entries.path: Gods/Gods/Demon Lords/Caldera, The Cataclysm/T2 - Huge XL.md

entries.title: T2 - Weight for It
entries.content: Tags: - The training never stops. You are constantly under the effects of High Gravity and it does not affect your speed or carrying capacity, no matter the tier. In gravity neutral scenarios (ie gravity is normal), you can temporarily turn off this gravity training as a free action, providing the following benefits. - It no longer requires a swift action in order to take the Jump action and creatures can automatically jump as far and as high as a creature's Land Speed. When you jump, you ignore Fall Damage. - You can Dash as part of activating this ability, and as a swift action on each subsequent turn. - Multiply your Carry Weight by 10x You can typically only benefit from this surge of agility for a few minutes at a time, and only a few times a day, lest the gravity training be ineffective.
entries.path: Gods/Gods/Demon Lords/Caldera, The Cataclysm/T2 - Weight for It.md

entries.title: T3 - Perfectly Huge
entries.content: Tags: Su When you flex, you exert a weak gravitational force. Whenever a creature with Line of Effect is affected by forced movement, you can choose to pull them 10 ft closer to you as an instant action after the effect is resolved. You can cause any unattended loose object you can carry with 1% of your Carry Weight to fly into a free hand if it is within 30 ft.
entries.path: Gods/Gods/Demon Lords/Caldera, The Cataclysm/T3 - Perfectly Huge.md

entries.title: Moogen, The Murderer
entries.content: Moogen is the demon lord most often associated with death, darkness and murder. The forces of the Cataclysm will come busting down your door. Moogen's forces will watch you for days, waiting for when your death will have the most impact in inspiring fear and chaos in your family, town or society as a whole. The more impact the death has, the flashier it is and the longer it stains the memory of mortals, the better. Moogen's unique obsession with death has created a unique set of circumstances. Moogen is not a singular entity, but rather a position that can be taken by someone capable of outwitting, outmaneuvering and killing the current Moogen. Moogen delights in the constant dance of death that happens all around her as a result of the tasty allure of this lucrative opportunity. Because Moogen is more of a title, rather than a singular creature, the appearance can vary greatly, but a handful of features are passed down. Moogen always has the same type of white eyes with many circles within that seem to spiral and hypnotize. They always have enormous mouths capable of snapping open and devouring a creature of equal size whole. When undercover, the mouth in excess is covered in skin, but usually it is only partly covered in skin, making it appear as though Moogen has multiple mouths.
entries.path: Gods/Gods/Demon Lords/Moogen, The Murderer/Moogen, The Murderer.md

entries.id: T1 - From my Cold Dead Head
entries.title: T1 - From my Cold Dead Head
entries.content: Tags: Sp, Recharge You can Innately Cast Memory Movie, but only on a creature that has died within the last minute. The movie persists until the spell ends, and is not tied to this 1 minute window once cast.
entries.path: Gods/Gods/Demon Lords/Moogen, The Murderer/T1 - From my Cold Dead Head.md

entries.title: T1 - Shadow in the Night
entries.content: Tags: Su Uhhh
entries.path: Gods/Gods/Demon Lords/Moogen, The Murderer/T1 - Shadow in the Night.md

entries.title: T2 - Born in Darkness
entries.content: Tags: Su, Buff Ligma
entries.path: Gods/Gods/Demon Lords/Moogen, The Murderer/T2 - Born in Darkness.md

entries.title: T3 - Shadow Blending
entries.content: Tags: Su You have Camouflage in any Dim Light or Darkness, even against creatures with Nightvision and Darkvision.
entries.path: Gods/Gods/Demon Lords/Moogen, The Murderer/T3 - Shadow Blending.md

entries.title: The Eye of Moogen
entries.content: The iconography that Moogen and her followers use is centered around her eyes. They are white with many black circles encircling each other, growing smaller until they become a pupil. It is said that Moogen herself can gaze through any Eye of Moogen, such as those printed onto banners or that are featured in the Moogen spells. As such, great care is taken by any non-worshippers to remove any and all Moogen iconography from places they don't want any prying eyes.
entries.path: Gods/Gods/Demon Lords/Moogen, The Murderer/The Eye of Moogen.md

entries.title: Shira, the She-Devil
entries.content: Shira is the demon lord most known for making deals with mortals, and with subterfuge. A master of disguise, Shira always finds a way to hide amongst the crowd in parties held by those in the highest echelons of society. She will somehow simultaneously be the most beautiful and second most beautiful lady attending the party at once. She delights in the act of giving mortals exactly what they want, but then taking so much more in return. Her deals will always be carefully worded, often with a written contract. She may misrepresent things and lie to get people to sign, and if they believed her, its their fault for not reading the fine print.
