These devilish tendencies work to break down society by eroding trust, generating discontent and playing off of the selfish desires of mankind. You didn't honestly expect her help to be free, right dearest? Shira prefers a much more appealing and innocuous form to represent herself. She takes on the form of a voluptuous fox-like beastfolk, appearing mostly human, save for the ears and tail. The only thing that would even indicate she is a demon is vertical pupils of her eyes. When in private company, she has a casual attire she likes involving loose purple clothes, and she lets all nine of her tails out. When working, such as attending parties, she tends to dress exceptionally well and hides all but one of her tails.
entries.path: Gods/Gods/Demon Lords/Shira, The She-Devil/Shira, the She-Devil.md

entries.title: T1 - Party Animal
entries.content: Tags: Su While at a social gathering, such as a party, festival, ball etc, you can exist in two places at once. This otherwise functions as a Ninja shadow clone, but the clone disappears once the event ends or you begin combat. So long as both clones aren't in someone's line of sight at once, people do not question how you pop up so frequently.
entries.path: Gods/Gods/Demon Lords/Shira, The She-Devil/T1 - Party Animal.md

entries.title: T2 - Mistrust
entries.content: Tags: Su You gain a new use to +Deception. Sow Doubt - Roll a deception check after a few minutes of conversation (DC 10+creature's level). You can convince this creature that another creature has it out for them and seeks their downfall. They will always view what this creature does in the worst light possible, which can eventually cause great strife in the future. This will stay the case until someone uses detect intrusive presence (DC 10+your deception bonus). Usually someone only gets a check who is familiar that notices this unusual behavior. For creatures who are close to one another, such as family, friends or allies, the DC increases. Usually only +2 for people they see in a positive light, +5 for allies and +10 for those closest to them.

entries.title: T3 - Devil's in the Details
entries.content: Tags: Su You gain the ability to write magically binding Fiendish Pacts. A fiendish pact requires an agreement between two parties, typically via writing, but also can be done via verbal agreements. Such pacts bind the 2 creatures to these agreements, with immediate consequences if one does not abide by the agreement. Pact Basics Pacts, like written contracts in real life, are methods of binding 2 parties together to persuade some time of cooperation or exchange. It usually involves exchanging goods, services, information or boons. Typically pacts are formed when someone desires something, but the other doesn't want to grant this desire for free. Both parties mediate until a satisfactory transaction is agreed upon. Then the 2 parties must decide on a punishment for the other should they not fulfill their end of the bargain. Again, the 2 parties come to an agreement on what the other desires. Finally, some type of gesture is taken to finalize the deal, such as shaking hands, returning a bow or signing a contract. Some creatures may take issue with the order in which the parties perform their end of the bargain, but it is inevitably up to the creature with the power to make the pact what order things happen in. Legally Binding Pacts, unlike written contracts in real life, cannot be broken without evading consequence. Pacts are made with magic that does not ask for mediation or a jury. Disobeying the contract immediately afflicts the creature with whatever the punishment is. Any kind of agreement must be within the means of the creature to perform. In the event where someone is unable to hold up their end of the bargain, or does so in a way that is not in accordance with the deal, they are afflicted with punishment immediately. You cannot also say "I will grant you anything you wish" without the ability to grant wishes. Punishments must also be within the means of both parties. You cannot just say "if you don't pet this dog 10 times then you burst into flames and die" without being able to use some type of fire magic. If you agree on a punishment that you cannot inflict, then there is no consequence for not fulfilling their end of the bargain. It us up to you to decide if the other really CAN devour your eternal soul or not. Shira's Boons Shira, and her most dedicated, are capable of providing additional boons to those they make pacts with. Contracts without meaning, such as those made between allies to acquire more boons, will draw Shira's ire and are not recommended. Twist of Fate - The creature gains a luck dice. Lexicon - Give creature information about a subject as though asking Shira herself. The amount of information she gives typically depends on what was done in the contract. Good deeds get the bare minimum to satisfy the contract while deeds that align with Shira's interest are paid in dividends.
entries.path: Gods/Gods/Demon Lords/Shira, The She-Devil/T3 - Devil's in the Details.md

entries.title: Demon Lords
entries.content: The entities that have the largest degree of influence over the Demon Realm. To say they rule over it would be giving them too much credit. The demon realm is a disgusting and dangerous twisted realm that is beyond control. The demon lords tend to spend a lot more time focused on the mortal realm, always looking to spread their influence among mortals willing to eat the forbidden fruit. In particular many spend a lot of time in their mortal forms to blend in and mingle with mortals, delivering their sales pitch directly to the ears of their would-be followers. Demon lords are themselves demons, but they break the mold, bending the rules of what the typical demon can and can't do. They can transform other creatures into demons against their will, reproduce on their own, teleport long distances and create portals to other realms, among other things atypical of lesser fiends.

entries.title: Earth Mother
entries.content: Mother Earth is the god of all things untamed and natural. She is the oldest of all deities. It is said that she was the entity that created the realm of mortals. A piece of her exists inside of all living things. As such, she has no true domain. But there are locations where life is at its most dense that are considered sacred, like lush rain forests, coral reefs and the valleys between forested mountains. Many see Mother Earth as a benevolent force, but she is primarily an entity that seeks balance between sentient creatures and the rest of the world. Many great tragedies have happened to people in the past when mankind overstepped its boundaries: Floods, earthquakes and storms the likes of which the world is better off never seeing again. These stories and their lessons are passed down through those studying under the druidic arts so that way such tragedies can be prevented from happening again. She is often depicted as a resting land mass, the bends of her shoulders, knees and hips creating mountains. While out and about, she is humanoid in shape, thought she appears to be entirely composed of lush green vegetation.

entries.title: T1 - Animal Instincts
entries.content: Tags: Recharge Special: Only Beasts can have this boon By observing a creature, you can determine its LvL as a free action. By spending a use of Prayer, you can ignore the recharge tag.

entries.title: T1 - Friend of the Forest
entries.content: Tags: Su You can pacify Beasts and Plants. If used out of combat, you can usually cause it to run off or generally be okay with your presence. If used in combat, it is a standard action, has a range of 30 ft, and inflicts the pacified condition for 1 round (will negates).
entries.path: Gods/Gods/Earthly Deities/Earth Mother/T1 - Friend of the Forest.md

entries.title: T1 - Spirit Animal
entries.content: Tags: Su, poly, true You can transform into a specific small or smaller terrestrial or aquatic beast that must be chosen when you gain this boon. This grants you Scent (5 ft) and either a swim speed, climb speed or 40 land speed. You gain no other additional abilities a creature possesses. You can only transform into this beast. The transformation lasts until you end it. You can exit this form as a swift action.

entries.title: T2 - The Forest has Ears
entries.content: Tags: Su, Scrying You can touch a living tree as a standard action to project your sense of hearing into another tree within 100 ft of it, even one you cannot see.
entries.path: Gods/Gods/Earthly Deities/Earth Mother/T2 - The Forest has Ears.md

entries.title: T3 - Nature's Vigilance
entries.content: Tags: Sp You can innately cast Voice of the Forest once a week. As part of speaking, you can choose to manifest your spirit as visible deific aspect of the earth mother, allowing her to speak through you instead of the usual effects.

entries.title: T4 - Feywilds Transportation
entries.content: Tags: - The Feywilds is a place that mirrors the mortal plane, and if you know where to look, you can temporarily slip into the feywilds and back again to speed up travel. By slipping under fallen logs, climbing into tree branches and passing through waterfalls, you can always find a portal to the feywilds in a setting unmarred by civilization within 1 hour. While traveling through such areas, you can use this popping in and out of Planes to speed up overland travel and make tracking you almost impossible. You and any you bring along can travel 1,000 miles each day through this method. Any creature that lacks this boon cannot follow you unless they roll a DC 40 +Survival check to track you. In a +Chase, you can win 1 round using this feature.

entries.title: Frogg Saran
entries.content: Frogg Saran is chaos incarnate. He is known to give blessings and curses in equal measure. Frogg Saran is a very active force in the world of Nack, worming his way into many people's lives whether they like it or not. All this god seeks is to increase the amount of entropy in the universe. His followers start riots and revolutions merely to change the status quo. They reincarnate people just to see the reaction to their new body. Frogg Saran's odd fixation on the rubbery amphibious creature causes frogs to be revered by his followers. Special care should be taken when interacting with any frog or frog-adjacent creature, or risk unwittingly getting dragged into the machinations of this mad god. You never know when what looks like an ordinary frog could be a Giant Frog, a Lunatic, or worse yet, Frogg himself in disguise. The true form of Frogg Saran is unknown, though many suspect his true form is that of a smug frog, save for its eyes are replaced with gnashing maws. It is a common way he appears before mortals, but with other common occurrences being cheese and tentacles, some depict him as an otherworldly aberration, or a rodent.. Frogg and Prayer Because it is possible to have faith in Frogg with negative WIS, the traditional scaling for Prayer doesn't work. You instead get a point of prayer for each point of LUK you have, and it doesn't always work. When he does answer prayers, they are typically in unexpected ways, and tend to be useful in ways other than providing advice. This could mean the place you are looking for suddenly explodes, the handsome fellow you were interested in talking to reveals they are a homosexual, or a frog will lead the way. In any such case, the advice/assistance comes in a much more dramatic and roundabout way.

entries.title: T1 - Enlightened
entries.content: Decrease your WIS by 1. You gain a bonus to Will saves equal to your negative wisdom. The Lunatic already starts off with an improved version of this boon and cannot take it twice.

entries.title: T1 - Frog Shape
entries.content: Tags: Su poly, true You can change into a tiny frog. While in frog shape, you gain the following qualities from the Polymorph List. | Mad Hops | Increase the distance the jump action brings you by 5 ft. You can jump vertically as high as you can jump horizontally. | | -------- | --------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | | Tongue | You can make Drag attacks as melee attacks at range of 30 ft during your turn. | | Smol | Decrease to size tiny. Decrease Reach to 0. | | Speed | Gain a Swim speed equal to Land Speed | You can transform into or back from frog form as a free action 1/turn.

entries.title: T2 - Born Again Believer
entries.content: Tags: Sp, Recharge You can innately cast Reincarnate 1/day. When you cast reincarnate, you gain access to a random innate technique that you can use once, or that is immediately Prepared for the remainder of the day. While you have this technique, you lose access to reincarnation. After you expend the technique, you regain access to reincarnation. See chart below for determining what technique you gain. Using this technique in a particularly creative and chaotic way can potentially grant you GBP. Up to GM interpretation what qualifies. Prepared techniques might require you to use the skill bonus, or let your arm fall off for something like Rapid Recovery. When you truly believe, you are rewarded. Roll on this chart to see what technique tree, then roll d6 until you get a technique level you have access to. Then roll another dice in a similar method to determine what technique in that tier (something like a d10 should usually suffice). Ex. I roll 12 and get Cataclysm. Then I roll d6 and get 6, but I'm only level 5 and don't have access to tier 6 techniques. I roll d6 again and get a 1. I roll d8 and get a 1, giving me access to the 5G technique. | Roll | Technique Tree | | ---- | ------------------- | | 1 | Warfare | | 2 | Barbarism | | 3 | Arc | | 4 | Scoundrel | | 5 | Cataclysm | | 6 | Techniques/Martial Techniques/Pupper/Pupper | | 7 | Protector | | 8 | Beastmode | | 9 | Pyromancy | | 10 | God Mode | | 11 | Necromancy | | 12 | Brazzeria (T) | | 13 | Moogen, The Murderer | | 14 | Academy | | 15 | Psionics | | 16 | Shira | | 17 | Mother Earth | | 18 | Golden Order | | 19 | Alchemy | | 20 | Frogg Saran (T) |
entries.path: Gods/Gods/Earthly Deities/Frogg Saran/T2 - Born Again Believer.md

entries.title: T3 - Forbidden Knowledge
entries.content: Tags: Sp, Recharge You can expend a use of prayer in order to find out forbidden lore concerning a creature you can see. Choose a random creature within 30 ft of you and then roll serendipity. The higher the roll, the more pertinent the information. This knowledge is always presented in a numerical format such as "2 kings killed" for important/secret high roll info or "0 dogs pet" for low impact knowledge.

entries.title: T4 - frog
entries.content: Tags: (Su)

entries.title: Hades
entries.content: Hades is the god of the dead, and the dutiful arbiter of the underworld. Hades and those who work under him attend to the souls of the dead, carefully cataloguing and maintaining records of any souls that pass through the underworld and river Styx. Hades is an entity that takes his job very seriously, and has strict rules that apply to any inhabitants. He is often worshiped by initiate necromancers, much to his dismay. Hades despises necromancy, as it only causes problems for him. More adept necromancers understand the ire their activities bring to him, and tread lightly when around his worshipers. Hades is a massive humanoid with long dark hair and an exceptionally long mustache. Clad in the bones of the fallen, he wears a crimson toga and covers himself in other forms of regalia such as gold and jewels.

entries.title: Life After Death
entries.content: When a person dies in the world of Nack, their soul separates from their body and is quickly pulled down into the Underworld. Most people plunge into the Styx immediately, losing all sense of self and becoming an amorphous unrecognizable spirit before washing up on a line in front of Hades' desk. Depending on the type of life they lived, they are sent to different areas of the Underworld. Many are given tasks to perform, the most popular of which are record keeping and patrolling the halls for those who would steal Hades' treasure or try to escape the Underworld. Particularly strong-willed people are able to resist the pull towards the Styx for a bit longer, and are able to maintain most of their appearance, albeit a bit more ghostly. Creatures that curry favor with Hades are given access to the bodies they had in life and are still privy to many of their core memories. Any and all attempts at resurrection show up on Hades' desk after the fact, and he decides how he wants to approach them on a case by case basis. Acts performed by other gods are out of his control, but noted for future dealings. Every instance of resurrection is considered a debt that must eventually be repayed. Sometimes when creatures cheat death, Hades will instruct his followers to hunt them down.

entries.title: T1 - Call of Hades
entries.content: Tags: Su Recharge, Curse You gain a unique Special Attack, usable 1/day. If you miss or Graze, its daily use is not consumed. Call of Hades On-hit apply a unique curse to a creature. The creature cannot use any means, magical or mundane, to return back to life if it killed while this curse persists. Backup bodies in an alchemy lab, the favor of gods, a Necromancer's advanced rejuvenation, it all matters not. When you attack a magical "copy" of a creature, such as Ninja's shadow clone or a Shadow Projection, it applies the curse to the original creature instead of the copy. The curse can be removed by performing an elaborate 24 hour ritual to appease Hades, which costs 2,000g. If this curse successfully denies an attempt at resurrection, gain 1 GBP.

entries.title: T2 - Visiting Hours
entries.content: Tags: Su You gain the ability to bring back the dead!...Temporarily at least. With a 1 minute ritual you can allow those grieving to say one last goodbye. You have to unambiguously identify a creature that you knew that has died within the past ten years while adjacent to the remains with a keepsake of the creature. Only a small portion of the remains is necessary. If the creature is willing to return (the creature knows who is bringing them back and who is visiting to make this decision), then a makeshift body that resembles what they looked like in life slowly materializes over the cast time. This does not disturb the remains. The creature is a simulacrum of its former self with a sandy texture to it that slowly crumbles away. A creature knowledgeable of the spell can identify a creature created by this effect with a DC 10 +Religion check. The creature can walk and talk for 24 hours before it completely fades away. Due to the limited nature of the copy, if the creature suffers 10 points of damage, they immediately crumble and turn to dust, leaving behind the keepsake used in to create the copy. They cannot Attack, use Su abilities or cast spells. This effect is typically used after soldiers die to give loved ones a chance to say goodbye to their family and friends. It can also be used to collect information that otherwise would have been lost. An individual can only be the target of this effect once.

entries.title: T3 -
entries.content: Tags: (Su)

entries.title: T4 -
entries.content: Tags: (Su)

entries.title: The Underworld
entries.content: The Underworld is a physical location in the mortal realm, though its entrance is never something that stays in the same place for very long. Usually when too many people catch wind of its location, Hades will change it to another location. The entirety of the Underworld is Hades' domain, and he takes great care to make sure that he does not get unwanted guests, and that people do not escape. The underworld is split up into several parts. The Styx The River Styx in omnipresent throughout the Underworld. Wherever you stand, you can always see it snaking through the terrain. The entire Underworld is shaped around this mystical river. The liquid that flows through it changes based on the region of the Underworld. Pure in Elysium, lava in Asphodel and a crimson blood-like liquid in most other places. Exposure to the waters of the Styx has a variety of effects that depend on circumstances. Creatures who have lived exceptionally long lives are particularly resistant to the effects of the Styx while younger creatures are wiped clean very quickly. Taking a sip from the waters will not get rid of much but taking a plunge for just a minute can wipe away years of life. Typically more important memories are more resistant to the power of the Styx: Childhood memories, cherished loved ones, sense of self and important deeds. The Precipice Starting from the entrance/exit is the precipice. Guarding the exit is the mighty three-headed beast Cerberus, standing as the Underworlds eternal guardian. He freely allows creatures to enter, but never allows them to leave. The precipice largely consists of twisted caves where the River Styx snakes around. Spirits of the dead often linger in the precipice as long as their will allows before inevitably passing through the Styx and washing up in front of Hades' desk. Creatures from the mortal realm wander into the Precipice quite frequently, filling its halls with beasts or mischievous creatures who like to desecrate Hades' altars and commune with lost spirits to lead them astray.  Elysium This region. Asphodel This region. Tartarus This regio

entries.title: +Earthly Deities
entries.content: Those that reside directly in the realms of mortals whose powers are greater than that of +Demigods. Their domains can often be physically visited, with the potential to even meet the deity in person.

entries.title: +Generic Deific Boons
entries.content: Sometimes you may desire mechanical effects without deep lore tie-ins or you like a specific god for lore reasons but don't care for their boons. This section is to provide such people with more flavor-agnostic options to mix and match a bit more, or fill in the gaps.

entries.title: T1 - Fueled by Faith
entries.content: Tags: - The gods provide. You no longer need to eat, drink or sleep. You can also stretch other physical limitations to greater extremes, allowing you to hold you breath for several times longer or keep your eyes open for extended periods without discomfort. Your appearance is always as though you were thoroughly bathed and groomed. In order to benefit from Rest you must simply perform light activity (meditation, reading, keeping watch) for 8 hours instead of sleep.
entries.path: Gods/Gods/Generic Deific Boons/T1 - Fueled by Faith.md

entries.title: Brazzeria
entries.content: Her Smoliness the Goddess Brazzeria is the goddess of redemption, often revered by the Hellion her followers create. Brazzeria is unique in that she and her followers can see the good in all but the most depraved of souls. More importantly, they also believe such creatures can be saved. They often form close intimate and personal relationships with people and creatures that many find unsavory. Combine that with their tendency towards acceptance of sexual deviancy and Brazzeria has a mixed reputation. Regardless of what mortals think, the immortal fiends of the world fear Brazzerian priests and priestesses more than anything. Brazzerian missionaries will travel to the fiendish infestations in the world to capture as many as possible for conversion, even traveling into the demon realm itself to keep a steady flow of purification rituals. Brazzeria is depicted as a light-skinned, small catgirl with feathered wings. Her skin is mostly bare with a few golden tattoos. She wears minimal clothing, preferring to wear the coif portion of a nun's habit, and an ill-fitting one that allows her golden hair to spill out of it. Outside of that she wears stockings and sleeve warmers, leaving a censorship fairy to cover up the rest. MethodDetails/GainsCaptureFiends who are dead cannot be purified, and so therefore capturing them is an integral part of Brazzerian practices. Capturing creatures nets a similar type of bonus as "slay enemies of" on the default favor page, but the values are doubled if you manage to capture fiends.Secure SpecimenSome fiends are very rare, such as those who were born fiends. Capturing one such creature nets 500*(creatures lvl) GBP.Dethrone LordDemon Lords are the highest tier of creature that can be claimed. If killed, grants value as defeating a mortal enemy. If captured, grants 100k GBP.Dethrone DukeDemonic Dukes and Duchesses are a loose way of referring to other powerful and influential fiends, but that aren't as well known and feared by all mortals who hear their name.Half the kill and capture values of a demon lord.Next LevelIntroduce a romantic partner you have been together with for at least 3 months to the idea of polyamory. It is commonplace (though not compulsory) among dedicated to Brazzerians to mingle with one another, the demons they capture and the hellion they create.By opening a relationship to such opportunities, you gain 300 GBP.EnemiesDemonic cultists, monstrous fiendsMortal EnemiesProselytes of the Golden Order during a crusade against fiends.Leaders of demon hunting organizations.

entries.title: Brazzerian Purification
entries.content: The art of purification involves turning a fiend into mortal form, purging them of their inherently sinful natures. To do this requires a lot of time and effort to be successful. The bare minimum required in order to conduct the ritual is... Mana. 100\(creature's lvl)^2. (1=100, 2=400, 3=900...12=14,400) * Proficiency in planes. * The creature's name before it transformed. With all of the above ingredients, you can draw the magic circles required, power it, and initiate the ritual. The person in charge of performing the ritual rolls a DC 17 luck check to guess the creature's true name: A word in an ancient tongue meant to represent who a person is at the core of their soul. If successful, then it transforms into a hellion. If it fails, it is evaporated, leaving only a silhouette and any of its gear/clothing behind. Because this is such a slim chance of success, and creatures only get one shot, Brazzerian priests have done a great deal of research on how to improve the likelihood of a rituals success. Each of the below things can improve odds, adding additional bonuses to the luck check. * (+1) Double the mana cost. This can stack, but it must be doubled each time. * (+2) Convince the creature to be willing to transform, usually requiring appealing the the creatures ethos. * (Up to +3) Have detailed information on who the creature was in life. Major things, like events they regret, cherished family/friends, major accomplishments contribute more. * (Up to +3) Have items significant to what the creature was in life. Items like childhood treasures, wedding rings and signature weapons/armor contribute more. * (+2) Perform ritual in sanctified area. Such areas are rare instances where Brazzeria has visited the realm of mortals to perform miracles, and are closely guarded treasures. Other things can add to the bonus that aren't listed here as appropriate. Born Fiendish In very rare instances, creatures can be born fiends, such as when they are the child of a demon lord. Such fiends ignore most of the aforementioned rules: They can automatically succeed on the ritual if they are willing, and it always fails if they are not. Their changes tend to be less dramatic, allowing them to maintain most aspects of their fiendish form and powers, making them very rare catches for Brazzerians.

entries.title: T1 - Fiends are Friends
entries.content: Tags: Su You get advantage on +Persuasion checks made against Fiends and those tainted by fiends. You can concentrate for 1 minute to get a general sense of distance and direction of the highest concentration of fiends within 100 miles. By succeeding on a +Planes check, or touching a fiend, you can tell if its soul/psyche is too shattered to be redeemed. This is most common in monstrous fiends, or amalgamations of souls.

entries.title: T2 - Introductions
entries.content: Tags: Su With physical contact, you can automatically tell what a creatures real name is, if it has one. Intimate touch, such as kissing or...wrestling...gives you a nugget of info, as though you used the Psychic Reading technique.

entries.title: T3 - Beyond Corruption
entries.content: You cannot be Dominated by fiends. Any types of corrupting effects, such as Curses, Afflictions or other GM Magic have no effect on you if they originate from Fiends. You can perform Brazzerian Purification.

entries.title: Celeste
entries.content: The Celestial Archive, often shortened to Celeste, is the goddess of hoarding knowledge. To tasks are simple: The accumulation of the knowledge of all realms, and its safeguard within her archive. She encourages her followers to dive into the depths of the sea to dredge up artifacts, or explore remains of destroyed cities to find a single book. Though her followers are often encouraged to pursue ancient and lost knowledge, they are also encouraged to relinquish it to her. Because of this, she has a mixed reputation amongst academics and researchers as they do not appreciate her taking their tomes away. In particular, her reputation amongst elves is very antagonistic, as they tend to have much more organized and robust practices when it comes to preserving knowledge. But what is the point of the accumulation of knowledge without purpose? What Celeste is actually more interested in is shaping the direction of humanity through knowledge. This only creates more questions. Depending on the age, her answer may differ: To see what happens when the world order changes to favor a new being or perhaps to witness the how far mortals can fall and bounce back from. The intrinsic promise behind her worship is that when her followers give knowledge to her, they receive knowledge in return. The knowledge she gives is very selective, meant to nudge them towards a particular path she thinks is most interesting, or perhaps the path she believes will yield the most new knowledge far in the future. In this sense, she treats all of existence as though it were one big experiment where the knowledge she holds is the only control she can exert over it. Celeste is depicted as a young women with dull gray skin that has the consistency of paper. She hovers just above the ground while she reads, wearing robes that cover most of her body. While animated, her feet pop out of the bottom of the robes so she can walk around. Her head and neck are covered in what looks like loose sheets of paper resembling messy medium length hair. There is black text written on her robes in hair in tasteful patterns that vary depending on when she is seen.

entries.title: T1 - Good Reads
entries.content: Tags: Su, Teleportation You can visibly distinguish particularly novel pieces of literature. This manifests as a golden glow that outlines the outside of the book. This light appears over all one of a kind written works, but the glow is more vibrant the more esoteric the content within. Something like the diary cataloguing the daily life of a child would have a very dim glow. A scroll containing details about a saucy love affair shared between standing leaders of neighboring nations may glow brightly. A tome containing the recipe to an immortality elixir would be blindingly bright. This brightness recedes when the tome is opened, allowing the text to be read normally. You can also perform a 1 minute ritual in order to send such tomes directly to The Celestial Archive. During this time, it appears as though the pages of the work are all falling out of it, spiraling one by one into a shining portal. As such, it leaves a lot to be desired as a stealth operation. Particularly bright tomes are encouraged and rewarded while literature deemed unimportant will be punished. - For sending unlit or dim tomes, you are punished with disadvantage on your next +Knowledge check of significance (ie rolling something just to get rid of it is discouraged). - For sending lit tomes, you are granted a reroll, usable at any time for any type of knowledge check. - For sending the particularly rare blinding tomes, you can innately cast the sending spell once to ask Celeste for a piece of esoteric knowledge. She responds with only evidence and fact-based knowledge gathered over many centuries. Esoteric knowledge can be practically anything, and is still limited by her own knowledge. After all, if she knew everything, you wouldn't be sending her these books! If she believes she cannot adequately answer your question, she will say so, and grant you the opportunity to ask another.

entries.title: T2 - Commune With Text
entries.content: Tags: Su Whenever you hold a written work, you can concentrate on it for 1 minute in order to animate the physical object, causing it to speak as though it were the author as they were when the work was completed, published etc. The author is willing to discuss the contents of their work, but only to those they deem worthy of elucidation. By showing your commitment to their field through your an appropriate +Knowledge proficiency, or through a DC 20 +Persuasion check, you can convince them to speak on the subject. Other things can potentially convince the author of your worthiness as well, such as your knowledge of Necromancy, or having an item of significance. This conversation can cause you to learn more about the context the work was written in, the intended purpose of the book, where research contained in the book was performed and other metacontextual elements of the work. Because the entity that you speak with is a snippet of the author frozen in time, you cannot teach it anything, but you can potentially ask their opinion on something they do not know about (like current events). This discussion is something that lasts up to 1 hour, after which, the written work goes back to being whatever it was prior to its creation.

entries.title: Heavenly Deities
entries.content: Those that typically reside in realms deemed higher than the realms of mortals. These realms have a greater degree of order and prosperity than most others, with some variety in appearance and structure depending which god calls it their domain.

entries.title: Emissaries
entries.content: Each god has a single mortal that they choose as their most dedicated, trusted and powerful follower. This follower is known as the emissary of X god. Emissaries gain extra boons, and usually a direct line of communication to their deity.

entries.title: Power of God
entries.content: Gods are beings that have purposefully not been given stat blocks, because fighting a god via conventional means, that being swords and magic, is impossible. An ancient and powerful pact is in place that prevents the gods from seizing mortals as puppets to enact their will, and forces them to figure out more subtle and indirect means of acquiring what they want. This same pact makes them immune to conventional weapons. Gods have so much mana flowing through them that no singular person is close to powerful enough to even scratch them. This same mana allows them to perform incredible feats, often referred to as miracles. Gods are free to walk amongst mortals only sparingly, as doing so requires a great deal of mana. As such they usually prefer only to appear in their Deific Aspects which only allow them to exert a fraction of their power, and appear as a translucent spectral version of themselves. They can also appear as a mortal, unable to exert any of their usual deific powers, and usually having a hard time gathering the attention of mortals. They also use other indirect means, such as using their followers as vessels, projecting only their voice or having their messages appear in writing via ink or blood. Gods are not all-knowing, nor are they all-powerful. They have the ability to focus their attention on many places at once, with much greater detail in places where their followers exist. They hear and answer prayers while doing other things, but often still need to defer such tasks to close allies. Certain types of things may draw their focus, such as hearing a prayer related to a matter that interests them, or a ritual in their honor conducted by one of their favorite disciples, or an enemy defacing their places of worship. Gods must carefully consider what they focus their attention on, and what they do. Their existence may seem immutable, but the countless gods lost to time suggest otherwise. They must satisfy the desires of mortals, lest they lose their source of power. As such, gods tend to only give boons to the dedicated, and only make appearances amongst mortals if it can be flashy and garner a lot of attention. A constant form of pageantry they must perform in order to maintain their power and increase their influence.

entries.title: Tiering System
entries.content: So if gods come in tiers, what determines the tier of a deity? I'll do my best to describe how I conceptualize the ideas in my head to justify my choices. A god's tier is not set in stone and can change over time. Tier 1 These are level 12 characters with the Demigod Ascendance. They are incredibly powerful and show promise, but are very much still mortal. If they can capture the hearts and minds of enough people, they can continue to exist. Few demigods ever make a lasting impact. Other high level creatures with great spiritual or cultural significance can also fall into this category, with local legends and reverence fueling their strength. Something like the painted lady from TLA. Kings and emperors of the past have been ascribed deific power/significance through concepts like the divine right of kings in the west or the mandate of heaven in the east. Tier 2 These demigods are creatures that are still mortal, but are higher than level 12. They have managed to solidify a niche on the world with a group of dedicated followers that extends past that of a single generation from a single town. The Inevitable are considered T2 deities. They represent concepts such as death or time, and destroy those who seek to defy their authority and bend their laws. Their active role in the mortal realm prevents them from ever becoming more powerful deities. Tier 3 This is where gods become untouchable to mortals. They do no have stat blocks and can no longer directly intervene in the lives of mortals. See the power of god page for more details. Tier 3 gods are well known in many regions of the world. Entire races and countries subscribe to their way of life. Demon Lords are commonly T3 gods, as their followers are forced to constantly butt heads with creatures around the world, keeping their population and influence in check. Tier 4 The most powerful of all the deities represent forces so fundamental to existence that they cannot help but be relevant. Basically every intelligent creatures knows of, and must respect these deities, lest they feel their wrath. Other gods bend their knees to these deities and allow their followers to abide by certain practices, such as respect for the dead or reverence for the natural world, to avoid having their followers wiped out.

entries.title: Deific Boons
entries.content: The gods of have a vested interest in the goings on in the world of mortals, and what better way to get mortals to do your bidding then to give them your favor when they act in your interest? This system is designed to provide active incentives for characters to enact the will of the deities in the world: the more that you please a god with your actions, follow their tenets and donate to their causes, the more favor they grant to you. The amount of favor that the gods provide to you is broken up into tiers. The tier a favor that you're at is determined by the amount of God Benediction Points (GBP) you earn through various tasks. In general, you cannot earn the favor of a god by accident. In all but the most extreme cases, such as defeating the mortal enemy of a god, you must first learn about a deity to start gaining their favor. As such you cannot start accruing GBP until you have positive WIS, or speaking with priests, cultists or froggs to learn more about what the gods desire. Some classes, and characters with positive wisdom start off with GBP, and gain it passively as they level up by doing things like Mana Donation at local religious institutions, attending services, making offerings to their god or converting others. Every 10 GBP you gain an additional boon. | Wis | GBP Scaling | | --- | ------------ | | +1 | 10+(LvL) | | +2 | 10+2*(LvL) | | +3 | 11+3*(LvL) | By performing tasks that cater more closely to a specific god's desires, you can earn actively earn more GBP. As you advance in tiers the criteria for what grants additional GBP should also increase in difficulty. The inverse is also true: If you act in such a way that your god disapproves, you can lose GBP. Such losses can often be recouped through forms of repentance. Here's another handy chart to help you determine what boons you have based on your wisdom at later levels. | Wis | LV for T1 boon (10 GBP) | T2 (20) | T3 (30) | T4 (40) | T5 (50) | | --- | ----------------------- | ------- | ------- | ------- | ------- | | +1 | 1 | 10 | - | - | - | | +2 | 1 | 5 | 10 | - | - | | +3 | 1 | 3 | 7 | 10 | - | Not all gods are created equal. The older and more powerful a god is, the more boons they can offer. Level 12 characters that choose the Demigod Ascendance can only grant T1 boons, while ancient deities that everybody knows and respects like the Earth Mother can grant the highest tier boons. Some gods even have multiple boons that creatures can pick and choose from at certain tiers. Frogg Saran doesn't play by the rules of the other deities. He is the only deity you can accrue GBP for if you have negative wisdom, and you can't gain GBP from him passively. See his page for more details. GBP and Polytheism When you gain GBP through leveling up passively, you can decide to distribute these points however you wish between gods you worship. You can accrue boons from any number of gods. However...Some gods are completely incompatible. Namely Frogg Saran. No god would willingly associate themselves with any who abide by his way of life. Most deities are incompatible with Demon Lords as they have conflicting ethos. Other such examples can exist, such as the eternal struggle for relevance between Brazzeria and Artemis. If the ideals, practices and goals of the gods you worship do not conflict in major ways, then you can accrue GBP from multiple gods without incurring their wrath.

entries.title: Invoking Deities
entries.content: Deities can give their favor in specific ways, but invoking their name can give you a more general boon. Whenever you would perform a task that falls in line with the god's ethos, goals or tenets, you can choose to invoke their name as a free action. This could mean a contest of strength for The Cataclysm, a persuasion check made against a fiend for Brazzeria, or a survival check to track down a hunter exterminating wildlife for the Earth Mother. The DM is the final arbiter to decide if your request is answered. When you invoke a deity, you expend a use of prayer and gain a d8 to any single d20 roll (minus something like a random chart). If you invoke and your deity disapproves, you lose that use of prayer, and you get an impression of why your request for aid failed. You can only invoke a deity that you have curried favor for in most circumstances. When you invoke, you cannot do so again for 1 minute.

entries.title: Santa
entries.content: The Psionics tree is something that has been developed piecemeal over long periods of time, culminating into its current form under the guidance of an entity by the name of Santa. Those who are more well informed may make the connection that Santa is actually an alias for an entity known as Glass Reflection: An Elf that was a world-renowned Spy once upon a time. Enough time passes and the mortals forget who you are. Those who go to great lengths to find out more, trying to meet Santa or through dedication to the Chrimbus Spirit, may even learn her true identity: Colene Larella. She goes to great lengths to keep her true identity hidden, hiding behind aliases and intermediaries for all but the most important of interactions. Using a powerful device that amplifies her psychic powers, she scans the world for those who struggle, primarily children, and does her best to aid them in their trying times. She gives them gifts, sometimes in the form of presents, other times in companionship through a Poplin sibling.

entries.title: Demiplane
entries.content: Demiplanes are a step above extradimensional spaces. They exist in the same ether between planes, but they are typically much larger and are permanent. They are typically created via magic like Fairy Ring Retreat. It is technically possible to reach a demiplane with the correct tuning fork, but because demiplanes are typically private locations, it can be very difficult to obtain one, and the creators of the planes often have special abilities they can use to remove unwanted guests. Demiplanes are shaped in accordance with the desires of their creators, and thus vary wildly in form and function. Some are secret laboratories used by alchemists to hide their secrets and others are lavish buildings used for exclusive parties.

entries.title: Extradimensional Space
entries.content: Extradimensional spaces are locations that exist in an intermediary plane. They are small, self-contained bubbles in an unnavigable ether. These spaces are typically created by mages, such as through the Arcane Pocket technique. There are no conventional means to access extradimensional spaces other than through the portal created by the effect that caused them to spawn into existence. Extradimensional spaces are often temporary and fleeting.

entries.title: Feywilds
entries.content: Wonderland, the Emerald Dream, the Wild Lands. The Feywilds has many names, but it all refers to the same place. A long time ago, a group of elven druids dreamed of a better world. One where humanity did not, and could not, corrupt the beauty found in nature. They developed magic to visit each other in dreams and create entirely new worlds to exist in while the slept. Sleep was so cumbersome that they eventually developed a technique called trance that would function similarly to sleep, but that could be slipped in and out of much more easily. The waking and dreaming world blurred for some. This caused problems in the mortal realm as many of their people grew increasingly disconnected from what only some considered reality. In secret, the dreamers gathered the research and mana required to perform an elaborate ritual that would forever blur the line between the real and the imaginary. This ritual to create this "dream world" was disturbed and violence ensued. For many, the dream turned into a nightmare. The thoughts, feelings, desires and fears on that day shaped what the Feywilds would become. The dreamers were successful at creating their own world, but not in exactly the way they had hoped. The Feywilds is a plane that is entirely untamed wild land, usually filled with lush green and other bright colors. Any attempts to disturb this natural order by clearing out trees and building structures causes negative feedback from the plane itself. Such structures never last long. The types of creatures that exist in the Feywilds, known as fey, are born from the imaginations of the dreamers. Due to how the ritual went awry, some areas of the dream became nightmares. Many of the dreamers' fey creations were capricious, but those born from the nightmares are outright despicable. The regions they inhabit are dark forests and mist filled swampland that one can never feel safe in. The most well known creatures from the Feywilds are fairies. They are creatures with a wide array of sizes, shapes and purposes. The typical fairy can fit in the palm of your hand and loves to lead people astray for the fun of it. Others work to protect the purity of creatures, conveniently censoring areas considered inappropriate. Then tooth fairies are sinister creatures with an obsession for collecting teeth, especially straight out of a living creature's skull. A few fairies, known as the great fairies due to their size, power and influence, create havens inside of the Feywilds and the mortal realm. These are the most well known ways to get to and from the Feywilds. Other noteworthy creatures include the eternal and hags. The eternal are a race of creature imagined with many of the positive traits of elves, such as their ability to physically change through adulthood, their ability to introspect and learn from long periods of history. They exist in small groups, harmoniously with nature and their communities. The hags are much more sinister creatures. Witches of the wild, hags often live for hundreds of years, using magical rituals to even evade death. They keep in touch with their sisters and gather when they want to perform powerful magic. They have a soft spot in their hearts for mortals, whom they will assist, but always at a greater cost.

entries.title: Planes
entries.content: The world of Nick Nacks is one that is not one planet, or even one dimension. The subsections below are a list of the other dimensions, called planes, that have properties that differentiate it from the mortal realm.

entries.title: The Demon Realm
entries.content: You can read more about demons and how they came to be in the bestiary section for Fiends In short, certain negative events can cause creatures to transform into entirely different hostile creatures with new outlooks, powers and appearances known as fiends, or more colloquially, demons, devils, daemons etc. Fiends, generally speaking, don't care for much other than to consume, enslave, seek vengeance and destroy what other creatures hold beloved. As such, they are near universally reviled by all. Only the most sinister creatures who seek to direct fiendish hatred towards their enemies and Brazzeria think otherwise. Demons can generally be expected to cooperate with each other, or evildoers who align with their interest. Such alliances are generally shaky however, and often result in backstabbing and betrayal, with the terms being renegotiated whenever the demons see an opportunity. Particularly monstrous fiends are virtually impossible to tame. The realm of the demons was created by ancient demon lords looking to escape their would-be hunters in the mortal realm. It was purposefully created to be as hostile to mortals as possible, with complex tunnels, oceans of blood and seemingly endless freezing desserts. The worst of the mortal realm is the norm in the demon realm. The creation of this realm was a double edged sword however. A group of mortals that later became known as the Light Guardians figured out how to forcefully send demons to this world, effectively turning it into a prison. Banishment, while not a permanent solution, is often the easier one when fiends pour into unprepared villages in droves. Getting to and from the demon realm can be a bit of a chore without the correct magic. This is by design. The demon lords don't want mortals to freely move in to hunt their forces down. The mortals don't want hoards of demons destroying their countryside. Only through mortals that betray their kin, or the direct intervention of a demon lord can portals between the realms be opened permanently. Temporary means such as Plane Shift, or for Brazzerians seeking to purify demons, Hell and Back, also exist as methods to go to and escape the demon realm, but demons are almost never capable of using such magic. The demon lords themselves vary between the ages, but they tend to fall into 3 distinct categories: Destruction and strength, stealth and slaughter, subversion and subjugation. You can read more about the current demon lords in the Deific Boons section. Each of them has their own domain (large swathes of land thousands of miles wide) where they and their minions reside.

entries.title: The Shadow Realm
entries.content: Not much is known about the shadow realm by most, other than it is a prison for evil people that once tried to take over the world. To the Elven mages that created it though, they know the Shadow Realm to be an entirely subterranean world with large sets of interconnected tunnels. Since its creation though, it has changed a great deal. At the turn of the era to The Age of Darkness a group of heroes visited the Shadow Realm to find that its inhabitants had changed nearly as much as the realm itself. Their accounts revealed that the realm was now wrought with rivers of blood pooling out of cave mouths into enormous lakes. Tunnels of worked stone led to numerous rooms that were often dedicated to profane rituals or prisons. A unique breed of fiends obsessed with self-perfection took to the art of warping flesh, stealing it from others in order to graft onto themselves until they were no longer recognizable from the elves they once were. They would take creatures and "perfect" them by shaping them into horrifying monstrosities to do their bidding. Fleshwarping, as it was called, became a word powerful enough to send a shiver down your spine. Little was explored during this time, but considering the nature of the creatures that emerged from within during wars at the turn of the era, its safe to assume they many regions within the Shadow Realm are twisted in their own unique ways. Read more in the +Factions section to learn about the culture of the deep elves trapped in the Shadow Realm.

entries.title: +The Timeline
entries.content: The way that people organize history in Nack is a bit strange. Time is separated into blocks of centuries or decades following events that warped the world known as ages, and these ages are named after the world-threatening tragedy that was conquered in the previous age. Counterintuitively, this means that the names of ages tend to lag behind a bit. Sometimes this can be a boon, since the consequences of tragedy can be something that sticks around for many years after. Most of the content in +The Timeline was designed as the past to the current era, The Age of Darkness! As such, most of the framing of Nick Nacks involves all those events having happened hundreds of years ago. Entirely new deities have fallen and risen in these centuries, new types of magic and all kinds of things. Running games in past ages is actively encouraged! Let me know how it goes and how you changed the course of history! The ages are listed in sequential order, with each coming after the next. The oldest ages are largely only known thanks to ancient beings that have lived since then, such as Dragons, Giants or the rare Elf. The Age of Titans - 400 years The start of this age was marked by the defeat of the Titans, the progenitors to all modern day Giants. The Age of Dragons - 400 years The start of this age was marked by the defeat of a colossal dragon that drained the mana from the world around it, causing all kinds of negative side effects. This era marked a time where mostly Dragons dominated the world. The Age of Underground - 200 years The start of this age was marked by the successful banishment of many societies of humanoids that lived underground to The Shadow Realm. Sometimes colloquially known as "The Age of Man" because of how humanoids superseded dragons as the dominant species in Nack. The Age of Magic - 70 years The start of this age was marked by an organization of anti-mages known as Arc (L) somewhat successfully destroying foundational aspects of magic. The Age of Darkness - 70 years (present era) The start of this age was marked by the resealing of the Shadow Realm and the destruction of an all consuming darkness that threatened to consume the sun. The cold southern landmass of Simbasa has since been fighting against the shadowling, warriors that still cling to the Mortal Realm.

entries.title: The Age of Darkness
entries.content: Dawn of a New Age The turn of the age to the Age of Darkness was marked by a great war that swept across the entire world. It was spearheaded by the 3 royal dark Elf factions that were unsealed from The Shadow Realm by a powerful shadowling simply known as "The Darkness." It first landed on Nack and plunged into the underground, burned by the light of day. Realizing if the world was to be plunged in darkness, it would need to remove the sun, so it began a process to consume it. Each day was shorter than the last as the sun was visibly covered in the sky. The dark elves had a method of transforming surface dwelling elves into mindless drones that resembled their kind. One primary objective of the wars was to completely consume the elven population, and thus much of the fighting happened in The Land of Storms. The Flame Empire was at the peak of its power at this point in time and saw this as an opportunity to spread their empire to entirely new continents, and served as wartime allies to the forces of darkness. Always ready to pounce at opportunities to make money, The Ravens were also happy to facilitate the forces of darkness. During this time, a few heroes emerged: Zeke, The Luckiest Man Alive, Visa, The Oracle and Lale, The Eye of the Storm. They travelled the world to learn about the history of an ancient people that commanded light. Through their adventures, they became powerful enough to battle against many top generals, and even the elven queens themselves. The fallout of this war was felt for many decades to come. Slowly but surely the world returned to normalcy. With the deep elves and their forces defeated, any that remained scrambled into whatever dark corners of the world they thought they would not be found. The Land of Storms experienced yet another tragedy that decimated their population in the same generation. Many were spiteful and wanted to completely eliminate them all, so an organization was created to pay mercenaries to do it for them. Treaties were signed that provided them with reparations, at the expense of The Flame Empire. Eventually the elves learned to distinguish the difference between those who were born dark elves, and those who were forcefully transformed. For decades, tens of thousands of prisoners were kept. Many suffered even worse fates thanks to the twisted creations of the evil queens via fleshwarping. Eventually, a foreign alchemist created a powerful serum that could restore their minds, and to an extent, change their bodies. They would lose the dark obsidian skin and turn a light purple or pinkish color. These elves, halfway between, are colloquially known as night elves. In particular, the southern landmass of Simbasa filled with warring tribes was devastated by the spread of Shadowling taint. The entire southern coast was covered in a shade that allowed the shadowlings to continue roaming free.

entries.title: The Age of Dragons
entries.content: Dawn of a New Age At the turn of this new age, dragons were cursed to be nothing more than giant flying lizards by Drakthar, the World Ender, who consumed their power in order to fuel his own. Only the strongest and smartest of the dragons even remotely resembled what the typical dragon we imagine today looks like. Drakthar's highest generals were given dominion over different regions of the world, and were given powers many of the others were not: - Ueli was bestowed power over life - Muranith was bestowed power over magic - Druj was bestowed power over time - Suirei was bestowed power over the creatures that walked the earth - T'zarborn was bestowed power of the primordial forces beneath it Each of these individuals was forced to rule over their respective domain with impunity, harvesting mana from all living creatures to feed to their master. Chance meetings with one another during times of rebellion caused them to realize tales they had heard about the prosperous lands elsewhere were lies. Disillusioned and dissatisfied, they hatched a plan. They started to slack on their mana donations, consuming it for themselves slowly over time, or allowing close allies to build up their strength. Some elven and dwarven societies proved especially instrumental in strategizing the monumental task before them. The ultimate battle was a bloody affair, lasting nearly a whole year. Drakthar, once angered, chased his generals around the world, fighting any and all that did not bow their head. This chase would later be called "The Great Shade Offensive" after how dragons would block out the light from the sky wherever the battle took them. The elven mages Muranith trained set traps in his domain that they planned to be the killing blow. Drakthar proved resilient beyond reason, but in their darkest hour, an unexpected ally appeared: A dragon that descended upon the world from the stars that Suirei managed to reach out to in a dream named Memve'Ele. With his assistance, one final push was made, but with a slight change in strategy. They banished Drakthar to The Shadow Realm; a realm that he could not escape from in his weakened state. Once Drakthar was defeated, his power was restored to its natural place and was once again allowed to suffuse the world. In particular, the band of 5 dragons that defeated him became the first true dragons: the 4 legged quadrupeds with large wings jutting from their back. These progenitors made it their mission to spread the love so to speak, and granted all draconic creatures sentience. They eventually became known as The Dragon Aspects. Many other dragons had different evolutionary paths, such as the wyverns with winged arms, or serpentine dragons that used magic to fly. One group was bipedal and these were later called Proto-Dragons. They didn't have nearly the same offensive profile as other dragons, tending to be much smaller with some being unable to fly at all. The dragon aspects did their best to allow the world to heal after Over time, many groups of humanoids hunted down dragons. In particular, subsections of races that were confined to the underground parts of the world for many generations while others of their race were allowed to roam the surface. The deep Elf and Dwarf peoples, spearheaded this hunt. Part of it was the hundreds of years of animosity, but part of it was wanting to steal their power. In particular, tribes of elves and dwarves that were once forced to live deep underground used this time in history to become powerful civilizations off the backs of the mana and materials they hunted from dragons. One such group was the Proto Dragons. Already small in number, many were captured and sealed away using powerful curse magic so their mana could be harvested for generations. For many centuries, humanoid dragons all but disappeared. It would not until Gralth the Inferno finally reintroduced the species during the The Age of Magic, with the hopes of using the species as a way to improve dragon-humanoid relations. In the decades since, the species has been allowed to flourish, mostly forming tight family groups in Land of Earth.

entries.title: The Age of Magic
entries.content: First, a spot of history... During the The Age of Dragons, Muranith placed some of his power inside of rare gems and they were used as proxies to help teach others about magic that had been stolen from them by Drakthar, the World Ender. These gems were called "the Essence of Magic" for they took literally crystallized knowledge of specific types of magic in mana. It was this age that once again solidified ancient schools of magic like Necromancy, Psionics and the Arcane. For centuries, this worked as intended. As the dawn of the new age came, these gems were used and abused by unsavory individuals who wished to consume the essences of magic for themselves to gain infinite power. Muranith decided that mortals had learned enough, and took back his these gems and hid them away in the far reaches of the world, or in other realms entirely. Dawn of a New Age Hundreds of years later, an organization by the name of Arc (L) emerged in response to the absurd advances in magical techniques. It created a system of haves and have nots where those who could use magic took over the world, and those that couldn't were second class citizens. At least that's how it was spun. Those wronged by mages didn't care much about the accuracy, and instead rallied behind Arc's leader, Darius, who wanted to expose to the world the dangers of magic. He learned about the Essences of Magic when he discovered one during an archeological excavation. Despite how little magic training he had prior, he immediately became a master of conjuration (the closest analogue in modern times is God Mode). The stoic Darius revealed little about personal reasons as to why he became so obsessed with this goal. Perhaps the gem drove him mad and that was the reason Muranith took the essences away all those years ago. Nevertheless, Arc training involved rigorous anti-mage tactics, heavily relying on effects that disrupted the casting of spells. Using the essence of conjuration, Arc managed to develop the capacity to teleport entire armies, which they used in order to organize a full scale invasion of the biggest city in The Land of Earth, Emferal. In addition to teleporting in large numbers of his own forces, Darius made deals with powerful Fiends, allowing them to come to the Mortal Realm if only they turned their hatred of mortals towards the cities inhabitants first. He also received aid from Laira, The Sunken Sister who was an adept Necromancer interested in that particular essence of magic to add a healthy mix of undead to the mix. The battle was incredibly destructive, the city mostly reduced to rubble, but Arc's forces were eventually repelled by the members of an adventurer's guild known as The Goons, forced to retreat again via teleportation, but the damage had already been done. No battle of that scale every happened again in the age. This was mostly the result of the heroic deeds of Lance Crownguard, Niv the Protector and their close allies hunting down Arc and its members. The battle of Emferal had significantly weakened Arc, to the point where they needed to rely on dubious deals with The Ravens in order to scrape by. Eventually all alliances collapsed and Darius was desperate. He used his power of teleportation to steal Lance's loved ones to goad him into a final confrontation. This culminated in a battle in a private Demiplane Darius had created in order to store the essences he managed to gather and suppress their power. There he destroyed them in a last act of defiance before he was swiftly struck down by Lance's daughter Cinder. The reverberating effects of the essences being destroyed affected magic of all kinds. It was later referred to as "The Shattering." This event caused magic itself to be unstable. Every day of the week was plagued with a specific school of magic being completely unavailable, with the final day of each weak completely disabling all magic. The effects of the shattering lasted well into The Age of Darkness when Muranith was found in seclusion and convinced to return to rectify the events of the shattering. Without its leader, Arc was little more than a band of bandits clinging to a recognizable name. Emferal was rebuilt, largely off of the back of a new age of discovery ushered in by Aria Lyra and her new inventions, Rocks. Lyra Corp was so profitable in fact that she was able to used all of the wealth and power she accumulated to eventually create an entirely new continent: The Brotherhood of Steel. The spiritual successor to the Goons, The Cinder Squad, led by the titular Cinder, also played a major role in attracting all kinds of talent back to the destroyed town. Being a savior of the world has its perks for recruiting. It was also during this age that The Ravens reached the pinnacle of their power. Through a coordinated attack, they managed to control large swathes of land along the western coast of the Land of Earth, even spreading their influence into southwest and the Skyspear Peaks. There they sealed off the Kingdom of the Flying Boar's land access to the rest of the world by placing taxes on travel trough Skyspear Valley, often called "Border Valley" because of its importance with trade between neighboring nations.

entries.title: The Age of Underground
entries.content: Dawn of a New Age The turn of the age to the Underground was marked by the 3 dark elven queens, Shi'Sulm, Shi'Zen and Shi'Zara leading a coordinated attack on major population centers around the world, both for dragons and man. This coincided with a betrayal of one of The Dragon Aspects, T'zarborn, and his corruption by the hands of the Dark Iron Dwarves. One clan in particular, The Blackwing Clan, did not hunt down dragons as many of the neighboring clans did, instead opting to accept the aid and companionship of T'zarborn. A sinister plot was in the making to pull T'zarborn away from his fellow dragon aspects and fill his head with grandiose ambitions. This was a plan that would take time though, and the Blackwing Clan was extremely patient. They offered him aid in any way they could, such as to take care of his children. This service became more and more important in providing them with a safe haven in a world that was becoming increasingly hostile to dragon-kind over the centuries. One child in particular, T'zarzenite, was held in regard high above the rest for her peculiar talent in producing quantities of offspring expected from dozens of her kind. For this she was given the title "Broodmother." She was practically worshipped as much as her father was by the Blackwings. As the The Age of Dragons entered its 5th century, the Blackwing Clan's plan entered its final stage. Previous alliances crumbled one by one, and T'zarborn had little else to turn to that his still loyal friends in the Blackwings. It was now they finally decided to remind him of his importance in defeating Drakthar, the World Ender, and how he was the mightiest of the dragon aspects. The old ways were failing, and it was time to usher in a new age. One with him at the top. They were able to manipulate him into forcing T'zarzenite to bear an army practically all on her own so that way he could take control of the world. T'zarborn and the Blackwings first started with ulu The fallout of this war was felt for many decades to come.

entries.title: The World of Nack
entries.content: Where to start? Nack is my version of earth. Nack is filled with humanoids of varying sizes, shapes and lifespans. It has plants and animals, some of which you will recognize, and many you won't. There are plenty of other types of creatures that break our IRL classifications entirely: Elementals that are composed of normally inanimate substances, aliens from other worlds looking to steal our cows and creatures raised from the dead. The various regions of the world all have a story to tell. The Land of Storms is named such because there is a persistent drizzle over the continent, often increasing in intensity in storms. The Underworld is a place hidden away in the darkest reaches of the world, constantly shifting its position to prevent people from trying to get in to bring others back to life. The universe is suffused with magic! Those with the talent to use mana can bend the world to their will. Wizards can use it to conjure fire from their fingertips, healers can use it to cure those burns and warriors can use it to cause the earth to quake beneath their feet. Everybody is born with access to mana, and history is made by civilizations that know how to use it wisely. Gods are tangible creatures that can be interacted with. By acting in your gods name, you can receive insight into situations and special powers. Gods each have their own special interests in the world, such as maintaining the natural order, increasing the quantity of happiness in the world, or killing people just for the fun of it. Particularly dedicated followers may even get the chance to meet their god in person. There are bits of lore scattered all around the website already. The dedicated lore section is more for the world itself, its culture, history, cosmology and noteworthy people. Each race has its own lore section, gods have lore written in their sections, and eventually so will each bestiary entry.

entries.title: +Advice for Playing the Game
entries.content: Playing tabletop can be a daunting experience. With so many rules and mechanics that you are unfamiliar with, it may discourage you from playing. This section is to give you advice on how to make the process feel a bit less daunting. If you don't really care about what I have to say about playing the game, then skip ahead to the rules. This section is mostly intended for prospective players and game masters...Or dungeon masters, or keepers or whatever funny name you call it.
entries.path: Meta Discussions/Advice for Playing the Game/+Advice for Playing the Game.md

entries.title: +Rules, Mechanics and Expectations, Oh My!
entries.content: Many people going into TTRPGs believe they are ill-prepared. I am here to tell you that even after a decade of playing, that feeling has never gone away. I'll tell you something else, my friends and I look back at the time we first started playing with nostalgia. The experience of learning a new system together with your friends can cause you to butt heads sometimes, but it reveals something incredible that is unique to tabletop: The rules are more like guidelines. Sure they structure play, and you want rules to help you decide how xyz works, but you don't need to follow every rule. I'm not going to call the Tabletop Police Force because you think how this ability works is stupid and what were the developers thinking? I do recommend writing down how you change the rules somewhere though so you can refer back to it. This is a long winded way of me saying learning rules to a complex system takes time, and fumbling through the journey is half the fun. If your worries are that you won't do a good job or you'll forget the rules then make up rules how they make sense to you, or use this incredible website to find the rule. I made this game and I forget the rules, forcing me to have to look things up. Making shit up and altering the game in ways that make it more fun is what TTRPGs are all about.
entries.path: Meta Discussions/Advice for Playing the Game/+Rules, Mechanics and Expectations, Oh My!.md

entries.id: But Who is the Game Master
entries.title: But Who is the Game Master
entries.content: Out of everybody in the group, the game master, or GM, is the one who is undoubtedly going to be putting the most effort into the game. It can often feel like a bit of a drawing straws scenario where someone has to put up with the extra work so everybody can enjoy a game. Chances are if you are introducing a new game to your friends, you're going to be the one to run the game since you "know the rules" or whatever excuse your buddies come up with. I'm here to tell you you need not fret: Becoming a GM is easy! It's becoming a great GM that's hard. I'll give you a few hints and shortcuts that can make preparing a game from scratch, potentially even for the first time, much easier. Don't start from nothing! Chances are if you're playing this game, you love RPG games, anime, fantasy or all the above. You can steal all kinds of things from your previous experiences. You can steal characters, history, maybe even an entire world from your favorite media. Nintendo isn't going to sue you if you make your exploration game based off of Breath of the Wild...Probably. What they won't know won't hurt them. Start saving now. This is not financial advice. No but seriously. I'm still assuming a bit here, but I'm guessing you use something like twitter or YouTube for entertainment. Maybe you're even the artsy type and you browse Deviant Art or Pinterest. Though those places have become AI cesspools...Anyway, you're going to want to fill your worlds with people, places and things! Find pictures and videos that inspire you. Being inspired by something is your creative brain salivating at the idea of making it your own. Whether you find pictures of old ruins you want your players to explore, an awesome monster you want them to fight, or NPCs to fill your towns with, you need these things to make the world feel great. Maybe you can even commission someone if you're feeling generous and need something specific. I learned how to draw just to make pictures of my characters! See how others have done it. Just recently I decided I wanted to include a maze in my game. I have done mazes before, but they were pretty small and included a map. This made it so players could just see where they have been from me getting rid of the fog of war and made it feel like a process of elimination. But what do you do when things are more complicated? What if the maze is too big for a map? What if the maze changes? I wanted to try out something new to get a better maze feel. So I looked up how other people have done their labyrinth sessions. I decided to do something a bit more abstract where things the party found gave them clues and eventually when they gathered enough clues, they found their way through. Not exactly the true misery intended from a labyrinth, but we are trying to have fun after all. I wouldn't have had the idea if others hadn't tried it before me and posted their experience online. Maybe the weird stuff you want to do in your game you can learn from someone else's mistakes too!
entries.path: Meta Discussions/Advice for Playing the Game/But Who is the Game Master.md

entries.content: Not sponsored BTW. Matt Colville is a game designer that's been in the TTRPG space for a long time and has much more wisdom than I when it comes to these matters. If you hate reading and love watching youtube playlists for hours...Here you go! If you've got a question or just want to enhance an aspect of your game, its here. Also saves me the effort. c: https://www.youtube.com/playlist?list=PLlUk42GiU2guNzWBzxn7hs8MaV7ELLCP_
