entries.title: Genies!
entries.content: Here I put a bunch of soft measurements for how wishes are treated in various forms of media. For distribution, red means its widely distributed ruining a bit of the mystique behind the wish. Green properly restricts access to wishes. For limitations, red means that there are numerous limitations, making the wish feel a lot less wish-y with how little it can do. Green means little to no limitation. For Intent, red generally means the wish is out to get you, and a surefire way of replacing your hopeful optimism with despair. Green is is wish does everything in its power to fulfill your desires, even if they aren't explicitly stated. Orange is somewhere in between. Fairly Odd Parents Fairies Distribution Limitations Intent Fairly Odd Parents Genie Distribution Limitations Intent Aladdin's Genie Distribution Limitations Intent DnD 5e Wish Distribution Limitations Intent Pathfinder Wish Distribution Limitations Intent Shenlong/Porunga Distribution Limitations Intent

entries.title: Reactions Jank
entries.content: In NN, there are a wide variety of abilities that allow you to react to your opponents actions, like +Shield Actions, but sometimes those abilities allow retaliation. This usually works just fine since you get attacked in melee, you parry and then riposte as a Swashbuckler for instance. But what happens when the origin of the attack is somehow technically coming from a creature but at the same time it isn't? Take for example the Arboreal Army spell. It allows you to take control of vegetation and slap your enemies with it using your stats. What's a swashbuckler to do, riposte a tree? In such cases, when a creature is making attacks, casting spells etc. through some kind of proxy, it is still considered to be coming from the original attacker. This means that if your swashbuckler is threatening the druid while they smack you with a tree, you can parry the tree, then riposte against the attacker.

entries.title: +I'm New to Tabletop Role-Playing
entries.content: Welcome! This section will assume you know basically nothing. What is a Tabletop Role-Playing Game (TTRPG) Anybody can play pretend (role-play), but without any structure, things tend to fall apart. TTRPGs seek to create a foundation for you to make stories with your friends (game). They are designed to be played with pen, paper and dice (hence tabletop), but such things can also be substituted for dice rolling programs, word documents for character sheets and sharing your screen over discord for battle maps. For a game like Nick Nacks, the idea is to let you pretend to be in a living breathing fantasy world where strange creatures like elves and goblins are the norm. It assumes you are playing a small group of people with specialized training and high ambitions. This could mean preventing world ending catastrophes from ancient powerful forces like dragons, or it could mean trying to start up a business in a cutthroat world of magic and mayhem. The moment to moment gameplay typically involves the Game Master (GM) describing environments, mannerisms of people in the world and how the world reacts to the players actions. The GM plays the role of referee and author, writing the framework for the story and arbitrating how events unfold. Players are typically presented with problems, quests and enemies to overcome with clever solutions or overwhelming force, with the results of said encounters being decided by dice rolls. A very simple interaction could involve the following sequence. Game Master - Describes details about a scenario, say an unsavory bar at a coastal town. Player 1 - "I want to talk to the unsavory pirates." Player 2 - I'll watch from the sides to make sure nobody gets up to any funny business Game Master - "Describes how they react, what they say and how they brandish their weapons when someone unfamiliar approaches." Player 2 - "I don't like that. I'll get ready to sneak attack them." Player 1 - "Hold on, maybe they're friendly." Game Master - "They are most certainly not friendly. Player 2 roll stealth and see if you surprise...Unfortunately they see you coming, roll initiative." Combat is how you will be spending a good amount of your time. Many classes (like Fighter) are designed almost exclusively to excel in combat. More details on that below. What is combat? In fantasy, you will encounter a wide variety of problems: Magical rituals that need to be stopped, infestations of giant rats and shapeshifters weaved amongst political allies. Combat is not always necessary, as some players may enjoy trying to solve problems with their wits over their weapons. But combat is inevitable in some situations, where ideals clash and lives are on the line. Some players only really care about the combat. It takes a bit of playing the game yourself to figure out where you fall on the spectrum. Nick Nacks, and games like it, are turn based. The way initiative works guarantees a back and forth battle between the party of players and their foes. One player decides what they want to do, then an enemy, then an ally and so on. You fill your turns with spells and attacks until you complete your goal, none of your enemies remain, they surrender, or you need to make a hasty retreat. Do I have to do a funny voice? The role-playing aspect of Nick Nacks is what sets it apart from many other games like World of Warcraft or Skyrim. These games might advertise themselves as role-playing, but the choices you get to make are usually pretty slim. In skyrim when you approach a locked door, you can either pick it or use a key. Some doors don't even let you pick them for story reasons. Nice thief character! In tabletop when you approach a locked door, you can pick it, you can break it down with raw strength, explosives. You can use magical abilities to circumvent it by shrinking to fit into the key hole. You can dig under or over it. Your ability to solve problems has limitations, but your options are typically only limited to your ability to imagine a solution. Not to mention, this is just a simple problem. Imagine the kinds of creative solutions you'll need to employ during a prison break or finding who is spying on the king! "The funny voice" is short hand for speaking in character with an accent, and you do it as much as you want. Once upon a time, GMs frequently did not even allow people to speak in character. You can use your voice, some other voice, or you can choose to only describe what your character wants to say. You could also just decide to be mute! I'm no voice actor, and I focus more on trying to stay in character more than trying to get any voice right. You are playing a game after all. Your main priority should be to have fun!
entries.path: Meta Discussions/I'm New to Tabletop Role-Playing/+I'm New to Tabletop Role-Playing.md

entries.id: Pros and Cons of Nick Nacks
entries.title: Pros and Cons of Nick Nacks
entries.content: For more experienced TTRPG players, you might already have a good idea of what you like in a system, so this section will (hopefully) be an easy way to figure out if you will like Nick Nacks. Pros - Customization - A robust system designed to be played over and over again with characters that feel different every time. Customization with creative character ideas is the number one thing I set out to accomplish in Nick Nacks. - Balance - The second highest priority I have, that is absolutely necessary in order to make customization possible, is balance. Games that I have played struggle to maintain balance, particularly in the early/later levels and with their magic systems. I pay very close attention to features when they seem particularly powerful and particularly weak, and make adjustments as needed. - Adaptive - I don't have plans to print anything for this game, and regularly update this website. If a feature ends up a power outlier, or doesn't feel how it should, I will go back and change it to make it better. No longer will you need to wait decades for a new book to come out, contact the developers on twitter or pray your GM rules in your favor. If you want to see patch notes, or perhaps suggest new content, join the discord. - Free! - This website doesn't have a subscription and is more of a "pay want you want" via patreon. If you do what I did when I started playing, you can use dice rolling programs like rolz for your dice needs, discord for voice chat and screen sharing and your art program of choice for battle maps. Personally I make mine in pain tool sai, then share them in gimp because its easier to move layers around. Sai isn't free, but gimp is a nice alternative for making battle maps. Cons - Incomplete - As a solo developer who cannot dedicate 100% of my time to this project, you will inevitably find certain classes and spell lists with not as much content as you were hoping. You will also find artifacts of me copy and pasting things, or me making large scale rules changes and not catching every single feature it effects. Everything I start, I intend to finish, but starting things is a lot easier than finishing them. - Complex - When I make content, I always make considerations to speed up play, but inevitably if you want a robust system of customization, you increase the complexity of the game. This means that you will likely not be able to remember all the fine details of the hundreds of spells, class features and feats the game has to offer. I tried my best to organize this website as well as I could to make the process of looking things up fast, and because you're playing tabletop, you can adjust things to be more convenient on the fly and look things up later if you don't want to slow things down. - No Game World - One area in particular that will take a very long time to finish that is lower on the priority list is fleshing out a game world. I have introduced several mechanics that have world-building elements built into them such as mana, races and gods. Luckily, most of you probably already have high-fantasy game worlds you like. For those interested in lore, see The World of Nack for information on the timeline, places, people and factions that have been codified so far. - D20 System - For the math nerds out there, this can be a problem. The d20 is exceptionally good a delivering highlights and is quite simple. It is probably the most familiar type of action resolution dice, but as a tradeoff it is not the most mathematically sound. With a soft bounded accuracy system like Nick Nacks, its less of an issue, but there will be times where you reach the extremes of the dice and foil the balance I worked so hard to achieve. It also heavily restricts design space, particularly when it comes to gear upgrades that give numerical bonuses.
entries.path: Meta Discussions/Pros and Cons of Nick Nacks.md

entries.title: Pros and Cons of TTRPGs
entries.content: For people unfamiliar who are considering playing Nick Nacks, here are some of the pros and cons of the hobby as a whole. Pros * Never the Same Thing Twice - If you want to talk about replayability, TTRPGs are unmatched. Every story will have moments you never forget, either because of silly moments where you roll bad, or because of epic battles where you almost died. * The Perfect Hangout Hobby - If you are just looking for something to do with your friends and you have a lot of spare time, like if you play garbage games like League of Legends, then tabletop is a good substitute. * Cost Effective - Going out to eat, see movies or drinking might not cost much one night, but do it every week and it adds up. If you choose TTRPGs as your hobby, you typically only need the up front cost of dice and such, and that's if you play in person. Playing on line with the right combination of programs can be completely free! * True Role-Playing - Many RPG video games will not deliver particularly well on the role-playing side of things, giving you a generic blank slate protagonist and offer little in the way of interesting or compelling choices. TTRPGs let you play whatever you want, do whatever you want and do those things however you want them. Live and die by the sword, rally people with your conviction, use magic to forge a new path or bribe people with wealth and power. You can approach problem-solving in a way no other game can really deliver on. Cons * Time Consuming - In order to get the most out of TTRPGs, you will likely need to play for several hours a sitting, with multiple sittings a month. Lots of people play weekly, around 4 hours each session for potentially dozens of sessions to finish the story. * You get what you put in - Personally, I find this as a pro, but for many it can be a con. If you aren't particularly invested in the story, if you skip many sessions, or if you aren't paying attention when you are playing, you probably aren't going to enjoy TTRPGs much. I know especially these days it can be difficult for people to put down their phones and have a good time, and TTRPGs are one of those kinds of hobbies. * Scheduling - The sad reality of the hobby is that even if you miraculously find a few buddies to play with, they wont always be available on Monday night. My personal recommendation is to play no matter how many players show up, even if its only 1. Solo adventures can be fun too! Gives you time to deeply explore the characters closest to that player's character. Reschedule when you can. If you don't play very often, the game loses momentum and campaigns can slowly fade out of existence.
entries.path: Meta Discussions/Pros and Cons of TTRPGs.md

entries.title: +Chase
entries.content: Battlemaps are great! They allow you to visually represent things for set piece encounters. But what happens when the battle is on the move? Things like teleportation and tons of speed feel like a cop out and doesn’t bring the kind of tension you might want when trying to evade capture or running someone down. The chase rules exist as an alternative to combat to quantify certain types of strategies, advantages and random circumstances that can make your escape/chase feel a bit more dynamic. Keep in mind, chases require a great deal of improvisation since many abilities will be used for things that are atypical. The GM will need to create randomness (like parades, ornery animals in a pet store, oil spills) that can coincidentally benefit either side etc. Can also be used for races!

entries.title: Advantages
entries.content: When the chase first starts you need to determine advantage. Whoever has more advantages gets to decide their strategy in the first round of the chase. The party with advantage also gets a flat bonus to their saves, skill checks etc equal to the number of advantages they have over their opponent. Advantage can only be given to one party, so instead of applying to both, it applies to neither. Common forms of advantage include… | Name | Description | | ----------- | ---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | | Adaptation | You are better suited for the environment. This could mean that you are aquatic and your opponent isn't when you are escaping a prison ship, or it can mean it is night and you can see in the dark and your opponent can't. | | Numbers | Very simple, you have a significant numbers advantage. Typical for town guards, high profile nobles mansions being raided, guilds etc. | | Home Field | You know the terrain better than your opponent. You grew up in this town, you constructed this building etc. | | Speed | Determined by the weakest link. Whichever party has the faster slowest member gets an edge. | | Preparation | You knew this was going to happen ahead of time and planned accordingly, or you have plenty of traps set up in your house. | Note: Each round typically lasts long enough where these circumstances could realistically change. The guards might be familiar with the city, but what about the sewers underneath or the forest outside? Numbers can dwindle as one side gains the upper hand. Etc

entries.title: Random Events
entries.content: The 1st and 3rd round the DM should roll for events before strategies. These events only last one round. These events can vary wildly based on environment, time of day, weather etc. Events might not do anything, give one side advantage, or create entirely new strategies. Here are some examples. Night | Dice Roll | Random Event | | --------- | -------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | | 1 | Sudden bright light. Things get lit on fire, people turning on lanterns to see the commotion, stepping on bioluminescent plants etc. | | 2 | Nocturnal hunters. Wolves, giant owls or worse are in the area. | | 3 | Stand and deliver! Another random crime is happening in the area, you stumble on a group of camping bandits or are otherwise harassed by a third party of humanoids. | | 4 | | | 5 | | | 6 | | Urban | Dice Roll | Random Event | | --------- | ----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | | 1 | Social gathering. A large quantity of people are gathered or are gathering for some special event or celebration. | | 2 | Unusual vendors. Your chase brings you into an unusual marketplace, like a wet market, a pet store or a bomb salesman. | | 3 | Do not enter. Your chase brings you into a destroyed building, a junkyard or some other area sectioned off due to hazards. | | 4 | Mage Training. A magical demonstration is happening, creating a hazardous or annoying spell in the area like roiling magma, darkness or grease. | | 5 | Everybody look. Your chase causes a commotion worthy of attention, causing people to open their doors and windows in your path. | | 6 | | Wilderness | Dice Roll | Random Event | | --------- | ----------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | | 1 | Group animals. A group of animals occupies the area such as cattle, bison or lions. | | 2 | Dense vegetation. Bamboo shoots, thorny bushes or tall grass impair movement. | | 3 | Body of water. Your chase brings you alongside a river, lake or waterfall. | | 4 | Fairies. Capricious fey interfere in the chase | | 5 | | | 6 | | Weather | Dice Roll | Random Event | | --------- | ----------------------------------------------------------- | | 1 | Rain. It picks up very quickly and starts raining hard. | | 2 | Fog. A small fog cloud obscures sight in this area. | | 3 | Snow. A layer of snow coats this particular area. | | 4 | Wind. It blows in a specific direction with great intensity | | 5 | | | 6 | | Unusual/Rare Events | Dice Roll | Random Event | | --------- | ------------------------------------------------------------------------ | | 1 | Natural disaster. Lightning strikes, flash flood or earthquakes. | | 2 | Dragon attack. A dragon fitting of the local environment flies overhead. | | 3 | Portal. You stumble upon a portal leading to another dimension. | | 4 | | | 5 | | | 6 | |

entries.title: Win Condition
entries.content: The first thing you must determine in a chase is who is chasing and who is running. This will establish what strategies are available. Chases happen in rounds, with each round of a chase usually representing around 3 rounds of normal combat or ~20 seconds. At the start of each round, one side decides on a strategy for that particular round, determined first by who has advantage, then alternating each round. Strategies often require skill rolls, saving throws etc. Whoever is more effective that round earns a Win. In order to win a chase (either escaping or capturing), you need to earn 3 Wins. When you win as a chaser, it is assumed you manage to corner, bind and otherwise secure the escapees. When you win as the runner, it is assumed you evade your captors in a significant enough way that they are unable to find you without you pulling some dumb shit to get you caught again.

entries.title: +Minigames
entries.content: Mini games are used in order to portray non-combat scenarios where it's usually just contests of strength or skill. Some spells/abilities might have restrictions that are mostly based around combat (like rage) that should be allowed to persist through the contest.

entries.title: Arm Wrestling
entries.content: Arm wrestling contests are tests of strength that utilize the athletics skill. Each "round" of an arm wrestling contest is effectively like a round in combat for the purposes of determining how long single round buffs last. Each round calls for a contested athletics check, however you start off with lower dice, progressing to higher dice each round with the following progression. | Round | Dice Rolled | | ------------ | ----------- | | 1 | d6 | | 2 | d8 | | 3 | d10 | | 4 | d12 | | 5 and onward | d20 | Subtract the lower roll from the higher roll. That is how many points the winner of that round gets. The loser of each round gains no points. Once one creature hits 20 points, they win.

entries.title: D420
entries.content: Not really an official game mechanic I expect people to use, but more something fun that my friends and I do. At the start of each session, we essentially make a wish, usually giving our characters some esoteric power or causing an object or creature to materialize into being. We then use Rolz (I'm sure any electronic rolling service will suffice) to roll a d420. On a natural 420, it happens! On a natural 1 however, the opposite happens. The GM is still the final arbiter of how these rolls get implemented. Things that result in direct power increases (teehee I wanna be level 20), should be shamed. This roll is purely for whacky fun and memes. It's a fun way to let give the players a little taste of GM Magic and occasionally test the GM's improv skills. Because the odds are so low of getting a result (1/210), these kinds of events typically only happen a few times a campaign, if at all.

entries.title: Drinking Contest
entries.content: DC 10 check that increases by 1 for each drink. You add your strength to this roll. On 3 fails you lose. Bonuses that apply to poison/venom also apply to drinking games. Poison resistance gives a +2 bonus on these checks. Immunity is cheating.

entries.title: Beastfolk
entries.content: The furry race. | Language | Sylvan | | -------- | ---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | | Size | Medium or small | | Type | Humanoid, Beast | | Subrace | When you create your character, decide a specific animal you want to mimic, such as a cat, frog or cow. This decides some of the features you qualify for. | | Furry | Degeneracy +2 | | Major Default | | | ----------------------------------------------------------- | ------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------ | | Beast Speech | You can speak with Beasts as though you shared a common language. As beasts, this also functions with other beastfolk. | | Bestial Boon (Major) | Gain one from the list | | Minor Default | | | ----------------------------------------------------------- | --------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | | Hybrid Form (Su) (poly, true) | You can change your appearance to that of a specific (mostly) human, with only minor features hinting at your heritage such as ears and tail. This transformation lasts indefinitely. Transforming back is a standard action. | | Nightvision | You treat dim light as bright light. You treat darkness as dim light. | Replacement Features | Major Replace | | | ----------------------------------------------------------- | --------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | | Animal Instincts | Get a +2 bonus to two of the following skills: +Survival, +Perception, +Nature, +Stealth, +Insight | | Shapeshift (Su)(poly, true) | You can transform into the animal you resemble. You get 3 adaptation points to work with from the Polymorph List (general and beast only) that are chosen at character creation and cannot be changed.This transformation lasts indefinitely. Transforming back is a standard action. | | Pet (Sp) | When a creature spends a standard action to pat your head or stroke your fur/scales etc, it functions as the Head Pat technique, using your level as its effective CL. | | Scent | +Blindsense 10 ft | | Minor Replace | | | ----------------------------------------------------------- | ------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | | Cornered | Gain a Natural Attack. Bite, claw, horns etc. D10 base damage, or 2d6 with claws (if hands are free).Can be used as a swift action if you are below half +HP. | | Dulled Instincts | Select a single skill bonus from animal instinct. You cannot have this feature and animal instincts. | | Bestial Boon (Minor) | Choose one from the list | Lore | Numbers | Beastfolk are a highly varied type of people, which shapes and sizes similar to that of the beast they resemble. Beastfolk reach adulthood by 15 and tend to live to be around 70 | | -------------------------------------------------------------------- | -------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | | Appearance and Biology | |

entries.title: Bestial Boons

entries.title: +Races
entries.content: Races don't give ability scores. Now you can choose your race based on what features you want. Bring out the jacked fairies and the clumsy elves! Most races have a racial language, but also know common. All creatures are assumed to have a base speed of 30ft. Keep in mind a lot of the lore I use to describe races is used to differentiate a given race from the others. I personally don’t appreciate when elves are just long pretty people. I also make it to give people a starting point if they don't know how to roleplay or world build with a specific race. The descriptions you find here (or in any source really) are specifically tailored to a setting, or are the brain child of some other fantasy obsessed weirdo. They do not need to match how a specific race exists in your world if you have something else in mind. For example... In the world I built my Lore around, Nack, elves primarily live in a continent known as The Land of Storms which has an absurdly high level of rainfall. As such, they developed a spell to combat it called Umbrella, which Elves often inherently learn, separate from any additional magic training. Doesn't make much sense for a subterranean elf now does it? Note Races very often offer skill bonuses. Skill bonuses from race do not stack unless the two features are a pair of minor and major features. So you couldn't pick goblin and get perception from mixed blood elf's "nice ears" for instance. The only exception to this is if the skill bonuses only apply to specific circumstances, such as a cyborg's sentinel build. Format TraitsThese usually cover some of the race basics, such as creature type, starting language etc. Some races come with a specific feature baked in that can't be exchanged. You will know if a race has this when they only have 1 major or minor feature.Major FeaturesAll races have 2. These are the heavy hitting features that generally aren't shared between races and have a major impact on game play.Minor FeaturesAll races have 2. Supplementary features such as skill bonuses that are less impactfulAlternate FeaturesAny features that pop up in major/minor features are features that you can exchange with anything listed in their respective feature type (major replaces major, minor replaces minor).LoreMy take on the shapes, sizes, ages, cultures, behavior, biology and origin of various races. Take what you like, scrap what you don't.

entries.title: Amazon
entries.content: Snu snu race. | Language | Giant | | -------- | --------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | | Size | Medium | | Type | Humanoid, GiantChoose another type from father. You count as their race for all purposes. | | Major Default | | | ----------------------------------------------------------- | -------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | | Fitness | +10 land speed | | Embiggen | As a swift action you can increase to large size. As a swift action decrease back to medium. | | Minor Default | | | ----------------------------------------------------------- | ------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------ | | Well-Adapted | You do not suffer any negative effects from existing in one specific type of environment, such as volcanic, arctic or other such extremes. This doesn’t apply to things like toxic volcanic gas, but more things like elevation, water pressure and temperature. | | Warriors Call | At will increase the volume of your voice to three times it's normal volume. | Replacement Features | Major Replace | | | ----------------------------------------------------------- | ------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------ | | Battle Grace | Gain DR 3 vs poise damage. | | Power Through Healing | Gain 3 temp hp when out of combat for 1 minute. Get +1 to saving throws while you have temp hp. On-hit effects (like a venomous bite) happen after taking damage and thus the saves bonus doesn't apply. | | Greensight | Gain Greensight | | Minor Replace | | | ----------------------------------------------------------- | ---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | | Skill | Choose to get a +2 bonus to one of the following: +Persuasion, +Survival or +History | | Animal Guardian | Your beast companion gets 5 bonus hp. | | Lift | +100 Carry Weight | | Mixed Blood | Choose the minor feature of your father's race (including alternate features). You count as both races for all purposes. | | Survival Instinct Healing | You cannot die from being defeated. On the third round after you are defeated, you recover 1 hp. You gain a +30 bonus to speed on the first round you recover and negate the Knockdown effect from being defeated. | Lore | Numbers | Amazon tend to be around 6'6 and weigh around 250 lbs Amazon are adults by 15, tend to maintain their youth until their 60s, then experience the usual physical decline, tending to live until about 100 | | -------------------------------------------------------------------- | --------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | | Appearance and Biology | Amazon typically appear similar to other humanoids, but with a much larger build. They are tall mesomorphs that tend to have a lot of variety, even within the same family. Amazon exclusively birth other amazon and are always female. Because of their inability to interbreed with each other, they must seek other races to maintain their population. This results in a larger degree of variety than most other races, inheriting varying skin colors, tusks, horns etc. Amazon typically wear armor, but the armors tend to be more "tribal" in appearance. That is to say they use materials on hand like animal pelts or bone. Amazon typically grow attached to specific trinkets they collect during their adventures: Special hats, jewelry etc. It is incredibly common for Amazon to have elaborate body and face tattoos, typically being more pattern focused than being of specific objects or creatures, and always avoiding the eyes. Amazon particularly desperate for male attention will wear what their sisters call "horny armor" which practically provides little protection and only serves to draw the gaze of potential mates. | | Scenic Nowhere | Amazon typically only live in small communities of only a few hundred in the far off reaches of the world, where they typically aren't bothered: Icey peaks, deep jungles and volcanic wastes. This makes actually finding their home very difficult for an outsider, but an Amazon always has a 6th sense for how she might return home. Amazon often have unique adaptations that allow them to exist sustainably in such harsh environments. Sometimes this comes in the form of hunting or farming techniques, others it means biological adaptation to extremes in temperature.Amazon are typically wary of outsiders when close to home. Each tribe has a different policy depending on their history, but typically outsiders are judged by those most knowledgeable, the elders or by their chieftess. The chieftess is usually the most powerful and influential member of a particular tribe and is treated with great respect. A slight against the chieftess is a slight against the family as a whole, and is a quick way to get kicked out, or worse depending on the practices of that tribe. | | Family | Amazon have a particularly close bond with their family. They all share the same last name and rarely choose to change it, seeing their heritage as something to take pride in. They greatly emphasize the "it takes a village to raise a child" philosophy and every child is everyone's responsibility. As such the role of childbearing is seen less of a personal responsibility and more of a commitment to the continuation of the family. The role of mom specifically is also deemphasized, but what mother wouldn't show a bit of preferential treatment to one of their own. Amazon can typically expect their sisters, and especially their elders, to pick up on any slack with regards to child rearing as a result of being stretched thin by responsibilities. Amazon typically return back home if they experience great hardship, grow old or find a partner/get pregnant.To an Amazon, there is no greater threat than one that threatens their tribe. They will take up arms against anything if it threatens their way of life, and may be willing to resort to anything in order to protect their kin. | | Adventure and Duels | Nearly every young Amazon is expected to leave home at some point, usually in pairs with a sister of similar age. There is always the underlying goal of hoping to find love, but rarely is that the priority. They go out to perform specific tasks, carry on where their older sisters left of, or to simply make a name for themselves. As such they often find themselves living off the land and doing small jobs where they learn what was fact and what was fiction from all the stories they heard from their sisters growing up: The "civilized" world can often be just as cruel as the harsh biome they hail from, but in entirely new and frustrating ways. Things like land ownership and bumping heads on tiny doorways are oft-sited frustrations with most civilization.Regardless of style, all Amazon are combat-trained. As such, scrappy folks looking to test their mettle will challenge amazon to battles. Amazon will usually accept duels with females if they're in the mood, but will only duel males they are interested in or attracted to. If the amazon is victorious, they usually lose interest. | | Banishment | It does not happen often, but when an amazon betrays her family or does something the tribe sees as particularly heinous, they are banished: A specific type of mask-like tattoo is placed over their eyes that is always visible, even through magical transformations. Only through physically covering the face can the tattoo be hidden. The banished is stripped of their family name and forbidden from returning back home. Being banished from one tribe guarantees a life of hardship, as the tattoo is a universal sign to all amazon, making the banished unable to ever establish the bond of kinship again. Amazon instinctually detect and distrust any creature that bears the mark of the banished. |

entries.title: Anime
entries.content: The shiny race with crazy hair. | Language | Manganese | | -------- | --------- | | Size | Medium | | Type | Humanoid | | Major Default | | | ----------------------------------------------------------- | ------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------ | | Plot Armor Recharge, Healing | The first time per day you would be defeated, you negate whatever effect you suffered from that caused you to be defeated entirely, including its damage. When this happens, become unstoppable until the end of your next turn. | | Eternal Hope | When you roll a natural 1 on a d20, reroll it. You cannot reroll a reroll. | | Minor Default | | | ----------------------------------------------------------- | --------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | | Fearless | +2 bonus to saves against fear effects | | Subtitles | Whenever you speak manganese, any creature that can hear you sees your words appear at the periphery of their vision written in a language they understand, allowing them to understand you as long as they can read. | Replacement Features | Major Replace | | | ----------------------------------------------------------- | -------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | | Power of Friendship | When you gain the defeated condition, all allies within 60 ft of you gain advantage on their next d20 roll. When an ally gains the defeated condition, you get advantage on your next d20 roll. This ability can only activate once per round. Only functions in combat. | | Determination | Gain the determination special attack, usable with any attack roll. | | Tragic Backstory | 1/day you can tell the tale of your tragic backstory. Creatures within 30 ft of you become fascinated for 3 minutes (will negates). Share your tragic backstory via Memory Movie. You can Innately Cast it as a self only spell 1/day. | | Battlecry | When you take the Core Rules, How to Play/Actions/Common Actions/Full Round Actions/Charge action, you can move three times your speed and ignore difficult terrain. You can change directions once during this charge.Any round you charge, your allies also gain this benefit. | | Minor Replace | | | ----------------------------------------------------------- | --------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | | Theme Song | As a free action you can activate your theme song for no mechanical benefit. Theme songs fade into the background when you are not taking actions, and gets to the good part at an increased volume when you do. Your theme song makes you instantly recognizable as you adventure over time. Nobody else can have your theme song as long as you live. | | Big Anime PersonalitiesLewd | As a standard action, you can show off your assets to a creature within 30 ft. The creature gains blurred vision against all creatures other than you for one minute. | | Mixed Blood | Choose the minor feature of another race (including alternate features). You count as both races for all purposes. | | Loli | You are inherently sexual. Whenever something calls for romantic or sexual compatibility, you automatically qualify, whether the effect is beneficial or harmful. Your size decreases to small. | | Encouraging | You can use the aid action to increase an ally's skill checks for a +2 bonus, regardless of proficiency. | | Smug | Whenever a creature initiates an opposed skill check when you are present and fails, you can look at them smugly, causing them to falter. As an immediate action, you can cause them to be knocked down. | Lore | Numbers | Anime are usually around 5'6 and weigh around 150 lbs If they survive early adulthood, anime live to be around 70 | | -------------------------------------------------------------------- | --------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | | Appearance and Biology | Anime are very shiny, have enormous eyes, ridiculous hair and even more ridiculous outfits. Rarely will they ever see it that way though. The more colorful and eccentric someone is, the better they fit in with a crowd of anime. Anime are obsessed with being unique and will do everything in their power to make sure they have a different hair color, eye color, outfit, hair styles etc. They care deeply about being noticed. If anybody ever mentions their appearance, that is reinforcement that they are doing something right.Outside of these eccentricities, anime tend to closely resemble humans. Their skin and hair have a lot more luster that other humanoids. They can have deviations in their eyes, not only in their size, but also the shape of their irises. They also can sometimes have unusual extra features like the sharp teeth of a predator, an extra pair of ears on top of their head or whiskers. | | Cranked Up to 11 | Anime are always the most extreme version of whatever they embody. They are the most comically evil villain's, the most epic heroes and the dumbest drunks. They dive headfirst into things without even realizing what they are doing. Anime always go all in, rarely consider future consequences and regularly trust people they shouldn't. Anime also have the most intense reactions to things. Disgust causes them to turn purple and anger turns them red while they blow steam from their ears. They make tons of vocalizations to express their current emotional state. | | Main Quest | Anime always have some major event that they cling to during their childhood that shapes their life forever. Tragic deaths and witnessing incredible feats of heroism are the most common, but it could also be something inconsequential like being a virgin or not having any friends. Either way, this event will put the anime down a specific path to get vengeance, be the best there ever was or watch the world burn. As such, they always spend their teenage years and early adulthood in pursuit of this goal by getting special training, isolating themselves in their research and such. Anime make grandiose claims about their desires like "I want to become king" or "I want to be the strongest under the heavens" with regards to these desires.Once they get their fill of adventure or they accomplish their goal is when they typically settle down and live a normal life. Occasionally anime can be tempted to come out of retirement if a rival shows up or enemy they had thought defeated rears its head. |

entries.title: Boner
entries.content: The bone zone race. | Language | Necril | | --------------------- | ----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | | Size | Medium or Small | | Type | While boners are undead, they cannot be targeted by effects that control undead creatures. For all other purposes they count as humanoid and undead | | Vitality Recharge | Once per minute as a swift action, boners can produce a cloud of blue mist (doesn't obscure vision) in a 10 ft radius around them to hamper non-Boner undead around them. Such creatures are staggered for as long as they remain in the mist, and 1 round thereafter.Through prolonged exposure to this mist, an undead creature becomes a boner (provided it has a skeleton). If it doesn’t, it is instead destroyed in such a way to free its soul from necromantic control. | | Major Default | | | ----------------------------------------------------------- | --------------------------------------- | | Resistance | Gain resistance to necrotic damage. | | Minor Default | | | ----------------------------------------------------------- | ---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | | Trance | Resistance to saves against being Drowsy or Asleep. Instead of sleeping to rest, you enter a meditative state where you remain aware of your surroundings. | | Nightvision | You treat dim light as bright light. You treat darkness as dim light. | Replacement Features | Major Replace | | | ----------------------------------------------------------- | --------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | | Play Dead | You can collapse into a pile of bones on the ground and make all your equipment disappear as a standard action. This allows them to hide in plain sight and use +Disguise in place of +Stealth (if desired) with a +2 bonus. Exiting this ability is a free action. If the setting would be too unusual for a skeleton to be in (middle of a crowded street) or people see you activate this ability, then it doesn’t work. | | Lend a Hand | Boners can remove their arms and use them even while detached, albeit in a limited fashion. As a free action they can detach an arm. Disarmed limbs move at a rate of 5 feet per round back to their owner and reattach when they enter your space.You can use this ability to increase the reach of an attack only requiring one hand by 5-feet by wielding your sword arm in your other hand. You can also use this ability to "send in" your arm to places you can't reach or fit into. If you don't have line of sight, then you fumble around and feel for things.This detached arm can be attacked and otherwise harmed exactly as the effect had targeted you. | | Minor Replace | | | ----------------------------------------------------------- | ---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | | Big Boned | +100 Carry Weight | | Ghost Wrangler | You treat incorporeal creatures as though they were a corporeal for things like combat maneuvers, attacks, spells etc. | | Skill | +2 +Religion | Lore | Numbers | Varies based on humanoid bone'd. They weigh about 15% of their original body weight. Boners live to be about 50 before the animating force leaves their bones for good | | ---------------------------------------------------------------------- | --------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | | Appearance and "Biology" | Boners are the skeletons of humanoid creatures and vary a bit in shape and size based on their original race. Since their bodies are without skin and only bones, they are often mistaken for the nefarious creations of necromancers, but boners try to mitigate that with an extreme sense of style. They wear fancy outfits, outrageous hats and use their boney bodies as a canvas for artistic expression. It isn’t uncommon to see a Boner embed gems into their body, give themselves extensive tattoos or apply trails of melted gold in their cracked bones.A particularly unusual feature they possess is the ability to bend parts of their skulls. This allows them to make facial expressions (imagine Grim Adventures of Billy and Mandy). Most possess glowing lights in their sockets to replace their eyes. | | Vitality | Boners are the cure for undeath. They possess the ability to free the soul of the undead creature, granting it control over what's left of its body. Not only are they able, but they are obsessed with the idea of curing undead. You'd be hard pressed to find creatures with a greater distaste for undead. Most make it their mission to free what they see as imprisoned brothers and sisters. Vitality only works when exposed to the gas over a prolonged period of time, requiring undead to be captured instead of killed. Usually the process takes about 10 minutes for most undead, but particularly powerful ones can take hours or even days. Even undead that were quite fond of their previous forms have a drastic change in their mindset and are immediately disgusted by what they had become. | | 'Til Death do us Part | Boners typically remember very little, if anything, from their previous humanoid life, usually writing it off as déjà vu. Some of their personality remains intact and they have the same general shape, but most of their previous selves was erased when they washed ashore in the underworld through the river The Styx. They do remember everything they experienced while an undead monster. The quest to cure the world of necromancy is an impossible one, but it is one the boners accept with gusto, as it means they get to increase the size of their family. Boners develop a closeness with their captures. They always develop a very close bond with the first creature that they cure, unless that creature already had a partner. Lacking biology, they don’t care much for physical intimacy and pay little attention to gender or even species. Subsequent cures while a boner already has a partner are treated more like children. These comparisons are imprecise, considering most boners are of adult creatures and it can be difficult for most to imagine an intimate relationship without a physical component, but it serves as an easy way to explain to the meatbags. |

entries.title: Centaur
entries.content: The half and half quadrupeds. | Language | Sylvan | | ----------- | ---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | | Size | Medium | | Type | Humanoid, Beast | | Subrace | Choose One: Horse, Deer | | Mixed Blood | Gain a minor feature of another race (including alternate features). You count as both races for all purposes. Even if you get mixed blood from another feature, you can still only be a mix of maximum 2 races. | | Noble Steed | You are always treated as though you were mounted. | | Major Default | | | ----------------------------------------------------------- | ----------------------------------------------------------------- | | Stride | +10 Land Speed | | Quadruped | Your Poise increases by 10 and your Carry Weight doubles. | | Minor Default | | | ----------------------------------------------------------- | ------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------ | | Sprinter | You can Dash as a swift action. If you do so again within a minute, you become Fatigued. | Replacement Features | Major Replace | | | ----------------------------------------------------------- | --------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | | Deer: Prancing | Your are treated as though you are constantly under the effects of Enhanced Jump. It does not have the buff tag. | | Horse: Steed | Your size is large. | | Horse: Hooves | You have a Natural Attack that deals 2d6 crush damage. If you are currently being flanked, get a +2 bonus to attack with it.You can make a single simple hoof attack whenever you Disengage, unless the disengage action is granted through a technique. | | Stride+ | +10 Land Speed. Stacks with stride. | | Stampede | FRA move your speed+15, or double that in a straight line, moving through other creature's spaces. Any creature whose space you move through takes (2d8+LvL) crush dmg and is knocked down.Creatures can choose to either AoO or make a reflex save to negate knockdown and halve damage. | | Minor Replace | | | ----------------------------------------------------------- | ----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | | Skill | Get a +2 bonus to one of the following: +Perception, +Acrobatics, +Athletics | | Deer: In Headlights | While you are Shaken or Frightened, you also cannot be affected by Forced Movement. | | | | Lore | Numbers | Large centaurs tend to be around 7' tall and weigh around 900 lbsMedium centaurs tend to be around 5'6 and weigh around 200 lbsThey reach adulthood by 15 and live to be around 70 | | -------------------------------------------------------------------- | ------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------ | | Appearance and Biology | | | | | | | |

entries.title: Cyborg
entries.content: The part robot race. | Language | Based on creator | | ------------- | ----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | | Size | Varies | | Type | Humanoid, Construct | | Build Purpose | Choose one from the alternate major features. If you do not do tasks in line with your purpose, you acquire the purposelessness affliction (see below). | | Major Default | | | ----------------------------------------------------------- | ---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | | Extra Storage | Your forearms are mostly hallow, allowing you to store small items inside of them that you can easily retrieve at a moments notice. You can even store a single light weapon in each wrist. You gain a +4 bonus to conceal items. As a free action once per turn, you can pop your wrist and open to slip items in or out of this compartment. | | Minor Default | | | ----------------------------------------------------------- | --------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | | Cold Logic | Gain a +2 bonus to two intelligence based skills. You take a -2 penalty to all charisma based skills. | | Memory Bank | You can place an average book inside of your head. While inside you can read it while performing other tasks, albeit a bit more slowly. | Replacement Features | Major Replace | | | ----------------------------------------------------------- | ---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | | Build: Acrobatic | You can jump as a free action 1/turn. Your jump distance is a minimum of 20 ft, and you can jump as high as you can long jump. | | Build: Bot of Burden | +100 Carry Weight. Large size | | Build: Combat | When a creature fails to break your poise with a combat maneuver, you can bully it as an immediate action. | | Build: Handyman | You get an additional limb. This limb can store 3 types of handheld tools inside of it, such as a hammer, crowbar or farming implement. You can swap between these stored tools is a free action 1/turn. | | Build: Sentinel | You are ever vigilant, even while those around you rest. You get +2+perception, increasing to +4 when you keep watch. You do not need to breathe or sleep. You need 1/3 the usual amount of fuel/power to maintain functionality. To gain the benefits of rest, you must merely not perform any physically or mentally intense activity for 8 hours. | | Build: Shadow | +2 +Stealth, stealth proficiency, small size and nightvision | | Build: Spell Sniper | You get a +1 bonus to spell attacks. | | Build: Magic Conduit | You get a +1 bonus to CL | | Build: Explorer | Whether its making maps, archaeology or dungeon delving, you excel at adapting to the situation. 3/day you can add a d8 to a skill check or ability check while not in combat. This bonus can be added retroactively. | | 360 | You can rotate your upper body in circles with a special joint at your hips. You cannot be flanked. | | Defragment Recharge | You can change one of your skill proficiencies by concentrating for 1 minute. | | Minor Replace | | | ----------------------------------------------------------- | ---------------- | | Ocular Adjusment | Gain nightvision | Lore | Numbers | Cyborgs size is largely based the preferences of the creator. They usually tend to be 5'6 tall and weigh 350 lbsShadow Builds are typically half as tall and 1/4 the weightBots of Burden are typically 2x as tall and 4x the weight.The lifespan of a cyborg is dependent on the creature who created them. See creation and death for more info. | | -------------------------------------------------------------------- | --------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | | Creation (and Death) | Through a combination of engineering and magic, a talented individual can use the body of a creature who is recently deceased or on deaths door as a chassis for a cyborg. By augmenting the creature with replacement mechanical parts that circumvent the biological failings of a creature's meat parts, and offering a piece of their own soul, the creator can breathe new life into a completely new type of creature. Theoretically, with frequent repairs, a cyborg can live forever. However due to the soul sharing being a necessary part of the process, the life of the cyborg is inevitably tied to that of their creator. When their creator dies, they take the piece of the soul that resides in their creation with them. This does not immediately kill the cyborg, but rather gives them a sickness that is similar to dementia that slowly eats away at their mental faculties until nothing remains. | | Appearance and Biology | The aesthetics of a cyborg tend to be augmented humanoid: Limbs replaced by mechanical parts, additional seams in the skin to accommodate for added flexibility and visible nodes/circuits to channel electricity. Other than that, the cyborg's appearance tends to be based on the preferences of the creator and the body of the creature that was used in the process of creation. When it comes to what gets taken out and replaced with mechanical parts, that largely depends on the state the subject was in before the process of being turned into a cyborg. Ailments that affected mobility typically mean mechanical limbs, while those related to organ failure can cause most of the augmentations to be made internally. Either way there are usually new ways the creature can bend and new holes to accommodate for their need for electricity or fuel instead of food and water. Most of the engineers tend to favor function over form, but on occasion they can be compelled to keep particular biological functions in tact to make the cyborgs blend in better such as a digestive system or reproductive organs. Regardless, they do not appear to age, only physically changing in so far as they are repaired and augmented further. | | What is my Purpose? | Cyborgs inevitably need to have their brain essentially wiped clean as part of the creation process. In order to avoid total mental collapse, the creators need to fill that space in with something else: a purpose. Usually a creatures build and purpose coincide with each other: A sleek build honed for stealth built to assassinate political rivals or quick reflexes to counteract opponents in combat.No matter the purpose, the cyborgs are invariably bound by it, and must dedicate a large portion of their time to it. There is no finite amount of time a cyborg must schedule, but usually they must dedicate hours each day to their purpose, even if the task is indirect (I must protect my creator, therefore I will shop for them and buy them armor). Though even indirect methods will not substitute directly attending to their build purpose forever. If a cyborg spends too much time avoiding their purpose, they acquire a severe affliction called purposelessness (details below). | | Free Will | Cyborgs are like children when first created; they know little and are curious about everything. This usually passes quickly since they still have the mental faculties of an adult. Creators will quickly find them changing roles from parent to taskmaster as cyborgs are eager for direction. Once given the tools to do so, cyborgs can act completely independently. Though the words of their creator tend to carry more weight. Cyborgs might not have any biological memories from their previous life, but the memories of the soul remain. They leak into their unconscious thoughts and still serve as a guide for them concerning matters of morality. They might not remember the events that created these feelings, but the lessons they learned from them stick for good. As such, they function as and are usually treated like individuals rather than servants, though to the cyborg that line can be blurry. | T3 Affliction: Purposeless | Effect | Cure | | -------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | ----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | | Cyborg loses its free will and must achieve its purpose at all costs. It is obtained through abstaining from this purpose and so usually involves overcompensating for lost time. This could mean aggressively assisting creatures without them asking for it, picking fights for no other reason than to fight.Cyborgs are much more impressionable and will often do anything asked of them if it is aligned with their purpose while in this state. | Receiving a direct order in line with their purpose from creator requiring at least 1 day and completing it without rest.If creator is unavailable, things get more extreme. After 1 week of constantly serving their purpose without rest, they become drained 10 and the affliction is lost. If they are unable to serve their purpose, they drain 2 each day and cannot recover drain. |

entries.title: Drakonid
entries.content: The regal draconic scalies. | Language | Draconic | | ----------- | --------------------------------------------- | | Size | Medium, Small | | Type | Humanoid, Dragon | | Choose One: | Warrior (medium size), or Minion (small size) | | Major Default | | | ------------------------------------------------------------ | ----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | | Breath Weapon (Su) (damage type varies) recharge | You can take a deep breath and exhale to release a cone of elemental energy. The damage type is chosen on character creation and cannot be changed. Deals 2d8+LvL (9+lvl) damage in a 30 ft cone (reflex halves). Recharges after 1 minute. | | Resistance | You gain resistance to an energy type. If you have breath weapon, the resistance much match the damage type of it. | | Minor Default | | | ----------------------------------------------------------- | --------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | | Nightvision | You treat dim light as bright light. You treat darkness as dim light. | | Skill | If a Warrior, get a +2 bonus to one of the following: +Intimidation, arcana or +AthleticsIf a Minin, get a +2 bonus to one of the following: +Stealth, +Sleight of Hand, +Deception | Replacement Features | Major Replace | | | ----------------------------------------------------------- | ------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | | Natural Armor | Your body is covered in scales that protect you, but limit your movement and chafes against traditional armor. Your AC is 14+lvl. You cannot add DEX to AC and cannot wear armor. Natural armor also does not give poise. | | Warrior: Innate Techniques | You gain a series of innate techniques, each usable 1/day.LvL 1 - You gain Roar Lvl 3 - You gain Ferocious HowlLvl 5 - You gain Shockwave | | Goo Dragon (Su) (recharge) | Your breath weapon is unusual. It functions as Bubble Breath but the bubble capture only lasts 1 round. If you also have resistance, it applies to poison damage, as well as saves against venom and +toxins. | | Fat Tail | You have a large muscular tail. As a swift action, you can spin around and swipe at all creatures within your natural reach. They take 10+LvL poise damage (reflex negates). | | Minion: Ankle Biter | You gain a bite attack that deals d6 base damage. When a creature rolls a combat maneuver check against you and fails to break your poise, you can make a simple bite attack against them as an AoO. | | Warrior: Runs in the Family | Gain a technique point in the Protector tree. | | Minor Replace | | | ----------------------------------------------------------- | -------------------------------------------------------------------- | | Minion: Prehensile Limb | Your tail might be smaller, but proportionally its much more useful. | Lore | Numbers | Drakonids tend to be around 5'10 tall and weigh 200 lbs Drakonid reach adulthood by 15 live to be around 80 | | ----------------------------------------------------- | --------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | | Appearance and Biology | Drakonids are bulky humanoids with draconic heads, tails, teeth and scales. Their colorful scales cover nearly their entire bodies and give them a very regal look. Unlike true dragons, they do not possess wings. Other features remain that leave a lot to be desired when compared to true dragons, such as their large claws, and long muscular necks that allow them to effectively make use of their sharp teeth. In this sense, they more closely resemble dinosaurs.They inherit many aspects of reptilian biology, including their ability to lay eggs. Drakonid babies skip the helpless infant stage of development most other humanoids experience, able to crawl around immediately after hatching so they can get to wasting furniture with their breath weapon. | | Warrior Culture | The Drakonids reintroduced to the world were the children of Niv the Protector. After her adventures ended, her work continued, and still does continue to this day. Her family is the first of a handful to gain a significant cultural foothold, doing so through their services as protectors to travelers through the Land of Earth. As such, if someone has heard of a Drakonid, they are likely martials clad in metal wielding weapons of war.In general, Drakonid work to ensure order in society and safety to citizens. As such, the wanderer or adventurer type Drakonids people find tend to have poor family relations. | | Minions | Once Gralth the Inferno reintroduced Drakonids to the world, many other powerful dragons took notice. They to wanted to have envoys into humanoid society, but they wanted to maintain their loyalty. In doing so they created a different variation of Drakonid that is much smaller and much more obedient. These smaller Drakonid are referred to as "minions" due to their propensity to follow a more powerful true dragon.The servile nature of these creatures often makes other races have lowly opinions of minions, and as such, they tend to do poorly when it comes to diplomacy. Failing at tasks given by their masters | | History of Dragons | Drakonids are a race that fall in and out of popularity depending on when in +The Timeline you play in. They even have different names. During the The Age of Dragons, dragons were cursed to be nothing more than giant flying lizards by Drakthar, the World Ender, who consumed their power in order to fuel his own.Once Drakthar was defeated, his power was restored to its natural place and was once again allowed to suffuse the world. In particular, the band of dragons that defeated him became the first true dragons: the 4 legged quadrapeds with large wings jutting from their back. These progenitors made it their mission to spread the love so to speak, and granted all draconic creatures sentience. Many other dragons had different evolutionary paths, such as the wyverns with winged arms, or serpentine dragons that used magic to fly. One group was bipedal and these were later called Proto Dragons. They didn't have nearly the same offensive profile as other dragons, tending to be much smaller and many being unable to fly at all. Over time, many groups of humanoids hunted down dragons. Part of it was the hundreds of years of animosity, but part of it was wanting to steal their power. In particular, tribes of elves and dwarves that were once forced to live deep underground used this time in history to become powerful civilizations off the backs of the mana and materials they hunted from dragons.One such group was the Proto Dragons. Already small in number, many were captured and sealed away using powerful curse magic so their mana could be harvested for generations. For many centuries, humanoid dragons all but disappeared. It was not until Gralth the Inferno finally reintroduced the species during the The Age of Magic, with the hopes of using the species as a way to improve dragon-humanoid relations. In the decades since, the species has been allowed to flourish, mostly forming tight family groups in Land of Earth. |

entries.title: Dwarf
entries.content: The short and stocky beard dude race | Language | Dwarven | | -------- | ------------------------- | | Size | Medium | | Type | Humanoid | | Subrace | Choose One: Surface, Deep | | Major Default | | | ----------------------------------------------------------- | ------------------------------------------------------------- | | Hardy | Gain resistance to poison, +Toxins and Venom. | | Slow and Steady | Increase your Poise by 5 and +HP by 10 | | Minor Default | | | ----------------------------------------------------------- | ---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | | Night Vision | You treat dim light as bright light. You treat darkness as dim light. | | Beard of Holding | You get an extra inventory slot in your beard.
