You get Advantage on checks to conceal item in your beard. | Replacement Features | Major Replace | | | ----------------------------------------------------------- | ---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | | Racism | Choose a race. Any Attack Roll you make is treated as though it has the Bane property against this race. | | SaltbeardRecharge | As a free action 1/turn, coat a held weapon or piece of ammunition in your beard salt. Your next attack with it inflicts Taunted for one round (fort negates). You must quaff some food (a swift action), before you can use this feature again. | | Flame BelchRecharge, Fire | As a standard action, you can light and raise a flame to your mouth, then belch on an adjacent creature. They are set ablaze (reflex negates). It takes a swift action drinking an alcoholic beverage to recharge this ability. | | Stubborn | Whenever an effect would move you against your will, reduce the distance moved to 5ft as an immediate action. If you don't spend an action, reduce the distance of all forced movement by 5ft. | | Deep Dwarf: Darkvision | Gain Darkvision | | Deep Dwarf: Innate Casting | You gain Vanish, and Embiggen as innate techniques.Vanish has a cooldown of 1 minute. | | Minor Replace | | | ----------------------------------------------------------- | -------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | | Not Impressed | +2 bonus to saves against charm and lewd effects | | Skill | Choose one to get a +2 bonus to: +Insight, +Profession (mining, smithing, stonecraft) | | Mountaineer | You use your full speed to climb and ignore rocky difficult terrain, such as rubble, stairs etc. | | Value Scent | You possess blindsense (scent, 5ft). It is only usable on detecting the value of objects. You are automatically are able to discern the value of something with a quick sniff. Particularly valuable things such as rare magic items may have extremely powerful and wide scents as strongly scented creatures and objects normally would. | | Book of Grudges | You meticulously record all events that you perceive as negative in a government approved book. Slights against your character, betrayals, enemies, lies and the like are all written down in the book. By presenting this book in a suitable situation, such as incriminating someone, you get a +2 bonus to skill checks. | Lore | Numbers | Dwarves tend to be around 4' tall and weigh 150 lbs Dwarves reach adulthood by 15 and live to be around 120 | | -------------------------------------------------------------------- | --------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | | Appearance and Biology | Dwarves are universally male. Rather than reproducing through traditional means, they go mining to find more dwarves. Dwarves are a naturally occurring resource just like iron, and can be harvested as such. They usually appear inside of stony egg-like structures with the texture of hair. These eggs naturally form as a result of "dwarf energy" which is created through the act of mining. Dwarfling eggs can only form in unworked stone, making it necessary to go far out from home to find them. More dwarf energy is generated when dwarves mine, and dwarves have a more keen sense when it comes to finding dwarfling eggs, making it uncommon for other humanoids to discover them. There are a handful of ways to hatch a dwarfling egg. Some use specific solvents like a shampoo to soften the protective outer beard shell while others warm it up and comb at it when it softens. When hatched, dwarflings start off a bit more developed then birthed humanoids. With roughly a year head start, they can already move around on their own, eat most foods and understand basic physics. Dwarves are easily identifiable from their wide frames, short statures and incredible beards. They typically have a higher density of muscles and hair than most other humanoids. They take exceptional care of their facial hair, prioritizing it over the hair on the top of their head. Dwarves hatch with beards, so it is very rare to see one without a beard. Average dwarves tend to be wary of beardless dwarves. | | Vibe Check | Because dwarves have completely different biology when it comes to reproducing, their family structure doesn't follow that of creatures that give birth to their own young. Instead, dwarflings stick with whoever mined them during their early years. By the time they are teenagers, they can get a feel for their immediate family and decide whether or not they are a good fit for them. As such, dwarves form families (called Brotherhoods) similar to how most people find friend groups. Dwarves who don’t click with the family they were hatched into will readily leave to go find people that share ideals/lifestyle more closely. Dwarves that are part of a brotherhood share the same name, but are willing to drop it when they change or they think the brotherhood 'aint what it used to be. Dwarves who don't vibe with the local brotherhoods have a tendency to wander around the overworld until they find a nice craftsman or adventuring party to latch onto. Dwarves innately have a strong ability to read people, making this somewhat naïve approach to finding family more viable. Just because a dwarf is traveling doesn't automatically mean they aren't part of a brotherhood; Sometimes it is necessary to do business with surface dwellers. | | Round up the Lads | Because dwarves do not have genders, they are a lot less sensitive to the kinds of dynamics having sexual dimorphism causes in humanoids. It can take a bit of getting used to not bathing or taking a leak in the presence of ladies. Dwarves tend to treat women of other races just like the men. This comes with a whole host of problems, but it also comes with a few positives. A woman having a dwarf friend is a bit like having a woman having a gay guy friend, where they can feel at ease knowing that the dwarf isn't looking for every opportunity to get into her pants. Unfortunately for suitors, they are also aromantic, making such advances made against them almost always fly right over their head.Dwarves tend to express masculine ideals much more frequently. They enjoy one upping each other when it comes to tests of strength and especially endurance. A favorite is the drinking contest, which most dwarves will handily win when matched against non-dwarves. | | Secrets of the Dwarves | Larger brotherhoods or groups willing to cooperate can sometimes get up to some…Interesting shenanigans when it comes to the experimentation on dwarflings. Nearly everything related to the topic is a closely guarded secret, known only by a handful of elders in the brotherhood. Rumor has it though that the dwarves will use some arcane mining methods in order to increase the chances of dwarflings appearing inside of rare metals like gold. These oddities are usually substantially different from the standard dwarf, taking on some of the properties of the metal they form from. Golden dwarves, a favorite amongst the dwarven elite, tend to be softer, thinner and more magically talented. There are even some rumors of dwarfling eggs being used as part of the forging process in order to create metallic dwarves. If such things do exist and are not just fantasy stories told by bards looking to capture the interest of the common folk, then they have never seen the light of day. |

entries.title: Elf
entries.content: The old race with pointy ears. | Language | Elven | | -------- | ----------------------- | | Size | Medium | | Type | Humanoid | | Subrace | Choose One: Dark, Storm | | Major Default | | | ----------------------------------------------------------- | ----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | | Ageless Patience | You can choose to take 10 on skill checks instead of rolling for one skill. Get an additional skill you can use this feature with at level 5 and 10 | | Innate Casting | Gain a cantrip of your choosing. Cast Dream 1/day. At LV 5 - Cast dream council 1/day. | | Minor Default | | | ----------------------------------------------------------- | -------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | | Trance | +4 bonus vs sleep effects. Instead of sleeping to rest, you enter a meditative state where you remain aware of your surroundings | | Storm Elf:Innate Casting | You gain the Umbrella cantrip. You can cast it as an immediate action and it is dismissible as a free action. | Replacement Features | Major Replace | | | ----------------------------------------------------------- | ------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------ | | Arcane Sight | You automatically determine whether an item is magical or nonmagical just by seeing it. You can determine the properties of a magic item that you can see similar to how monster lore checks work (free action once per turn), even if you cannot closely examine it. | | Elven Archer | Increase the range of bows by 20. | | Steeped in Magic | Get a +1 bonus to your CL. | | Flelf | You are constantly under the effect of arcane pocket. Instead of your pocket, it affects your hair. +2 Floofyness | | Farsight | +2 bonus to +Perception. In ideal conditions you can clearly see things of medium size from up to 1 mile. Double the distance for each size category larger, half for each size category smaller. | | Dark Elf: Darkvision | Gain Darkvision | | Dark Elf: Tribal Boon | Choose one of the following tribes...Shi'Sara - Gain a technique point in the Cryomancy or Alchemy (T) tree.Shi'Zen - Gain a technique point in the Pyromancy or Psionics tree.Shi'Sulm - You gain Kindred as an Innate Technique. It can last indefinitely, but it can only be used on insects. If you take +HP damage during this transformation, you revert back to your true form. | | Minor Replace | | | ----------------------------------------------------------- | ------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | | L O N G | +5 Land Speed | | Skill | Get a +2 bonus to one of the following: +Perception, +History | | Storm Elf: Aquatic | You become Amphibious. You gain a Swim speed equal to 20+Land Speed. | | Dark Elf: Spider Climb | You gain a Climb speed equal to your Land Speed. As long as you have bare skin exposed on your hands, you can climb on any surface. If you have more skin exposed, you can even cling to the walls an use your hands to attack and cast spells. | Lore | Numbers | Elves are usually around 6ft tall and weigh 140 lbs Lifespan vary wildly with deaths by "natural causes" being exceptionally rare. As such an elven lifespan is theoretically infinite given ideal conditions. Elves reach adulthood at 20 and have a median age of 300 | | -------------------------------------------------------------------- | ---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | | Appearance and Biology | Elves are lithe androgynous humanoids with long and pointed ears as their most telling feature. Elves do not age in the traditional sense. Most humanoids start a slow decline once they reach adulthood, eventually becoming withered husks of their former selves. Elves become adults at about the same speed as humans, but when they grow old, they physically change based on things like their environment or their magical affinity. Forest hermits might eventually grow leaves and branches, stag horns or feathers. A wizard that specializes in cryomancy might turn a light shade of blue and grow cold to the touch. Changing in physical properties like these typically happen slowly over decades or even centuries. | | Ageless | Elves are unique among humanoids due to their seemingly infinite longevity and youthfulness. As such, they develop a very different perception of time and the world at large. Many details are often lost to the passage of time, but elves persist. While a generation of humans might pass every 20 years or so, a generation of elves often lasts over a century. As such they have a unique perspective on the rise and fall of empires, world level crises and the passage of time in general. Elves possess a unique form of wisdom that can either be used to aid other races, or it can cause them to become jaded as they see the same pattern of self destruction emerge multiple times amongst the humanoid societies they observe.Long life isn't all sunshine and rainbows. Elves can often develop a superiority complex; they are usually the smartest or wisest people in any given room. Yearly celebrations such as birthdays are largely ignored. Elves making friends with non-elves is a uncommon, since they won't stick around long enough to matter anyway. Elves can often seem apathetic towards the plights of other humanoids, but their reactions have merely been tempered by experience.Because elven society moves much more slowly than other humanoids, many of their practices can seem archaic, time-consuming and inconvenient. They are often purposefully designed to be so in order to make them take more time and be more inconvenient to replicate for other races. "See you again soon" takes on an entirely different meaning as the elf's perception of time is likely wildly different that what we, and what most non-elven creatures, think. They tend to be much more conservative than others, adhering to ancient traditions and worshiping less popular or older deities. Everybody is a kid at some point so they understand what it was like to some degree: What it is like to feel wonder in a world filled with the unknown. At some point people grow and the wonder and discovery of childhood is filled with responsibility and routine of adulthood. Elves eventually come to exist on some 3rd plateau of existence where they have eaten every food, vacationed in every country, saved the world, started a family, lost people they loved, turned to the dark side and were eventually redeemed and all the rest. Once you experience so much of what the world has to offer, once you have given so much of yourself to the world only to be forgotten, what is the meaning of your continued existence? | | Obsession | The aforementioned appearance changes are merely an outward representation of decades, maybe even centuries, of obsession. When traditional methods of acquiring meaning in one's life fail, elves have all the time in the world to dedicate to their projects. Spiteful elves may eventually have their conscious eroded to the point where the short-lived races are viewed as mice in their experiments, and are transformed into monsters. They might develop an entirely selfless desire to protect the natural world and view most any inhabitants that exploit it as a virus to be cured by any means necessary. Maybe at a young age they are exposed to a great evil and tirelessly work to exterminate it, and snuff out any reemergence for as long as they live.Turning people into monsters is abhorrent, and hunting demons is noble. Protecting nature is a lofty ideal, but usually most humanoids are only willing to go as far is needed to maintain their way of life: Appeasing the spirits for safety, enriching the soil for crops etc. So a single-minded dedication to nature could easily cause conflict once one too many trees are cut. These exceptionally long-lived elves that develop obsessions don't automatically cause conflict, but depending on their obsession, they very easily could. Entire elven societies form around the adaptive obsessions, for better or worse. | | Dark Elves | Dark elves are underground dwelling elves that have a long history of desiring more. Hundreds of years ago, there was an entire society of subterranean elves that mostly kept to themselves. Three royal families took it upon themselves to seize control of lesser tribes and force them to work towards their own desire: plunge the surface world into darkness. They were unsuccessful, and banished to The Shadow Realm.The tribes made a comeback and almost succeeded in consuming the sun in darkness, bringing about The Age of Darkness. In the modern era, Dark Elves are hunted by the Storm elves, who provide bounties for pairs of their ears.Dark Elves are generally split into three tribes, named after the family line that rules them.Shi'Sara - The dark elves that hail from the underground beneath the Land of Frost. They are adept alchemists and cryomancers. They specialize in the art of fleshwarping: permanently changing creatures into monsters using AlchemyShi'Zen - Hailing from the underground benath the Land of Fire, they make adept pyromancers and psychics.Shi'Sulm - Hailing from the underground beneath the Land of Earth, they are trained in the druidic arts, and in particular, form close bonds with insects.Because of the far reaching influence of these three tribes, most dark elves are assumed to be malicious on sight. |

entries.title: Eternal
entries.content: The legally distinct people-person race | Language | Sylvan | | ------------------------- | --------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | | Size | Medium or Small | | Type | Fey | | Telepathy(Su) | You are unable to speak, lacking the complex biology to produce language. You instead rely on your telepathy in order to communicate. You are still able to create vocalizations.This effectively makes you immune to Silenced. | | Major Default | | | ----------------------------------------------------------- | ------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------ | | Detect Desires (Su) | With physical contact, you discover information about others through psychic impressions. Brief physical contact gives you insight into the creatures immediate goals (what's for dinner, where am I going). Intimate contact longer than 10 seconds gives you insight into the creatures long-term goals (I will become hokage, I will make cat girls real). During this contact you can also choose to share your own short/long term goals as well. | | | | | Minor Default | | | ----------------------------------------------------------- | ---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | | Empathic Connection (Su) | You have advantage on skill checks against any creature you are in physical contact with, unless touching the creature would be actively detrimental, like with +Stealth. | | Community Bond (Su) | By touching another creature, you can add them to your community, up to 8 other people. All bonded creatures are capable of sending a signal of danger to their community (instant action). Every member gets a sense of direction and distance to the creature that sent the signal, and are aware of who sent it. A creature can remove its bond with 1 minute of concentration. | Replacement Features | Major Replace | | | ----------------------------------------------------------- | ------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | | Petal Dance (Su) | You gain the Waterbending Style, plant bending, as a water bender of your level, allowing you to manipulate loose vegetation. You're unable to leave the plants in a position that will cause long-term damage. | | Mind- Reader (Su) | You can innately cast detect thoughts at-will, but its range changes to touch and it loses the subtle tag. You can choose to substitute the will save for an attack roll. | | | | | | | | Minor Replace | | | ----------------------------------------------------------- | ------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | | Skill | Get a +2 bonus to one of following: +Persuasion, +History, +Insight | | | | | | | | | | | | | Lore | Numbers | Eternal are around 5' tall and weight around 100 lbs Small eternals tend to be about half the height and a third of the weight.Despite their name, eternals are not immortal. They reach adulthood at 20 and tend to live to about 80. | | -------------------------------------------------------------------- | --------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | | Appearance and Biology | Eternals are androgynous fey with very thin limbs and a similar overall "bipedal humanoid" shape. They have protrusions from the sides of the head or the face that resemble feathers that can flutter to and fro for additional expressiveness. The "default" is usually quite plain with mainly white with secondary green colors, and gem-like structures that help focus their psychic powers. Damaging this removes their ability to communicate telepathically unless they physically touch someone.The appearance of others can vary considerably, with some borrowing features of their appearance from plants or animals. They are fans of the long and flowing fabrics of robes and dresses. Many are capable of manipulating plantlike parts of their body to grow in special shapes.An odd quirk to the eternal is that they are genderless at birth. It isn't until they start developing romantic feelings for someone close to them over a long period of time where they undergo a rapid transformation to accommodate for their partners preferences. They still stay the same general shape, but with more desirable secondary characteristics. | | "For the Greater Good" | Most races are capable of empathy, but few hold it in as high a regard as eternals do. In self-contained groups, their collectivist ideals and cooperative nature lead to what some might consider a socialist utopian society. However eternal society is very fragmented, and the groups tend not to interact with each other. Groups rarely have more than 200 people in them.This does not mean that they are an insular people, just that it is very difficult for most to make the kinds of sacrifices that come naturally to eternals. While a human would readily accept no strings attached exorbitant wealth so they can indulge in the finer things of life, an eternal would question how this money was obtained. If people needed to suffer in order to produce this wealth, they would undoubtedly refuse it. Sustainability is of the utmost importance to eternals. Every action they take is an internal calculation where the desirable result is to lift everybody up. Even in personal relationships, squabbles are talked about amongst the community so grudges or trauma are worked through and "cured" before they balloon into major problems. | | Branching Paths | As part of the advanced calculus that goes on in the head of an eternal, sometimes they deem it necessary to leave their people for the greater good. These individuals are bound to have incredibly stressful lives, as they will constantly be surrounded and people that do not share their ideals. People that hoard wealth, subjugate others and hide their emotions frustrate eternals.Even with these hardships, they press on. They perform mental gymnastics to believe that one day all races can learn to be more like them, and they will be the ones to enact this change. They excel in any profession where their empathy and ideals can be put to good use such as diplomacy. In an adventuring party they lean towards supportive roles like healers. | | Intimacy | Eternals often struggle when mingling with other races. Many of the practices that they feel are commonplace between their own people are too intimate or unusual to generally apply to other races. Physical contact, sharing feelings, collaboration, mutual respect: these are the kinds of things that the eternal will find they give too much of, and others give too little of. This can make the transition difficult. This is even worse for eternals raised outside of their own society, who almost universally feel out of place. Eternals in an abusive household will invariably end up menaces.These things can complicate romantic relationships, but even worse still is the speed at which they develop deep emotional attachment. Eternal require a deep, intimate connection with someone that can typically only be obtained through years of interactions. As such it is very common for them to be in relationships with childhood friends, even those outside of their race. | | The Eternal One | The Eternal One is a collective conscience stored inside of something of significance, like a gem, or a tree. It is of great importance to the eternals since it holds the memories of their people, and is the central focus of rituals disposing of the crystalized memories of the dead. Each settlement has one, and it typically has a guardian that will consult it during times of great distress. Proclamations made by The Eternal One may as well be decrees from a god with how much severity and seriousness they are treated with. It is through these proclamations that eternals most commonly find themselves separating from their kin. |

entries.title: Exalted - Fallen
entries.content: The celestial race. Comes in 2 variants. 1 representing fallen angels, the other of divine bloodlines | Language | Celestial | | -------------------------------------- | ----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | | Size | Medium or Small | | Type | Humanoid, Celestial | | Subrace | This race of celestial humanoids comes in 2 flavors, Exalted and Fallen. See lore for more details. | | Exalted: Halo (Su) (Light) | You emit bright light in a 20 ft radius around you. This can be dimmed or turned back on as a free action 1/turn. While your halo radiates light, you are automatically viewed as a source of authority by NPCs. Guards will have a higher tolerance for your activities, commoners will be more willing to aid you in investigations regarding crime etc. This gives advantage on any skill check when dealing with people concerning law and order.Your halo dims when you are immersed in an effect with the Dark tag. | | Fallen: Clean Slate (Sp) | You can Mana Burn 1 to innately cast malleable memory, but only to remove memories. | | Major Default | | | ----------------------------------------------------------- | ----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | | Truespeech | You can speak to any creature that can understand or speak language. You understand all spoken words. This does not give you any insight into written words or non verbal language. | | Minor Default | | | ----------------------------------------------------------- | --------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | | Fertility | You can sense when a creature is pregnant by standing within 10 feet of the creature. With concentration, you can determine how far along they are and how many children there are. | | Innate Casting | You can innately cast cure wounds 1/day. | Replacement Features | Major Replace | | | ----------------------------------------------------------- | ---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | | Favored Enemy: Fiend | +2 to +Planes. If you succeed on a +Monster Lore check against fiends, get +2 to Attack against | | Favored Enemy: Undead | +2 to +Religion. If you succeed on a monster lore check against anUndead creature, get +2 attack against | | Deathless | The first time each day you would be drained, negate the drained effect. You recover 1 additional stack of drained each time you rest. | | Biblically Accurate | Your head is particularly unusual, resembling non-humanoid celestials. You have wings all over your head, heavenly rings with eyes instead of a head, a head cracked open to reveal the heavens or something similar.You have Resistance to holy damage. | | Minor Replace | | | ----------------------------------------------------------- | ---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | | Radiance (Recharge) | 1/day when you activate your halo's light, it causes foes within its radius to have blurred vision for 1 round (reflex negates) | | Passed Away | By making physical contact, you can tell the name of a corpse, or the name an undead had in life. You can also tell how long it has been dead. | | Glide | Glide upon feathered wings. Gain Glide1/day a swift action, you can increase the size and power of these wings for 1 minute, giving you a fly speed equal to your land speed. You can also take flight as part of the same action. | | Skill | Get a +2 bonus to one of the following: +Persuasion, +Medicine | Lore | Numbers | Exalted stand around 5'6 and weigh around 150 lbs Exalted are adults by 15 and live to be around 70 years old. Fallen typically appear as they did in adulthood for 50 years before passing. | | -------------------------------------------------------------------- | --------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | | Exalted | This race comes in 2 different forms, the more common is the exalted. These are creatures born from a mortal humanoid and a heavenly god. Exalted children usually only know one of their parents personally. Their heavenly parent usually only takes a passing interest in their mortal children, unless they are particularly devoted followers or they accrue a great deal of influence.Exalted that do know of their divine heritage often go to great lengths to impress their deific parent. The silence they experience is painful, but the satisfaction when they are finally acknowledged is immeasurable. Exalted can often feel great pressure to live in ways that reflect an unflinching moral compass. People will often put them on pedestals as the chosen ones of their god: A birthright that does not correspond with the personal thoughts and feelings of the exalted can create a lot of cognitive dissonance. Unhappy exalted tend to break away from their roots in very dramatic and drastic fashions. | | Fallen | The less common form is the fallen. These were creatures that passed away and through their deeds in life were granted access to Elysium in the afterlife. At one point or another though, they broke rules by dealing with fiends, sneaking people into the heavens or some other particularly heinous act. As punishment, they are forced to drink from the River Styx, forgetting much of their past selves. Then they are banished to the mortal realm, often living hallow lives, but never understanding where that feeling comes from. The touch of divinity never truly leaves them, however. Essentially reborn as adults, Fallen are often drawn to doing the same types of righteous things they did in life to earn their place among the greats. Fragments of memories from their previous lives remain, and what they remember can drastically change their outlook. Sometimes they can create hope that one day they may return, but in others, it only creates spite for the harshest punishment imaginable. | | Appearance and Biology | The exalted closely resemble the race of their mortal parent, but with bonus shinyness. They might have a metallic sheen to their hair, eyes that glow a solid gold, cracks along their body filled with gold or other such visually distinct changes. Universally they all have halos of some kind. Fallen resemble the appearance they had in life, but often with the color drained from them, supplemented by black. Their skin tends towards a gray porcelain, their hair, black. If they have wings, they will often have both black and white feathers. Exalted can reproduce freely with mortals, but without any attention from their deific parent, the children are born as their partners race. Fallen follow the same rules, but have no chance of ever creating another of their kind. | | Exalted vs Fallen | By their natures, fallen and exalted all tend to be first generation celestials, with the celestial blood diluting with each generation. As such, encounters between exalted and fallen tend to be uncommon. They tend to have mixed feelings about each other. For the exalted, they feel a mixture of pity and envy. Pity that the fallen have no place in the heavens and envy that they are unshackles from their heavenly duties. Oftentimes they will mistreat the fallen, giving them a "holier than thou" attitude. Others that feel they have failed their divine parents will reach out to the fallen and find a kinship that only someone abandoned by the gods can truly understand. For the fallen, exalted represent a possible connection to the divine. This presents an opportunity for redemption, so fallen are typically a lot more excited to find exalted than the other way around. Unfortunately, many will end up disappointed; Either by the lack of enthusiasm, or the lack of connection the exalted has with the divine. Being mistreated by the exalted can make fallen feel even more jaded than they were before about the gods and how they treat mortals. |

entries.title: Fairy
entries.content: The smol bullyable race. | Language | Sylvan | | -------- | ---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | | Size | Tiny. Tiny creatures have a reach of 0 ft and must move into the space of an enemy to Melee Attack them. | | Type | Fey | | Major Default | | | ----------------------------------------------------------- | ----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | | Tiny Wings | You hover at a speed of 30. During your turn you can use it to fly. Your poise is reduced by 5. | | Innate Technique (recharge) | You can innately cast fairy dust 3/day. Recharge after 1 minute. | | Minor Default | | | ----------------------------------------------------------- | ------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | | Sparkly | You emit light in a 20 ft radius. You can turn this off or on as a free action 1/turn. You create a sparkly substance you can apply to surfaces. You can use this to things like help identify someone or choose to leave an obvious trail of sparkling dust, making it easy to track you. You get advantage on skill checks when you can realistically be helped by such sparklyness (such as tracking, or +Perception to see through the disguise of people you've sparkled). | | Goo Goo Eyes (recharge) | Immediate action when you are attacked in melee, you give your foe a pleading gaze. They are unable to complete the attack, lose the action and cannot attack you until the start of their next turn (will negates). | Replacement Features | Major Replace | | | ----------------------------------------------------------- | ---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | | Moth-er | You share more in common with moths than just your wings. You are constantly under the effect of arcane pocket. Instead of your pocket, it affects your hair. +2 Floofyness | | Antennae: Sensory | Blindsense (scent, 10 ft) | | Lock-On (Elusive) | As a swift action, you can tether yourself to a creature within reach, matching their movement as long as they do not Teleportation, you don't lose line of sight and they don't move more than 100 ft in a turn.Your allies gain Seeking against this creature while you are locked-on. | | Minor Replace | | | ----------------------------------------------------------- | ------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | | Antennae: Emotional | While in contact with another creature, you experience their emotional state. You can pinpoint the origin of these feelings, giving you greater insight into this creature. Get advantage on +Insight and +Persuasion against creatures you are in contact with. | | Antennae: Expressive | You emit light in a 20 ft radius. You can turn this off or on as a free action 1/turn. You can cause your antennae to light up in specific patterns, allowing you to communicate in a way only other fairies can interpret. These lights can be seen a great distance away in the dark. | | Great Fairy (Su) (recharge) | As a swift action you can increase to large size for 10 minutes. | | Skill | Get a +2 bonus to one of the following: +Stealth, +Sleight of Hand, +Deception | Lore | Numbers | Fairies are Fairies reach adulthood by 12 and tend to live to be around 60 | | -------------------------------------------------------------------- | ----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | | Appearance and Biology | Fairies are thin, sparkly and usually have a pair of gossamer wings. They can grow small plants on their bodies such as flowers and vines. Some even have leafy hair.They have colors that match the natural world around them. If fairies stay in a new location for several months, their colors change to better blend in. This means that fairies that live in cold environments will likely have lighter tones with more blue, while forest-dwelling fairies will likely favor green and brown. Fairies will naturally change with the seasons as well, causing their hair to be a vibrant green in the spring/summer, and brown or red in the fall. Fairies naturally create a powder commonly referred to as "fairy dust" that has a wide variety of applications depending on what the fairy is adapted to. It can help with tracking, decoration and even combat. Used as fuel, it can produce fairy fire, which burns until put out and comes in a wide variety of colors. | | We do a lil' Trolling | Fey creatures are well known for being mischievous, and fairies are no exception. Their flight, small size and propensity for pranks make them prototypical as far as the trickster fey archetype is concerned. They will snatch things from windowsills, use their fairy dust to mess up paintings or paint jobs and use their small size and huge eyes to appear innocent and get out of trouble. Most fairies don't stray too far from mischievous. Even their evil plots are usually centered around something minor and silly like ruining someone's vegetables with their guerrilla gardening. As such, its usually difficult to take a fairy seriously, even if they are causing serious harm. On the opposite side, their good deeds also tend to not be anything spectacular, like helping a tipped over turtle or battling against a beehive.Fairies tend to be unconcerned with the world at large and its problems, or the long term consequences of their actions. They may end up the saviors of the world joining along some adventuring party, but they were just in it because they like giving the party's beast companion treats and the bard was cute. They may also end up as lackeys in some grandiose plot to take over the world because they were convinced it was gonna be some super awesome prank. | | Great Fairies | In general, fairies don't live in settlements with other humanoids. They live in the wilderness amongst animals, plants and other fey in little alcoves and clearings protected by a great fairy. Great fairies are massive in size and serve as the guardians to portals to the realm of the fey: the feywilds. These portals are usually decorated pools of shimmering water water decorated with all manner of colorful plants, toadstools and rocks. Great fairies typically reside directly inside this portal, partially submerged so they can interact with creatures in a manner that's at least a little closer to face to face. They can handily deactivate the portal, making interacting with the great fairy a necessary part of using the portal. They usually serve to protect any who wish to travel to and fro between the realms. They also serve as something of a border patrol, keeping the worst from both sides away from the portal.Great fairies are powerful magical creatures that few are willing to attack all on their own. Such plans are made all the more difficult by the entourage of allies they usually gather. Great fairies will naturally accrue a lot of followers and may even be inclined to create organizations around finding and protecting these portals. Because these locations are well protected and serve as a natural gathering spot for travelers, these are where the fairy population is its most dense. Many don't directly serve the great fairy, but are willing to perform menial tasks like sending messages and guiding people through the wilds. Sometimes begrudgingly, sometimes happily, seeing it as an opportunity to mess with humanoids less familiar with the poisonous mushrooms and dangerous sleepy monsters of the natural world. |

entries.title: Geniekin
entries.content: The earth, wind and fire (water too I guess) race. | Language | Primordial | | -------- | -------------------------------------------------------------------- | | Size | Medium or Small | | Type | Humanoid, Elemental | | Subrace | Choose one of the four elements: Earthkin, Firekin, Waterkin, Airkin | | Major Default | | | ----------------------------------------------------------- | --------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | | Movement | Air - Hover equal to Land Speed.Water - Swim equal to (land speed+20) and amphibious qualityEarth - Burrow 5Fire - +10 land speed | | Wish (Recharge) | You can speak the words "I wish…" as an instant action followed by a desire you have. Your next related task within an hour that requires a d20 roll you add 4 to it. | | Minor Default | | | ----------------------------------------------------------- | ------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | | Charged (recharge) | As a swift action, charge your weapon/unarmed/natural attacks with energy for 1 round. They deal an extra d6 damage of this type. This can also be added the Bender's elemental blast.Air - ZapWater - FrostEarth - PoisonFire - Fire | | Mixed Blood | Gain a minor feature of another race (including alternate features). You count as both races for all purposes. | Replacement Features | Major Replace | | | ----------------------------------------------------------- | ---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | | Feat | You get the bending initiate feat. It shares your elemental tag. Air, water and earth deal crush damage while fire does fire damage. | | Resistance | Air - ZapWater - FrostEarth - Poison, Toxin, envenomedFire - Fire | | Firekin:Kindling | You get +2 Attack against creatures set Ablaze. When you set a creature ablaze, the DC to get rid of it is 2 higher. | | Airkin:Storm Sight | You can see the silhouettes of creatures shrouded in clouds of gas.Mist, fog, clouds etc cannot grant creatures Total Concealment to you, regardless of distance. Ignore Partial Concealment of adjacent creatures in such environments. | | Earthkin: Diamond Skin | Ranged attacks cannot graze you. | | Earthkin:Gem Bod | When you are slain, you shatter into pieces. As long as most of these pieces are intact, they can be reattached, restoring them to life. This requires 500g worth of adhesive and 10 minutes of work. This process can also be done to restore a severed limb, requiring only 50g.Your memories are stored all throughout your body. When you sustain major damage, you lose some of your memories. Whenever you are defeated, you forget the minute leading up to when you were KO’d. When you are slain, or if you lose a limb, you forget the previous 24 hours. Losing more significant chunks permanently can give amnesia. | | Minor Replace | | | ----------------------------------------------------------- | ------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | | Earthkin:Metalhead | As a standard action, you can cause the metal in your body to converge in your hands in a specific shape. You can use this to create weapons, tools, shields and other types of metal equipment. This should generally be something that is lightweight, like a hammer or a dagger.You cannot use this feature until you absorb the metal object, or consume some more, and no more than 1/day. | | Earthkin/Waterkin:Deep Dweller | Gain nightvision. You treat dim light as bright light. You treat darkness as dim light. | | Firekin/Waterkin:Fire Fighter (Su) | By touching a fire, you can put it out and all fire within 10 ft. For magic effects, treat as Dispel Magic. | | Firekin: Fired Up (Light) | +10 speed on the first round of combat. You can choose to emit bright light in a 20 ft radius. You can turn this off or on as a free action 1/turn. | | Airkin: Gust of wind | As easily as you can breathe, you can blow on an object within 30 ft to push it 30 ft in any direction. You can exert enough force to move dense objects less than 10 lbs short distances, but objects with large surface area like signs can be affected more easily. | | Waterkin: Camouflage | While at least waist deep in water, you have Camouflage. | Lore | Numbers | Geniekin stand around 5'6 and weigh around 150 lbs Earth and water geniekin tend to be slightly heavier, while air and fire tend to be lighter Geniekin are adults by 15 and live to be around 70 years old. | | -------------------------------------------------------------------- | -------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | | Appearance and Biology | Geniekin are humanoids with roughly the same size and build as humans, but they come in 4 distinct variants based on their elemental affinity. Air geniekin tend towards light blue skin tones and white or blonde hair. They love feeling the wind blowing in their hair so they usually will go to great lengths to maintain a large head of hair. Air geniekin are the lightest of the bunch, rarely breaking 120 lbs for even the largest of them.Fire geniekin have red and orange skin tones, and hair that resembles fire blazing atop their heads. Male geniekin will also have facial hair with the same properties. They have a much higher body temperature than most humanoids, making them very popular in the winter.Water geniekin skin tones vary between blue and green, depending on whether they prefer salt or freshwater. Just like with firekin, their hair can also mimic their element. It looks as though it is in a constant state of flow. Most are amphibious, capable of living comfortably in land or sea. Earth geniekin are typically gray, brown, black or somewhere in between. Instead of hair, some grow crystalline structures on their head that they can mold to resemble hair. It lacks the same softness and malleability, but makes up for it with shinyness. Earthkin are the heaviest of the bunch. Their density causes them to be upwards of 200lbs. The "elemental-ness" of a geniekin can vary greatly. With some being closer to humans, and others that are made of stone for example. | | Like a Genie! | Geniekin are all the byproduct of genie wishes. One way or another, all of the humanoids generated through genie magic are geniekin. Wish for an army? Geniekin soldiers. Wish for a son? Geniekin. An entire planet complete with servants? Geniekin servants. Particularly large and grandiose wishes that people have made in the past have made it so the population of geniekin is steady beyond first generation wish creations. Created by magic, geniekin often feel drawn to studying magic in some way, shape or form. It makes them feel as though they are getting closer to finding the origin of their species. Many geniekin are still born with a spark of that genie's magic that created them all those years, perhaps even generations ago. All they need to do is speak "I wish" and their genie powers work to shape the reality to their liking…albeit in less impressive and more subtle ways. It's more like gently steering reality in a desired direction than shaping it. | | Avatar | Geniekin are the creators of the 4 Bender styles, learning from the original wielders of earth, wind, fire and water. They are often drawn to each other by their natural affinity for such magic. There are many settlements that started up as a result of several earth benders or water benders meeting up and starting a school to teach. The ability to bend their respective element is the primary uniting and binding force behind geniekin society. The most highly regarded individuals among geniekin are the bending masters. Benders will sometimes be given a final task by their masters to locate what they call "elemental wells." These are places that house large amounts of energy as a result of natural formations. Places like volcanoes, storms or tornadoes are common locations. These locations establish a much deeper connection between the bender and their element, and usually marks them as a master. Legend has it that when the world falls into chaos, there will be someone who is capable of mastering all 4 elements, drawing power from 4 different elemental wells, and using it to bring balance to the world. |

entries.title: Goblin
entries.content: The green shortstacks with big ears | Language | Goblin | | -------- | -------- | | Size | Small | | Type | Humanoid | | Major Default | | | ----------------------------------------------------------- | -------------------------------------------- | | Tactical Retreat | You can Disengage as a swift action. | | Rubber Body | You gain Rubbery as an innate technique. | | Minor Default | | | ----------------------------------------------------------- | ---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | | Nightvision | You treat dim light as bright light. You treat darkness as dim light. | | Skill | Get a +2 bonus to a one of the following: +Perception, Alchemy, or Engineering | Replacement Features | Major Replace | | | ----------------------------------------------------------- | --------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | | Strength in Numbers | When you flank with your companion creatures, their attack bonus is +4 instead of +2. | | Roll With It (Elusive) | When you take damage, you can choose to recoil as an instant action 1/turn. You move 10 feet in a direction of your choosing. | | Ankle Biter | You gain a bite attack that deals d6 base damage. When a creature rolls a combat maneuver check against you and fails to break your poise, you can make a simple bite attack against them as an AoO. | | Giant Ears | +2 perceptionEven for gobbo, your ears are exceptionally large. This allows you to clearly hear distant sounds, and focus your hearing much like you might your eyes. With line of effect you can clearly hear whispers 30 ft away, average conversation up to 100 ft away and shouting 300 ft away. This functions even in the presence of other noise, such as in crowds or noisy bars. Your ears are very bouncy and expressive. | | Minor Replace | | | ----------------------------------------------------------- | --------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | | Just a Little Kick (elusive) | When you shoot a gun, you can choose to let the recoil knock you back 10 ft in the opposite direction you shot. | | Friends Not Food | You can speak to beasts, but only if they your size or smaller. You cannot willingly harm such creatures. | | Mixed Blood | Gain a minor feature of another race (including alternate features). You count as both races for all purposes. | Lore | Numbers | Goblins are usually around 3 ft tall and around 30 lbs They reach adulthood by 10 and live to be around 40 | | --------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | -------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | | Appearance and Biology | Goblins are short green people with oversized heads. They have large eyes and larger mouths, often with sharp teeth that fall out and regrow frequently. Even considering their oversized head, their ears are enormous, sometimes growing to be comically large. Some can even be a little on the fuzzy side, with fluffy ears closer to that of an animal, or a tail with a tuft of fur at the end of it.Goblins are lazy, and tend to present themselves in a candid manner without a lot of attention to their physical appearance. This typically means they wear styles that require the smallest amount of maintenance: bald heads, no makeup, wearing the same things they wore as a kid, never changing out of their armor. Goblin engineers may just wear overalls and nothing else because its all it takes to get the job done. Goblins also tend to be less concerned with cleanliness than other races. If it isn't required, then its pretty low on the priority list. | | Mine | A goblin's short life span and dubious morals combined with their ravenous hunger and general laziness all combine to create a race that rarely has anything to show for their existence. While other races create cities and monuments, goblins prefer to move into wherever is mostly convenient. Goblins will treat the ruins of an ancient city as though they had always lived there, and will make up stories for how they made things. They will invade small towns to kick out their current inhabitants without giving much thought to the consequences when the survivors come to back to take their town backed by the local government. This lack of foresight, or perhaps lack of caring for the consequences of their actions contributes to the shortness of the goblin's lifespan. An actual city with tens of thousands of people or more started up and run by goblins is completely unheard of. Most goblin settlements tend to crumple under their own weight once they reach a certain size. Or they get too big for their britches and anger the wrong adventuring party, local rich person or monster, resulting in a hard reset in a new place. | | Leeches | Goblin tendencies make them terrible at governance, but that doesn't make them completely incompatible with "civilized" society. Small enclaves of goblins will often be a part of any city they aren't outright banned from. Whether their old family died, they were abandoned at a young age, or they fantasize about fancy living, goblins frequently find themselves wandering into towns with nothing but the clothes on their back and a dream of the good life. Alas, the talents they have accrued living out in the wilderness do little to help them live honest lives, and the city is hardly kinder than the outside. City goblins tend towards petty crime or sex work in order to make ends meet. As such, even the upstanding goblin citizens have a mighty uphill battle against the stereotypes of goblin raiders that haunt peoples dreams and the goblin whores that attend to their carnal desires.Their lack of experience with working in general makes them unreliable workers at best. Savvy employers often exploit young goblins, knowing they're going to burn out quick anyway. Might as well get the value while you can. The deception works both ways as the goblins are always finding ways to slip goods they're supposed to sell into their pockets, or take a little bit of money from the register when nobody is looking. | | Superior Genetics | While the picture people paint of goblins is typically negative, they still manage to maintain a steady(ish) population and a consistent place in peoples minds. They bring a certain energy to everything they do that is unlike most other races. Extreme optimism, lack of expertise combined with a general trust of whoever feeds them makes goblins excellent companions for maintaining morale. Due to their short-sightedness, every victory is worth celebrating and every plan with a huge payout is a banger idea. When they hit the jackpot they keep rolling. When they lose it all, they say double or nothin'. Every defeat they survive is a new opportunity.Goblins make up for their numerous negative traits with their ability to learn things quickly, and with novel approaches. Engineer is basically synonymous with inventor for goblins, as they refuse to use the principles they are educated on for practical purposes, always forging their own path. If it isn't a spell and it explodes, it was probably a goblin that made it. Goblins are also nearly as fond of race mixing as humans. It'd be more accurate to say they are just fond of breeding, but they aren't picky with partners. They have the hardware to accommodate for larger races, and they are happy to take good genes when they find them. Combined with their short lifespans, goblins can quickly morph to physically resemble whatever other race is most dense within a few generations. |

entries.title: Hellion
entries.content: The horny race. | Language | Infernal | | -------- | --------------- | | Size | Medium or small | | Type | Humanoid, Fiend | | Major Default | | | ----------------------------------------------------------- | ---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | | Darkvision | You can see in the dark as well as you could in light, albeit in grayscale. | | Innate Techniques | You can cast a series of spells, each 1/dayLvL 1 - Wave of DarknessLvL 3 - Cloud of DarknessLvL 5 - Encroaching Darkness | | Minor Default | | | ----------------------------------------------------------- | --------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | | Tail | You have an additional prehensile limb in a tail. | | Delicious and Nutritious Recharge | You can consume dirt, sand, ash, dust etc. for nourishment.You can regurgitate this substance to use the Pocket Sand technique. You must eat another meal of said substance to recharge this technique. | Replacement Features | Major Replace | | | ----------------------------------------------------------- | --------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | | Burning Heart | You gain resistance to fire damage. Anything you touch takes 1 fire damage per turn. This does not apply to attacks, but it does apply when you Drag, grapple or are dragged/grappled. | | Sacrificial Pact (Su) (Healing) | By cutting yourself and bleeding on another creature as a standard action, you take (3+LvL) True Damage, and heal the creature for double that amount. You can only take a maximum of your current hp in damage. | | Horny | You have pointed or ram-like horns and a skull that's harder than most. You gain a natural attack with d10 (crush or pierce) base damage. You can make a simple attack (which cannot be a combat maneuver) with these horns as a swift action, but its damage decreases to d4 and you become clumsy 1 On-hit from how disorienting it is to swing your head into creatures. | | Natural Armor | Your body is covered in scales, obsidian, or metal plates that protect you, but limit your movement and chafes against traditional armor. Your AC is 14+lvl. You cannot add DEX to AC and cannot wear armor. Natural armor also does not give poise. | | Minor Replace | | | ----------------------------------------------------------- | ------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------ | | Maw and Claw | Even without a weapon, you are always armed. You have a set of claws on your hands and a bite that deal d10 base damage. | | Glide | You gain Glide | | Alter Self (Su) poly, true | Choose one specific human shape that cannot be altered. Usable 1/day for 10 minutes. Minor fiendish features remain. | | Skill | Get a +2 bonus to one of the following: +Planes, +Sleight of Hand, or+Religion | Lore | Numbers | Stand around 5'6 and weigh around 150 lbs Small variants are about half as tall and 1/5 the weightHellion are adults by 15 and live to be around 70 years old. When created they usually form biologically in early adulthood. | | -------------------------------------------------------------------- | ---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | | Appearance and Biology | Hellion are roughly human-shaped, but can come in many different forms. Many have spaded or forked tails they can use to grab objects. Some have vestigial wings. The most common feature of a Hellion is the horns on their head. They come in all different shapes and sizes: Long curved horns like a ram, short pointed horns, multiple pairs of horns, only a single horn. They can superficially resemble the race they were before their fiendish transformation with pointed elf ears or orcish tusks, or they can more closely resemble the fiend they transformed into. Hellion can have wildly different skin colors from crimson red to a grayish blue. Some of them even have furry digitigrade lower halves with cloven hooves.Hellion that try to mesh well into society will dress in whatever the locals wear. As a result of how they are created, many take the opportunity to devote themselves to Brazzeria and wear her ceremonial garb. Though the stereotypical freshly created hellion will likely get piercings, decorate their horns in jewelry and wear indecent clothing just to be contrarian. | | Open Your Heart | Hellion are created by a Brazzerian purification ritual used on demons that were once mortal. Many fiends are beyond redemption, but those that aren't can be exposed to kindness, care and respect to slowly restore their humanity. Brazerrian priests and priestesses are able to read the hearts to fiends to tell whether they are closed or open, representing their receptiveness to healing the fiendish corruption. Over time they can (usually) be exposed to enough kindness to eventually open their heart and begin the transformation. Freshly transformed hellions have no memories of their previous life before being a fiend, but they do vividly remember what happened during their time as a fiend: All of the suffering they caused, all the lives they’ve taken and all of the kindness they were treated with in order to regain their humanity. After some rehabilitation, they are given a choice to join the cause or return back to society. | | Racism | Unfortunately for hellion, there are scars caused by fiends all over the world. Many people do not understand the intricacies of fiendish creation, or the humanity restoration the hellion undergo. As such, newly created hellion that do not stay under the protective wings of Brazzeria can often be in for a rough time. Small communities of hellion can often be found on the fringes of places where there is a great deal of Brazzerian influence. The racism can go both ways; providing hardship as well as opportunity. Hellion will often find themselves getting approached by unsavory individuals looking for a new hire in their organized crime ring. They will also find it difficult to shake off the "sex demon" stereotype perpetuated by the prolific and ever-present succubi/twinkubi. It does serve them well when finding dates or getting jobs at brothels at least. |

entries.title: Human
