entries.content: The default race. Humans with the Race Mixing feat replace traditional half breed races. Other races with the minor mixed blood feature are predominantly the original race, only slightly resembling the other parent. Humans are purposefully designed to be pretty generic. | Language | Any non-secret | | -------- | -------------- | | Size | Medium | | Type | Humanoid | | Major Default | | | ----------------------------------------------------------- | ------------------ | | Skill | Gain a bonus skill | | Feat | Gain a bonus feat | | Minor Default | | | ----------------------------------------------------------- | ------------------------------------------------------------------------- | | Intermingle | 1/day when you roll an opposed skill check, reroll it. | | Skill+ | Get a +2 bonus to any skill. | Replacement Features | Major Replace | | | ----------------------------------------------------------- | -------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | | Top Percentage | Get a +2 bonus to any skill | | Versatility | You can change the Ability Score used for 1 skill you are proficient in. | | Technique | Gain a technique point, only useable within your specialization(s). | | Signature Skill | Get the Signature Skill feat in a skill you are proficient in. Get it again at level 5. | | Minor Replace | | | ----------------------------------------------------------- | ---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | | Mixed Blood | Gain a minor feature of another race (including alternate features). You count as both races for all purposes. Even if you get mixed blood from another feature, you can still only be a mix of maximum 2 races. | Lore | Numbers | Humans stand around 5'6 and weigh around 150 lbsHumans are adults by 15 and live to be around 70 years old. | | --------------------------------------------------------------- | --------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | | Humans in Fantasy | Personally, I've never given humans much thought in fantasy. I don't think they should be particularly different than they are in real life because they serve as an anchor for people not accustomed to fantasy. Lots of players like to play self-insert characters, and lots of DMs like to have human settlements to provide places where they don't have to think much about culture, mannerisms, architecture etc. I think humans are just fine as is, no changes needed. They are a nice glue to keep all the mismatched wild races of fantasy together.THE MOST I would say about humans is their versatility in skills gives them an economic edge, making them more prosperous and more ambitious when expanding their territory. |

entries.title: Orc
entries.content: The big green bully race. | Language | Orc | | -------- | -------- | | Size | Medium | | Type | Humanoid | | Major Default | | | ----------------------------------------------------------- | ------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------ | | Bully | +2 bonus to Attack with combat maneuvers | | Ferocity (Recharge) | When you are reduced to zero hit points, you are not defeated until the end of your next turn. On your next turn you are staggered. If while in ferocity you are healed before you take your turn, you are not defeated at the end of your next turn, and this ability is not consumed.This ability can only activate once per minute. | | Minor Default | | | ----------------------------------------------------------- | ---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | | Nightvision | You treat dim light as bright light. You treat darkness as dim light | | Skill | Get a +2 bonus to one of the following: +Athletics, +Intimidation, +Survival | Replacement Features | Major Replace | | | ----------------------------------------------------------- | ------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------ | | Bullying Blow | As a swift action, enhance your next melee attack this turn to deal poise damage equal to its damage. | | Vengeful | Whenever a creature does damage to you, you can mark them as an immediate action. You gain +2 Attack against them until you mark someone else. | | Bathe in Blood | Any turn you take bleed damage (AKA it isn't negated), your slashing weapons gain the bleed property. As a free action, you can lick your blade to take 1 bleed damage. | | Blood Scent | You gain Seeking against wounded creatures. | | Porc | +Blindsense (Scent, 10 ft) | | Savagery | Get a technique point in the barbarism tree | | Eyes on the Prize | Your lazy eye is always watching. You cannot be flanked unless at least 3 creatures threaten you | | Cataracts | Your stubbornness against lesser creatures who say "you can't stare at the sun" manifests in your ability to stare directly into the sun and emerge still able to see. You have Immunity to the blurred vision and blinded conditions, but suffer -4+Perception. | | Minor Replace | | | ----------------------------------------------------------- | ----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | | Lift | +100 Carry Weight | | Tenacious recharge | 1/day Reroll a Fortitude or Will save. | | Gate Crusher | Get a +2 bonus to Brute Force. You can roll such a check as a free action during a Core Rules, How to Play/Actions/Common Actions/Full Round Actions/Charge 1/turn. | | Stoic | You need to be shaken, sickened or clumsy 2 before you suffer any numerical penalties from said conditions. | | Fire Lord's Chosen | Whenever you deal fire damage with one of your actions, deal 1 more damage. | Lore | Numbers | Orcs are usually around 6' and weigh 200 lbs, with a great deal of variance both up and down Orcs are adults by 10 and usually live to be about 50 | | -------------------------------------------------------------------- | ---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | | Appearance and Biology | The most telling feature of the orc is the shape of their skull. They have large jaws with an underbite in order to accommodate for their tusks. They have short, sometimes pointed ears. Orcs are much larger than most humanoids, typically being taller and having a much higher muscle density. Even the females share this quality. Orcs need to kill in order to survive. A healthy orc diet is meat, meat and more meat. But more than just meat, the act of killing itself energizes an orc, and gives their skin more color. Killing more powerful creatures and humanoids is worth more than a rabbit. The less an orc kills, the more they gray out. The more they kill, the more green they become. Orcs that kill extensively turn a dark red hue. The amount that needs to be killed isn't something that is easily translated into game mechanics. Decide what's right for you based on the pace of your campaign.Orcs are very generous with the beatings they give their body, even when it comes to fashion. They get body and face tattoos, piercings all over and they use scars to trace patterns in their flesh. They commonly dress themselves in furs and leathers, or whatever handy material they can harvest from the creatures they hunt: There's no easier way to show off your greatest kill than to wear in on your sleeve. Literally. | | Barbarism | No two words fit well together quite like orc barbarian. The prototypical barbarian lives only by killing other creatures. Depending on how annoying their neighbors are, this could even mean other humanoids. Individual orcs tend to keep to themselves. It is only when large groups of orcs gather together where they start becoming a problem for anything around them. It isn't uncommon for a charismatic leader to arise among tribes and unify a few hundred (or more). Then emboldened by their numbers, they try to take over surrounding towns. More often than not, these types of battles happen between orcs and other orcs. Orcs have long and drawn out histories of their clan and the surrounding ones, with shaky alliances and even shakier reasons for breaking them. The call for glory in battle is often answered with an almost religious zeal. It isn't all blood in guts within the walls of orc villages. They take great interest in animals; and not just to eat them! They make excellent Beast Trainers and in general are talented when it comes to the druidic arts. Even the barbarians have animal spirits they use to inform their bestial fighting styles. Orcs are surprisingly mindful when it comes to maintaining a natural equilibrium in their local ecosystem, sometimes taking extreme matters into their own hands when they see others disturb this balance. Unfortunately, they don't share the same degree of respect for the cities and civilizations of others. | | Friend or Foe? | Because of their proclivity for violence, orcs are near universally feared and avoided unless necessary. Red orcs in particular are avoided at all costs. Orc culture can be very intimidating to outsiders who are accustomed to less violence. So their questions become "can I trust an orc?" or "are orcs dangerous?" This typically depends on the situation. If you hear the war horns, most definitely. But most situations aren’t nearly as cut and dry. Travelling along the road and seeing a half dozen group eyeing your party might make a merchant sweat a little. Much to the dismay of traveling merchants, they will likely end up in many situations with orcs where they are forced to choose between their goods or their life. Orcs are bullies that use the threat of violence as leverage in order to get what they want. Unfortunately for orcs, they are also prone to misdirection. Convince them that there is a much more lucrative option and they might spare you and your goods. | | Outsiders | Orcs that don't align with a particular tribe tend to be drifters, hopping into whatever group their particular talents and morals are well suited for. Common entries are mercenary groups, organized crime and state militaries. Most people tend to view orcs with the same kind of wariness one affords a natural predator; respect, but stay cautious. This serves them well when they want to rise through the ranks of a military, but does little to aid in friendly relations with others. Rarely does a town have a neighborhood filled primarily with orcs, but they tend to pepper any town with more than a few thousands people. Separated from traditional orc culture, orcs can find great success with studies of magic and the arts just like most other people, but they have a bit of an uphill battle getting past stereotypes. |

entries.title: Pints
entries.content: The genetically engineered small race. | Language | Any non-secret | | --------------- | ------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------ | | Size | Small | | Type | Humanoid | | Mood Swing | Your skin color is prone to frequent changes that you cannot control. In return, you see everyone else's mood as a colorful aura around them. You get a +2 bonus to opposed skill checks and other interpersonal skills like +Persuasion. Creatures can identify your overall mood by just looking at your colors. Creatures get a +8 bonus on +Insight checks against you.You can also flavor text this as something else, like visible spirit hovering nearby displaying emotions (ala Elden Ring's Ranni). | | Mutation: Minor | Pick one from the list under replace minor features. | | Major Default | | | ----------------------------------------------------------- | ---------------------------------------------------- | | Mutation: Major | Pick one from the list under replace major features. | | Minor Default | | | ----------------------------------------------------------- | ------------------------------------------------------------------------------------ | | Skill | Get a +2 bonus to one of the following: Alchemy, +Stealth, +Medicine | Replacement Features | Major Replace | | | ----------------------------------------------------------- | -------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | | Mutation: Aquatic | You have gills, webbed fingers etc. You are amphibious and have a swim speed equal to land speed+20. You also have an addition set of transparent eyelids that give you advantage on saves against effects that inflict blurred vision and blinded. | | Mutation: Expanded Intellect(Recharge) | Your cranium is abnormally large and good at remembering things. 1/day you can perfectly recall anything you have ever experienced in your entire life. This is usually limited to a single freeze frame for things you have seen, such as a page in a book. Or it is limited to a minute or so of spoken words. For events as part of backstory, consider rolling luck to see if it actually happened to determine of its realistic to recall something about it. | | Mutation: Chameleon | You can change the color of your skin as a free action whenever you hide. Gain Concealment against creatures farther than 5 ft away from you. You cannot use this feature to start hiding while directly observed, but can do so to prowl in areas lacking concealment/cover after using it out of sight. | | Mutation: Pipsqueek (Su)(poly, true) | As a standard action, you can transform into a specific tiny terrestrial animal such as a rat, a lizard or a chicken. You choose what animal you become at character creation and it cannot be changed. You also transform into this creature when defeated. You can transform back to your humanoid form as a free action. | | Minor Replace | | | ----------------------------------------------------------- | --------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | | Mixed Blood | Your ancestors were once another race. Inherit a minor trait from it | | Mutation: Prehensile Tail | You have a long and powerful tail that can be used to hold and manipulate objects, but not wield equipment like weapons, shields and staves. | | Mutation: Bioluminescence | Your hair, eyes or skin can emanate light in a 20 ft radius. You can turn this on or off 1/turn as a free action. | | Mutation: Adhesion | Your body has little hairs or suction cups to help you cling to surfaces. You gain a climb speed equal to your land speed, and can use it along any solid surface, even up smooth walls and along ceilings.You get a +8 bonus to Climbing checks. | Lore | Numbers | Pints tend to be 2'6'' tall and weigh ~20 lbs They reach adulthood by 15 and live to be about 70 | | -------------------------------------------------------------------- | --------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | | Shortstackium | Once upon a time there existed a large and prosperous city inside of the Land of Earth called Pint. The natural ebb and flow of the world caused resources like food to grow scarce. Many solutions were tried and failed but one mad scientist saw the solution that nobody else could: Half resource consumption by shrinking everyone down to half size. He spent his entire life being looked down upon, and now it was going to be his turn. He created a chemical compound he called shortstackium that, even in small doses, could cause people to shrink in size significantly. He found clever ways to get this substance into the food and water supply. He put trace amounts into his potions that he sold to adventurers. It was very fast acting, causing people to shrink by the time they awoke the next day. Due to its complexity and strangeness, it took a few weeks to identify the problem and another month to eliminate most of its circulation, getting its creator thrown in jail in the process. His solution was quite effective, but it completely transformed people unwillingly, often with strange side effects. No alchemist has ever managed to figure out how to reverse the effects, and the effects are passed down through children. | | Appearance and Biology | Because shorstackium was most effective on humans, the typical pint most closely resembles humans, but pints resembling other races also exist. A great deal of genetic mutation happened as a result of initial incident. This has caused all kinds of changes to the pints. | | | |

entries.title: Plantalones
entries.content: The plant people | Language | Sylvan | | -------- | ------ | | Size | Medium | | Type | Plant | | Major Default | | | ----------------------------------------------------------- | ------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | | Photosynthesis | While not indoors and exposed to direct sunlight, you have fast healing 1. Minor amounts of shade is not enough to disable this, but large overgrowths/canopies are.You do not need to consume food when you photosynthesize for at least an hour each day. | | Garden Variety | You gain the hide in plain sight quality, usable in any place with vegetation around your size. | | Minor Default | | | ----------------------------------------------------------- | ----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | | Skill | +2 nature | | Vines | You have vines that can move around and manipulate objects within reach. This functions like an additional prehensile limb. You can use it to hold, but not wield, equipment. | Replace Features | Major Replace | | | ----------------------------------------------------------- | -------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | | Vines | You are persistently under the effects of Vine Whip. | | Life Charged | You have resistance to Necrotic damage. | | Soothing Fragrance recharge | As a standard action, release a powerful aroma that effects all creatures within your natural reach.In combat this applies the pacified condition for 1 round (will negates).Out of combat this applies charm to creatures for 1 hour (will negates).Creatures with Scent have disadvantage on their save. | | Ohhh Banana Healing | You grow delicious fruits, vegetables or juice using your body that can be consumed. Any ally other than you can pluck and consume one of these treats as a swift action if you are within their natural reach, healing 5 hp. They lose this healing property if not immediately eatenAny enemy can choose to eat one of these fruits instead of the usual effect of breaking your poise.You grow 3 fruits at the start of each day. | | Rooted | As a swift action, you can reduce your speed to 0 until the start of your next turn. This resets your poise. | | Sowing Seeds | As a standard action, or automatically when you die, you can expel a seed the size of a mouse from your body. By planting this seed in fertile soil, you can grow it over the course of a week. At the end of this week, your current body withers and your spirit inhabits this new body.Whenever this cycle happens, you become drained 1 and you can redistribute all of your skill points into new skills (if desired). You still maintain your previous age, along with any permanent ailments. | * | Minor Replace | | | ----------------------------------------------------------- | ---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | | Friends | You can speak with plants and plant creatures as though you shared a common language. Inanimate plants tend to not have much to say other than what has directly interacted with them, or causes them distress. | | Flowering | Your body emerges from a big flower that you are attached to. You locomote via wriggling vines instead of legs. Your speed is reduced by 5 but your poise is increased by 5. | | Scrub | Your size is changed to small.You cannot swim. Instead you can walk along the surface of liquids. The first round you do you move at full speed. The second round it is treated as difficult terrain and the third you fall in and begin drowning (even if trained in athletics or otherwise have a swim speed, amphibious etc). | * Lore | Numbers | | | -------------------------------------------------------------------- | --- | | Appearance and Biology | |

entries.title: Polymerization
entries.content: The the gooey fusion race. | Language | Depends on origin race | | ----------- | -------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | | Size | Depends on origin race | | Type | Ooze, humanoid | | Gooeyness | +2 | | Origin Race | Polymerizations (shortened to Poly or Polymer) are fusions between a unique ooze creature and a creature they consumed. As such, they share the size and racial language of a specific race. | | Mixed Blood | Gain a minor feature of another race (including alternate features). You count as both races for all purposes. Must match origin race. | | Major Default | | | ----------------------------------------------------------- | ------------------------------------------------------------------ | | Stretch | During your turn, your natural reach increases by 5 ft. | | Blindsense | Tremorsense, 5 ft | | Minor Default | | | ----------------------------------------------------------- | -------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | | Compression | You are able to move through spaces designed for creatures 1 size smaller than you without treating it as difficult terrain. You are able to move through spaces designed for creatures 2 sizes smaller than you by treating it as difficult terrain | Replacement Features | Major Replace | | | ----------------------------------------------------------- | ---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | | Bounce Off | As an immediate action when you are hit with crush damage from a Melee Attack, you can force the attacking creature 10 ft back | | Minor Replace | | | ----------------------------------------------------------- | --------------------- | | Amorphous | AC+11 to Crit you | Lore | Numbers | Height and weight are roughly the same as the creature they consumedPoly lead a relatively short life and split at 40. | | -------------------------------------------------------------------- | ----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | | Consumption | Once upon a time an alchemist created an ooze capable of forming a special bond with a person it consumes. They tried to kill it after it had consumed someone close to them, but poly only multiply when killed after absorbing a creature. Their death triggers a reaction where they split into two unintelligent amorphous blobs that seek creatures to consume. Since this event, they have slowly spread. Some non-poly use these slime creatures as a method to continue their life after suffering health complications, other people fear their hunger for intelligent life, but most know nothing about the origin of poly. | | Appearance and Biology | Poly share the general shape of the person they consumed. While poly are somewhat amorphous, this is their "true form" that they do not deviate from too much. As oozes, they are often viscous and jello-like, coming in a variety of colors. Pressing your finger to their skin, it would bend, but it would be difficult to break. They are generally at least semi-transparent.Despite having the ability to remove imperfections, such as scars, missing teeth, slices of ear missing, poly keep these qualities. Instead the alterations they make usually add completely new features, like antennae, horns or tattoos.Poly generally have "barbie bod" smoothing over their intimate parts, and can get away with not wearing any clothing, but many still do. When they do wear clothing, they always choose things that are exceptionally elastic to accommodate for their stretching formsDespite having little in common with humanoids in terms of their biology, they will usually still try to mimic how creatures hold things with fingers and eat with their mouths. It can still be a bit awkward watching their food slowly dissolve in their goo (one reason why they like to wear clothing). | | Memories of the Past | While it is clear that a poly loses its soul when it dies and splits, all poly are essentially clones of their "parents" combined with some other creature unfortunate enough to be eaten. Some aspects of their predecessors' consciousness seep into their own. As such, poly often have unusual senses of self. Many of their thoughts are dominated by the memories and feelings of the creature they consumed. While they rest, their dreams are often filled with memories of their previous life. Consuming creatures with particularly strong convictions can lead to the poly working to achieve similar goals. Some poly learn more about their origin and feel guilt towards what must have happened in order for them to exist. Other poly don't understand why they have a head filled with the memories of an outsider, and do everything they can to avoid things that trigger these alien memories. |

entries.title: Poplin
entries.content: The toy companion race. | Language | Racial language of bonded sibling | | -------------- | --------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | | Size | Small | | Type | Humanoid, Construct | | Bonded Sibling | Poplin are born from the wishes of children as they play with their toys. They never physically grow like their sibling does, but they grow as people in tandem. Poplins die when their bonded sibling die. When their sibling takes damage, they know. They can always tell the direction of their sibling. | | Miniaturize | As a standard action, you can shrink drastically to the size of an action figure, only a few inches tall. This decreases your speed to 15 and causes you to deal half damage with any of your attacks, but gives you hide in plain sight among toys. You can grow back to your usual size as a free action. | | Major Default | | | ----------------------------------------------------------- | ----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | | Amiibo (Companion) | With 8 hours of work and 100g worth of materials, you can create a toy/plushie etc. that mimics the appearance of you or another creature. If you make it out of another creature, that creature must perform a brief ritual requiring one minute of contact to enable its magical effects. This amiibo functions like an old school telephone, capable of sending and receiving calls, but is limited specifically between the amiibo and the creature that it was constructed to resemble. There are modes to the amiibo that make it silent, vibrate or make loud noise in order to indicate contact. Amiibos of bonded siblings do not cost companion slots. It's also no substitute for interacting with them in person. | | Minor Default | | | ----------------------------------------------------------- | ---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | | Toy Speech | While in miniature form, you gain the ability to speak to other inanimate toys. This can include things like plushies, action figures, dolls, rubber duckies and anything similar. They are generally only able to communicate things that they have seen or heard recently. | | Skills | Get a +2 bonus to one of the following: +Deception, +Disguise, +Stealth | Replacement Features | Major Replace | | | ----------------------------------------------------------- | ------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------ | | Magic Wisdom Ball | You gain an additional use of prayer. If you had none, gain one. The answer is revealed to you through a physical object inside of your body. Other people can use your prayer to ask questions. This functions similarly to a magic conch or magic 8 ball-esque item. | | Transformer (poly, true) | As part of your build you were designed to also be a tool or a more complex toy. Once you choose what form you can take, it cannot be changed without significant physical augmentation. As a standard action you can transform into or back from this alternate form. You function as a masterwork tool for any creature using you, giving them a +2 bonus, or +4 if you are trained in the skill (treated as assisting). You could also transform into other objects such as a pogo stick (increase jump height to movespeed) or a bike (increase speed to 50). While in this form, you can only perform your intended purpose, move or transform back. | | Paperback | Instead of a toy, you are a drawing brought to life. This reduces your weight to practically nothing and makes you two-dimensional, allowing you to squeeze into places with great ease. You can also Hide in Plain Sight, but only against one specific creature by turning sideways.You can even fold yourself into pieces in order to take shapes like paper airplanes to gain limited boons as a standard action. While in this form, you can only perform your intended purpose, move or transform back. | | Minor Replace | | | ----------------------------------------------------------- | ----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | | Wind Up | You have a mechanism that allows you to perform very high jumps by storing kinetic energy. "Winding up" requires 3 standard actions. When wound up, you can boost the next jump action that you take to allow you to jump as high and as far as you can move. | | Bobblehead | Your head is built to be much better at absorbing shock. Creatures need to roll 11+AC to crit you. | | Kung-Fu Grip | DR 10 vs Disarm | Lore | Numbers | Poplin are around 2'6, and weigh around 30 lbs Polpin do not age. Their life is inherently tied to another, and they live as long as their bonded sibling | | -------------------------------------------------------------------- | --------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | | Magic of Life | As animated toys, poplin cannot reproduce, nor can they be created consistently. Instead there is a mysterious force that animates the toys of children experiencing hardship. Wizards debate the origin, but the transformation is obviously magical, so its probably another powerful anonymous mage. Most non-scholars attribute their creation to Santa. Either way, these creatures come into being with the same mentality as the young children that create them: that of a curious ignorant creature with a generally positive outlook on the world, especially their bonded sibling, whom they immediately feel kinship towards. | | Appearance and Biology | Poplins often have strange appearances that are facsimiles of humanoid creatures. Common ones are plushies and action figures. As such they often have seems and stitches, very visible joints and shiny, smooth or fluffy surfaces. They are often able to move in unnatural ways, such as turning their limbs all the way around or turning inside out to change their appearance; toys are creative. Usually poplin are afforded a bit more mobility than most other toys and plushies, with bending fingers and flapping lips to allow for fine manipulation and speech.Poplin generally stay about the same in appearance as they age from when they are magically willed into existence unless they go out of their way to replace parts of themselves. During old age, they start to literally fall apart as their sibling deteriorates. Their colors fade, seams pop and joints creak. | | Sibling Squabbles | The way that poplin view their bonded sibling is just that, as though they were siblings. There generally a great degree closeness between a poplin and their sibling. They often play together, help each other out, fight over who gets the last cookie etc.Childhood is usually the easy part, as the poplins dependence on their sibling doesn't matter as much for carefree children with a lot of free time. Poplin often find it difficult to get out of the naïve mindset of a child. So once their sibling becomes an adult, they can often become less important. Suddenly all of those sleepovers and woodland adventures are replaced by the reality and responsibility of adulthood. Poplin can find it difficult coming to terms with living apart from their bonded sibling, often being seen as just the toys they resemble. Nothing is more crushing than hearing something like "Oh that's just a toy I used to play with as a kid" from their sibling. Those that adjust well settle for frequent family gatherings, settling as coworkers or adventurers. Poplin separated from their sibling, young or old, are incomplete. They are missing an important part of themselves. Parents who take the poplin away or siblings that distance themselves in adulthood put themselves in danger, for there are few fixations stronger than that of a disgruntled poplin separated from their sibling. Poplin separated from their bonded siblings invariably become menaces to the people or society they see as the cause of their torment. |

entries.title: Puppest
entries.content: Legally distinct fiendish furry. | Language | Infernal | | -------- | ----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | | Size | Medium | | Type | Humanoid, Beast, Fiend | | Ignite | You have resistance to fire. When you take fire damage, you gain Fast Healing 2 for 3 rounds. | | Major Default | | | ----------------------------------------------------------- | ------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | | Muncher | You gain a bite and claw Natural Attack. They deal d8 pierce and slash damage respectively. If your hands are empty, your claw damage increases to 2d6.You can use them to make a MultiattackMake a simple claw and bite attack. | | Minor Default | | | ----------------------------------------------------------- | ------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | | Skill | Get a +2 bonus to one of the following: +Perception, +Planes, +Persuasion | | Charming | You can innately cast Charming Gaze 1/day. | Replacement Features | Major Replace | | | ----------------------------------------------------------- | ------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------ | | Speedy | Get a +10 bonus to Land Speed | | Scent | You have Scent (5 ft) | | Breath Weapon (Su) fire recharge | You can take a deep breath and exhale to release a cone of fire. Deals 2d8+LvL (9+lvl) damage in a 30 ft cone (reflex halves). Recharges after 1 minute. | | Minor Replace | | | ----------------------------------------------------------- | ---------------------------------------------------------- | | Natural Climber | You get a Climb speed equal to your land speed | | Fuzzy | While not wearing any armor, your AC is 11+DEX+LvL | Lore | Numbers | | | -------------------------------------------------------- | -------- | | Appearance | | | Biology | |

entries.title: Shades
entries.content: The definitely not Midna race | Language | Necril | | -------- | -------- | | Size | Small | | Type | Humanoid | | Major Default | | | ----------------------------------------------------------- | ------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | | Darkvision | You can see in the dark as well as you could in light, albeit in grayscale. | | Hairy (Concentration) | You have an additional hand that forms from your hair that can be used to hold objects, but not wield or activate them. You can also use this limb to perform combat maneuvers such as grapple. If you concentrate (free action), you can extend the reach of this hand to 10 ft, or 20 ft when in Dim Light or Darkness. This single limb is more than enough to hold your own body weight and lift you to high places. If your hair is ever cut, it grows back within a week. | | Minor Default | | | ----------------------------------------------------------- | ---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | | Body Art | Your skin consists of 2 primary colors, and you can shift them around to create varying patterns.You can inscribe faintly glowing lines along surfaces by tracing with your fingers in a specific color, chosen at character creation. You can automatically remove the lines with a wave of your hand. Dispel magic automatically gets rid of all writing. Some scrubbing also gets the job done. If you draw on your own skin, only you can remove them. | | Skill | Get a +2 bonus to the following: +Stealth, +Disguise, +Religion | Replacement Features | Major Replace | | | ----------------------------------------------------------- | ------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------ | | Shade Magic (Su) | You have a hover speed of 30 ft. | | Shadow Realm (Su) (Teleportation) | After channeling for 1 minute without taking damage, you can open up a 10x10 ft portal to your own pocket dimension for 1 round. The shadow realm is a black mostly featureless void with a tangible floor, but not much else. You can bring in any number of creatures, or any amount of objects so long as it can be brought in within 1 round. This dimension can only exist as long as you are inside of it and alive. If you leave, are banished or die, the contents of the dimension are immediately ejected to the same point you entered from.You need not see your destination to use this ability. | | Shadow Friend (Su) (Recharge) (Scrying) | You can animate your shadow and have it walk around. You can see and hear through your shadow. Your shadow can move anywhere as long as there is a continuous stream of dim light or darkness connecting you and your shadow. As a shadow, you must move along a continuous surface at all times. If 2 surfaces are not connected, you cannot move between them. You can easily move along walls or ceilings however. While animating your shadow, a tether connects you that is significantly darker than even complete darkness, visible to creatures with nightvision or Darkvision. If the shadow or the tether is attacked, or if the shadow/tether is exposed to bright light, shadow friend immediately ends. | | Insubstantial (Su) (mana burn) | As a FRA, you can Mana Burn 1 in order to pass through a surface no thicker than 1 ft. Once you appear, you are staggered for 1 round. By placing your hand on a wall, you can tell when you can and cannot use this ability. | | Minor Replace | | | ----------------------------------------------------------- | --- | | | | Lore | Numbers | Shades tend to be 3'6 tall and weigh 40 lbsThey reach adulthood by 12 and live to be about 60 | | -------------------------------------------------------------------- | -------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | | Appearance and Biology | Shades are short in stature with mostly smooth skin that is a mixture of a dull dark color, usually black, and some other low saturation brighter color, usually blue. These two colors can form unique patterns on the skin, much like how animal fur patterns exist. Their eyes and hair tend to be more vibrant colors, contrasting their dull skin. They grow hair on their heads much faster than other races, forcing them to get frequent haircuts. Thanks to their magical abilities, their hair can appear even longer than it is. They also inscribe tattoos on their skin for added customization. Shades possess the usual sex organs present in most humanoids, but their innate magical abilities gives them the ability to hide most features on their skin to give themselves "barbie bod," making their body smooth and featureless. As such, some opt to not even wear clothing. Most do wear clothing, and they prefer things that show off their skin and are frilly like dresses and veils. | | Shadow Cursed | There exists a land in Nack considered uninhabitable by most known as the Shadow-Cursed Lands. These islands are plagued with eternal darkness as a result of a curse placed upon them by the vampiric overlords that desired more suitable living conditions. As such, all kinds of undead spawn inside of this area. The souls of heroes that tried to cleanse this land serve as ammunition for all of the necromantic research that happens within.Many years ago, an enclave of goblins was forced to flee by sea and was chased to one of these shadow-cursed islands. The goblins thought they had lost their pursuers, but the adventurers were merely unwilling to follow, having heard the rumors of the treacherous environments that often lie within. The adventurers claimed their gold for a job well done, and the goblins were forgotten. Snap back to the present day where the shadow-cursed islands have become a much greater focus of adventuring parties and that same island was explored. Much to their surprise, the adventurers found creatures settling in these lands that weren't interested in stealing their souls. More and more sightings of this magical small race with strange dexterous hair were noted all around the world. As scholars began compiling data about these Shades as they called themselves, they were able to trace their history back to that group of goblins all those years ago. Shades have since become the envoys between the shadow-cursed isles and the rest of the world, working as guides and teachers. | | Twilight Princess | Aside from the physical changes the goblins experienced, they also went through many cultural shifts. They learned a new language that they used to read records etched into the abandoned stony ruins they initially called home and to communicate with the locals. Through this learning, one of their leaders was able to befriend a local witch, and received some magical boons in return. Over time the relationship between the goblins and this witch deepened. The goblins would happily attend to her needs, acquiring rare materials, scouting out new islands to set up bases and dispatching pests. Soon enough she was referred to as the twilight princess and highly revered by all.After generations of goblins living in the shadow cursed lands and receiving these magical boons, they stopped being goblins after a certain point. Their skin and hair had completely changed colors to more closely resemble their princess. Their relationship with the twilight princess deepened their magical capabilities. The more treacherous environment and dependence on the princess had changed their culture. One day without warning, the twilight princess disappeared, leaving behind only an infant that closely resembled her and a letter to her people, who she referred to as her little shades. The name stuck and since then, the twilight princess has been treated as royalty. She continues to guide the shades through life in the shadow cursed lands. |

entries.title: Shroomkin
entries.content: The mushroom grower race. | Language | Sylvan | | ------------------------------------ | --------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | | Size | Medium | | Type | Humanoid, Plant | | Mycotoxic Flesh (Toxin) | You excrete a subtle poison from your pores. Prolonged contact (basically anything that isn't an attack) from something as innocuous as a handshake or hug is enough to proc the poison. When the poison takes hold, the creature stands there in a stupor, waiting for a command (Fortitude negates). This allows you to make a simple request that can be performed quickly and easily (kiss me, do a flip, open that door). Repeated exposure eventually makes creatures immune to your specific toxin.You can subtley touch someone to infect them with this toxin by using Thievery with a -5 penalty, as though you were snatching something affixed to them. | | Major Default | | | ----------------------------------------------------------- | ----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | | Palliative Caps (Healing) | You grow edible mushrooms using your body that can be consumed. Any ally other than you can pluck and consume one of these treats as a swift action if you are within their natural reach, healing 5 hp. They lose this healing property if not immediately eatenAny enemy can choose to eat one of these fruits instead of the usual effect of breaking your poise.You grow 3 fruits at the start of each day.These mushrooms deliver your mycotoxic flesh toxin. | | Minor Default | | | ----------------------------------------------------------- | --------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | | Rent Free | With a 1 minute ritual, you can infect a creature with spores to establish a mental connection with them. You can speak telepathically with this creature over any distance. You can only form a mental bond with 1 creature, booting the previous one if you bond anew. You automatically established this connection with your parents and children and they do not consume your slot. | | Skill | Get a +2 bonus to one of the following: Alchemy, +Persuasion, +Nature | Replacement Features | Major Replace | | | ----------------------------------------------------------- | ------------------------------------------------------------ | | Hardy | Gain resistance to poison, +Toxins and Venom. | | Minor Replace | | | ----------------------------------------------------------- | ------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | | Goomshroom Blessed | You can innately cast Bubble 1/day. | | One of Us | You can speak with plants/plant creatures as though you shared a common language. Unintelligent inanimate plants are limited to simple single word responses. You get a +2 bonus on skill checks related to plants when interacting with them. | | Mixed Blood | Choose the minor feature of another race (including alternate features). You count as both races for all purposes. | Lore | Numbers | Shroomkin are generally around 5' tall and weigh about 120 lbs They are adults by 10 and live to be about 50 | | ------------------------------------------------------------------------- | ------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------ | | Appearance | Shroomkin are humanoids whose most telling feature is their ability to grow mushrooms on their body. They feel a certain kinship towards the fungus and form a symbiotic relationship with it. The mushrooms are most common on their head, but they can grow all over: Its only a matter of where they groom that decides where they grow. In order to not be seen as monsters, most shroomkin tend to keep most of their body clean. Another telling feature is that shroomkin never have white sclera, always favoring darker and duller colors. Because shroomkin easily blend together with other races, they come in a wide variety of colors. The type of mushrooms they grow on their skin can also change very quickly due over a few generations. Certain mixes with shroomkin can create wildly different mushroom flavors from a hellion's spicy shroom to a water geniekin's minty shroom. | | Biology | Just like the mushroom itself, there's a lot more happening under the hood with shroomkin and their relationship to fungus. They constantly maintain a thin layer of mucous, making their skin always well-moisturized and slick to the touch. Shroomkin typically use this poisonous excretion as a method to mess with people, but can be used to remedy a great deal of situations, provided they can make contact to deliver it.An interesting quirk to shroomkin biology is that when they conceive, instead of gestating over a long period of time, they grow a special mushroom (cluster?) on the exterior of their body that their child(ren) will eventually sprout from over the short period of a few weeks. Shroomkin babies are very small when compared to races of similar sizes, but they are a lot more mobile, capable of crawling, clinging and even climbing after only a few hours. This accelerated physical development continues until they reach adulthood, but their mental lags behind a few years by comparison, leading most "adult" shroomkin to more closely resemble teenagers in how they act. Shroomkin males paired with other races produce offspring of the mother's race. | | Goomshroom | There is a specific type of fungus that only grows in areas blessed by the persistent drizzle of a goo dragon. With the shape and patterns of a mushroom and the colors and antennae of the goo dragon, the goomshroom is a treasured item that is always treated with great care. These sentient fungi live several times longer than the shroomkin and usually serve the role of a "village elder" who will pass down stories from generation to generation and be their voice of wisdom.Shroomkin will sometimes call upon the goo dragon during times of great crises. There is no guarantee they will come, but the good nature of the goo dragon means it will usually answer the call and come to their aid. Other groups of shroomkin have a very close relationships with the goo dragons and they serve as the settlement's protector. | | Blessing of the Shroomlette | The Shroomlette is the shroomkin god of happiness, rest and fertility. One of the few gods that exists in the realm of mortals. She resides in a garden filled with all kinds of oddities that rests on the clouds above a mushroom forest. There she resides, forever resting in a shiny cradle, waiting to be tucked in by a visitor. The Garden is commonly used as a place of mediation since all forms of violence are forbidden within the domain of the Shroomlette.Occasionally when couples are unable to conceive or sometimes for more mysterious reasons, people will receive a blessing from the Shroomlette: A stork will deliver a baby shroomkin girl directly to their abode with mysterious origins. Many see this as a blessing, but others who aren't happy about their newfound responsibility see it as a burden, and do everything they can to get rid of it. Those that don't treat the child with love and care incur the wrath of the Shroomlette. With poor enough treatment, she may even curse them to be an adult trapped inside of a babies body for a time to experience the some of the anguish they put the child through. |

entries.title: Afflictions
entries.content: Certain monster abilities, as well as the defeated condition, give permanent debuffs to characters. These debuffs represent physical/mental wear and tear beyond the catch-all of hp. The longer you are away from home, the more debuffs you are likely going to accrue. Debuffs universally MUST have some finite method of curing them. Many of them can be cured just by taking a day off: Spend a night at the local bar or brothel, spend time with family, visit a theme park etc. Others require something more specific. Debuffs come in tiers, the higher the tier, the greater the debuff. In the heat of combat, you do not gain the afflictions. Afflictions are accumulated after combat. Roll separately for each time you were defeated in combat. How do you decide what tier you get? Roll a luck check. If you roll below 1, you get a Tier 3 affliction. If you roll below 6, you get a Tier 2 affliction. Anything 6 or above and you get a Tier 1 affliction. Exceeding a 20 negates the affliction. The luck check gets a penalty to the roll equal to afflictions all added up. Tier 1 give a -1 penalty, Tier 2 gives -2 and T3 gives a -3 penalty.

entries.title: Tier 1
entries.content: Mental Scar | # | Name | Effect | Cure | | --- | ----------------- | --------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | ---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | | 1 | Phobia | While in combat with a specific creature type (usually the type that inflicted the affliction) you are shaken 2. | General | | 2 | Depressed | Your interests no longer interest you. You take a -2 penalty to skill checks. | General | | 3 | Burnout | Your mana burn is not cured by rest and mana burn 1 | General | | 4 | Insomnia | Your group collectively has a -2 penalty to initiative from your inattentiveness and how you need to be frequently woken up. | You must rest in a settlement of some kind in a comfortable setting designed for rest (hotel, inn etc). | | 5 | Dependence | Negative thoughts overcome you when alone and unloved. Every minute you are not near allies you are drained 5 (max 15, cannot kill you). | You must receive coddling from your mom for one night. If unavailable, you must hire a professional mom. | | 6 | Icky | You always feel gross. Take a shower! If one steps into this area, you must do everything in your power to get out of this area at the start of your turn. | General | | 8 | Anxious | You do not act in combat until all of your other allies have acted and you are forced to act. | General | Grievous Wounds | # | Name | Effect | Cure | | --- | --------------- | ------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | ---------------------------------------------------------------------- | | 1 | Sore Muscles | You are Fatigued | General | | 2 | Sprained Ankle | Your Land Speed is reduced by 5 | Only move around as though your Land Speed was 15 lower for 2 days | | 3 | Twisted Wrist | Any turn you use equipment with damaged hand you take 3 True Damage | Do not take true damage from this affliction for 2 days | | 4 | Dry Eyes | Frequent poking, debris or wind causes you to have Disadvantage on perception. | General | | 5 | Upset Stomach | Forced Movement causes you to become Sickened 2. | General | | 6 | Neck Pain | You can't twist your head around quickly, and 2 threatening foes flank you no matter their position | General | | 7 | Concussion | You forget everything that happened since you went to bed last night. | General, recall vaguely what happened. | | 8 | Festering Wound | You are Drained 5. | General |

entries.title: +Character Creation
entries.content: For when you want to let fate decide what kind of character you are playing.

entries.title: Abnormalities
entries.content: Another feature that can help you decide if your character is a weirdo or not. If you want a relatively average character, don’t roll this. Roll a d20, then roll a d3. The d20 represents what feature is in question and the d3 represents whether the abnormality tends towards being positive (1) unusual (2) or negative (3). You can roll a few times to decide a few unusual features, decide you want a specific number of each feature, then roll a few d20s, or not roll at all. Depending on the race, some of the features described as weird or negative may actually be positive or completely normal. Some features may be completely absent from your race. An easy way to create a new negative feature is to completely remove or damage certain features (one eye). Feel free to go down the whole list if you're a mad lad (though the ladies might not appreciate some of the hair options) D20 RollBody PartEffect1EyesPositive: Your eyes have a very vibrant and pronounced color, or a rare color (including scaleras)Strange: You have heterochromia, their color changes based on your mood or they are wide openNegative: You have a lazy eye, or one of your eyes is much smaller/closed more2NosePositive: Your nose is small, and placed appropriately on the faceStrange: Instead of a nose you have nostrils, a snout or some other method of olfactionNegative: You have an enormous, bulbous or hairy nose, or perhaps it points upwards3MouthPositive: You have luscious/colorful lips or a sparkling white smileStrange: Your teeth more closely resemble that of a predator, your tongue is exceptionally longNegative: You have large buck teeth, you're a mouth breather or you permanently frown4HairPositive: You hair color is vibrant, or it is particularly soft to the touchStrange: You have an unnatural hair color, it grows obnoxiously long and fast, or in strange pattersNegative: You are balding, greasy, or your hair is very knotty and unwieldy5VoicePositive: You have a golden voice, an exotic accent or an incredibly wide rangeStrange: You're monotone, your voice echoes, or changes when extremely angryNegative: You frequently have voice cracks, always sound like you're sick or have an impediment6Body ShapePositive: You are toned/muscular or thin, with curves in all the right placesStrange: You have exaggerated body proportions, making you very top heavy or bottom heavyNegative: You have a beer belly, are dangerously thin or have asymmetry in limb size7EarsPositive: Your ears end in a point, or have some other appealing shapeStrange: Your ears fold over onto themselves, or droop slightlyNegative: Large ears, chipped ears 8Sex CharacteristicsPositive: Ideal or exaggerated proportions, bigger and better, pleasant shapesStrange: You borrow characteristics from other races or produce large quantities of fluidsNegative: PP smol, flat as a board, low libido or low/no functionality9SkinPositive: Smooth and without blemishes, with maybe some well placed freckles or dimplesStrange: You are an uncommon color, have patterns, or you have regions with scales/feathersNegative: Vitiligo, extreme paleness, high visibility veins or frequent moles10TailPositive: Ideal size for maintaining balance and appearance, a charming shape at the tipStrange: Absurd length, have one when you typically shouldn'tNegative: Misplaced base of tail, weird angle, asymmetrical fur or scales, cut short or vestigial11FacePositive: You have good symmetry, a strong jaw, ideal chin, round cheeksStrange: You have coloration around your eyelidsNegative: You have asymmetry, double chin, massive forehead or a Neanderthal brow12ScarsPositive: None, or only a handful that don’t obscure important regions of the body/faceStrange: Your scars form shapes and patterns, turning into an art formNegative: Numerous large scars with unfortunate placements on the face or intimate regions13NailsPositive: Your nails always seem to be the correct length, polishedStrange: Your nails more closely resemble claws or are retractableNegative: Your nails are thick, discolored or unusually long14HandsPositive: Thin lengthy fingers with few blemishes or wrinklesStrange: Paws, additional opposable fingers, spiky knucklesNegative: Knobby fingers, a missing or bonus digit, thick veiny hands15HornsPositive: You have symmetrical horns placed at the top of the foreheadStrange: Your horns mimic other animals, like a moose or ram, you have multiple pairs or just oneNegative: Your horns are asymmetrical, cracked or broken16Body HairPositive: Completely hairless or ideal placementStrange: Your body hair makes you appear more like a specific type of animalNegative: Patchy or thin, or a thick coat completely covering most of your body17LimbsPositive: You have long legs, and limbs that transition well between body partsStrange: Your limbs are thicker by the forearms than biceps, or calves rather than thighsNegative: You have short stubby legs, or long arms that approach reach your knees18Facial HairPositive: You can grow nice sideburns, beard, mustache or all the aboveStrange: You can grow very long facial hair (Chinese master, wizard beard), even including eyebrowsNegative: Your facial hair is sporadic and insubstantial, you have a neckbeard19OdorPositive: You always seem to smell pleasant, like flowers or fruitStrange: Your natural aroma is something very specific, but not good or bad, like carrots or grassNegative: You're extra greasy and sweaty or you frequently belch and flatulate20Bonus SpacePositive: Your eyelashes are shiny and sparkly when you blink, your hair naturally flows backwards as though the wind were blowing it back, your face always remains sparkling clean, even when the rest of your body is dirtiedStrange: You can change the color of your skin into any primary or secondary color, your breath is always cold, you have body/face tattoos, your bones protrude from your body in places, you naturally growl when upset or angry, you have dwarfismNegative: You are a hunchback, you have an unusually bulbous head that grows larger at the top, your neck is incredibly wide, even wider than your head, you have an underbite,

entries.title: Age
entries.content: Roll a d100 and add 20. The result you get is the percentage of the way through your life essentially. Multiply this value by the average lifespan of your chosen race. Example - Human with a roll of 75 70(.75)=52 years old The added 20 is to basically make sure you aren't a child.

entries.title: Height+Weight
entries.content: Roll a d10 and then flip a coin. The d10 represents the variance as a percentage and the coin flip decides up (heads) or down (tails). If you roll a 1 or 10, you can roll again. Example - Human are average 5'6 and weigh 150. I roll a 10 and get heads. 66\1.1=72 in or 6 ft, 150\1.1=165 lbs If you roll two 1s or 10s, you can choose to be ludicrously tall or smol. Some people do not have weight correlate exactly with height, so you can choose to roll weight separately if you're feeling like having a chance of being incredibly wide and short or tall and thin.

entries.title: Banes
entries.content: | # | Name | Effect | | --- | ------------------ | ---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | | 1 | Soft Spoken | Your voice is very quiet. Your natural speaking voice is a whisper and shouting to you is speaking normally to most others. | | 2 | Thin | Decrease your max +HP by 3 | | 3 | Wanted/Indebted | Someone out there has a bounty on your head. Expect there to be an organization or group (of a similar level) trying to bring you to justice, or collect the money they are owed (10,000g). | | 4 | Extra Screening | Guards of all creeds will stop you without fail to inspect you for anything suspicious. | | 5 | WE'RE GONNA DIE | You are Shaken 1 while below half hp. | | 6 | LOW HP | Whenever a creature lives with | | 7 | Mana Burnt | Your mana pool decreases by 1 | | 8 | Genetic Failure | You only get 1 minor trait from your race. | | 9 | Allergies | At the start of each day roll a DC 3 luck check. Failure means your allergies are acting up and you are sickened 1 for the day. This sickened is considered Permanent Status. | | 10 | No Ragrets | You have a silly, unsavory or inappropriate tattoo that you got when you were younger in a very obvious spot, like the neck, face, or covering a large portion of your arms/legs/back/chest. | | 11 | Addiction | You have cravings. Alcohol, pancakes, picture of colorful birds. You must have it. You must spend 100 gold on these things each week. You can preemptively spend money to get you through the future.If you ever can't satisfy your addiction, you are fatigued until you do. This fatigue is considered permanent status. | | 12 | Bad Luck Brian | You must look like a well-renowned criminal, or share the name of some ancient demon. For whatever reason, commoners don't seem to trust you. | | 13 | Tourretes | Whenever you fail a skill check, you spout out a curse word or phrase, leaning towards a specific one more often than not. | | 14 | Stalker | There is an unusual person or creature that follows you wherever you go. They try to evade you if pursued. | | 15 | Excommunicated | Choose one T3 or T4 god (see the Tiering System). The followers of this god hate you and hunt you down to earn GBP. | | 16 | Wound's Teach | Subtract 1 from all sources of healing. | | 17 | Momma's Boy | You live with your mom.
