You need to get permission from them before you leave for anything that you aren't home in time for dinner, or past your bedtime.If you travel frequently, mom's coming with. | | 18 | Unfortunate Events | Get a -2 penalty to serendipity checks. | | 19 | Chadington | Any love interests you form will always have competition from someone far more intelligent, handsome, and successful than yourself, known to you as soon as you make a move. | | 20 | Attached | You have a special item (such as from your childhood) that you hold close to your heart. While it is not on your person, you are shaken 1. if it is ever lost or destroyed, you are shaken 1 for 1 week before you get attached to a new object.This shaken is considered a permanent status. |

entries.title: Boons
entries.content: For every point of luck you possess, roll on this chart. Permanent increases to luck also include a bonus boon. You can roll multiple of the same result (for most results), but you can reroll if you aren't feeling like +15 hp is your thing. | # | Name | Effect | | --- | ------------------------------- | ---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | | 1 | Mysterious Strange Recharge | You can shout to the heavens for a savior, and a creature from theHumanoids section of the bestiary of your LvL or lower comes to your aid. They help you escape danger, or otherwise help you solve a problem. Once you do this, you cannot do it again for once every 3 months. | | 2 | Wide | Your max +HP increases by 5. | | 3 | Lottery | You get an additional 3k starting gold. You also own Property. An luxurious home in an exotic paradise. | | 4 | Thane Privelege | If you are harassed by guards or similar types of authorities for any reason, you can announce some form of special title that you received from royalty, dissipating the situation and immediately gaining forgiveness. Once you do this, you cannot do it again in the same settlement.You do not have to decide where this works before hand. Make up whatever works on the spot. | | 5 | Natural Talent | You gain 2 +Cantrips as innate techniques | | 6 | Eager to Serve | You have a follower that is a non-combatant. They are an apprentice, a big fan or are indebted to you. They will aid you in any way they can. (use commoner stats). | | 7 | Lucky 7s | You treat rolls of 7 as 17 | | 8 | Dicey | Gain a luck dice, usable 1/day. | | 9 | Suffused | Your Mana pool increases by 1. | | 10 | Copycat | The first time each day an enemy casts a spell that affects you, you get a scroll in your pocket of that spell that disappears by the end of the day. | | 11 | Second Chance Healing | Whenever you die, don't. You instantly become Drained for half your max hp. Heal to your new max hp. | | 12 | Pandora's Box (Recharge) | You start play with an ornate box. Once per day when you open the box, it provides you with an item to aid you in your current struggle (decided by DM). Items are typically cheap. | | 13 | Party Animal | You can innately cast a level 1 or 2 Nack spell of your choosing. | | 14 | Superior Genetics | Choose another minor feature from your race | | 15 | Secret Technique | Choose a level 1 technique to gain as an innate technique. | | 16 | Family Heirloom | You start play with Gear Upgrades worth up to 6k in total. | | 17 | Genetic Abomination | Get a minor race trait from another race. You have a part of you that looks mismatched from this race. | | 18 | That Would Never Happen | Get a +2 bonus to serendipity checks. | | 19 | Get Lucky | You have advantage on any check that involves romance or seduction. | | 20 | Serene Grace (recharge) | 1/day you can spend an immediate action to add LUK to the DC of one of your abilities. |

entries.title: Resurrection Chart
entries.content: When a character is resurrected, roll a d4. | 1 | Bad | | --- | ------- | | 2-3 | Neutral | | 4 | Good | After that roll your respective chart. Characters with positive LUK can decide to Reroll their result. Characters with negative luck roll twice and have the GM decide what effect happens. Bad stuff | Dice Roll | Result | | --------- | ----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | | 1 | Mini Me: A childlike mini version of yourself is resurrected alongside you. Mini Me is a simple creature that is happy with life as long as it gets to travel with you. If Mini Me is separated from you for 3 days, your max hp decreases by 5 until you reunite. If separated for 3 months, it dies and this max hp decrease becomes permanent. | | 2 | All you felt in death was drowning. Now you are deathly afraid of water. If forced to swim, automatically start drowning in water, fail all swim checks. | | 3 | Extreme Fear (DC 10 luck check or be frightened, roll until succeed per combat) of creature/creature type that killed you. | | 4 | You are comatose for 1 week. Your body cannot move but you can still sense everything happening around you. | | 5 | Resurrection functions as reincarnate | | 6 | An enemy that you killed at some point is resurrected at the same time as you. They want revenge, their new purpose is to destroy you at all cost. | | 7 | Fear of Mortality: You start combat shaken 1 until you or your allies defeat a foe. | | 8 | You physically age 3d12 years. | | 9 | Celestials sense great evil within you. Take a –4 penalty to CHA checks withCelestials. | | 10 | You cannot lie. Only through omission can you not speak the truth. | | 11 | Eternal Rest: You take a -4 penalty to saving throws against effects that Asleep effects. | | 12 | During your short eternity you were very "close" with a creature from the beyond.If male, a child will find you claiming to be your spawn. If female, a surprise is growing within you. | | 13 | Big Hands: Your hands increase in size by 50%. | | 14 | Biggest Fan: A particularly sneaky and intelligent undead creature will follow you around constantly and try to hang out with you. If killed, it reforms in the nearest graveyard and continues its quest to hang out. | Neutral | Dice Roll | Result | | --------- | ---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | | 1 | A single horn grows from the center of your forehead. If you already had one, you grow another of to the side. | | 2 | You suddenly speak a random language. You also lose one of your old languages (other than common). | | 3 | Whenever you attack, you attack nonlethally. Cannot kill another creature. | | 4 | You awaken with a brand on your ass. Asking the right people will reveal who did it (a strange imp?). Speaking with them may reveal a way to remove it. | | 5 | Another soul latched onto you. You can communicate with it if you concentrate. Sometimes pops into your thoughts. (luck check to see how good/helpful this soul is, higher is better) | | 6 | Grow a(n additional) tail. | | 7 | You look like a rotting corpse in your reflection. | | 8 | You now constantly know the time of day no matter the condition. Your sense of time is impeccable. | | 9 | You grow antennae with a keen sense of touch. | | 10 | Your senses have become a bit jumbled. You now smell with your ears, hear with your eyes, see with your tongue, and can taste and touch with your entire body. | | 11 | Swap Genders | | 12 | The Fates had determined you were to be reincarnated, but the ritual has cheated them. You are haunted by dreams of a young couple mourning the loss of the newborn you were to be. Every month or so, you will have dreams and nightmares as the childless couple, your parents, try and fail to grow their family. Over the years they grow bitter, bicker with one another, and live miserable lives.Only finding them and apologizing will make the dreams stop. | | 13 | Whatever killed you leaves a horrific scar that can never be removed (savage burns, large gashes etc). | | 14 | Your eyes turn a solid color (ROYGBIV+black+white+gray) | | 15 | Mood Eyes: (Red=mad, blue=sad, pink=affection, orange=happy, yellow=surprise, Violet=disgust, black=afraid). | | 16 | You can read written text via touch. | | 17 | A tattoo of a celestial marks the entirety of your back. (A DC 20 +Planes check reveals its effect). The next time one of your close allies or friends dies near you, you immediately die and their life is restored. | | 18 | Share Memory: You gain a significant memory of the creature that killed you or a significant creature that contributed to your death. | | 19 | Green Thumb: All plants you tend to manage to pull through somehow. Plants like you. | | 20 | Blessing(?) of Frogg Saran: 1/hour you can fire sliced cheese from your hand as a ranged attack. It harmlessly slaps against your opponent, but it might confuse them, make them paranoid, make them laugh or make them realize the power you hold. | | 21 | You come back slightly resembling another creature type. (1-Aberration, 2-Fiend, 3-Celestial, 4-Fey, 5-Construct, 6-Animal, 7-Plant, 8-Dragon). There is no particular mechanical change with this, but people might treat you differently due to your appearance change. | | 22 | The sound of a truly epic (fiendish/celestial) performance accompanies the resurrection. | | 23 | You are haunted by the ghost of Big Smoke. He doesn’t actually exist for anyone other than you. He exclusively calls you CJ, asks you about food and keeps talking about a train. | | 24 | Stone Faced: Almost all facial gestures you make are very subtle compared to what they used to be. You get a +4 bonus to +Deception checks but suffer a –4 penalty to all other CHA skill checks. | | 25 | Stone Face: Your face is made out of stone, almost as though your face is just a mask that is impossible to take off. | | 26 | Can I Keep it?: A small undead animal is bound to your soul and relies on you to exist. it stays within 5 feet of you and acts as you wish. If it moves beyond this range it teleports back to you. | | 27 | There is always a frog somewhere in your field of vision. When you look at it, it changes position to be at the edge again. Everybody else can interact with this frog normally. | | 28 | Love at First Sight: You now have a crush on someone present during the resurrection (decide randomly). | | 29 | Anchor Arms: The muscles in your arms appear to grow incredibly large, but it does not affect your strength. If you already had high strength, they get even bigger. If you had low strength, you look ridiculous. | | 30 | Floofy: Your hair grows incredibly long and other parts of your body get exceptionally fluffy. Floofyness +2 | | 31 | Censorship: When naked, natural elements of the terrain move to maintain modesty. | Good Stuff | Dice Roll | Result | | --------- | ---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | | 1 | You understand, write and speak all Languages | | 2 | There is an incomplete map tattooed to your back. It's enough to get the rough idea of where it may be leading, but just barely. | | 3 | A friend or some other helpful person gets resurrected at the same time as you. They appear lying next to you. They are eternally bound to you, immortal as long as you live. | | 4 | Second Sight: You grow a perpendicular third eye on your forehead. You can use this eye to cast detect thoughts 1/day. | | 5 | Spirit Limbs: You can interact with Incorporeal creatures as though they were corporeal | | 6 | Your spirit is forever bound to a small item near the location of your resurrection. Should you ever get 30ft away from this item, you are defeated until it Is returned to you. You cannot die while this item is within 30 feet of you. | | 7 | | | 8 | Dragon Sight: The area around your eyes is coated in scales and your eye color changes to match. You gain Darkvision. | | 9 | You lived as an animal during your time of death. You now feel a kinship towards animals. You have Speak with Animals prepared. | | 10 | Hellion say "you smell like home." +4 to CHA checks with hellion and fiends. | | 11 | You can grow and shed your hair into any shape/length you please at will. | | 12 | You mysteriously gain a piece of knowledge. You can call upon this knowledge whenever you make an INT-based check. You automatically succeed. When you use this, the knowledge fades and you can't use feature again. | | 13 | You gain the ability to play instruments out of thin air. Like an air guitar, but with all instruments. You get a +4 bonus to all instrument-based +Profession checks. | | 14 | You no longer need to sleep and can enter the dreams of adjacent sleeping creatures. Though they are the masters of their own world and can force you to leave. | | 15 | You are now incredibly knowledgeable on the creature that killed you, as though you succeeded on a +Monster Lore check to identify all its features. | | 16 | Blood Sight: By sacrificing 15 ft of movement, you can focus on a visible creature. This allows you see the rough percentage of +HP they are missing. | | 17 | You awaken from the resurrection with a non magical silver ring clasped in your hands. It feels wrong to put it on anybody's hand who you don’t truly love. Once put on, GM decides if the love is true. If it isn't, the ring fades away. If it is, you gain a Succ bond with them where both of you gain its benefits. | | 18 | You can use Telepathy to communicate with creatures you are in direct contact with. | | 19 | Whenever you bleed, you bleed water. Creatures like vampires will be very disappointed. Effects that require blood don’t function properly. You can still take bleed damage, however. | | 20 | Read the Room: You can tell the attitudes of NPCs towards you just by looking at them (good, bad, neutral). | | 21 | Blessing of Frogg Saran: You gain the ability to transform heathens into sweet rolls. This functions like the besmolder's candy ray. This ability can only turn creatures into sweet rolls. 1/day. | | 22 | You more closely resemble a (1-Hellion, 2-Exalted, 3-Fallen, 4-Geniekin). You don't gain their racial features, but you can take their racial feats. | | 23 | You no longer age and will essentially live until you are killed. | | 24 | Be Right Back: As an immediate action you can choose to disappear from existence until the end of your next turn. 1/day. | | 25 | Blessing of NJU: Gain a +8 bonus to an +Athletics check 1/day. Become Fatigued for 1 minute after. | | 26 | You no longer dream when you sleep. When you do sleep, your spirit separates from your body and you are aware of your surroundings as you would be when you were awake. Only by taking damage or a creature spending a standard action to shake you can you wake up. | | 27 | Cool hat: You acquired an exceptionally fancy hat worth 1k gold. It gives you an air of nobility when wearing it. | | 28 | Mega Cookie: You are blessed by the bakers from the great beyond. This cookie can resurrect a creature that has been dead for less than 1 minute. |

entries.title: Gambit
entries.content: Using a rule system inherently poses limitations on the actions that a player can take. The idea of gambits is to let players take their cool ideas and let them play out despite the games mechanics. If your player wants to do something flashy and dramatic that isn't necessarily covered by the rules (or only vaguely), then it counts as a gambit. The intention behind gambits is to create situations that have incredibly high risk and high reward. Before performing a gambit, the player tosses out a few ideas on how they plan to perform the action and the GM makes suggestions and/or adjustments based on circumstance. The GM should make it clear to the player what their roundabout odds are for completing a given gambit. Benefits for performing a gambit can include things like not taking fall damage with a plunging attack, gaining extra movement by swinging on things through danger or surviving otherwise deadly circumstances. Drawbacks for failing when attempting a gambit can include things like being CC'd, losing poise, losing hp or having their actions interrupted and made impossible. In extreme cases if players do something very silly, like jumping up cliffs or battling forces of nature, they may accumulate afflictions, or other long lasting debuffs.

entries.title: Group Skill Checks
entries.content: In certain circumstances, creatures may want to work together perform the same task. If it is something that can tangibly benefit from group effort (pushing over a large boulder, creating a spell etc.), use the bonus of the creature with the highest modifier, then get +1 for each assisting creature and +2 for each assistant creature that is proficient in the task at hand. In other circumstances, you may want to use skills as a group when you need to, even if it less than optimal, such as group stealth with a clumsy heavy armor user. In such scenarios, you use the bonus of the weakest link as though everybody were assisting them.
entries.path: Skills/General Thoughts/Other Skill Rules/Group Skill Checks.md

entries.title: Taking 10
entries.content: When not under duress, you can take 10 on skill checks, automatically treating your dice rolls as though you had rolled a 10. You normally cannot take 10 on opposed skill checks. Good for profession and general crafting.

entries.title: Taking 20
entries.content: When not under duress, and with a lot of time on your hands, you can automatically achieve the result of rolling a 20. It takes 20 times as long as the task would normally take. It is also assumed that during this time, you fail several times, making it ill-suited for a situations where there are consequences on a fail (such as smashing a wall or deactivating an explosive).

entries.title: General Thoughts
entries.content: All creatures are proficient in perception, unless they are mindless Skill rolls are how players interact with the world without hitting it with their sword. Even if certain actions have a skill associated with them, you don't necessarily need to roll a skill check (no need to roll deception if they already believe you, no need to roll athletics in still water, etc). When one is required, it is prompted by the DM.  D20 can be very swingy, creating dramatically unexpected results. That is its strong suite. As such using a skill roll as a barrier for entry can create all or nothing scenarios. There's nothing wrong with saying "the lock is too complex for you to pick," but you can't be upset when immediately after the martials of the party start smashing into the door. Just like with attack rolls and saving throws, there should be lesser versions of success. If you barely fail the DC to scale a wall, it lowers your speed instead of falling. If you barely fail a stealth check while trailing someone, they can tell someone is in the bushes, but not who.  Skill bonuses by default only use the relevant ability modifier. Proficiency in a skill grants you a 2+(lvl) bonus to the skill. Certain aspects of a skill might only be available to characters that are proficient in the skill and are denoted with a (P). The most advanced part of a skill require the Signature Skill feat and are denoted with a (SS). When you have multiple spells and/or items that give bonuses to skills, only take the highest bonus. Standard skill DCs are what should be used in the absence of a set or opposed DC. They are as follows… | Difficulty | DC | | ----------------- | -- | | Mundane | 0 | | Requires Focus | 5 | | Trained | 10 | | Difficult | 15 | | Very Difficult | 20 | | Masterful | 25 | | Nearly Impossible | 30 | | Ascended | 40 | Some skills are opposed to other skills, while other skills will include examples comparable this chart.

entries.title: Skill Check
entries.content: Umbrella term that includes all skills. Some features give bonuses to skill checks in general rather than a specific skill.

entries.title: Skill Proficiency
entries.content: Skill proficiencies are represented by a numerical value, typically based on class+INT. When you are proficient in a skill, you add 2+LvL to all skill checks related to this skill. You also get any feature underneath the skill with a label of (P).

entries.title: +Acrobatics
entries.content: Used for making risky jumps, traversing difficult terrain, and avoiding AoOs. Most actions are made as part of movement. Uses DEX.

entries.title: Avoid AoO (P)
entries.content: Acrobatics is rolled against all threatening creatures’ reflex saves. The DC of their reflex save is equal to your acrobatics roll. If they succeed, your ,movement Provokes as normal.

entries.title: Balance
entries.content: Acrobatics can be used to cross slippery surfaces coated in things like ice or oil. The default DC is 10, but changes to a creature's DC if it originates from an ability. Failing the check gives you the knocked down condition until the start of your next turn. It can also be used to traverse or stand on incredibly small and unstable space such as a cliff side or a fallen tree branch.

entries.title: Example DCs
entries.content: DifficultyDCExamplesMundane0WalkingRequires Focus5Walk across a rickety bridgeTrained10Walk across a fallen log to a large tree across a riverDifficult15Slip along the edge of a cliff where your feet barely fitSimple gymnastics, like doing a flip or a handstandVery Difficult20Slip along the edge of a cliff where your feet don't fitGymnastics like walking while hand standing or jumping/balancing on small surfacesMasterful25Walk across a tightropeComplex gymnastics like flipping off of bars/branches and then standing on them.Nearly Impossible30Fencing on a tightropeAscended40Ride a unicycle across a rope while hand standing on the pedals Common modifiers to make the task more difficult can include… - Things such as the surfaces you interact with are moving or are wet. - Surfaces you interact with are patchy or broken. Common modifiers to make the task less difficult can include - Guard rails (natural or manufactured) - Appropriate equipment, such as a long object for balance

entries.title: Excuse Me (SS)
entries.content: You can move through creatures' spaces without provoking from them, but you cannot end your turn inside of a creature's space. Strafing no longer provokes AoOs.

entries.title: Hopping (SS)
entries.content: You can Jump as a free action once each turn.

entries.title: Improved Jump (P)
entries.content: Your default jump distance is increased by 5 ft per point in DEX or STR.

entries.title: Leap of Faith
entries.content: A special type of jump action. Roll acrobatics to determine your jump distance/height to see if you can jump farther than your default jump distance. The distance you jump is equal to your acrobatics roll in feet, or half that in height. This still requires a swift action to perform.

entries.title: Soften Fall (SS)
entries.content: When you take the jump action, you reduce falling damage by your acrobatics roll.

entries.title: Wall Kick (P)
entries.content: As long as there are 2 solid surfaces within 5 feet of each other, you can continually hop between them to ascend, requiring no action. Only the upward distance you cover counts against your movement.

entries.title: +Athletics
entries.content: Used for climbing and swimming, as well as moving heavy objects and breaking things. Uses str.

entries.title: Aquatic Combatant (P)
entries.content: With athletics proficiency, you take no penalty to Underwater Combat with melee attacks.

entries.title: Breaking Objects
entries.content: Should we push over the tree or just cut it down? Players may find themselves in situations where it is unclear whether it would be more effective to just hack at their problem with their swords or try to break it open with brute strength. A lot of that depends on what it is you're trying to break and what it is you're using to break it. As a general rule, if the substance that you're using is more tough, and could reasonably damage the object you'd like to destroy (hammers aren't good at cutting ropes for instance) then you can damage/break the object just by swinging at it. Some consideration should still be taken about the size and scale of the object. Adamantine might cut through stone like butter, but a 3-ft thick stick of butter would still take quite a bit of effort to cut through. Use the following chart to determine roundabout how "tough" something is. | Fragile | Paper, Glass, Cloth | | ------------ | ----------------------------------- | | Average | Wood, Plastic, Leather | | Solid | Stone, Common Metals, Force Effects | | Adamantine | Rare Metals, Dragon Scales | | Transmundane | Lightsaber, DM Magic |

entries.title: Breakout
entries.content: There are a large number of scenarios where a character is pinned down by something other than another creature: Getting tangled in a web, caught in a vortex or buried under rock. In such scenario you can use breakout to escape such conditions. The action used for breakout varies based on the extremity of the effect. The DC to breakout of an effect that another creature created via a spell or ability is their DC

entries.title: Brute Force
entries.content: For when there are no lockpicks or beasts of burden, you have elbow grease. The DC to break an object, such as a cage or door, is based on its material and reinforcements. You can also use athletics to represent bursts of adrenaline that allow you to surpass your normal carry weight to push over statues and punch boulders into lava.

entries.title: Catch Fall (P)
entries.content: As an immediate action, you can attempt to grab onto something to halt your falling, or to prevent an ally within reach from falling. The default DC to do this is 20. Creatures falling farther than 30 feet increase this DC even higher.

entries.title: Climbing
entries.content: Assuming ideal climbing conditions, no check is required, such as when climbing an average tree. Spaces you climb through are considered difficult terrain. With athletics proficiency, you climb at full speed.

entries.title: Example DCs
entries.content: | Difficulty | DC | Example | | -------------- | --- | ----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | | Mundane | 0 | Climb a surface with plenty of footholds (tree)Swim in still waterTear paper or shatter glass | | Requires Focus | 5 | Swim in deep water that is moving | | Trained | 10 | Climb a surface with few footholds, like the side of a houseBreak small/flimsy wooden structures, like branches or chairs | | Difficult | 15 | Swim in tumultuous waters | | Very Difficult | 20 | Climb at Break a sturdy wooden object, like a table | | Masterful | 25 | Match the speed of most rivers, escape whirlpools and currentsBreak solid stone objects, like boulders | | Nearly | 30 | Break solid metal objects, like manacles | | Ascended | 40 | Climb a smooth wall or ceilingSwim up a waterfallBreak adamantine objects or objects made of magic force | Common modifiers to make the task more difficult can include… - The surfaces you are interacting with are wet or moving. - You are swimming in substances other than water that are more grimy or viscous\ - Extra reinforcements to enhance the structural soundness and prevent breakage Common modifiers to make the task less difficult can include... - You are climbing at a >90 degree angle, or the surface was designed to be climbed\ - Flotation devices (driftwood, floaties) - Age or fundamental flaws/weaknesses in the structure How much of something you want to destroy can also influence how difficult it is. If you want to tear a brick out of a wall, that would make breaking the stone easier. If you want to break down the entire wall, that is much more difficult.

entries.title: Power Lifter (SS)
entries.content: Double your carry weight. When you carry an ally, they are not made clumsy.

entries.title: Swimming
entries.content: Assuming ideal climbing conditions, no check is required, such as when swimming in still water. Spaces you swim through are considered difficult terrain. With athletics proficiency, you swim at full speed.

entries.title: Triathlete (SS)
entries.content: You gain an additional 20 speed each turn you can use to climb and swim. To swim in water it must be at least waist deep. To climb a surface it must be at least as tall as you are, able to withstand your weight and continuous.

entries.title: +Deception
entries.content: Simply put, lying. This skill is almost always opposed to +Insight. Uses CHA.

entries.title: Convey Secret Message (P)
entries.content: By speaking in roundabout terms and making subtle gestures, you can communicate a simple idea (we need to leave, time to strike) while in company that might scrutinize. The DC to convey this message is 15. The intended recipient automatically understands the message. For anybody else listening, its insight DC to read between the lines is (10+your deception bonus).

entries.title: Convince Truth
entries.content: Speak falsehoods to cover your ass or to soften the truth. Common modifiers to make the task more difficult can include… - The person you are deceiving witnessed events you are lying about firsthand - The claims you are making are incredibly outlandish or outright impossible (angels and demons are the same, the sky is green)\ - The person you are deceiving is knowledgeable on the subject matter. Common modifiers to make the task less difficult can include - The person you are deceiving is inattentive. They are sleepy, drunk or in a hurry - The person you are deceiving is a friend or close ally

entries.title: Create Diversion
entries.content: As a standard action, you can shout something at your enemies to briefly get them to look the other way so you can give them the slip. Out of all creatures currently attending you, roll deception against the creature with the highest +Insight. If you succeed, you are able to hide and people don't know where you went. This does not prevent people from being unable to find you again using perception.

entries.title: Enigma (SS)
entries.content: You have resistance against any effects that would read your thoughts or otherwise pry into your mind.

entries.title: Feint
entries.content: As a swift action, roll deception against a creature within reach. DC (10+target's LvL) or (10+target's +Insight bonus) whichever is higher. If successful, your opponent is DD until the end of your turn, or until after your first attack is resolved. This is to allow offensive (bigger attack, no parrying etc) and defensive (avoid AoO, deny retaliation strikes) uses for feint. Note - It is intended that both attacks from Dual Wielding benefit from feinting.

entries.title: Inception (SS)
entries.content: Convince a creature that one of your ideas is their own idea. By speaking with a creature over the course of a few minutes you can plant an idea in their mind that they then act on as if it was their own. You take a -10 penalty to your deception check vs their insight.

entries.title: Sucker Punch (P)
entries.content: If you are in some kind of standoff where you aren't quite fighting your enemy yet, you can use this skill as a replacement for stealth to surprise your enemy with a melee attack. The enemy must be aware of you in order sucker punch, otherwise it is stealth. Doesn't actually require unarmed a strike. Certain situations where you are not able to get close to your aggressors, such as having bows pointed at you on from a garrison above you, make sucker punching impossible.

entries.title: +Disguise
entries.content: Pretend to be someone you’re not. Rolled against perception. Uses cha.

entries.title: Costume Expert (SS)
entries.content: You no longer take a penalty to disguise when changing your race or gender. You can now use disguise to pretend to be a monstrous creature (skeleton, phoenix, giant). These disguises tend to be very bulky and impractical. You cannot do anything physically intensive while in such a disguise, such as +Acrobatics, +Athletics or combat without the disguise falling apart.

entries.title: Craft Disguise
entries.content: Spend 10 minutes perfecting your makeup, outfit and wig to appear as someone you're not. Disguise checks are usually unnecessary unless you fall under scrutiny, the person knows you/what you're pretending to be or you're trying to enter an exclusive club. Creatures get bonuses to their perception if they know you or what you're disguised as particularly well. You take a -2 penalty to disguise checks when you try to change your race and when you change your apparent gender. When you initiate a disguise, you tell your DM your bonus, they apply any appropriate modifiers and roll it in secret.

entries.title: Impressionist (P)
entries.content: Creatures proficient in the disguise skill have the ability to alter their voice significantly. If a creature can’t see you (or while you are disguised), you can pretend to sound like someone else whose voice you have heard, or something more exotic like an animal. If a creature sees you and the voice is a mismatch, then they will quickly see through obvious facades, but can potentially be used to misdirect. You typically only roll when the creature is familiar with who you mimic.

entries.title: Zero to Hero (SS)
entries.content: You can change your disguise into something completely different as a FRA. If someone was looking for you, they automatically get a check to see through your disguise if they see you again.

entries.title: +Insight
entries.content: Pick up on nonverbal cues and tone of voice to glean more information. Uses WIS.

entries.title: 5 Steps Ahead (SS)
entries.content: When a creature fails to feint you, they are DD to you until the end of your next turn.

entries.title: Detect Intrusive Presence (P)
entries.content: You can use insight to determine when a creature is under duress from a spell or some other kind of effect that has a lasting impact on their mind, such as if they are charmed or under the effects of Possess Body. You must actively search for this presence by speaking with a creature for 1 minute. Roll insight vs the DC of the effect. If the effect has no DC, assume 12+inflicting creature's level.

entries.title: Detect Truth
entries.content: Whenever a creature makes a statement, you can roll insight (opposed by +Deception) to see if what they say aligns with what they think. If you succeed, you determine that something is fishy. Prying further about the deception may cause the creature to reveal the truth or become hostile depending on context. If you roll this too often, your GM can silence you. No using insight multiple times on the same thing. Don’t be silly.

entries.title: Intrusive Thoughts (SS)
entries.content: By having a conversation with a creature for one minute, you can glean into their surface thoughts as you continue this conversation. Make an insight check with a -10 penalty, if you succeed you can read their surface thoughts for the remainder of the conversation.

entries.title: +Intimidation
entries.content: Be spooky and coerce creatures. Uses the higher value of either your STR, or your CHA.

entries.title: Big Shot (SS)
entries.content: Any minion creature automatically starts shaken 1 while in combat with you.

entries.title: Boo (SS)
entries.content: When you demoralize a Minion, they become Frightened. This condition lasts until they gain Total Cover from you for 1 round and take no action other than to escape.

entries.title: Coercion (P)
entries.content: By having a conversation with a creature that lasts at least one minute, or by defeating a creature in combat, you can shape how they respond to you or anybody asking about you in the future. The strength of this creature's conviction is dependent on your intimidating prowess. You can make the creature keep their mouth shut about something you did to them. Any checks to gather information from this creature about you or your party change to (10+intimidation bonus). You can greatly exaggerate what you did to further spread fear and grand tales of your strength. Any knowledge checks that creatures make on you when hearing about information that spreads from this creature changes to this DC it would be higher than the usual DC, or else they learn false info.

entries.title: Demoralize
entries.content: Tags: Fear As a standard action (or a swift action with proficiency), inflict shaken 1 on an enemy that can see or hear you within 30 feet. Must have line of sight. They roll a will save vs (10+your intimidation bonus). They are shaken as long as you are nearby, and for 1 minute after. You can demoralize a creature repeatedly to increase the penalty. If they pass their save, their shaken value decreases by 1.

entries.title: PTSD (P)
entries.content: You can permanently scar a creature so that your mere presence is enough to fill it with dread. Creatures you defeat in combat are automatically shaken 1 in your presence permanently. If you successfully demoralize a creature afflicted with PTSD, you can choose to increase their shaken penalty or make them frightened.

entries.title: Alchemy
entries.content: The blanket skill for most of fantasy (mad) science. Used for the creation of potions, poisons and other strange but useful concoctions. Identifies Oozes with +Monster Lore.

entries.title: Create Alchemical Item
entries.content: Alchemy can make all kinds of different things: common things include healing wounds, inflicting wounds, preventing sickness, adhesives, solvents, starting fires and temporary adaptations. Uncommon things include cloning and permanent transformations. All found under the Alchemical Items section.

entries.title: Identify Alchemical Item
entries.content: Creation DC With a quick wiff or taste, you can determine the effects of an alchemical substance.

entries.title: Reverse Engineer
entries.content: Creation DC+5 You can take an unfamiliar substance and learn what it's made out of with the intention of creating it again in the future. A success indicates that you managed to write down the necessary ingredients and processes to create the item in the future.

entries.title: Arcana
entries.content: The skill used whenever magic is involved. Common uses include figuring out what spell someone is casting, determining the magical properties of items and identifying the effects of persistent spells. Identifies Dragons with +Monster Lore.

entries.title: Identify Active Spell
entries.content: DC 10+spell lvl, free action 1/turn You examine the magical effect to determine what spell it is, and what the spell does.

entries.title: Identify Magic Item
entries.content: DC=Craft DC, +10 if cursed, standard action You learn all of the qualities of a magic item that you are adjacent to.

entries.title: Identify Spell Being Cast
entries.content: DC 15+spell lvl, no action You must be able to clearly see or hear the creature in order to identify a spell that they are casting. Useful for determining whether or not you want to dispel something, or figuring out what spell a creature cast that has no immediate visible effect.

entries.title: +Cryptozoology
entries.content: Used in the knowledge of creatures that should not be. Cryptozoologists are particularly astute at remembering legends that pertain to creatures, and gleaning hoaxes. Identifies Aberrations and Cryptids with +monster lore.

entries.title: Astronomy
entries.content: Cryptozoology can be used as a substitute for any kind of scientific research with regards to space, such as astronomy and astrophysics.

entries.title: Identify Hoax
entries.content: You can use cryptozoology in place of +Insight to glean if a creature is deceiving you or exaggerating details about the description of a creature they have encountered or heard about through word of mouth.

entries.title: Legendary Creatures
entries.content: Using a name or a basic description as a prompt, you can recall legends of ancient, powerful or noteworthy creatures. Such creatures usually have tales that have been written about them, or have stories that have been passed down that you can recount. Uses the "relevancy to the world" DC of (22-lvl).

entries.title: What do they want
entries.content: Creatures from the stars often have very strange interests in the mortal world. If you succeed on a +Monster Lore check against such a creature, you also learn about its interests in the world, such as requiring humanoids to reproduce or a keen interest in bovines. You can also glean this information from witnessing the phenomenon itself. Using the same DC as monster lore, you can witness cattle kidnapping or host infections and glean what type of creature is doing it

entries.title: Engineering
entries.content: Used to determine things related to structures, such as building them, finding weaknesses or the ability to make basic assumptions about its design (ex. a factory this large needs an exhaust vent somewhere). It can also be used to identify technology such as elevators, vehicles and other mechanical objects. Identifies Constructs using +Monster Lore.

entries.title: Hacking
entries.content: Where there exists technology, there exists a method to disable or gain control of it, even if only temporarily. Hacking always requires a hands-on approach.

entries.title: Identify Function
entries.content: Identify the purpose of particularly complex machinery. Depending on the size and scope of what you're looking at, this could only be a glance, or it could require a few minutes, and usually requires a hands-on approach.

entries.title: Structural Quirk
entries.content: Large scale buildings such as castles, watchtowers, walls, factories, prisons etc. Are all built with specific purposes, facilities and defenses in mind. You can use engineering to figure out aspects of these buildings like where exhaust/waste pipes lead, how far underground you might need to take to go underneath the structure, structural weaknesses etc.

entries.title: +History
entries.content: Used to recall events that happened in the past and prominent historical figures. Historians are good at identifying the flags of fallen nations, reading in dead languages and recognizing relics from the past. Does not have a specific creature type you can use monster lore with. Can be a suitable substitute for famous or infamous creatures hundreds or thousands of years old.

entries.title: Example DCs
entries.content: DifficultyDCExamplesMundane0Recalling an event/creature that happened/existed in the past 20 years.Requires Focus5Recalling an event/creature that happened/existed in the past 20-99 years.Trained10Identifying how a ritual took place in an ancient ruin, and how you might activate these mechanismsUnderstanding the roundabout significance of murals and other artsDifficult15Recalling an event/creature that happened/existed 100+ years agoVery Difficult20Understanding the roundabout significance of heiroglyphs/ancient languageMasterful25 Nearly Impossible30Creating full translations of hieroglyphs.Recalling an event/person that happened/existed 1000+ years agoAscended40Receiving ancestral memories, or briefly gazing into the past to see an event Common modifiers to make the task more difficult can include… - The event was specifically wiped from history, or the records of it are largely destroyed/kept secret - The text/piece you are examining is missing significant chunks - The event/entity was of little significance Common modifiers to make the task less difficult can include - You have a specific cultural significance to this history, or you were a local who lived through the era - The text/piece you are examining is in pristine condition, or based on a language you know - The event/entity was of great significance

entries.title: Identify Artifact
entries.content: Certain items may be tied to creatures or entities from the past. By identifying the craftsmanship and symbols on items, you can determine their origin/purpose.

entries.title: Interpret Hieroglyphs
entries.content: Ancient people often like to communicate without using the written word. You can interpret the meaning or significance in paintings, carvings, murals, statues and the like.

entries.title: Recall Past Events
entries.content: Major battles, great tragedies and turning points in civilization are all of interest to a historian. With some kind of prompting, you can recall information related to these events dating back tens, hundreds or even thousands of years.

entries.title: Recall Prominent Figures
entries.content: There are some names that withstand the test of time due to their accomplishments, inventions and atrocities. With some kind of prompting, you can recall details about specific people, creatures or cultures. This can include things such as their names, species, class, nicknames, organizations etc.

entries.title: Tell Story
entries.content: You can use your vast knowledge pool to weave together important threads in history, making for a story that is both entertaining and informative.

entries.title: +Local
entries.content: Used to know about cities, countries, individual people. Specifically their culture, traditions, legends, government, laws, customs, politics and prominent organizations Identifies Humanoids and Giants with +Monster Lore. Special: When using monster lore against humanoids that have class levels, you learn information about the class the creature possesses.

entries.title: Classes and Monster Lore
entries.content: Your GM is inevitably going to pit you up against fighters and Beast Trainers and all kinds of other creatures with classes. If you succeed, you learn the creatures level and everything about its +Class and archetype. If you exceed this DC by 5, you learn everything about the creature's fighting style, such as feats and techniques they often use.

entries.title: Recall Customs
entries.content: When dealing with foreign cultures, you can recall information about how to conduct yourself. This can be particularly useful when trying to gain someone's favor, such as royalty. You can also recall information about holidays, festivals and ceremonies.

entries.title: Recall Useful Locations
entries.content: You can recall the answer to a specific simple question such as "where can I buy potions?" Or "where does the royalty live?" Or "where can I hire someone to build something?" Something along those lines. These questions can be applied to the town you're in or, with a higher DC, something much bigger like a country or continent.

entries.title: Recognize Name or Face
entries.content: You can attach a name to a face, or vice versa. You generally understand aspects of this creatures reputation, their affiliations, their general capabilities etc. Exceptionally high rolls might even land you some secrets or gossip related to this creature.

entries.title: +Nature
entries.content: Used to detect oddities in the natural order and how to restore it. Can also be used to determine the weather, identify naturally occurring things like ores and flowers and elements unique to the Feywilds. Identifies Plants, Fey and Beasts using +monster lore. You know about natural processes like photosynthesis, the reproduction of creatures, and tectonic activity. You can do things like figuring out if a volcano is about to erupt, what could be causing plants to die or how long until an egg hatches. Identify Elements of Nature If it doesn't have a stat block and it isn't man-made or magic, then it probably falls under natural element. Nature allows you to identify animal behavior. It also lets you identify types of plants and minerals and their uses/dangers. It also allows you to tell if something is unnatural. Identify Weather Predict the weather, usually only for the day, but with higher checks, even farther into the future. You can also tell if these effects are naturally occurring or created by something unusual like magic.

entries.title: Example DCs
entries.content: DifficultyDCExamplesMundane0Identifying something common, like cropsRequires Focus5Identifying something common, but more specific like clayTrained10Determine if a weather effect is magicalDetermine when an egg will hatchIdentifying specific common species/substances like rose or coalDetermine the nature of "natural" phenomenon: this river was dammed by a beaver, volcanic debris killed these plants, natural substances leaked into this water to make it toxicDifficult15Determine if a small scale instance is "unnatural," such as if a creature had alchemical augmentationsIdentify specific uncommon species/substances like sequoia or goldVery Difficult20Figure out the days weather, and figure out the origin of anomalous weatherIdentify endangered/extinct species or rare/depleted resources like dodo or diamondDetermine if a large scale instance is "unnatural," such as if a forest was dying via the intervention of malicious creaturesMasterful25Determine the nature of "unnatural" phenomenon, such as what the source of is pollution, why the rain/river stopped, low fish populationNearly Impossible30Figure out tomorrow's weatherAscended40Identify the properties of entirely new species/substancesPredict natural disasters a week in advance Common modifiers to make the task more difficult can include… - Unable to interact with object (smell, taste, touch, as appropriate for substance) Common modifiers to make the task less difficult can include - Guides, handbooks and sketches

entries.title: Identify Elements of Nature
entries.content: If it doesn't have a stat block and it isn't man-made or magic, then it probably falls under natural element. Nature allows you to identify animal behavior. It also lets you identify types of plants and minerals and their uses/dangers. It also allows you to tell if something is unnatural.

entries.title: Identify Natural Phenomenon
entries.content: You know about natural processes like photosynthesis, the reproduction of creatures, and tectonic activity. You can do things like figuring out if a volcano is about to erupt, what could be causing plants to die or how long until an egg hatches.

entries.title: Identify Weather
entries.content: Predict the weather, usually only for the day, but with higher checks, even farther into the future. You can also tell if these effects are naturally occurring or created by something unusual like magic.

entries.title: +Planes
entries.content: Used to determine the nature of other planes. Can also be used to determine the destination of portals and where they might be located. Identifies Fiends, Celestials and Elementals.

entries.title: Disable or Destroy Portal
entries.content: DC 30 A task that is very difficult by one's self. Failure could mean getting stuck on the wrong side of the portal or hyper charging the portal to attract extraplanar creatures.

entries.title: Find Portal Location
entries.content: DC 30 Portals are strange phenomenon that tend to come and go, but there are signs that they exist miles away from where the portal is actually located, if you know where to look.

entries.title: Identify Portal
entries.content: DC 10 In the presence of a portal, you can tell where its destination plane is. Rolling higher can give more specific locations, more details about the portals creation and how often it gets used.

entries.title: +Religion
entries.content: Used in understanding/identifying gods and their followers. Church hierarchies, religious practices, symbols and scripture are all covered by this skill. Identifies Undead with +monster lore. It is assumed that if you are of a specific religion, you can do many of these things without rolling for your own faith. (DC 10) Followers of gods often wear regalia, use specific types of seals and put their gods symbol on their stuff. Sometimes just by looking at a building or a person you can tell where their religious affiliations lie. Recall Tenets (DC 15) When prompted by specific actions, you can tell whether or not these actions align with the deity in question. Good for finding fakers or guiding the faithful with poor memory. Accrue GBP (DC 15) You can figure out what kinds of things would appease a specific deity and earn you GBP. Identify Chosen (DC 20) Gods will often play favorites when it comes to specific people, peoples or items/places when deciding who will receive their blessing.

entries.title: Identify Chosen
entries.content: DC 20 Gods will often play favorites when it comes to specific people, peoples or items/places when deciding who will receive their blessing.

entries.title: Identify Religious Symbol
entries.content: DC 10 Followers of gods often wear regalia, use specific types of seals and put their gods symbol on their stuff. Sometimes just by looking at a building or a person you can tell where their religious affiliations lie.

entries.title: Recall Tenets
entries.content: DC 15 When prompted by specific actions, you can tell whether or not these actions align with the deity in question. Good for finding fakers or guiding the faithful with poor memory or habits.

entries.title: +Knowledge
entries.content: All knowledge uses INT. What you know. Knowledge is broken up into many different skills based on subject and each one has deals with the +Monster Lore of specific creature types. Monster lore isn’t its own skill, but it has its own functions separate from the rest of individual knowledge skills. If a subject or creature falls into multiple categories, then players can choose whichever they like more, but an individual character can't roll multiple checks with different skills. ALL knowledge requires proficiency to roll.

entries.title: +Monster Lore
entries.content: Adventurers are assumed to know basic things about monsters, like how constructs often lack anatomy or the properties of incorporeal creatures. Monster lore checks are for figuring out more information about specific monsters. Bestiary stat blocks are broken up into 4 sections: Defense, Offense, Weaknesses/Deep Lore and Other Traits. Rolling a monster lore check is something you can do as a free action once per turn. It is assumed that you also vocalize what you learn to your team. The base DC of a monster lore check is (10+creature's lvl). By succeeding on a monster lore check, you can figure out a creature's basic stats and one other category. Every 5 over the DC you roll, you figure out another category. If a creature has nothing in a category, you choose another. Creatures with the unique tag in their entry increase monster lore DC by 5. Humanoids with class levels function a bit weird. See Classes and Monster Lore for more details. Signature Skill Nerd Lore Dump

entries.title: Lore Dump (SS)
entries.content: You can rapidly assess the battlefield and communicate your knowledge. You can roll an unlimited number of monster lore checks each turn.

entries.title: Nerd (SS)
entries.content: When you succeed on a monster lore check, gain knowledge about an additional category.

entries.title: Who are you
entries.content: If you want to know who an individual creature is and their relevance to the world, rather than their stats and abilities, the DC is usually dependent on how popular they are. Typically (22-LvL) with additional reductions to the DC if they are famous, or in a position of power. This could also include increases to the DC if they work to keep their identity and exploits hidden, or live remotely.

entries.title: +Linguistics
entries.content: The lawyer skill that gets languages as a bonus. Uses INT.

entries.title: Cliff's Notes (SS)
entries.content: You can summarize 10 standard pages of written text per one minute of reading.

entries.title: Decode (P)
entries.content: You can write things in ways that are difficult to interpret without a cipher, such as disguising your forbidden ritual as a cookbook. The DC to your code is 10+(linguistics bonus). Creatures that know the code can read encoded text, but creatures that don't will interpret the text as something else. To read encoded text, you must first crack the code, usually requiring at least a week of research. At the end of the week, roll a linguistics check versus the writer’s DC to crack the code.

entries.title: Interpret Text
entries.content: You peruse a book, contract or other written work to quickly summarize what it says in one minute. The DC for most works is 10, but it changes based on the size and complexity of the work at hand. Failure indicates more time is necessary to understand what the text contains. Finding oddities in a contract requires an opposed linguistics check.

entries.title: Languages
entries.content: Common Languages | Common | Pretty much every intelligent creature speaks this. | | ---------- | ------------------------------------------------------- | | Infernal | Language of fiends | | Celestial | Language of celestials | | Sylvan | Language of fey | | Primordial | The language of elementals and ancient beings | | Aquan | The language of sea creatures. Functions underwater | Racial languages Racial languages are typically named after the race that created them. Names that aren't self evident are put in (parenthesis) | Goblin | Manganese (anime) | Draconic | | ------ | ----------------- | -------- | | Giant | Orc | | | Elven | Giant | | Uncommon languages | Morse Code | A binary code of two sounds mixed to create letters in an alphabet. Communication in Morse code is very slow and impractical, but still serves a useful function for subtle communication. | | ------------- | ---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | | Sign Language | An entirely visual language that consists of hand gestures that translates directly to common. | | Necril | Language of undead | Secret languages | Druidic | Typically only passed down from teacher to student with specialized training in nature magic. | | -------------- | ------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------ | | Thieves Cant | The language of thieves and criminals. It's more like speaking in code than another language entirely. | | Semaphore | A code that uses the position of flags to create letters in an alphabet. Communication and semaphore is very slow and impractical, but still serves a useful function for subtle communication. | | Bronze | A unique language that is only understood by creatures with negative intelligence. Typically only spoken by barbarians. | | Fairy Flare | A language that is only used by a specific type of fairies that cause their bioluminescent antennae to flash in specific patterns. Even if you know the language, it cannot be spoken without the proper biology/equipment, only understood. | | Ancient Dragon | A particularly old dialect of draconic, spoken only by particularly old creatures that lived during The Age of Dragons. |

entries.title: Polyglot (P)
entries.content: With proficiency in linguistics you gain a new language for every level you have, and every new level you gain. You cannot learn secret languages without special forms of training.

entries.title: Read Lips (SS)
entries.content: With a clear view of a creature's face, you can understand what the creature is saying as long as you understand the language they are speaking in.

entries.title: Secret-ish (SS)
entries.content: You can select secret languages as bonus languages. Immediately gain 1 when you acquire signature skill.

entries.title: Write Official Documents (P)
entries.content: Make contracts, forge signatures and make (fake?) documents look official. Noticing oddities in such documents requires an opposed linguistics check.

entries.title: +Medicine
entries.content: Be a doctor in a world filled with people whose touch brings people back to life. Uses WIS or INT

entries.title: Assess the Wounded
entries.content: A creature that is wounded can be examined to determine how they were injured, how long ago it happened and whether they are in grave danger of dying. This also functions on corpses. The more complex the ailment and the longer the duration since it happened, the higher the DC. Duration may reverse in scaling in the case of diseases, where more time passing makes the symptoms more obvious.

entries.title: Band-Aid Fix (SS)
entries.content: You can apply first aid as a swift action.

entries.title: First Aid (P)
entries.content: As a standard action you can cure creatures of status ailments to a creature within reach. You can remove all sickened stacks, bleed and blurred vision. The DC of removing any of these conditions is the DC of the ability that inflicted them. If they have no DC, then assume the class/offense trait DC. When you succeed in providing first aid to heal a condition, the creature heals 1 HP (Healing). Performing first aid without a first aid kit imposes a -5 penalty.

entries.title: Frankenstein's Secrets (SS)
entries.content: Permanent devastating effects to a creature's body, such as having a limb severed or an eye gouged out can now be restored with a DC 30 check. The wound must be fresh and the body part is intact. Failure inflicts drained 2 and destroys the body part.

entries.title: Identify Affliction or Curse (P)
entries.content: By spending a minute examining someone who suffers from an affliction or curse, you can determine its exact effects. You also know the exact method on how to cure the affliction or remove the curse. T1 afflictions have a DC of 5. +5 to DC for each tier. Curses have a DC of 10+spell lvl. If the effect was not a spell, then DC 10+(1/2 lvl of entity who inflicted curse).

entries.title: Improvised Implements (SS)
entries.content: You take no penalty to medicine checks when you do not have a first aid kit. When you provide first aid, you heal 3 hp (Healing)..

entries.title: Long Term Care (P)
entries.content: Resting under the care of a creature that is proficient in medicine lets you recover 2 drain every day instead of 1. You also qualify for removing many types of Afflictions, both physical and mental.

entries.title: Prevent Recovery (P)
entries.content: If you have defeated creatures in your custody that you don't want to be waking up at inconvenient times, you can choose to treat them so that way they do not naturally recover hp (including regeneration and FH). This requires a few minutes to perform, and lasts 24 hours.

entries.title: +Perception
entries.content: All creatures are considered proficient in perception, unless they are mindless. Uses WIS. Perception is the mechanism through which players can notice the unnoticed in the imagination game. Traps, hidden loot, secret entrances, unusual stimuli are a handful of the many things this skill is used for. Conditions such as bad weather and listening through walls can make perception difficult, or even impossible. Sleeping gives a -5 penalty. Detect Hidden Presence Once a round, by spending 15ft of movement, or a swift action, you can make a perception check to find hidden creatures/objects, or their general direction if they have total cover. Signature Skill One Eye Open * You take no penalty to perception from sleeping. * You perceive and remember things that happened around you while sleeping when you wake.  * You can choose to automatically wake up for any reason.  * You are not DD by default when asleep. Quick Scan You can Detect Hidden Presence as a free action, but still only once per turn.

entries.title: Detect Hidden Presence
entries.content: Once a round, by spending 15ft of movement, or a swift action, you can make a perception check to find hidden creatures/objects, or their general direction if they have total cover.

entries.title: Keep Watch
entries.content: This is a downtime activity most commonly performed by people to protect areas, such as guards to a palace or a adventurer at camp. A creature who is keeping watch can do little more than eat snacks, wonder around and make idle chatter while they keep watch. A creature who is adequately keeping watch (AKA isn't sleeping on the job or indulging in other activities), automatically gets a perception check to notice would-be intruders and adversaries. Guards also often get checks to see through the disguises of criminals, or finding concealed items if they are frisking people.

entries.title: One Eye Open (SS)
entries.content: * You take no penalty to perception from sleeping. * You perceive and remember things that happened around you while sleeping. * You can choose to automatically wake up for any reason. * You are not DD by default when asleep.

entries.title: Quick Scan (SS)
entries.content: You can Detect Hidden Presence as a free action, but still only once per turn.

entries.title: +Persuasion
entries.content: Convincing others to do things with your words. Uses CHA.

entries.title: Contacts (SS)
entries.content: You always know at least a few places in town drifters are welcome. The time to gather information is always less than 10 minutes.

entries.title: Dice vs Roleplay
entries.content: Persuasion is one of those skills where you can reasonably skip past lots of dialogue in order to get the desired result more quickly and efficiently. However, this is a role-playing game. So regardless of how big someone's persuasion bonus is, no matter the suggestion, you must persuade something a creature could conceivably be willing to do on their own, in the right circumstances. On the opposite end, even if you do not possess the persuasion skill, you might still be able to convince an NPC of something if you present the right information in the right circumstances. A gruff pirate might not be interested in a -1 CHA fighter, but if he says he knows where treasure is the pirate has been hunting for decades, that can change his mind. tl;dr Role-play and dice are 2 different tools to acquire the same result, each with their own benefits and drawbacks. Neither should be dismissed as nonviable and one should not be required in order to succeed at the other. The skill is generally useful when you are uncertain about a lot with the NPC in question, like with Serendipity, or you want to advance the plot more quickly.

entries.title: Example DCs
entries.content: DifficultyDCExamplesMundane0Assistance with a simple task, like cutting veggiesGathering information on a topic people are concerned withRequires Focus5Assistance with a time consuming task,Trained10Gathering less relevant information: did x person wander into town lately?Difficult15Gathering dangerous information, the kind of thing that makes people say "you didn't hear it from me, but…"Very Difficult20Convince people to do something dangerous and potentially life threatening, like escorting you through wilderness, standing up against a bully or breaking the lawMasterful25Gathering secretive information, such as hideouts, passwords or scandalsNearly Impossible30Convince people to do something against their ethos, but could be conceivable, such as killing baby monsters, putting someone out of their misery or ditching the religion of a corrupt priestAscended40Convincing people inconceivable things, like killing family members, nobles donating their wealth to charity or rallying commoners against an ancient dragon (given adequate circumstances) Common modifiers to make the task more difficult can include… - Racism - Your questions/requests are offensive or closely guarded secrets - You are not well liked by the person/people you are persuading Common modifiers to make the task less difficult can include - You are well liked by the person/people you are persuading\ - You hold a position of authority over the person/people you are persuading\ - The information you are looking for is well known or noteworthy

entries.title: Gather Information
entries.content: When in a settlement, you can roll persuasion to find the answer to a simple question by speaking to people in town. The time it takes and the difficulty scales with the size of the settlement, people's general attitude toward you and how knowledgeable they are on the subject. Asking about where someone lives in a village could take minutes, but asking in a large city could take hours. Creatures proficent in persuasion automatically find information with a DC of (10++Persuasion bonus) or lower.

entries.title: Mercy Breeds Cooperation (SS)
entries.content: When you defeat a foe, you can instead choose to give it the time out condition for 1 hour.

entries.title: Persuade
entries.content: When you ask something of someone, such as information or aid, they may be on the fence about whether or not they should answer and persuasion gets them to your side. The difficulty of the check is based on how well you know the person, how difficult the request is and what the consequences of telling you might be. Even if you do persuade someone to do something, it may come with caveats such as favors or payment.

entries.title: +Profession
entries.content: The skill for the working class fellas. Defaults to WIS, but see the experienced feature. If you are ever quizzed on particular aspects of a profession and you are not proficient, you must roll an intelligence check to answer correctly (DC 10 for most professions). Proficient creatures automatically know most aspects of their profession. Hard Work Every day you work, you earn money. See economics for more details.\ Your talent could be anything from music, to craftsmanship to working as an innkeeper. Experienced (P) You can select what ability score to use to determine your profession bonus for all your professions. Your given ability score colors the approach you have to your professions.

entries.title: Experienced (P)
entries.content: You can select what Ability Score to use to determine your profession bonus for all your professions. Your given ability score colors the approach you have to your professions.

entries.title: Hard Work
entries.content: Every day you work, you earn money. See Economics for more details. Your talent could be anything from music, to craftsmanship to working as an innkeeper.

entries.title: Recall
entries.content: If you are ever quizzed on particular aspects of a profession and you are not proficient, you must roll an WIS check to answer correctly (DC 10 for most professions). Proficient creatures automatically know most aspects of their profession.

entries.title: +Sleight of Hand
entries.content: Pocket things while staying undetected. Quietly open locks and turn off dangerous devices. Uses DEX.

entries.title: Conceal Weapon (P)
entries.content: You can store light weapons in all kinds of places. As a swift action you can draw up to two concealed weapon to attack a creature with. If they fail a perception check vs your sleight of hand, they are DD this turn. If you're in a crowded area, or in some kind of standoff where you aren't quite fighting your enemy yet, you can even use this skill as a replacement for stealth to surprise your enemy. The enemy must be aware of you in order use a concealed weapon, otherwise it is stealth.

entries.title: Disable Device (P)
entries.content: Pressure plates and dangerous magic crystals are at your mercy if you’re able to get your hands on them if you notice them before you get roasted. You can temporarily or permanently disabled the dangerous dungeon decorations with a DC based on the complexity of the device. Simple tripwires are easy enough but a soul gem firing arcane bolts could be tricky. If you pass the DC by 5 or more, you can repurpose the trap to suit your needs. The more difficult the DC, the longer it takes to disable, with an upper limit of 30 seconds. Disabling devices without thieves' Tools imposes a -5 penalty.

entries.title: Fast Hands (SS)
entries.content: You can pick any lock and disable any devices as a FRA.

entries.title: Is....this your sword (SS)
entries.content: You can use thievery to steal sheathed and stored weapons at no penalty, and replace them with other items of similar size/shape.

entries.title: Pick Lock (P)
entries.content: Open locked objects with a DC dependent on the complexity of the lock. Mages and very skilled locksmiths might have nearly impossible locks while common doors are child's play. The more difficult the DC, the longer it takes to open a lock, with an upper limit of 30 seconds. Failing a check has no consequences unless time is short or there are other security measures in place, allowing you to take 20. Picking locks without thieves' Tools imposes a -5 penalty.

entries.title: Thievery
entries.content: If a target is unaware of you or otherwise distracted, you can attempt to steal items loosely attached to their person or in their pockets. Rolled versus the +Perception of the individual. Anybody else vigilantly standing guard also gets a check with a penalty based on circumstance. You can also pilfer unattended objects, with anybody nearby or standing watch getting a check to notice your misdeeds. Objects that are in some way affixed to a creature, such as a sword in its hilt, take a -5 penalty to SoH when you try to steal them.

entries.title: +Stealth
entries.content: Moving around while remaining unseen. If someone is looking for you in your immediate vicinity and you don't want to be seen, you roll stealth. Uses DEX. Special: Stealth and size Size provides no numerical benefit or penalty. The only thing that size effects relative to stealth is your ability to gain cover: Larger creatures have a harder time figuring out places to hide, while smaller creatures find it very easy.

entries.title: Dampen Presence (SS)
entries.content: Blind sense does not function against you.

entries.title: Hide
entries.content: As long as you have Total Cover, Partial Concealment or Total Concealment, you can attempt to hide from your enemies as an immediate action. You remain hidden until no longer have total cover/concealment, you cast a spell, use a special ability, attack or someone spots you with +Perception. When you attack a creature after successfully hiding from them, they are DD until the end of your turn.

entries.title: Pass Without Trace (P)
entries.content: Characters with stealth proficiency can travel without leaving an obvious trail behind. Attempts to track them suffer a -5 penalty.

entries.title: Stalker (SS)
entries.content: Whenever you are Trailing (P) someone, you can get really up close and personal. You no longer take a -5 penalty for getting up close and personal to hear conversations and such.

entries.title: Trailing (P)
entries.content: Use hide to trail someone without them noticing you. It is assumed that you keep your distance, only observing broad strokes what they do, but not things they say, write down etc. If you want to get a closer look to find such details, you take a -5 penalty to stealth. Using stealth to stalk people can potentially become impossible without first circumventing protections against spying. How often you roll stealth checks is determined by the situation, opposed by the creature you stalk. | Every 10 | Target is wary, is in a place you are unwelcome or has bodyguards on watch | | --------------- | -------------------------------------------------------------------------- | | Every few hours | Target is in a public place with limited spaces to hide | | Every few days | Target is isolated in an environment with ample hiding spots |

entries.title: +Survival
entries.content: The day to day things that need to be done when out and about adventuring far from home in the wilderness. Uses WIS.

entries.title: Advanced Tracking (SS)
entries.content: You can use survival to track creatures that Fly and swim using scent, even if you aren't aquatic. It's harder to determine "ideal conditions" in these types of environments, so the DC is mostly based off of how long ago the creature was present.

entries.title: Example DCs
entries.content: | Difficulty | DC | Examples | | ----------------- | --- | ------------------------------------------------------ | | Mundane | 0 | Navigating a road | | Requires Focus | 5 | Navigating a beaten path | | Trained | 10 | Navigating a location with landmarks, like a forest or | | Difficult | 15 | | | Very Difficult | 20 | | | Masterful | 25 | | | Nearly Impossible | 30 | | | Ascended | 40 | |

entries.title: Extract Resources
entries.content: You can skin creatures, extract organs/fluids and other resources from recently killed creatures. This usually takes 1 minute. You get an amount of value equal to (10+the creature's LvL). Each one requires 1 inventory slot. Particularly dangerous or delicate operations require proficiency and a survival check (usually 10+creature's level), with higher value multipliers. A failed attempt represents the resources being destroyed.

entries.title: Live off the Land (P)
entries.content: Characters proficient in survival can sustain themselves and 4 other medium or smaller creatures in environments with suitable sources of food and water. Each size above medium doubles its resource consumption.

entries.title: Navigation
entries.content: You can attempt to not travel in circles when moving through unfamiliar territory. Failure indicates that a lesser amount of progress is made towards reaching your destination, or arriving at an unintended destination.

entries.title: Sniff Them Out (SS)
entries.content: You gain +Blindsense (Scent 5 ft) If you already have scent, increase its range by 5 ft.

entries.title: Tracking (P)
entries.content: You can attempt to follow footprints, broken branches, scent trails and other disturbed natural elements to figure out where a creature has been and where it’s going. With ideal conditions you can follow trails made days or even weeks ago (if you don't believe me, look at dino footprint fossils :^). The longer it's been since the creature has been there and the less ideal the conditions, the higher the DC.

entries.title: Skills
entries.content: Here's the list STR Skills +Athletics +Intimidation DEX Skills +Acrobatics +Stealth +Sleight of Hand INT Skills +Linguistics Alchemy Arcana +Cryptozoology Engineering +History +Local +Nature +Planes +Religion WIS Skills +Perception +Medicine +Insight +Profession +Survival CHA Skills +Deception +Disguise +Intimidation +Persuasion

entries.title: Acid Splash
entries.content: +Cantrips Tags: Poison Action: Standard | Range | 30 ft | | -------------- | ---------- | | Target/AoE | 1 creature | | Duration | 1 round | | Save | - | Your hand, or your staff/seal turn a sickly green color and start bubbling. You flick it towards a creature, making a Ranged Spell Attack, and a sticky glob of caustic goo flies at them. Deals CL poison damage.
